PT P
PN CN108467383-A
TI Cogeneration of ethylene carbonate in ethylene oxide device by contacting mixed solution containing ethylene carbonate and ethylene glycol with mixed gas, reacting absorption liquid with catalyst, and removing unreacted carbon dioxide gas.
AU LIU X
   LV Q
   BIE F
AE LV Q (LVQQ-Individual)
   SHANDONG LUANN COAL CHEM ENG TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201870288U
AB    NOVELTY - Cogeneration of ethylene carbonate in ethylene oxide device comprises contacting mixed solution containing ethylene carbonate and ethylene glycol with mixed gas from outlet of ethylene oxide reactor in absorption tower to absorb ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide in mixed gas; reacting absorption liquid containing ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide in esterification reactor under action of esterification catalyst to generate ethylene carbonate; and removing unreacted carbon dioxide gas by conventional method using mixed liquid after reaction, and collecting reaction liquid.
   USE - Method for cogeneration of ethylene carbonate in ethylene oxide device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has small primary investment, and small water consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The absorption is carried out at 5-80 degrees C and 0.5-5.0 MPa. The esterification is done at 100-200 degrees C for 10-120 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethylene carbonate and ethylene glycol have weight specific gravity of 0.1-10 and gas-liquid weight ratio of 1.0-3.0. The esterification catalyst comprises halogenated alkaline earth metal, alkylamine, quaternary ammonium salt, organic halogenated sulfonate or organic halogenated hydrazine, halogen sulfide, brominated or iodinated alkali metal, organotin, antimony, bismuth, organic halogen, phosphonium, anion exchange resin, tungsten or molybdenum oxide and/or molecular sieve.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN114320447-A
TI Method for high-efficiency utilization and            secondary sealing for carbon resource of deep-difficult            coal mining , involves performing low temperature and            high pressure compression treatment to capture carbon            dioxide, and using seamless steel pipe to inject            supercritical carbon dioxide into coal bed.
AU XIAO Y
   LIU Q
   TIAN X
   ZHU Y
   LI Z
   SONG D
   HE X
   QIU L
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202256035E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling a deep drilling                hole from a ground to a difficult mining coal layer                (S101). The permeability of a sampling sample, the                gas content and a difference of carbon dioxide and                gas adsorption are tested (S102). A position of the                ground drilling, drilling distance and drilling                depth are determined. The drilling is adopted                (S103) to extract gas of the coal bed. A gas power                station is built (S104) on the ground and a carbon                storage workshop. The extracted gas is burned to                generate electricity. The generated energy is                extracted during an extraction process and a liquid                injection process. The carbon dioxide generated                (S105) by gas combustion is collected. Low                temperature and high pressure compression treatment                is performed (S106) to the capture carbon dioxide.                The supercritical carbon dioxide is injected (S107)                by using the seamless steel pipe into the coal bed                through the drilling hole.
   USE - Method for high-efficiency utilization and                secondary sealing for carbon resource of                deep-difficult coal mining.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensuring high safety with                performance stable , and uses single drilling to                realize efficient utilization and secondary sealing                of deep coal mining area carbon resource, making                use process of the carbon resource completely                isolated from the outside, strengthening the                greenhouse gas emission reduction and the control                of important coal-fired flue gas pollutant. In                addition, the gas generating amount can be used by                the outside to save cost, low carbon and                environment-friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for high-efficiency utilization and                secondary sealing for carbon resource of                deep-difficult coal mining. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   Step for drilling a deep drilling hole from a                ground to a difficult mining coal layer                (S101)
   Step for testing the permeability of a                sampling sample, the gas content and a difference                of carbon dioxide and gas adsorption (S102)
   Step for adopting drilling to extract gas of                the coal bed. (S103)
   Step for building a gas power station on the                ground and a carbon storage workshop. (S104)
   Step for generating the carbon dioxide by gas                combustion is collected. (S105)
   Step for performing low temperature and high                pressure compression treatment to the capture                carbon dioxide. (S106)
   Step for injecting the supercritical carbon                dioxide by using the seamless steel pipe into the                coal bed through the drilling hole (S107)
   Step for withdrawing the seamless steel pipe,                permanent sealing the drilling hole, when the                entire process is in a closed state. (S108)
DC Q49 (Mining (E21)); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN109966913-A; CN210079250-U
TI Microwave discharge plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas device, comprises carbon dioxide bottle and methane bottle, where carbon dioxide bottle and methane bottle are respectively connected to first cavity through gas pipe.
AU PAN J
   SUN C
   ZHANG C
   XIU X
   SONG Y
   DU J
AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 201960594M
AB    NOVELTY - A microwave discharge plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas device comprises a carbon dioxide bottle and a methane bottle, where the carbon dioxide bottle and the methane bottle are respectively connected to the first cavity through a gas pipe, and the gas pipe is provided with a flow meter, the first cavity is connected with a microwave resonant cavity, and the microwave resonant cavity is connected with a catalytic reforming reactor, and the catalytic reforming reactor is a double-layered cylindrical structure, the other end of the inner layer of the catalytic reforming reactor is connected with the second cavity, and the first cavity and the second cavity are respectively provided with a methane concentration sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, and the refrigeration mechanism, the heating mechanism, the methane concentration sensor and the carbon dioxide concentration sensor are electrically connected to the single chip microcomputer, respectively.
   USE - Microwave discharge plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas device.
   ADVANTAGE - The microwave discharge plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas device has better effect of calculating the conversion rate of the plasma catalytic reforming reaction according to the gas concentration detected by the sensor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A microwave discharge plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas device comprises a carbon dioxide bottle and a methane bottle, where the carbon dioxide bottle and the methane bottle are respectively connected to the first cavity through a gas pipe, and the gas pipe is provided with a flow meter, the first cavity is connected with a microwave resonant cavity, and the microwave resonant cavity is connected with a catalytic reforming reactor, and the catalytic reforming reactor is a double-layered cylindrical structure, one end of the inner layer of the catalytic reforming reactor is connected with the microwave resonant cavity, the outer layer is provided with a water passage, and the water passage forms a circulation loop with the water pipe, one side of the microwave cavity is provided with a microwave power source, the microwave power source is connected to a pulse modulator, and the pulse modulator is connected to a rectangular waveguide, and the rectangular waveguide extends into the microwave cavity, the middle portion of the water pipe is covered with a refrigeration mechanism and a heating mechanism, the other end of the inner layer of the catalytic reforming reactor is connected with the second cavity, and the first cavity and the second cavity are respectively provided with a methane concentration sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, and the refrigeration mechanism, the heating mechanism, the methane concentration sensor and the carbon dioxide concentration sensor are electrically connected to the single chip microcomputer, respectively. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for microwave discharging plasma catalytic reforming greenhouse gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is nickel/aluminum oxide catalyst.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)

PT P
PN CN108272695-A; CN108272695-B
TI Extraction of cosmetic tea polyphenol by drying            and crushing tea, adding distilled water, homogenizing,            water bath heating, controlling temperature, adding            ethanol, stirring, cooling, solvent extracting with            tetrahydrofuran and processing.
AU 
AE CHANGSHA XIAOXIN NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU JIMI BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018567259
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of cosmetic tea polyphenol involves                drying and crushing tea, adding distilled water as                solvent, homogenizing to obtain water paste agent,                water bath heating, controlling water paste agent                temperature, adding ethanol, stirring, naturally                cooling to obtain slurry a, solvent extracting with                tetrahydrofuran to obtain slurry b, and                ultrasonically processing to obtain slurry c;                supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, adding                methanol, ethyl acetate and Polysorbate (RTM:                polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid esters) in                carbon dioxide fluid, extracting to obtain tea                polyphenol crude product, separating tea polyphenol                crude product A from slurry, supercritical carbon                dioxide extracting residual slurry, adding acetone                and methanol in carbon dioxide fluid, extracting to                obtain tea polyphenol crude product B, and                separating tea polyphenol crude product B from                slurry; and adding crude products A and B, adding                acid, adjusting pH value, and separating and                purifying.
   USE - Method for extracting cosmetic tea                polyphenol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is scientific and                environment-friendly, and has short time, low                energy consumption and high extraction rate and                purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of cosmetic tea polyphenol                involves: (A) drying and crushing tea into 60-80                meshes, adding distilled water as solvent,                homogenizing into vacuum high-speed shearing                homogenizer to obtain water paste agent, water bath                heating, controlling water paste agent temperature                at 60-80 degrees C, adding ethanol, stirring for                20-30 minutes at speed of 200-300                revolutions/minute (rpm), naturally cooling for                0.5-1 hour to obtain slurry a, solvent extracting                with tetrahydrofuran to obtain slurry b, and                ultrasonically processing for 10-15 minutes at                ultrasonic wave frequency of 15-18 KHz to obtain                slurry c; (B) supercritical carbon dioxide                extracting, adding methanol, ethyl acetate and                Polysorbate (RTM: polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty                acid esters) in carbon dioxide fluid, extracting at                45-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours at pressure of 18-25                MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-25 L/hour to                obtain tea polyphenol crude product, separating tea                polyphenol crude product A from slurry at 35-45                degrees C at pressure of 3.5-5.5 MPa, supercritical                carbon dioxide extracting residual slurry, adding                acetone and methanol in carbon dioxide fluid,                extracting at 45-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours at                pressure of 18-25 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate                of 20-25 L/hour to obtain tea polyphenol crude                product B, and separating tea polyphenol crude                product B from slurry at 35-45 degrees C at                pressure of 3.5-5.5 MPa; and (C) adding crude                products A and B, adding acid, adjusting pH value                to 3.5-5.0, and separating and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dosage ratio of                  tea and distilled water is 1:10-15. The                  concentration of ethanol is 10-20%. The dosage                  ratio of tea and ethanol is 1:0.5-0.8. The total                  amount of methanol, ethyl acetate and polysorbate                  is 17-23%. The weight ratio of methanol, ethyl                  acetate and Polysorbate (RTM: polyoxyethylene                  sorbitan fatty acid esters) is                  1-2:0.8-2.5:1.2-2.2. The total dosage of acetone                  and methanol is 15-20%. The weight ratio of                  acetone and methanol is 1.2-1.6:0.5-0.8. The                  concentration of acetic acid is 20-25%.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)
MC A10-E07; A10-E08A; A10-E08B; A12-V04C; D08-B09A3

PT P
PN CN109516489-A
TI Recycling method for zero discharge of wastewater involves sequentially performing calcium chloride synthesis, sodium hypochlorite synthesis, carbon dioxide and dry ice synthesis, anti-freeze brick synthesis, and potassium sulfate synthesis.
AU WANG F
AE INNER MONGOLIA BADI IND TECHNOLOGY CO (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 201929491P
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling method for zero discharge of wastewater involves sequentially performing calcium chloride synthesis, sodium hypochlorite synthesis, carbon dioxide and dry ice synthesis, anti-freeze brick synthesis, and potassium sulfate synthesis. The calcium chloride synthesis involves reacting hydrochloric acid and limestone to form a calcium chloride solution, carbon dioxide gas and water, adjusting pH of calcium chloride solution to 8.5-9, filtering, drying and granulating. Sodium hypochlorite synthesis involves reacting sodium hydroxide and chlorine in an absorption tower to form sodium hypochlorite, and waste sulfuric acid of the dry liquid chlorine is used as raw material for synthesizing potassium sulfate. The carbon dioxide and dry ice synthesis involves washing carbon dioxide gas generated in the synthesis of calcium chloride, compressing, cooling, purifying, drying and liquefying to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, and performing deep processing of gas to obtain dry ice.
   USE - Recycling method for zero discharge of wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The generation of waste gas and waste residue can be prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling method for zero discharge of wastewater involves sequentially performing calcium chloride synthesis, sodium hypochlorite synthesis, carbon dioxide and dry ice synthesis, anti-freeze brick synthesis, and potassium sulfate synthesis. The calcium chloride synthesis involves reacting hydrochloric acid and limestone to form a calcium chloride solution, carbon dioxide gas and water, adjusting pH of calcium chloride solution to 8.5-9 to obtain a reaction liquid, passing the reaction liquid through a plate and frame filter, drying the filtrate, and granulating. Sodium hypochlorite synthesis involves reacting sodium hydroxide and chlorine in an absorption tower to form sodium hypochlorite, and waste sulfuric acid of the dry liquid chlorine is used as raw material for synthesizing potassium sulfate. The carbon dioxide and dry ice synthesis involves washing carbon dioxide gas generated in the synthesis of calcium chloride, compressing, cooling, purifying, drying and liquefying to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, and performing deep processing of carbon dioxide gas to obtain dry ice. Anti-freeze brick synthesis involves mixing calcium chloride filter mud, river sand, stone material, cement and water, stirring uniformly to obtain concrete, placing in a mold, and curing. Synthesis of potassium sulfate involves reacting spent sulfuric acid used for drying chlorine in the synthesis of sodium hypochlorite with potassium chloride, cooling and separating to obtain potassium sulfate and hydrochloric acid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for recycling system for zero discharge of wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydrochloric acid to limestone is 2.0-2.4:1. The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 35 %mass. The concentration of calcium carbonate in limestone is more than 95%. The anti-freeze brick is prepared using a mixture of 25-35 pts. wt. calcium chloride filter mud, 15-25 pts. wt. river sand, 25-35 pts. wt. stone, 8-12 pts. wt. cement and 8-12 pts. wt. water. The filter mud contains 0.3-0.7 %mass iron hydroxide, 0.2-0.4 %mass magnesium hydroxide, 0.06-0.1 %mass aluminum hydroxide, and remaining calcium chloride.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E31-C; E31-N05C; E33-C; E34-B02; E34-C02; E34-D02; E35-U03

PT P
PN CN108614076-A
TI Method for evaluating geological storage of carbon            dioxide, involves selecting core sample to determine            core sample characteristics, cutting core sample and            surface polishing, followed by performing, determining,            analyzing and calculating.
AU YUAN G
   CAO Y
   ZAN N
   WANG Y
   XI K
   YANG T
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
GA 2018803359
AB    NOVELTY - A geological storage of carbon dioxide                evaluating method involves selecting core sample to                determine the core sample characteristics, cutting                the core sample and surface polishing, performing                indoor water rock simulation experiments,                processing the core sample after the experiment to                remove the solution, analyzing the core sample,                determining the reaction mineral, analyzing the                solution ion after the test reaction concentration,                calculating the mineral saturation index,                determining the indoor water rock simulation                experiment, under the constraints of actual                geological conditions, the mineral composition of                the oil layer in the target area, the pore ratio                and the average chemical composition of the                formation fluid, the initial mineral and fluid data                of the oil layer used to a numerical simulation                model, long-term carbon dioxide charging of                simulated oil and gas layers, quantitative                evaluation of carbon dioxide dissolved storage and                carbon dioxide mineral storage.
   USE - Method for evaluating geological storage of                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables evaluating geological                storage of carbon dioxide with improving the                accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A geological storage of carbon dioxide                evaluating method involves selecting a core sample                to determine the core sample characteristics,                cutting the core sample and surface polishing the                cut core sample, performing indoor water rock                simulation experiments on core samples, processing                the core sample after the experiment to remove the                solution after the experimental reaction, analyzing                the core sample after the test reaction,                determining the reaction mineral and the new                mineral type and composition after the experiment,                analyzing the solution ion after the test reaction                concentration, calculating the mineral saturation                index, determining the reaction process of the                indoor water rock simulation experiment, carrying                out long-term numerical simulation experiments,                under the constraints of actual geological                conditions, the simulated temperature is T' and the                simulated partial pressure of carbon dioxide is the                time of simulation, the mineral composition of the                oil layer in the target area, the pore ratio and                the average chemical composition of the formation                fluid, the initial mineral and initial fluid data                of the oil layer per unit area of 1 cm3 used to                establish a numerical simulation model, long-term                carbon dioxide charging of simulated oil and gas                layers, quantitative evaluation of carbon dioxide                dissolved storage and carbon dioxide mineral                storage.

PN CN113351172-A
TI Preparing denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material comprises e.g. uniformly dispersing the graphene oxide in deionized water, ultrasonically stirring, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust pH to obtain mixed solution.
AU PAN Y
   WANG G
   ZHAO Y
   LIANG Y
   LIU S
AE ANHUI YUANCHEN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7584U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material comprises (1) uniformly dispersing the graphene oxide (GO) in deionized water under stirring, ultrasonically uniformly stirring, (2) adding specific amount of tetraethyl silicate to the absolute ethanol solution to obtain composite solution B, (3) slowly dropping solution A into solution B under stirring to obtain mixed solution C, (4) placing the mixed solution C of step (3) in a 30 degrees C water bath, (5) adding ethanol to the gel in step (4) for aging, and adding n-hexane for replacement, (6) taking out the sample replaced in step (5) and freeze-drying to obtain dry silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel, (7) dissolving specific amount of catalyst active component precursors, catalyst additives, and cosolvent oxalic acid in deionized water, (8) adding the solution D obtained in step (7) to the silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel, and (9) adding compound E in step (8) to a toluene solution of gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption material: has denitration and carbon dioxide adsorption; and can realize carbon dioxide capture while realizing flue gas nitrogen oxides (NOx) treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material comprises (1) uniformly dispersing the graphene oxide (GO) in deionized water under stirring, ultrasonically uniformly stirring, and adding hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH to obtain a mixed solution A, (2) adding specific amount of tetraethyl silicate to the absolute ethanol solution to obtain composite solution B, (3) slowly dropping solution A into solution B under stirring to obtain mixed solution C, (4) placing the mixed solution C of step (3) in a 30 degrees C water bath, adding 10% ammonia ethanol solution dropwise, adjusting the pH to 7, and then placing in a 40 degrees C water bath until it gels, (5) adding ethanol to the gel in step (4) for aging, and adding n-hexane for replacement, (6) taking out the sample replaced in step (5) and freeze-drying to obtain dry silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel, (7) dissolving specific amount of catalyst active component precursors, catalyst additives, and cosolvent oxalic acid in deionized water to obtain mixed solution D, (8) adding the solution D obtained in step (7) to the silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel, soaking, and drying, calcining to obtain compound E, and (9) adding compound E in step (8) to a toluene solution of gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane and soaking it at 80 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, drying at 80 degrees C, and obtaining the denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorption material prepared by the method for preparing denitration synergistic carbon dioxide adsorption material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the concentration of graphene oxide (GO) is 10 mg/ml, and the pH of the mixed solution is 2. The mass of tetraethyl silicate and ethanol in the step (2) is 1:3. In the step (3), the dropping speed of the mixed solution B is 10 ml/minute, and the mass ratio of tetraethyl silicate and graphene oxide is 100:(1-9). The catalyst active component precursor in the step (7) is ammonium metavanadate, or manganese nitrate. The catalyst additive is ammonium metatungstate, or ammonium heptamolybdate, and the mass ratio of active component oxide and dry silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel is 1-6 wt.%. The mass ratio of catalyst promoter oxide to dry silicon dioxide-graphene aerogel is 1-6 wt.%. The cosolvent is oxalic acid, and the mass ratio of the precursor and the active component is 1:1. In the step (9), the concentration of gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 5-10 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (5), the aging time is 6 hours, the single replacement time is 12 hours, and the number of replacements is 5 times. In the step (6), freeze-drying time is 30 hours at -50 degrees C. In the step (8), the soaking time is 30 minutes, the drying temperature is 60 degrees C, and the calcination time is 3-7 hours at 400 degrees C.

PN CN110104669-A
TI Preparing calcium bicarbonate from calcium carbide            slag comprises e.g. breaking up calcium carbide slag,            adding water, oxidizing agent or precipitating agent            for purification treatment, obtaining suspension            slurry, reacting, and converting.
AU YIN X
AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201970695F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium bicarbonate from calcium                carbide slag comprises (i) breaking up calcium                carbide slag at room temperature and pressure,                adding water to obtain suspended slurry, then                adding oxidizing agent, or precipitating agent for                purification treatment, and obtaining suspension                slurry which is non-toxic, and has no odor, and                (ii) placing non-toxic suspension slurry obtained                in the step (i) in a pressure stirring tank,                pressing carbon dioxide captured by industrial                carbon, reacting under pressure, converting calcium                hydroxide, and calcium carbonate crystal minerals                in calcium carbide slag into water-soluble calcium                bicarbonate for liquefying, forming a solution in                which both calcium bicarbonate, and carbon dioxide                are supersaturated, precipitating impurity                compounds of silicone, aluminum, iron, and other                heavy metal ions in supersaturated carbon dioxide                solution as solidified precipitates, and mixture of                calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution                containing solid impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                bicarbonate from calcium carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: removes poisonous odor, impurities                in calcium carbide slag, and facilitates                utilization; has simple process flow; is convenient                for automatic control of production line; has less                device investment, no secondary pollution hazard,                low production energy consumption, excellent                product quality, and purity is &#8805; 97%; and                main raw materials are only carbon dioxide captured                by industrial waste slag carbide slag and carbon                capture and emission reduction projects, which are                low in cost, and competitive in market.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium bicarbonate from calcium                carbide slag comprises (i) breaking up calcium                carbide slag at room temperature and pressure,                adding water to obtain suspended slurry, then                adding oxidizing agent, or precipitating agent for                purification treatment, and obtaining suspension                slurry which is non-toxic, and has no odor, (ii)                placing non-toxic suspension slurry obtained in the                step (i) in a pressure stirring tank, pressing                carbon dioxide captured by industrial carbon,                reacting under pressure, converting calcium                hydroxide, and calcium carbonate crystal minerals                in calcium carbide slag into water-soluble calcium                bicarbonate for liquefying, forming a solution in                which both calcium bicarbonate, and carbon dioxide                are supersaturated, precipitating impurity                compounds of silicone, aluminum, iron, and other                heavy metal ions in supersaturated carbon dioxide                solution as solidified precipitates, and mixture of                calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution                containing solid impurities, (iii) subjecting                mixture of calcium bicarbonate supersaturated                solution containing solid impurities obtained in                step (ii) for filtering or suction filtering under                pressure maintaining condition to obtain filter                residue, and filtrate, and filtrate is                supersaturated solution containing calcium                bicarbonate, and (iv) adding stabilizing agent to                filtrate obtained in step (iii) for homogenizing,                discharging the pressure mixing tank to room                pressure while discharging carbon dioxide, allowing                to stand the resulting mixed solution,                crystallizing, and filtering to obtain calcium                bicarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of water, and calcium carbide slag                  solids in suspended slurry, i.e. the water/solid                  ratio is 20-5:1. The oxidizing agent is chlorine                  dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, sodium percarbonate,                  and/or ferrate, and the amount of the oxidizing                  agent is 0.50-0.005 wt.% of the calcium carbide                  slag. The precipitating agent is copper sulfate,                  and/or copper chloride, and the precipitating                  agent is used in an amount of 0.20-0.002 wt.% of                  calcium carbide slag. In the step (iv), the                  crystal is negative pressure concentrated                  crystal. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  temperature in pressure stirring tank is                  0-35&#176; C, the pressure in pressure state                  is 1-10 MPa, and pressure state lasts for 0.3-1.5                  hours. The stabilizing agent is triethanolamine                  and sodium bicarbonate, and/or triethanolamine or                  sodium bicarbonate. The stabilizing agent is used                  in an amount of 0.15-2 wt.%, preferably 0.2-1.5                  wt.% of the filtrate obtained in the step (iii).                  In the step (iv), recycling the carbon dioxide                  discharged from pressure relief for the step (ii)                  as carbon dioxide for carbonation, and                  solidifying impurities. The recycled water which                  is recycled by crystallization or filtration is                  used as suspension slurry in the step (i).

PN CN109368671-A
TI Preparing high-purity lithium fluoride comprises e.g. adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to stirrer, passing food-grade carbon dioxide into lithium carbonate suspension, and passing lithium hydrogen carbonate through precision filter.
AU GUO J
AE DONGYING SHIDA SHENGHUA NEW ENERGY CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2019204295
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity lithium fluoride comprises e.g. (i) adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to a stirrer containing a sufficient amount of deionized water at a normal temperature to obtain a suspension of lithium carbonate, (ii) passing food-grade carbon dioxide into the lithium carbonate suspension, and completing the reaction to obtain a lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, (iii) passing the lithium hydrogen carbonate solution through a precision filter to obtain a pure lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, (iv) heating the lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, decomposing to obtain a lithium carbonate suspension and carbon dioxide, passing the suspension through a solid-liquid separator to obtain a lithium carbonate wet product, and reacting carbon dioxide with lithium hydroxide in an agitator to obtain a lithium carbonate suspension, and (v) adding a certain amount of deionized water to a fluorine-containing reactor under stirring, and adding the lithium carbonate wet material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity lithium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product with a purity of 99.99%; recovers carbon dioxide emitted during the reaction process; is environmentally friendly and economical; and improves the yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity lithium fluoride comprises (i) adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to a stirrer containing a sufficient amount of deionized water at a normal temperature to obtain a suspension of lithium carbonate, (ii) passing food-grade carbon dioxide into the lithium carbonate suspension, and completing the reaction to obtain a lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, (iii) passing the lithium hydrogen carbonate solution through a precision filter to obtain a pure lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, (iv) heating the lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, decomposing to obtain a lithium carbonate suspension and carbon dioxide, passing the suspension through a solid-liquid separator to obtain a lithium carbonate wet product, and reacting carbon dioxide with lithium hydroxide in an agitator to obtain a lithium carbonate suspension, (v) adding a certain amount of deionized water to a fluorine-containing reactor under stirring, adding the lithium carbonate wet material, introducing hydrogen fluoride gas, controlling the pH value, releasing the gas through water absorption, absorbing a small amount of hydrogen fluoride, and then introducing into a stirrer containing lithium hydroxide, (vi) centrifuging to obtain a filter cake, and rinsing the filter cake with deionized water for 2-3 times, (vii) drying the pure lithium fluoride wet product to obtain a dried lithium fluoride, and (viii) repeating the step (ii) in the lithium carbonate suspension, circularly sieving the lithium fluoride, taking out the lower layer as the finished product, returning the upper layer to the step (v), and stirring and reusing the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of lithium carbonate and deionized water is 1:10-50. In the step (iii), the precision filtration is made of polypropylene, polytetrafluoroethylene, titanium or SUS316L stainless steel with a pore size of 0.1-10 mu m. In the step (viii), the pore size of the sieve is 20-200 mesh. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide gas feed rate is 10-500 l/minute, the reaction time is 60-180 minutes, the stirring speed is 50-500 revolutions/minute (rpm), the mass ratio of lithium carbonate and carbon dioxide is 1:0.5-1, and the reaction temperature is 5-50 degrees C. In the step (iv), the decomposition temperature is 60-90 degrees C. In the step (iv), lithium hydroxide monohydrate and deionized water is added in a mass ratio of 1:8.6-45, and lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide monohydrate is added in a mass ratio of 1:3-5. In the step (v), the lithium carbonate wet material and deionized water is added in a mass ratio of 1:15-30, then stirred for 50-500 rpm, reacted at 20-80 degrees C, and the end point pH value is controlled at 1-5. In the step (vii), the vacuum drying is carried out with a vacuum degree of - 20-- 100 kPa, and drying at 120-200 degrees C for 1-12 hours.

AU CHEN G

PN KR2022100743-A
TI Fuel cell system used for producing electricity,            comprises dry reforming reactor for reforming fuel gas            into reformed gas using dry reforming catalyst, which            is matrix having perovskite crystal structure, solid            oxide fuel cell comprising anode, electrolyte and            cathode.
AU GUNTAE K
   OH J K
   JOO S
AE DRM CATALYST CO LTD (DRMC-Non-standard)
GA 2022939486
AB    NOVELTY - A fuel cell system comprises a dry reforming                reactor for reforming a fuel gas containing methane                and carbon dioxide into a reformed gas containing                hydrogen and carbon monoxide using a dry reforming                catalyst, a solid oxide fuel cell comprising an                anode, electrolyte and a cathode to which the                reformed gas is supplied. The dry reforming                catalyst is a matrix having perovskite crystal                structure including transition elements (T) and                (T'). The transition element (T) includes an eluate                eluted from the matrix to the surface. The matrix                is lanthanide-transition metal-based oxide.
   USE - Fuel cell system used for producing                electricity (claimed) in automobiles, domestic and                industrial applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly system                economically produces electricity from green house                gases by supplying the reformed gas to the anode of                the fuel cell without separating hydrogen from                reformed gas, and uses catalyst with low                deactivation and high conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fuel cell system comprises a dry reforming                reactor for reforming a fuel gas containing methane                and carbon dioxide into a reformed gas containing                hydrogen and carbon monoxide using a dry reforming                catalyst, a solid oxide fuel cell comprising an                anode, electrolyte and a cathode to which the                reformed gas is supplied. The dry reforming                catalyst is a matrix having perovskite crystal                structure including transition elements (T) and                (T'). The transition element (T) includes an eluate                eluted from the matrix to the surface. The matrix                is lanthanide-transition metal-based oxide of                formula: RaEbT'xT1-xO3-~s, where R is one or more                lanthanide group chosen from lanthanum, E is                alkaline earth metal group chosen from calcium, T'                is iron, T is chosen from cobalt, nickel                (preferred), copper, manganese, ruthenium, rhodium,                palladium, iridium, platinum, gold, and/or silver,                a, b and x are 0-1 (excluding 0 and 1), a+b is 1,                sigma is 0 or 1, which is a value for making the                matrix electrically neutral. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for production of electricity from                gases comprising methane and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reformed gas                  further comprises unreacted methane and carbon                  dioxide, and is supplied to the anode without                  separating hydrogen. The system further includes                  a blower for supplying air to the cathode, an                  anti-oxidation gas supply for supplying an inert                  gas or a reducing gas to the fuel cell to prevent                  metal oxidation, and/or mass flow controller for                  measuring and controlling a flow rate of gas                  supplied to the anode and/or cathode. The                  transition element (T') suppresses the elution of                  transition element (T) by reducing the size of                  the eluate. The matrix is preferably                  lanthanum-calcium-iron-nickel oxide of formula:                  La0.9Ca0.1Fe0.5Ni0.5O3-~s. The eluate contains                  nickel, and iron, which reduces the elution of                  nickel by reducing the size of the eluate. The                  anode is made of a material including a                  praseodymium-barium-manganese-based oxide                  double-layer perovskite crystal structure                  material of formula: PrBaMn2-xT1xO5+~s, where T1                  is chosen from cobalt, nickel, and/or iron, x is                  0-1 (excluding 0 and 1), which is chosen from                  praseodymium-barium-manganese-cobalt oxide of                  formula: PrBaMn2-xCoxO5+~s,                  praseodymium-barium-manganese-nickel oxide of                  formula: PrBaMn2-xNixO5+~s,                  praseodymium-barium-manganese-iron oxide of                  formula: PrBaMn2-xFexO5+~s,                  praseodymium-barium-manganese-cobalt-nickel oxide                  of formula: PrBaMn2-x(Co, Ni)xO5+~s or                  praseodymium-barium-manganese- nickel-iron oxide                  of formula: PrBaMn2-x(Ni, Fe)xO5+~s. The cathode                  is made of a material including a                  praseodymium-barium-strontium-cobalt oxide-based                  perovskite crystal structure material of formula:                  PrBaaSrbCo2-xT2xO5+~s, where T2 is chosen from                  iron, copper, manganese, and/or nickel,                  preferably                  praseodymium-barium-strontium-cobalt-iron                  oxide-based perovskite crystal structure material                  of formula: PrBa0.5Sr0.5Co2-xFexO5+~s. The                  electrolyte is made of                  lanthanum-strontium-gallium-magnesium oxide                  (La-Sr-Ga-Mg-O3)-based material. Preferred                  Property: The eluate has size of 5-10 nm.                  Preferred Process: The catalyst is obtained by                  preparing the matrix, and eluting the transition                  element (T) from the matrix to the surface to                  form an eluate by heat-treating the matrix in a                  reducing gas atmosphere. During the                  heat-treatment step, the size of eluate is                  controlled by temperature and transition element                  (T'). Preferred Condition: The heat treatment is                  performed at 700-850 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing gas                  includes hydrogen gas, ammonia gas, carbon                  monoxide gas, methane gas, and/or propane                  gas.

PN KR2022074337-A
TI Manufacture of bio-car for producing slow-acting fertilizer, involves manufacturing biomass as bio-char, adding biochar, injecting high moisture organic waste, mixing, heating biochar, and thermally decomposing or gasifying organic waste.
AU KWON P T
AE YUKI IND CO LTD (YUKI-Non-standard)
GA 2022770007
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a bio-car involves manufacturing biomass (organic waste, pruning eggplant, rice husk, rice straw, bean stalk, mushroom medium, amino acid by-product, street tree pruning, sewage sludge, livestock manure, food dry powder) as bio-char, as an organic fertilizer, soil conditioner or BIO-SRF product, adding high-temperature biochar that has not been pyrolyzed and cooled in TLUD into a large mixer, injecting high moisture organic waste in a certain ratio, mixing, heating the biochar, thermally decomposing or gasifying high-moisture organic waste at high temperature to produce a soil improver or organic fertilizer in the form of pellets with no or minimal moisture content to produce a soil improver or organic fertilizer that does not cause gas disturbance.
   USE - Manufacture of bio-car for producing slow-acting fertilizer for reducing carbon dioxide by recycling organic waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces biochar for obtaining a soil improver or organic fertilizer without causing gas disturbance, controls carbonization and recovery in the biochar manufacturing process using biomass, additionally reduces carbon dioxide because without using external energy, and controls the duration of fermentation by forming a surface coating. The fertilizer is manufactured in the form of a slow-acting fertilizer, so that it is easy to manufacture, transport, and store, and the effect on crops can be continuously exerted, and at the same time, there is an effect of reducing damage to crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing a slow-acting fertilizer using bio-char for reducing carbon dioxide from recycled organic waste, which involves preparing slow-acting fertilizer using the biochar, forming biochar by decomposing and drying biomass into small particles and igniting using combustion gas, pulverizing the obtained biochar, mixing the obtained bio-cha powder and livestock meal (specifically, cow meal, pig meal, chicken meal, etc) to obtain a mixture, and mixing the mixture with a 1-5 wt.% solution of at least one natural binder chosen from molasses, starch, and bentonite to form granules or pellets.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacturing a bio-car involves mixing at least one of rice hull bio-cha (rice hull charcoal) and wood bio-char (wood charcoal) manufactured by the TLUD (TOP LIT UP DRAFT) method and the biomass comprising a high water content (amino acid by-product, Sewage sludge, livestock manure, food dry powder, etc) to produce a mixture with a moisture content of 20% or less that can produce pellets. The production of slow-acting fertilizer involves forming the biochar by decomposing and drying the biomass into small particles and igniting the slow-release fertilizer using combustion gas, pulverizing the biochar, obtaining a mixture by mixing the biochar powder with nitrogen, phosphoric acid and potassium fertilizers; and preparing a granule or pellet shape by mixing a 1-5 wt.% solution of at least one natural binder chosen from molasses, starch, and bentonite in the mixture, adding natural binder made of molasses, starch, and bentonite solution to the mixture extends the duration of fermentation by coating the surface of the granules and pellets to be formed, thereby extending the time for the internal organic components and fertilizer components to elute to the outside, controlling the appropriate period of ineffectiveness due to the difference in the growing period of crops, decomposing and drying biomass into small particles and igniting using combustion gas to form biochar, pulverizing the biochar, mixing the biochar powder with earthworm fecal soil and insect fecal soil to obtain a mixture, preparing the mixture in the form of granules or pellets by mixing 1-5 wt.% f water or a solution of one or more natural binders chosen from molasses, starch, and bentonite in the mixture, mixing earthworm fecal soil and insect fecal soil with biochar and using as a slow-acting fertilizer, mixing the earthworm fecal soil and insect fecal soil in an amount of 10-500 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of biochar, adding water or a binder to a mixture of the biochar, earthworm fecal soil and insect fecal soil, and using water or binder in an amount of 10-100 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of mixture of the biochar, earthworm fecal soil and insect fecal soil and binder comprising 1-5 wt.% natural binder solution containing at least one of molasses, starch, and bentonite, molding into granular or pellet shape using a generally used granular or pellet molding machine, forming spherical granules with a diameter of 1-30 mm and pellets in the form of a cylinder with a length of 10-200 mm and a diameter of 2-20 mm, and obtaining final product by mixing the biochar and fecal soil mixture with water or a binder, stirring, molding, drying, transporting, and packaging.

GA 202273723S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S2) the                measured data in the target area. The measured data                includes a location information, a carbondioxide                (CO2) concentration data, a nitrogen dioxide (NO2)                concentration data and meteorological data. The                emission dispersion models are established (S3).                The number of emission sources is calculated (S4)                in the target area and the location of each                emission source based on the measured data and the                emission dispersion model based on the genetic                algorithm. The CO2 intensity and NO2 intensity                emitted by each emission source is calculated (S5)                based on the levenberg-marquardt algorithm. The                carbon-nitrogen ratio of each of the emission                sources is calculated (S6) according to the CO2                intensity and the NO2 intensity.
   USE - Method for monitoring multi-point source                carbon nitrogen ratio based on vehicle measuring                system
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing a vehicle-mounted                device to measure carbon dioxide concentration near                a target area, nitrogen dioxide concentration,                position information, meteorological data and                establish a multi-point source discharge diffusion                model, so as to quantize each emission source                carbon emission and intensity of nitrogen                discharge, thus, improving nitrogen discharge                monitoring timeliness and precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) a multi-point source carbon nitrogen                ratio monitoring system based on vehicle measuring                system; (2) an electronic device; and (3) a                non-transitory computer readable storage medium                storing program for monitoring multi-point source                carbon nitrogen ratio based on vehicle measuring                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the multi point source carbon-nitrogen ratio                monitoring method based on vehicle measuring                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

   Step for calculating the nitrogen oxide and                carbon dioxide intensity emitted by each emission                source based on the algorithm (S5)
   Step for calculating the carbon-nitrogen                ratio of each of the emission sources according to                the intensities (S6)

GA 202244978L

A partition plate (8) is fixedly connected to the inner wall of the induction frame (6). A top surface of the induction frame is provided with air inlet holes that are located on the side of the partition plate close to the outer pipe. AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a degradable material, involves (a) providing delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide, (b) combining delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide with a catalyst to obtain a reaction mixture, and (c) subjecting the reaction mixture to conditions which allows delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide to react in the presence of the catalyst to obtain a product composition including polymeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide, where the polymeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide is hydrolytically degradable.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a degradable material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces or eliminates side reactions, provids improved degradable polymer structures and allows end product after transformation of delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide for valuable product which includes carbon dioxide. The degradable material has excellent tunable thermal properties and mechanical performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) A method for producing a hydrolytically degradable material, involves perfroming above mentioned steps (a)-(c) to form a product composition including dimeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide; or (i) providing carbon dioxide and an olefin in a first reaction mixture, (ii) subjecting the first reaction mixture to conditions which allows the carbon dioxide to be chemically bonded with the olefin to form a monomer, (iii) combining the monomer with a catalyst to form a second reaction mixture, and (iv) subjecting the second reaction mixture to conditions which allows the monomer to react in the presence of the catalyst to form a product composition including a polymer of the monomer, where the polymer is hydrolytically degradable;
   (2) A composition comprising polymeric delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide compound of formula (I), and dimeric delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide compound of formula (II); and
   (3) The hydrolytically degradable material comprising a polymer of delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide and a dimer of delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide.
   x = 0-15; n = 0-15; and R = a protic alcohol, an amine and a guanidine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a bifunctional catalyst. The catalyst is 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene. The product composition further comprises dimeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide. The monomer is delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide. The olefin is an acyclic olefin chosen from 1,3-butadiene and isoprene, or a cyclic olefin chosen from cyclohexadiene, norbornadiene and alpha-phellandrene. The step of providing delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide involves using delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide being at a purity of at least 95%. The step of providing delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide involves a sub-step of distillation in order to achieve the purity of at least 95%.Preferred Properties: The first product including dimeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide has a purity of at least 95%. The second product including polymeric-delta-valerolactone 2-ethylidene-6-hepten-5-olide has a purity of at least 95%.The hydrolytically degradable material has a mass average molar mass of 5000-100000 g/mol and a dispersity of 1.5-2.5.

PN WO2021188790-A1; US2021293408-A1; AU2021236673-A1
TI Injector for use in supercritical carbon dioxide            combustion system, has outer cylindrical tube whose            inner diameter swirls non-solid, and inner diameter of            inner tube swirls another non solid such that non solid            remain swirled and stratified.
AU CUSANO D M
AE PARAMETRIC SOLUTIONS INC (PARA-Non-standard)
   PARAMETRIC SOLUTIONS INC (PARA-Non-standard)
   G2 POWER INC (GTWO-Non-standard)
GA 2021B02746
AB    NOVELTY - The injector (1) has an outer cylindrical tube                (2) comprises an inner diameter and a first end and                a second end. The first end is connected to a                source of a first non-solid and the second end                comprises an annular exit (3,5) or circular exit.                An inner cylindrical tube (4) comprises an inner                diameter and a first end and a second end. The                first end is connected to a source of a second                non-solid and the second end comprises an annular                or circular exit. The inner diameter of the outer                cylindrical tube is configured to swirl the first                non-solid and the inner diameter of the second                cylindrical tube is configured to swirl the second                non-solid such that the first non-solid and the                second non-solid remain swirled and stratified into                separate layers of flow for each gas as they flow                through the exits of the inner and outer tubes. The                first non-solid is supercritical carbon dioxide and                the second non-solid is methane.
   USE - Injector for use in combustion system i.e.                supercritical carbon dioxide combustion system, for                injecting reactants into combustion chamber.
   ADVANTAGE - The injector injects reactants into a                combustion chamber in such a way that combustion is                locally inhibited. Delaying the reaction moves the                high temperature combustion species and reactions                away from the injectors and other combustion                chamber hardware improving durability and                survivability of such hardware. The swirled inert                delays the mixing of swirled non-solid in the                combustion chamber thus preventing reactions for                some distance beyond the point of injection of the                injector.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method of injecting an inert into a                combustion chamber; and
   (2) a method of injecting a non-solid into a                combustion chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective and partial                section view of an injector designed in accord with                the flow of CO2 and certified fraud examiner (CFE)                through and after exiting the injector.
   Injector (1)
   Outer cylindrical tube (2)
   Annular exit (3,5)
   Inner cylindrical tube (4)
   Second gas (8)

TI Apparatus for providing platform for selling or renting container, has notification unit that notifies administrator or consumer of container when abnormality occurs in container.
AU KIM C
AE SPACEPORT INC (SPAC-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3793B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving (S300) registration of container information from container provider. The consumer information is received (S310) from container consumer. The container history information is updated (S320) when a container sale or lease occurs between a container supplier and a container consumer based on the container information and the consumer information. A determination is made (S340) to check whether there is an abnormality based on the status information received from the container. The administrator or consumer of the container is notified (S350) that the abnormality occurs, when an abnormality occurs in the container. The state information including temperature, humidity, or carbon dioxide concentration is received from a container. The normal range of a preset temperature, humidity, or carbon dioxide concentration for each container is compared with the state information received from the container.
   USE - Method for providing container trading or rental platform.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing a platform for selling or renting containers between the container providers and the container consumers, so that abnormal condition such as temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration of the container can be notified to a container manager in real time to enable immediate processing. The method enables rationally calculating shipping cost of a container in consideration of distance or travel time, and re-renting the leased container immediately without recalling it, thus reducing collection cost and providing a discount on shipping cost to a lessee.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an apparatus for providing platform for selling or renting container; and
   (2) a computer-readable medium storing program for providing platform for selling or renting container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for providing a container platform.
   Step for receiving registration of container information from container provider (S300)
   Step for receiving consumer information from container consumer (S310)
   Step for updating container history information when a container sale or lease occurs between a container supplier and a container consumer based on the container information and the consumer information (S320)
   Step for determining whether there is an abnormality based on the status information received from the container (S340)
   Step for notifying the administrator or consumer of the container that the abnormality occurs, when an abnormality occurs in the container (S350)

PN CN112299455-A
TI Preparation of industrial grade or battery grade lithium carbonate from crude lithium carbonate includes mixing raw material of lithium carbonate and pure water, crushing to obtain slurry, carbonizing and agglomerating dried materials.
AU ZENG G
AE PINGXIANG TUOYUAN IND CO LTD (PING-Non-standard)
GA 202113923Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of industrial grade or battery grade lithium carbonate from crude lithium carbonate includes mixing raw material of lithium carbonate and pure water, crushing to obtain slurry, carbonizing, cooling, sending insoluble impurities precipitate solid phase and mixture to filtration separation process, filtering carbonized material, sending filter residue to temporary storage of solid waste residue, sending filtrate to pyrolysis process, pumping filtrate to pyrolysis tank for pyrolysis reaction to obtain pyrolysis mother liquor containing calcium (II) and lithium carbonate crystal, releasing mixture of carbon dioxide and steam, treating gas by dehumidifier unit, removing water, recycling in carbonization process, sending pyrolysis liquid to centrifugation process, adjusting pyrolysis liquid, cooling mother liquid, returning to slurrying process for recycling, sending centrifuged lithium carbonate to drying process, agglomerating dried materials and crushing particles.
   USE - The method is for preparation of industrial grade or battery grade lithium carbonate from crude lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, easy operation, low cost, low energy consumption and high purity of prepared product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of industrial grade or battery grade lithium carbonate from crude lithium carbonate comprises mixing raw material of lithium carbonate and pure water, crushing to obtain slurry, sending to pressurized carbonization tower, sending carbon dioxide gas to pressurized carbonization tower, carbonizing, cooling, sending insoluble impurities precipitate solid phase and mixture to filtration separation process, filtering carbonized material by filter press, sending filter residue to temporary storage of solid waste residue, sending filtrate to pyrolysis process, pumping filtrate of filter press to pyrolysis tank for pyrolysis reaction to obtain pyrolysis mother liquor containing calcium (II) and lithium carbonate crystal, releasing mixture of carbon dioxide and steam, treating gas by dehumidifier unit, removing water, recycling in carbonization process, sending pyrolysis liquid to centrifugation process, adjusting pyrolysis liquid, sending to centrifuge for centrifugation, cooling mother liquid by cooling tower or water chiller, returning to slurrying process for recycling, sending centrifuged lithium carbonate to drying process, agglomerating dried materials and crushing particles by self collision, friction or collision with the inner wall of equipment through high-speed air flow.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid-liquid ratio adjusted by pyrolysis material and liquid is 1:4. The carbonized material is lithium bicarbonate solution and/or insoluble impurities.

TI Extracting concentrated ammonia water used e.g. in ammonia evaporating system in coking plant by heating raw material ammonia water, heating by heating mechanism at bottom of decomposer, and separating out sulfuric acid and carbon dioxide.
AU ZHAO C
AE ZHAO C (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 2020A9385D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting concentrated ammonia water in ammonia evaporating system in coking plant involves passing raw material ammonia water through heat exchanger, heating raw material ammonia water, entering decomposer, heating through heating mechanism at bottom of decomposer, separating out sulfuric acid and carbon dioxide in raw material ammonia water, sending raw material ammonia water into ammonia evaporating tower for steaming ammonia, entering evaporated ammonia gas and large amount of water vapor into decompressor from ammonia evaporating tower, cooling by decompressor, condensing most of water vapor back to ammonia evaporating tower, entering high ammonia concentration vapor into gas-liquid separator, further separating, returning vapor phase in water vapor as much condensed reflux liquid into ammonia evaporating tower for steaming, entering vapor into condensing cooler to form concentrated ammonia water, and entering concentrated ammonia water tank for user to use.
   USE - The method is used for extracting concentrated ammonia water in ammonia evaporating system in coking plant in power plant boiler desulfurization technique, factory coke oven exhaust gas, tubular furnace exhaust gas, boiler exhaust gas desulfurization and denitration process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting concentrated ammonia water in ammonia evaporating system in coking plant involves passing raw material ammonia water with temperature of 25-30 degrees C through heat exchanger, heating raw material ammonia water to 60-70 degrees C and 80-90 degrees C, entering decomposer, heating through heating mechanism at bottom of decomposer, separating out sulfuric acid and carbon dioxide in raw material ammonia water, sending raw material ammonia water into ammonia evaporating tower for steaming ammonia, entering evaporated ammonia gas and large amount of water vapor into decompressor from ammonia evaporating tower, cooling by decompressor, condensing most of water vapor back to ammonia evaporating tower, entering high ammonia concentration vapor into gas-liquid separator, further separating, returning vapor phase in water vapor as much condensed reflux liquid into ammonia evaporating tower for steaming, entering vapor into condensing cooler to form concentrated ammonia water with 18-20% ammonia content, and entering concentrated ammonia water tank for user to use. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for coking plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The temperature of bottom of decomposer is 92-100 degrees C. The temperature of ammonia at outlet of decompressor is 88-92 degrees . The concentrated ammonia water temperature in condensing cooler outlet is 30-35 degrees C. The temperature of top of ammonia evaporating tower is 100-102?C. The temperature of bottom of ammonia evaporating tower is 100-103 degrees C. The pressure of top of ammonia evaporating tower is less than or equal to 0.25 kg/mu. The pressure of ammonia evaporating tower bottom is less than or equal to 0.35 kg/mu. The ammonia evaporating tower tower top has ammonia content of 5-8%, evaporating efficiency of 97-99% and direct steam consumption of 160-200 kg per kg of raw material ammonia water.

AU CHO Y G

   GLUKHIKH S A
   TEMRYUKOV A P
   GLUKHIKH A S
   TEMRYUKOVA V A
AE BIOPRIN CO LTD (BIOP-Non-standard)
GA 2020212441
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomass of aerobic microorganisms involves culturing mixed microorganisms by continuously saturating gaseous hydrocarbons and initiators when nutrient medium is supplied under continuous biosynthetic chemical conditions and removing accumulated biomass. The gaseous hydrocarbon is mixed by mixing aeration head. The aeration and saturation is performed, and nutrient medium mixed by continuously saturating gaseous hydrocarbon and initiator is to be carried out in manufacturing step and biosynthesis process step of nutrient medium. The nutrient medium is prepared by filtering gas hydrocarbons and initiators by gas filter through pre-dissolving device, and then pre-dissolved in the nutrient medium, and when gaseous hydrocarbon and initiator are saturated then nutrient medium is primarily supplied to lower portion of fermenter in a cooled state, which is used as a coolant for controlling temperature of culture medium.
   USE - Method for producing biomass of aerobic microorganisms for feed and food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce biomass of aerobic microorganisms, which is environmentally friendly method without stress on microorganisms, and promotes efficient mixing and aeration of culture medium by generating an upward vortex of culture medium by aeration head.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biomass of aerobic microorganisms involves culturing mixed microorganisms by continuously saturating gaseous hydrocarbons and initiators when nutrient medium is supplied under continuous biosynthetic chemical conditions and removing accumulated biomass. The gaseous hydrocarbon is mixed by aeration aeration head. The aeration and saturation is performed, and nutrient medium mixed by continuously saturating gaseous hydrocarbon and initiator is to be carried out in manufacturing step and biosynthesis process step of nutrient medium. The nutrient medium is prepared by filtering gas hydrocarbons and initiators by gas filter through pre-dissolving device, and then pre-dissolved in the nutrient medium, and when gaseous hydrocarbon and initiator are saturated then nutrient medium is primarily supplied to lower portion of fermenter in a cooled state, which is used as a coolant for controlling temperature of culture medium. The mixed culture medium uses carbon dioxide as a carbon source. The method further involves using microorganism strain that uses dissolved hydrogen as an energy source in culture medium, and when carbon dioxide is excessive then carbon dioxide is used for photosynthesis of turquoise Spirulina algae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mixed aeration head has a structure that generates a vortex flow that rotates and rises around its own axis in fermenter by using injection energy of nutrient medium. Preferred Components: The initiator comprises air, and gaseous hydrocarbon comprises methane and methane analogs.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composition comprises (a) expanding and culturing lactic acid bacteria, Bacillus and Aspergillus niger, mixing the expanded bacterial solution to lactic acid bacterial solution, Bacillus bacterial solution and Aspergillus niger bacterial solution to obtain bacterial solution, (b) taking 2-6 pts. wt. PT P
PN CN110142064-A
TI Processing SCR denitration catalyst regeneration            involves oxidizing catalytically the deactivated SCR            denitration catalyst by using a catalytic oxidation            furnace, and oxidizing carbon remaining in the catalyst            to carbon dioxide.
AU QIU Z
   LIAO X
   GUO R
   GUAN Z
   LIU J
   YUAN Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ELECTRIC POWER (UYSI-C)
GA 201974054N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing SCR denitration catalyst                regeneration involves oxidizing catalytically the                deactivated SCR denitration catalyst by using a                catalytic oxidation furnace, and oxidizing carbon                remaining in the catalyst to carbon dioxide to be                separated from the catalyst in a gaseous state,                raising the temperature of the catalytic oxidation                furnace to 500-550 degrees C, calcining the SCR                denitration catalyst at this temperature, cooling                to room temperature, and then taking out the SCR                denitration catalyst. The catalytically oxidized                SCR denitration catalyst is rinsed with deionized                water, then dried, and then vacuum suctioned by a                vacuum suction tube to remove the substances                adhering and blocking in the micropores of the                catalyst. The vacuum-sucked SCR catalyst immersing                in a cleaning solution, and flushing the catalyst                with a cleaning solution to wash away impurities in                the catalyst. The SCR catalyst is then subjected to                a drying treatment.
   USE - Method for processing SCR denitration catalyst                regeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process SCR denitration                catalyst regeneration has good regeneration effect,                low conversion rate of SO2/SO3, and has low                regeneration cost and environmental                friendliness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing SCR denitration catalyst                regeneration involves oxidizing catalytically the                deactivated SCR denitration catalyst by using a                catalytic oxidation furnace, and oxidizing carbon                remaining in the catalyst to carbon dioxide to be                separated from the catalyst in a gaseous state,                raising the temperature of the catalytic oxidation                furnace to 500-550 degrees C, calcining the SCR                denitration catalyst at this temperature, cooling                to room temperature, and then taking out the SCR                denitration catalyst. The catalytically oxidized                SCR denitration catalyst is rinsed with deionized                water, then dried, and then vacuum suctioned by a                vacuum suction tube to remove the substances                adhering and blocking in the micropores of the                catalyst. The vacuum-sucked SCR catalyst immersing                in a cleaning solution, and flushing the catalyst                with a cleaning solution to wash away impurities in                the catalyst. The SCR catalyst is then subjected to                a drying treatment, and finally the SCR denitration                catalyst is purged with compressed air under a                negative pressure to remove dust particles adhering                to the surface of the catalyst and in the                pores.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The SCR denitration                  catalyst regeneration process further includes                  repeating step until the requirements of quality                  control and denitration effect evaluation are                  met. The preparation method of the cleaning                  liquid includes mixing a penetrating agent, a                  surfactant and an acid, adjusting the pH value to                  2-5, stirring for 10-60 minutes, mixing                  uniformly, and preparing the cleaning liquid.                  Preferred Component: The penetrant is isooctyl                  oxyethylene ether phosphate. The surfactant is                  ammonium dodecyl sulfate. The acid is sulfuric                  acid. Preferred Composition: The cleaning                  solution contains 0.1-5.0 wt.% penetrating agent                  and 0.1-5.0 wt.% surfactant, and 9-99.8 wt.%n                  acid, and the molar concentration of the acid is                  0.018-2.0 mole per liter. Preferred Condition:                  The drying temperature is 60-120 degrees C, dried                  for 2-12 hours, and the temperature increase rate                  is 5-10 degrees centigrade/minutes.
