PN DE202019101426-U1; WO2020182811-A1; EP3937742-A1; US2022168691-A1
TI Ignition chimney for carbon-containing fuel e.g.            charcoal for barbecue, has catalyst which is provided            to catalyze oxidation of carbon monoxide to carbon            dioxide by oxygen and is arranged above location for            fuel.
AU BRUDER R
   KAYA S
AE INOXAIR GMBH (INOX-Non-standard)
   INOXAIR GMBH (INOX-Non-standard)
   INOXAIR GMBH (INOX-Non-standard)
GA 201938828J
AB    NOVELTY - The ignition chimney (1) has housing (3), a                lower combustion chamber (4) for easily ignitable                lighters (5) formed in housing, and an upper                combustion chamber (6) for carbon-containing fuel                (2) formed in the housing. The lower and upper                combustion chambers are separated from one another                by a gas-permeable separating element (7). The                upper side (8) of separating element is made to                face upper combustion chamber, so as to form a                receiving location for fuel. The separating element                is designed, such that igniter exhaust gases (9)                produced in ignited state of lighter is passed                through separating element and impinged on fuel                resting on separating element. A catalyst (11) is                provided to catalyze oxidation of carbon monoxide                to carbon dioxide by oxygen and is arranged above                location for fuel. The fuel off-gases (12) produced                in ignited state of fuel is partially conducted to                catalyst or through catalyst, and portion of carbon                monoxide present in the fuel off-gases is oxidized                to carbon dioxide.
   USE - Ignition chimney for carbon-containing fuel                such as coal and charcoal for barbecue.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow resistance is lowered easily for fuel                exhausts to pass through the catalytic converter,                so as to avoid stagnation effects on the inflow                side of the catalytic converter. The risk of carbon                monoxide poisoning by exhaust gases during                combustion of the fuel is reduced. The fuel exhaust                gases containing the toxic carbon monoxide are                caused to pass through the catalyst and partially                contact the catalyst by arranging the catalyst                above the location for receiving the fuel, so that                the proportion of carbon monoxide in the fuel                exhaust gases is reduced effectively. The catalysts                are proven to be very resilient and are easily                cleaned with a moderate stream of compressed                air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                ignition chimney with catalyst unit.
   Ignition chimney (1)
   Housing (3)
   Lower combustion chamber (4)
   Ignitable lighter (5)
   Upper combustion chamber (6)
   Separating element (7)
   Upper side (8)
   Igniter exhaust gas (9)
   Catalyst (11)
   Fuel off-gases (12)

PT P
PN EP3449997-A1; WO2019042734-A1; CA3074255-A1; AU2018326205-A1; CN111093801-A; IN202047012696-A; EP3675986-A1; US2020298171-A1; BR112020003166-A2; JP2020531271-W; IN395780-B; US11344838-B2; RU2020111707-A; CN111093801-B; JP7145934-B2
TI Temperature swing adsorption method for removing            target component e.g. carbon dioxide from gaseous            mixture e.g. flue gas, involves separating water from            second output stream, and producing water-depleted            stream.
AU JOSS L
   HEFTI M
   MAZZOTTI M
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2019229279
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adsorpting of target                component over a solid adsorbent. A target                component-loaded adsorbent and one waste stream                (112) depleted of the target component are                provided. The target component-loaded adsorbent is                heated to a desorption temperature and desorption                of an amount of target component. The partially                regenerated adsorbent and a first output stream                (116) containing the desorbed target component are                provided. The partially regenerated adsorbent is                cooled to a conditioning temperature. The solid                adsorbent is brought to a temperature lower than                desorption temperature and one second output stream                (117) containing an amount of the target component                and containing water is provided. The water is                separated from second output stream and one                water-depleted stream is produced. The                water-depleted streams are subjected to adsorption                process.
   USE - Temperature swing adsorption method for                removing target component such as carbon dioxide                from gaseous mixture such as flue gas. Can also be                used for treating flue gas of ammonia or methanol                or urea plant or fossil fuel fired power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is made significantly faster due to                the energy subtracted by the heat of adsorption,                which is beneficial for the productivity of the                cycle. The optional heat exchange reduces the time                required for the subsequent desorption process. The                waste streams clean the adsorbent material by                displacing and desorbs the target component before                conditioning, so that recovery is increased and                adsorbent capacity for the target component is                greater in the following cycle. The adsorbent is                directly contacted with a heating or cooling                medium, which avoids the installation of heat                exchange bodies, thus the thermal inertia is                reduced and a better heat exchange is ensured. The                absence of a contact between the adsorbent and                heating or cooling medium ensures a higher working                capacity of the adsorbent and provides more freedom                to select the heat exchange fluids.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagrams of                temperature swing adsorption process for removing                the carbon dioxide from a flue gas.
   Reactors (101-103)
   Gaseous mixture (111)
   Waste stream (112)
   First output stream (116)
   Second output stream (117)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN109320391-A
TI Preparing low-cost ethylene comprises filling chromiumoxide/copper oxide supported X5 solid catalyst in column tube, pressurizing, conducting chemical reaction and separating residual gas.
AU CHEN H
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 201917283L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-cost ethylene comprises (i) filling chromiumoxide/copper oxide supported X5 solid catalyst in column tube of isothermal fixed-bed reactor and controlling catalytic reaction temperature by high-pressure high-temperature water between the tubes, (ii) taking purified raw material gas hydrogen and carbon dioxide, heating and exchanging with mixed gas after reaction and pressurizing high temperature water, heating and conducting chemical reaction through catalytic reaction bed, (iii) separating reaction product water from mixture gas and heat exchanging feed gas, air cooling to room temperature after the reaction, heating boiler by pressurized water to 128 degrees C and using regulated temperature water for the catalytic reaction bed, and (iv) separating residual gas after ethylene by complexation is used as fuel for pressurized water heating furnace and subjecting mixed dry gas after the reaction to complex separation of the ethylene system.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-cost ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The ethylene is non-toxic and harmless and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-cost ethylene comprises (i) filling chromiumoxide/copper oxide supported X5 solid catalyst in column tube of isothermal fixed-bed reactor and controlling catalytic reaction temperature by high-pressure high-temperature water between the tubes at 128 degrees C, (ii) taking purified raw material gas hydrogen and carbon dioxide, where the molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 5:4, heating at pressure of 0.7 MPa and exchanging with mixed gas after reaction and pressurizing high temperature water, heating to 128 degrees C and conducting chemical reaction through catalytic reaction bed at feed gas space velocity of 3300 h-1, (iii) separating reaction product water from mixture gas and heat exchanging feed gas, air cooling to room temperature after the reaction, heating boiler by pressurized water to 128 degrees C and using regulated temperature water for the catalytic reaction bed, and (iv) separating residual gas after ethylene by complexation is used as fuel for pressurized water heating furnace and subjecting mixed dry gas after the reaction to complex separation of the ethylene system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of chromiumoxide/copper oxide supported X5 solid catalyst comprises (a) soaking the commercially available X5 resin in methanol for 10 hours, removing organic impurities and pigments contained in the resin, filtering and washing with deionized water until pH 7, and (b) activating resin with a 23% hydrochloric acid solution for 10 hours, taking out the filter, washing again with deionized water to pH 7, filtering, placing the resin in the drying oven, drying at 80 degrees C and taking out the grinding to obtain X5 resin powder, (c) dissolving 3.6 g chromium nitrate and 14.4 g copper nitrate in 280 ml water for 10 minutes, continuing magnetic stirring at 60 degrees C for 4 hours and adding 36 g X5 resin powder, (d) slowly adding ammonium carbonate precipitant under vigorous stirring, adding dropwise to the solution pH 9.3, placing at room temperature for 12 hours, filter and washing with deionized water to pH 7, and (e) drying in a dry box at 110 degrees C overnight.

PN CN108946996-A
TI Deep dephenolization of chemical wastewater involves quenching and tempering phenolic chemical wastewater, deacidifying and deaminating to remove carbon dioxide, extracting waste form wastewater by liquid-liquid and solid-phase extractions.
AU WU Y
   WANG H
   ZHANG X
   LIU Y
   WU H
   WANG F
   WANG W
   XIONG C
AE HUALU ENG & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
   XIAN YEZONE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION IND (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0549A
AB    NOVELTY - Deep dephenolization of chemical wastewater involves quenching and tempering phenolic chemical wastewater, deacidifying and deaminating to remove carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and ammonia, adjusting pH, passing sewage to extraction and dephenolization tower, adding methyl isobutyl ketone or diisopropyl ether as extractant to carry out liquid-liquid extraction and dephenolization, extracting raffinate, passing extract to extractant separation tower, separating extractant, retaining in extractant storage tank, flowing extractant from extractant storage tank to extraction dephenolation tower for liquid-liquid extraction, flowing another extractant from extractant storage tank to solid phase extraction tower to regenerate solid phase extracting agent, passing sewage to solid phase extraction tower, adding resin, modified zeolite and micro-nano foam ceramic to extract organic substances, saturating, heating, regenerating, extracting polyphenol, and crude phenol.
   USE - Deep dephenolization of chemical wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively recycles phenolic substances in the sewage and meets the requirements of subsequent biochemical treatment. The total phenol removal rate is high. The extractant loss is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deep dephenolization of chemical wastewater involves quenching and tempering phenolic chemical wastewater, deacidifying and deaminating to remove carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and ammonia, adjusting pH value to 6-10, passing sewage to extraction and dephenolization tower, adding methyl isobutyl ketone or diisopropyl ether as extractant to carry out liquid-liquid extraction and dephenolization, extracting raffinate having pH of 3-8.5 from dephenolation tower to extractant recovery tower, passing extract to extractant separation tower, separating extractant from extractant recovery tower and separation tower, retaining in extractant storage tank, flowing extractant from extractant storage tank to extraction dephenolation tower for circulation for liquid-liquid extraction, flowing another extractant from extractant storage tank, passing to solid phase extraction tower to regenerate the solid phase extracting agent, passing sewage to solid phase extraction tower, adding resin, modified zeolite and micro-nano foam ceramic to extract organic substances such as phenols in the water, saturating, heating, regenerating by an extractant, passing condensate to an extraction dephenolization tower, passing regeneration liquid to extractant separation tower, extracting dephenolized sewage of polyphenol by solid phase extraction agent, passing to biochemical treatment section, and passing crude phenol to the subsequent crude phenol refined section.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The chemical sewage is high concentration phenol ammonia sewage having phenol content of 2000-8000 mg/L, polyphenol content of 1000-5000 mg/L, total ammonia amount of 6000-20000 mg/L, chemical oxygen demand of 15000-35000 mg/L, and pH of 8-12. The extract after solid phase extraction tower has total phenol content of less than 150 mg/L, chemical oxygen demand of 600-2000 mg/L, total phenol removal rate of more than 96%, and polyphenol removal rate more than 85%. The extraction ratio is 1:1-20 after the extraction dephenolation tower.

PT P
PN CN108715544-A
TI Method for manufacturing water permeable brick involves reacting ceramic powder, fly ash, titanium powder, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, stearic acid, pre-expanded polystyrene particles, sodium silicate hydrate and carbon dioxide gas.
AU CHEN Y
AE JIANGSU HONGFA SPONGE CITY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018881767
AB    NOVELTY - The method for manufacturing water permeable brick involves (1) mixing ceramic powder, fly ash, titanium powder, iron oxide, aluminum oxide and stearic acid uniformly to obtain mixed powder and adding mixed powder to powder boiling tank, (2) placing expandable polystyrene particles in closed container, heating, pre-expanding and cooling, (3) spraying surface of pre-expanded polystyrene particles with sodium silicate hydrate until all the particles are coated with a layer of sodium silicate hydrate and leaving mixture still for increasing viscosity and rheology of coating, (4) adding polystyrene particles coated with sodium silicate hydrate to sieve of powder boiling tank, introducing carbon dioxide gas, (5) mixing mixture of polystyrene particles and mixed powder and extruding, (6) molding, heating and cooling.
   USE - Method for manufacturing water permeable brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of brick ensuring high strength and increased water permeability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for manufacturing water permeable brick involves (1) grinding and dispersing ceramic block to form ceramic powder, drying fly ash, titanium powder, iron oxide, aluminum oxide and stearic acid, respectively, mixing ceramic powder, fly ash, titanium powder, iron oxide, aluminum oxide and stearic acid uniformly to obtain mixed powder and adding the mixed powder into powder boiling tank, (2) placing expandable polystyrene particles in closed container, heating with water vapor, pre-expanding the polystyrene particles and cooling to room temperature in air, (3) spraying the surface of pre-expanded polystyrene particles with sodium silicate hydrate, and continuously turning the polystyrene particles during the spraying until all the particles are coated with a layer of sodium silicate hydrate, leaving the mixture still for increasing the viscosity and rheology of coating, (4) adding polystyrene particles coated with sodium silicate hydrate to sieve of powder boiling tank, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into inlet pipe for period of time to uniformly mix the polystyrene particles and mixed powder, (5) mixing the mixture of polystyrene particles and mixed powder into block compressor and extruding into brick-like mixture, (6) adding the brick-like mixture in closed stainless steel forming mold, placing the formed permeable bricks in electric furnace and heating and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The step (1) involves mixing ceramic powder, fly ash, titanium powder, iron oxide, aluminum oxide and stearic acid at mass ratio of 140:40:4:5:1.

PN US2018279623-A1; US10244764-B2
TI Reducing contaminants in air, comprises applying titanium dioxide-containing growth media i.e. liquid growth media and/or a gel growth media to e.g. root of Myrtus communis plant, growing the plant, and exposing the obtained plant to air.
AU SALAMA K F
AE UNIV IMAM ABDULRAHMAN BIN FAISAL (UYIM-Non-standard)
GA 2018767774
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing contaminants in air, comprises: applying titanium dioxide-containing growth media to at least one of a root, stem and leaf of Myrtus communis plant, and growing the Myrtus communis plant, where the growth media comprise liquid growth media and/or a gel growth media to form titanium dioxide-containing Myrtus communis plant; and exposing the titanium dioxide-containing Myrtus communis plant to contaminant-containing air comprising at least one contaminant that comprises carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, formaldehyde, carbon monoxide, benzene, toluene, xylene and ethyl benzene, where a contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air is reduced by 10-98% relative to a contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air prior to the exposing.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing contaminants in air, where the contaminant comprises carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, formaldehyde, carbon monoxide, benzene, toluene, xylene or ethyl benzene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air by 10-98% relative to a contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air prior to the exposing; reduces the formaldehyde concentration from 0.2-0.3 ppm to 10-200 ppb after exposing for 4-8 hours; reduces combined contaminant concentration of the carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, formaldehyde, carbon monoxide, benzene, toluene, xylene and/or ethyl benzene from 300-400 ppb to 10-290 ppb after exposing for 4-8 hours; and provides the portion of the plant, which is capable of reducing a contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air at a rate of 0.7 ppb/hour to 50 ppm/hour.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a portion of Myrtus communis plant, comprising: titanium dioxide, where the portion of the plant is at least one of root portion, stem portion and leaf portion, the portion of the plant is capable of reducing a concentration of a contaminant in contaminant-containing air in a closed environment at a rate of 2-98% greater than a portion of the plant without titanium dioxide in the closed environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air is 1 ppb to 100 ppm prior to exposing, and the contaminant concentration in the contaminant-containing air is 0.25 ppb to 50 ppm after exposing. The titanium dioxide-containing Myrtus communis plant is exposed to the contaminant-containing air for 24-96 hours. The titanium oxide is collocated with a chlorophyll containing organelle of the portion of the plant. Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide-containing growth media: have 0.5-10 ppm titanium dioxide; and do not contain potassium silicate. An average particle size of the titanium nanoparticle is 10-25 nm. The infused titanium dioxide is in the form of nanoparticles. The portion of the plant has a density of titanium oxide over a chlorophyll containing surface of 2-50 titanium dioxide nanoparticles/ mu m2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Caramel with high content of prebiotic oligosaccharides is prepared by heating solution of saccharide starting material containing D-fructose in water in an airtight container and under carbon dioxide pressure of 1-40 bar, where carbon dioxide gas is used as acid catalyst for caramelization.
   USE - Preparation of caramel with high content of prebiotic oligosaccharides for use in product for animal or human consumption to induce increase of Bifidobacteria or Lactobacillus in the intestinal tract; for use in pharmaceutical product for prevention or treatment of pathologies, in animals or in humans; and for use in pharmaceutical product for prevention or treatment of pathologies affecting the digestive system, in animals or in humans, where pathologies of the digestive system are Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, colon cancer, or infectious diseases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process does not require separation step and does not produce any waste. The resulting caramel exhibits prebiotic properties, promoting the development of beneficial intestinal flora, and offers reparative effect on damaged colon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) caramel with high content of prebiotic oligosaccharides comprising greater than or equal to 40% prebiotic oligosaccharides of fructose dianhydrides type and glycosylated fructose dianhydrides with degree of polymerization of 3-25 and isomeric distribution of fructose dianhydrides, where the major isomer is alpha -D-fructofuranose beta -D-fructopyranose 1,2':2,1'-dianhydride; (2) use of caramel in an amount of 1-30 wt.% in a product for animal or human consumption to induce increase of Bifidobacteria or Lactobacillus in the intestinal tract; (3) use of caramel in an amount of 1-30 wt.% in a pharmaceutical product for prevention or treatment of pathologies, in animals or in humans; and (4) use of caramel in an amount of 1-30 wt.% in a pharmaceutical product for prevention or treatment of pathologies affecting the digestive system, in animals or in humans, where pathologies of the digestive system are Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, colon cancer, or infectious diseases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Prebiotic oligosaccharides are fructose dianhydride type and glycosylated fructose dianhydrides; and saccharide starting material is D-fructose, sucrose, or any oligo- or polysaccharide containing fructose as a constituent, particularly fructobiose, such as palatinose or leucrose, fructooligosaccharides, such as 1-kestose or nystase, fructans, and inulin. Saccharide starting material containing D-fructose is used at a ratio of 1:10-10:1 with respect to another sugar for food use, particularly glucose, galactose, sucrose, maltose, lactose, raffinose, or cyclodextrins. Food acid selected from acetic acid, citric acid, and phosphoric acid is added to reaction mixture in a ratio of 1-20 wt.% relative to the saccharide starting material. Concentration of saccharide starting material in water is 60-95% (w/v), preferably 70-90% (w/v). Caramel additionally contains greater than or equal to 1 other sugar different from fructose, particularly glucose, galactose, sucrose, maltose, lactose, raffinose, cyclodextrins, or reversal oligosaccharides formed from these sugars, and greater than or equal to 1 component selected from vitamins, flavorings, dyes, prebiotics, and/or probiotics. Preferred Conditions: Heating temperature is 70-140 degrees C, preferably 80-115 degrees C. Heating time is 1-168 hours, preferably 24-72 hours. Preferred Process: Upon addition of food acid, heating proceeds for 6-12 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antiulcer; Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Cytostatic; Antimicrobial. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN RU2016141877-A; RU2662529-C2
TI Purifying municipal sewage by treatment with cavitation, rotating magnetic field with ferromagnetic elements and with magnetostriction, ultrasound, and UV generated in energy-saturated field of process activation device, and fractionating.
AU GOREVANOVA T B
   BELOV O P
AE GOREVANOVA T B (GORE-Individual)
   BELOV O P (BELO-Individual)
GA 2018381285
AB    NOVELTY - Municipal sewage is purified by averaging composition of medium, alkalinizing, treating with cavitation in rotating magnetic field with ferromagnetic elements, performing ozonation, filtering, separating fractions, purifying previously communal effluents from non-specific inclusions, homogenizing, adding alkaline, further subjecting suspension to treatment with magnetostriction, ultrasound, and UV generated in an energy-saturated field of a process activation device, treating resulting emulsion with ozone, precipitating, normalizing pH, saturating with carbon dioxide, and fractionating.
   USE - Purification of municipal sewage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Municipal sewage is purified by averaging composition of medium to be purified, alkalinizing, treating with cavitation, rotating magnetic field, and ferromagnetic elements to disperse polluting components with the destruction of chemical and biological structures at the molecular level, until an emulsion is obtained, performing ozonation, filtering, separating fractions, purifying previously communal effluents from non-specific inclusions, homogenizing, adding alkaline at a rate of 0.1-1 g/dm3 until a suspension with pH of 10.5-11.5 is obtained, further subjecting suspension to treatment with magnetostriction, ultrasound, and UV generated in an energy-saturated field of a process activation device to disperse all components to produce emulsion that has the property of rapid stratification due to the absence of electrostatic shells in the newly formed molecules, treating resulting emulsion with ozone for additional oxidation and formation of water-insoluble salts, precipitating, normalizing pH, saturating emulsion with carbon dioxide until required pH is obtained, and fractionating gravitationally in a thin film filter to obtain dense sludge and water, which is further purified in the filters of fine post-treatment. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for purification of communal effluents comprising storage device, mixer, device for preparation and dosing of a reagent in the form of calcareous milk, devices for creating a cavitation process in rotating magnetic field with ferromagnetic elements, ozonation device, filters, device for separating non-specific inclusions, homogenizer for accumulation and homogenization, intermediate tank equipped with fecal pumps having cutting blades, emulsion saturation unit with carbon dioxide, thin-film fraction filter, fine post-cleaning filter, and mixer.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PN CN107929091-A
TI Anti-wrinkle essence for health care product or            cell culture medium in medical dressing, contains skin            collagen as fish skin collagen, is prepared by breaking            tissue, thawing fish skin, adding water to skin,            crushing mixture.
AU ZHOU X
AE GREAT BEAUTY BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GREA-Non-standard)
GA 201833437G
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-wrinkle essence contains skin                collagen.
   USE - Anti-wrinkle essence for health care product                or cell culture medium in medical dressing (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-wrinkle essence improves extracting                rate, improves purity, is eco-friendly, helps to                improve skin keratinocytes, whitens skin, has                antioxidant effects and repaired the damaged cells                caused by ultraviolet or other cosmetic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for extracting high-activity fish skin collagen,                which involves:
   (A) breaking tissue, thawing fish skin, adding                water to skin in mass ratio of 10:0.2-1;
   (B) crushing mixture and pulverizing, crushing                and grinding with a ultrasonic intensity of                500-1000 Watts/liter for 30-60 minutes to obtain                slurry;
   (C) removing salt-soluble impurities by mixing                slurry with sodium chloride solution, mixing and                soaking for 10-15 hours, centrifuging to remove the                supernatant to obtain the first precipitate;
   (D) removing acid-soluble impurities by mixing                the first precipitate and citric acid in weight                ratio of 1:5-9, and soaking for 6-12 hours;
   (E) centrifuging to remove the supernatant to                obtain the second precipitate;
   (F) performing enzymatic treatment by mixing                second precipitate with water and adding second                precipitation of 2-6% papain;
   (G) mixing weight ratio of the second                precipitate to water is 1:20-40 at 35-38 degrees C                for 6-12 hours;
   (H) control the pH of the reaction solution to                4.5-5.5, centrifuging, and taking supernatant to                obtain extract;
   (I) degreasing treatment, mixing crude extract                with carbon dioxide supercritical degrease into                sealed autoclave and introducing carbon dioxide                high-pressure pump at 40 degrees C, where                concentration of carbon dioxide of 0.8 g/mL at 20                megaPascal for 10-20 minutes;
   (J) performing refining treatment by taking                product and adding into 0.4-0.9 moles/L solid                sodium chloride, salting and salting out, salting                out, collecting by centrifugation by using 0.4-0.8                moles/L acetic acid to dissolve; and
   (K) dissolving the solution by                ultrafiltration, desalting and concentrating,                freeze-drying the ultrafiltration retentate to                obtain desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anti-wrinkle                  essence comprises first component which is 8 g                  fish skin collagen freeze-dried powder, and                  second component comprises 5-20 wt.% hyaluronic                  acid solution (1% concentration), 0.1-1 wt.%                  sodium citrate, 2-10 wt.% resveratrol, 2-10 wt.%                  arbutin, 51-82.9 wt.% de-ionized water. Preferred                  Condition: The molecular weight cut-off of the                  ultrafiltration membrane is 2000 kiloDalton, less                  than 3000 kiloDalton, preferably 2200, 2500 or                  2600 kiloDalton. Preferred Process: The                  ultrafiltrate on freeze dryer is used under                  cryopreservation conditions, where pre-freeze                  phase is set at 20-50 degrees C, continue to                  maintain for 30-60 minutes. The sublimation                  phase, where the pressure in the vacuum oven is                  controlled at 0.15-0.22 megaBar by using two                  Sublimation. The first sublimation temperature is                  raised from (-40)-(-15) degrees C. This process                  is maintained for 90 minutes. The second                  sublimation temperature is increased from (-15)-0                  degrees C for 120 minutes. The desorption phase                  in which the pressure in the vacuum box is                  controlled at 0.16 to 0.22 megaBar, and the                  temperature is raised from 0-30 degrees C for 40                  minutes, and then maintained at 30 degrees C for                  200 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); ; D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PN CN107760883-A; CN107760883-B
TI Removing iron from nickel-cobalt solution rapidly comprises e.g. preparing calcium hydroxide slurry, adding nickel-cobalt solution to be treated into the reactor, introducing compressed air into the reactor and replacing carbon dioxide gas.
AU HU J
   KOU Z
   CAO D
   MA Q
   ZHAN Y
   HE Y
   XU H
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
   LANZHOU JINCHUAN SCI PARK CO LTD (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 201819948W
AB    NOVELTY - Removing iron from nickel-cobalt solution rapidly comprises e.g. preparing 10-20% calcium hydroxide slurry, adding nickel-cobalt solution to be treated into the reactor, introducing compressed air into the reactor, replacing carbon dioxide gas in the solution, heating solution in the reactor, adding calcium hydroxide slurry into the solution in the reactor to make pH value of solution to 0.5-1.5, introducing sulfur dioxide and air mixed gas into the solution in the reactor, reacting, and adding calcium hydroxide slurry to adjust the pH value of the solution.
   USE - The method is useful for removing iron from nickel-cobalt solution rapidly.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures less than 0.01 g/l iron in the solution and less than 0.5% residue containing cobalt; has more than 99.8% iron removal rate; is fast and short process flow; saves energy consumption and production cost; adopts low equipment maintenance cost; and has small amount of waste slag discharge and metal loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing iron from nickel-cobalt solution rapidly comprises (i) preparing 10-20% calcium hydroxide slurry, (ii) adding nickel-cobalt solution to be treated into the reactor, introducing compressed air into the reactor for 5-10 minutes, replacing carbon dioxide gas in the solution, (iii) heating solution in the reactor, controlling the temperature at 25-40 degrees C, (iv) adding calcium hydroxide slurry into the solution in the reactor to make pH value of solution to 0.5-1.5, introducing sulfur dioxide and air mixed gas into the solution in the reactor, reacting for 40-45 minutes, adding calcium hydroxide slurry to adjust the pH value of the solution in the reactor to 1.5-3, reacting for 40-45 minutes, adding calcium hydroxide slurry to adjust the pH value of the solution in the reactor to 3-4.5, and reacting for 1.5-2 hours, and (v) after the reaction is finished, closing the sulfur dioxide gas valve, blowing the compressed air into sulfur dioxide gas, transferring reaction solution into the filter device to filter, introducing the filtrate into the next step, and piling filter residue stack for disposal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the sulfur dioxide is mixed with air in three stages, first in the total header, then enter the cyclone for further mixing, finally, it is fully mixed in a multi-stage elbow, and the mixing ratio of sulfur dioxide and air reaches more than 98%. The ratio of sulfur dioxide to air is 0.8-10:100. In the method, the amount of sulfur dioxide and air mixture per cubic solution in the reactor is more than 20 m3/hour. In the method, after the reaction was completed, the solution in the reactor was not completely removed, leaving 10% solution, the iron slag in the solution was used as seed crystal to accelerate the next reaction. In the method, the reactor solution was heated with direct steam.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-B; M25-H; M25-J

PN CN107751419-A
TI Preparing walnut powder involves soaking walnut in citric acid solution, drying, crushing, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, mixing walnut oil, sesame, skim milk, vitamin E and linoleic acid and homogenizing mixture.
AU ZHAO Y
   CAO L
AE HARBIN MEISEN FOOD MFG CO LTD (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 201820160T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing walnut powder involves (a) collecting fresh walnuts, soaking in citric acid aqueous solution, drying and crushing, (b) extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, reducing pressure, separating and taking the walnut oil, (c) adding sesame, skim milk, vitamin E and linoleic acid to the walnut oil, and centrifuging, (d) homogenizing the mixture, (e) stirring and mixing the homogenized mixture, controlling the temperature, vacuum emulsifying, homogenizing and sterilizing mixture, and (f) vacuum concentrating the sterilized mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing walnut powder for infants.
   ADVANTAGE - The walnut powder is rich in linolenic acid, folic acid, vitamins and nutrients and promotes the growth of infants. The method ensures high extraction rate of oil, provides evenly emulsified product, allows even mixing of grease, and improves the fluidity of the oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing walnut powder involves (a) collecting fresh walnuts, washing, soaking in 10-20% citric acid aqueous solution for 20-30 minutes, removing out, cleaning, drying, crushing and sieving through 50-100 mesh sieve, (b) extracting the crushed walnut by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 50-55 degrees C and extraction pressure of 30-35 MPa for 2-2.5 hours, reducing pressure, separating and taking the walnut oil, (c) adding 5-8 %mass sesame, 3.5-5.0 %mass skim milk, 4.0-5.0 %mass vitamin E and 1.5-2.0 %mass linoleic acid to the walnut oil, maintaining at 50-55 degrees C, and centrifuging at 500-800 revolutions per minute for 30-40 minutes, (d) homogenizing the mixture, controlling the temperature to 70-80 degrees C, carrying out two-stage homogenization comprising primary homogenization at pressure of 20-25 MPa, and secondary homogenization at pressure of 6-10 MPa, (e) stirring and mixing the homogenized mixture, performing vacuum emulsifying and homogenizing at -0.05 to -0.08 MPa for 15-20 minutes, and sterilizing mixture, and (f) vacuum concentrating the sterilized mixture under vacuum pressure of 65-70 kPa at 35-40 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In step (b), extraction is carried out at pressure of 32 MPa and temperature of at 50 degrees C for 2.2 hours. The step (d) involves controlling temperature at 75 degrees C, carrying out primary homogenization at pressure of 22 MPa and secondary homogenization at pressure of 8 MPa. The step (e) involves pasteurizing at 110-130 degrees C for 10-15 seconds.

PT P
PN IN201721001830-A
TI Composition used for protecting rebar of concrete structure, is obtained by mixing powder compositions obtained by neutralizing amine solution with carbon dioxide-oxygen mixture and carboxylic acid, with binder, granulating, and compressing.
AU KAMAT M K
AE MADAN K K (MADA-Individual)
GA 2018121451
AB    NOVELTY - A concrete penetrating rebar protection composition is obtained by neutralizing the amine solution by bubbling a gaseous mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen, and maintaining the temperature of the resultant mixture at 30-80 degrees C, to obtain powder composition (A), and neutralizing the amine solution by slowly adding a mixture of carboxylic acid and maintaining the temperature of the resultant reaction mixture at 30-80 degrees C, to obtain powder composition (B), mixing powder composition (A) and powder composition (B) with a binder, granulating, and compressing.
   USE - Composition is used for protecting rebar of reinforced concrete structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition efficiently protects the rebar even in the presence of chloride ions, and easily and economically prevents corrosion of the rebar even in highly-corrosive media.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A concrete penetrating rebar protection composition is obtained by mixing monobasic amine and dibasic amine in ratio of 1:3-7:8, dissolving the resultant mixture of monobasic amine and dibasic amine in an organic solvent chosen from methyl alcohol, ethyl alcohol and acetone, obtaining an amine solution, neutralizing the amine solution by bubbling a gaseous mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen, and maintaining the temperature of the resultant mixture at 30-80 degrees C, to obtain powder composition (A), and neutralizing the amine solution by slowly adding a mixture of carboxylic acid and maintaining the temperature of the resultant reaction mixture at 30-80 degrees C, to obtain powder composition (B), mixing the powder composition (A) and powder composition (B) in ratio of 30:70-80:20, with a binder, granulating the resultant mixture, compressing the granulated mixture to obtain tablets, and packing the resultant tablets in a tablet holder and inserting into a concrete structure, such that tablets release the corrosion inhibiting vapors which migrated to a distance of 20 cm or less, to form a monomolecular protective coating with thickness of 19-21 mu m on rebar surface, thus protecting the concrete structure from corroding at cathodic and anodic corrosive sites.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The monobasic amine is dicyclohexylamine. The dibasic amine is monocyclohexylamine. Preferred Process: The mixture of carboxylic acid is prepared by mixing benzoic acid, salicylic acid and cinnamic acid in ratio of 30:20:18-23:21:19.

PN CN108999701-A
TI Hierarchical combustion based ultra-critical            carbon dioxide semi-closed pure oxygen combustion            generating system, has main gas turbine connected with            main combustion chamber where dioxide output channel is            connected with combustion chamber.
AU YU X
   ZHAO D
   LIU H
   WANG L
   SHI C
   SONG Z
   QIN Z
   LI W
AE SHANGHAI MICROPOWERS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A37738
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a driving force generation                system formed with a main combustion chamber. A                main gas turbine is connected with the main                combustion chamber. A power generating component is                connected with the main gas turbine. A secondary                gas turbine is connected with the main combustion                chamber through a secondary combustion chamber. A                fuel supply system is connected with the main                combustion chamber and the secondary combustion                chamber. A high-pressure carbon dioxide output                channel is connected with the secondary combustion                chamber. A low-pressure carbon dioxide output                channel is connected with the main combustion                chamber.
   USE - Hierarchical combustion based ultra-critical                carbon dioxide semi-closed pure oxygen combustion                generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes high-temperature and                high-pressure smoke gas secondary combustion                process by a turbine pump so as to improve                efficiency and power density, and utilizes a waste                heat recycling component to improve energy-saving                efficiency, and can effectively reduce high                pressure carbon dioxide collecting cost so as to                easily realize carbon dioxide capture and recycling                process, thus ensuring zero discharge of the carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram                of a hierarchical combustion based ultra-critical                carbon dioxide semi-closed pure oxygen combustion                generating system.
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

PN CN108952865-A
TI Ultra-critical hydrothermal combustion carbon            dioxide circulation generating system, has system main            unit provided with hydrothermal combustion reaction            device, dioxide circulation generating device and            auxiliary heating device.
AU LI Z
   ZHONG W
   LIU X
   SHAO Y
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2018A0411E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a system main unit provided                with an ultra-critical hydrothermal combustion                reaction device, an ultra-critical carbon dioxide                circulation generating device and an auxiliary                heating device. The ultra-critical hydrothermal                combustion reaction device is provided with a coal                water slurry storage tank (1), a high pressure                water-coal-slurry pump (2) and two heat exchangers                (3, 5). An oxygen conveying component is connected                with a fuel inlet of an ultra-critical hydrothermal                combustion reactor (6). A carbon dioxide separator                (10) is provided with a gas phase outlet and a                liquid-solid phase outlet.
   USE - Ultra-critical hydrothermal combustion carbon                dioxide circulation generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system generates heat by methane                combustion by utilizing carbon dioxide as                circulating working substance and coal combustion                supercritical hydrothermal as a main heat source,                promotes high-efficient coal conversion process,                comprehensively realizes coal high efficiency power                generation process so as to realize zero emission                of sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides, dust and other                pollutants and carbon dioxide greenhouse gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram                of an ultra-critical hydrothermal combustion carbon                dioxide circulation generating system.
   Coal water slurry storage tank (1)
   High pressure water-coal-slurry pump                (2)
   Heat exchangers (3, 5)
   Ultra-critical hydrothermal combustion                reactor (6)
   Carbon dioxide separator (10)

PN JP7133819-B1
TI System used for supplying hydrogen, consists of            formic acid generator that generates formic acid by            artificial photosynthesis from water and air or exhaust            carbon dioxide, formic acid storage tank, formic acid            decomposition device, power generation unit and path            for circulating carbon dioxide.
AU MORI K
   NISHIKAWA Y
   KANEI M
   NISHINO H
   TOMISHIMA H
   HIROKAWA A
   AKEDA H
   YAMADA M
   MINAMI S
   KOJIMA K
   AMAO Y
AE IIDA GROUP HOLDINGS CO LTD (IIDA-Non-standard)
   UNIV PUBLIC CORP OSAKA (UYPU-Non-standard)
GA 2022B9513V
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen supply system consists of a formic                acid generator that generates formic acid by                artificial photosynthesis from water and air or                exhaust carbon dioxide, a formic acid storage tank                for storing the formic acid generated by the formic                acid generator, a formic acid decomposition device                that decomposes formic acid supplied from the                formic acid storage tank into hydrogen and carbon                dioxide by a catalytic reaction in a deoxygenated                environment at normal temperature and normal                pressure, a hydrogen engine driven by using                hydrogen among the hydrogen and carbon dioxide                supplied from the formic acid decomposition device                as fuel, and a power generation unit using the                hydrogen engine as a drive source, and a path for                circulating carbon dioxide discharged unreacted                from the generator out of the hydrogen and carbon                dioxide supplied from the formic acid decomposition                device to the formic acid generation device.
   USE - System used for supplying hydrogen (claimed)                for energy supply system for practical house. Can                also be used for hydrogen fuel cell vehicle and                hydrogen engine vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively use solar energy to                supply hydrogen safely and efficiently as energy                source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for supplying hydrogen, which involves producing                and storing formic acid by artificial                photosynthesis from the hydrogen ions, electrons                and the atmosphere or exhaust carbon dioxide, using                formic acid generation device that applies a                mixture of titanium oxide fine particles, pigments,                and viologen compounds to the surface of a solid                substrate using hydrogen ions and electrons                obtained by photolyzing water into oxygen,                decomposing formic acid into hydrogen and carbon                dioxide in a deoxidized environment at normal                temperature and pressure with a catalyst made by                dispersing platinum fine particles with a                water-soluble polymer polyvinylpyrrolidone,                generating electric power by a power generation                unit driven by a hydrogen engine driven by using                hydrogen of the hydrogen and carbon dioxide                obtained by decomposition in the formic acid                decomposition step as a fuel and returning                unreacted carbon dioxide discharged from the power                generation unit of the hydrogen and carbon dioxide                obtained by decomposition in the formic acid                decomposition step to the formic acid generation                step for reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The formic acid                  generator comprises hydrogen ion generating unit                  for decomposing water to generate oxygen and                  obtaining hydrogen ions and electrons, and a                  formic acid generating unit for generating formic                  acid by artificial photosynthesis from                  atmospheric carbon dioxide and/or exhaust carbon                  dioxide using the formic acid generating device                  using hydrogen ions and electrons obtained by                  decomposing water by the hydrogen ion generating                  unit. In the formic acid generating device, a                  mixture of titanium oxide fine particles, a dye,                  and a viologen compound is applied to the surface                  of the substrate. The formic acid decomposing                  device decomposes formic acid into hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide in a deoxygenated environment at                  normal temperature and normal pressure with a                  catalyst comprising platinum fine particles                  dispersed in a water-soluble polymer                  polyvinylpyrrolidone. The formic acid                  decomposition apparatus includes a storage unit                  that stores at least formic acid, a reaction unit                  that decomposes the formic acid supplied from the                  storage unit into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, a                  control unit for controlling the reaction unit                  under deoxidization at normal temperature and                  normal pressure. The reaction unit has a catalyst                  in which the platinum fine particles are                  dispersed in a water-soluble polymer                  polyvinylpyrrolidone. The power generator further                  comprises a heat recovery unit for recovering                  heat generated by the hydrogen engine, and an                  inverter for converting the electric power                  generated by the power generation unit from                  direct current to alternating current.

PN CN114058803-A
TI Method for quenching carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, involves automatically adjusting flow rate and flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide sprayed by nozzles according to set temperature cooling curve of surface of metal workpiece, to perform controllable quenching and cooling on workpiece.
AU YANG J
AE YANG J (YANG-Individual)
GA 2022323458
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a supercritical fluid preparation tank (100) and a high-pressure heating device (200). The supercritical fluid preparation tank is used to provide a supercritical carbon dioxide quenching cooling medium for the system. The supercritical fluid preparation tank is used to introduce supercritical carbon dioxide into the heating device by utilizing the pressure difference to establish a preset quenching pressure in the heating device. The supercritical fluid preparation tank is used to spray supercritical carbon dioxide on the surface of the metal workpiece by using a pressure difference through multiple nozzles arranged in the heating device. The flow rate and flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide sprayed by several nozzles are automatically adjusted according to the set temperature cooling curve of the surface of the metal workpiece, so as to perform controllable quenching and cooling on the metal workpiece.
   USE - Method for quenching carbon dioxide supercritical fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical carbon dioxide as cooling medium, fully uses the gas high density under high pressure and uses pressure difference to control the cooling medium flow so as to control cooling speed, using the high thermal diffusion coefficient of supercritical fluid, low viscosity and extremely low surface tension to improve the cooling uniformity of the workpiece when quenching and cooling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide supercritical fluid quenching system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid quenching system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

AB    NOVELTY - A plant (100) for generating negative emissions of carbon dioxide (CO2) comprises gasifier (110); lime kiln (130) placed downstream of the gasifier; separator (150) placed downstream of the lime kiln; and CO2 permanent storage (170) placed downstream of the separator, where gasifier is suitable for receiving, as input, a fuel (111) and for producing, as output, a high-temperature syngas flow (114); lime kiln is suitable for receiving, as input, carbonate mineral (131) and high-temperature syngas flow produced by gasifier, the lime kiln being further suitable for producing oxide (134) and for releasing, as output, a flow of syngas (133) enriched with CO2; separator is suitable for receiving, as input, a gas flow containing CO2 to be treated to separately provide CO2 (151); and CO2 permanent storage is suitable for enclosing along time the CO2 provided by the separator.
   USE - A plant for generating negative emissions of carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system can produce negative emissions at low costs compared to known technologies or even generate an economic profit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for generation of negative emissions of CO2 which involves providing gasifier; feeding fuel to gasifier to obtain high-temperature syngas flow; conveying high-temperature syngas flow; providing lime kiln; feeding carbonate mineral to lime kiln; feeding high-temperature syngas flow to lime kiln to obtain oxide and gas flow enriched with CO2; retrieving oxide from lime kiln; conveying gas flow enriched with CO2; providing separator; feeding gas flow enriched with CO2 to separator; separately providing CO2; providing permanent storage for CO2; and conveying CO2 provided by the separator (150) to the permanent storage to enclose it along time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a process for the generation of negative carbon dioxide emissions.
   Plant for generating negative emissions of carbon dioxide (100)

   Fuel (111)

   Lime kiln (130)
   Carbonate mineral (131)
   Flow of syngas (133)
   Oxide (134)
   Separator (150)
   Carbon dioxide (151)
   Carbon dioxide permanent storage (170)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: The method further includes providing reformer between gasifier and lime kiln; feeding high-temperature syngas flow produced by gasifier to reformer; reforming, either catalytically or thermally, the hydrocarbons present in the syngas; and releasing high-temperature syngas flow enriched with hydrogen (H2) as output from the reformer. The method further includes providing water gas shift (WGS) reactor between lime kiln and separator; feeding high-temperature syngas flow enriched with CO2 and containing carbon monoxide and water (H2O) produced by lime kiln to WGS reactor; promoting WGS reaction; and releasing syngas flow enriched with CO2 and H2 as output from WGS reactor. The method further includes providing, between lime kiln and separator, an exploiting device; feeding syngas flow enriched with CO 2 to exploiting device; exploiting syngas for producing electric power, and/or heat, and/or useful chemicals; and releasing off-gas flow as output from exploiting device. The method further includes feeding gas flow containing H2 to separator and separately providing H2. The method further includes mixing oxide produced by lime kiln or relevant hydroxide with ocean water and/or with atmospheric air.

PN EP4039638-A1; WO2022167387-A1
TI Producing carbon monoxide for preparing phosgene            for synthesis of organic isocyanate, comprises            synthesizing carbon monoxide via reformer process,            purifying carbon monoxide-containing product gas and            separating carbon dioxide.
AU VOR D E L
   BULAN A
   WEBER R
   SCHMIDT T
   LETMATHE B
   ZABALZA G
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2022A06099
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon monoxide comprises                synthesizing carbon monoxide via reformer process                (6), where methane and water vapor (3) with at                least carbon dioxide (8a) are converted to carbon                monoxide-containing product gas at 500 degrees C                with the supply of thermal energy, purifying the                carbon monoxide-containing product gas, separating                carbon dioxide and optionally separating water                and/or hydrogen to obtain carbon monoxide,                providing carbon dioxide for addition to reformer                process, supplying thermal energy to the reformer                process for the synthesis of carbon monoxide by                combustion of fuel containing hydrogen produced by                means of regenerative energy, converting the                generated renewable energy and electrical energy                into heat.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon                monoxide for preparing phosgene for synthesis of                organic isocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes catalyst suitable for                electrically heated surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for synthesis of carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the production of carbon monoxide.
   Water vapor (3)
   Reformer process (6)
   Carbon dioxide (8a)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methane is taken                  from a fossil source and/or a biological source.                  The regenerative energy is either wind power,                  solar energy or hydropower. The hydrogen used for                  the combustion of hydrogen-containing fuel is                  provided by electrolysis process e.g. water                  electrolysis from water to hydrogen and oxygen,                  chloro-alkali electrolysis and hydrochloric acid                  electrolysis, where the electrical energy used                  for the electrolysis process is generated by                  regenerative energy. Preferred Method: The                  synthesis in the reformer process is carried out                  at least 700 degrees C, preferably 850-1000                  degrees C with a pressure of 10-40 bar,                  preferably 18-25 bar using a catalyst supported                  on a solid phase. The reformer process comprises                  (a) conversion of at least methane and steam into                  a mixture containing at least carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen, and (b) reacting the mixture from                  addition of carbon dioxide to product gas                  containing carbon monoxide. The method comprises                  separation of water, where water separated in the                  process is recycled in water electrolysis. The                  hydrogen is separated off using a cold box, where                  the hydrogen produced from the residual gas after                  further residual gas treatment into hydrogenation                  of organic nitro compound to produce organic                  amine as raw material for organic isocyanate. The                  synthesis of phosgene takes place from carbon                  monoxide and chlorine, preferably reaction of                  phosgene with at least one organic amine to form                  at least one organic isocyanate compound and                  hydrogen chloride. At least 80% of the energy                  used for the entire process is carried out using                  electricity generated from renewable                  energy.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of production of                carbon monoxide that do not support the                claims.

PN CN114736731-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process of            Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises placing            pretreated Ganoderma lucidum spore powder in extraction            reactor, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide            fluid, extracting, carrying out separation, starting            extraction, releasing spore oil.
AU BAI H
   TAO Y
   TAO Z
   TAO L
   LAN Y
   KONG Y
   TIAN K
   LI X
AE TAIAN ACAD AGRIC SCI (TAIA-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG ZHIRENTANG PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022996760
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                process of Ganoderma lucidumspore oil, comprises                (1) selecting Ganoderma lucidumspore powder for use                and pre-process, (2) placing the pretreated                Ganoderma lucidumspore powder in the extraction                reactor, (3) introducing the supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid, and extracting the reaction kettle                by heating, and the extraction pressure is 25-30                MPa, (4) carrying out the separation by a                separation kettle, and controlling the separation                pressure to be 6-8 MPa, and (6) starting the                extraction, and releasing light yellow Ganoderma                lucidumspore oil from the outlet of the separation                kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction process of Ganoderma lucidum                spore oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problems of low purity,                instability and difficulty in removing plasticizers                in the separation and purification of Ganoderma                lucidumspore oil; does not contain any harmful                chemical reagents, which ensures product quality,                is greener and healthier, and can better satisfy                consumers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  Ganoderma lucidumspore powder is specifically                  from mature and broken Ganoderma lucidumspore                  powder. In the step (1), the steps of                  preprocessing Ganoderma lucidumspore powder                  comprises (a) subjecting the mature and broken                  Ganoderma lucidumspore powder to impurity removal                  treatment, (b) crushing the Ganoderma                  lucidumspore powder after impurity removal is at                  low temperature, and (c) making Ganoderma                  lucidumspore powder into granules with a diameter                  of 0.5-3 mm. The time of Ganoderma lucidumspore                  powder crushing is 40-60 minutes, the temperature                  is controlled below 15&#176;C, and the roller                  gap is controlled below 10 filaments. In the step                  (3), the flow rate of the supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid is 25 l/hour. In the step (3), the                  extraction temperature is 40-45&#176;C, and                  the extraction time is for 60-90 minutes. In the                  step (4), the separation temperature is                  40-45&#176;C.Preferred Components: The wall breaking rate                  of Ganoderma lucidumspore powder is over 99%, and                  the moisture content is controlled below                  2.5%.

TI Producing gemstone from corpse of living being, comprises burning dead body to ashes, extracting carbon dioxide from carbon from ashes and/or during burning of carbon from dead body, and generating intermediate product from carbon dioxide.
AU DIENER C
AE DIENER ELECTRONIC GMBH & CO KG (DIEN-Non-standard)
GA 202234536C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a gemstone (38) from a corpse (10) of a living being, where the gemstone (38) comprises carbon from the corpse, comprises (a) burning the dead body (10) to ashes (16); (b) extracting carbon dioxide (14) from carbon from the ashes (16) and/or during burning of carbon from the dead body; (c) generating an intermediate product (26) from the carbon dioxide, where the intermediate product comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbonate anions (25b); (d) generating elemental carbon (32) from the intermediate product; and (e) creating the gemstone from the elemental carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a gemstone from a corpse of a living being.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces gemstones from carbon from human or animal remains while increasing amount and purity of carbon recovered from remains and controlling composition of gemstones.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for gem stone comprising carbon from a dead body of a living being.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the ash is supplied with oxygen (20), and reacting the carbon in the ash with the oxygen. The carbon dioxide in the step (b) is generated by burning the dead body with a carbon-free fuel (12). The carbon dioxide is produced in the step (b) by the ash is supplied with hydrochloric acid (18), with which the carbon in the ash reacts to form carbon dioxide, the carbon is composed in particular of calcium carbonate ( 17) provided in cremated bones of the dead body. The intermediate product is produced in the step (c) in the form of a carbonate, with the carbon dioxide is supplied with an alkaline lye (22) and with the carbon dioxide reacting with the alkaline lye to form the carbonate. The carbon dioxide is supplied with caustic soda (24), the carbon dioxide (14) reacting with the caustic soda (24) to form sodium carbonate (25a). The intermediate product is produced in method step (c) in the form of dry ice (28), the carbon dioxide is frozen to form the dry ice (28). The method produced the elementary carbon, where the intermediate product is fed with heating an alkali metal, alkaline earth metal and/or an earth metal (30) with which the intermediate product. Magnesium (34) is added into the intermediate product. The method produces the gemstone from the elementary carbon, a hydrocarbon-containing gas (42) is produced, the hydrocarbon-containing gas in a chemical vapor deposition, preferably plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition. The method produces the gemstone, where molecular hydrogen (40) is supplied to the elementary carbon, which reacts with the elementary carbon to form methane (44) deposited in a chemical vapor deposition. The method produces the gemstone, calcium (52) is added into the elementary carbon, which reacts with the elementary carbon to form calcium carbide (54), and then the calcium carbide and water (50) is supplied, with which the calcium carbide reacts to form ethyne (46) deposited in a chemical vapor deposition. Preferred Components: The fuel contains ammonia (13) and/or hydrogen (40). The oxygen is supplied in the form of plasma and/or in a plasma reaction.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)

PN JP2022036649-A
TI Determination of melting of pig iron, involves comparing measured carbon dioxide concentration in exhaust gas generated during pig iron dissolution treatment, with theoretical concentration of carbon dioxide, to determine whether pig iron is dissolved.
AU IIJIMA M
   SOYA K
   IIO Y
   OHARA T
   MORIOKA K
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 202255308P
AB    NOVELTY - Melting of pig iron is determined by comparing carbon dioxide concentration measured by sampling an exhaust gas generated during pig iron dissolution treatment and theoretical carbon-dioxide concentration in exhaust gas calculated from exhaust gas temperature, auxiliary fuel gas flow rate, fuel flow rate, and intruding air quantity, with each other. The exhaust gas is compared with the theoretical concentration in the exhaust gas. The pig iron is dissolved when the actual measured carbon-dioxide concentration is higher than the theoretical carbon carbon-density concentration by a predetermined value or more. Measured oxygen concentration is lower than theoretical oxygen concentration when actually measured oxygen concentration becomes lower than actual oxygen concentration. The amount of molten pig iron estimated on the basis of elapsed time from the point of time when the iron is determined to be dissolved by the method for determining the melting of the iron, is estimated.
   USE - Determination of melting of pig iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The melting of pig iron is determined with high accuracy.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

TI Producing propylene comprises contacting reaction            mixture containing propane and carbon dioxide in            flow-through catalytic unit with fixed catalyst bed            which is metal oxide catalyst deposited on            carrier.
AU TEDEEVA M A
   KUSTOV A L
   PRIBYTKOV P V
   IGONINA M S
   AYMALETDINOV T R
   KIM K O
   KUSTOV L M
AE UNIV MISIS NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYMI-Non-standard)
   UNIV MISIS NAT RES & TECHNOLOGY (UYMI-Non-standard)
GA 2022735522
AB    NOVELTY - Producing propylene from propane during                dehydrogenation of propane in the presence of                carbon dioxide, comprises contacting a reaction                mixture containing propane and carbon dioxide in a                flow-through catalytic unit with a fixed catalyst                bed, which is a metal oxide catalyst deposited on a                carrier. The chromium oxide is used as the metal                oxide catalyst in a content of 2-15 wt.%. The                silica gel with a specific surface area of more                than 180-1000 m2/g is used as the carrier. The                process is carried out at 550-700 degrees C under                supercritical conditions for carbon dioxide at a                pressure of 75-100 atm when a reaction mixture                containing a mixture of carbon dioxide and propane,                taken in a molar ratio of 3-20, respectively, is                fed into the reactor and the flow rate of the                reaction mixture is 1.3-10.3 mmol/minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                propylene.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.

PT P
PN CN111575725-A; CN111575725-B
TI Electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide to graphene involves passing carbon dioxide gas into electrolysis device, inserting wrapped carbon nanosheets, centrifugal separation of graphene suspension and washing and freeze-drying.
AU REN Z
   ZHANG C
   YU Z
   GAO S
   LIU L
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2020851487
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide to graphene comprises: (i) passing carbon dioxide gas into electrolysis device, electrochemical dissociation reaction occurs in the device and carbon nanosheets are generated on the cathode of the device, and wrapping the carbon nanosheets on the cathode; (ii) inserting wrapped the nanosheets as cathode, the stripping anode into the electrolyte of the stripping electrolysis device and at the same time inserting into the stripping cathode and the stripping power is turned on, in the stripping electrolysis device, the ions in the electrolyte enter the nanosheet under the action of the electric field and the internal water electrolysis generates gas and electrochemically strips carbon nanosheets to obtain graphene suspension; (iii) centrifugal separation of the suspension and washing to remove soluble impurities to form solid product; and (iv) freeze-drying to remove moisture to form graphene.
   USE - The method is useful for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide to graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides graphene with high efficiency, environmental protection and low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (1), the molten carbonate is at least one of lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate. The reaction temperature during the dissociation reaction is 650-850 degrees C. The cathode in the electrolysis device is a stainless steel wire mesh, and the anode in the electrolysis device is a stainless steel plate or a nickel-chromium-iron alloy plate. The surface of the wire mesh is plated with zinc, nickel or chromium, the surface of the anode in the electrolysis device is plated with platinum or iridium, and the distance between adjacent anodes and cathodes in the electrolysis device is 1-3 cm. The electrochemical dissociation reaction that occurs in the electrolysis device is an intermittent operation type and carbon dioxide gas is continuously introduced during the reaction, where, during the electrochemical dissociation reaction in the electrolysis device, the output current of the electrolysis power supply is divided into three consecutive stages, the current density of the first stage is 0.5-2 A/m2, and the duration is 5-15 minutes, the current density of the second stage is 4-5 A/m2, and the duration is 3-6 minutes, the current density is 15-30 A/m2, and the duration is 200-400 minutes. The stripped cathode is a stainless steel electrode plated with platinum, and the stripped cathode and the stripped anode are passed through a regenerated cellulose-based dialysis membrane or modified isolation by cellulose-based dialysis membrane. The electrolytic solution in the stripping electrolysis device is a ammonium sulfate solution with a concentration of 0.2-2 mol/l, sodium sulfate solution or potassium sulfate solution. The output current density of the stripping power supply is 10-20 A/m2 and the duration is 6-12 hours.

The carbonated injection water comprises one or more salts at a total combined salt concentration of 5000-7000 ppm.
   USE - Method for carbonated water flooding of underground hydrocarbon reservoir used in system (claimed) for oil recovery.

The sulfate ions has a concentration of at least 400 ppm. The carbonated injection water comprises carbon dioxide at a mole fraction of at least 0.001, preferably 0.005-0.05.
