PN CN109852473-A
TI Preparing composite antioxidant liposome comprises e.g. cooling gas carbon dioxide, converting into liquid carbon dioxide, adding lipid material and fat-soluble antioxidant, injecting into reactor, stirring uniformly and heating.
AU WANG H
   PAN L
   GU K
   XIE W
   ZHANG P
   LI Z
AE UNIV HENAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHY-C)
GA 201953314P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite antioxidant liposome comprises (i) cooling gas carbon dioxide to less than 0 degrees C, pressurizing to 4-6 MPa and converting into liquid carbon dioxide, (ii) adding the lipid material and the fat-soluble antioxidant to the autoclave, and injecting the liquid carbon dioxide into the reactor, stirring uniformly and heating to 30 degrees C, (iii) adding the aqueous solvent in which the water-soluble antioxidant is dissolved to the absorption tank, and opening the valve of the high-pressure reactor, and the fluid in the high-pressure reaction tank flows into the absorption tank through the nozzle, so that the solution in the tank is in a misty fluidized state, and (iv) releasing the liquid in the absorption tank.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite antioxidant liposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good stability, is added to the oil-milk composite food system, does not contain toxic and harmful organic chemical reagents, is simple and easy, and is suitable for industrial scale production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lipid material is soybean phospholipid, fatty acid glyceride and/or cholesterol. The fat-soluble antioxidant is vitamin E, t-butyl hydroquinone, resveratrol and/or rosemary extract. The water-soluble antioxidant is vitamin C. The aqueous solvent is a phosphate buffer solution or distilled water. Preferred Method: The lipid material and the fat-soluble antioxidant are dissolved in absolute ethanol and then added to the autoclave.

PN CN109160516-A
TI Jointly capturing mineralized carbon dioxide            involves pouring the previously dried solid waste and            salt-containing brine into reaction kettle, and adding            additive to reaction vessel.
AU LIU R
   WANG X
   WANG Q
   HE Z
   XIAO T
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 201907303G
AB    NOVELTY - Jointly capturing mineralized carbon dioxide                involves pouring the previously dried solid waste                and salt-containing brine into a reaction kettle                according to a solid-liquid ratio of 0-1g/ml, and                adding ammonia water, sodium hydroxide, potassium                hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium                hydrogencarbonate, potassium carbonate and                potassium hydrogencarbonate as an additive to the                reaction vessel, and heating to 30-80 degrees C                under closed conditions. The mass ratio of the                additive to the brine is 0.5-1.5g/l. The simulated                flue gas is introduced into the reaction vessel as                the flue gas before decarburization. The pressure                of the flue gas in the reaction kettle is                stabilized at 1-4MPa. The simulated flue gas                comprises 0-30% carbon dioxide and balance amount                of nitrogen. The simulated flue gas is added to                maintain the pressure in the reactor at 1-5MPa                during the reaction. The heating is stopped after 2                hours of reaction.
   USE - Method for jointly capturing mineralized                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to jointly capture                mineralized carbon dioxide in a simple and rapid                manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt-containing                  brine comprises underground or industrial brine,                  seawater, concentrated seawater, salt field                  wastewater, and industrial wastewater.

   USE - Preparation of self-supporting ILs and CMP film by electropolymerization for improving separation performance of carbon dioxide and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving long-cycle stability of the membrane material by confining the ionic liquid in the pores of CMP. The introduction of ionic liquids with strong affinity for carbon dioxide molecule improves diffusion coefficient and permeation flux of carbon dioxide in the membrane, and improves separation performance of carbon dioxide and methane mixed gas. The ionic liquid will not overflow due to large gas flow under long-term working conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves placing thiophene monomer derivative with electrochemically active polymerization sites in a beaker, adding a polar organic solvent such as dichloromethane to fully dissolve the monomer, adding ionic liquid with a strong affinity for carbon dioxide molecules, as an electrolyte, and ultrasonically processing obtained uniform solution in an electropolymerization system which is a three-electrode system including a reference electrode as silver/monovalent silver ion electrode stored in an organic electrolyte, a working electrode as fluorine-doped tin oxide conductive glass or a double-channel anodic aluminum oxide (AAO) substrate with a conductive coating, and a counter electrode as titanium or platinum conductive metal flake. The preparation of 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate (BMIMBF4) and poly(1,3,5-tris(2-thienyl)benzene) (PTTB) film material involves ultrasonically dissolving 0.1 M (BMIM)(BF4) and 0.01 M 1,3,5-tris(2-thienyl)benzene (TTB) with methylene chloride and polycarbonate mixed solvent, in the volume ratio of 20:1, using double-channel AAO substrate with conductive coating as the working electrode, ultrasonically cleaning and drying with acetone, water and ethanol, using titanium plate as counter electrode, silver/monovalent silver ion as reference electrode by cyclic voltammetry method in the scanning voltage range of 0-1.5 V, scanning speed of 0.01 V/second, soaking and washing with methylene chloride for one or more times until the solution is colorless, and drying in vacuum at 60 degrees C. The preparation of BMIMBF4 and poly(tris(4-(2-thienyl)phenyl)amine) (PTTPA) film material involves ultrasonically dissolving 0.1 M (BMIM)(BF4) and 0.01 M tris(4-(2-thienyl)phenyl)amine (TTPA) with methylene chloride and polycarbonate mixed solvent, in the volume ratio of 20:1, using fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO) conductive glass with conductive coating as the working electrode, ultrasonically cleaning and drying with acetone, water and ethanol, using titanium plate as counter electrode, silver/monovalent silver ion as reference electrode by cyclic voltammetry method in the scanning voltage range of 0-1.5 V, scanning speed of 0.01 V/second, soaking and washing with methylene chloride for one or more times until the solution is colorless, and drying in vacuum at 60 degrees C, replacing with a solvent with surface tension of more than 30 mN/m, such as dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide, and vacuum drying to obtain target product at 60 degrees C. The preparation of BMIMBF4 and PTTPA film material involves ultrasonically dissolving 0.1 M tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate ((N4444)(BF4)) and 0.01 M TTPA with methylene chloride and polycarbonate mixed solvent, in the volume ratio of 20:1, using FTO conductive glass with conductive coating as the working electrode, ultrasonically cleaning and drying with acetone, water and ethanol, using titanium plate as counter electrode, silver/monovalent silver ion as reference electrode by cyclic voltammetry method in the scanning voltage range of 0-1.5 V, scanning speed of 0.01 V/second, soaking and washing with methylene chloride for one or more times until the solution is colorless, and drying in vacuum at 60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The thiophene monomer derivative is any one of 1,3,5-tris(2-thienyl)benzene and tris(4-(2-thienyl)phenyl)amine. The organic solvent is any one of acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide, dichloromethane and propylene carbonate. The ionic liquid is any one of (BMIM)BF4 and (N4444)(BF4).

PN WO2019170779-A1; CN111819163-A; EP3762350-A1; US2021047246-A1; JP2021516612-W; US11261137-B2; JP7152495-B2
TI Methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon            dioxide in reactant stream used for producing methane,            involves using catalyst comprising alumina and            nickel-active composition and manganese in preset molar            ratio.
AU GEISBAUER A
AE CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
GA 2019783466
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon                dioxide in a reactant stream comprising carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide, hydrogen and more                than 1 ppb sulfur, involves using a catalyst                comprising alumina, a nickel-active composition and                manganese. The molar ratio of nickel with respect                to manganese in the catalyst is 1.0-15.0,                preferably 2.0-12.0.
   USE - Methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon                dioxide used for producing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables methanation of carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with improved                selectivity, activity and stability of                sulfur-containing streams.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                catalyst for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or                carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen in the                presence of more than 1 ppb sulfur.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The catalyst is prepared                  by co-precipitation, or impregnating alumina with                  a solution comprising nickel. The method involves                  passing the reactant stream through a reactor                  with a catalyst, and contacting the catalyst with                  the reactant stream at more than 150 degrees C,                  preferably more than 200 degrees such the                  catalyst absorbs the sulfur contained in the                  reactant stream and simultaneously catalyzes the                  methanation reaction. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of nickel with respect to manganese                  in the catalyst is preferably 7.5-9.5. The molar                  ratio of aluminum with respect to nickel in the                  catalyst is 0.1-0.9, preferably 0.3-0.7. The                  solution comprising nickel further comprises                  manganese. The reactant stream preferably                  comprises 4 ppb to 50 ppm sulfur. The                  nickel-active composition has crystallites having                  a diameter of less than 20 nm, preferably less                  than 10 nm. The catalyst absorbs the sulfur                  contained in the reactant stream to 90%,                  preferably completely sorbed off. Preferred                  Properties: The catalyst has a carbon dioxide                  uptake capacity at 35 degrees C of more than 200                  mu mol/g, preferably 200-300 mu mol/g.

PT P
PN CN109126381-A; CN109126381-B
TI Removing carbon dioxide in the industrial gas            comprises adopting pressure swing adsorption device,            where the pressure swing adsorption device comprises at            least four adsorption tower.
AU ZHANG Z
   LI S
AE UNIV BEIJING PEKING PIONEER TECHNOLOGY (UYBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV BEIJING PEKING PIONEER TECHNOLOGY (UYBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019049624
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide in the industrial gas                comprises adopting pressure swing adsorption                device, where the pressure swing adsorption device                comprises at least four adsorption tower, passing                carbon dioxide-containing industrial gas through                the absorption tower at a certain pressure,                subjecting each adsorption tower to six basic steps                e.g. adsorption step, high pressure nitrogen                replacement step, re-pressurization step, low                pressure nitrogen flushing step, final charging                step and adsorbing step.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon                dioxide in the industrial gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, improves                the hydrogen recovery rate of the product and has                strong commonality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) removing carbon dioxide in an industrial                gas using pressure swing adsorption device;                and
   (2) use of pressure swing adsorption device                for removing carbon dioxide from industrial                gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pressure swing                  adsorption device further comprises a plurality                  of control valves for controlling the air flow,                  setting the timing step of multi-process by                  controlling the switch of the valve, alternately                  using multi-tower to realize continuous                  decarburization of the industrial gas containing                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: The high                  pressure nitrogen replacement step comprises                  introducing high pressure nitrogen having the                  same pressure of industrial gas from the inlet                  end of the adsorption tower, pushing out the gas                  in the dead space and desorbed from the adsorbent                  gas by entry of high pressure nitrogen along the                  air inlet direction, the low pressure nitrogen                  flushing step comprises introducing low-pressure                  nitrogen gas from product end after equalization                  to obtain deeper desorption of the adsorbent                  layer, washing absorption bed layer, discharging                  flushing gas to the air inlet direction, where                  the amount of flushing nitrogen is 2-15% molar                  ratio of the intake air amount, preferably 4% to                  8% molar ratio of the amount of intake air, the                  pressure is 0.05-0.5 MPa, preferably 0.05-0.2 MPa                  gauge pressure. The high pressure nitrogen                  replacement step further comprises directly                  introducing replacement tail gas in another                  absorption tower finalized for pre-adsorption, or                  introducing in the replacement tail gas buffer                  tank to recover the effective gas component in                  the adsorption tower, where the amount of                  nitrogen substitution is 20-50% molar ratio of                  the intake air amount when the displacement tail                  gas is pre-adsorbed into another adsorption tower                  that has been finished charging, when the                  replacement exhaust gas is in the replacement                  exhaust gas buffer tank, the amount of                  replacement nitrogen gas is 5-25% molar ratio of                  the intake air amount, where the pressure of the                  replacement exhaust gas buffer tank is 20-60% of                  the adsorption pressure and the volume of                  replacement exhaust gas buffer tank is 1-8 times                  of the adsorption tower. The adsorption step                  comprises selecting at least one of adsorption                  tower for feeding industrial gas according to                  different amounts of gas processed, introducing                  industrial gas from the inlet end of the                  adsorption tower, adsorbing carbon dioxide gas as                  impurity adsorbent, taking the remaining gas as                  product gas led out from the upper part of the                  tower, where the adsorption pressure is 0.3-4.5                  MPa, preferably 0.5-3 MPa guage pressure, the                  adsorbent in the adsorption tower is molecular                  sieve, activated carbon, silica gel and other                  selective adsorbent of carbon dioxide, the                  equalization step comprises discharging gas in                  the tower to the outside of tower against the                  intake direction until the pressure in the tower                  drops to atmospheric pressure, partially                  desorbing the absorbent, the final charging step                  comprises flushing low pressure nitrogen, taking                  product gas from the product end gas, taking                  replacement tail gas from the air inlet end of                  air inlet, finally pressurizing adsorption tower                  and the pressure in the adsorption tower reaches                  the absorption working pressure, the                  pre-adsorption step comprises introducing the                  replacement tail gas from the adsorption tower in                  the high pressure nitrogen displacement step into                  the tower from the inlet end to the end of the                  final charging for pre-adsorption, or                  pressurizing the gas in the replacement exhaust                  gas buffer tank by the booster pump to reach the                  adsorption pressure and introducing into the                  tower from the end of the final charging to                  perform pre-adsorption, proceeding column to the                  adsorption step of next cycle. The pressure swing                  adsorption cycle comprises a high pressure                  nitrogen replacement and low pressure nitrogen                  flushing step, realizing increasing hydrogen                  recovery rate and canceling the evacuation step,                  where the recovery rate of hydrogen is more than                  99.5%.

TI Cathode useful for carbon dioxide electrolysis, and converting biogas containing carbon dioxide and methane into syngas, comprises molybdenum, tungsten, ruthenium, rhenium, iridium and osmium.
AU YANG J I
   PARK J C
   JUNG H
   YUN H S
   HOU X
   YANG J
   HA S
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2018426518
AB    NOVELTY - Cathode comprises molybdenum, tungsten, ruthenium, rhenium, iridium and osmium.
   USE - The cathode is useful for carbon dioxide electrolysis (claimed), and converting biogas containing carbon dioxide and methane into syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode has high performance and high activity for long period of time at high temperature, and low sheet resistance, and performs oxidation even at 750 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrolyzing carbon dioxide using cathode;
   (2) producing cathode, comprising (a) preparing molybdenum trioxide, (b) reducing the molybdenum trioxide to molybdenum dioxide through hydrothermal reduction reaction, (c) depositing the molybdenum dioxide in suspension state and then layering it on the electrolyte, and (d) further applying mixture of molybdenum dioxide particles and binder to the applied molybdenum dioxide layer;
   (3) carbon dioxide electrolysis cathode produced by the above method; and
   (4) strontium-doped lanthanum manganite and gadolinium-doped cerium oxide anode having pore shape.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molybdenum is molybdenum dioxide. The tungsten is tungsten dioxide. The ruthenium is ruthenium dioxide. The rhenium is rhenium dioxide. The rhenium is rhenium dioxide. The osmium is osmium dioxide. Preferred Method: The electrolysis is carried out in presence of carbon dioxide and water. In the step (b) and (c) molybdenum is in state of being suspended in mixture of water and ethylene glycol. The method further comprises (e) supplying molybdenum between the steps (b) and (c) at 450 degrees C for 2 hours to enhance the crystallinity of molybdenum dioxide. The step (c) is carried by electrostatic spray deposition using electrolyte containing yttrium-stabilized zirconia at substrate temperature of 300 degrees C with molybdenum dioxide suspension fluid flow rate of 2 ml/hour, and distance between the nozzle and the substrate of 6 cm under load voltage of 10 kV for 2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder is polyvinyl alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112711002-A
TI Estimating point source carbon dioxide emissions based on simulation measurement involves obtaining simulation observation data of carbon dioxide emissions from the chimney, and setting the initial conditions of the observation.
AU QIU R
   HAN G
   SHI T
   PEI Z
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202151555J
AB    NOVELTY - Estimating point source carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions based on CO2 DIAL simulation measurement involves obtaining CO2-DIAL simulation observation data of CO2 emissions from the chimney, and setting the initial conditions of the observation, establishing a coordinate system, and calculating the position of each CO2-DIAL measuring point of CO2 concentration. The observation direction of CO2 DIA is selected as the estimation direction, and used the CO2 concentration at each measurement point in CO2 DIAL as the estimation point, and used linearized Gaussian diffusion model to establish linearized equations. An observation direction is selected as the verification direction, and obtained the CO2 concentration in this direction as the verification point. The CO2 concentration calculation value of the verification point and the estimation point are compared, and the CO2 emission amount of the chimney is obtained when the error is the smallest.
   USE - Method for estimating a point source carbon dioxide emission based on carbon dioxide-DIAL simulation measurement.
   ADVANTAGE - The inversion method is combined with the carbon dioxide differential absorption laser radar based on the ground. The method can high precision the intensity of the discharge of the point source carbon dioxide. The method provides important supplementary unit for monitoring and verifying the human carbon dioxide emission.
DC T01 (Digital Computers); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems)
MC T01-J13; W06-A06C; W06-A06H2; W06-A06H9

TI Producing reconstituted tobacco leaflets, comprises mixing and stirring stock and slaked lime, injecting carbon dioxide into mixed and stirred stock and slaked lime, and producing fiber-bonded calcium carbonate stock.
AU AHN J
   KIM C S
   NAM S Y
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
   HANIL CEMENT MFG CO LTD (HANI-Non-standard)
GA 2020690064
AB    NOVELTY - Producing reconstituted tobacco leaflets, comprises (a) preparing stock, (b) preparing slaked lime, (c) mixing and stirring the stock and the slaked lime, (d) injecting carbon dioxide into the mixed and stirred stock and the slaked lime, and (e) producing the fiber-bonded calcium carbonate stock by introducing carbon dioxide. The stock of step (a) has passed through a refiner. In the step (c), the stock and slaked lime are mixed at a weight ratio of 1:15-1:20. In step (d), calcium carbonate is obtained by carbon dioxide capture, input carbon dioxide is 800 l/minute under 2 Mpa and 100% gas, and the reaction is carried out for 1 hour 40 minutes to 2 hours after the injection of carbon dioxide. The step (c) is performed in a leaflet manufacturing facility or adjacent to a leaflet manufacturing facility. In step (d), the introduced carbon dioxide reacts with the slaked lime. In the step (e), carbon dioxide reacts with the slaked lime to form the fiber-bonded calcium carbonate stock.
   USE - The method is useful for producing reconstituted tobacco leaflets.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the amount of calcium carbonate required for the production of leaflets, and is environmentally friendly.

TI Utilizing carbon dioxide catalytic reforming to            prepare synthesis gas comprises passing raw material            gas containing toluene and carbon dioxide gas into            low-temperature plasma reactor and carrying out            catalytic reforming reaction in presence of catalyst to            prepare synthesis gas.
AU YU H
   ZHANG Z
   DAI J
   LIU L
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 202294931S
AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing carbon dioxide catalytic reforming                to prepare synthesis gas comprises passing raw                material gas containing toluene and carbon dioxide                gas into a low-temperature plasma reactor equipped                with a catalyst, and carrying out a catalytic                reforming reaction in the presence of a catalyst to                prepare synthesis gas, where catalyst includes                active components and a carrier, the active                components are nickel and iron, and the carrier is                an aluminum-magnesium mixed oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for utilizing carbon                dioxide catalytic reforming to prepare synthesis                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The improves the conversion rate of toluene                feed gas and carbon dioxide gas and the selectivity                of product hydrogen and carbon monoxide through the                synergistic effect of the catalyst and the low                temperature plasma reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalytic reforming                  reaction temperature is 100-250&#8451;, the                  reaction pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa, and the                  residence time of the toluene-containing raw                  material gas and carbon dioxide gas in the                  dielectric barrier discharge region is 2-30                  seconds. The power of the low temperature plasma                  reactor is 10-100 W, and the discharge frequency                  is 1-10 kHz. The flow rate of the feed gas                  containing toluene is 1-3 ml/minutes, and the                  flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 5-30                  ml/minutes and includes the step of feeding                  protective gaseous gas into the low temperature                  plasma reactor, while feeding the raw material                  gas containing toluene and carbon dioxide gas                  into the low temperature plasma reactor, the raw                  material gas containing toluene, carbon dioxide                  gas and protective gas and the total inlet flow                  rate of the gas is 100 ml/minutes and the volume                  space velocity of the mixed raw material gas                  after the toluene-containing raw material gas,                  and the carbon dioxide gas and the protective gas                  are mixed is 1000-14000/hours. The preparation                  method of the catalyst comprises (a) dissolving                  metal nickel salt, metal iron salt, metal                  magnesium salt and metal aluminum salt in water,                  stirring to obtain a mixed solution; and (ii)                  adding mixed solution, the precipitant solution                  and the alkaline solution dropwise to the                  reaction device at the same time, stirring                  reaction during the dropping process, aging,                  washing the obtained precipitate to neutrality,                  and drying precipitate, calcining and reducing to                  obtain the catalyst. In the step (ii),the                  stirring reaction temperature is 55-65&#8451;                  and the stirring speed is 600-850                  revolutions/minute, the aging temperature is                  55-65&#8451;, and the aging time is 15-20                  hours, the drying temperature is                  100-110&#8451;, and the drying time is 20-30                  hours, the calcination step is carried out in an                  air atmosphere, the calcination temperature is                  550-650&#8451;, and the calcination time is                  5-7 hours and the reduction step is carried out                  in a reducing atmosphere, the reduction                  temperature is 750-850&#176; C, the reduction                  heating rate is 5-15&#176; C/minutes, and the                  reduction time is 0.5-1.5 hours.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  active components in the catalyst is 21-25wt%,                  the molar ratio of nickel and iron is                  (0.96-2.2):(0.4-1.38), preferably, the molar                  ratio of nickel and iron is 4:1 and the mass                  ratio of magnesium and aluminum in the carrier is                  (1.632-2.208): (0.731-0.989). In the step (ii),                  the molar ratio of the metal nickel salt, metal                  iron salt, metal magnesium salt and metal                  aluminum salt is (0.96-2.2): (0.4-1.38):                  (6.8-9.2): (2.7-3.7), the concentration of metal                  nickel salt in the mixed solution is 0.06-0.12                  mol/l, preferably, the metal nickel salt is                  nickel nitrate hexa hydrate, and the metal iron                  salt is iron nitrate nano hydrate, the metal                  magnesium salt is magnesium hydrate hexa hydrate,                  the metal aluminum salt is aluminium nitrate nano                  hydrate, the volume ratio of the mixed solution                  and the precipitant solution described in step 2)                  is (1-2):(1-5), preferably, the precipitant                  solution is a sodium carbonate solution, the                  concentration of sodium carbonate solution is                  0.8-1.2mol/l, the alkali solution is a sodium                  hydroxide solution, the concentration of the                  sodium hydroxide solution is 1.5-2.5 mol/l, and                  the pH value of the reaction solution is                  controlled by the sodium hydroxide solution to be                  9.5-10.5. The raw material gas containing toluene                  is toluene gas and/or tar gas after tar                  gasification, and the synthesis gas includes                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen and the protective                  gas is nitrogen, helium, neon and/or argon. The                  low temperature plasma reactor is a dielectric                  barrier discharge plasma reactor.

PN CN110344071-A; CN110344071-B
TI Electrically reducing carbon dioxide device comprises electrochemical reaction unit comprising cathode chamber, anode chamber, membrane electrode, cathode feeding unit is connected to the feed port of the cathode chamber.
AU WANG X
   TAO Y
   ZHANG H
   KANG P
AE CARBON ENERGY TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201989123R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrically reducing carbon dioxide device comprises electrochemical reaction unit comprising a cathode chamber, an anode chamber, and a membrane electrode. The membrane of the membrane electrode is an anion exchange membrane. A cathode feeding unit is connected to the feed port of the cathode chamber and the humidified carbon dioxide -containing gas is input to the cathode chamber.
   USE - Used as electrically reducing carbon dioxide device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has simple technology; is environmentally friendly; has low equipment requirement, and low manufacturing cost; is suitable for industrial syngas production; and achieves excellent economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrically reducing carbon dioxide, comprising introducing pure water or 0.001-2 mol/l alkali metal carbonate or bicarbonate solution into the anode chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the electrically reducing carbon dioxide device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The humidified carbon dioxide-containing gas has relative humidity of 20-90%. The device further comprises anode feed unit coupled to the feed port of the anode chamber, to which pure water or 0.001-2 mol/l alkali metal carbonate or bicarbonate solution is supplied. The volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing gas is 10-100%. Preferred Method: The method further comprises purging the anode chamber with gas that does not react in the electrochemical reaction unit. The purged gas is carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen and/or argon. The method further comprises transporting the carbon dioxide-containing gas to the cathode chamber of the electrochemical reaction unit, where the electrochemical reaction unit includes the cathode chamber, an anode chamber, and a membrane electrode, membrane of the membrane electrode is cation exchange membrane, delivering pure water to the anode chamber and performing electrochemical reaction. The method further comprises separating the reaction product of the cathode chamber, and returning the unreacted carbon dioxide gas to the cathode chamber for recycling. The reaction temperature in the electrochemical reaction unit is 10-90 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.01-10 MPa, and the reaction tank pressure is 2-4 V.

TI Absorption of carbon dioxide gas in e.g. ammonia synthesis, involves adding feed solution containing methyldiethanolamine to feed tank, adding glycine catalyst, and setting flow ratio of carbon dioxide mixed gas and air to preset value.
AU SUSIANTO
   ARIANI
   SUPRAPTO
   ALTWAY A
AE POLYTECHNIC NEGERI MALANG (POLY-Non-standard)
GA 201981350S
AB    NOVELTY - Absorption of carbon dioxide gas involves preparing an absorber equipment, pumps, compressors and temperature control equipment, adding 25 L feed solution containing 30 vol.% methyldiethanolamine solvent to a feed tank, adding 3 vol.% glycine catalyst, setting flow ratio of carbon dioxide mixed gas and inlet air to 5:1, adjusting the solvent flow rate to 0.2 L/minute, maintaining for 10 minutes to attain stable state, operating at variable temperatures of 30 degrees C, 40 degrees C and 50 degrees C at feed flow ratio of 0.2-0.6 L/minute and glycine catalyst ratio of 2-5%.
   USE - Absorption of carbon dioxide gas in synthesis of ammonia, fertilizer industry and liquefied natural gas refinery.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide gas can be absorbed with improved absorption capacity.

PN DE102017006067-A1
TI Blast furnace process comprises supplying top gas            as reducing gas by recycling the carbon dioxide-related            top gases of oxygen blast furnace after previous carbon            dioxide separating at ambient temperature and injecting            pulverized coal.
AU AASLEPP H
AE AASLEPP H (AASL-Individual)
GA 2018A3936K
AB    NOVELTY - Blast furnace process comprises supplying top                gas as reducing gas by recycling the carbon                dioxide-related top gases of the oxygen blast                furnace after previous carbon dioxide separating at                ambient temperature in the blow mold plane through                the central channel of the blowing device and                heating to 900&#176; C by shaft nozzles,                separating from the recirculated reducing gas,                oxygen, methane and injecting pulverized coal                through the outer channel of the device and                burning. An inert separation jet is created between                them by oxidation of methane with oxygen to water                and carbon dioxide to separate the core jet with                the recycling gas from the outer combustion                jet.
   USE - The method is useful for oxygen blast furnace                for top gas recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces high levels of carbon                dioxide emissions.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022183248-A1
TI Composition useful for increasing plant's natural            processes for absorbing carbon dioxide from atmosphere,            increasing production of glucose in plants and trees,            and releasing carbohydrates into soil, comprises            bioflavonoid, and organic acid.
AU JOYCE K J
AE AUSSAN LAB PTY LTD (AUSS-Non-standard)
GA 2022B6077R
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises at least one                bioflavonoid, and at least one organic acid.
   USE - The composition is useful in form of liquid                for increasing plant's natural processes for                absorbing carbon dioxide from atmosphere,                increasing production of glucose in plants and                trees, and releasing carbohydrates into the soil                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition enables to simultaneously                increase plant's natural processes for absorbing                carbon dioxide from atmosphere and releasing                carbohydrates into the soil, and reduces impact of                global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for simultaneously increasing a plant's natural                processes for absorbing carbon dioxide from                atmosphere and releasing carbohydrates into the                soil, which involves forming a liquid composition                comprising at least one bioflavonoid and at least                one organic acid, diluting the liquid composition                by adding a diluting agent at a rate of 0.25-20                ml/l liquid composition, and spraying a first                application of the diluted liquid composition to                leaves of a plant early in the growing season of                the plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises at least one wetting agent. The                  composition comprises at least one nutrient. The                  composition comprises 0.25-20 ml in a topical                  composition with respect to 1 l of water or other                  diluting agent. The topical composition is                  suitable for application to roots and leaves of                  plant by foliar application or as an irrigation                  additive. The composition comprises 10-20 wt.%                  bioflavonoid, 30-60 wt.% organic acid, and/or                  10-20 wt.% wetting agent. The composition                  comprises 0.5-5 wt.% micronutrients of one or                  more boron, magnesium and calcium, where the                  composition is applied through plant foliar or                  irrigation treatments with at least one of plant                  growth regulators or plant nutrients. The liquid                  composition further comprises one or more of                  boron, magnesium and calcium at a size particle                  (&#956; m) capable of plant absorption.                  Preferred Method: The method further involves                  spraying a second application of the diluted                  liquid composition to the leaves of a plant at                  least 3-7 days after first application. The                  method involves adding diluting agent at a rate                  of 0.4-4 ml/l liquid composition. The method                  involves spraying first application and second                  application on the leaves of the plant in a light                  mist or fine spray to cover the leaves. Preferred                  Component: The wetting agent is naturally plant                  derived surfactant. The bioflavonoid is naturally                  plant derived. The organic acid is naturally                  produced. The bioflavonoid is chosen from                  Citrusfruit plant extracts, including grapefruit,                  lemon, lime, orange, tangerine, and bitter orange                  extract. The diluting agent is water. The organic                  acid is chosen from mild organic acids such as                  malic acid, citric acid, lactic acid, salicylic                  acid, caprylic acid and tartaric acid.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of evaluation of                simultaneous increase in plant's natural processes                for absorbing carbon dioxide from atmosphere and                releasing carbohydrates into the soil that do not                support the claims.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN114733509-A
TI Method for improving oxygen vacancy concentration            on surface of cerium oxide catalyst used for catalyzing            low-carbon alkane and carbon dioxide, involves reducing            cerium oxide using hydrogen-hydrogen sulfide gas            mixture.
AU CHEN D
   ZHANG L
   MEI Y
   LUO Y
   YAN J
   HE D
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202296035B
AB    NOVELTY - A method for improving oxygen vacancy                concentration on surface of cerium oxide catalyst                involves reducing cerium oxide using                hydrogen-hydrogen sulfide gas mixture at                500-800&#176;C to obtain cerium oxide catalyst                with high oxygen vacancy concentration.
   USE - Method for improving oxygen vacancy                concentration on surface of cerium oxide catalyst                used for catalyzing low-carbon alkane and carbon                dioxide to prepare synthesis gas (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, efficient, stable, safe,                provides cerium oxide catalyst with more oxygen                vacancies for activating reactant molecules to                promote the forward reaction, and improves the                conversion rate of low-carbon alkanes to carbon                dioxide. The hydrogen sulfide in the reaction feed                does not cause the catalyst to be deactivated by                sulfur poisoning. This shows that the cerium oxide                catalyst co-treated with hydrogen sulfide and                hydrogen has excellent sulfur resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of cerium oxide catalyst for catalyzing                low-carbon alkane and carbon dioxide to prepare                synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen-hydrogen                  sulfide mixed gas uses nitrogen as carrier                  gas.Preferred Composition: In the mixed gas, the                  concentration of hydrogen is 5-90 vol.% and the                  hydrogen sulfide concentration is 100-5000                  ppm.Preferred Condition: In application method,                  reaction is carried out at 550-800&#8451; at                  reaction space velocity of 1000-10000                  mL/g.hour.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN114713225-A
TI Preparing titanium dioxide nanosheet photocatalyst            modified by copper cluster with oxygen vacancy            comprises using lithium chloride and potassium chloride            as fluxing agent, adding copper chloride at the same            time, and melting the titanium dioxide nanosheet.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG Q
   CHENG H
   WANG P
   LIU Y
   ZHENG Z
   HUANG B
   ZHANG M
   WANG Z
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2022923682
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium dioxide nanosheet                photocatalyst modified by copper cluster with                oxygen vacancy comprises using lithium chloride and                potassium chloride as fluxing agent, adding copper                chloride at the same time, and melting the titanium                dioxide nanosheet in an inert atmosphere to obtain                a titanium dioxide catalyst modified by copper                cluster with oxygen vacancies through the molten                salt.
   USE - The titanium dioxide nanosheet photocatalyst                modified by copper cluster with oxygen vacancy is                useful in catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction to                produce carbon monoixide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The titanium dioxide nanosheets are promoted                to exhibit more excellent photocatalytic activity                and cycling stability during the carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) titanium dioxide nanosheet photocatalyst                modified by copper cluster with oxygen vacancy                prepared by above-mentioned method; and
   (2) use method of the titanium dioxide                nanosheet photocatalyst, comprising catalyzing                carbon dioxide reduction under saturated carbon                dioxide and full light conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the titanium dioxide nanosheet is as follows: in                  a hydrofluoric acid solution, tetrabutyl titanate                  is used as a titanium source, and the titanium                  dioxide nanosheet is prepared by a hydrothermal                  method. The temperature of the molten salt                  reaction is 450-550&#8451;, preferably                  470-520&#8451;, the reaction time of the                  molten salt is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 1.7-2.2                  hours. The heating speed of the molten salt                  reaction is 5-10&#8451;/minute.Preferred Component: In the fluxing agent,                  the mol ratio of lithium chloride and potassium                  chloride is 1:1-1.5:1. The mol ratio of titanium                  dioxide, potassium chloride, lithium chloride and                  copper (II) chloride is 0.4:1:1:0.004.

TI Preparing titanium-zirconium bimetallic oxide catalyst useful for strengthening carbon dioxide desorption comprises dissolving soluble metal titanium salt and urea in water, mixing solution A, B and C, heating and reacting, cooling and filtering, washing precipitate with water and grinding.
AU PAN B
   JI L
   LI J
   HUA M
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 202242162B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium-zirconium bimetallic oxide catalyst comprises (i) dissolving soluble metal titanium salt and urea in water to obtain mixed solution A; (ii) dissolving soluble metal zirconium salt and urea are dissolved in water to obtain mixed solution B; (iii) mixing solution A, B and C, heating and reacting, cooling and filtering to obtain precipitate D; and (iv) washing precipitate D with water, drying, calcining and grinding.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for strengthening carbon dioxide desorption and in the process of catalytic desorption of carbon dioxide-rich amine solvent, preferably ethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, N-methylmonoethanolamine, and/or 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (claimed)
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst ensures regeneration temperature is reduced to less than 100 degrees Celsius; and can effectively improve the total carbondioxide desorption amount.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of catalyst in the process of catalytic desorption of carbon dioxide-rich amine solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the titanium-zirconium double metal oxide catalyst, the molar ratio of titanium and zirconium is (1-9):3. The specific surface area of the titanium-zirconium double metal oxide catalyst is 70-200 m2.g-1 and/or the hydroxy content of the titanium zirconium double metal oxide catalyst is 4.3-6.2x10 20 OH/g. The molar ratio of the soluble metal titanium salt or the soluble metal zirconium salt and urea is 1:5. In the step (i), the soluble metal titanium salt is titanium sulfate, titanium chloride, titanium nitrate, and titanyl sulfate and/or the soluble metal zirconium salt is zirconium sulfate, zirconium chloride, zirconium nitrate or oxychloride type of zirconium. The amount of the titanium-zirconium double metal oxide catalyst is 0.5-1.5 wt.%, and the desorption temperature is 80-99degrees Celsius.Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the heating reaction temperature is 150-180degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 18-20 hours. In the step (iv), the drying time is not less than 24 hours, the calcination temperature is 300-600degrees Celsius, and the calcination time is not less than 4 hours.

PN CN113244923-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide methanation reaction            catalyst by coal or biomass comprises e.g. drying and            pulverizing coal or biomass, uniformly mixing, drying,            pyrolyzing, activating and molding.
AU ZHANG J
   YANG W
   ZHOU Y
   YANG E
   LI R
   MA X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 202196857M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide methanation reaction                catalyst by coal or biomass comprises (i) drying                and pulverizing coal or biomass raw materials into                raw material particles, (ii) adding raw material                particles and metal salt reagent pretreated in step                (i) to organic matter aqueous solution and                uniformly mixing, drying, pyrolyzing at 400-700                degrees C for 30-240 minutes under protection of                pyrolysis atmosphere to produce pyrolysis gas, coal                tar or biomass oil, and metal-loaded pyrolysis                semi-coke crude products, (iii) adding metal-loaded                pyrolysis semi-coke crude product in step (ii) to                activation solution for stirring, activating for                30-240 minutes, drying and molding to obtain carbon                dioxide methanation reaction catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide methanation reaction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes pyrolysis of coal or                biomass to synthesize precursor of catalyst in situ                (i.e. crude product of metal-loaded pyrolysis                semi-coke), which realizes coupling of three                operating units of coal or biomass pyrolysis,                catalyst preparation and CO2 methanation reaction;                is simple, economical, and suitable for industrial                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of                  raw material particles is 50-300 mu m. The                  biomass raw material is peanut shells or corn                  stalks. The metal salt reagent includes nickel                  nitrate, iron nitrate, cobalt nitrate and/or                  cerium nitrate. The dosage of raw material                  particles and metal salt reagent is calculated                  based on mass ratio of raw material particles and                  metal element in metal salt reagent (1-50):1. The                  preparation of organic-aqueous solution comprises                  mixing methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, acetone,                  cyclohexanone, ether, and/or propylene oxide with                  water. The pyrolysis atmosphere includes                  hydrogen, synthesis gas, methane, carbon dioxide,                  nitrogen and/or argon. The activation solution is                  acid potassium permanganate solution, perchloric                  acid solution, concentrated sulfuric acid                  solution or hydrogen peroxide solution. The feed                  ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is (3-4):1,                  the reaction pressure is normal pressure or                  slightly positive pressure, the reaction                  temperature is 300-550 degrees C, the reaction                  space velocity is 3-15 l/(g(cat).h), and the                  constant temperature reaction time is 1-10                  hours.

TI Large heat input gas electric vertical welding for            thick plates involves using single wire under            protection of carbon dioxide gas and DC reverse            polarity to weld from bottom to top along height            direction of erected thick plate.
AU QU C
   XIA L
AE BAOSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2021230866
AB    NOVELTY - Method of a large heat input gas electric                vertical welding for thick plates involves using a                single wire under the protection of 100% carbon                dioxide gas and DC reverse polarity to weld from                bottom to top along the height direction of the                erected thick plate, where the welding groove                adopts a V-shaped groove, and the gap between the                groove in the height direction is gradually                increases from bottom to top, the carbon dioxide                gas flow rate at the welding starting point is                20-30 l/minute, the carbon dioxide gas flow rate at                the subsequent welding position is 25-35 l/minute,                and the welding current at the starting welding                point is less than the welding current subsequent                welding location. The welding wire is not swung at                the welding position.
   USE - The method is useful for a large heat input                gas electric vertical welding for thick                plates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method, the                  chemical composition of deposited metal of the                  controlled weld comprises less than or equal to                  0.06% carbon, 1.3-1.9% manganese, less than or                  equal to 0.4% silicon, 0.1-0.3% molybdenum,                  0.6-2% nickel, less than or equal to 0.015%                  sulfur, less than or equal to 0.015% phosphorus,                  0.02-0.06% titanium, less than or equal to 0.006%                  boron, and iron and other unavoidable impurities                  (remaining quantity).

   USE - Tube bundle reactor for converting carbon dioxide to methanol.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for operating tube bundle reactor, which involves feeding the gaseous starting material into the tubes, operating the reactor, closing the individual pipes or groups of pipes when the amount of educt is reduced, and opening the tubes when the amount of educt is increased.

PN CN111662422-A; CN111662422-B
TI New metal complex covalent organic framework material is used in the preparation of cyclocarbonate by the reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds.
AU DENG W
   ZHOU W
   REN G
   SUN L
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2020921769
AB    NOVELTY - Metal complex covalent organic framework material (I) is new.
   USE - Metal complex covalent organic framework material used in the preparation of cyclocarbonate by the reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal complex covalent organic framework material (I) is new.
   R1=-OAc, -Cl, -ClO4 or -Et2;
   R2=-H, -tBu, or -OMe;
   M=Zn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Cr, Mn or V; and
   R3=-CH2-CH2-, -Ph- (-Ph- is a phenyl group) or -Ch- (-Ch- is a cyclohexyl group).
   . INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the folloiwng:
   (1) a method for preparing metal-based covalent organic framework material of formula (I); and
   (2) a method for using metal-based covalent organic framework material (M-COF) of formula (I) in the preparation of cyclocarbonate by the reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds, which involves mixing M-COF, epoxy compound and tetrabutylammonium bromide in proportion; and introducing carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the mixture, and after the reaction is completed, obtaining high-purity cyclic carbonate through purification and separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The method for preparing the metal complexed covalent organic framework material of formula (I), which involves taking 5,5'-((5'-(4-((3-(tert-butyl)-5-formyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)ethynyl)phenyl)-(1,1': 3' , 1''-triphenyl)-4,4''-diyl)bis(acetylene-2,1-diyl))bis(3-(tert-butyl)-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde) and amine compounds as organic ligands. Thr tetrahydrofuran and absolute ethanol are used as reaction solvents, and added acetic acid solution to react to obtain a crystalline covalent organic framework material. The covalent organic framework material, the metal salt compound and the monobasic fatty alcohol are mixed and reacted to prepare the metal-based covalent organic framework material (M-COF) of formula (I). Preferred Conditions: The ratio of 5 ,5'-((5'-(4-((3-(tert-butyl)-5-formyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)ethynyl)phenyl)-(1,1': 3', 1''-triphenyl)-4,4''-diyl)bis(acetylene-2,1-diyl))bis(3-(tert-butyl)-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde) and amine compounds is 1:1-3. The volume ratio of tetrahydrofuran, absolute ethanol and acetic acid solution is 1-16:4:1, and the concentration of the acetic acid solution is 0.1-10 mol.L-1. The reaction temperature for preparing the metal-based covalent organic framework material is 20-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 12-72 hours. The molar ratio of M-COF to epoxy compound is 1:1-25, and the molar ratio of tetrabutylammonium bromide to epoxy compound is 1:5-1000. Preferred Components: The amine compound is ethylene diamine, cyclohexane diamine or phenylene diamine. The metal salt compound is acetate, chloride, perchlorate, sulfate, nitrate or diethyl salt; in some embodiments, the monohydric fatty alcohol is anhydrous methanol, anhydrous ethanol, anhydrous propanol or anhydrous butanol. The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, 1,2-epoxyhexane, phenyl ethylene oxide, 1,2-epoxy Dioxane, 1,2,7,8-diepoxyoctane, 1,2-epoxy-2-methylpropane, trans-2,3-epoxybutane, trans-1,2-distyrene oxide, 1-allyloxy-2,3-epoxypropane, 1,2-epoxy-3-phenoxypropane or 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide.

The coppersalt comprises copper nitrate, copper acetate orcopper oxalate. The titanium salt comprises tetrabutyl titanate. The molybdenum comprises ammonium molybdate. Preferred Compositions: The metal salt includes a phosphonium salt, a titanium salt, a copper salt, and a molybdenum salt, where molar ratio of the metal ions of the onium salt, the titanium salt, the copper salt, and the molybdenum salt is 0.95-1:0.8-0.5:0.1-0.4:0.1. Preferred Process: The method further involves adding a metal salt and citric acid to water and heating and stirring to a gel state in a water bath at 60-90 degrees C, drying at 150-250 degrees C to obtain a precursor, and then pre-curing the precursor in an air atmosphere 900 STCM cathode material is obtained by calcination at 1200 degrees C for 5 hours. The molar ratio of the citric acid to the metal ion in the metal salt is 1.5-2:1.

Preferred Method: In the step (i), pretreatment is carried out by pre-treating an alkali solution, water washing, dissolving acid, precipitating, filtering and calcining or pre-treating acid solution, precipitating, drying and calcining.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heater is an                  electric heating wire heater. The display module                  is an LED digital tube display screen. The                  display module comprises 3-8 word digital tubes.                  The temperature and humidity sensor is STH11. The                  carbon dioxide sensor is MH-410D. The single chip                  is STC89C52 chip. Preferred Method: The method                  comprises (i) calculating control parameters A, B                  , C, A, B and C represent temperature, humidity                  and carbon dioxide concentration respectively,                  (2) set initial value e(n-1)=e(n-2)=0, (3) sample                  input y(n), (4) calculating e(n) and Delta u(n),                  (5) output u(n)=u(n-1)+ Delta u(n), reset                  e(n-2)=e(n-1) , e(n-1)=e(n), (6) when the                  sampling time is reached, and proceed to the next                  sampling. The adjustment comprises sampling                  information data, the single-chip computer                  queries the corresponding fuzzy control table                  according to the system error and the deviation                  change rate, and obtains KP, KI, Kd, the setting                  value of each parameter is PID operation,                  incremental PID is only related to the nearest                  n-2, n-1, n error. The MCU only needs to save                  three errors, the incremental PID formula is:                  u(n)=Kp(1+T/Ti+                  Td/T)en-Kp(1+2Td/T)en-1+Kp(Td/T)en-2+u(n-1),                  where u(n), u(n-1) are respectively sampled                  output value at time n, n-1. The output value at                  n-1, Kp, Ti, Td are the proportional, integral                  and differential coefficients respectively, en,                  en-1, and en-2 are the deviation values at the                  sampling instants n, n-1, n-2, respectively is                  the sampling period, A = Kp (1+T/Ti + Td/T), B =                  Kp (1+2Td/T), C = Kp (Td/T), A, B and C represent                  temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide                  concentration.

   LIAONING SHENGSHI RESOURCES ENVIRONMENTA (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8859H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light burned magnesium oxide and enriching carbon dioxide by self-circulating pyrolysis of flue gas involves utilizing carbon dioxide (CO2) generated in the calcination process to self-circulate pyrolyzing magnesite to produce light burnt magnesia, and collecting remaining portion of CO2.
   USE - Method for preparing light burned magnesium oxide and enriching carbon dioxide by self-circulating pyrolysis of flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the emission of CO2 gas, and makes the entire production process of light burned magnesia more energy-saving and environment friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The light burned magnesium oxide and enriching carbon dioxide is prepared by self-circulating pyrolysis of flue gas by finely grinding the magnesite ore to obtain magnesite ore powder, obtaining ore powder that meets the calcination demand through flotation, drying magnesite ore powder to dry the water brought in during the flotation process, and passing ore powder into the preheating device for preheating. The preheated ore powder is sent to a calciner for calcination to obtain a gas-solid mixture of lightly burned magnesia and CO2. The gas-solid mixture obtained by calcination is passed into the separation device for gas-solid separation, and the obtained light burnt magnesia product enters the silo after cooling treatment. The CO2 flows back to the previous preheating device and drying device to provide heat for the preheating device and drying device and CO2 temperature is lowered. The temperature is raised to the temperature of calcining magnesite to produce light burnt magnesia. The CO2 gas is passed into the calcination furnace to provide heat for calcination. The calcination requirement is that the temperature reaches 700-1000 degrees C, and the flotation particle size ranges from 10-75 micrometer. The heat required for the drying and preheating process is provided by CO2 generated by the calcination and decomposition of magnesite. The high-temperature light-burned magnesia undergoes heat exchange with CO2 after providing heat for the preheating device and the drying device through a heat exchanger. The CO2 that provides heat to the drying device needs to be dehumidified by the dehumidification device. The remaining CO2 gas is enriched for resource utilization of carbon dioxide.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Fabricating electrode for carbon dioxide reduction involves immersing counter electrode, reference electrode, and porous conductor in electrodeposition electrolyte solution containing silver precursor, and pulse electrodeposition.
AU HAM Y
   KIM M J

   KIMDONGGWON
   KIM S G

AE UNIV CHUNG ANG IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYCU-C)
   UNIV FOUND SOONGSIL IND COOP (UNSO-C)
   UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 201969955N
AB    NOVELTY - Fabricating electrode for carbon dioxide reduction involves immersing counter electrode, reference electrode, and porous conductor in electrodeposition electrolyte solution containing silver precursor, and pulse electrodeposition, forming silver catalyst on porous conductor by applying pulse current while performing ultrasonic treatment on electrode for electrodeposition. The silver catalyst is provided on porous conductor.
   USE - Method for fabricating electrode for carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to fabricate electrode that has good economic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide reduction device, which comprises electrode for carbon dioxide reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous conductor comprises carbon paper, graphene, carbon nanotubes, and titanium. The electrode for electrodeposition further comprises amine additive. The silver catalyst is dendritic crystal. Preferred Conditions: The pulse current has pulse off time (Toff) of 0.5-4 seconds. The current density at pulse on time Ton of pulse current is 5-20 mA/cm2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI New bacterium naturally oxidizing hydrogen and            genetically modified to overexpress gene encoding            lactate dehydrogenase, useful for producing lactate            from carbon dioxide, preferably for producing            exclusively of L-lactate or D-lactate.
AU BOISART C
   CHABOT N
AE ENOBRAQ (ENOB-Non-standard)
   ENOBRAQ (ENOB-Non-standard)
   ENOBRAQ (ENOB-Non-standard)
GA 201948353F
AB    NOVELTY - Bacterium naturally oxidizing hydrogen and                genetically modified to produce lactate from carbon                dioxide, where the bacterium is genetically                modified to overexpress at least one gene encoding                a lactate dehydrogenase, is new.
   USE - The bacterium is useful for producing lactate                from carbon dioxide, preferably for producing                exclusively of L-lactate, or D-lactate                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing lactate from carbon dioxide, comprises                cultivating the bacterium in the presence of carbon                dioxide as the only carbon source, and recovering                lactate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The bacterium is                  genetically modified to overexpress: at least one                  gene encoding an endogenous lactate                  dehydrogenase; at least one gene coding for an                  exogenous lactate dehydrogenase, derived from a                  bacterium (preferred), a fungus, a yeast or a                  mammal; at least one gene encoding L-lactate                  dehydrogenase, where the expression of at least                  one gene encoding D-lactate dehydrogenase is at                  least partially inhibited; and at least one gene                  encoding a D-lactate dehydrogenase, where the                  expression of encoding L-lactate dehydrogenase is                  at least partially inhibited. The bacterium is                  genetically modified to at least partially                  inhibit at least one degradation pathway of the                  competing pyruvate of the lactate synthesis                  pathway. The synthetic route of                  polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB) is at least partially                  inhibited. The expression of at least one,                  preferably 3 genes comprising genes encoding                  acetyl-coenzyme-A (CoA) acetyltransferase,                  acetoacetyl-CoA reductase and                  poly(3-hydroxybutyrate) synthase is at least                  partially inhibited. The expression of at least                  one gene comprising genes encoding a                  phosphoenolpyruvate synthase and a pyruvate                  carboxylase is at least partially inhibited. The                  conversion route of acetyl-CoA to acetate and/or                  acetaldehyde is at least partially inhibited,                  preferably the expression of at least one gene                  comprising genes encoding a phosphate                  acetyltransferase, an acetate kinase and                  acetaldehyde dehydrogenase is at least partially                  inhibited. The expression of at least one gene                  encoding ferricytochrome-C oxidase lactate is at                  least partially inhibited.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN113461654-A
TI Use of bio-based metal-organic aerogel material in carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction, and is prepared by e.g. dissolving 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid and metal ligand in organic solvent, washing resulting gel-shaped product with solvent, and soaking product in tert-butyl alcohol.
AU LUO X
   WANG G
   SHAN S
   FANG Q
   ZHI Y
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021B8021E
AB    NOVELTY - Use of bio-based metal-organic aerogel material in carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction, where the material is prepared by (i) dissolving 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid and metal ligand in 14-20 ml organic solvent, transferring the reaction solution to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, reacting at 100-200degrees Celsius for 4-24 hours, cooling down to room temperature, washing the resulting gel-shaped product with a solvent for 2-3 times, soaking the product in tert-butyl alcohol, shaking for 30-40 hours, changing the tert-butyl alcohol for every 10-12 hours and freeze-drying to obtain the bio-based metal-organic aerogel material, and (ii) sequentially adding epoxy compound and the bio-based metal-organic aerogel material to the high pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide with a purity of 99.99% to replace the air in the reaction kettle, reacting for 4-36 hours at a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.5-2 Mpa and 50-100degrees Celsius, and cooling to obtain a cyclic carbonate.
   USE - The material is useful in carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has large specific surface area, low skeleton density and rich pores by the hot melt method; utilizes bio-based metal organic aerogel as a heterogeneous catalyst to catalyze the carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction to prepare cyclic carbonate; prepares catalyst which is simple, environmentally friendly, economical, and small in amount used in the catalyzed carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction; has high conversion rate, and mild reaction conditions; and is suitable for industrialized production and market promotion and application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ligand is chromium salt, iron salt, aluminum salt, zinc salt, and/or copper salt. The mass ratio of the bio-based metal-organic aerogel material and epoxy compound is 1: 100-1: 500.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid and metal ligand is 1:2~2:1. The organic solvent for reaction is N, N-dimethyl formamide, ethanol, and/or methanol. The washing solvent is tertiary butyl alcohol and/or water. The epoxy compound is epoxy chloropropane, propylene oxide, epoxy cyclohexane, 1,2-epoxy cyclopentane, and/or 1,2-dimethyl ethylene oxide.

GA 202104091J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor whose outlet is connected to an inlet of an oil separator through a pipeline. A gas outlet of the oil separator is connected to an inlet of a gas cooler through the pipeline. An outlet of the gas cooler is connected to an inlet of a liquid receiver through the pipeline. An outlet of a drier is connected to an inlet of a low temperature end of a heat regenerator through the pipeline. A pipeline is located at an inlet of an ice making evaporator.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide continuous ice making circulation control system for a carbon dioxide injector and a heat regenerator in a low-temperature refrigerating area.

The system reduces the refrigeration during the ice-removing period, shortens the ice-making time, and improves the unit time ice-making yield. The compressor inlet is the carbon dioxide gas from the gas phase outlet of the gas-liquid separator, so that the hot gas releases heat in the evaporator to liquefy. The collected liquid enters into the compressor, so that the compressor causes the liquid impact, thus realizing continuous ice making and improving the ice making efficiency.

Judgment is made (S200) to check whether the carbon                dioxide concentration is less than preset                concentration and the coil pipe temperature is less                than preset temperature. The scoring system is used for representing the                corresponding relationship between the history                operation information and history prediction                information of the fresh air conditioner and the                probability score of the fresh air conditioner at                the next advance opening time of the fresh air                conditioner.
   USE - Control method of fresh air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables automatically finishing                carbon dioxide concentration adjustment in a                stopping state by controlling the carbon dioxide                concentration adjustment of the fresh air                conditioner, and judging whether to turn ON the                fresh air conditioner according to a user's home                condition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the control method of fresh air conditioner.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for detecting carbon dioxide                concentration in a chamber and coil pipe                temperature of an indoor heat exchanger, when the                new air conditioner is in the stopping state                (S100)
   Step for judging to check whether the carbon                dioxide concentration is less than preset                concentration and the coil pipe temperature is less                than preset temperature (S200)
   Step for controlling blower to operate at a                set speed, and controlling the compressor to                operate at first heating frequency (S300)

The one side of the air inlet pipe is fixedly connected with a steam pipe (21) and is set on one side of the top of the tank body. The air inlet pipe is filled with carbon dioxide gas more than 550 degrees C degrees and the steam pipe is filled with saturated steam. The motor is fixedly connected to the bottom of the tank body, the oxygen removing component is located at the bottom of the inner side of the tank body and is connected with the output shaft of the motor. The oxygen removing component comprises an outer frame, a connecting plate and a copper plate. The outer frame is connected with the output shaft of the motor and the middle part is hollow. The connecting plate is inserted in outer frame and multiple copper plates are inserted at the bottom of the connecting plate. The copper plate is propped against the bottom of the outer frame.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide high temperature cracking reaction kettle.
   ADVANTAGE - The kettle: can crack produced excess carbon dioxide into pure carbon monoxide and hydrogen, reuse carbon dioxide waste gas and avoids direct emission of air pollution.
