PN WO2019204938-A1; CA3098176-A1; EP3784821-A1; US2021123146-A1; EP3784821-A4
TI Carbon capture method for electrocatalytically            converting carbon dioxide emissions to carbon            compound/s, involves absorbing and reacting carbon            dioxide, supplying aqueous solution, applying potential            difference, and separating product gas.
AU BERLINGUETTE C
   SALVATORE D
   LI T
   GOLDMAN M
   LEES E
AE UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
   UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
   UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
GA 201990480A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon capture method involves absorbing and                chemically reacting gaseous carbon dioxide to form                bicarbonate and/or carbonate in an aqueous                solution, and supplying the aqueous solution at a                cathode of an electrochemical reactor comprising an                anode and the cathode separated by a bipolar                membrane. A potential difference between anode and                cathode is applied to cause an electrochemical                reaction yielding product gas comprising one or                both of gas phase carbon dioxide and gas phase                carbon monoxide, the product gas is separated from                the aqueous solution.
   USE - Carbon capture method for electrocatalytically                converting carbon dioxide emissions and/or ambient                carbon dioxide to carbon compound/s such as                syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed in a apparatus that                can be operated at relatively low temperature or                ambient pressure. It involves direct                electrochemical reduction of bicarbonate or                carbonate that avoids acidification of the                electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for carbon capture, which                comprises:
   (A) a contactor having a fluid inlet and a                fluid outlet, where the contactor is configured to                bring a gas comprising carbon dioxide into contact                with an aqueous solution provided at the fluid                inlet;
   (B) an electrochemical reactor comprising an                anode and the cathode separated by a bipolar                membrane and a power supply connected to apply a                potential difference between the anode and the                cathode, where the cathode side of electrochemical                reactor comprises a fluid inlet, and a fluid outlet                of the contactor that is in fluid communication                with the fluid inlet of the electrochemical reactor                such that the aqueous solution is delivered to the                electrochemical reactor after contacting the gas;                and
   (C) a product gas separator located to collect                product gas produced by the electrochemical                reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves generating hydrogen gas at the cathode                  of the electrochemical reactor, where the product                  gas comprises the hydrogen gas. The pressure of                  the aqueous solution at the cathode of the                  electrochemical reactor is maintained at less                  than or equal to 2 atmosphere. The composition of                  product gas is controlled by adjusting a                  magnitude of the potential difference. Preferred                  Conditions: The aqueous solution comprises                  bicarbonate at a concentration of greater than or                  equal to 3 moles/liter. A current flowing in the                  electrochemical reactor as a result of the                  applied potential has a current density at the                  cathode of greater than or equal to 100                  milliAampere/cm2. The electrochemical reactor is                  operated at less than or equal to 150 degrees C                  and not exceeding a boiling point of the aqueous                  solution. The aqueous solution comprising strong                  base is flown through a cathode chamber of the                  electrochemical reactor, where the aqueous                  solution has a pH above 7, preferably greater                  than or equal to 8. The pH of aqueous solution                  when supplied for chemically reacting the gaseous                  carbon dioxide is 8-10. The aqueous solution has                  a first pH at an input of the electrochemical                  reactor and a second pH at an output of the                  electrochemical reactor and the second pH is                  higher than the first pH. The concentration of                  carbon dioxide (CO2) in the aqueous solution is                  below 7 mM. The product gas comprises CO2, carbon                  monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2), where the                  product gas comprises less than or equal to 50%                  CO2. The molar ratio of CO to H2 in the product                  gas is greater than 1, preferably less than 1.                  Absorbing and chemically reacting the gaseous                  carbon dioxide comprises flowing the gaseous                  carbon dioxide through a filter wetted by the                  aqueous solution. The aqueous solution comprises                  a caustic alkaline solution, preferably potassium                  hydroxide or sodium hydroxide, and an enzyme that                  is active for promoting the chemical reaction of                  the carbon dioxide to yield bicarbonate and/or                  carbonate. The enzyme comprises a carbonic                  anhydrase enzyme catalyst. The aqueous solution                  is circulated back to absorb and chemically react                  with more gaseous carbon dioxide after separating                  the product gas from the aqueous solution. The                  concentration of carbonate and/or bicarbonate in                  the aqueous solution at the fluid inlet of the                  electrochemical reactor is at least 0.5 M. The                  cathode is in contact with the bipolar membrane,                  and comprises a porous gas diffusion layer. The                  apparatus comprises a cathode catalyst at the                  cathode where the cathode catalyst is preferably                  silver and/or gold.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Making foamed article selected from article of            apparel, footwear, or sporting equipment, comprises            maintaining vessel having solid foamable material            article and carbon dioxide at predetermined pressure            and temperature.
AU WATKINS R L
   KHLYABICH P
   HANSON L R
   CHANG Y
AE NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
   NIKE INNOVATE CV (NKEN-C)
   NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
GA 2022B17520
AB    NOVELTY - Making a foamed article comprises placing an                article and carbon dioxide in a vessel, and                maintaining the vessel at a first pressure and                first temperature. The article comprises a solid                foamable material which is a thermoplastic                elastomeric material comprising first thermoplastic                elastomers. The first pressure and first                temperature are the pressure and temperature at                which the carbon dioxide is a liquid and the liquid                carbon dioxide is soluble in the solid foamable                material. The maintenance includes holding the                article and the liquid carbon dioxide in the vessel                for a duration of time sufficient for a portion of                the liquid carbon dioxide to infuse into the solid                foamable material of the article. The infused                article is optionally exposed to a second pressure                and second temperature at which the carbon dioxide                remains infused within a portion of the solid                foamable material.
   USE - Method for making a foamed article selected                from an article of apparel, footwear, or sporting                equipment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the weight or density of                the articles as compared to articles without foamed                materials, i.e., articles comprising only solid                materials. The foamed article can be a stabilized                foam article, from which residual carbon dioxide is                removed. Thermoplastic materials can be reclaimed                and reformed into new articles, thus reducing waste                and promoting recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making a foamed article comprises placing an                article and carbon dioxide in a vessel, and                maintaining the vessel at a first pressure and                first temperature. The article comprises a solid                foamable material which is a thermoplastic                elastomeric material comprising first thermoplastic                elastomers. The first pressure and first                temperature are the pressure and temperature at                which the carbon dioxide is a liquid and the liquid                carbon dioxide is soluble in the solid foamable                material. The maintenance includes holding the                article and the liquid carbon dioxide in the vessel                for a duration of time sufficient for a portion of                the liquid carbon dioxide to infuse into the solid                foamable material of the article. The infused                article is optionally exposed to a second pressure                and second temperature at which the carbon dioxide                remains infused within a portion of the solid                foamable material. The article is subjected to a                third pressure and third temperature at which the                carbon dioxide infused in the solid foamable                material phase transitions to gas. The solid                foamable material is expanded into a foamed                material for forming the foamed article. The                article is configured as a series of regions                including a first region including a first regional                material and a second region including a second                regional material. The first regional material or                the second regional material or both the first                regional material and the second regional material                are solid foamable materials.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a foamed                article, which comprises a first region comprising                or consisting essentially of a first regional                material and a second region comprising or                consisting essentially of a second regional                material, where the first regional material or the                second regional material is a foamed material which                is a physically-expanded foam formed of a                thermoplastic elastomeric material comprising first                thermoplastic elastomers.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                footwear.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The first                  thermoplastic elastomers comprise thermoplastic                  elastomeric polyolefin homopolymers or                  copolymers, thermoplastic elastomeric polyamide                  homopolymers or copolymers, thermoplastic                  elastomeric polyester homopolymers or copolymers,                  thermoplastic elastomeric polyurethane                  homopolymers or copolymers, thermoplastic                  elastomeric styrenic homopolymers or copolymers,                  or combination.

TI Field recycling device used for heptafluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas of annular net cabinet has mixed gas separating device, recycling pipeline and carbon dioxide gas recharging replacement pipeline.
AU LUO J
   LUO M
   LIU Z
   YE T
   LI Z
   LIU W
   QIU X
AE HUNAN CHANGGAO COMPLETE ELECTRICAL APPLI (HUNA-Non-standard)
   XUANCHENG POWER SUPPLY CO STATE GRID ANH (SGCC-C)
   CHINA ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID ANHUI ELECTRIC POWER CO LTD (SGCC-C)
GA 202220600A
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises recycling pipeline, mixed gas separating device, carbon dioxide gas recycling pipeline, heptafluoroisobutyronitrile recycling pipeline and carbon dioxide gas recharging replacement pipeline. The recycling pipeline comprises air inlet (1), first pressure gauge (P1), first valve (V1), second valve (V2), first compressor (3), first one-way valve (4), first air cooling device (5) and first buffer tank (6). The carbon dioxide gas recovery pipeline comprises seventh valve, second buffer tank, second compressor, second one-way valve, second air cooling device and eighth valve. The carbon dioxide gas recharging replacement pipeline comprises carbon dioxide gas storage tank and pressure-reducing pressure maintaining valve. The air inlet, first pressure gauge, first valve, second valve, first compressor, first one-way valve, first air cooling device and first buffer tank are orderly connected in series.
   USE - The field recycling device is used for heptafluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas of annular net cabinet.
   ADVANTAGE - The device solves problem of how to ensure box body is not deformed while recovering mixed gas in air chamber of annular net cabinet, and ensures pressure of air chamber and air chamber will not be deformed due to extraction of arc extinguishing gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises recycling pipeline, mixed gas separating device, carbon dioxide gas recycling pipeline, heptafluoroisobutyronitrile recycling pipeline and carbon dioxide gas recharging replacement pipeline. The recycling pipeline comprises air inlet (1), first pressure gauge (P1), first valve (V1), second valve (V2), first compressor (3), first one-way valve (4), first air cooling device (5) and first buffer tank (6). The carbon dioxide gas recovery pipeline comprises seventh valve, second buffer tank, second compressor, second one-way valve, second air cooling device and eighth valve. The carbon dioxide gas recharging replacement pipeline comprises carbon dioxide gas storage tank and pressure-reducing pressure maintaining valve. The heptafluoroisobutyronitrile recovery pipeline comprises ninth valve, third compressor, third one-way valve, third air cooling device, tenth valve and purifying tank. The air inlet, first pressure gauge, first valve, second valve, first compressor, first one-way valve, first air cooling device and first buffer tank are orderly connected in series. The output end of first buffer tank is hermetically connected with input end of mixed gas separating device. TI Converter flue gas carbon dioxide collecting system comprises e.g. sequentially connected flue gas pretreatment device flue gas combustion device and carbon dioxide purification and recovery device, first combustion reactor, and converter.
AU LIU P
   XU H
AE WISDRI ENG & RES INC LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 201927071G
AB    NOVELTY - Converter flue gas carbon dioxide collecting system comprises a flue gas pretreatment device, a flue gas combustion device and a carbon dioxide purification and recovery device which are sequentially connected. The intake end of the flue gas pretreatment device is connected to the converter. The flue gas combustion device comprises a first combustion reactor, a second combustion reactor, an air intake line, and an air discharge line. The first combustion reactor and the second combustion reactor are respectively filled with an oxidized state oxygen carrier and a reduced state oxygen carrier. The outlet end of the pretreatment device and the air intake line are respectively connected to the intake ends of the first combustion reactor and the second combustion reactor through a reversing valve. The outlet ends of the first combustion reactor and the second combustion reactor are respectively connected to the recovery device and the air discharge line through the second diverter valve.
   USE - Used as converter flue gas carbon dioxide collecting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: oxidizes carbon monoxide in the flue gas into carbon dioxide by chemical chain combustion; increases the carbon dioxide concentration in the flue gas; provides a basis for the storage and resource utilization of the converter flue gas carbon dioxide; simultaneously collects and recovers carbon dioxide; and makes full use of the waste heat and chemical energy of high temperature flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for collecting carbon dioxide capture by using converter flue gas carbon dioxide collecting system, comprising (i) conveying the high-temperature flue gas generated by the converter smelting into the pretreatment device for cooling, performing cooling pretreatment, and discharging the pretreated flue gas from the pretreatment device, (ii) conveying the flue gas to the first combustion reactor through the reversing valve, oxidizing the flue gas with the oxidation state oxygen carrier in the first combustion reactor to form a main component of carbon dioxide flue gas, reducing the oxidized oxygen carrier to a reduced state oxygen carrier, simultaneously transporting the air through the reversing valve to the second combustion reactor, conveying transferring the air and the reduced state oxygen carrier in the second combustion reactor to oxidative regeneration reaction occurs to generate high temperature oxygen-poor air, and oxidizing the reduced-state oxygen carrier to an oxidation state oxygen carrier, (iii) conveying the flue gas mainly comprising carbon dioxide discharged from the first combustion reactor to the carbon dioxide purification and recovery device through the second reversing valve, performing purification and recovery, passing the high temperature oxygen-depleted air discharged from the second combustion reactor through the second reversing valve, and discharging through the air discharge line, (iv) separately detecting the concentration of carbon monoxide in the reaction product in the first combustion reactor and the concentration of oxygen in the reaction product in the second combustion reactor, and if the concentration of carbon monoxide and the concentration of oxygen respectively reach their set values, simultaneously switching the reversing valve and the second reversing valve, and conveying the flue gas discharged from the flue gas pretreatment device to the second combustion reactor, where the air enters to the first combustion reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the converter flue gas carbon dioxide collecting system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidized state oxygen carrier and the reduced state oxygen carrier comprise nickel group, copper group, iron group, cobalt group, and/or manganese group carrier.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pretreatment device comprises a vaporization cooling flue and a coarse dust remover for removing large particle dust in the converter flue gas. The vaporization cooling flue is connected to the converter. The air outlet of the coarse dust remover is connected to the reversing valve. A furnace opening seal protection member is set between the intake end of the pretreatment device and the converter. The seal protector is set at the furnace opening of the converter. The carbon dioxide purification and recovery device comprises a high temperature waste heat boiler, a gas purification mechanism, a compressor, and a carbon dioxide storage tank which are sequentially connected. The gas purifying mechanism is a ceramic fiber filter. The ceramic fiber filter comprises a dust collector, a ceramic fiber tube and a backflushing component for backflushing the filter surface of the ceramic fiber tube. The ceramic fiber tube is mounted to the orifice plate of the dust collector. The ceramic fiber tube is a closed end pipe made of high temperature resistant ceramic fiber. The converter flue gas flows from bottom to top within the ceramic fiber filter. A low temperature waste heat boiler, an induced draft fan and a chimney are sequentially connected to the air discharge line. Preferred Method: In the step (i), during the process of feeding the high-temperature flue gas into the pretreatment device, carbon dioxide is blown around the furnace mouth of the converter to suppress the air from being mixed into the high-temperature flue gas. In the step (iii), the high temperature oxygen-depleted air discharged from the second combustion reactor is recovered by waste heat recovery through a low temperature waste heat boiler.

TI Supercritical exfoliation of single-layer or few-layer graphene comprises e.g. using drying kettle and nozzles in process flow and drying kettle and adding graphite powder, dispersant or cosolvent into first kettle body.
AU HU Y
AE HU Y (HUYY-Individual)
GA 2020B40205
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical exfoliation of single-layer or few-layer graphene comprises e.g. (i) using drying kettle and nozzles in the process flow and the drying kettle comprises a first kettle body and a second kettle body; (ii) adding graphite powder, dispersant or cosolvent into the first kettle body; (iii) adjusting the temperature in the drying kettle to reach the temperature setting value, adding carbon dioxide into the first kettle body, adjusting the internal pressure of the first kettle body to reach the first preset value and circulating the carbon dioxide fluid in the kettle; and (iv) adjusting the pressure in the second kettle body to reach the second preset pressure value after stopping the circulation of the carbon dioxide fluid in the kettle, turning on the valve of the nozzle, passing the fluid in the first vessel through the nozzle and collecting in the second vessel.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical exfoliation of single-layer or few-layer graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can utilize physical process of peeling, and mild processing conditions; does not damage the structure of graphene; utilizes non toxic carbon dioxide; and is economical, easy and simple and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical exfoliation of single-layer or few-layer graphene comprises (i) using drying kettle and nozzles in the process flow and the drying kettle comprises a first kettle body and a second kettle body; (ii) adding graphite powder, dispersant or cosolvent into the first kettle body; (iii) adjusting the temperature in the drying kettle to reach the temperature setting value, adding carbon dioxide into the first kettle body, adjusting the internal pressure of the first kettle body to reach the first preset value and circulating the carbon dioxide fluid in the kettle; (iv) adjusting the pressure in the second kettle body to reach the second preset pressure value after stopping the circulation of the carbon dioxide fluid in the kettle, turning on the valve of the nozzle, passing the fluid in the first vessel through the nozzle and collecting in the second vessel; and (v) collecting the up-to-standard materials in the collection chamber of the second tank body, leaving substandard materials in the second tank body through the nozzle, passing the first tank body and repeating the above step (iii) and (iv) process until preparing a single-layer or few-layer graphene material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (v), the second tank body is provided with an electric field for distinguishing standard materials from non-standard materials. In the step (iv), the nozzle is set as an anti-clogging nozzle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the weight ratio of graphite powder and dispersant/ latent solvent is 1:0.1-1:10. In the step (ii), the graphite powder is natural graphite powder or treated expanded graphite powder. In the step (ii), the dispersant is sodium lauryl sulfate and sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate. In the step (ii), cosolvent is ethanol. In the step (v), the standard material is 1-5 layers of graphene and the non-standard material is more than 5 layers of graphene. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the temperature setting value of the first kettle body is 35-90 degrees C and the preset value of the first pressure in the first vessel is 20-40 MPa. In the step (iv), the circulation time of carbon dioxide fluid is 20-120 minutes and in the step (v), the second pressure setting value of the second tank body is 7 MPa.

TI Reducing carbon emissions associated with waste            gas by selectively removing hydrogen sulfide from waste            gas, combining waste gas and steam in water-gas shift            unit, separating carbon dioxide from shift gas, and            recovering carbon dioxide.
AU STERN D L
   SUNDARAM N
   NORTHROP P S
   ONEAL E J
   HARANDI M N
   DALY K B
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202286188X
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon emissions associated with waste gas are                reduced by introducing first and second effluents                in a gasifier of a partial oxidation unit to                produce a waste gas, where first effluent comprises                one or more hydrocarbon containing feeds, and                second effluent comprises air, enriched air with                oxygen or oxygen; selectively removing hydrogen                sulfide from waste gas; combining waste gas and                steam in a water-gas shift unit to produce a shift                gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide;                separating the carbon dioxide from the shift gas in                a carbon capture unit to produce a carbon                dioxide-enriched effluent and an effluent                comprising a hydrogen and nitrogen-enriched                mixture; and recovering the carbon dioxide from the                carbon dioxide enriched effluent.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing carbon                emissions associated with waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods enable centralizing in a single                location the carbon dioxide capture and without the                need for ductwork to combine large volumes of gases                (e.g., flue gas) from individual furnace stacks.                Furthermore, existing fuel gas headers can be used                to distribute low carbon fuel gases. The methods                also provide lower capital expenditure/operating                expenses (CAPEX/OPEX) for the carbon capture                process. Additionally, benefit of combining partial                oxidation with air, WGS, and carbon capture to                produce a mixture of hydrogen/nitrogen fuel gas is                that nitrogen can act as a diluent to prevent                excessive flame temperatures in burners.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for facility                for reducing carbon emissions from waste gas                produced from partial oxidation comprising at least                one partial oxidation unit comprising one or more                gasifiers configured for producing a waste gas and                heat from one or more feeds, in presence of air                and/or oxygen; water-gas shift unit configured to                receive the waste gas and steam for producing a                shift gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide;                carbon capture unit configured to receive the shift                gas for at least partially removing the carbon                dioxide from the shift gas and for producing a                carbon dioxideenriched gas and an effluent                comprising a hydrogenand nitrogen-enriched                mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Waste gas comprises                  a gas selected from hydrogen; carbon monoxide;                  carbon dioxide; and/or nitrogen. Waste gas is a                  syngas FLEXIGAS (RTM: Not given). Output products                  from the water-gas shift unit have a hydrogen                  content of 30-80 vol.% and a carbon dioxide                  content of 5-30 vol.%. Carbon dioxide enriched                  effluent has a carbon dioxide content of about 50                  vol.% or greater.Preferred Processes: Partial oxidation is a                  Flexicoking process. The method further comprises                  removing particulate matters using a water wash                  and/or an interstage compressor water                  condensation and at least partially; removing                  nitrogen from the air of the second effluent and                  removing carbon dioxide from the shift gas to                  produce a hydrogen-enriched shift gas. Removing                  carbon dioxide from the shift gas is performed                  using two different separation technologies in                  series as a two-step process. Heat recovered from                  the partial oxidation unit is used to generate                  steam for the water-gas shift reaction and/or                  carbon dioxide removal. Recovering carbon dioxide                  is carried out by passing the carbon                  dioxide-enriched effluent through an absorbent                  system. The method further comprises recovering                  hydrogen from the effluent comprising a hydrogen-                  and nitrogen-enriched mixture. Converting the                  effluent comprising the hydrogen- and                  nitrogen-enriched mixture into ammonia comprises:                  increasing hydrogen concentration to about 3:1                  relative to nitrogen. The method further                  comprises at least partially or entirely removing                  nitrogen from the air of the second effluent in                  the gasifier of the partial oxidation unit to                  produce an hydrogen-enriched waste gas; shifting                  a portion of carbon monoxide of the                  hydrogen-enriched waste gas in the water-gas                  shift unit to hydrogen and carbon dioxide; and                  recovering carbon dioxide to produce methanol or                  its derivatives. The method further comprises                  processing the effluent comprising the hydrogen-                  and nitrogen-enriched mixture in one or more                  refinery fuel gas headers, wherein the effluent                  can optionally be combined with one or more fuel                  gases. The method further comprises producing a                  second waste gas effluent and heat from a second                  partial oxidation unit upstream of the water-gas                  shift unit. The method further comprises                  combining the second waste gas effluent with the                  waste gas produced from partial oxidation of the                  first effluent and the second effluent; and                  generating steam from the heat, where the steam                  is introduced to the water-gas shift unit, and/or                  the carbon capture unit. Water-gas shift unit and                  the carbon capture unit are replaced by a                  carbonate fuel cell operated in reverse with                  power supplied to produce a low pressure (about 3                  bara or less) product mixture comprising hydrogen                  and nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Absorbent system is an                  amine absorbent system selected from piperazines                  and/or ethanolamines.

AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide in a device for electrolysis of a gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, where device comprises a cathode and an anode, and the cathode is at least partially porous, comprise utilizing at least one electrolyte on the cathode side during the electrolysis which comprises a salt comprising a cation e.g. an ammonium ion, alkylammonium ion, dialkylammonium ion and trialkylammonium ion, and optionally substituting the alkyl radicals having 1-20 carbon atoms, where cation is an ammonium ion.
   USE - The electrolyte is useful in the electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) device for the electrolysis of a gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, comprising a cathode, the cathode being at least partially porous, an anode, cathode gas space on one side of the cathode, which is connected to the cathode and is designed to provide a gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide such that at least part of the carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide comes into contact with the cathode, at least one first gas supply, which is connected to the cathode gas space and is designed to supply a gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to the cathode space, at least one first gas discharge, which is connected to the cathode gas space and is designed to discharge a gas comprising at least one product of an electrolytic conversion of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide from the cathode gas space, an electrolyte space on the other side of the cathode, which is designed to contain an electrolyte, at least parts of the electrolyte coming into contact with the cathode, at least one first electrolyte feed, which is designed to feed the electrolyte into the electrolyte space, and at least one first electrolyte discharge, which is designed to discharge at least part of the electrolyte from the electrolyte compartment; and
   (2) use of an electrolyte in the electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte has a conductivity of greater than or equal to 50 mS/cm, preferably greater than or equal to 200 mS. The salt comprises an anion e.g. sulfate, acetate, formate, triflate, carbonate, hydrogen carbonate, and/or oxalate. The electrolyte is an aqueous electrolyte and has a pH of less than 7, preferably 3-5. Preferred Method: The electrolysis is carried out at a temperature of greater than or equal to 36 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 60 degrees C. The ammonia, alkylamine, dialkylamine and/or trialkylamine are released, which preferably condenses out of the product gas or are washed out with a neutral to acidic solution. The ammonia released from the electrolyte and/or alkylamine, dialkylamine and/or trialkylamine released is reacted such that it is fed back to the electrolyte as a salt, and/or ammonia released from the electrolyte and/or alkylamine, dialkylamine and/or trialkylamine released is supplied to the electrolyte. The electrolysis is carried out at a current density of greater than or equal to 100 mA/cm2, preferably 300 greater than or equal to mA/cm2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further comprising a recovery unit which is connected at least to the first gas discharge and is designed to convert from the gas comprising at least one product of an electrolytic conversion of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide an ammonia released from the electrolyte and/or a released alkylamine, dialkylamine and/or to recover trialkylamine. The recovery unit comprises a gas scrubber and/or a unit for condensing out. The recovery unit is connected to the at least one first electrolyte feed. The recovery unit comprises a synthesis unit in which the salt of the electrolyte is produced, and the synthesis unit is connected to the first electrolyte feed. At least one separator is provided between the anode and the cathode, and/or the anode is designed as a membrane coated with anode catalyst or as a membrane-electrode arrangement.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing polycarbonate, comprises producing                chlorine and/or hydrogen by chloralkali                electrolysis of alkali metal chloride, preferably                sodium chloride, in aqueous solution, providing                purified carbon-dioxide gas stream by providing                carbon-dioxide gas stream, cleaning carbon-dioxide                gas stream of secondary components, preferably                nitrogen oxide, sulfur compounds, dust, water,                oxygen and hydrochloric acid optionally by means of                adsorption, gas scrubbing or catalytic treatment to                obtain a gas stream of purified carbon dioxide,                feeding hydrogen together with purified                carbon-dioxide gas stream into an reverse water gas                shift reaction (RWGS) zone and converting the                starting materials according to the RWGS principle                to a product gas mixture containing unreacted                starting material, steam, carbon monoxide,                optionally by-products, namely lower hydrocarbons,                preferably methane, separating water from the steam                from product gas mixture, and separating unreacted                carbon dioxide from the gas mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                polycarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the use of fossil raw                materials as a starting material, improves the                carbon footprint of polycarbonate production in                order to protect the earth's atmosphere; produces                product with improved sustainability and extended                service life or the color (stabilizers), to                simplify processing (e.g. mold release agents, flow                aids, antistatic agents) or to adapt the properties                of the resulting polycarbonate material to specific                ones loads (impact modifiers e.g. rubbers; flame                retardants, colorants and glass fibers).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing polycarbonate, comprises producing                chlorine and/or hydrogen by chloralkali                electrolysis of alkali metal chloride, preferably containing unreacted                starting material, steam, carbon monoxide,                optionally by-products, namely lower hydrocarbons,                preferably methane, separating water from the steam                from the product gas mixture, separating unreacted                carbon dioxide from the gas mixture of the RWGS                reaction obtained from above separation, preferably                by means of amine scrubbing, and recycling of the                unreacted carbon dioxide into the RWGS reaction,                separating the unreacted hydrogen in the RWGS                reaction from the gas mixture of carbon monoxide                and hydrogen obtained after separation, preferably                using a cold box, and recycling of the separated                hydrogen into the RWGS reaction, feeding the                remaining carbon monoxide from the separation into                the phosgene synthesis, and feeding the previously                formed chlorine into the phosgene synthesis,                synthesizing phosgene from carbon monoxide and                chlorine, and reacting phosgene with diol. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device                system utilized in the above method.

The alkali metal hypochlorite is generated.

CO2 supply assembly and crude oil supply assembly connection through the reaction chamber of the multi-way valve to the reaction chamber, the reaction chamber is provided with at least one blind hole connected with the reaction chamber, and the reaction chamber with partial side wall light, measuring component comprises pressure detecting element is set on the pipeline of the multi-way valve; the light-permeable sidewall outer part located in the reaction chamber of the reaction assembly, and a pressure detecting element and reaction element electrically connecting the processing device. This utility model can analyze CO2 under the condition of a microscopic pore throat and diffusion process and determining the CO2 of the crude oil in the crude oil of diffusion coefficient.

EA    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a carbon dioxide supply module (10), a crude oil supply module (20), a diffusion reaction module (30) and a measurement module (40). The diffusion reaction component has a reaction chamber for a diffusion reaction between carbon dioxide and crude oil. The carbon dioxide supply module and the crude oil supply module are connected to a reaction chamber through a multi-port valve (50). The measurement component includes a pressure detecting portion that is provided on a pipeline of the multi-way valve. The pressure measuring element measures a reaction pressure in the reaction chamber. The reaction recording component records the volume change of carbon dioxide and the volume change of crude oil in the reaction chamber. The processing device determines the diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide in crude oil according to the reaction pressure, the volume of carbon dioxide and the volume of crude oil.
   USE - Device for measuring diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide in crude oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The device analyzes the diffusion process of carbon dioxide with crude oil under the condition of micro-pore throat and determines the diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide in crude oil. The measurement efficiency of the diffusion coefficient is improved. The sapphire glass sheet has high hardness and high wear resistance, so that the outer wall surface of the reaction chamber is effectively prevented from being scratched or damaged in the using process.

GA 201867373K
AB    NOVELTY - The dehumidification air conditioner controls                the air passed through an adsorption zone (2) of a                humidity adsorption rotor (4) to temperature                adjustment by an after cooler (11). A supply                destination is supplied as the supply air. The                return air from the supply destination is mixed                with the air passing through a pre-cooler (8). A                portion of the air passed through an intercooler                (9) is branched and is passed through a purge zone                (7) of the moisture adsorption rotor. The portion                of the air is heated by a regeneration heater (13)                and is passed through a regeneration zone (3) of                the moisture adsorption rotor. The air passing                through the regeneration zone of the moisture                adsorption rotor is mixed with the portion of the                outer side air, is heated with the regeneration                heater, and is passed through the regeneration zone                of a carbon dioxide adsorption rotor (1). The air                passing through the regeneration zone of the carbon                dioxide adsorbing rotor is exhausted to the outer                side air of apparatus.
   USE - Dehumidification air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The dehumidification air conditioner lowers                the dew point temperature in the drying chamber                such as a dry room by using the carbon dioxide                adsorption rotor and the humidity adsorption rotor,                and reduces the carbon dioxide concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The dehumidification air conditioner has a                carbon dioxide adsorption rotor divided into two                zones such as a regeneration zone and an adsorption                zone. A humidity adsorption rotor is divided into a                regeneration zone, an adsorption zone and a purge                zone. The outer side air is cooled and dehumidified                by a pre-cooler and is passed through an adsorption                zone of the carbon dioxide adsorption rotor. The                air passing through the adsorption zone is cooled                by an intercooler. The portion of the air passing                through the pre-cooler is branched and sent to a                purge zone of the humidity adsorption rotor. The                remaining portion of the air is sent to the                adsorption zone of the carbon dioxide adsorption                rotor. A condenser of a heat pump of the pre-cooler                is used as the regeneration heater of the carbon                dioxide adsorption rotor. The amine support solid                absorber is supported on carbon dioxide adsorption                rotor using organic type binders, such as an                inorganic type binder or vinyl acetate type, and an                acryl type. The carbon dioxide adsorption material                made of the oxide of cerium is supported as the                carbon dioxide adsorption rotor by using the                inorganic type binder. The humidity adsorption                rotor adsorbs carbon dioxide simultaneously with                moisture in a predetermined dew point region.

TI Method for generating integrated carbon gasification-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power, involves entering pressurize air to air separation device, followed by sending vaporized liquid oxygen to gas turbine combustion chamber.
AU TIAN Y
   QIAO Y
   XIE K
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
GA 201935746X
AB    NOVELTY - An integrated carbon gasification-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generating method involves entering a pressurized air to an air separation device to obtain a liquid oxygen and a liquid nitrogen, gasifiying a coal, generating a combustion power, expanding the pumped nitrogen, vaporizing, entering a pressurized coal powder to a coal gasifier, purifying the gas, sending to a power generation system, generating a supercritical carbon dioxide, entering the purification gas to a combustion, promoting rotation, generating a generator electric power, subjecting the combustion flue gas to supercritical carbon dioxide power generation, circulating, exchanging with water to generate circulating water vapor, circulating with a pressurized liquid oxygen, separating a gas, recovering the carbon dioxide, pressurizing a water to obtain high pressure water, discharging the remaining water, vaporizing the pressurized liquid oxygen and sending to a gas turbine combustion chamber.
   USE - Method for generating integrated carbon gasification-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An integrated carbon gasification-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generating method involves entering 0.5-0.8 MPa pressurized air to an air separation device for air separation to obtain a liquid oxygen and a liquid nitrogen, using a pumped pressurized liquid oxygen heat exchange vaporization for a coal gasification and a combustion power generation, expanding the pumped pressurized liquid nitrogen and vaporizing to generate electricity, entering a pressurized coal powder and high pressure oxygen and water vapor to a coal gasifier for gasification reaction, purifying the generated high-pressure and high-temperature gas by waste heat recovery using a supercritical carbon dioxide waste heat boiler, purifying by a fine dust removal, desulfurization, dechlorination and degreasing, sending to a power generation system, and generating a high-temperature supercritical carbon dioxide by a turbine, entering the high-pressure purification gas to a combustion of a gas turbine together with the high-pressure oxygen, promoting the high-speed rotation of a compressor and a generator by a circulating water vapor, compressing the compressor air to 0.5-0.8 MPa, and generating a generator electric power, subjecting the high-temperature combustion flue gas to supercritical carbon dioxide power generation by supercritical carbon dioxide waste heat boiler heat exchange, where the coolant is first-stage pressurized liquid oxygen or/and liquid nitrogen, after cooling circulating a supercritical carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide high-pressure pump, exchanging a medium temperature flue gas with the high pressure water to generate circulating water vapor, circulating with a secondary pressurized liquid oxygen or/and liquid nitrogen, after the cooled flue gas is dehydrated, separating a liquid flue gas, recovering by a distillation tower to recover carbon dioxide as a product for sale, pressurizing a part of a dewatered water by a water pump to obtain high pressure water, discharging the remaining water, vaporizing a pressurized liquid oxygen and sending to a gas turbine combustion chamber.

PN WO2022204233-A1
TI New zinc complex used in an apparatus for carbon            dioxide capture.
AU SHEEHAN S W
   CHEN C
AE AIR HOLDINGS CO INC (AIRH-Non-standard)
GA 2022C5321P
AB    NOVELTY - Zinc metal containing organic complex (I) is                new.
   USE - (I) is useful in an apparatus for carbon                dioxide capture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) improves the hydration of carbon dioxide                at a zinc metal center, including                ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and its derivatives                and efficiently utilizes sorbents with improved                sorption and desorption characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Zinc complex of formula ((L1)nZn(L2)m) (I) is                new.
   L1=bidentate, tridentate, tetradentate,                pentadentate, hexadentate, heptadentate or                octadentate ligand coordinated to Zn in at least a                k2 fashion, where each L1 is bound to Zn through at                least one donor heteroatom comprising O or N;
   L1=mono-, di-, tri-, tetra-, penta- or                hexaanionic;
   L2=-OH or -OH ;
   n=1 or 2; and
   m=0 or 1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   capturing carbon dioxide from a gas feed                stream, comprising contacting the gas feed stream                with a solution of the zinc complex in water and                optionally a co-solvent to react the carbon dioxide                with water to form a solution of hydrated carbon                dioxide, where the zinc complex comprises at least                one ligand coordinated to zinc; and
   an apparatus for carbon dioxide capture                comprising a solution of the zinc complex in water                and optionally a co-solvent, where the zinc complex                comprises at least one ligand coordinated to                zinc.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The complex is soluble in                  water, and the complex is stable under basic                  conditions. The complex further comprises a                  cation, where the complex has of formula                  (({(L1)nZn(L2)m}w)z(A1y)x) (II).z=charge of the {(L1) Zn(L2) } fragment, and                  is 0, -1, -2 or -3;A1=cation;x=charge of the A1 cation, and is +1 or                  +2;w=(yx)/z; andy=(wz)/x.The cation comprises ammonium, lithium,                  sodium, potassium, cesium, calcium, or magnesium.                  The complex is stable at a pH of 7-16, preferably                  8-10. The solution of the zinc complex is                  homogeneous. The co-solvent comprises                  ethanolamine, propylene carbonate or an ionic                  liquid. The ligand is a bidentate, tridentate,                  tetradentate, pentadentate, hexadentate,                  heptadentate, or octadentate ligand coordinated                  to zinc in at least a k2 fashion. The ligand is                  not a cyclen or porphyrin ligand. The homogeneous                  zinc complex is the complex. The solution of a                  homogeneous zinc complex further comprises a                  salt. The salt is a carbonate salt or potassium                  carbonate. The solution comprises 0.001-20 M                  salt. The solution comprises 0.00001-10 M the                  homogeneous zinc complex. Preferred Method: The                  method is carried out at a temperature of                  20-50&#176; C. The method further comprises                  releasing gaseous carbon dioxide from the                  solution, where releasing gaseous carbon dioxide                  from the solution comprises heating the solution                  of hydrated carbon dioxide is heated to a                  temperature of 50-200&#176; C, preferably                  80&#176; C. The releasing gaseous carbon                  dioxide from the solution comprises decreasing                  the pressure, where the pressure is decreased to                  a pressure of 0.1-760 Torr. The releasing gaseous                  carbon dioxide from the solution comprises a                  change in chemical potential. The change in                  chemical potential comprises an increase in                  relative humidity, where the gas feed stream is                  air.
   Preferred Definitions:
   L1=not a cyclen or porphyrin ligand and is                EDTA, glutaric acid, nitrilotriacetic acid,                triazacyclononane, trispyrazolylborate,                terpyridine, porphine, corrin,                tris(2-aminoethyl)amine, triethylenetetramine,                12-crown-4, 15-crown-5, 16-crown-6,                (2,2,2)cryptand, glycine, salen,                2-(pyridine-2-yl)propan-2-ol, niacin, picolinic                acid, 2-acetylpyridine, iminodiacetic acid,                oxalate, glutaric acid, ethylene                glycol-bis(&#946; -aminoethyl                ether)-N,N,N',N'-tetraacetic acid,                diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid, or                ethylenediamine-N,N'-bis(2-hydroxyphenylacetic                acid) (all optionally substituted by at least one                H, OH, amino, imine, sulfate, sulfonyl, alkyl,                heteroalkyl, alkoxy, aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl,                heterocycloalkyl, acetyl, carboxylate or                glycolate).
   EXAMPLE - 129 g Glutaric acid was slowly added to 1.5 l                toluene, which was heated to 60&#176; C while                vigorously stirring for 1 hour to partially                dissolve the glutaric acid. 81.3 g Zinc oxide was                added to the suspension and the mixture was stirred                at 60&#176; C for an additional 4 hours. The                light blue-purple suspension was cooled down to                room temperature and filtered using a vacuum                filtration system, and washed with acetone three                times, resulting in a light purple powder. The                powder was further dried in a vacuum oven at                60&#176; C, 20 mmHg for 24 hours to give 175 g                a fluffy purple zinc glutarate powder.

TI Method relates to electrochemical technologies,            namely to methods for utilizing carbon dioxide.
AU BEDNARZHEVSKII S S
AE BEDNARZHEVSKII S S (BEDN-Individual)
GA 2022813643
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to electrochemical                technologies, namely to methods for utilizing                carbon dioxide, and can be used to reduce gas                emissions from industrial enterprises, protect the                environment and reduce the greenhouse effect. A                method for the utilization of carbon dioxide is                presented, which involves the removal of                hydrocarbonate ions of HCO3
- and carbonate ions of CO3
2- from water after aeration with carbon                dioxide at an excess pressure of 0.3-0.7 MPa with                preliminary softening and cooling of water to a                temperature of 4-7C. Aeration is carried out                simultaneously with electrolysis and cooling of the                working solution to a temperature of 4-7C. The                gaseous products of electrolysis are removed,                unutilized carbon dioxide is isolated from them.                Return it for recycling. Waste water is discharged                with solid and dissolved products of electrolysis.                They clean it of them and return it for reuse.                Water can be pre-carbonated with carbon dioxide,                and then electrolysis is carried out in it. To                increase the efficiency of the method in the                electrolysis process, you can set the pH of the                water from 9 to 11.
   USE - Electrochemical technologies.
   ADVANTAGE - Method provides an increase in the efficiency                of carbon dioxide utilization.

PN WO2019136374-A1; EP3735311-A1; US2021060484-A1; EP3735311-A4
TI Device used for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises liquid flow path comprising proton-coupled redox active species of aqueous solution or suspension on oxidation decreases pH and on reduction increases pH.
AU AZIZ M J
   KWABI D G
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
   HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
   HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 201960760T
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises liquid flow path comprising region (R1) comprising a inlet (A) and outlet (A) and an aqueous solution or suspension comprising proton-coupled redox active species on oxidation releases one or more protons to decreases pH and on reduction takes up one or more electrons to increases pH. (R1) is configured to receive a gas comprising carbon dioxide via the inlet (A) allow the gas to contact the aqueous solution or suspension, and to release the gas depleted of carbon dioxide via the outlet (A). Region (R2) comprising an electrode is fluidically connected to region (R1), and region (R3) comprising an outlet (B) is fluidically connected to region (R2). Region (R3) is configured to release carbon dioxide outgassing from the aqueous solution or suspension via the outlet (B) and region (R4) comprising an electrode is fluidically connected to the region (R1) and (R3).
   USE - Device for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces the energy input and eliminates thermal energy required to capture carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of capturing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The oxidized form of the proton-coupled redox active species comprises quinone, phenazine, alloxazine, isoalloxazine, and polyoxometalate, present in the aqueous solution or suspension at concentration of at least 0.5 M. The device comprises electrochemical cells and further comprises ion-conducting barrier arranged between regions (R2) and (R4). R3 comprises inlet (I2) fluidically connected to outlet (O2) connecting carrier gas source. Preferred Property: The device has pH of region (R3) is less than 8 and region (R1) is greater than 8.

PT P
PN CN108971707-A
TI Vertical butt welding of thick plate useful for            vertical butt joint welding operation comprises e.g.            performing vertical butt positioning of workpiece,            testing dimensional parameters, and connecting carbon            dioxide automatic welding device.
AU LIU Y
   LI J
   LIU J
   XU J
   LIU B
AE GUANGZHOU SHIPYARD INT CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 2018A2726G
AB    NOVELTY - Vertical butt welding of thick plate for the                vertical butt joint welding operation comprises (i)                using a positioning tool to perform vertical butt                positioning of a welded workpiece, (ii) using                carbon dioxide automatic welding equipment to test                the dimensional parameters of the welded workpiece                and a weld bevel, (iii) connecting a carbon dioxide                automatic welding device to the corresponding                vertical butt welding parameters according to the                size parameters of the workpiece to be welded and                the weld bevel detected, and (iv) welding of the                carbon dioxide automatic welding device to the weld                of the workpiece to be welded through a carbon                dioxide flux cored wire.
   USE - The method is useful for vertical butt joint                welding operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is in accordance with the vertical                butt welding parameters; has the carbon dioxide                automatic welding equipment that welds the weld of                the welded workpiece through the carbon dioxide                flux cored wire; has high degree of automation;                forms evenly formed weld; and improves the quality                and the strength of the weld.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The dimension                  parameters of the workpiece to be welded and the                  weld bevel comprises the thickness of the                  workpiece to be welded, and the thickness                  difference, assembly gap, weld length and angle                  of the weld bevel. In the step (iii), the                  vertical butt welding parameters comprises the                  number of weld beads of the weld, the number of                  layers of the weld, the welding current, voltage,                  and the welding speed. The preset weld bevel has                  an angle ranging from 30-45 degrees , the                  thickness of the preset workpiece is 10-60 mm,                  and the preset assembly gap is 4-12 mm. Preferred                  Method: In the step (iii), the width of the weld                  seam is obtained according to the size parameter                  of the workpiece to be welded and the weld bevel,                  when the weld width is greater than 16 mm, and                  the carbon dioxide automatic welding device                  performs split welding. In the step (iii), the                  thickness of the weld is greater than 6 mm, and                  the carbon dioxide automatic welding device                  performs layered welding.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)

GA 201863429T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a viscosifying proppant involves (a) coating proppant particles with a polymerizable precursor material of a carbon dioxide-philic material to form coated proppant particles and (b) polymerizing the polymerizable precursor material to convert the coated proppant particles to a viscosifying proppant having a carbon dioxide-philic coating with a solvable portion that viscosifies a carbon dioxide-based fluid when the viscosifying proppant is added to the carbon dioxide-based fluid.
   USE - The viscosifying proppant is used for making viscous hydraulic fracturing fluids to treat a subterranean formation including a rock, coal, or shale formation. The viscosifying proppants is used to stimulate groundwater wells, precondition or induce rock cave-ins for mining operations, dispose waste by injecting it deeply into rock, measure stresses in the Earths crust, generate electricity in enhanced geothermal systems or increase injection rates for the geologic sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The viscosifying proppant improves viscosity of carbon dioxide-based fluid, treats subterranean formations by overcoming the problems associated with waterless fracturing fluids such as inadequate viscosity and prevents subterranean formation from closing or collapsing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The proppant particles are chosen from oxides, silicates, sand, ceramic, resin, plastic, mineral, glass, graded sand, treated sand, resin-coated sand, other resin-coated particles, bauxite, sintered bauxite, glass particles, glass beads, and their combinations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step of coating the proppant particles involves contacting the polymerizable precursor material with the proppant particles in a fluidized bed process. The method further involves coating the proppant particles with an adhesion promoter before coating with the polymerizable precursor material and roughening the proppant particles before coating the proppant particles to produce proppant particles having a rough surface with an arithmetic average roughness (Ra) of greater than or equal to 1 nm. The step of polymerizing the polymerizable precursor material involves heating the coated proppant particles and/or exposing the coated proppant particles to UV light. The carbon dioxide-philic coating does not dissociate from the proppant particles when the viscosifying proppant is added to a water-based fluid, where greater than or equal to 90 wt.% carbon dioxide-philic coating is not crosslinked. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-philic material is chosen from polysaccharide acetates, polyethylene glycols, partially fluorinated oxygen-containing polymers, oxygenated polymers, crosslinked oxygen-containing polystyrenes, polyvinyl acetates or their combinations.

AU HUANG Y

   LU Y
   WU K
   DUAN L
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2021D7446U
AB    NOVELTY - The granulating and recycling system has a blast furnace (1), a high-pressure slag conveying device (2), a slag granulation fluidized bed device (3), a supercritical carbon dioxide re-compression power circulation system (4) and a low-temperature slag heat recovery device (5). The high pressure slag conveyor device is arranged between the blast furnace and the slag particle fluidization bed device. The slag granulating fluidized bed device is connected with the low temperature slag heat recycling device. The supercritical carbon dioxide re-compression power circulating system is respectively provided with the slag granulating fluidized bed device and the low temperature slag heat recycling device.
   USE - Blast furnace slag granulating and waste heat recycling system for use in building material industry and cement concrete indispensable, gas turbine industry, aircraft industry and ship engine system.
   ADVANTAGE - The blast furnace slag granulating and waste heat recycling system uses supercritical carbon dioxide near the critical point (7.4-7.5 MPa, 32-32degrees Celsius) high constant pressure specific heat Cp and high heat transfer performance and so on, using supercritical carbon dioxide gas to quickly cool the high temperature slag and crushing. The slag latent heat released by blast furnace slug, is fully absorbed so that the waste heat furnace slug slag is more effectively recycled. The system uses the supercritical gas as cooling medium, so that the recovery efficiency of waste heat is improved and high-quality slag product is produced.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing steel or molten iron containing                materials (22) with reduced emissions of carbon                dioxide involves producing direct reduced iron                (DRI) (18) in a direct reduction furnace (12) with                a reducing gas (6) comprising hydrogen (16);                melting portion of the DRI in a melting furnace                (20) and generating hot gasses (24); producing                steam and/or hot water (28) using the heat                contained in the hot gases; producing hydrogen from                the steam and/or hot water by electrolysis; and                feeding portion of the hydrogen to the direct                reduction furnace as a component of the reducing                gas to produce the DRI.
   USE - The method or system is useful for producing                steel or molten iron containing materials with                reduced emissions of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - In the method or system, the emission of                carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases is                significantly low. Specifically, the synergistic                system can produce steel or molten-iron containing                material by integrating a direct reduction plant,                DRI melting furnace, a heat recovery unit, a steam                turbine-electricity generator and an electrolysis                unit with lower carbon dioxide emissions than the                currently used steel-making systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                (10) for producing steel or molten iron containing                materials with reduced emissions of carbon dioxide,                comprising a direct reduction furnace to produce                DRI with a reducing gas comprising hydrogen; a DRI                melting furnace to melt the DRI generating hot                gases; a heat recovery unit (26) to produce steam                and/or hot water using the heat contained in the                hot gases; and an electrolysis unit (34) to produce                hydrogen from the steam and/or hot water, which                hydrogen is fed to the direct reduction furnace as                a component of the reducing gas to produce                DRI.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system                for producing steel.
   Reducing gas (6)
   Direct reduction furnace (12)

   Hot gases (24)
   Hot water (28)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves: (a) generating electric energy (42)                  utilizing the steam (40); and using the electric                  energy to produce the hydrogen by electrolysis;                  (b) producing oxygen (36) by electrolysis and                  using portion of the oxygen to raise the                  temperature of reducing gas prior to its feeding                  to the reduction reactor; (c) producing oxygen by                  electrolysis and using portion of the oxygen in                  the electric-arc furnace where the steel or                  molten iron containing materials are produced; or                  (d) obtaining portion of the water used for                  producing hydrogen by electrolysis from condensed                  water vapor contained in an exhausted reducing                  gas stream extracted from the direct reduction                  furnace as top gas. The DRI is melted in an                  electric-arc furnace and high-temperature gasses                  are generated.

   USE - Used as carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent reduces                regeneration energy generated in process of                adsorption and desorption of carbon dioxide, and                maintains structural stability from moisture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises a porous                metal-organic skeleton in which polyamine having                primary amine group is introduced. The porous                metal-organic skeleton includes metal based                compounds of formulae                (M2(4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-biphenyldicarboxylate)) (I),                (M2(2,5-dioxido-1,4-benzenedicarboxylate)) (II),                (M2(1,5-dioxide-2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylate))                (III) and (M2(4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-triphenyl                dicarboxylate)) (IV).
   M = Mg, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu or Zn.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising (a)                Mixing porous metal-organic skeleton, organic                solvent, and polyamine containing primary amine                group, and reacting with an ultrasonic mill, and                (b) obtaining porous metal-organic skeleton in                which polyamine having a primary amine group is                introduced from the reaction product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface of the                  porous metal-organic skeleton includes                  hydrophobic polymer material including                  polydimethylsiloxane, polytetrafluoroethylene and                  polyvinylidene fluoride. The polyhydric amine is                  composed of 15 compounds e.g.                  N1-isopropylethane-1,2-diamine,                  ethane-1,2-diamine,                  N1,N2-diethylethane-1,2-diamine,                  2-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)ethanamine,                  1-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)propan-2-amine and                  2-methyl-1-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)propan-2-amine. The                  step (b) comprises centrifuging the reaction                  product to obtain a white solid, and washing and                  pretreating white solid. The method further                  comprises treating surface of the porous                  metal-organic skeleton with at least one                  hydrophobic polymer material.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109939679-A; CN109939679-B
TI Atomic-level dispersion copper carbon dioxide            conversion catalyst comprises titanium dioxide            nanosheet and distribution on titanium dioxide            nano-sheet of atomic-scale copper, copper ion can be            added in form of soluble salt.
AU JIANG Z
   YUAN Z
   CHEN J
   YANG G
   MIAO W
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 201958499W
AB    NOVELTY - Atomic-level dispersion copper carbon dioxide                conversion catalyst comprises titanium dioxide                nanosheet and distribution on titanium dioxide                nano-sheet of atomic-scale copper.
   USE - Atomic-level dispersion copper carbon dioxide                conversion catalyst used in field of environmental                protection (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The atomic-level dispersion copper carbon                dioxide conversion catalyst has low cost, wide raw                material source of atomic-scale dispersion of                copper cocatalyst, realizes separation of titanium                dioxide photo-induced carrier, and improves carbon                dioxide photo-reduction activity of titanium                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing atomic-level dispersion copper carbon                dioxide conversion catalyst, which involves:
   (A) dispersing titanium dioxide nano sheets in                water to stir uniformly to obtain titanium dioxide                nano sheet dispersion, adding copper ion to                titanium dioxide nanosheet dispersion;
   (B) introducing carbon dioxide gas to obtain                mixed solution;
   (C) sealing mixed solution and then                irradiating with xenon lamp; and
   (D) washing obtained solid product and dry to                obtain catalyst in which monoatomic copper is                distribute on surface of titanium dioxide surface                nanosheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of                  titanium dioxide sheet is between 3-10 nanometer.                  The atomic copper loading is 0.34-3.4 wt.% of                  titanium dioxide nanosheet. The ratio of titanium                  dioxide nanosheet and water                  is(0.03-0.1)g:(50-100).mu.g/L. The copper ion can                  be added in form of soluble salt of copper                  includes copper acetate, copper nitrate and                  copper chloride. Preferred Process: The method                  further involves opening reactor completely, and                  oxygen is sufficiently oxidized to atomic grade                  copper, copper oxide formed after oxidation is                  gradually convert into copper ion in original                  solution as stirring time is extended, and then                  carbon dioxide gas is re-introduced and enables                  photodeposition of atomic copper and carbon                  dioxide photoconversion.

AE GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)

GA 201879401D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining concentration of carbon dioxide by a carbon dioxide sensor. Rotating speed of a fresh air fan is determined according to the carbon dioxide concentration in indoor environment. The fresh air fan is controlled according to the rotating speed. The fresh air fan is controlled to close when the Carbon dioxide concentration is less than predetermined minimum concentration. The fresh air fan is controlled to run at maximum rotation speed when the Carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than a predetermined maximum concentration value.

TI Gas-liquid two-phase flow pipeline corrosion            degree prediction method, involves establishing carbon            dioxide corrosion model, inputting numerical simulation            result to carbon dioxide corrosion model, and obtaining            pipeline corrosion rate.
AU ZHU F
   ZHOU Z
   LI Q
   LIU W
   LI M
   DONG X
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAO X
   TAN J
   ZHANG D
   LI S
   XU G
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201845698H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting natural gas                pipeline state information, where the natural gas                pipeline state information comprises temperature,                pressure and medium composition of carbon dioxide                content. A momentum conservation equation, an                energy conservation equation and a mass                conservation equation are established. A natural                gas pipeline gas-liquid two phase fluid model is                constructed. The natural gas pipeline state                information is analyzed by the natural gas pipeline                gas-liquid two phase fluid model. A pipeline fluid                numerical simulation value is calculated to obtain                temperature of a pipe section. A pipe erosion                damage probability is calculated. A carbon dioxide                corrosion model is established. A numerical                simulation result is input to the carbon dioxide                corrosion model. A pipeline corrosion rate is                obtained.
   USE - Gas-liquid two-phase flow pipeline corrosion                degree prediction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting service life of                the natural gas pipeline by calculating the                pipeline corrosion rate, realizing carbon dioxide                natural gas pipeline corrosion degree calculation                process and simulating a two-phase flow natural gas                pipeline carbon dioxide corrosion prediction                process according to a computer numerical                value.
