AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting Angelica sinensisextract                using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction                method involves (i) crushing dried angelica, and                sieving with 20-40 mesh sieve, (ii) placing                A.sinensispowder at 27&#8451; for 3-3.5                minutes, rapidly heating to room temperature, and                repeating for 2-3 times, and pressurizing, (iii)                placing pretreated powder in carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction instrument, (iv) draining                and recovering carbon dioxide gas while                decompressing, (v) recovering, dehydrating,                recovering and separating the substances in the                kettle to obtain volatile oil containing water,                adding anhydrous sodium sulfate, removing water                from the oil, and filtering to obtain                A.sinensisvolatile oil, (vi) extracting the                A.sinensisresidue by dynamic reflux technique, and                separating to obtain A.sinensisresidue and                A.sinensisactive ethanol solution, (vii) heating                and concentrating and (viii) spray drying the                solution.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting A.sinensisextract using                carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method                involves (i) crushing the cleaned and dried                angelica, and sieving with 20-40 mesh sieve, (ii)                placing A.sinensispowder at 27&#8451; for 3-3.5                minutes, rapidly heating to room temperature, and                repeating for 2-3 times, uniformly pressurizing to                20-40 MPa for 2-3 minutes, rapidly releasing                pressure, and repeating for 2-3 times, (iii)                placing pretreated powder in carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction instrument at 30-36 MPa                and 40-60&#8451; with a carbon dioxide flow                rate of 16-24 L/hour for 75-100 minutes, (iv)                draining and recovering carbon dioxide gas while                decompressing, (v) recovering, dehydrating, and                separating the substances in the kettle to obtain                volatile oil containing water, adding anhydrous                sodium sulfate, removing water from the oil, and                filtering to obtain A.sinensisvolatile oil, (vi)                extracting the A.sinensisresidue by dynamic reflux                technique using 70% ethanol as extraction solvent,                and separating to obtain A.sinensisresidue and                A.sinensisactive ethanol solution, (vii) heating                and concentrating the obtained ethanol solution                until no alcohol exists to obtain angelica active                substance solution and (viii) spray drying the                solution, to obtain A.sinensisactive dry                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (ii) further                  involves vacuumizing the powder for 1-2 minutes,                  quickly recovering to indoor air pressure and                  repeating the process for 2-3 times.Preferred Composition: The solid-liquid                  ratio of anhydrous sodium sulfate to volatile oil                  in step (v) is 1:3. The ratio of residue to 70%                  ethanol in the step (vi) is 1:10.Preferred Conditions: The pretreated powder                  is supplied into the carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction instrument at a pressure of 34 MPa,                  50&#8451; and at carbon dioxide flow rate of                  20 L/hour for 90 minutes. The extraction of                  residue in step (vi) is performed for 2 hours for                  3 times using microwave-assisted extraction                  having frequency of 2000-4000 MHz. The                  A.sinensisactive ethanol solution is heated and                  concentrated for more than 60 times in step                  (vii). A mechanical power is used in step (vi) to                  force the solution to reflux.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Composition: The starting gas mixture comprises more than 60 mol.% carbon dioxide, 0.0001-5 mol.% sulfur dioxide and 5-20 mol.% water, where the intermediate gas mixture comprises 65-75 mol.% carbon dioxide and less sulfur dioxide and water than the starting gas mixture. Preferred Method: The method (A) involves subjecting the starting gas mixture to a first cooling step at a first pressure level and from a first temperature level to a second temperature level, forming a first condensate and a first gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a portion of the first gaseous remainder to a compression step from the first pressure level to a second pressure level, forming a second condensate and a second gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a portion of the second gaseous remainder to a further cooling step to a third temperature level, forming a third condensate and a third gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a portion of the third gaseous remainder to a drying step to obtain a dried gas mixture, and using at least a portion of the dried gas mixture as the intermediate gas mixture which is subjected to the method (B). The heat is transferred in the method (A) to a portion of the liquefied gaseous overhead product, and forming an evaporated carbon dioxide product which is compressed to provide a compressed carbon dioxide product. The absorbing step involves using an absorption column in which a liquid bottom product and the gaseous overhead product is formed, where at least a portion of the liquid bottom product is treated in a waste handling step, forming a fraction depleted in carbon dioxide and enriched in sulfur dioxide as compared to the liquid bottom product, and recycling a fraction at least in portion to the Claus process. The method involves forming the fraction in the waste handling step, enriching the fraction in carbon dioxide and depleted in sulfur dioxide as compared to the liquid bottom product and recycling at least in portion to the absorbing step. Preferred Components: The first gaseous remainder is enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in sulfur dioxide and water in relation to the starting gas mixture. The second gaseous remainder and dried gas mixture are each enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in sulfur dioxide and water in relation to the starting gas mixture and also in relation to the first gaseous reminder. The fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide is at least in portion produced as or processed to a liquid fraction. Preferred Conditions: The first temperature level is 50-300 degrees C and the second temperature level and the third temperature level each are 0-20 degrees C. The first pressure level is 0-1 bar and the second pressure level is 20-30 bar.

TI Pond water diversion system based on carbon, iron and light regulation comprises blower device, carbon dioxide absorbing device, aeration device, filling device, dosing device and pH monitoring device.
AU LI M
   AN M
   MA N
   WU H
   YE S
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)

GA 201962911C
AB    NOVELTY - Pond water diversion system based on carbon, iron and light regulation comprises blower device, carbon dioxide absorbing device, aeration device, filling device, dosing device and pH monitoring device, one end of the carbon dioxide absorbing device and blower device is located on the pond bank, the other end of the carbon dioxide absorbing device is connected with the aeration device set at the bottom of the pond, the lighting device is vertically set at the center of the pond, the pH monitoring device is fixed on the lighting device, the dosing device is located on the pond bank, where the medicine filled in the dosing device is a ferric chloride solution.
   USE - Used as pond water regulating system based on carbon, iron and light regulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system removes carbon dioxide and aeration device by using carbon dioxide absorption device to perform the supplementation of aeration and light-filling device, regulates carbon, iron and light without destroying the growth of the fish, inhibits growth of harmful algae, promotes growth of beneficial algae, improves water quality of fish ponds, improves quality of farmed aquatic products, and reduces cost of breeding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for transferring water to pond by using pond water diversion system based on carbon, iron and light regulation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view of pond water regulating system based on carbon, iron and light regulation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component for absorbing carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide absorbing device is a strong alkali solution, where the strong alkali solution comprises sodium hydroxide solution or potassium hydroxide solution. The ferric chloride solution is a supersaturated solution.

PT P
PN CN109422276-A; CN109422276-B
TI Molecular sieve doped with a transition metal,            useful for the separation of carbon dioxide and methane            from a mixture of carbon dioxide and methane.
AU WANG S
   KE Q
   SUN T
   GU Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201923369U
AB    NOVELTY - Molecular sieve doped with a transition metal,                where the molecular sieve has a RHO molecular sieve                configuration, and is determined by nuclear                magnetic resonance spectroscopy and inductively                coupled plasma spectrometry. The molecular sieve                comprises a chemical composition of the following                molar ratio X2O3: aYO2: bZO: cMO, and X is a                trivalent element, the Y is a tetravalent element,                the Z is a divalent transition metal element and                the M is a monovalent or multivalent cation and                X-ray in the diffraction measurement, the molecular                sieve has a characteristic peak having at least                four interplanar spacings (d).
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful for the                separation of carbon dioxide and methane from a                mixture of carbon dioxide and methane, the                separation of carbon dioxide and nitrogen from a                mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, the                separation of methane and nitrogen from a mixture                of methane and nitrogen and as molecular sieve                catalyst for tail gas treatment (all claimed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the transition metal-doped molecular                sieve, comprising taking at least a polyvalent                crown ether, cesium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                transition metal nitrate, acetate or oxalate, a raw                material of a trivalent element compound and a                tetravalent element compound, carrying out                hydrothermal synthesis reaction, obtaining a                precursor of the molecular sieve, removing the                organic substance in the precursor of the molecular                sieve, introducing a monovalent or multivalent                element cation M.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Y is a tetravalent                  element and comprises silicon, Ge, tin and/or                  titanium elements, the X is a trivalent element                  and comprises atleast one boron, aluminum, Ga,                  iron or indium elements, the Z is a divalent                  transition metal element and comprises magnesium,                  manganese, zinc, iron, nickel, chromium, cobalt                  or copper elements, the M is a monovalent or                  multivalent cation, and comprises one of a                  valence ion of hydrogen, lithium, sodium, cesium,                  potassium, silver, magnesium, manganese, zinc,                  iron, nickel, copper, chromium, cobalt and/or                  ammonium ions. The Y is silicon. The X is                  aluminum and boron, preferably aluminum. The M is                  preferably hydrogen ion, ammonium ion or copper                  ion. The polyvalent crown ether is a mixture of                  18-crown-6 and other crown ethers, the other                  crown ether comprises one or a mixture of                  12-crown ether-4, 15-crown-5, 18-crown-6 or                  24-crown-8 and the mass ratio of 18-crown-6 in                  the crown ether mixture is 50-100%, preferably                  80-95%. The raw material compound of the                  tetravalent element Y includes an oxide, a                  chloride, a sulfide or a metal salt of silicon,                  Ge, tin or titanium and/or other tetravalent                  elements. The raw material compound of the                  trivalent element X includes oxides, hydroxides,                  chlorides or metal salts of boron, aluminum, Ga,                  iron, indium and/or other trivalent elements. The                  raw material compound of the divalent transition                  metal element Z comprises a simple substance, an                  oxide, a hydroxide, a chloride or a transition                  metal element of magnesium, manganese, zinc,                  iron, nickel, chromium, cobalt, copper and other                  transition metals, the metal salts, the manganese                  ion-containing cation solution of the step (d)                  containing a monovalent cation solution, a                  divalent cation solution or a trivalent cation                  solution. The monovalent cation source includes                  hydrogen ion, lithium ion, sodium ion, potassium                  ion, cesium ion, silver ion or ammonium ions,                  sulfides, hydroxides, chlorides, nitrates,                  sulfates, acetate or oxalate, the divalent cation                  source includes oxides, sulfides, etc. of                  magnesium ion, zinc ion, iron ion, nickel ion,                  copper ion, Sr2+, calcium ion, beryllium ion,                  hydroxide, chloride, nitrate, sulfate, acetate                  and/or oxalate, the trivalent cation source                  includes oxides, sulfides, hydroxides, chlorides,                  nitrates, sulfates, acetates, oxalates in iron                  ion, chromium ion and cobalt ion plasmas mixture.                  Preferred Method: The method comprises adding a                  polyvalent crown ether, sodium hydroxide or                  barium hydroxide in a molar ratio of                  (1.0-2.0):(0.5-1.5):1:(12-30) in the reaction                  vessel, heating deionized wate, stirring to                  dissolve, mixing uniformly, taking the                  supernatant liquid, adding the divalent                  transition metal element Z, the trivalent element                  X and the tetravalent element Y in the                  composition of the above molecular sieve                  according to the molar ratio of oxide of                  X2O3:YO2: ZO =1:20-25:0.1-0.2 raw material                  compound, aging, obtaining an initial gel,                  synthesizing the precursor of the molecular sieve                  hydrothermally and after transfer to the reaction                  vessel, filtering, washing, drying, and then                  heating and activating the molecular sieve                  precursor in the molecular sieve precursor after                  the completion of the reaction to obtain a                  calcined substrate, mixing the calcined with the                  cation solution containing manganese in a mass                  ratio of 1:(2-10), where the mass concentration                  of the solution is 10-80%, ion exchanging the                  calcined substrate and the cation solution for                  1-10 times at 50-100 degrees C, where the each                  ion exchange time is 1 hour, calcining the                  ion-exchanged molecular sieve in air at 400-600                  degrees C for 3-6 hours, cooling after completion                  of the calcination, taking out and placing in a                  drying dish for storage. The synthesis method is                  static heating or rotary heating. The reaction                  temperature of hydrothermal synthesis is 90-300                  degrees C and the reaction time is more than                  48-720 hours. The synthesis method is preferably                  rotary heating, the reaction temperature of the                  hydrothermal synthesis is 120-250 degrees C,                  preferably 140-180 degrees C, the reaction time                  is 72-240 hours, preferably 120-144 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids.    ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple.

The                  feeding mode of raw material gas (car bon dioxide                  and hydrogen) adopts continuous feeding,                  continuously reacts in catalyst bed, and the gas                  product and liquid product that the reaction                  produces are continuous discharge, collecting                  high-boiling heavy components with a hot tank                  connected to the reactor, collect low-boiling                  light components with a cold tank connected to                  the hot tank, the heating temperature of the hot                  tank is maintained at 373-403 K, and the cooling                  tank temperature was maintained at                  273-283K.

AE UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)

   SHANDONG QIHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201953883G
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioning method for preserving fresh fruits and vegetables while controlling damage caused by Penicillium involves adding the fruits and vegetables into sealed container, replacing gas in sealed container with a certain volume ratio of oxygen/carbon dioxide mixed gas, where volume of oxygen is 70% or more with respect to volume fraction of mixed gas, and remaining carbon dioxide, and sealing the container after inflation.

PN WO2019103585-A1; CA3082981-A1; CN111491892-A; EP3713873-A1; US2021261412-A1; US11383980-B2; RU2776054-C2; RU2020120430-A
TI Decomposition of calcium sulfide into calcium            oxide and sulfur dioxide, involves providing reaction            comprising calcium sulfide and source of carbon,            oxidizing source of carbon, and reacting calcium            sulfide with carbon dioxide.
AU DHIBA D
   OMARI L
   CHAOUKI J
   MOHADDESPOUR A
   CHOVKI YA
   MOKHADDESPUR A
AE OCP SA (OCPO-Non-standard)
   OCP SA (OCPO-Non-standard)
   OCP SA (OCPO-Non-standard)
   OCP SA (OCPO-Non-standard)
GA 2019483958
AB    NOVELTY - Decomposition of calcium sulfide into calcium                oxide and sulfur dioxide involves providing a                reactor comprising calcium sulfide and a source of                carbon, oxidizing the source of carbon so as to                generate carbon dioxide, reacting the calcium                sulfide with carbon dioxide to produce carbon                oxide, sulfur dioxide, and carbon monoxide. The                oxygen and carbon content in the oxidation process                is based on the mass ratio of carbon to calcium                sulfide which is 0.15-0.35, and the mass ratio of                oxygen to carbon which is 5-25.
   USE - Decomposition of calcium sulfide into calcium                oxide and sulfur dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor is a                  continuous reactor such as fluidized bed reactor                  or rotary kiln comprising inlet and outlet for                  flowing oxygen through the calcium sulfide and                  the source of carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  carbon to calcium sulfide is preferably 0.25, and                  the mass ratio of oxygen to carbon is preferably                  8. Preferred Condition: The oxidation process in                  which reactor is heated at temperature of                  900-1200 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of carbon                  comprises at least one of coal, coke, charcoal,                  and shale oil.

PT P
PN CN109576314-A; CN109576314-B
TI Preparing microalgae oil comprises adding microalgae medium and mixed microalgae seed solution into a photo-bioreactor, maintaining the pH of the culture system and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU SHI W
   WANG P
   LI X
   SUN Q
   ZHANG L
   FAN Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2019341511
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microalgae oil comprises adding microalgae medium and mixed microalgae seed solution into a photo-bioreactor, maintaining the pH of the culture system to 8-12 and introducing 5-45 vol.% carbon dioxide, where the mixed microalgae comprises Chlorella sp. SF-B1 (CGMCC No. 11005), Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 and/or Scenedesmus obliquus FSH-Y2.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microalgae oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the tolerance and solubility of microalgae culture system to the high concentration carbon dioxide, improves the carbon fixation efficiency and improves yield of microalgae grease and production of biodiesel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the photo-bioreactor, the pH of the culture system is maintained at 9-11 and the carbon dioxide having a volume content of 10-30 vol.% is introduced. The preparation method of Chlorella SFB1 seed liquid comprises adjusting the pH of the medium to 7-9, maintaining temperature to 10-30 degrees C for 24 hours and illumination intensity to 2000-20000 Lux and shaking the culture to a logarithmic growth phase, where the light-dark time ratio is 14:10. The preparation method of the seed liquid of Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 and Scenedesmus obliquus FSH-Y2 comprises adjusting the pH of the medium to 10-12, maintaining the temperature to 20-30 degrees C for 24 hours and the light intensity to 2000-10000 Lux and shaking the culture to logarithmic growth phase, where the light dark time ratio is 14:10. The temperature of the mixed culture is 10-30 degrees C, the photoperiod is 24 hours, the light-dark time ratio is 14:10, the light intensity is 2000-20000 Lux and the mixing culture until the end of the growth stable period. Preferred Components: The Chlorella SFB1 under the microscope is green, unicellular algae, solitary and the shape of the cells is spherical and elliptical with the pigment bodies having a diameter of 5-6 mu m. The microalgae medium uses BG11, SE or BBM to culture the liquid medium of the microalgae. The volume ratio of the Chlorella SF-B1 seed solution and the microalgae medium added into the reactor is 1:20-1:5. The volume ratio of the seed liquid and the microalgae medium of the Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 and/or Scenedesmus obliquus FSH-Y2 added into the photo-bioreactor is 1:20-1:5. The volume ratio of Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 and Scenedesmus obliquus FSHY2 seed solution when both microalgae are present is 5:1-1:5. The mixed culture gas is a gas or a flue gas containing carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide content is 5-45 vol.% and the NOx content is not greater than 800x 10-6 (vol./vol.).
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN RU2650399-C1
TI Method of obtaining extractive substances from giant hyssop anisic comprises supercritical carbon dioxide feed.
AU TYRKOV A G
   YURTAEVA E A
AE UNIV ASTRAKHAN (UYAS-Non-standard)
GA 201834820L
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the essential oil industry. Method of obtaining extractive substances includes grinding the leaves of giant hyssop anisic and its processing in ambient of above-critical carbon dioxide. Extraction is carried out for 120 minutes at a temperature of 60-62 degrees C and a pressure of 55 MPa, the supercritical carbon dioxide feed rate is 20 ml/min using an extractor with a 200 ml separator.
   USE - Essential oil industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to increase the yield of the desire product by 6% of the mass of air-dry raw materials without residual detrimental solvents and improve the coloration due to the appearance of a dark green color.

PN CA3105087-A1; US2021364214-A1
TI Device for applying carbon dioxide in solid form to target, has collection hopper defined by housing with bottom hopper opening below lower run of conveying component for receiving individual bodies of aggregated carbon dioxide snow released from receptacles.
AU HENRY J
   HENRY J A
AE HENRY J (HENR-Individual)
   HENRY J A (HENR-Individual)
GA 202180001D
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a housing (24) defining an enclosed chamber (25). An inlet duct (17) is in communication with the top of the housing at a longitudinally intermediate location between the rotational axes of the conveying component (37). Multiple receptacles are supported on the endless component (38) for rotation in a first rotational direction so as to move across the inlet duct for receiving the carbon dioxide in the snow phase to form multiple individual bodies of aggregated carbon dioxide snow. A collection hopper (21) is defined by the housing with a bottom hopper opening (63) below the lower run of the conveying component for receiving the individual bodies of aggregated carbon dioxide snow released from the receptacles and subsequently releasing individual bodies of aggregated carbon dioxide snow for subsequent conveyance to the target.
   USE - Device for applying carbon dioxide in solid form to target such as fire.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides a simple arrangement for forming individual solid bodies of carbon dioxide which requires minimal input power. The device is very simple. The snow production chamber is in the form of an elongated duct which is longitudinally elongated in the longitudinal direction of the conveying component so as to be arranged in parallel adjacent relation, thus allowing the device to occupy less space overall. All of the oxygen injectors of the device includes valves which are fluidically communicated with an external environment of the housing to introduce ambient air having oxygen into the housing, which simplifies the device so as not to rely on a dedicated supply of oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of device for applying carbon dioxide in solid form to target.
   Device for applying carbon dioxide in solid form to target (10)
   Inlet duct (17)
   Collection hopper (21)
   Housing (24)
   Enclosed chamber (25)
   Conveying component (37)
   Endless component (38)
   Bottom hopper opening (63)

PT P
PN US2021095907-A1; CA3094688-A1; US11047606-B2
TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system, has controller for obtaining temperature value of carbon dioxide output by condenser, determining whether condenser is operating properly using temperature value of carbon dioxide and obtaining values of input current and input voltage provided to set of fans.
AU KRISHNAMOORTHY N K
   GREINER P J
AE HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
   HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
GA 202132597V
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a condenser (2) for cooling carbon dioxide. A set of fans (30) affects cooling operations of the condenser. A controller (202) obtains a temperature value of carbon dioxide output by the condenser, determines whether the condenser is operating properly using the temperature value of the carbon dioxide and obtains values of input current and input voltage provided to the set of fans. The controller determines a set of different current thresholds using a model that relates input current to input voltage and determine a number of in-operational or faulty fans of the set of fans using the set of different current thresholds and values of the input current.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides failure mode of condenser and troubleshooting information to improve technician's ability to determine cause of failure of condenser and reduce servicing time by providing technician with the mode or cause of failure of condenser. The controller facilitates notifying the technician regarding the failure mode of condenser, so that pressure within a receiving tank is regulated by gas bypass valve to a pressure of approximately bar. The system facilitates use of copper tubing/piping for the downstream carbon dioxide lines of the system. The controller can determine a number of in-operational or faulty fans of the multiple fans using values of the input current and the input voltage and a model that relates input current to input voltage for known numbers of in-operational or faulty fans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a controller for operating and diagnosing a condenser;
   (2) a method for controlling and diagnosing a condenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide refrigeration system.
   Condenser (2)
   Fans (30)
   Carbon dioxide refrigeration system (100)
   Diagnostics system (200)
   Controller (202)

PN CN111658932-A
TI Respirator used for spontaneous breathing provocation test, has fourth and third pipelines whose proximal ends are provided with carbon dioxide concentration monitoring device to detect carbon dioxide content of inhaled and exhaled gases.
AU ZHU Z
   YU W
   WANG Y
   GUO X
   CHEN M
AE NANJING DRUM TOWER HOSPITAL (GLYY-C)
GA 2020922516
AB    NOVELTY - The respirator has a first gas source (1) and a second gas source (2) that respectively provide carbon dioxide and oxygen. The first gas source is connected to a first pipeline (8). The second air source is connected to a second pipeline (9). The first pipeline and the second pipeline are respectively connected to a gas mixing controller (5). The output end of a breathing controller (7) is connected to the tracheal passage of patient through a fourth pipeline (11). The carbon dioxide and oxygen in the first gas source and the second gas source are mixed in a certain proportion through the gas mixing controller, and adjusts the parameters through the breathing controller, and finally passes into the trachea of patient. The proximal end of a fourth pipeline and a third pipeline (10) are respectively provided with a carbon dioxide concentration monitoring device (6) for simultaneously detecting the carbon dioxide content of the patient's inhaled gas and exhaled gas.
   USE - Respirator used for spontaneous breathing provocation test used in treatment of severe brain injury, cerebral hemorrhage, cerebral infarction and other severe brain injuries.
   ADVANTAGE - The respirator changes the waiting for the patient to slowly produce carbon dioxide in the existing conventional test, to the precise control of the mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen from the outside and continuous mechanical ventilation. The carbon dioxide in the body can reach the level required by the test, and the patient is well tolerated, without multiple blood tests, the test success rate is high, and the promotion prospects are broad.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural schematic diagram of the respirator. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   First gas source (1)
   Second gas source (2)
   Gas mixing controller (5)
   Carbon dioxide concentration monitoring device (6)
   Breathing controller (7)
   First pipeline (8)
   Second pipeline (9)
   Third pipeline (10)
   Fourth pipeline (11)
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment)

PT P
PN CN110144343-A; CN110144343-B
TI Preparing mesoporous silicon-based material immobilized carbonic anhydrase for the preparation of a carbon dioxide absorber for capturing carbon dioxide in a flue gas, comprises adding aqueous solution of carbonic anhydrase to a suspension.
AU SHAO P
   DU M
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201974005Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesoporous silicon-based material immobilized carbonic anhydrase for the preparation of a carbon dioxide absorber for capturing carbon dioxide in a flue gas, comprises adding 4-10 mg/mL aqueous solution of carbonic anhydrase to a suspension of 6-8 mg/mL mesoporous silicon-based material, and stirring at 25-30 degrees C for 1 hour. After the reaction is completed, the reaction mixture is obtained by centrifugation, and the precipitate is washed with deionized water 2-3 times to obtain a mesoporous silicon-based material fixed carbonic anhydrase. The carbonic anhydrase aqueous solution is prepared by dissolving carbonic anhydrase in deionized water. The mesoporous silicon-based material suspension is prepared by uniformly suspending a mesoporous silicon-based material in deionized water. The ratio of the mesoporous silicon-based material suspension mesoporous silicon-based material to the carbonic anhydrase is 1:0.2-0.6.
   USE - Method for preparing mesoporous silicon-based material immobilized carbonic anhydrase for the preparation of a carbon dioxide absorber for capturing carbon dioxide in a flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare mesoporous silicon-based material immobilized carbonic anhydrase, which enhance the absorption rate of carbon dioxide in the potassium carbonate solution, so that it is applied to strengthen the integrated vacuum carbonate absorption process to capture carbon dioxide in the flue gas, and increase the carbon dioxide absorption rate under the same conditions. The rate is 1.57 times higher than the pure 2% potassium carbonate solution, which significantly improves the economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for the application of mesoporous silicon-based material, which involves mixing a mesoporous silicon-based material with carbonic anhydrase and potassium carbonate and water to obtain a carbon dioxide absorbent, where the absorbent, the mass concentration of the potassium carbonate is 2% or 20%, and the mass concentration of the mesoporous silicon-based material fixed carbonic anhydrase is 1 mg/mL.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mesoporous silicon-based material is MCM-41, SBA-15 or KIT-6, preferably SBA-15. The mesoporous silicon-based material suspension has a ratio of the mesoporous silicon-based material to the carbonic anhydrase of 1:0.4. The centrifugal process has a rotational speed of 12,000 rotation/minute and a time of 6-7 minute.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC D05-A01A5; D05-A01B4; D05-A01C2; D05-A04; J01-E02B; J01-E02H2
IP B01D-053/14; C12N-011/14

PT P
PN CN114322237-A
TI Method for purifying air in indoor by using air conditioner, involves sending rotating driving instruction to control rotary drive mechanism drive filter cores to rotate preset angle such that filter cores in air cavity exchange position.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHANG F
   LEI H
AE HISENSE SHANDONG AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (HISG-C)
GA 202256638J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor carbon dioxide concentration (S1). A first filter core and a second filter core are controlled (S2) to absorb the carbon dioxide in a room when the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is greater than a preset carbon dioxide concentration threshold value. A control heating unit is started (S3) for heating the first filter core located in a first air cavity when the temperature of the first filter core reaches the preset temperature threshold. A rotating driving instruction is sent (S4) to control a rotary drive mechanism to drive the first filter core and the second filter core to rotate at preset angle when the indoor carbon dioxide concentration difference before and after releasing is less than or equal to the preset concentration such that the first filter core and the second filter core in the second air cavity exchange a position.
   USE - Method for purifying air in an indoor by using an air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables driving the filter core to exchange position according to the rotary driving instruction, so that the first filter core and the second filter core alternately absorb and release carbon dioxide, thus realizing repeated utilization of the filter unit and improving continuous and efficient operation of the air purifying device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an air purifying device; (2) a computer readable storage medium for purifying air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrsting an air purification method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Obtaining indoor carbon dioxide concentration (S1)
   Controlling first filter core and a second filter core to absorb the carbon dioxide in a room when the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is greater than a preset carbon dioxide concentration threshold value (S2)
   Starting control heating unit (S3)
   Sending rotating driving instruction (S4)
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC Q74-T20; Q74-T09; Q74-A02B; T01-S03; T01-J08A
IP F24F-011/39; F24F-011/54; F24F-011/61; F24F-011/64; F24F-011/77; F24F-110/70; F24F-140/20

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide corrosion fatigue test device for use in e.g. petrochemical industry, has control system for controlling carbon dioxide test loop, fatigue testing machine and high pressure kettle to realize corrosion fatigue test of material in super critical carbon dioxide environment.
AU ZHOU R
   NING N
   HOU S
   HE X
   LIU X
   WANG B
   TANG L
   LI J
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C4567T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a supercritical carbon dioxide test loop connected with a high pressure kettle inlet and an outlet. A sample (29) is installed on a sample table in a high-pressure kettle (24) through a clamp (30). A fatigue testing machine loads a sample through a stretching shaft (31). The fatigue test machine loads the sample through the stretching shaft to realize fatigue loading of the sample. A control system controls the supercritical and fatigue testing loop, the fatigue loading machine and the high pressure kettle to realize corrosion fatigue test of material in a super critical carbon dioxide environment. The high pressure kettle is made of high temperature alloy material.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide (S-CO2) corrosion fatigue test device for use in energy source power, petrochemical industry and new combustion engine, fourth generation nuclear power, thermal power and solar generating set.
   ADVANTAGE - The control system controls the supercritical carbon dioxide test loop, fatigue testing machine and autoclave, so as to realize the corrosion fatigue test of material in the carbon dioxide environment, thus realizing long-term stable operation of the device. The device is provided with over-temperature, overpressure automatic protection function and power-off protection function, thus ensuring the device to be stable and reliable for a long time. The gas component in the system can be monitored in real time, and the tail gas recycling device can be provided with good environmental protection, thus saving the device purchase cost, and providing accurate and reliable result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a supercritical carbon dioxide corrosion fatigue testing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic digram of a supercritical carbon dioxide corrosion fatigue test device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

GA 2021182319
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-matched intermittent methane cracking                hydrogen production coupled carbon monoxide-carbon                dioxide enrichment reaction comprises introducing                methane into catalyst-filled methane catalytic                cracking reactor through reaction gas inlet                pipeline, performing methane cracking reaction                under 0.1-3 MPa at 300-900&#176; C, purging                with nitrogen gas, introducing different gasses and                performing carbon elimination reaction according to                amount of energy available.
   USE - Energy-matched intermittent methane cracking                hydrogen production coupled carbon monoxide-carbon                dioxide enrichment reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method completes decarbonization reaction                to obtain pure carbon monoxide, enriches carbon                dioxide and has less energy consumption.

The introduction of oxygen                  gas for carbon elimination reaction comprises                  closing methane gas regulating valve, carbon                  dioxide gas regulating valve, nitrogen gas                  regulating valve, hydrogen gas regulating valve,                  carbon monoxide gas regulating valve and exhaust                  gas regulating valve and opening oxygen gas                  regulating valve and carbon dioxide exhaust gas                  regulating valve.

   USE - The highly selective flue gas desulfurization absorbent is useful for absorption of sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide in flue gas including coal-fired power plant flue gas, natural gas power plant flue gas, cement kiln gas and fluid catalytic cracking flue gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The flue gas desulfurization absorbent has extremely high selective desulfurization efficiency, can selectively absorb more than 85-95% of sulfur dioxide in the flue gas, and absorb less carbon dioxide or even not absorb carbon dioxide.

The concentration of amine in the desulfurization absorbent is 20-25 wt.%.

PT P
PN CN110982992-A
TI Ruhrstahl-Heraeus vacuum decarbonization involves            filling ladle with molten steel in refining station,            vacuumizing, blowing-in inert gas and carbon dioxide            gas in turns into rising pipe and ladle bottom, and            breaking vacuum.
AU LIU B
   LI H
   ZHU G
   CHEN B
   DENG X
   SHAO X
   ZHANG H
   JI C
   LIU G
   LUO Y
   DONG W
   YIN N
AE SHOUGANG GROUP CO LTD (SHGG-C)
GA 202031415R
AB    NOVELTY - Ruhrstahl-Heraeus (RH) vacuum decarbonization                involves filling a ladle with molten steel in an RH                refining station, vacuumizing, blowing-in an inert                gas into the rising pipe, stopping inert gas into                the rising pipe, blowing carbon dioxide gas into                the rising pipe at 1100-2600 L/minute, while                blowing carbon dioxide gas to the bottom of the                steel ladle at 100-1000 L/minute, stopping blowing                in of carbon dioxide gas into the rising pipe and                steel ladle bottom, blowing inert gas into the                rising pipe, stopping blowing in of inert gas into                the rising pipe, and breaking the vacuum to obtain                qualified molten steel.
   USE - RH vacuum decarbonization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is quick, efficient and                economical.

PN KR2020016565-A; KR2176439-B1
TI Amine adsorbent useful in carbon dioxide capture process for inhibiting generation of secondary amine, comprises a porous support and metal ions of alkali or alkaline earth metals.
AU YUN Y I
   IL-SU C
   JEONG J Y
   HO C J
   HEE Y S
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202015123Q
AB    NOVELTY - Amine adsorbent comprises a porous support and metal ions of alkali or alkaline earth metals.
   USE - The amine adsorbent is useful in carbon dioxide capture process for inhibiting generation of secondary amine.
   ADVANTAGE - The amine adsorbent inhibits generation of secondary amine which is a starting material of toxic substances, preferably nitrosamine in the carbon dioxide capture process, improves performance of filter by simultaneously removing various harmful gases, extends filter replacement cycle, and captures high purity carbon dioxide by removing amine with excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing amine adsorbent, comprising (a) preparing metal salt solution in which metal ion precursor is dissolved, (b) impregnating the porous support in the metal salt solution, and (c) drying the impregnated porous support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amine adsorbent comprises 0.78-2.44 wt.% metal ions. The amine adsorbent has pore size of 400-600 Angstrom , specific surface area of 1130-1620 m2/g. Preferred Components: The porous support is made of activated carbon, zeolite, silica gel, activated alumina, carbon molecular sieve, activated carbon fiber or metal-organic framework (MOF). The metal ion is potassium or calcium ion. The concentration of the metal salt solution in the step (a) is 0.5-2 M. Preferred Method: The step (c) is carried out by drying the impregnated porous support at 90 degrees C, and speed of 10-20 revolutions/minute under vacuum of 150-200 torr.
   EXAMPLE - Porous support was impregnated in potassium precursor metal salt solution (produced by dissolving potassium nitrate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium hydroxide, potassium chloride, or potassium dihydrogen phosphate in water at concentration of 0.5-2 M (mol/l)) and calcium precursor metal salt solution (produced by dissolving calcium nitrate, calcium chloride or calcium phosphate in water at concentration of 0.5-2 M (mol/l)) with different impregnation amounts for greater than or equal to 1 hour, and dried at 90 degrees C and speed of 10-20 revolutions/minute under vacuum of 150-200 torr for 4 hours to obtain amine adsorbent.

TI Processing salt lake brine using serpentine involves mixing pre-activated treated serpentine powder into salt lake brine, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting, filtering, and transferring serpentine residue with strong alkali silica.
AU ZHU P
   LI Z
   WANG Y
   QIAN G
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 201915892P
AB    NOVELTY - Processing salt lake brine using serpentine involves putting pre-activated treated serpentine powder into salt lake brine having mass ratio of magnesium to lithium of not less than 24.3:1, mixing, introducing carbon dioxide into salt lake brine mixture to form serpentine-lake brine-carbon dioxide reactant system solution, reacting, filtering solution when reaction reaches equilibrium, removing serpentine residue silica with strong alkali, adding remaining solid in filter liquid, continuously introducing carbon dioxide into filtrate, continuously reacting remaining solid, adjusting pH value of filtrate, and controlling pH value to precipitate magnesium carbonate.
   USE - Method for processing salt lake brine using serpentine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption and cost, and reduces cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves:(A) crushing serpentine, ball milling for 1-5 hour at speed of 300 revolutions/minute (rpm), sieving through 100-300 meshes sieve to obtain pre-milled serpentine powder or crushing serpentine, ball milling to obtain serpentine powder, and calcining at 500-800 degrees C for 15-240 minutes to obtain calcined pre-activated treated serpentine powder;(B) mixing serpentine powder with salt lake brine at solid-liquid mass to volume ratio of 0.1:10 (g/mL) to 1:1 (g/mL), adding to high pressure reactor, blowing carbon dioxide, controlling equilibrium pressure of carbon dioxide at 0.1-100 MPa, reacting at 10-80 degrees C for 30-500 minutes, stirring at speed of 100-500 revolutions/minute, controlling pH to 5-12, and mineralization coupling reacting to precipitate magnesium carbonate; and(C) filtering, collecting intercepted serpentine residue, collecting brine filtrate, remove serpentine residue silica with alkali solution, neutralization reacting at 10-100 degrees C for 30-100 minutes, adding remaining serpentine solid into brine filtrate to form reaction mixed liquid, blowing carbon dioxide, continuously reacting remaining serpentine solid, controlling pH value to 5-12, and continuously precipitating magnesium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali solution is sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide.

PN CN109779582-A
TI Method for in-situ extracting hydrocarbon compounds, involves drilling heating well and production well in circled oil shale mining area, followed by re-injecting gas separated into ground for heating by oil-water separation system.
AU SUN Y
   KANG S
   LI Q
   GUO W
   DENG S
   ZHAO S
   LIU Z
   YANG Q
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 201948202G
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon compounds in situ extracting method involves drilling heating well and production well in the circled oil shale mining area, fracturing the oil shale formation, pumping the well, and installing a blowout preventer, injecting carbon dioxide into the well, and discharging the air, passing the discharged gas into a sodium hydroxide solution tank, and absorbing the carbon dioxide to discharge the air, closing the production well, injecting carbon dioxide through the heating well, and closing the heating well, heating the injected carbon dioxide, cracking the kerogen in the oil shale into small molecular asphalt, raising the carbon dioxide, oil shale oil and oil shale gas mixture to the ground through the production well to obtain the hydrocarbon compound by the oil-water separation system, re-injecting the gas separated into the ground for heating by the oil-water separation system in the next well to absorb carbon dioxide to obtain an organic small molecule gas product.
   USE - Method for in-situ extracting hydrocarbon compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrocarbon compounds in situ extracting method involves drilling heating well and production well in the circled oil shale mining area, fracturing the oil shale formation between the heating well and the production well to increasing the permeability and connectivity of the oil shale formation, pumping the well, lowering the downhole heater and packer to the oil shale layer, and installing a blowout preventer, continuously injecting a certain pressure of normal temperature carbon dioxide into the well through the heating well, and discharging the air in the well and the oil shale layer through the production well, passing the discharged gas into a sodium hydroxide solution tank, and absorbing the carbon dioxide to discharge the air, closing the production well, injecting carbon dioxide through the heating well, and closing the heating well to reaching the predetermined pressure, heating the injected carbon dioxide by a downhole heater to achieve a supercritical environment and keeping it for a certain period of time, cracking the kerogen in the oil shale into small molecular asphalt and detaching from the oil shale matrix under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide, raising the carbon dioxide, oil shale oil and oil shale gas mixture to the ground through the production well to obtain the hydrocarbon compound by the oil-water separation system, re-injecting the gas separated into the ground for heating by the oil-water separation system in the next well or by passing through a sodium hydroxide solution tank to absorb carbon dioxide to obtain an organic small molecule gas product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon compound comprises aliphatic hydrocarbon, alicyclic hydrocarbon, aromatic hydrocarbon or hetero atom compound. The organic small molecule gas product comprises alkane having 1-6 carbon, olefin having 2-6 carbon, hydrogen sulfide or hydrogen.

PT P
PN CN112938973-A; CN112938973-B
TI Preparing special activated carbon for pressure swing adsorption with high separation coefficient, involves pulverizing coal, and then adding coal tar to coal powder and placing into kneader for kneading to form coal paste.
AU ZHOU D
   WANG J
   CAO X
   DU W
AE HUAIBEI DAHUA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2021733773
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing special activated carbon involves pulverizing 60-80 pts. wt. coal to 200 mesh with a pass rate of 90%, adding 20-40 pts. wt. coal tar to the coal powder and placing into the kneader for kneading to form coal paste, adding 10-15 pts. wt. composite catalyst aqueous solution during the kneading process, where the composite catalyst aqueous solution comprises 2-3 pts. wt. catalyst A coated on the surface of the carbon column, 1-1.5 pts. wt. catalyst that can form effective adsorption pores for carbon dioxide and methane B is directly dissolved in water and kneaded for 10 minutes to make the coal paste fully absorb the composite catalyst aqueous solution, placed into the hydraulic press to form a cylindrical carbon column of F1.5-3 millimeter, the charcoal column be air-dried naturally until the moisture content of 7%.
   USE - Method for preparing special activated carbon used for pressure swing adsorption with high separation coefficient.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide/nitrogen, methane/nitrogen separation coefficient, methane adsorption amount and reduces nitrogen adsorption amount.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing special activated carbon involves pulverizing 60-80 pts. wt. coal to 200 mesh with a pass rate of 90%, adding 20-40 pts. wt. coal tar to the coal powder and placing into the kneader for kneading to form coal paste, adding 10-15 pts. wt. composite catalyst aqueous solution during the kneading process, where the composite catalyst aqueous solution comprises 2-3 pts. wt. catalyst A coated on the surface of the carbon column, 1-1.5 pts. wt. catalyst that can form effective adsorption pores for carbon dioxide and methane B is directly dissolved in water and kneaded for 10 minutes to make the coal paste fully absorb the composite catalyst aqueous solution, placed into the hydraulic press to form a cylindrical carbon column of F1.5-3 millimeter, the charcoal column be air-dried naturally until the moisture content of 7%, placed the dried carbon column into the carbonization furnace for carbonization, the carbonization temperature is 450-500 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 40-50 minutes. The carbon column is heated, and the catalyst A on the surface of the carbon column loses water quickly. The surface of the carbon column is covered with a film. The film makes the activity of water molecules inside the carbon column more intense. The activity of water molecules forms a channel of carbon structure inside the carbon column, and the remaining film structure limits the increase in the amount of nitrogen adsorption during the subsequent activation process, placed the carbonized carbon column into an activation furnace for activation. The activated gas is water vapor. The activation temperature is 850-950 degrees C. During activation, the water vapor pressure of 0.3 megapascal. The catalyst B is formed under the action of water vapor and temperature, the catalyst A continuously suppresses the amount of nitrogen adsorption, the activation time is 4-6 hours to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst A is sodium silicate and the catalyst B is a mixture of chloride, nitrate, and phosphate.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2020247378-A1; US2020388468-A1; TW202113900-A; CN113939891-A; KR2022016879-A; JP2022534997-W
TI Ion source assembly used in apparatus for            improving ion implantation performance, comprises ion            source chamber, source gas supply set to provide            molecular carbon source gas to ion source chamber and            has a source gas flow controller.
AU SPORLEDER D
   BASSOM N
   COLVIN N
   AMEEN M
   XU X
   COLVIN N K
   BASSON N
AE AXCELIS TECHNOLOGIES INC (AXCE-C)
   AXCELIS TECHNOLOGIES INC (AXCE-C)
   AXCELIS TECH INC (AXCE-C)
GA 2020C3017S
AB    NOVELTY - Ion source assembly comprises ion source                chamber, source gas supply configured to provide a                molecular carbon source gas to the ion source                chamber. A source gas flow controller is configured                to control a flow of the molecular carbon source                gas to the ion source chamber; an excitation source                is configured to excite the molecular carbon source                gas for forming carbon ions and radicals. An                extraction electrode is configured to extract the                carbon ions from the ion source chamber, for                forming an ion beam; an oxidizing co-gas supply is                configured to provide an oxidizing co-gas to the                ion source chamber; and an oxidizing co-gas flow                controller configured to control a flow of the                oxidizing co gas to the ion source chamber. The                oxidizing co-gas is configured to decompose within                the ion source chamber and react with carbonaceous                residues and atomic carbon from the molecular                carbon source gas in the ion source chamber.
   USE - Ion source assembly used in apparatus for                improving ion implantation performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The ion source assembly facilitate ion                implantation processes by removing carbonaceous                material or residue generated from the                disassociation and ionization of molecular carbon                source gasses which accumulates on structures                associated with the ion source, which leads to                poisoning of cathode and reduce the efficiency of                the cathode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) ion implantation system for improving                performance of an ion source in an ion implanter                comprises a molecular carbon source gas controller                that controls a supply and a rate of a molecular                carbon source gas (211) to an ion source chamber                (206); a co-gas controller (222) that controls a                supply and a rate of an oxidizing co-gas to the ion                source chamber, where the oxidizing co-gas reacts                with the molecular carbon source gas and                carbonaceous residue to form carbon dioxide, and                carbon monoxide; and a vacuum pump (234) coupled to                the ion source chamber, where the vacuum pump is                configured to remove the carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide from the ion source chamber, where chamber                poisoning is reduced and ion source lifetime is                increased; and
   (2) method of increasing ion source lifetime                in an ion implanter, involves introducing a                molecular carbon source gas into an ion source                chamber of the ion implanter; introducing an                oxidizing co-gas into the ion source chamber of the                ion implanter; exciting the molecular carbon source                gas within the ion source chamber to create a                plasma which includes disassociated and ionized                carbon; reacting the disassociated and ionized                carbon with the oxidizing co-gas, for producing one                or more of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and                reducing poisoning of the ion source chamber and                increasing ion source lifetime; and removing the                one or more of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                from the ion source chamber by a vacuum pump                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an ion                implantation system
   Ion source chamber (206)
   Molecular carbon source gas (211)
   Source Gas Mass Flow Controller (219)
   Co-gas controller (222)
   Vacuum pump (234)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molecular carbon                  source gas comprises toluene, cycloheptatriene,                  or p-xylene. The oxidizing co-gas comprises                  oxygen (O2), ozone (O3), nitrous oxide (N2O),                  nitrogen dioxide (NO2), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2),                  water vapor (H2O), or carbon dioxide (CO2). The                  source gas flow controller and oxidizing co-gas                  flow controller are configured to simultaneously                  introduce the respective molecular carbon source                  gas and the oxidizing co-gas into the ion source                  chamber. The source gas flow controller and                  oxidizing co-gas flow controller are configured                  to sequentially introduce the respective                  molecular carbon source gas and the oxidizing                  co-gas into the ion source chamber. The source                  gas flow controller and oxidizing co-gas flow                  controller are configured to pre-mix the                  respective molecular carbon source gas and the                  oxidizing co-gas prior to introduction into the                  ion source chamber. The molecular carbon source                  gas comprises an aromatic carbon molecule. The                  oxidizing co-gas is oxygen and a ratio of the                  oxidizing co-gas to the molecular carbon source                  gas is 1.0 and 4.0. The oxidizing co-gas is                  oxygen and a ratio of the oxidizing co-gas to the                  molecular carbon source gas is between 1.5 and                  3.5. The method further involves introducing an                  inert gas into the ion source chamber and                  ionizing the inert gas, for generally preventing                  a cathode surface associated with a cathode of                  the ion source chamber from oxidizing. The inert                  gas comprises argon.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110862844-A
TI Parallel production of hydrogen from gas-rich natural gas using low-grade coal comprises e.g. sending low-grade coal from coal preparation system, removing sulfur and carbon dioxide in rich gas and producing product natural gas.
AU PENG X
   YU T
   ZHANG H
   LV B
   JIN F
   LI J
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202020907W
AB    NOVELTY - Parallel production of hydrogen from gas-rich natural gas using low-grade coal comprises e.g. (i) sending low-grade coal from the coal preparation system prepared by the gasification reduction process to obtain upgraded coal, coal tar, and rich gas; (ii) removing the sulfur in the rich gas; (iii) converting all unsaturated hydrocarbons of the pre-desulfurized rich gas; (iv) removing sulfur in the hydrogenated rich gas to obtain a desulfurized rich gas; (v) removing the carbon dioxide in the desulfurized rich gas through a decarbonization process to obtain a methane synthesis gas; (vi) adopting a methane synthesis process with methane synthesis gas; and (vii) passing methane product stream into a liquefaction process, and the cryogenic liquefaction process is used to produce methane with a volume percentage of not less than 90%, and producing product natural gas in parallel to produce hydrogen with a volume percentage of not less than 90%.
   USE - The method is useful for parallel production of hydrogen from gas-rich natural gas using low-grade coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, utilizes fully volatile components in low-rank coal; and produces natural gas has fewer impurities and high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Parallel production of hydrogen from gas-rich natural gas using low-grade coal comprises (i) sending low-grade coal from the coal preparation system prepared by the gasification reduction process to obtain upgraded coal, coal tar, and rich gas, where the gas-rich component comprises 28-40 wt.% methane, 5-20 wt.% carbon monoxide, 25-40 wt.% hydrogen, 5-20 wt.% carbondioxide, 2-8 wt.% ethane, 1-4 wt.% ethylene, 0.5-3 wt.% propene, 0.4-2.5 wt.% propane, 0.2-2 wt.% Methylcyclopropane , 2000-6000ppm hydrogen sulfide and ammonia content is less than 100ppm; (ii) removing the sulfur in the rich gas through a pre-desulfurization process to obtain a pre-desulfurization and rich gas; (iii) converting all unsaturated hydrocarbons of the pre-desulfurized rich gas into corresponding saturated hydrocarbons through a hydrogenation process, and simultaneously converting organic sulfur into hydrogen sulfide to obtain a hydrogen rich gas; (iv) removing sulfur in the hydrogenated rich gas through a desulfurization process to obtain a desulfurized rich gas; (v) removing the carbon dioxide in the desulfurized rich gas through a decarbonization process to obtain a methane synthesis gas, so that the hydrogen-carbon ratio in the methane synthesis gas is (2.9-3.1): 1; (vi) adopting a methane synthesis process with methane synthesis gas at least 4 methanation reactors are connected in series, so that in the presence of a methanation catalyst, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the desulfurized rich gas react to synthesize methane to obtain methane product stream; and (vii) passing methane product stream into a liquefaction process, and the cryogenic liquefaction process is used to produce methane with a volume percentage of not less than 90%, and producing product natural gas in parallel to produce hydrogen with a volume percentage of not less than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises passing desulfurized and rich gas through a hydrogen-rich stream or a carbon monoxide-rich stream, and the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide is (3-10): 1. The pre-desulfurization process includes using a pre-desulfurization solution, passing rich gas from the lower part of the first desulfurization device, and comes into countercurrent contact with the pre-desulfurization solution sprayed from the upper part of the first desulfurization device and removing hydrogen sulfide in the rich gas, and the pre-desulfurization solution comprises at least one of a Palladium sulfide catalyst, methanol, or NDH solvent. In the pre-desulfurization process and / or desulfurization process taking electrode is provided, and a applying DC voltage exceeding 10KV to the electrode. The pre-desulfurization process includes the use of a filtering device which is loaded with an adsorption material or the pre-desulfurization catalyst. The method comprises removes particulate matter in the rich gas by a dust removal process before entering rich gas into pre-desulfurization process. The desulfurization process includes using a desulfurization liquid, passing hydrogenation-rich gas from a lower part of the third desulfurization device, and is in countercurrent contact with the desulfurization liquid sprayed from the upper part of the third desulfurization device, utilizing hydrogenation-rich gas for removing of hydrogen sulfide in the desulfurization solution containing NHD solvent. In the desulfurization process, introducing pressurized gas so that the pressure is 0.2-1.0 MPa, and maintaining temperature at 20-30 degrees C. The decarburization process includes using a decarburization liquid, passing hydrogenation-rich gas from a lower part of the decarburization device is in countercurrent contact with a decarburizing liquid sprayed from the upper part of the decarburizing device and removing carbon dioxide from the hydrogen rich gas, the decarburizing liquid containing NHD solvent. Preferred Components: The pre-desulfurization process includes a second desulfurization device using a resistance wire and a pre-desulfurization catalyst. The resistance wire includes nickel and chromium, pre-desulfurization catalyst includes at least one of basic copper carbonate, copper oxide, copper hydroxide, basic zinc carbonate, zinc oxide, zinc oxide, or zinc hydroxide, the rich gas is passed into the second desulfurization device, and the resistance wire heats the rich gas to 200-500 degrees C, so that hydrogen sulfide gas in the rich gas is decomposed to form elemental sulfur and filtering elemental sulfur to obtain the pre-desulfurized rich gas.

PN CN110839654-A
TI Composite nano-air purification material used for e.g. eliminating fatigue, comprises dimethyl fumarate, nano-titanium dioxide, nano-sweet wormwood, zinc oxide, nano-radix bupleuri, nano-mint, linalool, geranium oil and glass fiber.
AU LIN J
   LI C
AE NANTONG YIHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202020119X
AB    NOVELTY - Composite nano-air purification material comprises 12-32 wt.% dimethyl fumarate, 8-25 wt.% nano-titanium dioxide, 1-2 wt.% nano-sweet wormwood, 1-2 wt.% zinc oxide, 1-3 wt.% nano-radix bupleuri, 1-10 wt.% nano-mint, 10-16 wt.% linalool, 10-18 wt.% geranium oil and 6-15 wt.% glass fiber.
   USE - The composite nano-air purification material is useful for refreshing brain, balancing qi and blood, eliminating fatigue and enhancing memory.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite nano-air purification material inhibits harmful bacteria in air, resists pathogenic factor, prevents cross infection of viruses, settles and decomposes a variety of harmful gases such as formaldehyde and benzene in the newly decorated living room in a short time, reduces fatigue of long-time driver, volatilizes cleanly in the air without leaving any residue, has degradation and decomposition effects that cannot be achieved by other air-cleaning material, and high-efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing the composite nano-air purification material, involving (i) preparing by nano-sweet wormwood, zinc oxide, nano-radix bupleuri, nano-mint, linalool, geranium oil and glass fiber respectively by adopting existing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction technology which involves performing raw material extraction, filtering extraction liquid, separating particles, feeding to storage tank, supplementing fresh carbon dioxide, separating and discharging solute, sending to an atmospheric storage tank to send a finished product tank, where the equipment used in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction technology for preparing the above raw materials are all the same, and the main equipment comprises extraction kettle, separation kettle, rectification tower, carbon dioxide high-pressure pump, temperature controller and pressure controller, where the combined form of the equipment has one portion of the second extraction tower, (ii) preparing nano-titanium dioxide powder by adopting an existing laser gas phase synthesis method using equipment comprising synthesis reaction device, laser generation device, powder collection device, raw material evaporation device, gas supply device and vacuum device, (iii) preparing dimethyl fumarate through a direct esterification method of existing fumaric acid and methanol by washing fumaric acid, methanol and catalyst-reflux-cold water with 10% sodium carbonate solution, crystallizing and drying to obtain a finished product, where the weight ratio of methanol and fumaric acid is (6-10):1, and the catalyst comprises sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid or some novel catalyst, and (iv) weighing the prepared component materials, adding the prepared dimethyl fumarate to a stainless steel container with a stirrer to heat at 103-104 degrees C, uniformly stirring, adding the prepared nano-titanium dioxide in the side while stirring, and when the temperature is cooled to 70 degrees C, adding the nano-titanium dioxide together with the prepared nano-sweet wormwood, nano-radix lithospermi, nano-radix bupleuri and nano-mint while stirring, adding the prepared linalool, geranium oil and glass when the temperature is cooled to 50 degrees C, cooling and using a die or a tablet press to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite nano-air purification material comprises 15-30 wt.% dimethyl fumarate, 10-25 wt.% nano-titanium dioxide, 1-2 wt.% nano-sweet wormwood, 1-2 wt.% zinc oxide, 1-3 wt.% nano-radix bupleuri, 1-10 wt.% nano-mint, 10-16 wt.% linalool, 10-18 wt.% geranium oil and 6-15 wt.% glass fiber.
   ACTIVITY - CNS-Gen.; Muscular-Gen.; Nootropic.
