TI Controlling catalyst regeneration in catalyst regeneration zone, involves e.g. introducing oxygen containing gas, partially regenerating stream of spent catalyst having coke from methanol to olefin reaction zone, separating and recycling.
AU SENETAR J J
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2020127103
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling (P1) catalyst regeneration in a catalyst regeneration zone (14), comprises: (a) introducing an oxygen containing gas into a catalyst regeneration zone; (b) partially regenerating a stream of spent catalyst from an methanol to olefin (MTO) reaction zone (18), where the spent catalyst includes coke; (c) separating regenerated catalyst from a flue gas, where the regenerated catalyst has a reduced amount of coke and the flue gas comprises carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; (d) recycling a portion of the flue gas to the catalyst regeneration zone with the oxygen containing gas; and (e) maintaining a ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in the flue gas to be at least 0.5.
   USE - The processes are useful for controlling catalyst regeneration in catalyst regeneration zone and partially regenerating catalyst from MTO reaction zone.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: utilizes controlling a ratio of air to flue gas in the oxygen containing gas in order to achieve the partially regenerated catalyst by maintaining at least one of parameter comprising an amount of oxygen in the flue gas to be less than 2 vol.%, a ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in the flue gas to be at least 0.5, or an oxygen utilization in the catalyst regeneration zone of at least 90%; control regeneration of spent catalyst that achieve a regenerated catalyst; and partial regenerate catalyst that is desirable because of the improved selectivity towards light olefin production with a partially regenerated catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) controlling (P2) catalyst regeneration in a catalyst regeneration zone, comprising steps (a) and (b), (c1) separating regenerated catalyst from a flue gas, where the regenerated catalyst has a reduced amount of coke and the flue gas comprises oxygen, (d1) recycling a portion of the flue gas to the catalyst regeneration zone with the oxygen containing gas and (e1) maintaining an amount of oxygen in the flue gas to be less than 2 vol.%; and
   (2) partially regenerating (P3) catalyst from an MTO reaction zone, comprising passing a stream of oxygen containing gas into a catalyst regeneration zone, passing a stream of spent catalyst from an MTO reaction zone to the catalyst regeneration zone, where the spent catalyst includes coke, combusting coke off of the spent catalyst to provide a partially regenerated catalyst comprising 1-4 wt.% coke, recycling a portion of a flue gas stream to the catalyst regeneration zone as the oxygen containing gas, and controlling a ratio of air to flue gas in the oxygen containing gas in order to achieve the partially regenerated catalyst by maintaining at least one of parameter comprising an amount of oxygen in the flue gas to be less than 2 vol.%, a ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in the flue gas to be at least 0.5, or an oxygen utilization in the catalyst regeneration zone of at least 90%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a process flow diagram of an MTO reaction and catalyst regeneration.
   Spent catalyst stream (10)
   Oxygenate reaction zone (12)
   Regeneration zone (14)
   Regenerator (16)
   MTO reaction zone (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide is maintained by adjusting an oxygen content of the oxygen containing gas introduced into the catalyst regeneration zone. The oxygen content is adjusted by controlling a ratio of air to flue gas the in oxygen containing gas. The flue gas further comprises oxygen, where the process (P1) further comprises maintaining an amount of oxygen in the flue gas to be less than 2 vol.%. The amount of oxygen in the flue gas is maintained by adjusting an oxygen content of the oxygen containing gas introduced into the catalyst regeneration zone. The oxygen content is adjusted by controlling a ratio of air to flue gas in the oxygen containing gas. The flue gas further comprises carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where the process (P2) further comprises maintaining a ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in the flue gas to be at least 0.5. The process (P3) further comprises: adjusting a processing condition associated with the MTO reaction zone; adjusting the ratio of air to flue gas in the oxygen containing gas in response to the adjusted processing condition of the MTO reaction zone; at least one of sensing at least one parameter of the process and generating a signal or data from the sensing, generating and transmitting a signal or generating and transmitting data; receiving the signal or data; adjusting the ratio of air to flue gas in response to the received data or signal; and adjusting the ratio of air to flue gas in the oxygen containing gas in order to maintain a constant velocity of spent catalyst particles within a reactor of the catalyst regeneration zone. Preferred Components: The ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in the flue gas is maintained to be greater than 2. The regenerated catalyst comprises 1-4 wt.% coke.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU VOROBJOV F
   SOGAARD E G
AE UNIV AALBORG (UYAA-Non-standard)
GA 201874022E
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting cannabinoids from Cannabis plant                material, comprises: (a) providing a Cannabis plant                material including leaves and/or flowers with a                water content of at most 60 wt/wt.%, preferably at                most 10 wt/wt.%; (b1) subjecting the Cannabis plant                material to a first extraction step (in a pressure                tank) with a one-phase solvent system comprising                supercritical carbon dioxide and optionally a                non-polar co-solvent, at 30-60 degrees C; (c1)                collecting a first liquid extract from the pressure                tank; (d) collecting/removing a solid product from                the pressure tank comprising Cannabis plant fiber                material with a reduced content of cannabinoids;                (e) dissolving the first liquid extract from the                pressure tank in ethanol, subjecting the solution                to at least one winterization step, filtrating the                solution to remove precipitated matter, and                removing the ethanol to collect a second liquid                extract; and (f) optionally heat treating second                liquid extract.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                cannabinoids from Cannabis plant material                (claimed), where the cannabinoids are useful as                antibiotics, antiinflammatory drugs, analgesics and                antispasticity drugs. No biological data                given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: allows for complete extraction of                cannabinoids from Cannabis plant material, thus                making it possible to utilize hemp leaves for                dietary supplements and/or animal feed; and                utilizes the carbon dioxide, which is stabile even                at high pressures and temperatures and can be                easily recovered at ambient conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting cannabinoids from Cannabis plant                material, comprises: (a) providing a Cannabis plant                material including leaves and/or flowers with a                water content of at most 60 wt/wt.%, preferably at                most 10 wt/wt.%; (b1) subjecting the Cannabis plant                material to a first extraction step (in a pressure                tank) with a one-phase solvent system comprising                supercritical carbon dioxide and optionally a                non-polar co-solvent, at 30-60 degrees C; (c1)                collecting a first liquid extract from the pressure                tank; (d) collecting/removing a solid product from                the pressure tank comprising Cannabis plant fiber                material with a reduced content of cannabinoids;                (e) dissolving the first liquid extract from the                pressure tank in ethanol, subjecting the solution                to at least one winterization step, filtrating the                solution to remove precipitated matter, and                removing the ethanol to collect a second liquid                extract; (f) optionally heat treating the second                liquid extract at 100-150 degrees C, preferably in                an inert atmosphere to decarboxylate any acid form                of the cannabinoids to the neutral form of the                cannabinoids; (g) dissolving the second liquid                extract, optionally heat treated, in a non-polar                solvent; (h) extracting the solubilized second                liquid extract with an aqueous basic solution, and                collecting the non-polar solvent phase as a third                liquid extract; and (i) optionally removing the                non-polar solvent to collect the neutral form of                the cannabinoids. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for Cannabis plant fiber material with a                reduced content of cannabinoids produced by the                process, where the cannabinoid content is 0.05% at                the most, and the pressure within the pressure tank                during the extraction steps are at least 400 bar or                at most 400 bar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous basic                  solution is an aqueous solution of an inorganic                  base comprising alkali metal hydroxides, alkali                  metal carbonates, alkaline earth metal hydroxides                  and/or alkaline earth metal carbonates.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises: (b2a) injecting a one-phase solvent                  system comprising supercritical carbon dioxide                  and optionally a non-polar co-solvent into the                  pressure tank, and subjecting the Cannabis plant                  material to a second extraction step at 30-60                  degrees C; or (b2b) in situ forming a one-phase                  solvent system within the pressure tank by                  injecting carbon dioxide and a co-solvent into                  the pressure tank, and subjecting the Cannabis                  plant material to a second extraction step at                  30-60 degrees C; (c2) collecting/removing a                  secondary first liquid extract from the pressure                  tank; and (c3) optionally, repeating the steps                  (b2) to (c2). The first and/or second and/or                  further extraction step is performed for at least                  30 minutes before collecting/removing a first                  liquid extract from the pressure tank. In the                  process, after performing the first extraction                  step, the injection of the carbon dioxide and the                  optional co-solvent into the pressure tank is                  performed simultaneously with collecting/removing                  a liquid extract from the pressure tank. The                  injection of the carbon dioxide and the optional                  co-solvent is performed into the bottom of the                  pressure tank, while the collection/removal of a                  liquid extract is performed in the top of the                  pressure tank. The pressure within the pressure                  tank during the extraction steps is at least 200                  bar. Preferred Components: The one-phase solvent                  system further comprises a co-solvent comprising                  pentane, cyclopentane, hexane, cyclohexane,                  heptane, octane, benzene and/or toluene.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Analgesic;                Muscular-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108149053-A
TI Preparing titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium alloy comprises e.g. blowing carbon dioxide, methane, argon, and oxygen mixed gas, melting titanium alloy, carrying out reaction, adding silver powder.
AU XU S
   SUN X
   QI Z
AE UNIV SHANDONG JIANZHU (SDSJ-C)
GA 201847329X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium alloy comprises e.g. blowing carbon dioxide, methane, argon, and oxygen mixed gas to titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium carbide particle precursor powder, melting titanium alloy, to obtain titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride reinforcing particles, carrying out reaction, adding silver powder to titanium alloy melt and copper powder, carrying out mechanized mixing, carrying out metamorphic treatment, refining, pouring, to obtain ingot through 105o corner with back pressure of equal channel angle extrusion deformation with high strength, to obtain nano titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium alloy material, where the titanium alloy substrate utilizes titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material, chemical composition of alloy includes 6.5-9.75% aluminum, 1.5-2.5% vanadium, and titanium.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances strength of titanium-based alloy material, wear resistance property and oxidation resistance property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium alloy comprises blowing carbon dioxide, methane, argon, and oxygen mixed gas to titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium carbide particle precursor powder, melting titanium alloy, to obtain titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride reinforcing particles, carrying out reaction, adding silver powder to titanium alloy melt and copper powder, carrying out mechanized mixing, carrying out metamorphic treatment, refining, pouring, to obtain ingot through 105o corner with back pressure of equal channel angle extrusion deformation with high strength, to obtain nano titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium alloy material, where the titanium alloy substrate utilizes titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material, chemical composition of alloy includes 6.5-9.75% aluminum, 1.5-2.5% vanadium, titanium, where the mass ratio of mixed gas having methane, argon, oxygen, carbon dioxide is 8-45:8-35:8-35:8-70, the pressure of mixed gas is 0.05-0.3 MPa, the gas flow rate is 0.02-0.15 m3/minute, preparing titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boron nitride particle precursor by adding quartz sand powder, titanium hydride, titanium dioxide, manganese powder, sodium chloride powder, borax with absolute ethanol, uniformly stirring, carrying out ultrasonic vibration processing, adding nano-sized activated carbon powder, continuing carrying out ultrasonic oscillation process, refilling argon gas in vacuum furnace, calcining at 1550-1800 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling with a furnace, grinding, to obtain precursor powder having an average particle size of 40-100 nm, adding silver powder to titanium alloy melt and copper powder with microparticle having an average particle size of 40-100 nm, carrying out mechanized ball milling for 24 hours, preparing titanium-based silicon carbide-silicon carbide-titanium carbide particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material, where the titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle and titanium-based alloy base material is in a mass ratio of 5-10:95-90.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium alloy preparation method includes adjusting reaction time, temperature reflex, adjusting composition of mixed gas, decomposing three products of reactions, carrying out reaction, carrying out mutual promotion, carrying out equilibrium process of decomposition, to obtain final equilibrium reaction speed, where the size of reinforcing particles is 0.1-1 mu m, adding silver to titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle, smelting reinforced titanium-based alloy material melt powder and copper powder, where the silver powder and copper powder is in a mass ratio of 50:50. The amount silver powder and copper powder is 1 wt.% in titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium carbide particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material, the silver powder and copper powder is added to titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material melt in a mass ratio of 1:99, smelting, carrying out mechanized mixing, carrying out metamorphic treatment, refining, pouring, to obtain ingot of titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium carbide particle-reinforced titanium-based alloy material, heating equal channel corner of ingot at 400-500 degrees C through the back of mold channel with a corner of 105o, carrying out squeeze (ECAP) deformation for 4 times, testing, to obtain titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium hydride particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material has high strength and toughness, adding copper to titanium carbide-silicon carbide-titanium boride particle reinforced titanium-based alloy material and silver metal combination with broad-spectrum antibacterial property.
DC M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); M29 (Changing the physical structure of nonferrous metals and alloys - including tempering, annealing, work-hardening and recrystallising (C22F).)
MC M26-B06; M26-B06A; M26-B06C; M26-B06S; M26-B06T; M26-B06V; M29-A; M29-B; M29-C

PT P
PN CN111185162-A
TI Photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide            hydrogenation catalyst obtained by dispersing titanium            oxide in first deionized water, adding chloroauric acid            solution and ruthenium chloride solution and adding            sodium borohydride solution.
AU WANG Z
   LIAO M
   TANG H
   YU W
   CHEN W
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV FUJIAN NORMAL FUQING BRANCH (UYFJ-C)
GA 202048679S
AB    NOVELTY - Photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation catalyst, is claimed. Titanium oxide                is loaded with gold-ruthenium alloy.
   USE - Used as photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst promotes the activation of carbon                dioxide molecules and enhances the efficiency of                the photocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) dispersing                titanium oxide in the first deionized water and                adding chloroauric acid solution and ruthenium                chloride solution and adding sodium hydroxide                solution to adjust the pH to 10 to obtain a                precipitate; and (ii) adding sodium borohydride                solution to perform the reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i),                  titanium oxide and the first deionized water are                  mixed in a weight-volume ratio of 1 g: 50-150 ml                  and the concentration of the chloroauric acid                  solution is 0.03-0.07 M, the addition amount of                  the chloroauric acid solution is 1-3%, the                  concentration of the ruthenium chloride solution                  is 0.2-0.4 M and the addition amount of the                  ruthenium chloride solution is 1-3%. The                  concentration of the sodium borohydride solution                  is 0.6-0.8 M, and the addition amount of sodium                  borohydride solution is 0.8-2%. The volume ratio                  of tetrabutyl titanate, ethanol solution and                  second deionized water is 15:50:100. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), sodium hydroxide is                  stirred for 10-30 minutes. In the step (ii),                  sodium borohydride solution is added and stirred                  for 2-4 hours to carry out the reduction                  reaction. The preparation of titanium oxide                  comprises dissolving tetrabutyl titanate in                  ethanol and stirring for 20-40 minutes, dropping                  into the second deionized water and stirring to                  evaporate to obtain a white powder, calcining at                  380-550 degrees C for 1-3 hours.

PN WO2022038090-A1
TI Plant used for producing hydrogen-rich stream from hydrocarbon feed, has autothermal reformer, carbon dioxide removal section arranged to receive shifted syngas stream from shift section and separate carbon dioxide-rich stream from shifted syngas stream, and fired heaters.
AU CHRISTENSEN S S
   SAHAI A
   AASBERG-PETERSEN K
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202227671X
AB    NOVELTY - Plant has: autothermal reformer (ATR) (110) arranged to receive hydrocarbon feed and convert to stream of syngas (3); a shift section comprising water gas shift (WGS) units (150) arranged to receive stream of syngas from ATR and shift it in WGS process, thus providing a shifted syngas stream (4); a carbon dioxide removal section (170) arranged to receive the shifted syngas stream from shift section and separate carbon dioxide-rich stream (10) from shifted syngas stream, thus providing hydrogen-rich stream (8) and high-pressure flash gas stream, and fired heaters arranged to pre-heat the hydrocarbon feed prior to being fed to ATR. The plant is arranged to feed at least portion of hydrogen-rich stream as hydrogen fuel for fired heaters. The plant is absent of hydrogen purification unit, such as pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit, hydrogen membrane and/or cryogenic separation unit.
   USE - The plant is used for producing hydrogen-rich stream from hydrocarbon feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant enables the provision of low carbon hydrogen fuel and utilization of high-pressure flash gas for provision of carbon-free or low-carbon substitute to hydrocarbon gases, such as natural gas, as fuel gas in plant and/or process, provides simple and cost-effective process, transforms hydrocarbon gas as energy carrier and as fuel to low carbon fuel, utilizes substantial portion of hydrogen provided from hydrogen producing plant as carbon-free fuel for use in plant, instead of using hydrocarbon gas such as natural gas as fuel, reduces the carbon dioxide emissions connected with use as fuel of hydrocarbon gases, such as natural gas, saves the costs of capturing carbon from hydrocarbon gas, such as industrial gas containing significant amounts of hydrocarbons, biogas, or natural gas, achieves high hydrogen purity in hydrogen-rich stream, improves carbon capture, has high plant efficiency, energy efficiency and energy consumption in terms of natural gas used in process, reduces the cost by maintaining same carbon dioxide purity, and ensures increase in plant efficiency, which means reduction in natural gas consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Plant has: autothermal reformer (ATR) (110) arranged to receive hydrocarbon feed and convert to stream of syngas (3); a shift section comprising water gas shift (WGS) units (150) arranged to receive stream of syngas from ATR and shift it in WGS process, thus providing a shifted syngas stream (4); a carbon dioxide removal section (170) arranged to receive the shifted syngas stream from shift section and separate carbon dioxide-rich stream (10) from shifted syngas stream, thus providing hydrogen-rich stream (8) and high-pressure flash gas stream, and fired heaters arranged to pre-heat the hydrocarbon feed prior to being fed to ATR. The plant is arranged to feed at least portion of hydrogen-rich stream as hydrogen fuel for fired heaters. The plant is absent of hydrogen purification unit, such as pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit, hydrogen membrane and/or cryogenic separation unit. The carbon dioxide-removal section is amine wash unit which comprises carbon dioxide- absorber and carbon dioxide-stripper, and also high-pressure flash drum and low pressure flash drum, thus separating carbon dioxide-rich stream, hydrogen-rich stream and high pressure flash gas stream. The plant is arranged to feed at least portion of high-pressure flash gas stream to unit or stream of plant. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for producing hydrogen-rich stream from hydrocarbon feed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of ATR-based hydrogen process and plant.
   Stream of syngas (3)
   Shifted syngas stream (4)
   Hydrogen-rich stream (8)
   Gas turbine generator unit (13)
   Air (14)
   Oxygen stream (15)
   Autothermal reformer (110)
   Water gas shift units (150)
   Carbon dioxide removal section (170)

PT P
PN CN108383728-A
TI Preparing diethyl carbonate by one-pot method using carbon dioxide comprises e.g. preparing electrode-induced solid carbon solution, stirring uniformly, placing in glass tank, and mounting on support capable of fixing other items.
AU LIU J
AE LIU J (LIUJ-Individual)
GA 201864262T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diethyl carbonate by one-pot method using carbon dioxide comprises e.g. (1) preparing electrode-induced solid carbon solution, stirring uniformly, placing in glass tank, and mounting on support capable of fixing other items; where the electrode-induced carbon fixation solution is obtained by dissolving ammonium bromide in solvent and stirring and mixing, the ammonium bromide is methyl ammonium bromide, phenyl ammonium bromide or decyl ammonium bromide, the solvent is tetrahydrofuran or N,N-dimethylformamide; (2) respectively fixing the two electrodes of copper and titanium, inert gas conduit and the carbon dioxide gas feed pipe on the support of the induction electrolytic cell, and introducing dry inert gas until the air of carbon-inducing solution is displaced by the electrode; and (3) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the electrode-inducing carbon fixation solution, so that carbon dioxide saturated atmosphere is formed in the solution; turning on the power.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing diethyl carbonate by one-pot method using carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing diethyl carbonate by one-pot method using carbon dioxide comprises (1) preparing electrode-induced solid carbon solution, stirring uniformly, placing in glass tank, and mounting on support capable of fixing other items; where the electrode-induced carbon fixation solution is obtained by dissolving ammonium bromide in solvent and stirring and mixing, where the mass of ammonium bromide is 3-20% of the mass of the electrode-induced carbon fixation solution, the ammonium bromide is methyl ammonium bromide, phenyl ammonium bromide or decyl ammonium bromide, the solvent is tetrahydrofuran or N,N-dimethylformamide; (2) respectively fixing the two electrodes of copper and titanium, inert gas conduit and the carbon dioxide gas feed pipe on the support of the induction electrolytic cell, and introducing dry inert gas until the air of the carbon-inducing solution is displaced by the electrode; (3) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the electrode-inducing carbon fixation solution, where mass of the carbon dioxide gas is 45-85% of the mass of the electrode-induced carbon fixation solution, so that carbon dioxide saturated atmosphere is formed in the solution; turning on the power, where control voltage is 1.6-12 V, the current is 0.1-12 A, and electrode induces the reaction for 20-50 minutes to obtain the induction liquid; and (4) after the end of the electrode induction, slowly transferring induction liquid to four-port round reflux reactor equipped with temperature control system, slowly adding methanol into the reactor, where the molar ratio of methanol to carbon dioxide is 2:1, adding catalyst at the same time, where the addition mass of the catalyst is 0.2-1 wt.% of the methanol; refluxing at 40-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours, dropwise adding ethanol from the feed port, maintaining dropwise addition process, where molar ratio of ethanol to methanol is 1:1; after the completion of the dropwise addition, the refluxing at 40-95 degrees C for 2-5 hours; cooling to below 30 degrees C; transferring the mixed reaction solution to separator, washing with 10-40 wt.% cold brine for 2-5 times, washing with hot brine at 60 degrees C for 1-3 times until the residual catalyst is completely removed, where the upper layer is a product, lower layer is water and impurities, finally washing with hot water at 60 degrees C for 1 or 2 times, extracting product with dichloromethane, drying mixture over anhydrous magnesium sulfate to obtain mixture of diethyl carbonate and dichloromethane; distilling off methylene chloride and by-product dimethyl carbonate from the distiller, when there is no liquid outflow at 95 degrees C, adjusting the vacuum of the distiller to 10-50 mmHg, collecting distillate at 50-70 degrees C, where distillate is diethyl carbonate; in the above reaction, the carbon dioxide is introduced in excess, and the amount of ethanol added is excessively added to the added methanol based on the mass of the methanol; the conversion rate is 83-87.3% based on the amount of ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the inert gas is helium gas or argon gas. In the step (4), the catalyst is magnesium acetate, triethylene glycol-dilauric acid complex or ethylene glycol-ammonium magnesium carbonate complex. Preferred Method: In the step (3), adding the voltage value and the current value multiple times to perform the electrode induction reaction, and the total reaction time is 20-50 minutes.

PN US2022161197-A1
TI Apparatus for reducing fuel consumption in a flue gas generator, comprises membrane unit comprising outer and inner surface, and is configured such that first gas comprising first concentration of nitrogen, oxygen, water and carbon dioxide is drawn into outer surface and passes through the membrane.
AU KOLODJI B
AE KOLODJI B (KOLO-Individual)
GA 202272632V
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a membrane unit 102 comprising an outer surface and an inner surface. The membrane unit is configured such that first gas comprising a first concentration of nitrogen, a first concentration of oxygen, a first concentration of water, and a first concentration of carbon dioxide is drawn into the outer surface and passes through the membrane. A permeate stream exits the inner surface, where the permeate stream comprises a second concentration of nitrogen, a second concentration of oxygen, a second concentration of water, and a second concentration of carbon dioxide. The second concentration of oxygen is greater than the first concentration of oxygen, second concentration of water is greater than the first concentration of water, second concentration of carbon dioxide is greater than the first concentration of carbon dioxide, and second concentration of nitrogen is less than the first concentration of nitrogen. The apparatus comprises vacuum-generating device 108.
   USE - Apparatus for reducing fuel consumption in a flue gas generator.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus utilizes a membrane unit to capture components from atmospheric air, including carbon dioxide, enriches carbon dioxide concentration, and delivers the enriched concentration of carbon dioxide to a sequestering facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Apparatus comprises a membrane unit comprising an outer surface and an inner surface. The membrane unit is configured such that first gas comprising a first concentration of nitrogen, a first concentration of oxygen, a first concentration of water, and a first concentration of carbon dioxide is drawn into the outer surface and passes through the membrane. A permeate stream exits the inner surface, where the permeate stream comprises a second concentration of nitrogen, a second concentration of oxygen, a second concentration of water, and a second concentration of carbon dioxide. The second concentration of oxygen is greater than the first concentration of oxygen, second concentration of water is greater than the first concentration of water, second concentration of carbon dioxide is greater than the first concentration of carbon dioxide, and second concentration of nitrogen is less than the first concentration of nitrogen. The apparatus comprises a vacuum-generating device which applies a vacuum to membrane unit, a permeate collection device which collects the permeate stream, and a flue gas generator which receives at least a portion of the permeate stream.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for reducing fuel consumption in a flue gas generator by exposing a membrane unit to atmospheric air, where membrane unit comprises an outer surface and an inner surface, and membrane unit is configured such that as a first gas comprising a first concentration of oxygen, a first concentration of carbon dioxide and a first concentration of nitrogen is drawn into outer surface and passes through membrane unit and a permeate stream exits the inner surface where permeate stream comprises a second concentration of oxygen, a second concentration of carbon dioxide and a second concentration of nitrogen, where second concentration of oxygen is greater than the first concentration of oxygen, and second concentration of carbon dioxide is greater than first concentration of carbon dioxide, and second concentration of nitrogen is less than the first concentration of nitrogen; applying a vacuum to the membrane unit to increase a flow of the permeate stream; and providing at least a portion of the permeate stream to an air intake of a flue gas generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an array or bank formed by multiple membrane unit.
   Membrane unit (102)
   Vacuum-generating device (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves cooling at least a portion of a flue gas stream from flue gas generator resulting in a stream of condensed water and a stream of partially dehydrated flue gas stream. The membrane unit comprises a sheet element that contains a sheet element comprising edges attached to a ribbed sheet, where a membrane envelope is defined between the sheet element and the ribbed sheet. The membrane envelope comprises an open edge through which a permeate stream is channeled to a delivery conduit. The vacuum is applied by a liquid ring compressor. The vacuum is applied by a bellows unit. The flue gas generator is pressurized. The flue gas generator comprises an economizer. The method further involves transmitting at least a portion of the permeate stream from membrane unit to a secondary enrichment system disposed upstream of the air intake of the flue gas generator resulting in a second permeate stream having an enriched oxygen concentration

PT P
PN WO2020031063-A1; CN112533890-A; EP3833651-A1; US2021300849-A1; US11192843-B2
TI Producing methanol comprises combusting feed hydrocarbons with oxidant comprising oxygen to generate heat and heated gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and water and heating natural gas with heat from heated gas stream to synthesis gas.
AU BADGANDI S V
   SETHURAMAN B
   NAIR V S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202012996K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) methanol comprises combusting feed hydrocarbons with an oxidant that comprises 70-99.5 wt.% oxygen to generate heat and produce a heated gas stream at a temperature of 1200-1800 degrees C, where the heated gas stream comprises carbon dioxide and water, heating natural gas with heat from the heated gas stream, to a temperature sufficient to reform the natural gas and produce synthesis gas, where the heating of the natural gas concurrently cools the heated gas stream to form a cooled gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and water, and reacting the synthesis gas and at least some of the carbon dioxide from the cooled gas stream under reaction conditions sufficient to produce methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is more efficient and environmentally friendly; utilizes oxidant i.e. pure oxygen to burn fuel thus reducing fuel consumption and increasing synthesis gas production in reformer (e.g. a steam reformer); optimizes a methanol plant and result in increased economic benefit e.g. utilizes carbon dioxide that carbon dioxide is not imported to the methanol production process; utilizes pure carbon dioxide (or almost pure carbon dioxide) in-situ from burner flue gas, that would normally be emitted to atmosphere can be used in forming valuable products i.e. methanol and urea; and utilize pure oxygen offers natural gas savings as compared to conventional reformer for methanol production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) producing (P2) methanol combusting a first methane stream with an oxidant that comprises 70-99.5 wt.% oxygen to generate heat and produce a heated gas stream at a temperature of 1200-1800 degrees C, where the heated gas stream comprises carbon dioxide and water, heating a second methane stream, with heat from the heated gas stream, to a temperature sufficient to reform the second methane stream and produce synthesis gas, where the heating of the second methane stream concurrently cools the heated gas stream to form a cooled gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and water, and reacting the synthesis gas and at least some of the carbon dioxide from the cooled gas stream under reaction conditions sufficient to produce methanol; and
   (2) producing (P3) methanol comprising separating a methane stream into a first methane stream and a second methane stream, flowing the first methane stream to a burner, separating air, in an air separation unit, to produce an oxidant comprising 70-99.5 wt.% oxygen, 1-30 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 0.1-5 wt.% nitrogen, flowing the oxidant to the burner, combusting the first methane stream with the oxidant to generate heat and produce a heated gas stream at a temperature of 1200-1800 degrees C, where the heated gas stream comprises carbon dioxide and water, flowing the heated gas stream to a reformer, flowing the second methane stream to the reformer, heating the second methane stream, with heat from the heated gas stream, to a temperature sufficient to reform the second methane stream and produce synthesis gas, where the heating of the second methane stream concurrently cools the heated gas stream to form a cooled gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, flowing the synthesis gas to a methanol synthesis reactor, flowing at least some of the carbon dioxide of the cooled gas stream to the methanol synthesis reactor, and reacting in the methanol synthesis reactor, the synthesis gas and at least some of the carbon dioxide of the cooled gas stream under reaction conditions sufficient to produce methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: separating air, in an air separation unit, to produce the oxidant; producing ammonia and/or urea with raw materials that include nitrogen from the air separation unit; separating carbon dioxide from water in the cooled gas stream and using the separated carbon dioxide in the step of reacting the synthesis gas; separating a methane stream into a first methane stream and a second methane stream, and flowing the first methane stream to a burner, where the first methane stream comprises the feed hydrocarbons combusted with the oxidant; and flowing the heated gas stream to a reformer, and flowing the second methane stream to the reformer, where the second methane stream comprises the natural gas that is heated. The step of reforming the natural gas comprises reacting the natural gas with water. Preferred Components: The air separation unit comprises cryogenic distillation unit, and/or pressure swing adsorption/membrane separation unit. The oxidant further comprises 1-30 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 0.1-5 wt.% nitrogen. The combusting results in oxidation of 95-100 wt.% of the feed hydrocarbons. The cooled gas stream comprises 55-65 wt.% carbon dioxide, 35-45 wt.% water, 0.1-3 wt.% nitrogen. The portion of the carbon dioxide from the cooled gas stream is used in an ammonia and/or urea production process. The portion of the carbon dioxide from the cooled gas stream is used as a diluent in the combusting of the feed hydrocarbons. The flow of the carbon dioxide from the cooled gas stream to the combusting of the feed hydrocarbons is varied to control flame temperature of the combusting. The flame temperature of the combusting is 1500-3000 degrees C. The conversion rate of natural gas to methanol is 70-95%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

Each of the reactors (A) is preset with a target gas ratio (b1) higher than the target gas ratio (a1) and becoming higher as the reactor (A) is located on the downstream side. When the acquired gas ratio (b) is smaller than the target gas ratio (b1), ratios (b) and (b1) combined with each other and amount of hydrogen corresponding to the difference between the ratios is supplied from the additional gas supply unit. The unit (B) computes acquisition gas ratio (b) of the reactor (A) of the highest upstream side using acquisition gas ratio (a) and the quantity of the hydrogen supplied by reactor (4). The apparatus further has a thermal medium circulation unit, which circulates the thermal medium which is heat-exchanged with respect to reactors (4) and (B). When the ratio (a) is more than ratio (a1), the temperature of the thermal medium is increased at the time of circulating to the reactor (A), and flow volume of the thermal medium is reduced, and vice-versa. The apparatus further consists of a reactor (C) connected to downstream side of reactor (4) in series, acquisition unit (D) for acquiring acquisition gas ratio (c) which is ratio of hydrogen with respect to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas supplied to reactor (C).

GA 202104946C
AB    NOVELTY - The gas turbine system (10) has a compression unit (100), a combustion unit (200) that mixes and combusts compressed air from the compression unit with fuel, a turbine unit (300) with a rotation shaft (310) connected to the compression unit and a blade connected to the rotation shaft, and that generates power by combustion gas emitted from the combustion unit, and a power generation unit (400) that performs power generation with the power generated by turbine unit. A collection unit (500) recovers carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas of turbine unit. PT P
PN CN112144073-A; CN112144073-B
TI Preparing ethanol acetic acid by electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide under heteropolyacid ion liquid-indium double-catalysis system comprises e.g. preparing polyoxometalate ionic liquid and taking polyoxometalate ionic liquid.
AU LI C
   SUN W
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202103113K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethanol acetic acid by electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide under heteropolyacid ion liquid-indium double-catalysis system comprises (i) preparing polyoxometalate ionic liquid and (ii) electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide including taking the polyoxometalate ionic liquid obtained in the step (i) as the cathode electrolyte, using dilute sulfuric acid as the anode electrolyte, indium sheet as the working electrode, the silver/silver chloride electrode as reference electrode, and platinum sheet as counter electrode, injecting carbon dioxide into the cathode electrolyte at a constant rate to keep the gas in the system saturated, and electrolyzing reducing carbon dioxide at -0.4 V to -1.2 V under constant potential to obtain ethanol and acetic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethanol acetic acid by electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide under heteropolyacid ion liquid-indium double-catalysis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; has mild reaction conditions; solves the technical problems that the existing carbon dioxide reduction reactions are generally 2e-, 4e-shift reactions, the deep reduction of carbon dioxide cannot be achieved, and the complex preparation process of the ionic liquid in the existing reaction system; solves the problem of scarcity and shortage of existing polyacid ionic liquids; and increases energy efficiency by 4-5 times. The obtained ethanol has 7-16% Faraday efficiency. The obtained acetate has 14-67% Faraday efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the polyoxometalate ionic liquid is prepared by (a) orderly adding deionized water, acetonitrile, concentrated sulfuric acid, aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate and sodium tungstate to a reaction container, stirring and mixing, carrying out the hydrothermal reaction, finishing reaction, cooling at room temperature, adding tetrabutyl ammonium bromide, stirring the reaction, filtering, washing, air drying to obtain orange powder as ionic liquid precursor (n-Bu4N)4SV2W10O40, and (b) orderly adding acetonitrile, deionized water, concentrated hydrochloric acid and the ionic liquid precursor (n-Bu4N)4SV2W10O40 obtained in the step (a), stirring and mixing, carrying out the hydrothermal reaction, finishing reaction, cooling at room temperature, adding tetrabutyl ammonium bromide, stirring the reaction, filtering, washing, air drying to obtain yellow powder, recrystallizing for 3-5 times to obtain yellow crystals (n-Bu 4N)3SV-W11O40, and dissolving the yellow crystal (n-Bu4N)3SV-W11O40 in acetonitrile to obtain a polyoxometalate ionic liquid. In the step (i) (a), the stirring and mixing are carried out for 1.5-2.5 hours. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 20-24 hours. The stirring is carried out for 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step (i) (b), the stirring and mixing are carried out for 1.5-2.5 hours. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 20-24 hours. The stirring is carried out for 1.5-2.5 hours. The solvent used for the recrystallizing comprises acetonitrile. Preferred Components: In the step (i) (a), the volume of the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate and deionized water is 1.5:(25-35). The volume of the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate and acetonitrile is 1.5: (30-40). The volume of the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate and concentrated sulfuric acid is 1.5:(1-3). The volume of the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate and the mass of sodium tungstate is 1.5 ml:(0.8-1.5) g. The ratio of the mass of sodium metavanadate to the volume of deionized water in the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate (10-14) g:100 ml. The ratio of the volume of the aqueous solution of sodium metavanadate to the mass of tetrabutyl ammonium bromide is 1.5 ml:(0.5-1) g. In the step (i) (b), the volume ratio of deionized water to acetonitrile is 15:(50-55). The volume ratio of deionized water to concentrated hydrochloric acid is 15:(5-10). The ratio of the volume of the deionized water to the mass of the ionic liquid precursor (n-Bu4N) 4SV2W10O40 obtained in the step (a) is 15 ml:(0.2-0.4). The mass ratio of the ionic liquid precursor (n-Bu4N)4SV2W10O40 obtained in the step (a) and tetrabutyl ammonium bromide is 0.3:(0.5-1). The concentration of (n-Bu4N)3SV-W11O40 in the obtained polyoxometalate ionic liquid is 1-3 mmol/l.

PN CN114606537-A
TI Electrolytic copper extraction from organic acid            ultra-coarsening waste liquid used for printed circuit            board, by injecting organic acid super-roughening waste            liquid to electrolytic cell with cathode copper plate            and anode titanium plate, electrolyzing, precipitating            metal copper, and reacting.
AU FAN X
   LI X
AE SHENZHEN SUNTAK MULTILAYER PCB CO LTD (SHHE-C)
   DALIAN CHONGDA ELECTRONIC CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 202282697S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for electrolytic copper extraction from                organic acid ultra-coarsening waste liquid,                involves (i) injecting an organic acid                super-roughening waste liquid to an electrolytic                cell with a cathode copper plate and a anode                titanium plate, (ii) adding formic acid in the                organic acid ultra-coarsening waste liquid, and                (iii) electrolyzing the organic acid                ultra-roughening waste liquid through an                electrolytic cell, during the electrolysis process,                precipitating metal copper on the cathode copper                plate, performing reaction of chlorine ion                oxidation to generate chlorine gas on the anode                titanium plate, adding the generated chlorine gas                and formic acid, and performing chemical reaction                to produce carbon dioxide gas and water-soluble                hydrogen chloride.
   USE - The method is used for electrolytic copper                extraction from organic acid ultra-coarsening waste                liquid for manufacturing printed circuit                board.
   ADVANTAGE - By adding formic acid in the organic acid                super-coarsening waste liquid, the toxic chlorine                generated in the electrolysis process is directly                oxidized by formate in the solution is carbon                dioxide gas and water-soluble hydrogen chloride, so                one is capable of avoiding chlorine pollution and                harm to the health of the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The anode titanium                  plate used in step (i) is a                  ruthenium-iridium-titanium anode plate with a                  ruthenium-iridium anti-corrosion coating.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the chlorine generated during the add-on of                  formic acid and the electrolytic organic acid                  ultra-roughening waste liquid is 2:1. The copper                  ion content in the organic acid ultra-roughening                  waste liquid is 40 g/l. The formic acid used in                  step (ii) is added in an amount of 65 g per liter                  of organic acid super-coarsening waste                  liquid.Preferred Method: The electrolysis process                  in step (iii) involves (iiia) when the copper ion                  content in the organic acid ultra-coarsening                  waste liquid is greater than or equal to30 g/l,                  controlling the current during electrolysis at                  1200 A, (iiib) when the copper ion content in the                  organic acid ultra-coarsening waste liquid is                  reduced to 20-30 g/l, controlling the current                  during electrolysis at 1000 A, (iiic) when the                  copper ion content in the organic acid                  ultra-coarsening waste liquid is reduced to 10-20                  g/l, controlling the electric current during                  electrolysis at 700 A, (iiid) when the copper ion                  content in the organic acid ultra-coarsening                  waste liquid is reduced to 5-10 g/l, controlling                  the electric current during electrolysis at 500                  A, and (iiie) when the copper ion content in the                  organic acid super-roughening waste liquid is                  reduced to less than 5 g/l, stopping electrolysis                  and discharging the organic acid super-roughening                  waste liquid in the electrolytic cell.Preferred Conditions: In steps (iiia) and                  (iiib), the sampling analysis time to copper ion                  content in electrolysis process is 6-8 minutes.                  In step (iiic), the sampling and analysis time                  for copper ion content in the electrolysis                  process is 6-10 minutes. In step (iiid), the                  sampling and analysis time for copper ion content                  in the electrolysis process is 4-6 minutes. The                  cathode copper plate in the electrolytic cell is                  replaced with a new one every month to recover                  and separate out the elemental copper attached to                  the cathode copper plate.
DC M28 (Electrolytic and electrothermic production and refining of non-ferrous metals - excluding heat treatment (C25).); V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC M28-C02; M28-C01; M28-A; V04-R01A4; X25-R02; X25-H

   GUO K
   LI Y
   HUANG Q
AE PANZHIHUA PANXI VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (PANZ-Non-standard)

GA 202253863K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ecological brown sugar comprises (a) squeezing sugarcane by a juice extracting device to obtain sugar cane juice, (b) filtering the obtained sugarcane juice through an 80-200 meshes sieve to obtain a filtered juice and a sieve residue, (c) adding lime milk to the filtered juice, adjusting the pH value to 6.0-6.5, allowing to stand for 1-2 hours under the protection of carbon dioxide to remove the residue, and (d) heating the sieved juice to 90-95degrees Celsius under the protective of nitrogen or carbon dioxide, removing the formed floating condensation polymer and filtering to obtain the sugar juice clear liquid, (e) boiling the clear liquid of sugar juice at 120-125degrees Celsius to obtain normal pressure sugar paste, continuously stirring during the boiling process and quickly evaporation the water of the sugar caine juice, and (f) obtaining the sugar paste.

The monatomic copper/boron dual site multifunctional catalyst comprises copper single atomic sites and boron atomic sites.

PT P
PN CN114134114-A
TI Culturing natural killer cells, comprises inoculating placental MNC cell suspension, adding interlukin-15 to culture flask, mixing, placing culture flask in carbon dioxide incubator, adjusting cell density, discarding supernatant and resuspending cell pellet in phosphate buffered saline.
AU LIU Q
   XIAO H
   WEI Q
AE BOYA LIFE STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BOYA-Non-standard)
GA 202238814T
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing natural killer cells, comprises (i) inoculating placental MNC cell suspension with a cell density of 0.5-1.5x106/ml into a culture flask i.e.T75 culture flask, adding 50ng/mL interlukin-15, 10ng/ml FLT3-L and 10ng/ml OK432,to the culture flask, mixing, placing the culture flask in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide incubator; (ii) adding interlukin-15, FLT3-L, OK432 into the culture flask every 48 hours, adding natural killing cell complete medium to the culture flask every 48-72 hours, and adjusting the cell density to 0.5-1.5x106/ml, transferring the cell suspension to G-rex culture flask and continue to culture for 14 days when the total volume of cultured cell suspension reaches 200 ml; (ii) sucking cell suspension out in the centrifuge bottle after culturing and, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minutes, for 8 minutes, at 25degrees Celsius, discarding the supernatant and resuspending cell pellet in phosphate buffered saline to obtain natural killer cells.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing natural killer cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits excellent technical effects as described in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i) and (ii), the method utilizes ferrous nitrate, threonine, serum substitute, tyrosine, L-glutamine, phosphate buffered saline, potassium dihydrogen phosphate solution and sodium hydroxide solution. The tissue cleaning solution comprises sodium chloride, penicillin, streptomycin and amphotericin B.The placental leaflet tissue cell suspension is prepared by using Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS) buffer and dulbecco's modified eagle medium /F12 . The tissue digestion solution is prepared by phosphate buffered saline type I collagenase solution, HBSS buffer to type II collagenase powder and thermolysin powder. The phosphate buffered saline is prepared using potassium dihydrogen phosphate solution and sodium hydroxide solution. The HBSS buffer is comprises 8 g sodium chloride, 0.4 g potassium chloride, 0.1 g magnesium sulfate hepta hydrate, 0.1 g magnesium chloride hexa hydrate, 0.06 g sodium phosphate dibasic dihydrate, 0.06 g monopotassium phosphate, 1.0g of glucose, 0.14 g calcium chloride, and 0.35 g sodium bicarbonate.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foaming polypropylene bead comprises (i) placing the polypropylene particles, a dispersing medium, a dispersing agent and a dispersing auxiliary agent into a closed high-pressure resistant kettle according to a specific weight ratio and forming a dispersing system under the continuous stirring action, (ii) heating the dispersion system in the kettle to a specific temperature and filling a specific carbon dioxide and keeping for a specific time, (iii) releasing the dispersion into normal pressure to obtain expanded polypropylene beads, washing the dispersing agent attached to the surfaces of the expanded polypropylene beads with cleaning water, dehydrating and drying to obtain finished expanded polypropylene beads, where the polypropylene particles comprise 94-99.94 wt.% polypropylene, 0.01-2 wt.% crystallization nucleating agent, 0.02-4 wt.% organic phosphate compound and 0.03-0.3 wt.% foam cell nucleating agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing foaming polypropylene bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: inhibits the agglomeration in the kettle of particle or outer agglomeration of bead; can reduce the dosage of the dispersing agent and surface attachment content of foaming polypropylene bead product less than 200 ppm; and ensure the excellent quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The length of the polypropylene particle is 0.8-2.5 microm and the average single weight is 0.5-1.8 mg. The crystal nucleating agent is a polypropylene alpha-type crystal nucleating agent. The crystallization nucleating agent comprises dibenzylidene sorbitol crystallization nucleating agent, substituted aryl heterocyclic phosphate crystallization nucleating agent, cyclic organic carboxylate crystallization nucleating agent, amide crystallization nucleating agent and/or rare earth crystallization nucleating agent. The organic phosphate compound comprises phosphate monoester compound, a phosphate diester compound and a phosphate triester compound. The foam cell nucleating agent comprises silicon dioxide, zinc borate, talc powder, barium sulfate and calcium carbonate, and the particle size of the foam cell nucleating agent is 3-6 microm. The dispersion medium is deionized water. The dispersing agent is silicate mineral powder with the average particle size of 0.1-2 microm, preferably, the dispersing agent is washing kaolin.The dispersing aid is an anionic surfactant, preferably, methyl stearate polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfonate and sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate. The weight ratio of the methyl stearate polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfonate to the sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate is 0.3-3:1. In the step (iii) the mass ratio of the cleaning water to the expanded polypropylene beads is 0.2-5:1.Preferred Method: The polypropylene is a random copolymer polypropylene, the melting point is 130-155degrees Celsius and the melt index is 6-8 g/10 minutes. In the step (i) taking 100 pts. wt. polypropylene particles, 100 pts. wt. dispersion medium, 200 pts. wt. dispersing agent, 0.03-0.3 pts. wt. dispersing agent and 0.005-0.3 pts. wt. dispersing auxiliary agent. In the step (iii) the cleaning water contains 0.005-0.5 wt.% anionic polyacrylamide surfactant.

PN CN113751012-A
TI Preparing a catalyst used in solar concentrating photocatalytic methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction comprises e.g. dissolving cerium sulfate in water, performing hydrothermal synthesis, immersing catalyst carrier in mixed system solution of nickel, grinding and performing calcination.
AU ZHANG Y
   FENG Q
   YIN Z
   LIU L
AE CHENGDU TANWEI GUANGZHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202205854W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a catalyst comprises (1) dissolving cerium sulfate in water to obtain solution A, taking potassium hydroxide, dissolving in water to obtain solution B, pouring solution B into solution A, stirring at room temperature, and performing hydrothermal synthesis to obtain cerium dioxide, (2) centrifugally washing the cerium dioxide, drying, grinding and calcining to obtain a catalyst carrier, (3) preparing a 1-5% nickel sulfate solution, adding ammonia water dropwise to the constantly stirring nickel sulfate solution, adjusting the pH value of the solution to 11 to obtain a mixed system, and immersing the catalyst carrier in the mixed system solution of nickel, stirring at room temperature for 24 hours, taking out, washing, drying, grinding and roasting to obtain nickel/cerium dioxide, and (4) preparing a 1-10% rare earth salt solution, immersing the nickel/cerium dioxide in the rare earth salt solution, drying in oil bath, grinding and performing calcination.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in the solar concentrating photocatalytic methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction (claimed), and rare earth field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (4), the rare earth element in the rare earth salt is lanthanum, samarium, europium, holmium, ytterbium or lutetium. The loading amount of rare earth metal is 1-10% of the catalytic carrier.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the concrete reaction process includes providing 1.736 g cerium sulfate, dissolving in 10 ml deionized water to obtain solution A, providing 10.6 g potassium hydroxide, dissolving in 70 ml deionized water to obtain solution B, pouring solution B into solution A, reacting at a stirring rate of 400 revolutions/minute, and then performing hydrothermal synthesis to obtain cerium dioxide, where the hydrothermal synthesis temperature is 200degrees Celsius and the holding time is 24 hours. In the step (2), drying the cerium dioxide at 60degrees Celsius for 24 hours, and performing calcination at 400-600degrees Celsius for 2 hours. In step (3), the ammonia water mass percentage is 28%, and the washing method is to wash 5-6 times with deionized water. The drying method is vacuum drying at 80degrees Celsius, where the time is greater than or equal to 24 hours, and the calcination temperature is 400degrees Celsius, mixing the catalyst carrier and the nickel salt solution according to the dosage ratio of 1 g:100 ml. In step (4), the calcination temperature is treated at 300-400degrees Celsius for 2 hours, and the oil bath is placed in an oil bath for 48 hours.

The compressive strength of magnesium carbonate slag products is improved.

PN CN113651354-A
TI Mixed fatty acid for removing vanadium from coarse titanium tetrachloride with high vanadium content, comprises carbon-carbon double bond to alpha-H and carboxyl in mixed fatty acid and prepared by selecting titanium tetrachloride.
AU LI L
   WANG X
   ZHOU L
   QIU S
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2021D58061
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed fatty acid comprises carbon-carbon double bond to alpha-H and carboxyl in the mixed fatty acid, where the ratio of carbon-carbon double bond:(a-H and carboxyl) is 0.6-0.75.
   USE - Mixed fatty acid used for removing vanadium from coarse titanium tetrachloride with high vanadium content.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed fatty acid has high efficiency removing vanadium oxychloride in high vanadium-containing crude titanium tetrachloride, and balances the viscosity of vanadium removing tailings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for removing vanadium from coarse titanium tetrachloride with high vanadium content, involving:
   (1) selecting high vanadium-containing crude titanium tetrachloride containing vanadium oxychloride;
   (2) putting the high vanadium-containing crude titanium tetrachloride containing the vanadium oxychloride in the vanadium removing reactor;
   (3) heating by the heater, adding the mixed fatty acid to the vanadium removing reactor, and then the system temperature in the vanadium removing reactor to 140-150degrees Celsius; and
   (4) monitoring the gas amount of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide generated by the reaction, when the gas amount of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide generated by the reaction reaches a predetermined range and terminating the vanadium removal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed fatty acid is at least one of oleic acid, linolenic acid, arachidonic acid, eicosapentaenoic acid, docosahexaenoic acid and palmitic acid, caprylic acid, capric acid, lauric acid, myristic acid and palmitic acid. stearic acid and peanut acid. Preferred Conditions: The mixed fatty acid is prepared by oleic acid and palmitic acid. The mol ratio of carbon-carbon double bond to alpha -H and carboxyl in the mixed fatty acid is 0.7. The mol content of the vanadium oxychloride in the high vanadium-containing rough titanium tetrachloride containing the vanadium oxychloride is 1.09-4.36%. The heating is carried out by a heater. rising the temperature of the reaction system to 115-125degrees Celsius at a speed of 10-20degrees Celsius/minutes. The mol ratio of the mixed fatty acid to the vanadium oxychloride in the high vanadium-containing rough titanium tetrachloride containing the vanadium oxychloride is 0.25-0.35. The gas concentration of the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide is 1ppm, the vanadium removal is stopped.

PN CN112794299-A; CN112794299-B
TI Preparing ferrous phosphate by wet-process phosphoric acid, comprises e.g. adding alkaline magnesium compound into phosphoric acid solution to adjust pH, filtering, then adding ferrous sulfate into obtained filtrate, and post-processing.
AU ZHANG Y
   HAN P
   SANG Z
   CHEN Z
   HU Y
   GE C
AE WUHAN TECHNOLOGY INST (WUHT-C)
   WUHAN DELIAN CHEM CO LTD (WUHT-C)
   WUHAN TECHNOLOGY INST (WUHT-C)
GA 2021566056
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ferrous phosphate by wet-process phosphoric acid, comprises (i) adding alkaline magnesium compound into phosphoric acid solution at 40-100 degrees C to adjust pH to 3-6, and filtering to obtain filter residue one and filtrate one, (ii) adding ferrous sulfate to the filtrate one, then introducing carbon dioxide, adding alkaline magnesium compound to adjust pH of the solution to 3-5 to obtain product ferrous phosphate hydrate and filtrate two, where the pressure of carbon dioxide above the filtrate is 0.01-1 Mpa, and (iii) adding Add alkaline magnesium compound to the second filtrate until the pH value of the solution is greater than or equal to 6, and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue three and filtrate three, when the dry basis content of aluminum in the filter residue there is less than or equal to 2%, the filter residue is used to replace or partially replace alkaline magnesium compound in step (ii).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ferrous phosphate by wet-process phosphoric acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can prepare high purity ferrous phosphate; has short preparation process, and simple operation; and reduces production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline magnesium compound is magnesium hydroxide, basic magnesium carbonate, magnesium carbonate and/or magnesium bicarbonate. The alkaline magnesium compound is magnesium hydroxide or magnesium carbonate. The alkaline magnesium compound is magnesium hydroxide or magnesium carbonate. The molar ratio between ferrous sulfate and phosphoric acid in phosphoric acid solution is 1.3-2:1.

AB    NOVELTY - Moisturizing and repairing DIY soft film comprises gel component, oxygen injection component and powder component in a weight ratio of (40-50):(18-22):3, where each portion of gel component comprises phase (A) comprising 7-50 pts. wt. humectant (I) and 1-5 pts. wt. film forming agent, phase (B) comprising 1-5 pts. wt. emulsifier and 1-5 pts. wt. humectant (II), and phase (C) comprising 23-87.78 pts. wt. water, each of oxygen injection component comprises phase (D) comprising 80-120 pts. wt. humectant (III), and phase (E) comprising 0.1-1 pt. wt. carbon dioxide, and each of powder component comprises 60-150 pts. wt. filler powder.

   ADVANTAGE - The soft film promotes the release of oxygen from red blood cells to increase the oxygen content of skin, and promotes the skin metabolism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using soft film, involving adding oxygen injection component and powder component to gel component, uniformly stirring, coating on skin, and naturally condensing to obtain soft film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The humectant (I) comprises 1-10 pts. wt. glycerol, 1-5 pts. wt. hydrolyzed sodium hyaluronate, 0.2-1 pt. wt. sodium acetylated sodium hyaluronate, 0.2-1 pt. wt. sodium hyaluronate crosslinked polymer, 0.2-1 pt. wt. hyaluronic acid, 0.2-1 pt. wt. silanol, 1-5 pts. wt. panthenol, 0.4-2 pts. wt. glucomannan, 0.3-1.5 pts. wt. potassium chloride, 1-10 pts. wt. propylene glycol and 1-5 pts. wt. polyglycerol-10. Each portion of gel component further comprises phase (F) comprising 1-5 pts. wt. humectant (IV), 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. chelating agent and 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. emollient, and phase (G) comprising 0.01-0.5 pt. wt. fragrance (I) and 0.01-0.5 pt. wt. skin conditioner (I). In phase (F), the humectant (IV) is propylene glycol, the chelating agent is p-hydroxyacetophenone, and the emollient is hexylene glycol. In phase (G), the skin conditioner (I) is muscle peptide. In phase (D), the humectant (III) comprises 40-60 pts. wt. white sugar, 20-30 pts. wt. lactose and 20-30 pts. wt. maltose syrup, and the filler powder comprises 20-50 pts. wt. mother-of-pearl powder, 20-50 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl starch sodium phosphate and 20-50 pts. wt. conchiolin powder. The phase (D) further comprises 0.0002-0.002 pt. wt. skin conditioner (II), 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. fragrance (II) and 0.0001-0.001 pt. wt. pigment, where the skin conditioner (II) comprises 0.0001-0.001 pt. wt. carnosine and 0.0001-0.001 pt. wt. oat peptide, and each portion of powder component further comprises 0.001-0.05 pt. wt. skin conditioner (III) comprising ceramide. Preferred Method: The gel component is prepared by adding phase (C) to emulsifying device, increasing the temperature to 80-85 degrees C, stirring, sequentially adding humectant (I) and film forming agent, uniformly stirring and dispersing, maintaining the temperature at 80-85 degrees C and continuously stirring to obtain mixture, and heating phase (B) at 65-75 degrees C, uniformly stirring and melting, adding to obtained mixture, homogenizing and stirring, cooling after completing emulsification to less than 38 degrees C, and filtering to obtain gel component. The gel component preparation involves heating phase (B) at 65-75 degrees C, uniformly stirring and melting, adding to obtained mixture, homogenizing and stirring, after completing emulsification, cooling to 45-50 degrees C, adding pre-mixed dissolved phase (F), uniformly stirring and continuously cooling, when the temperature is reduced to less than 40 degrees C, adding pre-mixed dissolved phase (G), uniformly stirring the mixture, continuously reducing the temperature to less than 38 degrees C, and filtering to obtain gel component. The oxygen-injecting component is prepared by uniformly mixing phase (D) components, and filling phase (E) carbon dioxide to obtain oxygen-injecting component. The powder component is prepared by uniformly mixing raw materials to obtain powder particle component.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PT P
PN CN112438223-A
TI Transporting aquatic product living body involves adding dry ice and transportation water into living body transportation box, closing cover body and fully dissolving dry ice and transportation water.
AU HAO Y
   TONG Y
   DONG D
AE YUMIAO SHANGHAI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (YUMI-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI BOKAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021243689
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding dry ice and transportation water into a living body transportation box. The cover body is closed. The dry ice and the transportation water are fully dissolved to form high concentration carbon dioxide solution. Fresh water product needed to be transported is put into the high concentration carbon dioxide solution in the living body transportation box to soak for 1-2 minutes. A certain amount of transportation water is added into the living body transportation box. The water dissolved oxygen detector installed in the living body transport box detects the dissolved oxygen in the transport water in the living body transport box in real time. The oxygen is supplied to the transport water by the oxygen supply pipe located in the living body transport box. The aquatic product is taken out from the living body transportation box after reaching the destination. The aquatic product is put into a breeding box with aeration device to be recovered.
   USE - Method for transporting aquatic product living body by dry ice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, small water product stress reaction, good anesthetic effect of aquatic product, no food safety hazard, high transportation survival rate and low transportation cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The round honeycomb active carbon is added for transportation in the living body transport case. The aquatic product comprises fish, shrimp and shell. Preferred Conditions: The water for transportation uses clean water with the salinity of 12-15.

PN CN112441968-A; CN112441968-B
TI Synthesis of methyl 2-amino-3-bromoisonicotinate involves preparing 2-amino-3-bromopyridine-4-carboxylic acid using e.g. diisopropylamine and tetrahydrofuran, mixing with hydrochloric acid methanol solution, heating and reacting.
AU XU Y
   DAI H
AE ALI BIOLOGICAL NEW MATERIALS CHANGZHOU (ALIB-Non-standard)
GA 202124409E
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of methyl 2-amino-3-bromoisonicotinate (D) involves (1) adding diisopropylamine and tetrahydrofuran into reactor, reacting under nitrogen protection, cooling, adding n-butyl lithium, stirring, heating, stirring, cooling, adding 3-bromo-2-fluoropyridine (A), stirring, protecting with carbon dioxide, heating, and reacting to obtain 3-bromo-2-fluoropyridine-4-carboxylic acid (B), (2) adding the compound (B) and ammonia into autoclave, heating and reacting to obtain 2-amino-3-bromopyridine-4-carboxylic acid (C), (3) mixing the compound (C) and hydrochloric acid methanol solution, heating and reacting.
   USE - Synthesis of methyl 2-amino-3-bromoisonicotinate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high purity product with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of methyl 2-amino-3-bromoisonicotinate of formula (D) involves (1) adding diisopropylamine and tetrahydrofuran into reactor, reacting under nitrogen protection, cooling, adding n-butyl lithium, stirring, heating, stirring, cooling, adding 3-bromo-2-fluoropyridine of formula (A), stirring, protecting with carbon dioxide, heating, and reacting to obtain 3-bromo-2-fluoropyridine-4-carboxylic acid of formula (B), (2) adding the compound (B) and ammonia into autoclave, heating and reacting to obtain 2-amino-3-bromopyridine-4-carboxylic acid of formula (C), (3) mixing the compound (C) and hydrochloric acid methanol solution, heating and reacting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (1), the solid-to-liquid ratio (g/mL) of compound (A) to tetrahydrofuran is 1:12. The mass ratio of compound (A) and diisopropylamine is 5:3-5. The volume ratio of n-butyllithium to tetrahydrofuran is 1:3-6. The solid-to-liquid ratio (g/mL) of compound (B) and ammonia in the step (2) is 1:15-20. In step (3), the solid-to-liquid ratio (g/mL) of the compound (C) and the hydrochloric acid methanol solution is 1:20-25.

PN US2021060486-A1; WO2022101323-A1
TI Facility for producing gaseous biomethane by purifying biogas from landfill, has dryer which is arranged downstream deoxo to receive oxygen depleted gas flow capable of removing water from oxygen depleted gas flow.
AU PRINCE G
AE WAGA ENERGY (WAGA-Non-standard)
   WAGA ENERGY (WAGA-Non-standard)
GA 2021248827
AB    NOVELTY - The facility has a compression unit that is configured for compressing initial gas flow of biogas to be purified. A VOC purification unit is arranged downstream of compression unit to receive compressed initial flow of biogas and is provided ith an adsorber loaded with adsorbents capable of reversibly adsorbing VOCs to produce VOC-depleted gas flow. A membrane is arranged downstream of VOC purification unit to receive VOC-depleted gas flow and subject the VOC-depleted gas flow to membrane separation. A cryodistillation unit is provided with a heat exchanger and a distillation column. A deoxo is arranged downstream to cryodistillation unit to receive gas distillate from distillation column capable of converting oxygen present in gas distillate into carbon dioxide and water to produce an oxygen depleted gas flow. A dryer is arranged downstream the deoxo to receive the oxygen depleted gas flow capable of removing water from oxygen depleted gas flow.
   USE - Facility for producing gaseous biomethane by purifying biogas from landfill.
   ADVANTAGE - The moisture is easily removed with temperature swing adsorption. The nitrogen is easily adjusted in the distillation column to compensate any excess of carbon dioxide produced in deoxo.
