TI Preparing micro-nano silver powder, comprises e.g.            mixing silver compound, dispersant and potassium            sulfate, and ball milling in low-temperature stirring            ball mill, performing hydrothermal reaction, cooling,            separating, washing and drying.
AU HE R
   ZHU W
   LUO Y
   BAI W
   YUE H
   LIN D
   LI C
   REN Y
   WANG Q
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 202115215P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-nano silver powder, comprises                (i) mixing silver compound, dispersant and                potassium sulfate to obtain mixed raw materials,                and placing materials in a ball milling tank of                low-temperature stirring ball mill, then adding                zirconium oxide milling balls, and ball milling by                using liquid nitrogen as ball milling medium to                obtain ball milling materials, (ii) adding ball                milling raw materials to ball milling reaction                kettle, and adding zirconium oxide milling balls                and alcohol to perform wet ball milling reaction to                obtain silver-containing mixture, and (iii) adding                silver-containing mixture and reducing agent A into                supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, sealing the                reactor, then introducing carbon dioxide, releasing                pressure, and adding all reacted materials into                hydrothermal reaction kettle, adding reducing agent                B, and performing hydrothermal reaction, cooling to                room temperature, separating the reaction product,                washing, and drying to obtain micro-nano silver                powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing micro-nano                silver powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can produces micro-nano silver                powder with high tap density, excellent                dispersibility, narrow powder grain diameter                distribution, excellent stability and conductivity,                excellent adhesive force and printing                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iii): introducing                  carbon dioxide for 1-3 hours at 45-55 degrees C                  under pressure of 15-20 MPa; and hydrothermal                  reaction is carried out at 100-180 degrees C for                  4-6 hours. Preferred Components: In step (i):                  silver compound is silver citrate, silver                  nitrate, silver carbonate, silver acetate or                  silver trifluoroacetate; and dispersant is                  carboxymethyl cellulose, ammonium polyacrylate,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyethylene glycol,                  polymethacrylamide or lauric acid. In step (ii):                  alcohol is isopropanol, isobutanol or                  pentaerythritol.

PN CN112255256-A
TI Quantitative evaluation method of movable ratio of shale oil, involves selecting multiple representative shale oil reservoir rock cores based on geological condition, and determining total signal strength of original retained hydrocarbon.
AU FU D
   MA Y
   YANG F
   DUAN Z
   TIAN T
   MA L
   HE D
AE SHAANXI COAL GEOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202111236Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting multiple representative shale oil reservoir rock cores based on geological condition. The rock core is processed according to nuclear magnetic resonance two-dimensional spectrum test related requirement. The total signal strength of an original retained hydrocarbon in a shale is determined through position characteristic of different hydrogen-containing components on the two-dimensional spectrum. A pressure relief valve of a displacing tank is opened after displacement is finished. A movable shale oil ratio is obtained based on carbon dioxide displacement experiment.
   USE - The method is useful for evaluating quantitative movable ratio of shale oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables obtaining index parameter of residual hydrocarbon content in a shale reservoir and residual hydrocarbon content after displacement based on nuclear magnetic resonance two-dimensional spectrum process; enables simulating carbon dioxide throughput experiment under stratum condition so as to replace the shale by oil; enables utilizing shale oil underground mining process so as to improve characteristic of accurate and convenient data calculation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the evaluating quantitative movable ratio of shale oil (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN112138694-A
TI Preparing graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon in degradation of trichloroethylene, by uniformly mixing graphitic carbon nitride precursor, pine wood chips and silver orthophosphate.
AU LV H
   SHEN B
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202103106H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon involves uniformly mixing graphitic carbon nitride precursor, pine wood chips and silver orthophosphate to obtain a mixed material precursor, keeping the mixed material precursor at 300-600 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain the product, where the ratio of the graphitic carbon nitride precursor, pine sawdust and silver orthophosphate is (1-5):(1-5):1.
   USE - The method is used for preparing graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon in degradation of trichloroethylene such as ethylene, carbon dioxide and water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption rate of the composite material to trichloroethylene increased by 2-4 times, compared with the single material, after the adsorption equilibrium, at the 4 hour of visible light irradiation, the catalytic degradation efficiency of graphitic carbon nitride, silver orthophosphate, biomass carbon and graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon on trichloroethylene were 29%, 31%, 25%, and 98%. The analysis results of degradation products show that: graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon achieves high-efficiency degradation of trichloroethylene, and the degradation products are mainly carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon in degradation of trichloroethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for achieving the uniform mixing is: mixing the graphitic carbon nitride precursor, pine sawdust and silver orthophosphate, and putting into the ball milling tank of a planetary ball mill for ball milling for 2-24 hours, where the mass sum of the graphitic carbon nitride precursor, pine sawdust and silver orthophosphate is M, and the ratio of the M to the grinding balls in the planetary ball mill is 1:(50-100), and the revolution speed of the planetary ball mill during ball milling is 300-700 rpm. Preferred Conditions: The heat preservation step of the mixed material precursor at 300-600 degrees C is: placing the mixed material precursor in a furnace portion and raising the temperature to the 300-600 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: The graphitic carbon nitride precursor is melamine and/or urea. The environment when the graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon degrades trichloroethylene is under visible light or dark environment, when the environment is a dark environment, the trichloroethylene aqueous solution is continuously stirred during degradation. The environment when the graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon degrades trichloroethylene is first degraded under dark conditions for 10-24 hours, and degraded under visible light irradiation for 2-6 hours. Preferred Property: The pH of the trichloroethylene aqueous solution is adjusted to 5-9, and the graphite phase carbon nitride/silver/biomass carbon is put into the trichloroethylene aqueous solution.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112010892-A
TI Extracting egg yolk lecithin rich in docosahexaenoic acid as raw material for e.g. biomedicine, by separating egg white, egg shell and egg yolk, adding protease, enzymatically hydrolyzing, centrifuging, freeze-drying and extracting.
AU LIU Y
   ZHU S
   ZHANG M
   YANG R
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   MILLE DAIRY SHANGHAI CO LTD (MILL-Non-standard)
   MILLE SHANGHAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MILL-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4656H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting egg yolk lecithin rich in docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), involves (i) separating egg white, egg shell and egg yolk to obtain egg white protein, egg shell calcium source functional materials and egg yolk fresh liquid, adding protease to the egg yolk liquid, enzymatically hydrolyzing the egg yolk protein, separating lecithin rich in DHA, inactivating the enzyme, centrifuging and freeze-drying to obtain a enzymatically hydrolyzed egg yolk powder, (ii) using supercritical carbon dioxide to extract the enzymatically hydrolyzed egg yolk powder to obtain egg yolk oil and defatted egg yolk powder, and separating the egg yolk oil, and (iii) adding food grade ethanol as an entrainer, and continuously performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of defatted egg yolk powder to obtain egg yolk lecithin rich in DHA.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting egg yolk lecithin rich in DHA as high-quality raw materials for biomedicine and food processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains the natural activity of lecithin, has simple process, high product selectivity and no solvent residue, realizes the high-efficiency extraction of DHA-rich egg yolk lecithin, and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves freeze-drying the separated egg white to obtain egg white protein powder, and calcining the eggshell at 500-600 degrees C to obtain calcium source functional materials. Preferred Conditions: The enzymatic hydrolysis is carried out at 20-50 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the enzymatically hydrolyzed egg yolk powder to obtain egg yolk oil is carried out at 30-50 degrees C and extraction pressure of 6-9 MPa for 2-4 hours. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of egg yolk lecithin rich in DHA is carried out at 25-45 degrees C and pressure of 15-20 MPa for 1-3 hours, and the pressure of the separation tank is maintained at 3-8 MPa. Preferred Components: The protease is a neutral protease with broad-spectrum action, where the added amount is 80-400 U/g of egg yolk. The molecular structure of egg yolk lecithin is not damaged, and DHA is mainly located at the 2C position on the glyceride structure of lecithin. The fatty acid composition of the egg yolk oil comprises omega -3 and omega -6 unsaturated fatty acids, where the content of oleic acid, linoleic acid, linolenic acid and DHA is greater than or equal to 65%. Preferred Composition: The amount of food grade ethanol added as an entrainer is 5-15 wt.% of the amount of the raw material. The DHA content in the obtained egg yolk lecithin product is 20-26 mg/g fresh egg yolk, which is converted into a DHA content of 300-442 mg per fresh egg.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN111437834-A; CN111437834-B
TI Performing in-situ heterostructure construction            based on zinc indium sulfide nanosheets is used in            photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, involves            adding divalent zinc salt, trivalent indium salt and            sulfur source.
AU YU Y
   ZHAO L
   ZHUANG Z
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202071780R
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in-situ heterostructure                construction based on zinc indium sulfide                nanosheets involves adding divalent zinc salt,                trivalent indium salt and sulfur source to mixed                solution of deionized water and ethanol, dissolving                them, and making uniformly dispersed reaction                precursor liquid. The reaction precursor liquid is                transferred to stainless steel autoclave lined with                polytetrafluoroethylene, and constant temperature                reaction is conducted in drying box, then cooled,                centrifuged, washed, and dried until water is                completely volatilized to obtain ultra-thin zinc                indium sulfide nanosheets in yellow solid powder.                The 30 mg of the ultra-thin ZnIn2S4 nanosheets                prepared above is placed in 30mL ammonia solution,                stirred at constant temperature for certain period                of time, and grown heterojunction in situ on the                ZnIn2S4 nanosheets. The centrifugal separation,                washing, and drying are performed until water is                completely volatilized to obtain final                product.
   USE - Method for performing in-situ heterostructure                construction based on zinc indium sulfide                nanosheets is used in photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform in-situ                heterostructure construction based on zinc indium                sulfide nanosheets that has excellent performance                in reduction, simple preparation process, short                cycle, low cost and good economic and environmental                benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent zinc salt                  is non-toxic zinc acetate dihydrate Zn(CH3COO)2                  2H2O. The trivalent indium salt is non-toxic                  indium chloride tetrahydrate. The sulfur source                  is thioacetamide. Preferred Conditions: The molar                  ratio of the added divalent zinc salt, trivalent                  indium salt and sulfur source is 1:2:4. The                  volume ratio of the mixed solution of deionized                  water and absolute ethanol is 1:1. The washing                  solvent is deionized water and absolute ethanol                  which are washed twice respectively. The aqueous                  ammonia solution is mixed solution of V ammonia                  water and V deionized water in ratio of                  2:1.

PN CN111088112-A
TI Extraction of wet algae oil using switchable butanediamine solvent by mixing N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine and microalgae biomass, oil extracting, centrifuging, introducing carbon dioxide gas, completely dissolving, and standing.
AU CHENG J
   YANG W
   LIU J
   CEN K
   ZHOU J
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Z
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020388116
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of wet algae oil using switchable butanediamine solvent involves mixing switchable solvents N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine and microalgae biomass with water content of 5-85% at volume ratio of 4:1, continuously stirring at room temperature for 24 hours, cell wall-breaking and oil extracting to obtain organic phase rich in oil and algae residue extracted from oil, separating and removing algae residue by centrifugation to obtain organic phase rich in oil, adding deionized water to form two-phase layer comprising upper layer organic phase rich in oil and lower layer water phase, introducing carbon dioxide gas to switch N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine from hydrophobic form to hydrophilic form, completely dissolving lower layer water phase, separating oil from organic phase N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine, standing and separating to obtain upper layer microalgae oil and lower layer water phase.
   USE - The method is used for extracting wet algae oil using switchable butanediamine solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy-saving, realizes green efficient extraction, recycles extractant for recycling, improves economical efficiency and solves environment problem.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves taking separated water phase, introducing nitrogen to switch N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine from hydrophilic form to hydrophobic form in water phase, dividing into upper layer organic phase and lower layer water phase, standing and separating organic phase and collecting supernatant to obtain switchable solvent N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine. The N,N,N',N'-tetraethyl-1,4-butanediamine is prepared by mixing and dissolving 73-730 g diethylamine and 54-540 g 1,4-dibromobutane in deionized water, heating to 50 degrees C, continuously stirring under nitrogen condition, nucleophilic substitution reacting for 7 hours to complete alkylation, adding 10-100 g sodium hydroxide powder, adjusting pH to 10-12, centrifugally separating to obtain upper layer organic phase, and placing in vacuum drying box organic phase for 24 hours. Stirring is done using magnetic stirrer at speed of 100-500 revolutions/minute (rpm).

The combustion chamber is connected to the water drainage line from the gas separator at the point of supply of geothermal water to the heat exchange equipment. A tank is installed on the water supply line to the well for the content of carbon dioxide at the outlet of heat exchange equipment, while water outlet line is arranged to supply geothermal water to the heat exchange equipment and scaling sensors are connected to the valve regulating the flow of gas into the combustion chamber.
   USE - Geothermal power plant used in design and operation of geothermal heating and hot water supply stations.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy efficiency of geothermal water sources with associated combustible gases is increased by increasing the stability of geothermal water and protecting heat exchange equipment and the well from carbonate deposits.

   CHEN S
   YAN X
   FAN B
   WANG Y
   LI R
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201999076A
AB    NOVELTY - An ordered mesoporous titanium-aluminum composite oxide is prepared by taking titanium source, aluminum source, block copolymer-type nonionic surfactant, organic carboxylic acid, deionized water and absolute ethanol as raw material, dissolving deionized water, block copolymer-type nonionic surfactants, organic carboxylic acid, titanium source and aluminum source in absolute ethanol to obtain clear solution, refluxing at 60-150 degrees C to obtain inorganic titanium covered with surfactant micelle sol and aluminum hydroxyl species, removing solvent in surfactant micelle sol, and calcining at 400-650 degrees C. The molar ratio of titanium source:aluminum source:organic acid:deionized water:absolute ethanol:block copolymer-type nonionic surfactant is 5-50:10-100:5-80:100-300:500-3000:1.
   USE - The ordered mesoporous titanium-aluminum composite oxide used as catalyst for carbon dioxide oxidative dehydrogenation of ethylbenzene to styrene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has highly regular and ordered two-dimensional hexagonal phase mesoporous structure and titanium/aluminum species in pore wall reaching uniform atomic dispersion, and shows extremely high reactivity and styrene selectivity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the ordered mesoporous titanium-aluminum composite oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum source is aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum isobutanol, tert-butanol, aluminum nitrate, aluminum chloride and/or aluminum sulfate. The titanium source is titanium isopropoxide, tetrabutyl titanate, methyl ester titanate or titanate. The composite oxide has mesoporous aperture of 5.0-12.0 nm, specific surface area of 180-460 m2/g, pore volume of 0.3-1.3 cm3/g, aluminum/titanium atomic ratio of 0.2-20 and crystal grain size of less than 2 nm. Preferred Method: Refluxing is done for 6-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The block copolymer-type nonionic surfactant is of formula EOnPOmEOn, EOnBOmEOn or EOnPS with polyoxyethylene as hydrophilic block, nonionic block copolymer, polypropylene oxide, polybutylene or polystyrene as hydrophobic block. The organic carboxylic acid is citric acid, glacial acetic acid, oxalic acid or tartaric acid.n=10-200;m=5-120;EO=ethylene epoxide;PO=propylene oxide;BO=epoxy butylenes; andPS=polystyrene.

PN CN110302825-A; CN110302825-B
TI Preparation of transition metal nitrogen-carbide            composite electrocatalytic material used for            electrocatalysis in carbon dioxide reduction, involves            mixing aqueous ammonia with soluble metal salt and            adding sodium polyacrylate.
AU WU D
   GUO J
   LIU W
   CHEN J
   CAO K
   WANG H
   GAO Z
   XU F
   JIANG K
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
   UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 2019972420
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of transition metal nitrogen                carbide composite electrocatalytic material                involves mixing aqueous ammonia with soluble metal                salt in a molar ratio of 4:1-20:1 to form 0.001-1 M                metal ammine complex ion aqueous solution, adding                sodium polyacrylate to metal ammine complex ion                aqueous solution in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:5,                stirring, lyophilizing to obtain an intermediate,                placing the intermediate in a porcelain boat and                tube furnace, passing the shielding gas, heating at                700-1100 degrees C for 1-3 hours at a heating rate                of 3-10 degrees C/minute, immersing in dilute                sulfuric acid for 2-8 hours, suction filtering,                washing with secondary water to neutralize and                drying in a vacuum drying oven.
   USE - Preparation of transition metal nitrogen                carbide composite electrocatalytic material used                for electrocatalysis in carbon dioxide                reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The transition metal nitrogen carbide                composite electrocatalytic material is economically                prepared by a simple method using easily available                raw materials without solvent. The                cobalt-nitrogen-carbide composite electrocatalytic                material has low initial potential and current                density, and high catalytic current density,                catalytic stability and catalytic selectivity and                has Faraday efficiency of up to 93%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble metal salt                  comprises nitrate, acetate or chloride of copper,                  silver, zinc, manganese or cobalt. The shielding                  gas is nitrogen or argon. Preferred Process:                  Preparation of transition metal nitrogen-carbide                  composite electrocatalytic material, preferably                  cobalt-nitrogen-carbide composite                  electrocatalytic material involves mixing aqueous                  ammonia with soluble metal salt of cobalt                  chloride in a molar ratio of 10:1 to form metal                  ammine complex ion aqueous solution having                  concentration of 0.01 M, adding sodium                  polyacrylate to the metal ammine complex ion                  aqueous solution in mass ratio of 1:3, stirring,                  adsorbing, freeze-drying to obtain an                  intermediate, placing the intermediate in a                  porcelain boat and tube furnace, passing through                  nitrogen gas, heating at 1100 degrees C for 1                  hour at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute,                  immersing in dilute sulfuric acid for 2-8 hours,                  suction filtering, washing with secondary water                  to neutralize and drying in a vacuum drying                  oven.

PN CN109759703-A
TI Welding AZ91 magnesium alloy ultrafine crystal sheet material involves grinding or cleaning surfaces of portions to be welded of the workpiece to be welded, and fixing workpiece to be welded after grinding or cleaning on the welding fixture.
AU SHEN Y
AE HUNAN AUTOMOTIVE ENG VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019468849
AB    NOVELTY - Welding an AZ91 magnesium alloy ultrafine crystal sheet material involves grinding or cleaning the surfaces of the portions to be welded of the workpiece to be welded, fixing the workpiece to be welded after grinding or cleaning on the welding fixture, and setting the process parameters in which the carbon-dioxide laser output beam mode is TEM01, the laser wavelength is 10.6 mu m, and the laser beam diameter before focusing is 30-40 mm. The carbon-dioxide (CO2) laser has a focal depth L of 0.2-0.5 mm and a focal diameter d of 0.8-1.0 mm. The welding current is 5-7 Ampere, and the welding speed is 1-8 m/minute.
   USE - Method for welding AZ91 magnesium alloy ultrafine crystal sheet material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to weld AZ91 magnesium alloy ultrafine crystal sheet material, which does not require special equipment or equipment, has low cost, good welding performance and easy application, and avoid welding cracks, undercuts, pores, pits and partial penetrated welds by studying the dimensional relationship between the welding speed, the welding current and the penetration depth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The workpiece to be welded is cleaned by a chemical method in which the welded workpiece is allowed to stand in a dilute nitric acid solution having a concentration of 2.5-3.0 wt.% for 30 to 45 seconds, placed in a 3-4.5 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution for 20-25 seconds, and then the surface is washed with deionized water and allowed to dry. The gas protection of the welding is carried out by mixing CO2, nitrogen (N2) and helium (He), and the volume ratio of the three is 1:5-8:15-18. The flow rate of the gas protection is 8-12 L/minute. The welding current is 5.5-6.5 Ampere, and the welding speed is 5-8 m/minutes.

PN US10287943-B1
TI Emissions management system for heavy-heavy duty            diesel engine for large cargo-transport vehicles, has            after-treatment system for injecting amount of diesel            exhaust fluid comprising urea compound upstream of            catalytic reduction system.
AU SCHILTZ M
AE CLEAN POWER TECHNOLOGIES LLC (CLEA-Non-standard)
   M & R DEV (MRMR-Non-standard)
GA 201941393T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an exhaust thermal management                system for facilitating rapid diesel oxidation                catalyst (DOC) and selected catalytic reduction                (SCR) thermal activation. A diesel oxidation                catalyst, a diesel particulate filter and a                selective catalytic reduction system (230) are                provided in an after-treatment system. The                after-treatment system injects an amount of diesel                exhaust fluid (231) comprising urea compound                upstream of the selective catalytic reduction                system to reduce a portion of nitrogen oxide                emission to nitrogen, carbon dioxide and water. A                software program monitors and precisely controls a                natural gas substitution rate (125), a dosing rate                for diesel fuel and a Natural gas injection                rate.

TI Method for suppressing sooting in vacuum carburizing method, involves introducing oxidizing gas composed of carbon dioxide into furnace during carburization period when carburizing gas is introduced into furnace.
AU URYU K
   KAGOTANI Y
   TAKANO Y
AE KASEI SEISAKU KK (KASE-Non-standard)
GA 201912139A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing a carburizing gas into a furnace maintained at a high temperature higher than a pressure-reduced atmosphere and a carburizable temperature, to carburize a iron-based workpiece in a furnace. An oxidizing gas composed of carbon dioxide is introduced into the furnace during the carburization period when the carburizing gas is introduced into the furnace. The carburizing gas and the oxidizing gas are introduced into the furnace at a molar ratio of carburizing gas: oxidizing gas is equal to 1:1/25-1:3/2.
   USE - Method for suppressing sooting in vacuum carburizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon released from the carburizing atmosphere gas is transformed into carbon monoxide, and excess carbon generated during the carburization period is consumed. The carbon is prevented from floating in the atmosphere in the furnace and becoming soot and adhering to the surface of the workpiece subjected to the vacuum carburization treatment. Excess cementite in the surface layer of the workpiece is reduced or eliminated. Therefore, the precipitation of cementite is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the heat pattern of suction suppressing method in the vacuum carburizing method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Method for preserving and preventing damage of small tomato, involves loading small tomato into container, sealing and pumping container to vacuum state and charging mixed gas of oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen into container.
AU XIAO J
   HOU W
   XUAN Y
AE GUANGZHOU PANYU POLYTECHNIC (GPYP-C)
GA 201902486P
AB    NOVELTY - A small tomato damage preserving and preventing method involves loading a small tomato into a container having a surface of low barrier material, sealing the container, pumping the container to a vacuum state and charging a mixed gas of 1-3 vol.% oxygen, 4-6 vol.% carbon dioxide and 91-95 vol.% nitrogen into the container.
   USE - Method for preserving and preventing damage of small tomato.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preserving and preventing damage of small tomato with prolonged preserving period and inhibited moisture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a small tomato packaging box, comprising case body and two buffer structure layer, where the buffer structure layer is formed as the container and the outer surface of the buffer structure layer is coated with a low barrier material, each of the buffer structure layers is fixed to an inner side of the casing at a predetermined interval, the buffer structure layer comprises fixed layer and multiples of placement holes for placing small tomatoes is provided on the fixed layer and the placement holes are arranged in isolation and the buffer structure layer is made of plant fibers.

PN CN108822420-A
TI Preparing low-density foam heat-insulating material comprises e.g. placing the foamed particles into foaming machine, heating and melting, injecting carbon dioxide foaming gas into the end of the unit, mixing, extruding, cooling and shaping.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
AE YANG C (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019124168
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-density foam heat-insulating material comprises (i) mixing 20 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 5 pts. wt. graphene and 8 pts. wt. triacetin, and stirring at 150 degrees C under 500 revolutions/minute for 1.5 hours to obtain mixture A; (ii) adding 4 pts. wt. silane coupling-modified silica and 40 pts. wt. polystyrene resin into the mixture A, and stirring at 80 degrees C under 80 revolutions/minute for 1 hour to obtain mixture B; (iii) mixing 12 pts. wt. glycol and 10 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate at normal temperature, stirring at 50 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, adding mixture B, stirring at 50 degrees C under 40 revolutions/minute for 1 hour, extruding and granulating, and cooling at room temperature to obtain foamed particles; and (iv) placing the foamed particles into a foaming machine, heating and melting, injecting carbon dioxide foaming gas into the end of the unit, mixing uniformly, extruding through a die, cooling and shaping.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-density foam heat-insulating material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves heat insulating property; and has good insulation effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation of silane coupling-modified silica comprises (a) adding 4 pts. wt. sodium silicate and 20 pts. wt. lime milk into 1000 ml deionized water, mixing uniformly, adding 0.5 pts. wt. glass fiber into the mixture, mixing and stirring at 150 revolutions/minute, and controlling the reaction temperature to 60 degrees C with a reaction time of 45 minutes to obtain calcium silicate gel; (b) carrying out hydrothermal synthesis reaction for dehydrating calcium silicate gel, performing reverse washing with 80 degrees C hot water under stirring condition, and controlling the stirring speed to 50 revolutions/minute with a reaction time of 25 minutes; (c) adding washed dealkalized calcium silicate gel into deionized water, adjusting the water-solid ratio in the slurry to 20:1, and performing mixing and stirring; (iv) stirring uniformly and placing in the reaction kettle at 180 degrees C, controlling the temperature for 2 hours with a stirring speed of 100 revolutions/minute, heat preservation at 180 degrees C for 1 hour with a stirring speed of 50 revolutions/minute, cooling to room temperature after completing the reaction, filtering, molding and drying to obtain hard calcium silicate; (v) mixing 4 pts. wt. hard calcium silicate and 6 pts. wt. coupling agent KH560 into three-mouth flask, adding 50 pts. wt. acetone, and ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, adding 1 pts. wt. triethylamine, and reflux reacting at 80 degrees C for 4 hours; and (vi) after cooling to room temperature after finishing the reaction, filtering, washing with acetone for three times, drying at 60 degrees C for24 hours.

GA 2018936656
AB    NOVELTY - Fluoropolyester-type detergent comprises 8-15 pts. wt. fluoropolycarboxylate polyester resin, 3-8 pts. wt. 12-phenylbutyryl stearic acid, 2-7 pts. wt. perfluorooctane sulfonic acid, 10-20 pts. wt. pentaerythritol tetra-n-heptanoate, 10-18 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 10-25 pts. wt. activated magnesium oxide, 20-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 18-40 pts. wt. solvent, 1-4 pts. wt. accelerating agent, 2-6 pts. wt. dispersion stabilizer and 0.5-2 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Used as fluoropolyester-type detergent.
   ADVANTAGE - The detergent has high-temperature resistance, anti-carbon performance, strong dispersing ability, base number of (380-420mgKOH/g), low-pour point, good anti-high-temperature performance, stable performance and good high-temperature lustration property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the detergent, comprising (i) completely mixing fluoropolycarboxylate polyester resin, 12-phenylbutyryl stearic acid, perfluorooctane sulfonic acid, pentaerythritol tetra-n-heptanoate, calcium hydroxide, activated magnesium oxide, solvent, accelerating agent and dispersion stabilizer, and neutralizing and reacting at 35-60 degrees C, and (ii) subsequently adding water, heating at 65-80 degrees C, reacting with carbon dioxide gas, degreasing the reaction product, and removing solvent by vacuum distillation; and #lubricating oil composition, comprising fluoropolyester-type detergent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The detergent comprises 12 pts. wt. fluoropolycarboxylate polyester resin, 5 pts. wt. 12-phenylbutyryl stearic acid, 4 pts. wt. perfluorooctane sulfonic acid, 14 pts. wt. pentaerythritol tetra-n-heptanoate, 14 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 16 pts. wt. activated magnesium oxide, 30 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 28 pts. wt. solvent, 2 pts. wt. accelerating agent, 4 pts. wt. dispersion stabilizer and 1 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The dispersion stabilizer is triglycerol monostearate. The solvent is toluene or xylene. The accelerating agent is trimethylamine or methanol. Preferred Method: The fluoropolycarboxypolyester resin is prepared by reating tetrafluorosuccinic acid, tetrafluorobutanediol, 3-fluoro-1,2-propanediol and citric acid, and polymerizing at 180-220 degrees C in the presence of a catalyst. The 12-phenylbutyryl stearic acid is prepared by reacting phenylbutyric acid and 12-hydroxystearic acid at 120-160 degrees C under catalysis using xylene as a watering agent. The aeration rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 60-300 ml/minutes. The detergent is added in an amount of 1-3% by mass based on the mass of the base oil. The base oil is synthetic ester base oil.

PN CN108404956-A
TI Black titanium dioxide composite catalyst used for reduction of carbon dioxide comprises black titanium dioxide nanotube semiconductor coated with graphitic carbon nitride semiconductor compound on surface.
AU WANG Y
   LI X
   XIA Q
   GE Z
AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 201866030B
AB    NOVELTY - A black titanium dioxide composite catalyst comprises black titanium dioxide nanotube semiconductor and 2-10 wt.% graphitic carbon nitride semiconductor compound. The graphitic carbon nitride is coated on surface of black titanium dioxide nanotube.
   USE - Black titanium dioxide composite catalyst used for reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the black titanium dioxide composite catalyst comprising taking titanium sheet with thickness of 0.05-0.2 mm, pretreating in hydrofluoric-nitric acid-water mixed solution for 10-50 seconds, taking pretreated titanium sheet as anode, graphite as cathode and mixed solution of 0.6-1.2 wt.% ammonium fluoride and 4-7 wt.% water in ethylene glycol solution as electrolyte, anode oxidizing at 20-100 V for 20-200 minutes, washing, drying to obtain titanium dioxide nanotube, placing in tubular furnace, performing hydrogen reduction at hydrogen gas volume concentration of 3-15%, pressure of 0.5-5 MPa and temperature of 300-600 degrees C for 1-8 hours to obtain black titanium dioxide nanotube, soaking in graphitic carbon nitride soaking solution for 5-20 hours, taking out, vacuum-drying at 50-80 degrees C for 5-10 hours and naturally cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite catalyst contains 4-7 wt.% graphitic carbon nitride semiconductor compound. The hydrofluoric acid has concentration of 15-30 wt.%. The nitric acid has concentration of 50-60 wt.%. The volume ratio of hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid and water is 1:3:6. The soaking solution is obtained by dispersing 0.02-0.1 g graphitic carbon nitride in 100 mL deionized water, and ultrasonically processing for 30-240 minutes.

TI New organoaluminum compound used for manufacturing            adsorbing material, comprises structure including            trivalent aluminum ions, ligand derived from aromatic            dicarboxylic acid, and ligand derived from heterocyclic            dicarboxylic acid.
AU OMURA T
   SETOYAMA N
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2018405261
AB    NOVELTY - An organoaluminum compound comprising a                structure including trivalent aluminum ions, a                ligand (a) derived from an aromatic dicarboxylic                acid chosen from isophthalic acid (I) and its                derivative, coordinated to trivalent aluminum ions,                and a ligand (b) derived from a heterocyclic                dicarboxylic acid (II), is new.
   USE - New organoaluminum compound is used for                manufacturing adsorbing material (all claimed) e.g.                gas-separation material for selectively adsorbing                and removing carbon dioxide from mixed gas of                carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon, and water                separation material for selectively adsorbing and                removing moisture content from humidified                atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbing material containing the                organoaluminum compound has excellent safety and                workability, desired specific surface area and pore                volume, and high water-vapor maximum adsorption                amount and high water-vapor adsorption amount at                low pressure side.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The isophthalic acid is of formula (I). The                heterocyclic dicarboxylic acid is of formula                (II).
   R = H, alkyl, hydroxy, or alkoxy;
   X = N, S, or O;and
   n = 2 or 3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) adsorbing material, which is a porous                component containing the organoaluminum                compound;
   (2) preparation of organoaluminum compound;                and
   (3) manufacture of adsorbing material, which                involves heat-processing the organoaluminum                compound in a poor solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of                  organoaluminum compound involves mixing the                  aromatic dicarboxylic acid and heterocyclic                  dicarboxylic acid to an aluminum compound, and                  heat-processing the obtained mixture. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of the ligand (a)                  with respect to the ligand (b) is 99-20:1-80. The                  heterocyclic dicarboxylic acid is                  pyridinedicarboxylic acid.
   EXAMPLE - In a screw tube, 400 mg aluminum sulfate                octahydrate, 190 mg 1,3-benzenedicarboxylic acid,                10 mg 3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid and 2 ml                N,N'-dimethylformamide were mixed, and the obtained                mixture was heated in an atmosphere at 90 degrees C                for 5 days. The resulting white precipitate was                recovered by filtration to obtain aluminum                metal-organic framework including                1,3-benzenedicarboxylic acid and                3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid in a molar ratio of                95:5. The aluminum metal-organic framework had a                theoretical value of nitrogen-to-carbon ratio of                0.0088, an actual measurement value of                nitrogen-to-carbon ratio of 0.0073,                Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific surface area of 676                m2/g, a pore volume of 0.30 ml/g, and a water-vapor                extraction amount of 0.14 g/g.

   USE - Used as composite steel slag grinding                aid.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite steel slag grinding aid: has                excellent grinding effect; and does not affect the                activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite steel slag grinding aid comprises                60-80 pts. wt. polyallylamine mixture solution,                8-10 pts. wt. grinding particle, 8 -10 pts. wt. egg                yolk hydrolyzate, 8-10 pts. wt. tea seed meal                hydrolyzate, 3-5 pts. wt. thickener, 4-8 pts. wt.                thixotropic agent and 4-8 pts. wt. inorganic salt,                where the preparation of composite steel slag                grinding aid includes mixing polyallylamine mixture                solution, grinding particle, egg yolk hydrolyzate,                tea seed meal hydrolyzate, tackifier, thixotropic                agent and inorganic salt, then performing                high-speed shearing at room temperature,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide and                discharging to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The polyallylamine mixture                  solution is prepared by mixing polyallylamine and                  water in a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12 to obtain                  polyallylamine mixed solution. The grinding                  particle is prepared by mixing fly ash and                  sepiolite powder in a mass ratio of 5:1-10:1. The                  preparation of egg yolk hydrolyzate comprises                  mixing egg yolk and water at a mass ratio of                  1:10-1:15, adding 3-5 wt.% egg yolk alkaline                  protease and stirring at a constant temperature                  for 3-5 hours, then inactivating enzyme and                  concentrating to obtain final product, where the                  egg yolk is chicken egg yolk, duck egg yolk or                  goose egg yolk. The preparation of tea seed meal                  hydrolyzate comprises mixing tea seed meal and                  water at a mass ratio of 1:5-1:8, adding 6-8 wt.%                  cellulase and 2-10 wt.% pectinase in tea see meal                  quality, then stirring for 6-8 hours at constant                  temperature, inactivating the enzyme and                  concentrating to obtain final product. The                  tackifier is prepared from coal tar and                  carrageenan in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:3. Preferred                  Components: The thixotropic agent is gas phase                  silicon dioxide, organic bentonite, hydrogenated                  castor oil or polyamide wax. The inorganic salt                  is composed of potassium permanganate and                  ammonium nitrate in a mass ratio of                  20:1-50:1.

TI Preparation of graphene/solid electrolyte-coated silicon composite material by chemical vapor depositing silicon anode material particles, dispersing into solvent, adding ammonia and oxide-based precursor ester, centrifuging, and drying.
AU CI L
   AI Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
   UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201806007Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene/solid electrolyte-coated silicon composite material includes placing silicon anode material particles to high temperature furnace, purging gaseous carbon source and carbon dioxide gas mixture, chemical vapor depositing, dispersing into solvent, adding ammonia, stirring, adding oxide-based precursor ester, stirring, centrifuging, and drying; and adding lithium hydroxide monohydrate, high temperature reacting, and cooling.
   USE - Method for preparation of graphene/solid electrolyte-coated silicon composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has double-layer coating, improved electronic conductivity, Coulomb efficiency and rate performance, reduced negative electrode material and electrolyte electrochemical reaction, and improved high current charge and discharge performances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of graphene/solid electrolyte-coated silicon composite material comprises placing 1 pt. wt. silicon anode material particles to high temperature furnace, purging gaseous carbon source and carbon dioxide gas mixture, chemical vapor depositing under inert gas atmosphere, dispersing into 300-2000 pts. wt. solvent, adding 5-50 pts. wt. 25-28% ammonia, stirring, adding 1-80 wt.% oxide-based precursor ester, stirring until ester is hydrolyzed into oxide coating, centrifuging, and drying; and adding lithium hydroxide monohydrate, high temperature reacting under inert gas protective atmosphere, and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high temperature furnace reaction is done at 800-1200 degrees C at heating rate of 5-40 degrees C/minute for 1 minute to 6 hours. The drying is done at 50-150 degrees C. The second step heating is done at 450-1000 degrees C and at heating rate of 1-30 degrees C/minute for 30 minutes to 12 hours. Preferred Components: The oxide coating is alumina, silica or titania. The silicon anode material particles comprise 1-20 nm thick graphene and 1-100 nm thick solid electrolyte.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous carbon source has volume content of 1-90%. The mixed solvent comprises absolute ethanol and water. The gaseous carbon source is acetylene, methane, ethane, ethylene, propylene, butane, or carbon monoxide.

GA 201800983G
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor (100) has flexible chambers                (104, 107, 108) connected to a wobbling element                (103), where each chamber includes an intake                passage and a discharge passage. The intake passage                contains a one-way valve (109). The wobbling                element sequentially presses down and pulls up the                chambers, where gas is drawn into each chamber                through the one-way valve responsive to the                wobbling element pulling up each chamber, and the                drawn gas is compressed to higher pressure and                discharged through the discharge passage responsive                to the wobbling element pressing down each                chamber.
   USE - Multistage oil-free gas compressor for medical                applications for compressing gas or gaseous matters                or fluids such as carbon dioxide and                refrigerants.
   ADVANTAGE - The flexible chambers form expandable and                collapsible compressing chambers that are pressed                down by the wobbling element to compress gas                trapped. The flexible one-way valve allows gas to                be sucked into the intake passage and prevents gas                from being pushed out from under the flexible                chamber through the intake passage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective sectional                view of a multistage compressor.

TI New pyridine/bipyridine conjugated microporous            polymer used for carbon dioxide adsorption and            separation.
AU LIAO Y
   HE Y
   CHENG Z
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2019795556
AB    NOVELTY - Pyridine/bipyridine conjugated microporous                polymer (I), (II) or (III), is new.
   USE - The pyridine/bipyridine conjugated microporous                polymer is used for carbon dioxide adsorption and                separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has certain specific surface area                and pores and good reversible adsorption-desorption                performance; and exhibits good adsorption                performance and carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity                as carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pyridine/bipyridine conjugated microporous                polymer of formula (I), (II) or (III), is new. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of                pyridine/bipyridine conjugated microporous                polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic ketone                  compound is 1,4-diacetylbenzene,                  1,3-diacetylbenzene or 2,6-diacetylpyridine and                  2,2'-bipyridyl-5,5'-diformaldehyde at molar ratio                  of 1:2. The solvent is pyridine. The organic                  solvent is chloroform. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of ammonium acetate and reaction raw                  material is 5:1-20:1 where reaction raw material                  is mixture of 2,2'-bipyridyl-5,5'-dicarbaldehyde                  and aromatic ketone compound. The ratio of                  solvent and 2,2'-bipyridyl-5,5'-diformaldehyde is                  50-100:1 (mL/mmol). Preferred Method: The washing                  process uses deionized water and organic solvent                  at 60 degrees C for 24 hours. The drying step is                  done by vacuum-drying at 60-100 degrees C for                  20-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Pyridine/bipyridine                  conjugated microporous polymer is prepared by                  mixing 2,2'-bipyridyl-5,5'-dicarbaldehyde and                  aromatic ketone compound, adding ammonium acetate                  and solvent, stirring, heating in oil bath at                  110-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours, filtering,                  washing and drying.

PT P
PN CN114045516-A
TI Catalyst used for electrically catalyzing and reducing carbon dioxide for preparing formic acid for e.g. leather production, rubber manufacturing and textile dyeing, comprises tin nanoparticles and bismuth oxide nano sheets, where tin nanoparticles are deposited on bismuth oxide nano sheets.
AU MA T
   YE J
   ZHANG N
   YANG C
   PENG J
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202230014U
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises tin nanoparticles and bismuth oxide nano sheets, where the tin nanoparticles are deposited on the bismuth oxide nano sheets.
   USE - The catalyst is used for electrically catalyzing and reducing carbon dioxide for preparing formic acid (claimed) for leather production, rubber manufacturing and textile dyeing, and as chemical for industrial application, and substitute raw material of intermediate and fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst enables the catalyst to effectively convert carbon dioxide to formate at high current density and selectivity of high formic acid. The catalyst provides higher carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, improves the stability of the carbon dioxide-intermediate, and also limit the rate step length from the initial electron transfer step to the subsequent step hydrogenation step. Due to high electronegativity, the interface between tin and bismuth is very good for charge transfer. The enhanced edge site exposed nano-sheet structure also can promote the mass transfer of carbon dioxide and formic acid ion in the reaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst, which involves (i) dissolving bismuth nitrate and urea in ethylene glycol solution, stirring and dissolving, performing a hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging the solid product, washing and drying to obtain bismuth oxide nanosheets, (ii) dispersing the bismuth oxide nanosheet obtained in step (i) and polyperfluorosulfonic acid in a mixture of water and ethanol, uniformly mixing to obtain a homogeneous catalyst ink, drop-casting the catalyst ink on the substrate, drying, and attaching bismuth oxide nanosheet to carbon paper to obtain bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode, and (iii) mixing and dissolving sodium pyrophosphate, tartaric acid, water and tin chloride to obtain a deposition solution, soaking the bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode prepared in step (ii) in the deposition solution as a working electrode to perform electrolysis, and depositing tin nanoparticles on the bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode to obtain tin-bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode; and
   (2) the use of the catalyst for electrically catalyzing and reducing carbon dioxide for preparing formic acid, which involves using the catalyst to make a working electrode of a battery, where the electrolyte of the battery is an aqueous solution containing a proton donor, and the battery comprises a cathode chamber and an anode chamber, placing the working electrode in the cathode chamber, passing carbon dioxide to the electrolyte in the cathode chamber, and applying electricity to reduce carbon dioxide to formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst further comprises a carbon paper substrate, where the bismuth oxide nanosheets are attached to the carbon paper. The proton donor is potassium bicarbonate.Preferred Method: The tin nanoparticles are deposited on the bismuth oxide nanosheets through an electrochemical reaction. The step (i) involves taking 0.182 g bismuth (III) nitrate pentahydrate and 0.08 g urea and dissolving in 25ml ethylene glycol solution, stirring until all chemicals are dissolved, transferring the solution to an autoclave, sealing and reacting at 150degrees Celsius for 12 hours, cooling the autoclave to room temperature, centrifuging the solid product, washing with ultrapure water and absolute ethanol for 3 times, and drying the sample at 60degrees Celsius for 12 hours to obtain bismuth oxide nanosheets. The step (ii) involves dispersing 5 mg bismuth oxide nanosheets and 50 mul polyperfluorosulfonic acid solution in a mixture of 500 mul water and 450 mul ethanol, ultrasonically treating to form a homogeneous catalyst ink, drop-casting the catalyst ink onto carbon paper and drying at room temperature to obtain bismuth oxide nanosheet electrodes. The step (iii) involves dissolving 3 g sodium tetraborate decahydrate and 0.1608 g tartaric acid in 50 ml deionized water, adding to 0.7255 g tin chloride, and stirring until dissolved to obtain a deposition solution, soaking the bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode in the deposition solution as the working electrode, using silver/silver chloride and graphite as the reference electrode and counter electrode, and electrodepositing tin nanoparticles on bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode using cyclic voltammetry to obtain tin-bismuth oxide nanosheet electrode. The application involves using an H-type electrochemical battery as the battery, separating the battery by a proton exchange membrane to obtain an anode chamber and a cathode chamber, before electrocatalysis, using a magnetic stirrer to stir the electrolyte in the cathode chamber, introducing carbon dioxide to the electrolyte in the cathode chamber to saturate the proton donor in the electrolyte, and performing electrocatalytic reaction by electrifying.

PT P
PN CN113751062-A
TI Preparing porous copper-based catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce ethanol, involves dissolving template agent and soluble salt of copper in solvent, depositing on molecular sieve by equal volume dipping process.
AU BIAN W
   LUO S
   GAO L
   GUO T
   SONG X
AE INNER MONGOLIA ERDOS POWER METALLURGY (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2022045549
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous copper-based catalyst involves dissolving template agent and soluble salt of copper in solvent, depositing on molecular sieve by equal volume dipping process, drying, roasting to obtain the loaded molecular sieve, reacting the obtained loaded molecular sieve with a strong base solution, removing the template agent after the reaction, drying to obtain a sample, mixing the aluminum source, the silicon source, the strong base, the template agent, and the obtained sample to obtain a gel-embedded body, curing, drying to obtain a gel, crystallizing the obtained gel, washing, drying and calcining to obtain the copper-based catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing porous copper-based catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce ethanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared catalyst has high specific surface area, cost-effective, high activity, high ethanol selectivity and high stability, and has good application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application method of producing ethanol, which involves: (a)adding the porous copper-based catalyst into a solid bed reactor;(b)reducing the catalyst in situ by allowing hydrogen and nitrogen mixed gas to passing through the solid bed reactor;(c) reacting the carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas through a solid bed reactor to obtain ethanol, where the volume fraction of hydrogen in the hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas is 1-10%, the linear velocity of the hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas is 0.4-14 m/seconds, the temperature of the bed layer during passing is 300-400degrees Celsius, and the in-situ reduction time is 1-3 hours, most preferably, the volume fraction of hydrogen in the hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas is 5%, the linear speed of the hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas passing through is 4 m/seconds, the temperature of a bed layer when the hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas passes through is 350degrees Celsius, and the in-situ reduction time is 1.5 hours, and the carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas, the volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 1-5:1, the linear velocity of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas is 0.4-14 m/seconds, the reaction pressure is 1-5 megapascal, and the bed temperature in the reaction is 200-350degrees Celsius, most preferably, in the carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas, the volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 3:1, the linear velocity of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas is 4 m/seconds, the reaction pressure is 1.5 megapascal, and the bed temperature is 300degrees Celsius during the reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The template agent is selected from cesium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, etraethylammonium hydroxide, ethylenediamine, and triethylamine. The soluble salt of the copper is selected from copper nitrate trihydrate, copper chloride dihydrate, and copper sulfate pentahydrate, preferably, copper nitrate trihydrate. The molecular sieve is selected from Y-type molecular sieve, Beta-type molecular sieve, ZSM-5 molecular sieve preferably, Beta-type molecular sieve. The strong base is selected from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, and cesium hydroxide, preferably, potassium hydroxide The aluminum source is aluminum sulfate octadecahydrate. The silicon source is selected from silicon dioxide, methyl orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate, and propyl orthosilicate. Preferred Process: The preparation of porous copper-based catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce ethanol specifically involves dissolving template agent and soluble salt of copper in solvent, depositing on molecular sieve by equal volume dipping process, drying at 70-140degrees Celsius for 6-18 hours, preferably, at 110degrees Celsius for 12 hours, roasting at 300-600degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours, preferably, at 450degrees Celsius for 3 hours to obtain the loaded molecular sieve, reacting the obtained loaded molecular sieve with a strong base solution at 70-90degrees Celsius for 20-40 minutes, preferably, at 80degrees Celsius for 30 minutes, removing the template agent after the reaction, drying to obtain a sample, mixing the aluminum source, the silicon source, the strong base, the template agent, and the obtained sample to obtain a gel-embedded body, curing for 2-12 hours, preferably, 6 hours, drying at 70-140degrees Celsius for 6-18 hours, preferably, at 90degrees Celsius for 12 hours to obtain a gel, crystallizing the obtained gel at 120-220degrees Celsius for 24-72 hours, washing, drying at 70-140degrees Celsius for 6-18 hours, preferably, at 90degrees Celsius for 12 hours and calcining at 300-600degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours, preferably, at 550degrees Celsius for 4 hours to obtain the copper-based catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The consumption of the strong alkali solution is 20-40 mL per gram molecular sieve, preferably 30 mL. The loading amount of copper is 10%. The silicon-alumina ratio of the molecular sieve is 20-200, preferably, 25. The concentration of the strong alkaline solution is 0.1-0.2 mol L-1, preferably, 0.15 mol L-1. The prepared gel has the silicon-alumina ratio and the type of the synthesized molecular sieve consistent with the used molecular sieve. The ratio of the amount of the strong alkali to the total amount of the aluminum source and the silicon source is 0.03-0.15:1, preferably, 0.07:1. The ratio of the amount of the template agent to the total amount of the aluminum source and the silicon source is 0.05-0.40:1, preferably, 0.20:1.

PT P
PN WO2022211109-A1
TI Production of useful-hydrocarbon e.g. olefin,            involves generating gas containing carbon monoxide and            hydrogen from mixed gas containing methane-containing            hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide, generating            useful-hydrocarbon, and recycling.
AU YUKI K
   TAKASHI F
   MASAYUKI F
   YUICHIRO B
   TOMOHIKO M
   YUKI I
AE FURUKAWA ELECTRIC CO LTD (FURU-C)
GA 2022C77200
AB    NOVELTY - Production of useful-hydrocarbon (CC) involves                dry-reforming (S10) to generate a gas (DD)                containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen from a                mixed gas (AA) containing a methane-containing                hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide, generating (S20)                useful-hydrocarbon by generating a gas (EE)                containing useful-hydrocarbon from the carbon                monoxide and the hydrogen in the gas (DD), and                recycling (S30) to separate a carbon                dioxide-containing gas (BB) from the gas (EE) and                supplying the gas to the dry reforming step.
   USE - Production of useful-hydrocarbon e.g. olefins,                aromatic hydrocarbons, gasoline, liquefied                petroleum gas and liquid hydrocarbons (all claimed)                for use as aviation fuel. Uses include but are not                limited to ethylene, propylene, benzene, toluene,                xylene, naphtha, kerosene, jet fuel, light oil,                heavy oil, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses easily obtainable raw                materials such as methane-containing hydrocarbon                and carbon dioxide, and ensures efficient                production for a long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                useful-hydrocarbon production device, which                comprises a dry reforming section to generate the                gas (DD) from the mixed gas, a useful-hydrocarbon                generating section supplied with the gas (DD) to                generate the gas (EE), and a recycling section to                separate the carbon dioxide-containing gas from the                gas (EE).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of                production of useful-hydrocarbon. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   AAMixed gas
   BBCarbon dioxide-containing gas
   CCUseful-hydrocarbon
   DD,EEGases
   S10Dry-reforming process
   S20Useful-hydrocarbon generating                process
   S30Recycling process
   S40Hydrogen supplying process
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (S30), the                  supply amount of the carbon dioxide-containing                  gas in the step (S10) is adjusted based on the                  ratio of total carbon mole number (MHC) of                  hydrocarbons supplied to the step (S10) with                  respect to the total carbon mole number (MCO2) of                  carbon dioxide in the mixed gas and carbon                  dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing gas                  supplied to the step (S10). The method further                  involves separating hydrogen from the gas (EE)                  and supplying (S40) hydrogen to the step (S20) to                  adjust the supply amount of hydrogen to be                  supplied to the step (S20) according to the                  number of moles of hydrogen in the step (S20).                  The mol ratio (MH/MCO) of the number of moles of                  hydrogen to the number of moles of carbon                  monoxide in the gas (DD) is adjusted. The method                  further involves a combustion step of separating                  a substance containing carbon atoms from the gas                  (EE) and burning the substance containing carbon                  atoms to generate carbon dioxide. The step (S30)                  supplies the carbon dioxide-containing gas and                  carbon dioxide generated in the combustion step                  to the step (S20). Preferred Composition: The                  carbon dioxide-containing gas has a carbon                  dioxide concentration of 50% or more. The mixed                  gas contains methane and carbon dioxide in a mol                  ratio of 1-9. PT P
PN CN115095306-A
TI Oil shale air/Carbon dioxide alternately injecting            in-situ combustion method, involves injecting normal            temperature air after combustion belt temperature is            reduced to start round production until combustion belt            breaks through production well development process is            finished.
AU ZHAO Z
   TIAN H
   QIN H
   MA T
   SONG W
   WANG J
   MENG X
   WANG L
   WANG T
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2022C5403K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fracturing an oil shale                reservoir. Initial communicate is formed for                providing an initial flow channel for injection                gas. Hot air at 500 degree Celsius to 800 degree                Celsius is injected from a gas injection well for                heating oil shale near a wellbore. Dry kerogen                combustion reaction is triggered by the wellbore to                form a stable combustion belt. Normal temperature                air is injected after forming a stable combustion                belt to maintain the dry kerogen combustion                reaction for providing heat needed by kerogen                cracking. Carbon dioxide gas is injected to                transfer the heat of the stable combustion belt to                an oil shale deep part to promote the kerogen                cracking. Oil gas generated from a production well                is efficiently displaced. The normal temperature                air is injected after combustion belt temperature                is reduced to start round production until                combustion belt breaks through production well                development process is finished.
   USE - Oil shale air/Carbon dioxide alternately                injecting in-situ combustion method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing carbon dioxide as                carrier gas to transmit combustion heat to the oil                shale deep part, thus improving combustion heat                utilization rate and increasing yield of the oil                gas, which is expected to realize oil shale in-situ                development of energy-saving synergistic and green                production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                an oil shale air/Carbon dioxide alternately                injecting in-situ combustion method. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-D06C; H01-D07; H01-C03; Q49-V35; Q49-A

PN CN114854988-A
TI Selectively separating vanadium and chromium from            vanadium and chromium material by using carbon dioxide,            comprises uniformly mixing vanadium-containing chromium            material and carbonate, heating, performing heat            preservation, naturally cooling sample, filtering and            separating solid liquid.
AU ZHOU G
   LIU S
   WANG L
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2022A6048H
AB    NOVELTY - Selectively separating vanadium and chromium                from vanadium and chromium material by using carbon                dioxide (CO2), comprises (i) uniformly mixing the                vanadium-containing chromium material and                carbonate, and pressing block, (ii) placing the                material after pressing into the alumina crucible,                placing into the vertical furnace, discharging the                air in the vertical furnace, continuously                introducing CO2 gas, heating to the set                temperature, and performing heat preservation,                naturally cooling the sample to room temperature,                and (iii) firstly crushing, adding soluble                solution, continuously stirring to obtain mixed                solution, filtering and separating solid                liquid.
   USE - Method for selectively separating vanadium and                chromium from vanadium-containing chromium material                by using carbon dioxide. Uses include but are not                limited to steel, battery, catalyst and other                industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of difficult                separation of vanadium and chromium, it is                obviously different from the traditional roasting                process. The obtained gas is carbon dioxide, which                can be used as fuel. The chromium in the vanadium                slag cannot be oxidized, after water leaching, the                leaching liquid has no chromium. The method only                selectively oxidized vanadium in                vanadium-containing chromium material by oxidant                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an                extracting method of vanadium; (2) a method for                extracting chromium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow block diagram the                invention for selectively separating vanadium and                chromium in vanadium and chromium material by using                CO2 method. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  vanadium-containing material comprises vanadium                  slag, vanadium titanomagnetite, vanadium mica                  and/or stone coal. In the step (i), the carbonate                  is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, calcium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium                  bicarbonate and/or manganese carbonate.

AU MCCORMICK D C
   MACDONALD M
   HOLLEY B M
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
   HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 2022817414
AB    NOVELTY - The super-critical carbon dioxide (sCO2) turbo generator rotor assembly comprises two bearings on a compressor-side and a turbine-side of a super-critical carbon dioxide turbo generator. A combination seal configuration in which leakage from a compressor cools the bearings and the generator. The configuration has a controlled-leakage seal axially interposed between the compressor and one of the bearings. A low-leakage seal such as a film riding face seal, is axially arranged between the other bearing and the turbine. A portion of the leakage from the compressor flows through the former bearing, around the generator and through the latter bearing.
   USE - Super-critical carbon dioxide (sCO2) turbo generator rotor assembly.
   ADVANTAGE - The parasitic pumping loss and generator efficiency can be balanced for efficiency gain. The leakage from the turbine is redirected as egress from the second bearing to a pump by the low-leakage seal. The combination seal configuration is configured such that at least a portion of the leakage from compressor flows through the first bearing, around the generator and through the secondary bearing and is redirected to the pump.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a super-critical carbon dioxide turbo generator rotor assembly with a generator operably interposed between a compressor and a turbine.

PN JP2022086426-A
TI Combustion apparatus e.g. hot water supply device, has control device that prohibits re-ignition of burner in period until predetermined stand-by time elapses after detecting occurrence of fire and occurrence of misfire of burner.
AU MIYAJIMA S
AE RINNAI CORP (RINN-C)
GA 202281092W
AB    NOVELTY - The combustion apparatus (1) has a burner (4). A combustion fan (10) supplies combustion air to the burner. A fire detection unit detects the occurrence of a fire in the burner. A carbon dioxide sensor (14) detects the carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust gas produced by the combustion operation of the burner. A controller (51) controls the burner operation. The controller prohibits the re-ignition of the burner, until a predetermined waiting time is elapsed, when the occurrence of a loss of fire of the burner is detected by the loss of fire detection part. The process is selectively executed, based on the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the carbon dioxide sensor, among the first re-ignition process to re-ignite the burner after the elapse of the standby time and the second re-ignition process to re-ignite the burner immediately after the detection of the occurrence of the lost fire.
   USE - Combustion apparatus such as hot water supply device and FF-type fan heater.
   ADVANTAGE - Combustion apparatus enables to perform re-ignition after the occurrence of a misfire of a burner in a mode suitable for the cause of occurrence of misfire.

   Burner (4)
   Combustion fan (10)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (14)
   Controller (51)

PN CN114277398-A
TI Preparing nano copper catalyst used for carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction , by preparing bicarbonate solution, dissolving organic acid salt and copper salt precursor in solution to obtain precursor solution, placing conductive substrate in solution, and performing electrochemical reduction.

   ZHU Y
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 2022522016
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-copper catalyst comprises (i) preparing bicarbonate solution, dissolving the organic acid salt and the copper salt precursor in the solution to obtain a precursor solution, (ii) placing the conductive substrate in the precursor solution obtained in the step (i), introducing at least one gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and at the same time, performing electrochemical reduction to obtain nano copper catalyst.

In the electrochemical reduction preparation process of the copper catalyst, the organic anion can coordinate with the copper of the catalyst and surface to form orientation crystal face, so as to improve the stability and activity of the nano copper catalyst. The method has excellent electro-catalysis CO2 reduction performance and stability.
