TI Co-production of methanol and methane used as fuel, involves reducing mixture of water vapor and carbon dioxide, compressing obtained synthetic gas and passing through methanol synthesis catalyst and methanization catalyst.
AU OHTSUKA H H I
   NORIOKA S
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 202250891H
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing methanol and methane using carbon dioxide, water, and electric power, involves (a) vaporizing water to generate water vapor, (b) reducing a mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide in a solid oxide type electrolytic cell to obtain a synthetic gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water vapor, (c) cooling the synthetic gas to separate and compress water, (d) separating water and passing the compressed synthetic gas through a methanol synthesis catalyst to synthesize methanol, (e) cooling the gas obtained to separate methanol, and (f) synthesizing methane by passing a synthetic gas from which methanol is separated through a methanization catalyst.
   USE - Co-production of methanol and methane used as component gas for city gas, and chemical or liquid fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high conversion efficiency and reduces equipment cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for equipment for co-producing methanol and methane, which comprises a steam generator (1) for vaporizing water to generate steam and a mixed gas of steam and carbon dioxide, electrolyzer (2), water separation/compression unit (3), methanol synthesis unit (4), methanol separation unit (5), and a methanization reaction unit (6) that synthesizes methane.

   Steam generator (1)
   Electrolyzer (2)
   Water separation and compression unit (3)
   Methanol synthesis unit (4)
   Methanol separation unit (5)
   Methanation reaction unit (6)

PN WO2021194585-A2; WO2021194585-A9; WO2021194585-A3
TI Treating carbon fiber involves exposing carbon fibers to plasma comprising carbon dioxide, and/or its reactive species, thus forming pre-treated carbon fibers; and exposing pre- treated carbon fibers formed to further surface treatment.
AU SHI Q
   THOMAS A
   CRAWFORD S
AE CYTEC IND INC (CYTE-C)
GA 2021B25872
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves exposing carbon fibers to plasma provided with carbon dioxide and a reactive species thus forming pre-treated carbon fibers. The pre-treated carbon fibers formed in a surface treatment is exposed thus forming the treated carbon fiber. The carbon fibers are the result of carbonization at a temperature of 1300 degrees C, at least 1350 degrees C, or at least 1380 degrees C prior to treatment. The carbon fibers are standard modulus carbon fibers, intermediate modulus carbon fibers, or high modulus carbon fibers.
   USE - Process for treating carbon fiber with plasma having carbon dioxide prior to a surface treatment and reinforced composite materials made from such modified carbon fibers used for aerospace industrial and automotive applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides carbon fibers with high strength and stiffness, high chemical resistance and low thermal expansion. The method enhances the interaction between carbon fiber and matrix resin, such as through electrochemical treatment or plasma treatment of carbon fibers. The carbon fibers can be formed into a structural portion that combines high strength, high stiffness, while having a weight that is significantly lighter than a metal component of equivalent properties. The treated carbon fibers have high interfacial shear strength (IFSS) in comparison to carbon fiber subjected to only plasma comprising carbon dioxide and/or a reactive species, and carbon fiber exposed to acetic acid, acrylic acid, allylamine, C0 2 /acrylic acid, or methane/ammonia each without carbon dioxide plasma pre-treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a composite material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a interfacial shear strength (IFSS) of carbon fiber subjected to the inventive process.

PT P
PN CN113000030-A
TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises graphene nitride, tetraethylenepentamine, and carrier.
AU WANG H
AE WANG H (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021759045
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 4-8 pts. wt. graphene nitride, 10-20 pts. wt. tetraethylenepentamine, and 10-15 pts. wt. carrier.
   USE - Carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent has stable desorption performance, the raw material adopts soybean as a nitrogen source, the soybean can adopt waste soybean as raw material, changing waste into valuable, and greatly reducing the cost, and has high practicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent, which involves:
   (A) taking graphene oxide, placing in a tubular furnace, calcining under 680-700 degrees C;
   (B) introducing ammonia gas, and preserving temperature in the ammonia gas atmosphere;
   (C) cooling to room temperature under the nitrogen environment after processing to obtain the graphene nitride;
   (D) taking titanium trichloride, placing in the mixed acid solution, mixing and stirring to obtain solution A;
   (E) taking soybean, removing seed coat, cleaning by de-ionized water, then soaking in diluted hydrochloric acid solution;
   (F) taking out and washing by de-ionized water, then placing in solution A;
   (G) adding surfactant, tetraethyl orthosilicate and ethanol, stirring and reacting for 3-5 hours, standing and soaking, drying and raising the temperature to 500-520 degrees C, keeping the temperature and calcining 2-2.2 hours;
   (H) cooling with the furnace to obtain the carrier;
   (I) taking the carrier and tetraethylene pentamine, methanol to dissolve, mixing and stirring under the 20-30 degrees C;
   (J) adding graphene nitride, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, constant temperature processing for 4-5 hours at 140-150 degrees C;
   (K) cooling to room temperature; and
   (L) drying under the 60-80 degrees C to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is mainly composed of soybean, surfactant, tetraethyl orthosilicate, titanium trichloride reaction. The surfactant is F127. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the water to the iron-containing solid waster is 8-12:1. The acid solution is 1mol/L of sulfuric acid. The mass ratio of the sulfuric acid to the solid component is 1-3:1. The 1M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution is used for adjusting pH value. The temperature of the baking process is 180 degrees C. The mixed acid solution comprises diluted hydrochloric acid and citric acid, the mass ratio of the diluted hydrochloric acid and citric acid is 1: 2. The heating rate is 1-2 degrees C/minute. The drying time is 10-12 hours. Preferred Process: The graphene nitride is prepared by nitriding graphene oxide surface. The method for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent involves taking graphene oxide, placing in a tubular furnace, calcining for 10-15 minute under the condition of 680-700 degrees C, introducing ammonia gas, and preserving temperature for 2-3 hours in the ammonia gas atmosphere, cooling to room temperature under the nitrogen environment after processing, obtaining the graphene nitride, taking titanium trichloride, placing in the mixed acid solution, mixing and stirring for 20-30 minute to obtain solution A, taking soybean, removing seed coat, de-ionized water to wash for 20-30 minute, then placing in diluted hydrochloric acid solution to soak for 20-24 hours, taking out and washing by de-ionized water, then placing in solution A, adding surfactant, tetraethyl orthosilicate and ethanol, stirring and reacting for 3-5 hours, standing and soaking for 20-22 hours, drying, raising the temperature to 500-520 degrees C, keeping the temperature and calcining 2-2.2 hours, cooling along with the furnace to obtain the carrier, taking the carrier and tetraethylene pentamine, methanol to dissolve, mixing and stirring for 6-7 hours under the 20-30 degrees C, then adding graphene nitride, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, placing in the 140-150 degrees C, constant temperature processing for 4-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, and then drying under the 60-80 degrees C to obtain the finished product.

PT P
PN CN112979556-A
TI Production of hydantoin involves preparing ammonia carbonylation reaction liquid using hydroxyacetonitrile, ammonia, carbon dioxide and water, removing unreacted ammonia and carbon dioxide, preparing aqueous hydantoin solution, cooling obtained decoloring solution, and separating hydantoin.
AU LI X
   YING G
   GENG H
AE BOZUN INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (BOZU-Non-standard)
GA 202172055P
AB    NOVELTY - A clean production of hydantoin involves (i) using hydroxyacetonitrile, ammonia or ammonia-releasing precursor compounds, carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-releasing precursor compounds, and water as raw materials, carrying out ammonia carbonylation reaction at a liquid level of more than 85% in the absence of zirconium to prepare ammonia carbonylation reaction liquid, (ii) removing unreacted ammonia and carbon dioxide from the ammonia carbonylation reaction solution, concentrating the obtained degassed reaction liquid, performing cyclization to the obtained concentrated liquid with solid acid, separating the solid acid, after the reaction is completed to obtain an aqueous hydantoin solution, (iii) decoloring the aqueous hydantoin solution, cooling the obtained decoloring solution for crystallization, and separating hydantoin and crystallization mother liquor.
   USE - Clean production of hydantoin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, clean, environmentally-friendly, produces hydantoin with high yield, does not produce by-products, overcomes the shortcomings of low yield and wastes in the hydantoin production, and avoids the low-value and difficult-to-handle sulfate by-products when using sulfuric acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for producing hydantoin, which comprises an ammonia carbonylation reaction unit containing an ammonia carbonylation reactor which does not contain zirconium, a cyclization reaction unit containing a stripping tower, an evaporation concentration system, an acidification cyclization reactor, and a solid-liquid separation device (D1) in fluid communication and connected to the ammonia carbonylation reaction unit in a fluid communication manner, and a crystallization unit containing a decolorization tank, a solid-liquid separation device (D2), and a crystallization tank in fluid communication and connected to the cyclization reaction unit in fluid communication.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amounts of the ammonia-releasing precursor compound and the carbon dioxide-releasing precursor compound are calculated as ammonia and carbon dioxide, respectively. The sulfonated silica is silica gel sulfonic acid. Preferred Process: The method involves using preferably hydroxyacetonitrile, ammonia, carbon dioxide and water are used as the raw materials, and performing ammonia carbonylation reaction at a liquid level of preferably 85-95% in the absence of zirconium. The method involves mixing the ammonia or ammonia-releasing precursor compound, carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-releasing precursor compound, and water, before adding the hydroxyacetonitrile to obtain an ammonium bicarbonate mixed liquid, and raising the temperature of the hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution and the ammonium bicarbonate mixture to 50-70 degrees C before mixing the hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution and the ammonium bicarbonate mixture. In step (ii), unreacted carbon dioxide and ammonia are removed from the ammonia carbonylation reaction liquid by steam stripping or under negative pressure conditions. The steam stripping is performed by controlling the bottom temperature of the stripping tower to 100-110 degrees C, and the top temperature to 98-100 degrees C. The ammonia and carbon dioxide removed in step (ii) are recycled to the step (i). The method involves subjecting the degassed reaction liquid to reduced pressure concentration treatment, and concentrating the degassed reaction liquid to 15-40 vol.% of its original volume. In step (ii), the solid acid is separated by centrifugation or filtration. In step (iii), the decoloring of hydantoin aqueous solution involves adding activated carbon, separating and removing the activated carbon by centrifugation or filtration to obtain the decolorizing solution, and cooling the obtained decolorizing solution to 0-30 degrees C for the crystallization. The hydantoin and the crystallization mother liquor are separated by centrifugation or filtration. The crystallization mother liquor is recycled to participates in the concentration process. The method involves one or more of batch, semi-continuous or continuous operation methods. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydroxyacetonitrile to ammonia or ammonia-releasing precursor compound is 1:(3.5-4.3). The molar ratio of ammonia or ammonia-releasing precursor compound to carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-releasing precursor compound is (1.3-1.4):1. The molar ratio of ammonia or ammonia-releasing precursor compound to water is 1:(10-12). The hydroxyacetonitrile comprises 20-65 wt.% aqueous solution of hydroxyacetonitrile. The residual amount of ammonia in the degassed reaction liquid is less than 50 ppm. In step (ii), the feed mass of the solid acid is 2-10 %mass of the concentrated liquid. In step (iii), the added amount of the activated carbon is 0.2-0.8 %mass of the mass of the hydantoin aqueous solution. Preferred Property: The hydroxyacetonitrile has pH of 2-4. Preferred Conditions: The ammonia carbonylation reaction is carried out at 100-125 degrees C and pressure of 2.0-4.0 MPa for 60-120 minutes. The cyclization reaction is carried out at 95-115 degrees C for 90-120 minutes. The decolorization is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 50-90 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid acid comprises one or more of sulfonated polymer material, sulfonated silica or sulfonated iron trioxide. The sulfonated polymer material is a sulfonated cation exchange resin.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.)

PN CN112264008-A
TI Preparation of palladium/titanium dioxide-(100) composite catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction by dispersing titanium dioxide-(100) nanocrystals in deionized water, heating, and adding chloropalladium acid solution.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 2021123002
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of palladium/titanium dioxide-(100) composite catalyst comprises dispersing titanium dioxide-(100) nanocrystals in deionized water, heating, stirring, adding chloropalladium acid solution, mixing to obtain reaction solution, adjusting pH using sodium carbonate buffer solution, heating, stirring, centrifugally washing, drying, grinding to obtain brown-yellow solid powder, calcining, and subjecting to hydrogen-temperature-programmed reduction (H2-TPR) reaction.
   USE - The method is for preparation of palladium/titanium dioxide-(100) composite catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction to form formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has better catalytic performance in the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation than that of palladium nanoparticles supported on palladium/titanium dioxide (101) and palladium/titanium dioxide (001) composite catalyst and far better than the commercial titanium dioxide (P25) catalyst supporting palladium nanoparticles of palladium/titanium dioxide (P25) catalyst, and simple preparation process with mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a palladium/titanium dioxide-(100) composite catalyst comprising 0.5-4 wt.% 1.5-5.5 nm metal palladium particles, and 15-30 nm anatase titanium dioxide nanocrystal that preferentially expose the (100) crystal plane and has ratio of (100) crystal plane of 80-90%; and
   (2) carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction to form formic acid comprising dispersing composite catalyst in sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution, ultrasonicating, adding to high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, adjusting pressure of the reactor through cylinder pressurizing valve, heating, filtering, and analyzing through ion chromatography.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of titanium dioxide-(100) nanocrystal and chloropalladic acid is 31.9-255.2:1. The mass ratio of sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution and composite catalyst is 16.8-33.6:1. Preferred Method: The H2-TPR reduction process is done at 200 degrees C for 1-2 hours under 5% hydrogen/nitrogen atmosphere.

PN CN112178685-A
TI Combustion optimization control system for heating furnace, has controller which adjusts flow rate of air regulating valve to first air flow based on current time and obtains second air flow is final flow size of air regulating valve.
AU DING C
   CHEN C
   CAO B
   LIU G
   LIU Z
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 202106408D
AB    NOVELTY - The combustion optimization control system has a gas composition online detector and a gas flow meter (5). The flue gas carbon-dioxide on-line detector (9) is provided at the entrance of the furnace tail flue of the heating furnace (1). Both the gas composition online detector (7) and the flue gas carbon-dioxide online detector are in communication connection with the controller. The controller (8) acquires the gas flow detected by the gas flow meter, gas composition detected by the gas composition online detector, the carbon-dioxide content in the heating furnace detected by the flue gas carbon-dioxide online detector and the current time. The controller obtains the first air flow, according to the gas flow rate and the gas composition, adjusts the flow rate of the air regulating valve (3) to the first air flow after a delay based on the current time and obtains a second air flow in which the second air flow is the final flow size of the air regulating valve.
   USE - Combustion optimization control system for heating furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The combustion optimization control system for heating furnace achieves precise control of the air flow of the heating furnace and reduces emissions and saves energy.

PN CN112058103-A; CN112058103-B
TI Preparing cellulose grafted carboxymethyl methyl imidazolium chloride gas separation membrane for recycling carbon dioxide, involves reacting cellulose acetate and carboxymethyl methyl imidazolium chloride salt, and replacing with nitrogen.
AU XU S
   ZHOU H
   JIA H
   ZANG Y
   XU J
   JIANG P
   MA W
   ZHAO W
   WANG X
AE UNIV QIQIHAR (UYQR-C)
GA 2020C7002V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cellulose grafted carboxymethyl methyl imidazolium chloride gas separation membrane involves adding cellulose acetate and 1-carboxymethyl-3-methyl imidazolium chloride salt into a reaction vessel with a condenser, replacing with nitrogen 5 times and filling with nitrogen. Tetrahydrofuran and pyridine are added to the obtained product, stirred and reacted at 70 degrees C for 48 hours. The reaction product is poured into methanol and stirred for 7 days. The mixture is centrifuged and the solid is collected, and the solid product is placed in a vacuum drying oven to dry at room temperature. The obtained graft product is dissolved in tetrahydrofuran, stirred for 3 hours to completely dissolve, and allowed to stand for a period of time until there are no bubbles in the casting solution. The membrane is spread on a clean glass plate, and after natural drying, the membrane from the glass plate is removed to obtain a gas separation membrane after grafting reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing cellulose grafted carboxymethyl methyl imidazolium chloride gas separation membrane (claimed) for separation and recovery of carbon dioxide in petroleum exploitation associated gas, natural gas, greenhouse gas and marsh gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the cellulose grafted carboxymethyl methyl imidazole chloride gas separation membrane with better effect of solving the new problem in the process of carbon dioxide gas-driven strengthening tertiary oil recovery, and separating and recycling carbon dioxide in petroleum exploitation associated gas and separation of carbon dioxide in natural gas, greenhouse gas and marsh gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of cellulose acetate to 1-carboxymethyl-3-methyl imidazolium chloride salt is 1-5:500-1000. The molar ratio of tetrahydrofuran, pyridine and methanol relative to the molar ratio of cellulose acetate is 10000-15000:500-625:60000-625000:1-5. The ratio of grafted product to tetrahydrofuran is 1-5 g:20-40 mL.

PN CN111184022-A
TI Plant extract sterilization and disinfection agent comprises hypochlorous acid, fendol, lemon extract, hydrogenated castor oil, sodium polyacrylate and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose.
AU LI Q
   WU F
AE HANGZHOU QLZW ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020486721
AB    NOVELTY - Plant extract sterilization and disinfection agent comprises hypochlorous acid, phytoncide, lemon extract, hydrogenated castor oil, sodium polyacrylate and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. The extraction method of lemon extract is supercritical extraction.
   USE - Plant extract sterilization and disinfection agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the plant extract sterilization and disinfection agent, which involves:
   (A) cutting lemon into fine particles;
   (B) placing the granular lemon fragments in the extractor of the supercritical extraction device, using high-pressure pump and a heater to convert the extractant from a normal temperature and pressure state to a supercritical fluid state, and the extractant uses carbon dioxide;
   (C) adding polysiloxane into supercritical fluid extractant to enhance the stability and dispersion of supercritical fluid to avoid carbon dioxide gasification;
   (D) passing the carbon dioxide in supercritical state into the extractor, dissolving extracted substance from the lemon chip sample and entering the separator with carbon dioxide, decompressing and cooling, and separating the extracted substance and carbon dioxide into gas and liquid;
   (E) adding sodium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid into the separator, and reducing the extraction material by increasing the osmotic pressure microbial activity to avoid spoilage of lemon extract;
   (F) performing filtration operation on the separated plant liquid to remove sodium chloride and residual sodium carbonate solid to obtain pure lemon extract;
   (G) using the relevant container to collect the extracted substance; and
   (H) adding the prepared lemon extract and hydrogenated castor oil into the mixed solution of hypochlorous acid and fendol, then adding sodium polyacrylate and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose to complete the preparation of product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Plant extract sterilization and disinfection agent comprises 15 wt.% hypochlorous acid, 15 wt.% phytoncide, 50 wt.% lemon extract, 13 wt.% hydrogenated castor oil, 6 wt.% sodium polyacrylate and 1 wt.% sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. Preferred Components: The lemon extract comprises lemon essential oil, citric acid and sodium citrate in 1:3:3 ratio. The hydrogenated castor oil is polyethylene glycol-40 hydrogenatedcastor oil. Preferred Processes: The hydrogenated castor oil is prepared by taking castor oil with a water content of less than 0.2% and an acid value of less than 0.5, preheating the castor oil to more than 130 degrees C, and passing the preheated castor oil into the hydrogenation tank, taking hydrogen with a purity of 98% or more, passing the hydrogen into the hydrogenation tank, and setting the gage pressure of the hydrogen to 0.180-0.200 MPa, keep the temperature in the hydrogenation tank to 150-180 degrees C for 8-10 hours, after the hydrogenation completed, using a heat exchanger to pass cold water into the hydrogenation tank to reduce the temperature, and adjusting the hydrogen pressure to 0.100-0.150 MPa, pressing castor oil into a filter press for filtration, and collecting to obtain hydrogenated castor oil.

PN CN110104647-A
TI Preparing biomass based nitrogen doped microporous carbon material comprises used in adsorbing carbon dioxide comprises placing biomass powder in tube furnace under activation gas, ultrasonic vibrating and doping carbon dioxide and water.
AU LIU D
   SU R
   ZHAO X
   HAO Z
   XU B
   JIA B
   LI S
   DONG L
AE UNIV JILIN AGRIC (UJIL-C)
GA 2019706962
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass based nitrogen doped microporous carbon material comprises (a) drying the biomass at 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, pulverizing and sieving to obtain biomass powder, (b) placing the biomass powder in a quartz glass reactor, placing in a temperature programmed tube furnace, introducing the activation gas, heating to 800-900 degrees C at a rate of 8-10 degrees C/minute for 60 minutes, cooling to obtain the product, washing the product with dilute hydrochloric acid, ultrasonically vibrating with deionized water, drying at 80 degrees C to obtain a biomass-based microporous carbon material, where the activation gas is mixture of nitrogen and ammonia in a volume ratio of 1:1 is passed at a flow rate of 200 ml/min and (c) doping carbon dioxide and/or water into the activation gas, allowing to stand at 800-900 degrees C in the tube furnace for 60 minutes under the condition of the total flow rate of the activated gas is 200 ml/minute and the flow rate of the ammonia gas is 100 ml/minute and cooling.
   USE - The carbon material is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material has a nitrogen content of 10.8%, specific surface area of 1989.56 m2/g and adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide reaches 4.88 mmol/g (0 degrees C) and 3.52 mmol/g (25 degrees C).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass is agricultural and forestry waste includes is straw, rice husk, firewood, bark or peanut shell. The biomass powder has 80-120 mesh. The concentration of dilute hydrochloric acid is 2 mol/l. Preferred Method: The flow rate of carbon dioxide in the activation gas is 25 ml/minute and the flow rate of water is 25 ml/minute. The ultrasonically vibration with deionized water is carried out for 3-5 times.

PN US2019096221-A1; US10482747-B2
TI Occupant detection system within vehicle for            detecting presence of living occupant e.g. human, has            processor that generates and communicates alarm signal            in response to rate of change of data associated with            signal exceeding threshold level.
AU HASKEW T A
   SAZONOV E
AE UNIV ALABAMA (UABR-C)
GA 201927992C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an electrical signal sensor                (13), an accelerometer (18) and a carbon dioxide                sensor (16). A processor in communication with a                memory, and executes computer-readable instructions                stored on the memory. A first signal is received                from the electrical signal sensor indicating                whether power to the vehicle is ON or OFF. A second                signal is received from the accelerometer                indicating whether the vehicle is in motion. A                third signal is received from the carbon dioxide                sensor associated with a concentration of carbon                dioxide in a cabin of the vehicle. A rate of change                of the data associated with the third signal is                compared with a threshold level of an expected rate                of change of carbon dioxide concentration within                the cabin of the vehicle. An alarm signal is                generated and communicated in response to the rate                of change of the data associated with the third                signal exceeding the threshold level.
   USE - Occupant detection system within vehicle for                detecting presence of living occupant e.g. human                and generates warning.
   ADVANTAGE - The system prevents injury and death to people                and pets that accidentally within a parked vehicle                and unable to get out of the vehicle. The system                detects both markers to remain in transport mode                when the vehicle has power in motionless or when                the system is not receiving an electrical signal                from the vehicle when the vehicle is moving.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                occupant detection system.

   Carbon dioxide sensor (16)
   Accelerometer (18)

PT P
PN CN108996524-A; CN108996524-B
TI Preparing bromide salt from bromine-containing waste involves taking bromine-containing waste and strong alkali such as sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide used as raw materials, and decomposing at high-temperature.
AU YANG C
   CHEN J
   WANG T
   SONG W
AE SHOUGUANG FUKANG PHARM CO LTD (SHOU-Non-standard)
   SHOUGUANG YONGKANG CHEM IND CO LTD (SHOU-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3597V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a bromide salt from a bromine-containing waste involves taking a bromine-containing waste and a strong alkali such as sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide used as raw materials, and decomposing at high-temperature to simultaneously form a bromide salt. The sodium bromide is prepared by reacting an alkyl halide compound (I) with sodium hydroxide and oxygen to give carbon dioxide gas, water and sodium bromide. The potassium bromide is prepared by reacting the compound (I) with potassium hydroxide and oxygen to give carbon dioxide gas , water and potassium bromide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a bromide salt from a bromine-containing waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: treats non-flammable or even flame retardant bromide waste into bromide salt; directly burns the organic components to form carbon dioxide; and completely treats the waste while the bromide salt is recovered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a bromide salt from a bromine-containing waste involves taking a bromine-containing waste and a strong alkali such as sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide used as raw materials, and decomposing at high-temperature to simultaneously form a bromide salt. The sodium bromide is prepared by reacting an alkyl halide compound of formula: CxHyBrz (I) with sodium hydroxide and oxygen to give carbon dioxide gas, water and sodium bromide. The potassium bromide is prepared by reacting the compound (I) with potassium hydroxide and oxygen to give carbon dioxide gas , water and potassium bromide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method involves: (1) adding the bromine-containing waste to a reaction tank, and then adding sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide and a small amount of water to the reaction tank; (2) heating the tank, so that the material in the tank is in the form of oil, stirring and mixing, and continuously heating to the reaction temperature; (3) combusting the materials after the reaction is completed, and cooling after combustion is completed; and (4) dissolving the combusted residues in water, filtering, then adding hydrobromic acid to adjust the pH value to neutral, decolorizing activated carbon according to the color condition, concentrating the bromide solution, filtering and drying to obtain a bromide salt.

TI Coke oven flue gas multi-pollutant cooperative control system, has cooler and flue gas heat exchanger whose condensed water outlet pipe are connected with desulfurization absorption tower and wet by process water pipe of liquid inlet pipe.
AU XU R
   ZHOU X
   REN L
AE MCC CAPITAL ENG & RES INC LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 201879801V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a coke oven flue gas multi-pollutant synergistic treatment system for comprising a hot-blast stove according to flue gas flow direction. A cooler and a chimney are connected through a first pipeline, a second pipeline, a third pipeline, a fourth pipeline, a fifth pipeline and a sixth pipeline. A carbon dioxide desorption device is provided with carbon dioxide desorption and fixed at a carbon dioxide bottom of a desorption tower that is stored with a carbon dioxide absorbent re-boiler. A catalytic oxidation is coupled with a silicon control rectifier denitration multifunctional catalytic reactor. A condensed water outlet pipe of the cooler and a flue gas heat exchanger are connected with a desulfurization absorption tower and a wet de-carbonizing by process a water pipe of a liquid inlet pipe.
   USE - Coke oven flue gas multi-pollutant cooperative control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system removes flue gas multi-pollutant from a comprehensive and an effective angle, designs sleeve cooperatively control multiple pollutants to discharge a process route, combines silicon control rectifier denitration, wet de-carbonizing, catalytic oxidation, carbon dioxide trapping, and wet smoke dislocation technology and improves energy effective utilization.

AE XIAOGAN QGU INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (XIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201828833X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a detection unit (1) comprising a temperature sensor (3) for detecting temperature in a greenhouse. A carbon dioxide concentration sensor (6) detects concentration of carbon dioxide in a greenhouse. An oxygen sensor (5) detects concentration of oxygen in the greenhouse. An air exchange fan (9) regulates concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide. A control end (7) is electrically connected with a display (11) by a wireless communication module (8). The air exchange fan and an electric heating lamp (10) are electrically connected with a controller (2).
   USE - Intelligent greenhouse cultivation control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ventilates an interior of the greenhouse by the air exchange fan so as to facilitate adjustment of oxygen and carbon dioxide concentration in the greenhouse for improving greenhouse cultivation effect. The system is provided with the electric heating lamp to increase light intensity and temperature in the greenhouse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control end is a waterproof shell control end. The display is a touch screen display. The electric heating lamp is a high penetrability electric heating lamp. The control end is electrically connected with a display (11) by a wireless communication module (8).

PT P
PN WO2018022740-A2; WO2018022740-A3; WO2018022740-A9; CA3031940-A1; US2019270073-A1; US11123712-B2
TI Mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst used to catalyze carbon dioxide oxidation of organic compound of carbon-carbon double bond, preferably ethylene to epoxide compound, preferably ethylene oxide, comprises silver oxide and/or ruthenium oxide.
AU LAIL M
   MOBLEY P
   PETERS J
   AKUNURI S N
   HLEBAK J J
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 201809221R
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst comprises a silver oxide and/or a ruthenium oxide.
   USE - The mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst is useful for catalyzing the carbon dioxide oxidation of an organic compound containing at least one carbon-carbon double bond to an organic epoxide compound, where the organic compound containing at least one carbon-carbon double bond is an alkene, preferably ethylene and the organic epoxide compound is an alkene oxide, preferably ethylene oxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst provides the epoxide product in both a co-feed and a transport mode of operation by utilizing carbon dioxide that avoids the need for an air separation unit for obtaining purified oxygen and carries the need for a purified carbon dioxide stream as feedstock.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) converting an olefin to an epoxide, comprising contacting the olefin with a mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst with carbon dioxide feed stream under temperature and pressure conditions so as to convert the olefin to the epoxide; and
   (2) preparing the mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst, comprising either (a) contacting a support material with the mixed metal molybdenum salt solution, (b) drying the support material and the molybdenum salt solution, and (c) calcining the dried support material and the molybdenum salt so as to prepare the mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst, or contacting a solution containing mixed metal molybdenum salts and an aluminum salt with an aqueous base solution so as to form a co-precipitate, drying the co-precipitate, and calcining the dried co-precipitate so as to prepare the mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst further comprises: a Group IA or Group IIA element promoter; and a support. The support is aluminum oxide (preferred), silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, aluminum phosphate, carbon and/or graphite. The mixed catalyst is: a mixed silver/molybdenum oxide catalyst; a mixed ruthenium/molybdenum oxide catalyst; or a mixed silver/ruthenium/molybdenum oxide catalyst. The molar ratio of silver oxide to molybdenum oxide is 1-0.05 to 1-20, preferably 1-1 to 1-4 and the molar ratio of ruthenium oxide to molybdenum oxide is 1-0.05 to 1-20, preferably 1-1 to 1-4, preferably 1-1 to 1-4. The mixed metal molybdenum salt solution is a solution of molybdenum and silver salts and/or a solution of molybdenum and ruthenium salts.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The olefin, the mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst and the carbon dioxide feed stream are reacted: together at the same time; sequentially in a chemical looping process; and together in a fluidized bed. Preferred Components: The olefin is an alkene, a cycloalkene, a diene or a phenylalkene. The alkene or diene is butadiene, butylene, ethylene or propylene.
   EXAMPLE - In a round- bottomed flask with a ground-glass neck, 3.5 g of ammonium molybdate was dissolved in 20 ml of water. Gravimetric balance was used to weigh out 15 g of gamma -alumina, and then the alumina was added to the round-bottomed flask containing the molybdenum solution. Water was added to completely cover the alumina. Rotary evaporator was used to remove the water from the solution. When the sample was air dried, calcination was performed by heating the solid at 120 degrees C for 2 hours to 350 degrees C at a 5 degrees C per minute ramp rate and holding for 3 hours to obtain mixed molybdenum oxide catalyst with an yield of 16.6 g.

PN WO2022145516-A1; KR2022094631-A
TI Continuous conversion of e.g. carbon dioxide            contained in exhaust gas, involves introducing carbon            dioxide into reaction unit and converting into            bicarbonate ions through unit comprising liquid of            water and carbonic anhydrase, and regenerating into            liquid from which bicarbonate ions are removed.
AU KIM J
   OK J
   KIM S
   KIM Y
   KIM H S
   KIM J B
   DOUBLE P
   GEUN K
   KIM H
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202287877D
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous conversion (P1) of carbon dioxide                involves (i) introducing carbon dioxide into a                reaction unit (100) and converting into bicarbonate                ions through a conversion reaction unit (110)                comprising a liquid (a1) comprising water and                carbonic anhydrase located near the water surface                of the liquid (a1), supplying a liquid (a2) into                the unit (100), and supplying the liquid (a1)                exceeding a predetermined water level in the unit                (100) overflows due to the supply of the liquid                (a2), (ii) introducing the liquid containing                bicarbonate into a reaction unit (200) and                regenerating into a liquid (a3) from which                bicarbonate ions are removed through a liquid                regeneration unit (210) in the reaction unit (200),                and (iii) directly or indirectly re-supplying the                regenerated liquid (a3) to the unit (100).
   USE - Continuous conversion of carbon dioxide                contained in exhaust gas or carbon dioxide                separated and purified from exhaust gas (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical continuous                conversion of carbon dioxide contained in exhaust                gas or carbon dioxide separated and purified from                exhaust gas, in short time with reduced energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                continuous carbon dioxide conversion system (1000),                which comprises the unit (100) including a chamber                (101) having an empty space, the conversion                reaction unit comprising liquid containing water                and carbonic anhydrase located near the water                surface of the liquid, filled in the empty space to                a predetermined water level, an inlet (120) located                at one side of the chamber (101) to receive liquid                (a2), and an outlet (130) provided to overflow and                drain when the liquid exceeds a predetermined water                level, the unit (200) including a chamber (201)                having an empty space, an inlet (220) through which                the liquid overflowed and drained from the unit                (100) flows in, the liquid regeneration unit for                removing bicarbonate ions contained in the liquid                to regenerate the liquid, and an outlet (230) for                discharging the liquid (a3), and a pump (300,320)                for directly or indirectly supplying the                regenerated liquid (a3) to the unit (100).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                continuous carbon dioxide conversion system.
   Reaction units (100,200)
   Chambers (101,201)
   Conversion reaction unit (110)
   Inlets (120,220)
   Outlets (130,230)
   Carbon dioxide inlet (140)
   Stirring unit (160)
   Liquid regeneration unit (210)
   Calcium carbonate discharge unit (240)
   Calcium carbonate precipitate (250)
   Pumps (300,320)
   Liquid reservoir (400)
   Carbon dioxide conversion system                (1000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The unit (100) further                  comprises a carbon dioxide inlet (140), and a                  stirring unit (160) to disperse the converted                  bicarbonate ions from the conversion reaction                  unit to the surrounding liquid. The unit (200)                  further comprises a calcium carbonate discharge                  unit (240) for discharging calcium carbonate                  precipitate in which bicarbonate ions in the                  introduced liquid are eventually converted                  through reaction with calcium ions. The outlet                  (o2) is located at a point higher than the                  thickness of the calcium carbonate precipitate to                  facilitate separation of the regenerated liquid                  except for the calcium carbonate precipitate. The                  pump (300) is located on a liquid reservoir                  serving as a source of liquid (a2) supplied to                  the unit (100), and a conduit connecting the                  liquid reservoir and the unit (100). The system                  further comprises a moving unit for moving the                  several units (200) so that the units (200) are                  sequentially supplied with the liquid from the                  unit (100).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (iii), the                  regenerated liquid (a3) is indirectly re-supplied                  in such a way that it is supplied to the unit                  (100) after being supplied to a liquid reservoir                  (400). In order to control both the flow rate of                  the liquid (a2) supplied from the liquid                  reservoir to the unit (100) through pump (300)                  and the flow rate of the liquid containing                  bicarbonate supplied from the unit (100) to the                  unit (200), the liquid overflowed from the unit                  (100) is supplied to the unit (200) by natural                  drainage method, and the regenerated liquid (a3)                  is supplied to the liquid reservoir by natural                  drainage method. The liquid (a2) is supplied to                  the unit (100) via a liquid intermediate                  reservoir between the liquid reservoir and the                  unit (100) by natural drainage method, and the                  liquid (a2) from the unit (100) is supplied to                  the unit (200) by natural drainage method. Carbon                  dioxide introduced into the unit (100) has a gas                  flow in which the remaining unconverted amount                  flows out to the upper surface of the liquid                  after being introduced from the upper surface of                  the liquid surface. In order to place carbonic                  anhydrase near the surface of the water without                  being affected by fluctuations in water level and                  to prevent it from being lost when the liquid                  overflows, the carbonic anhydrase is provided in                  a fixed state in a floating component, and the                  floating component is provided so as not to                  escape from the unit (100). The vicinity of the                  water surface is a liquid region from the water                  surface to a point at a depth of 10 cm. The flow                  rate of the liquid (a2) supplied into the unit                  (200) increases the bicarbonate ion conversion                  rate of carbon dioxide in the reaction unit                  (100), which is determined by the ratio of amount                  of calcium carbonate produced in a carbon dioxide                  conversion system (A) in which the conversion                  reaction unit equipped with carbonic anhydrase is                  arranged in the unit (100) with respect to amount                  of calcium carbonate produced in a carbon dioxide                  conversion system (B) except that there is no                  carbonic anhydrase in the unit (100). The amount                  of calcium carbonate produced in the two carbon                  dioxide conversion systems is filled until just                  before the liquid level of the unit (100)                  overflows, and calcium carbonate produced in the                  unit (200) after a predetermined time is elapsed                  when carbon dioxide and liquid (a2) at a                  predetermined flow rate are supplied to the unit                  (100) in a state where calcium ions are provided                  in the liquid regeneration unit of the unit                  (200). In order to disperse the bicarbonate ions                  converted in the process (i), the liquid in the                  unit (100) is stirred in the process (i). The                  liquid regeneration unit contains calcium ions,                  and the introduced bicarbonate ions are                  eventually converted into calcium carbonate                  through the calcium ions, so that the bicarbonate                  ions are removed. The process (p2) is batch                  circulation method in which several units (200)                  containing calcium ions are sequentially                  introduced into the process (ii) in the liquid                  regeneration unit, and the unit (200) that has                  completed the process (ii) is operated in batch                  circulation method that is reintroduced into the                  process (ii). The process (P1) further involves                  separating and discharging the regenerated liquid                  (a3) except for the calcium carbonate precipitate                  (250) in the unit (200), discharging calcium                  carbonate precipitates from the unit (200) from                  which the liquid (a3) is discharged, and                  measuring the concentration of calcium ions in                  the regenerated liquid, when calcium ions are                  contained above a predetermined concentration, or                  supplying the liquid (a3) to another reaction                  unit (200) in a state in which both the liquid                  (a3) and the precipitate are discharged, and                  re-injecting the regenerated liquid (a3) the                  process (iii), when calcium ions are contained in                  below a predetermined concentration.

TI Artificial forest creation system comprises e.g. a            suction pipe for sucking air, and a circulation pipe            for forming a passage through which the air sucked from            the suction tube circulates, and multiple air diffusers            branching from the circulation pipe.
AU CHUL L
AE LOWCARBON CO LTD (LOWC-Non-standard)
GA 2022A6534Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a circulation pipe (2) for                forming a passage through which air sucked from a                suction pipe (1) circulates. A set of air diffusers                (3) is branched from the circulation pipe, and                disperses and supplies air in a form of                micro-bubbles in a chamber (4). The chamber                collects carbon dioxide in the air by reacting the                air with a pre-stored basic alkali mixture. A                separator collects a reactant including the carbon                dioxide captured in the chamber, and separates a                carbon dioxide reactant and a waste solution from                the reactant. A discharge pipe (6) discharges the                remaining air from which the captured carbon                dioxide is removed. The basic alkaline solution and                water are mixed in a ratio of 1:1 to 1:5.
   USE - System for creating artificial forest.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces carbon dioxide by                collecting carbon dioxide in the air like a tree,                thus improving surrounding air quality in an area                where the system is built. The system produces                sodium carbonate or sodium hydrogen carbonate,                which is a useful resource using the captured                carbon dioxide, so that the system can be used in                different industrial fields, thus securing economic                feasibility compared to existing technologies. The                aeration device is provided in a form of an                aeration tank for dispersing the air in the form of                fine bubbles, and reacting the air with the basic                alkali mixture serving as a catalyst for capturing                carbon dioxide so as to improve efficiency of                capturing the carbon dioxide from the air. The                operating cost is low by solving disadvantages of                aqueous alkanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                artificial forest creation system.
   Suction pipe (1)
   Circulation pipe (2)
   Air diffuser (3)
   Chamber (4)
   Discharge pipe (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The basic alkali mixture is selected from                  the group consisting of silicon dioxide, aluminum                  oxide, iron oxide, titanium dioxide, magnesium                  oxide, manganese oxide, calcium oxide, sodium                  oxide, potassium oxide, and phosphorus trioxide.                  The metal is selected from the group lithium,                  chromium, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, gallium,                  strontium, cadmium and lead. The liquid                  composition is selected from the group consisting                  of sodium tetraborate, sodium hydroxide, sodium                  silicate, potassium hydroxide and hydrogen                  peroxide.

PN CN113763796-A
TI Experimental device for simulating carbon dioxide saturated fluid-surrounding rock interaction, has constant pressure and constant flow pump that is arranged between reactive fluid source and intermediate container.
AU WANG J
   ZHU D
   LIU Q
   HE Z
   DING Q
AE SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202203662F
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a carbon dioxide gas source and a reaction fluid source that are respectively connected with an intermediate container (3). A preheater (4) is respectively connected with the intermediate vessel and the reactor. The chemical signal acquisition device is connected to the reaction kettle. A booster pump (7) is arranged between the carbon dioxide gas source and the intermediate container. A constant pressure and constant flow pump (8) is arranged between the reactive fluid source and the intermediate container. Multiple control valves are provided between the carbon dioxide gas source and the booster pump, between the booster pump and the intermediate container, between the intermediate container and the preheater, and between the preheater and the reactor. A back pressure valve is arranged between the reaction kettle and the chemical signal acquisition device.
   USE - Experimental device for simulating carbon dioxide saturated fluid-surrounding rock interaction in real time online monitoring and recording and storing of oil and gas exploration practice.
   ADVANTAGE - The deep formation is simulated to the surface carbon dioxide saturated fluid surrounding rock system. The process of carbon dioxide-saturated fluid transforming the surrounding rock at different depths and layers is ensured. The reaction process is monitored online in real time, and recorded and stored.

PN US2020316492-A1
TI Carbon dioxide extraction system for subcritical and supercritical extractions for utilizing extraction pump, in which flow of material from exit port to gas-liquid extraction pump or condenser is controlled by valve for recirculation.
AU CHESS F J
AE CHESS F J (CHES-Individual)
GA 202097881V
AB    NOVELTY - The extraction system (10) has a tank containing gas which includes an inlet for filling the tank with gas and an outlet for allowing the gas to be transferred within the extraction system. A multifunctional dual-purpose gas-liquid extraction pump is in connection with the tank. An extractor pressure vessel is in connection with the multiffinctional dual-purpose gas-liquid extraction pump. A separator pressure vessel is in connection with the extractor pressure vessel. The separator pressure vessel has an entry port, an entry tube, a separation zone, a collection zone, an exit tube and an exit port. The exit port is connected to the gas-liquid extraction pump and an external condenser (24) by conduit. The flow of material from the exit port to either the gas-liquid extraction pump or the external condenser is controlled by a valve for recirculation within the extraction system.
   USE - Carbon dioxide extraction system for subcritical and supercritical extractions for utilizing multiffinctional dual-purpose gas-liquid extraction pump for pumping vapor or liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide extraction system eliminates several pressure vessels involved in separation of extracts from carbon dioxide and condensing carbon dioxide and eliminates associated heaters and chillers in such extraction systems. The carbon dioxide extraction system results in the reduction of pumping costs due to the circulating carbon dioxide remaining at one steady pressure at the inlet and outlet of the pump which allows the pump to work less hard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for extracting compounds from an organic material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide extraction system.
   Carbon dioxide extraction system (10)
   Extraction vessel (12)
   Separator (14)
   External condenser (24)
   Accumulator (26)

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide fluid natural textile dye composition prepared by mixing red pigment, curcumine and Gardenia blue pigment.
AU ZHENG J
   LIU J
   WANG S
   WANG Y
   JIAO M
   ZHANG X
   LIU F
AE UNIV ZHONGYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UZYT-C)
   ZHENGZHOU MANROU TEXTILE CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHONGYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UZYT-C)
GA 201833397N
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide fluid natural textile dye composition prepared by mixing red pigment, curcumine and Gardenia blue pigment in any ratio, is claimed.
   USE - Used as supercritical carbon dioxide fluid natural textile dye composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The dyeing system: detects the ratio change of three primary colors dye in real time; and avoids competing dyeing phenomenon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for dyeing system, comprising connected orderly carbon dioxide steel bottle, booster pump, dye kettle, dyeing kettle, and separating kettle between the fluid outlet of the dyeing kettle and the dye kettle fluid inlet is further provided with a circulating pump, and #and dyeing method comprising (i) placing the textile in the dyeing kettle, uniformly adding mixed dye into the dye kettle, connecting the whole dyeing system, pumping the carbon dioxide through booster pump, and controlling the temperature and pressure of the dye kettle at the same time so as to reach the preset value, (ii) closing valve of the booster pump and the separating kettle, opening the circulation pump, starting dyeing to make the fluid circulate between the dye kettle and the dyeing kettle, and detecting dye ratio change in the dyeing process online until after dyeing, (iii) finishing dyeing, cooling the system gradually, starting the booster pump when the temperature is lower than the glass temperature of the textile fiber, filling carbon dioxide, opening the separating kettle valve while the clean carbon dioxide flows through the textile, dissolving redundant dye away, vacuum extracting while passing through the separation kettle, making the carbon dioxide into gaseous, separating dye until the redundant dye on the textile is cleaned, and returning to the circulation tank after condensing gaseous carbon dioxide, and (iv) opening the dyeing kettle, taking out the textile, and finishing the whole dyeing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide steel bottle and pressure pump is further connected with the circulation tank in between. The carbon dioxide inlet of carbon dioxide outlet and circulation tank in the separation kettle is further provided with carbon dioxide loop. The heat exchanger is connected between the carbon dioxide steel bottle and the circulation tank. The fluid inlet of the dye kettle connected with a heat exchanger. The fluid outlet of the fluid exit of dye kettle and dyeing kettle is further connected with online monitor of the detector. The pressure of booster pump during pumping carbon dioxide is 20-28 MPa. Preferred Method: The dye kettle is maintained at 110-180 degrees C with 20-28 MPa pressure.

PT P
PN FR3104455-A1; WO2021121980-A1; FR3104455-B1
TI Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for forming recoverable products, involves contacting charge containing carbon dioxide and sacrificial compound with photocatalyst containing molybdenum sulfide nanoparticles, and irradiating.
AU DEVERS E
   BONDUELLE-SKRZYPCZAK A
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 202168174Q
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide involves (a) contacting a charge containing carbon dioxide and at least one sacrificial compound with a photocatalyst, and (b) irradiating the photocatalyst with at least one irradiation source so as to reduce carbon dioxide and oxidize the sacrificial compound in the presence of the photocatalyst activated by the irradiation source, and forming effluent containing at least portion carbon molecules of 1C or more, different from the carbon dioxide. The photocatalyst comprises a support and nanoparticles of molybdenum sulfide or tungsten sulfide. The photocatalyst is prepared by contacting water with the support so as to obtain a wet support, contacting the wet support with at least one hydrated metal acid comprising at least molybdenum or tungsten and having melting point of 20-100 degrees C, to form a solid mixture, and heating the solid mixture to form a photocatalytic precursor and carrying out sulfurization of the precursor.
   USE - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for forming carbon molecules for valuable products such as fuels and industrial chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide with improved performance. The photocatalyst absorbs the visible portion of the solar spectrum, to enhance a large proportion of the incident solar energy. The photocatalyst has excellent photocatalytic activity and is prepared by simple method and with possible load of molybdenum or tungsten.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide involves (a) contacting a charge containing carbon dioxide and at least one sacrificial compound with a photocatalyst, and (b) irradiating the photocatalyst with at least one irradiation source which produces at least one wavelength absorbable by the photocatalyst so as to reduce carbon dioxide and oxidize the sacrificial compound in the presence of the photocatalyst activated by the irradiation source, and forming effluent containing at least portion carbon molecules of 1C or more, different from the carbon dioxide. The photocatalyst comprises a support and nanoparticles of molybdenum sulfide or tungsten sulfide, having band gap of greater than 2.3 eV. The photocatalyst is prepared by contacting water with the support so as to obtain a wet support, contacting the wet support with at least one hydrated metal acid comprising at least molybdenum or tungsten and having melting point of 20-100 degrees C, to form a solid mixture, and heating the solid mixture with stirring to a temperature between the melting point of the hydrated metal acid to 100 degrees C to form a photocatalytic precursor and carrying out sulfurization of the photocatalytic precursor. The mass ratio between the metallic acid and the support is 0.1-2.5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of water introduced into the support is 10-70 vol.%. The support is a support based on alumina or on silica or on silica alumina. The support is a support based on a solid semiconductor chosen from group IVA elements, such as silicon, germanium, silicon carbide or silicon-germanium, group IIIA element and group VA element such as gallium phosphate, gallium nitride, indium phosphide, and indium gallium arsenide, element of groups IIB and VIA, such as cadmium sulfide, zinc oxide, or zinc sulfide, or group IB and VIIA element such as copper chloride, and silver bromide, elements of group IVA and VIA group such as lead sulfide, lead oxide, tin sulfide and lead tin telluride, elements of group VA and VIA, such as bismuth telluride and bismuth oxide, elements of group IIB and VA such as cadmium phosphide, zinc phosphide, zinc arsenide, elements of group VIIIB and VIA such as cobalt oxide, palladium oxide, iron oxide, iron oxide and nickel oxide, or elements of group VIB and VIA such as molybdenum sulfide, and tungsten oxide, elements of groups VB and VIA, such as vanadium pentoxide and niobium oxide and tantalum oxynitride or elements of groups IVB and VIA, such as titanium oxide and hafnium sulfide, or elements of groups IIIA and VIA, such as indium oxide and indium sulfide, or elements of VIA groups and lanthanides, such as cerium oxide, praseodymium oxide, samarium sulfide, terbium sulfide and lanthanum sulfide, and VIA group elements and actinides such as uranium oxide (UO2) and uranium oxide (UO3), or carbon nitride The photocatalyst comprises 4-50 wt.% molybdenum sulfide or tungsten sulfide. The surface density which corresponds to the quantity of atoms of molybdenum or of tungsten atoms, deposited per surface unit of support, is 0.5-12 atoms of molybdenum or tungsten per square nanometer of support. Preferred Process: The irradiation source is a natural or artificial irradiation source. The irradiation source emits wavelength of 280-2500 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrated metal acid is chosen from hydrated phosphomolybdic acid, hydrated silicomolybdic acid, hydrated molybdosilicic acid, hydrated phosphotungstic acid and silicotungstic acid hydrate. When the process (b) is carried out in the gas phase, the sacrificial compound is a gaseous compound chosen from water, ammonia, dihydrogen, methane or an alcohol. When the photocatalytic reduction is carried out in the gas phase, a diluting fluid is used in steps (a) and/or (b). When the process (b) is carried out in the liquid phase, the sacrificial compound is a liquid or solid oxidizable compound soluble in the liquid charge, chosen from water, ammonia, a alcohol, an aldehyde or an amine.

PT P
PN JP2018016500-A
TI Manufacture of mayenite compound electride for system used for e.g. producing hydrogen involves irradiating electromagnetic waves of predetermined frequency to mayenite compound in which anion is included in cage.
AU ISHIYAMA S
   IDEHARA T
   HOSONO H
   YOKOYAMA H
   TODA Y
AE UNIV FUKUI (UYFK-C)
   TOKYO INST TECHNOLOGY NAT UNIV CORP (TOKD-C)
GA 2018096924
AB    NOVELTY - A mayenite compound electride is manufactured by irradiating electromagnetic waves of frequency band with frequency of 0.001-20 THz to mayenite compound in which anion is included in cage which has crystal structure of calcium oxide and aluminum oxide (12CaO.7Al2O3).
   USE - Manufacture of mayenite compound electride for system used for producing hydrogen and manufacturing carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Mayenite compound electride is manufactured in low temperature and short time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) hydrogen production process which involves water-gas-shift-reaction process which generates mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen from carbon monoxide and water by water gas shift reaction, carbon dioxide reduction and hydrogen recovery process which recovers hydrogen while reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by making mayenite compound electride, which has electron in cage having crystal structure of 12CaO.7Al2O3, contact mixed gas and heating in which electron of mayenite compound is substituted by oxygen ion by taking in oxygen in cage in carbon dioxide reduction and hydrogen recovery process, and mayenite compound electride regeneration process which regenerates mayenite compound electride using manufacturing process of mayenite compound electride;
   (2) manufacture of carbon monoxide which involves carbon-dioxide-reduction process which reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by making carbon dioxide contact the mayenite compound electride which has electron in cage having crystal structure of 12CaO.7Al2O3, and heating in which electron of mayenite compound is substituted by oxygen ion by taking in oxygen in cage in carbon-dioxide-reduction process, and regeneration of mayenite compound electride;
   (3) hydrogen production system comprising water-gas-shift-reaction apparatus which generates mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen from carbon monoxide and water by water gas shift reaction, reductive reaction apparatus which discharges hydrogen while reducing emitted carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by mayenite compound electride, hydrogen recovery container which recovers discharged hydrogen, carbon monoxide recovery apparatus which recovers discharged carbon monoxide, regeneration reaction apparatus which electron mayenite compound is made into electride by substituting with oxygen ion, and electromagnetic wave irradiation apparatus which irradiates electromagnetic waves of 0.001-20 THz to mayenite compound; and
   (4) carbon monoxide manufacturing system comprising reductive reaction apparatus which reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by making carbon dioxide contact mayenite compound electride, carbon monoxide recovery apparatus which recovers discharged carbon monoxide, regeneration reaction apparatus which regenerates mayenite compound electride, and electromagnetic wave irradiation apparatus which irradiates electromagnetic waves of 0.001-20 THz to mayenite compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: Regeneration reaction apparatus is film forming apparatus which coats titanium on surface of mayenite compound in which electron is substituted by oxygen ion by taking in oxygen in cage in reductive reaction apparatus. Hydrogen production system includes polisher which polishes and removes titanium oxide which covers surface of mayenite compound electride by irradiating electromagnetic wave to mayenite compound. Electromagnetic wave irradiation apparatus is apparatus which irradiates wave of frequency band of 0.1-20 THz to mayenite compound. Carbon monoxide manufacturing system includes carbon dioxide recovery apparatus which recovers carbon dioxide by burning fossil fuel and supplies to reductive reaction apparatus, and contains electric power generating apparatus which generates electricity with combustion gas of fossil fuel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Electromagnetic wave is irradiated to mayenite compound in vacuum. Electromagnetic wave is irradiated by applying voltage to mayenite compound in presence of reducing agent. Titanium oxide which covers surface of mayenite compound electride is removed after coating titanium on surface of mayenite compound and irradiating electromagnetic wave. Water gas shift reaction is performed using carbon monoxide obtained at carbon dioxide reduction and hydrogen recovery process. Electric power is produced with electric power generating apparatus using heat energy arising in water gas shift reaction. Exhaust gas heating from electric power generating apparatus is used for heating in carbon dioxide reduction and hydrogen recovery process while using electricity generated with electric power generating apparatus. Carbon-dioxide collection process further involves burning fossil fuel. Carbon dioxide recovered by carbon dioxide collection process is reduced to carbon monoxide. Preferred Components: Anion is oxygen ion, and reducing agent is titanium. Preferred Parameters: Frequency band of electromagnetic waves is 0.1-20 THz.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-P; E31-A02C; E31-N05B1

PN FR3088650-A1; FR3088650-B1
TI System for absorbing carbon dioxide, has edge of secondary container that is offset from edge of main container so that liquid contained in main container is transferred to secondary container, when liquid overflows over edge of container.
AU SIMON J
   SIMON E
AE CONSTR MECANIQUES CONSULTANTS (CSME-Non-standard)
   CONSTR MECANIQUES CONSULTANTS (CSME-Non-standard)
GA 202043786G
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a circulation circuit (4) of a liquid (5) that is comprised of water and organisms comprising aquatic plants capable of absorbing carbon dioxide by photosynthesis. The circulation circuit is set for connecting a secondary container (3) to a main container (2). A driving apparatus (6) is configured to move the liquid in the circulation circuit. A mixing device (7) is connected to the circulation circuit and configured to mix carbon dioxide in the liquid. The main and secondary containers are provided with a bottom (17) and an edge (18) located along the contour of the bottom and forming an opening (16) located on one side of the container opposite the bottom. The edge of the secondary container is offset from the edge of the main container so that the liquid contained in the main container is transferred to the secondary container when the liquid overflows over the edge of the main container.
   USE - System for absorbing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system for absorbing carbon dioxide is simple to manufacture and easy to assemble. The system offers a very simple way to absorb carbon dioxide, and occupies a determined surface area while allowing a significant increase in the exchange surface necessary for photosynthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for absorbing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for absorbing carbon dioxide.
   System for absorbing carbon dioxide (1)
   Main container (2)
   Secondary container (3)
   Circulation circuit (4)
   Liquid (5)
   Driving apparatus (6)
   Mixing device (7)
   Opening (16)
   Bottom of secondary container (17)
   Edge of secondary container (18)

PT P
PN IN202041013720-A; IN358853-B
TI Biodegradable carbon dioxide generating bio-resin used for attracting blood sucking insects, preferably mosquitoes, comprises pre-treated activated carbon coated on ethylene-vinyl acetate.
AU PANAIYUR K M
AE MGH LABS PVT LTD (MGHP-Non-standard)
GA 202044012H
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable carbon dioxide generating bio-resin comprises pre-treated activated carbon coated on ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA).
   USE - The bio-resin is used for attracting blood sucking insects, preferably mosquitoes. The bio-resin is used for attracting and capturing different species of mosquitoes chosen from Culex quinquefasciatus, Anopheles stephensi, Aedes aegypti and Armigerous subalbatus. The trapping efficiency of the bio-resin is tested against Aedes aegypti. The result showed that the bio-resin exhibited 95% efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-resin is biodegradable, safe, non-toxic, economical and sustainable source of carbon dioxide that acts as lure to attract blood sucking insects, preferably mosquitoes. The bio-resin is safe for humans and pets and does not contain any unpleasant odor. The bio-resin is effectively incorporated in mosquito traps such as indoor and outdoor mosquito traps as compared with conventional light traps, and works independent of power supply. The bio-resin does not directly release any carbon dioxide to the surrounding, and is highly environmentally-friendly and continuously absorbs carbon dioxide from the surrounding air column and desorption of carbon dioxide produces zone of carbon dioxide equilibrium. The bio-resin is suitable for attracting mosquitoes inside home or in garden. The bio-resin is used in various devices of different sizes, and easily supplemented with conventional light traps, and has shelf life of 3 months. The bio-resin does not increase the level of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process of preparing the bio-resin, comprising (a) pre-treating activated carbon with carbon dioxide, (b) coating the pre-treated activated carbon over EVA bio-resin, and (c) drying the bio-resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated carbon is pre-treated with carbon dioxide. The pre-treated activated carbon layer forms a carbon dioxide zone, where rapid exchange and absorption of carbon dioxide occurs.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The bio-resin provides 0.5-5 ppm carbon dioxide in the zone. Preferred Conditions: The pre-treatment of activated carbon is performed by producing carbon dioxide from ascorbic acid and sodium bicarbonate, which is then adsorbed by activated carbon. The pre-treated activated carbon is coated over EVA bio-resin in a thickness of 0.1-10 mm. The pre-treated activated carbon is coated over EVA bio-resin using magnetic stirrer at 250 rpm for 2-30 minutes at room temperature. The bio-resin is air-dried for 5-6 hours or dried in oven for 2-3 hours, after coating with pre-treated activated carbon. Preferred Properties: The thickness of coating of pre-treated activated carbon over EVA bio-resin is 0.1-10 mm.
   ACTIVITY - Insect Attractant.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
