PT P
PN US2020046896-A1; US11419974-B2
TI Method for delivering carbon dioxide for use in            medical imaging procedure, involves transmitting carbon            dioxide from gas unit to control valve assembly for            delivery to patient as contrast media in controlled            dosages patient as media.
AU LEVY F
   LEVY K
AE LEVY F (LEVY-Individual)
   LEVY K (LEVY-Individual)
GA 202012713E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a source of                pressurized carbon dioxide (CO2). The source of                pressurized CO2 is a cartridge (28) filled with                compressed carbon dioxide. The source of                pressurized carbon dioxide is connected to a                compressed gas unit (1) for controlling delivery of                the CO2. The compressed gas unit includes a                pressure regulator (140) outputting CO2 at a                selected level of pressure. The pressure of the CO2                delivered by the compressed gas unit is regulated.                The pressurized carbon dioxide is transmitted from                the compressed gas unit to a control valve assembly                for delivery to a patient as a contrast media in                controlled dosages patient as a contrast                media.
   USE - Method for delivering carbon dioxide for                radiological imaging of arteries and veins of                patient vascular system in medical imaging                procedures. Can also be used in wound care and hair                loss treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluid delivery system prevents potentially                dangerous amounts of air from entering the fluid                and thus, administered to the patient. The system                exhibits a failsafe and foolproof operation, which                permits only reliable and accurately controlled                dosages of a medical fluid to be administered to a                patient. The system can be used safely and                effectively with virtually any source of carbon                dioxide or other medical fluid regardless of the                pressure or environment under which that fluid is                maintained. The system prevents an administered                medical fluid from flowing in an unintended                direction through the system. The system enables                controlled amounts of carbon dioxide to be                delivered to the patient in a safe and reliable                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side perspective view of                the compressed gas unit.
   Compressed gas unit (1)
   Cartridge (28)
   Conduit (45)
   Threads (46)
   Pressure regulator (140)

PN CN110498416-A; CN210825439-U
TI System for synchronously recovering carbon dioxide and nitrogen from boiler flue gas of coal-fired power plant, has PSA high purity nitrogen system for pressure swing adsorption of nitrogen and oxygen-containing flue gas.
AU ZHANG J
   WU H
   LI Y
   CHEN S
   WU Y
   WANG H
   ZHAO S

   DI J

   YANG X

   SONG Z
AE DONGYING GANGCHENG HEAT CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019A12530
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a flue gas pretreatment system (1) for cooling and dewatering the flue gas from the boiler. The PSA1 system (2) is provided for PSA pretreatment of flue gas and exhaust of nitrogen and oxygen. The PSA2 system (3) is provided for PSA purification of carbon dioxide in the desorbed gas of PSA1 system. The carbon dioxide compression purification system (4) is provided for PSA2 system carbon dioxide desorption gas pressure liquefaction and removal of water and corrosive impurities. The rectification storage system (5) is provided for carbon dioxide produced by the carbon dioxide compression purification system purified by a rectification tower and stored in a finished product tank. The pressure swing adsorption (PSA) high purity nitrogen system (6) is provided for pressure swing adsorption of nitrogen and oxygen-containing flue gas discharged from PSA1 system and discharging high purity nitrogen.
   USE - System for synchronously recovering carbon dioxide and nitrogen from boiler flue gas of coal-fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is convenient for external transportation in various forms, and the obtained industrial-grade nitrogen can be pressurized as needed for centralized use in the industrial park. The equipment has high operation stability, is not prone to blockage, and is easy to maintain.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a system for synchronously recovering carbon dioxide and nitrogen from boiler flue gas of coal-fired power plant. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   PSA high purity nitrogen system (6)

PT P
PN CN109488398-A
TI Method for realizing utilization of low-grade waste heat and collection and utilization of carbon dioxide in flue gas in natural gas distributed energy source, involves performing oil displacement process in field by using carbon dioxide.
AU KE D
   ZHOU Y
   XIE Y
   WANG S
   LIANG J
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 201927094G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating combustion power using natural gas by a combustion engine to generate high-temperature smoke gas. The high-temperature smoke gas is entered into a waste heat boiler for heating boiler water to generate high-temperature steam. The high-temperature steam is entered into a steam turbine generator unit to drive a steam turbine and a generator to generate power. The steam turbine generator unit is supplied with steam. The high-temperature flue gas generated by the engine is passed through the waste heat boiler to form low-temperature flue gas. Heat in the low-temperature flue gas is recycled by a heat pump machine set to reduce temperature of the smoke gas. The cooled smoke gas is entered into a carbon dioxide collecting device for collecting carbon dioxide. Oil displacement process in an oil field is performed by using the collected carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for realizing utilization of low-grade waste heat and collection and utilization of carbon dioxide in flue gas in a natural gas distributed energy source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving utilization effect of distributed energy source low-grade waste heat and reducing energy consumption during carbon dioxide collecting and using process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for realizing utilization of low-grade waste heat and collection and utilization of carbon dioxide in flue gas in a natural gas distributed energy source.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a system for realizing utilization of low-grade waste heat and collection and utilization of carbon dioxide in flue gas in a natural gas distributed energy source. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN US2018333159-A1
TI Reducing risk of sustaining traumatic brain injury            caused by traumatic event comprises identifying subject            at risk of sustaining traumatic brain injury and            increasing partial pressure of carbon dioxide in            blood.
AU SMITH D
AE SMITH D (SMIT-Individual)
GA 2018911085
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing the risk of sustaining a traumatic                brain injury to a subject caused by a traumatic                event comprises identifying a subject at risk of                sustaining a traumatic brain injury and increasing                the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the blood                of the subject partial pressure of carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The methods are useful for: reducing the risk                of sustaining a traumatic brain injury; and                treating a condition of a subject that is                responsive changes in intracranial pressure and/or                middle cerebral blood velocity (all claimed). No                biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases cerebral blood flow by                51% after inhaling 5% carbon dioxide, and increases                flow by 102% after inhaling 7% carbon dioxide or                energy absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a breathing apparatus (10) for increasing                the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the blood                of a subject partial pressure of carbon dioxide,                comprising a chamber (20) having an opening (22) in                communication with the mouth of the subject and at                least one outlet through which gas flows as the                subject inhales and exhales, an enlarged dead space                between the opening in communication with the mouth                and the at least one outlet, the dead space                defining a volume sized to retain a predetermined                portion of the carbon dioxide exhaled by the                subject to be inhaled or re-breathed by the subject                on the next inhalation; and
   (2) treating a condition of a subject that is                responsive changes in intracranial pressure and/or                middle cerebral blood velocity, comprising                increasing the partial pressure of carbon dioxide                (pCO2) in the blood of the subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a perspective view of a                breathing apparatus.
   Apparatus (10)
   Strap arrangement (14)
   Chamber (20)
   Opening (22)
   Scale (34)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step of increasing the                  partial pressure of carbon dioxide includes                  increasing the carbon dioxide content of the air                  inhaled by the subject above the ambient carbon                  dioxide content of the air. The step of                  increasing the carbon dioxide content of the                  inhaled air includes forcing the subject to                  re-breathe a predetermined portion of the carbon                  dioxide exhaled by the subject while inhaling                  ambient air. The step of forcing the subject to                  re-breathe a predetermined portion of the carbon                  dioxide exhaled by the subject includes placing a                  breathing apparatus over the mouth of the subject                  through which the subject must inhale and exhale,                  where the breathing apparatus includes an                  enlarged dead space volume in which the                  predetermined portion of the carbon dioxide                  exhaled by the subject collects to be inhaled or                  re-breathed by the subject on the next                  inhalation. The dead space volume is sized in                  relation to the tidal volume of air displaced by                  the subject during inhalation and exhalation. The                  dead space volume is approximately 10-50% of the                  tidal volume of the subject. The dead space                  volume is sized in relation to the weight of the                  subject. The breathing apparatus further                  comprises a portion for retaining the breathing                  apparatus in communication with the mouth of the                  subject. PT P
PN CN113026047-A; CN113026047-B
TI Electrode material useful in electrochemical catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to methanol, comprises single-atom catalyst/carbon paper composite material supported on copper oxide.
AU GUO W
   SUN X
   HAN B
   LIU Z
   ZHU Q
   CHEN C
   TAN X
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 202176839J
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode material comprises a single-atom catalyst/carbon paper composite material supported on copper oxide.
   USE - The electrode material is useful in the electrochemical catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material provides green and efficient way of carbon dioxide chemical fixation and resource utilization, improves utilization of carbon dioxide, and alleviating environmental impact. The electrochemical system is easy to operate, is easy to be recycled, and has excellent stability of cathode catalyst and industrial development value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electrochemical catalytic system comprising the electrode material and a reaction electrolyte, where the reaction electrolyte is an ionic liquid-water mixed solution; and
   (2) a method for electrochemically catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol involving in the electrochemical catalytic system, using carbon dioxide as a raw material to synthesize methanol through the reaction of electrode materials and electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The single-atom catalyst supported on copper oxide is prepared by (a1) dissolving copper compound and a second metal compound in water, adding an alkaline aqueous solution to the resulting solution, stirring and transferring the resulting solution to an autoclave for a high-temperature hydrothermal reaction, (a2) after the reaction is completed, centrifuging the reaction system to collect the solid product and vacuum drying at 50-150 degrees C to obtain a black powder, and (a3) calcining the obtained black powder in an air atmosphere at 300-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours, and plasma cleaning using a plasma cleaning machine to carrying plasma surface cleaning in a 10% (hydrogen volume ratio) hydrogen-argon mixed atmosphere for 30-120 seconds. The step of high-temperature hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 80-220 degrees C for 2-48 hours. The copper oxide-supported monoatom catalyst/carbon paper composite material is prepared by dispersing the copper oxide-supported monoatom catalyst in an organic solvent, and drip-coating the resulting dispersion on commercial carbon paper, where the dosage of the catalyst is 0.1-50 mg/cm2. In the method, the reaction potential is -1.6 to -2.8 V vs. silver/silver ion, and the reaction time is 0.5-120 hours. Preferred Components: The copper compound is chosen from at least one of copper sulfate, copper nitrate, copper chloride, copper acetate, copper acetylacetonate and their hydrates. The second metal compound is chosen from at least one of tin chloride, indium chloride, bismuth chloride, zinc chloride, cadmium chloride, iron chloride, cobalt chloride, and nickel chloride. The alkaline aqueous solution is selected from at least one of sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, sodium carbonate aqueous solution, potassium carbonate aqueous solution, sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution, and potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution. The reaction electrolyte comprises the ionic liquid comprising at least one of 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole tetrafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole trifluoromethanesulfonate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole perchlorate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole chloride salt, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole bromide, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole dihydrogen phosphate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole trifluoromethanesulfonate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole perchlorate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole chloride, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole bromide, and 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole dihydrogen phosphate. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the copper compound to the second metal compound is (1000:1)-(10:1). The pH of the alkaline aqueous solution is 10-14. The molar ratio of ionic liquid to water is (1:1)-(1:50).

   USE - Method of producing methanol used in manufacture of chemicals e.g. methyl tert-butyl ether, acetic acid and formaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of methanol with high productivity due to removal of water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the system for producing methanol from gaseous mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the process for producing methanol.
   System (100)
   Water-splitting reactor (102)
   Separation unit (104)
   Methanol generation unit (106)
   Reactant stream (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The water-splitting unit is a photocatalytic water-splitting unit, and/or a thermochemical unit. The separation unit comprises at least one separation unit (104). The methanol production unit (106) is a reverse water gas shift reactor in combination with a methanol reactor or a direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixture of hydrogen and oxygen comprises at least 70 mol.%, preferably 70-75 mol.% carbon dioxide. The hydrogen and oxygen reactant stream comprises at least 30 mol.% hydrogen. The hydrogen and oxygen molar ratio of the reactant stream is 1:1-3:1, 2:1-3:1 or 2.5:1, preferably 4:1 during separation process (b). Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises at least one of alkali metal and/or oxide, alkaline earth metal and/or oxide, transition metal and/or oxide, lanthanide and/or oxide, and/or post transition metal and/or oxide, preferably copper, zinc, aluminum and/or oxides. The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal is rubidium, strontium, and/or barium. The transition metal is yttrium, copper, nickel, titanium, zirconium, molybdenum, tungsten, silver, and/or zinc. The lanthanide is lanthanum, cerium, terbium, and/or ytterbium. The post transition metal is aluminum and/or bismuth. The catalyst is nickel/zinc/zirconium/cerium/yttrium, copper/zinc/zirconium/cerium/lanthanum, molybdenum/zinc/zirconium/cerium/yttrium, copper/bismuth/yttrium, and/or copper/zinc/zirconium/cerium/barium/aluminum. The methanol catalyst is copper/zinc/aluminum, copper/zinc/aluminum/cerium/yttrium, copper/zinc/cerium/silicon, and/or copper/zinc/aluminum/indium. Preferred Process: The process for producing methanol involves passing the hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixture through a membrane unit and/or liquid absorption unit, separating (b) oxygen from the mixture to obtain the hydrogen and carbon dioxide reactant stream, adjusting the hydrogen and carbon dioxide molar ratio of the reactant stream to a predetermined value during or after separating (b) or both, contacting the reactant stream with the catalyst to produce a reverse water gas shift reaction stream (RWGSR) comprising hydrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and water, and contacting the RWGSR stream with a methanol catalyst to produce methanol. Preferred Conditions: The mixture of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and oxygen is compressed to 0.1-10 MPa, or 0.1, 1, 5, 6 or 9 MPa. The methanol is produced at 200-500 degrees C, preferably 220 degrees C.

   HIREMATH V

AE UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACADEMIA COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
   UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACAD COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
GA 201861252A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide comprises dissolving the magnesium precursor and the fuel in a solvent, heating the solvent to evaporate some or all of the solvent, combusting the fuel to obtain a magnesium oxide powder and firing the magnesium oxide powder.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is a simple and simple; provides a fast adsorption rate without significantly falling. The adsorbent has greatly improved cycle characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) adsorbent produced by burning a magnesium precursor with the fuel containing an amine group, and having a mesoporous structure;
   (2) carbon dioxide adsorbing carbon dioxide using the adsorbent which is produced by burning a magnesium precursor with a fuel containing an amine group and having a mesoporous structure, by introducing carbon dioxide gas into the adsorbent at 75-300 degrees C; and
   (3) regeneration of adsorbent by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The combustion of the fuel is carried out at 250-350 degrees C or 350-450 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. The calcination is carried out for 2-10 hours. The average crystal size of the adsorbent is 5-25 nm. The adsorbent is heated to 250-300 degrees C. The heating is carried out: for 30-60 minutes; and in nitrogen atmosphere. Preferred Components: The adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide has a mesoporous structure. The fuel comprises amine group and carboxy group, where the fuel is glycine or urea. The molar ratio of fuel/magnesium precursor of 1-2. The magnesium precursor is magnesium nitrate or magnesium acetate. The adsorbent: has an average pore diameter of 10-30 nm; has average pore diameter of 14-25 nm; average pore volume of the adsorbent of 0.4-1cm3; BET surface area of 50-250 (preferably 65-230) m2/g.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing sodium bicarbonate from carbon dioxide            involves mixing sodium compound solution and ammonia            solution, introducing carbon dioxide to resulting            mixture to obtain sodium bicarbonate product, filtering            and drying.
AU SINTARAKO P
   TECHAKRITIKUL C
   JANSANG B
   GATEMALA H
   PADOUNGPANYASIN G
AE PTT PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTP-Non-standard)
   PTT PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTP-Non-standard)
GA 2020960669
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sodium bicarbonate from                carbon dioxide involves (a) preparing a solution of                sodium compound by dissolving sodium compound in                deionized water, (b) preparing ammonia solution by                dissolving ammonia in deionized water, (c) mixing                the obtained solutions together at                temperature-controlled conditions, in which the                final concentration of mixed solution is 1-10 mol/l                for sodium compound and 1-5 mol/l for ammonia                solution, (d) introducing carbon dioxide to the                resulting mixture at controlled conditions of flow                rate, time, temperature, and pressure, resulting in                sodium bicarbonate product with white solid                property, and (e) filtering the obtained product                out of solution and leaving still to dry at room                temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sodium                bicarbonate from carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe, provides high                purity product at atmospheric pressure and less                than 60 degrees C without by-products, reduces                emission, and enhances value of carbon dioxide from                natural gas separation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  carbon dioxide is 99.5-99.9 vol.%. Preferred                  Method: The rate for introducing carbon dioxide                  into the mixed solution of sodium compound and                  ammonia solution is 0.1-0.5 l/minute per the                  total liquid amount of 0.2 l. The step of                  introducing the carbon dioxide into the mixed                  solution of sodium compound and ammonia solution                  is carried out for 1-10 hours at a pressure of                  1-5 bar. The steps (a), (b) and (c) are carried                  out at 20-60 degrees C. The step of filtering                  further involves washing the filtered product                  using organic solvent after filtration. Preferred                  Components: The sodium compound comprises sodium                  carbonate and/or sodium hydroxide. The organic                  solvent comprises alcohol organic solvent,                  preferably ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for improving estimated carbon dioxide            concentration in indoor applications, involves            splitting change of volatile organic compound            concentration into human and non-human generated            volatile organic compound concentration changes.
AU SCHREIBER R
   MEYER C
AE IDT EURO GMBH (IDTE-Non-standard)
   IDT EURO GMBH (IDTE-Non-standard)
GA 201950705J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving estimated carbon dioxide                concentration involves observing temporal change of                volatile organic compound concentration, splitting                the change of volatile organic compound                concentration into human generated volatile organic                compound concentration change and non-human                generated volatile organic compound concentration                change, accumulating the human generated volatile                organic compound concentration change to human                generated volatile organic compound concentration,                resulting in total human generated volatile organic                compound concentration and calculating estimated                carbon dioxide concentration only from total human                generated volatile organic compound                concentration.
   USE - Method for improving estimated carbon dioxide                concentration by volatile organic compound sensor                e.g. metal oxide sensor, in non-industrial indoor                applications (claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to deodorant, air freshener, white board                cleaner, and food preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improved estimation of                carbon dioxide concentration with excellent                calculation procedure, low error of estimation of                carbon dioxide, reduced measurement noise, spikes                and notches using smoothing filter.

PN CN109706475-A; CN109706475-B
TI Gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst useful for reducing carbon dioxide.
AU FANG P
   HUANG J
   LIU X
   TONG Y
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201942652U
AB    NOVELTY - Gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst is claimed. The nanoelectrocatalyst is obtained by taking gold nanoparticles as the core, growing silver nano-shell layer on the surface, taking gold-silver nano-material as the skeleton, and loading with silver sulfide nanostructures, where the gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst has at least one dimension in the range of 0.1-999.9 nm.
   USE - The gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoelectrocatalyst: inhibits the hydrogen evolution reaction; improves the efficiency of the direct conversion of carbon dioxide into the alcohol product; and reaches the Faraday efficiency of the ethanol to 70%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst in reducing carbon dioxide, comprising introducing carbon dioxide into reaction substrate solution, taking gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanomaterial as catalyst, and applying voltage of -0.4 to -1.0 V to reduce carbon dioxide to carbon-containing compound in the reaction substrate liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) assembling gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanomaterials on different substrate surfaces to obtain working electrode, (ii) adding reaction substrate solution into the electrolytic cell, placing gold-silver/silver sulfide working electrode, reference electrode and counter electrode, and connecting to the electrochemical workstation, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction substrate solution, and (iv) applying voltage, and carrying out reduction reaction of carbon dioxide on the surface of the catalyst. In step (iii), the rate of introducing the carbon dioxide gas into the reaction substrate solution is 10-60 ml/minute, and the voltage is -0.4 to -1.0 V. Preferred Components: The length of gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst nanorod is 90-110 nm. The transmission electron micrograph of gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst is consistent with fig 1. The high resolution diagram of gold-silver/silver sulfide heterojunction nanoelectrocatalyst is consistent with fig 2. The particle diameter of gold nanoparticles is 20-30 nm, and the thickness of silver nanoshell is 90-110 nm. The solute of the reaction substrate liquid is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or sodium carbonate; and the solvent of the reaction substrate liquid is water and/or acetonitrile.

AU KUSHI T
   SHIRAKI M
   KIKUCHI T
   OTSUKA F
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
   UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reforming catalyst production involves heating and mixing compound containing an aluminum atom, a compound containing a magnesium atom, a compound containing a nickel atom, a compound having a carboxy group which is a complexing agent, and water to form complex. The formed complex is heated and mixed with a polyhydric alcohol to obtain complex polymer. The resulting complex polymer is heated to obtain a complex oxide, where the obtained composite oxide is subjected to reduction treatment to give the carbon dioxide reforming catalyst.
   USE - Method for producing carbon dioxide reforming catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the carbon dioxide reforming catalyst in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with reduced precipitation of precipitate carbon and high methane conversion rate.

TI Improving oil recovery within subterranean formation comprises forming barrier within first region of subterranean formation to isolate portion of first region from proximate second region, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU ALYOUSIF Z
   AL-TAQ A A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)

GA 202124305H
AB    NOVELTY - Improving oil recovery within a subterranean formation (104), comprises: forming a barrier within a first region (106) of the subterranean formation to isolate at least a portion of the first region from a proximate second region (108) of the subterranean formation, where the first region comprises a greater permeability than the second region, forming the barrier comprising introducing a first solution and a second solution into the first region of the subterranean formation to form a mixture, the first solution comprises an ammonium containing compound and the second solution comprises a nitrite containing compound and introducing carbon dioxide into the subterranean formation so that at least a portion of the carbon dioxide gas dissolves in the mixture to produce carbonic acid, the first solution and/or the second solution further comprise a foaming agent; and introducing carbon dioxide into the subterranean formation.
   USE - The method is useful for improving oil recovery within a subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: readily generates a foam within subterranean formations without the need for energy intensive injections of gas or foam; and reduces the loss of treatment materials and increases the volumetric sweep of the treatment method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving oil recovery within a subterranean formation (104), comprises: forming a barrier within a first region (106) of the subterranean formation to isolate at least a portion of the first region from a proximate second region (108) of the subterranean formation, where the first region comprises a greater permeability than the second region, forming the barrier comprising introducing a first solution and a second solution into the first region of the subterranean formation to form a mixture, the first solution comprises an ammonium containing compound and the second solution comprises a nitrite containing compound and introducing carbon dioxide into the subterranean formation so that at least a portion of the carbon dioxide gas dissolves in the mixture to produce carbonic acid, the first solution and/or the second solution further comprise a foaming agent and the production of carbonic acid causes the ammonium containing compound and the nitrite containing compound to react to generate nitrogen gas in the presence of the foaming agent to generate a foam within the first region of the subterranean formation, the foam providing the barrier; and introducing carbon dioxide into the subterranean formation, where the barrier diverts the carbon dioxide away from the first region and into the second region so that the carbon dioxide operates to displace at least a portion of a hydrocarbon present in the second region and discharge the hydrocarbon from the subterranean formation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates a wellbore during a carbon dioxide miscible displacement enhances oil recovery method after a treatment process with a foam.
   Installation (100)
   Injection well (102)
   Subterranean formation (104)
   First region (106)
   Second region (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first solution and the second solution are introduced into the subterranean formation separately. The first solution is introduced into the subterranean formation through a first conduit and the second solution is introduced into the subterranean formation through a second conduit. The first solution and the second solution are introduced into the subterranean formation in a serial manner. The first solution and the second solution are introduced into the subterranean formation simultaneously. The 50-99% of the carbon dioxide is diverted from the first region to the second region. The viscosity of the generated foam is 15 times greater to 50 times greater than the viscosity of the carbon dioxide.Preferred Components: The second solution comprises the foaming agent. The nitrite containing compound comprises sodium nitrite and the ammonium containing compound comprises ammonium chloride. The molar ratio of sodium nitrite to ammonium chloride is 1:1-2:1. The pH of a mixture of the first solution and the second solution is less than or equal to4. The mixture of the first solution and the second solution further comprises carbonic acid. The foaming agent comprises an acid tolerant surfactant. The viscosity of the generated foam is 1-50 cP. The first region is at least 10% more permeable than the second region. The gas to oil ratio of the hydrocarbons discharged from the subterranean formation is 50 standard cubic meters per standard cubic meter (Sm3/Sm3) to 200 Sm3/Sm3.

PT P
PN CN113308707-A
TI Gas diffusion electrode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide reaction, is obtained by selecting carrier as gas diffusion electrode in waterproof ventilating layer substrate according to hydrophobic treatment effect.
AU DUAN J
   ZHENG M
   YANG K
   CHEN S
   MA L
   LI Q
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2021A4311Y
AB    NOVELTY - Gas diffusion electrode is obtained by selecting a carrier as a gas diffusion electrode in a waterproof ventilating layer substrate according to hydrophobic treatment effect. Hydrophobic treatment of the gas diffusion electrode is performed through high temperature sintering or magnetic control sputtering. Conductivity of the waterproof ventilation electrode is enhanced by spraying.
   USE - The fas diffusion electrode is used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare hydrocarbon fuel. It is used as cathode electrode for alkaline flow electrolytic cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hydrocarbon fuel gas diffusion electrode, which improves conductivity and electro-catalysis performance of water-proof ventilation electrode by spray gun spraying method, researches influence of key factors on electrochemical reduction carbon dioxide performance by changing conditions, prevents electrolyte overflow in alkaline flow electrolytic cell, and forms ideal gas-liquid-solid carbon dioxide-electrolyte-copper-based catalyst three-phase interface for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of the gas diffusion electrode as cathode electrode for alkaline flow electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: When the carrier of the substrate material is sintered at high temperature, the hydrophobic modification treatment is carried out at 200-400 degrees C for 30-300 minutes. When the carrier of the substrate material is subjected to magnetron sputtering, the hydrophobic modification treatment is performed, the material range is 99.99% copper target, and the sputtering rate is not greater than 99.99%. The surface of the water-proof ventilation electrode layer is sprayed with copper powder by spray gun, crystalline flake graphite or conductive carbon powder material for enhancing the conductivity of the waterproof ventilation electrode, and electro-catalysis performance. The load range of the copper powder is 0.1-5 mg/cm2, or the mass range of the conductive carbon powder is 0.1-3 mg/cm. The mass range of the crystalline flake graphite is 0.1-3 mg/cm. During the spraying process, the binder perfluorosulfonic acid Nafion and/or PTFE is added and adjusted, and the mass concentration range is 0.1-5 mg/mL. The volume of the alkaline flow electrolytic cell is from 1mL to 20mL. The pH range of the electrolyte is 7-14, the ion exchange membrane uses anion exchange membrane FAA-3-50 or FAB-PK-130, the flow speed range of the electrolyte is 1-10 ml/minute, the flow speed range of the carbon dioxide gas is 20-100 mL/minute, and the whole reaction temperature range is 20-30 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrier of the substrate material is HESEN HCP120, HESEN HCP030N, YLS-30T or PTFE.

PN CN112796729-A
TI Fracturing quasi-dry liquid supercritical carbon dioxide acid involves using active water to replace positively until casing wellhead sees liquid, and filling oil jacket with liquid, setting packer in wellbore with pumping displacement.
AU ZHANG Z
   BAI J
   JIANG C
   ZHANG R
   LI J
AE KARAMAY KEMEILI CHEM CO LTD (KARA-Non-standard)
GA 202156609C
AB    NOVELTY - Fracturing quasi-dry liquid supercritical carbon dioxide acid involves using active water to replace positively at a pump displacement ranging from 0.3-0.5m3/minute until the casing wellhead sees liquid, and filling the oil jacket with liquid, setting the packer in the wellbore with a pumping displacement of 0.5-2.5m3/minute. The fracturing truck pumps liquid carbon dioxide with a volume of 50-500m3 into the formation, and the pumping displacement is 3-10m3/minute. The low-resistance viscosity-increasing liquid carbon dioxide and acid are injected into the formation according to one of a fixed ratio, varying ratio or fixed ratio discontinuous. The active water is injected, and the well is closed for 2-6 hours and then the well is opened for drainage.
   USE - Method for fracturing quasi-dry liquid supercritical carbon dioxide acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the maximum acid fracturing effect, reduces the consumption of water resources and cause little damage to the reservoir, and increases the production of carbonate gas reservoirs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution includes hydrochloric acid or organic acid, corrosion inhibitor, drainage aid, acid thickener, Span (RTM: A nonionic surfactant and emulsifier) 80 and water. The drainage aid is a fluorocarbon-based drainage aid. The active water comprises potassium chloride, drainage aid and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide drag reducing thickener is long-linked fatty acid, polyvinyl acetate, polyfluoroalkyl acrylate or fluorinated acrylate-styrene-sulfonated styrene. The solubilizer is low-molecular alcohol substance. The organic acid is formic acid or acetic acid. The corrosion inhibitor is imidazoline corrosion inhibitor. The acid thickener is made by copolymerization of acrylamide monomer and acid-resistant and salt-resistant monomer.

PN CN112500385-A; CN112500385-B
TI Preparing ultrafine particles of            7-O-farnesenyl-3'-O-methylgeraniol by applying            supercritical fluid enhanced solution dispersion            technology by dissolving geraniol derivative in organic            solvent, crystallizing, and feeding carbon            dioxide.
AU WANG Z
   WANG C
   HE S
   YANG Z
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 202128372Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultrafine particles of geraniol                derivative by applying supercritical fluid enhanced                solution dispersion technology, comprises (i)                dissolving geraniol derivative in an organic                solvent to obtain a composite solution; (ii)                adjusting the temperature of a crystallization                kettle to an experimental value, then passing                carbon dioxide into the crystallization kettle and                pressurize it to the set experimental value; (iii)                continuously feeding carbon dioxide, keeping the                temperature and pressure in the crystallization                kettle unchanged, and feeding the composite                solution into crystallization kettle; and (iv)                after finishing the composite solution,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide, adjusting                the carbon dioxide flow rate by the rotor flow                meter to maintain in a specific flow rate, at last,                releasing pressure to the standard atmospheric                pressure, opening the crystallizing kettle, and                collecting superfine particles, where the geraniol                derivative is                7-O-farnesenyl-3'-O-methylgeraniol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultrafine                particles of 7-O-farnesenyl-3'-O-methylgeraniol by                applying supercritical fluid enhanced solution                dispersion technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obtain                7-O-farnesenyl-3'-O-methylgeraniol with smaller                particle size, more uniform distribution and                improved bioavailability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature in the                  crystallizing kettle is 36 degrees C; and the                  pressure is 12 MPa. The volume flow of the                  compound solution is 1.4 ml/minute. The flow rate                  of carbon dioxide is 3.0-3.5 l/minute. Preferred                  Components: In the step (i) is an                  ethanol-dimethyl sulfoxide mixed solvent with a                  volume ratio of 20:1. The mass concentration of                  the composite solution is 10 mg/ml.

   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst MXene/carbon nanotubes                improves catalytic activity of catalyst, realizes                composite cathode of heterogeneous catalyst which                is in applied voltage drives lower                electro-catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing composite catalyst                MXene/carbon nanotubes, which involves dissolving                MXene in deionized water at room temperature, and                dispersing carbon nanotubes (CNTs) in ethanol,                mixing aqueous solution of MXene and ethanol                solution of CNTs, stirring at room temperature for                12-13 hours, centrifuging, and drying in vacuum to                obtain composite catalyst MXene/carbon nanotubes;                and
   (2) a method for preparing heterogeneous                catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide, which involves dispersing composite                catalyst MXene/CNTs in ethanol, dripping uniformly                onto surface of the polished glassy carbon                electrode GC, drying glassy carbon electrode GC in                oven for 1-2 hours to obtain MXene/CNTs modified                electrode, using MXene/CNTs modified electrode as                working electrode, platinum wire or platinum mesh                as counter electrode, silver/silver chloride                electrode as reference electrode, electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN US10131602-B1
TI Forming methanol, comprises decomposing mixture of indium nitrate hydrate and template agent to templated indium-oxide nanoparticles and irradiating mixture of e.g. carbon dioxide and photocatalyst with UV light to reduce carbon dioxide.
AU GONDAL M A
   DASTAGEER M A
   BAIG U
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2018906986
AB    NOVELTY - Forming methanol, comprises thermally decomposing a mixture of indium(III) nitrate hydrate and a template agent to form templated indium-oxide nanoparticles and then irradiating a mixture comprising water, carbon dioxide and a photocatalyst with UV light to reduce the carbon dioxide thus forming methanol, where the photocatalyst comprises the templated indium-oxide nanoparticles having a specific pore volume of 0.08-0.15 cm3/g.
   USE - The method is useful for forming methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not involve injecting hydrogen gas into the mixture due to the protons that are formed as a result of splitting water molecules may participate in reduction reactions of carbon dioxide; forms methanol at standard temperate and pressure without using an external heating source to elevate the temperature of the mixture, thus reducing the energy per one mole of methanol formed by at least 50%, preferably at least 90% as compared to conventional methanol production processes; and reduces the cost of producing methanol by at least 50%, preferably at least 90% as compared to the cost of producing methanol in the conventional methanol production processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst further comprises non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles, where a weight ratio of the non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles to the templated indium-oxide nanoparticles is 10:1-1:10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixture of the indium(III) nitrate hydrate and templating agent is thermally decomposed at a temperature of 600 degrees C. The irradiating is carried out without added hydrogen gas. The method further comprises injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture at a pressure of 10-80 psi during the irradiating. The UV light has a wavelength of 150-300 nm. The UV light is in a form of a single-frequency laser beam with a wavelength of 150-300 nm. A methanol yield is 400-500 mu mol/hour/g of the photocatalyst. A conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide to methanol is 30-45% by mole relative to a quantity of carbon dioxide, and a quantum efficiency of forming methanol is 1-4% by mole relative to a quantity of photons absorbed.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN JP2019123678-A; JP7052360-B2
TI Apparatus for producing methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, has flow path formation unit that forms flow path for transferring slurry from second reactor to first reactor, and pump which circulates slurry between reactors.
AU SAYAMA S
   ITO Y
   SAKAI M
   IMAGAWA H
   BABA N
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 201964652N
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a first reactor (10) and a second reactor (20), which accommodate slurry containing a catalyst which has occlusion and reduction performance of carbon dioxide. A source gas supply unit (15) supplies a source gas containing carbon dioxide to the first reactor. A reducing gas supply unit (25) supplies reducing gas containing hydrogen to the second reactor. A first flow path formation unit (13) forms a first flow path for transferring slurry from first reactor to second reactor. A second flow path formation unit (23) forms a second flow path for transferring slurry from second reactor to first reactor. A pump (30) circulates the slurry between first and second reactors.
   USE - Apparatus for producing methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The system configuration is simplified and the amount of methane produced is made steady IN the production of methane using catalyst having the ability to store and reduce carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The apparatus has a first concentration acquisition unit which acquires a carbon dioxide levels in the source gas. A first flow volume acquisition unit acquires the flow volume of the source gas. A second concentration acquisition unit acquires the carbon dioxide levels in the exhaust gas discharged from the first reactor. A second flow volume acquisition unit acquires a flow volume of the exhaust gas discharged from the first reactor. The pump responds according to a portion of each acquired value by first concentration acquisition unit, first flow volume acquisition unit, second concentration acquisition unit and second flow volume acquisition unit. A control unit controls one of the reducing gas from the reducing gas supply unit. The control unit includes a carbon dioxide levels in the source gas acquired by the first concentration acquisition unit, and changes the flow volume of pump in inverse proportion to both the flow volume of source gas acquired by the first flow volume acquisition unit. The control unit includes carbon dioxide levels in the source gas acquired by the first concentration acquisition unit, and carbon dioxide levels in the exhaust gas acquired by the second concentration acquisition unit from the amount of carbon dioxide in the source gas determined from the flow rate of source gas acquired by the first flow rate acquisition unit. The amount of carbon dioxide recovered in the first reactor is calculated by reducing the amount of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas determined from flow volume of exhaust gas acquired by the second flow volume acquisition unit. The supply amount of reducing gas from reducing gas supply unit is changed in proportion to both the obtained carbon dioxide recovery amount and the flow volume of pump. A third reactor is provided on first flow path and accommodates the slurry. A fourth reactor is provided on the second flow path and accommodates the slurry. A purge gas supply unit supplies a purge gas to one of third reactor and fourth reactor. A first gas-liquid separator is provided on the first flow path. A second gas-liquid separator is provided on the second flow path. The catalyst contains one of alkali metal which forms carbonate, and occludes carbon dioxide, alkaline-earth metal, and the metal which has methanation catalyst performance. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing methane involves supplying the source gas containing carbon dioxide to a first reactor accommodating slurry containing a catalyst which has occlusion and reduction performance of carbon dioxide. A reducing gas containing hydrogen is supplied to the second reactor containing the slurry. The slurry is circulated between first and second reactors.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for producing methane. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Apparatus for producing methane (1)
   First and second reactors (10,20)
   First and second flow path formation units (13,23)
   Source gas supply unit (15)
   Reducing gas supply unit (25)
   Slurry pump (30)

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has: a biogas treatment process                (510) configured to receive a biogas as an input                and to output a carbon dioxide stream; a sensor                configured to generate a signal corresponding to                the pH level in the water treatment system (500);                and a controller configured to receive the signal                from the sensor and to control injection of the                carbon dioxide stream into the water treatment                system as a function of the signal. The controller                is configured to control injection of the carbon                dioxide stream by regulating a flow rate of the                carbon dioxide stream into water to be treated. The                apparatus further comprises: injection devices                located at different depths within a basin of water                to be treated, where the controller is configured                to control each of the injection devices to control                injection of the carbon dioxide stream; and a                carbon dioxide aeration valve, where the controller                is configured to control the aeration valve to                adjust a level of carbon dioxide in the carbon                dioxide stream.
   USE - The apparatus is used for adjusting a pH level                in a water treatment system. The system is used for                regulating a pH level in a water treatment process                (claimed), for the storage infrastructure, or for                training personnel in handling of these acidic                chemicals. The system is used to treat other gas                mixtures in which the relative solubility of one                gas in the mixture is substantially higher than the                other gas in the mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The system and the method adjust the pH level                of a water treatment process resulting in a more                efficient disinfection process, and adjust the pH                level of the water treatment process without                requiring the dedicated storage and handling of                acidic chemicals. The system regulates or reduces                the pH level of water being treated without                requiring the costs for purchasing the chemical,                for the storage infrastructure, or for training                personnel in handling of these acidic chemicals.                The apparatus or the method is desirable to recycle                and reuse the water in which the carbon dioxide was                dissolved, and provides a super-saturated carbon                dioxide water stream from the biogas treatment                process, and minimizes the amount of sodium                bisulfite or sulfur dioxide required to neutralize                residual chlorine. The adjustment in the pH level                allows for approximately 25% reduction in the                amount of chlorine required to achieve the desired                disinfection due to increased levels of                hypochlorous acid present. The reduction in the pH                level by using the carbon dioxide water stream can                result in significant annual cost savings for                disinfection chemicals used in the water treatment                plant operations. The apparatus or the method                provides more effective disinfection of the                water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for adjusting a pH level in a water                treatment system; and
   a system for regulating a pH level in a water                treatment process
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram                representation of a water treatment system with                integrated biogas treatment and carbon dioxide                enhanced disinfection, of an apparatus for                adjusting a pH level in a water treatment                system.

TI Recovery of helium from feed gas mixture involves            subjecting feed gas mixture to membrane separation,            carbon dioxide removal and pressure swing adsorption,            and recycling portion of tail gas to position between            membrane separations.
AU SALAZAR DUARTE G
   BAUER M
   PLEINTINGER S
   KRAMER V
   JOHANNINK M
   SALAZAR D G
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202119233K
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of helium from a feed gas mixture                comprising helium, carbon dioxide and methane or                nitrogen involves subjecting the feed gas mixture                to a membrane separation (11) forming a retentate                (R1) and a permeate (P1), subjecting the permeate                (P1) to a carbon dioxide removal forming a gas                mixture depleted in carbon dioxide, subjecting the                gas mixture depleted in carbon dioxide to the                membrane separation (14) forming a retentate (R2)                and a permeate (P2), subjecting at least a portion                of the permeate (P2) to the pressure swing                adsorption forming a tail gas and the helium                product, and recycling at least a portion of the                tail gas to a position between the membrane                separation (11) and the membrane separation (14) in                the separation sequence (10).
   USE - Recovery of helium from a feed gas mixture                comprising helium, carbon dioxide and methane or                nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient recovery of                helium from feed gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of helium from a feed gas mixture                comprising helium, carbon dioxide and methane or                nitrogen involves subjecting the feed gas mixture                to a separation sequence including a membrane based                separation and an adsorption based separation                forming a helium product where the membrane based                separation is performed using a membrane separation                (11) and a membrane separation (14), and the                adsorption based separation is performed using a                pressure swing adsorption that at least a portion                of the feed gas mixture is subjected to the                membrane separation (11) forming a retentate (R1)                and a permeate (P1), subjecting the permeate (P1)                to a carbon dioxide removal (13) forming a gas                mixture depleted in carbon dioxide, subjecting the                gas mixture depleted in carbon dioxide to the                membrane separation (14) forming a retentate (R2)                and a permeate (P2), subjecting at least a portion                of the permeate (P2) to the pressure swing                adsorption forming a tail gas and the helium                product, and recycling at least a portion of the                tail gas to a position between the membrane                separation (11) and the membrane separation (14) in                the separation sequence. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for helium recovering system (100), which                comprises a unit for subjecting at least a portion                of the feed gas mixture to a separation sequence                including a membrane based separation and an                adsorption based separation forming a helium                product, unit for subjecting at least a portion of                the feed gas mixture to the membrane separation                (11) and to form a retentate (R1) and a permeate                (P1), unit for subjecting a portion of the permeate                (P1) to a carbon dioxide removal forming as gas                mixture depleted in carbon dioxide, unit for                subjecting at least a portion of the gas mixture                depleted in carbon dioxide to the membrane                separation (14) and to form a retentate (R2) and a                permeate (P2), unit for subjecting at least a                portion of the permeate (P2) to the pressure swing                adsorption and to form a tail gas and the helium                product, and a unit for recycling a portion of the                tail gas at a position between the membrane                separation (11) and the membrane separation (14) in                the separation sequence.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of helium                recovering system.
   Pretreatment (1)
   Separation sequence (10)
   Membrane separation (11, 14)
   Compression (12, 15)
   Carbon dioxide removal (13)
   System (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The feed gas mixture is                  obtained by submitting a starting gas mixture to                  a pretreatment (1) which involves removal of                  particles, a removal of liquids, a removal of                  heavy hydrocarbons, a removal of water, a removal                  of trace components including mercury, sulfur                  components and/or other trace components. The                  method further involves forming a further gas                  product poor in helium using at least a portion                  of the retentate (R1) and/or the retentate (R2).                  The method further involves subjecting the                  permeate (P1) or the portion thereof being                  subjected to the carbon dioxide removal to a                  compression (12) and/or the gas mixture depleted                  in carbon dioxide is or the portion thereof                  subjected to the membrane separation (14) to a                  compression (12) and/or the permeate (P2) or the                  portion thereof being subjected to the pressure                  swing adsorption to a compression (15). The                  method further involves combining tail gas or the                  portion thereof being recycled with the permeate                  (P1) or with the portion thereof being subjected                  to the carbon dioxide removal is also subjected                  to the compression (12) after being combined with                  the permeate (P1) or with the portion thereof                  being subjected to the carbon dioxide removal,                  subjecting the tail gas or the portion thereof                  being recycled to a further compression separated                  from the compression (12) and then combining with                  the permeate (P1) or with the portion being                  subjected to the carbon dioxide. The method                  involves subjecting helium product or gas product                  to a compression and/or a liquefaction. Preferred                  Components: The starting gas mixture or the feed                  gas mixture is natural gas or product of natural                  gas. Preferred Composition: The feed gas mixture                  contains 0.1-5 mol% helium and/or 0.5-60 mol% or                  at least 50 mol% methane and/or nitrogen. The                  retentate (R1) and (R2) contain 200 ppm or less                  helium. The permeate (P2) contains 20-40 mol.%                  helium. The helium product contains 90-99.9999                  mol% helium.

TI Carbon dioxide gas-soluble foaming agent useful            for dense storage layer, comprises foaming agent foam            stabilizer agent, foam accelerant, liquid film            enhancer, alkaline substance, cosolvent, and            water.
AU GUO Q
   DING H
   WU J
   NIU R
   JIANG Y
   LIU L
   ZHAO F
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 202256524M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas-soluble foaming agent                comprises 0.03-6.5% foaming agent 0.01-5% foam                stabilizer, 0.01-5% foam accelerant, 0.01-5% liquid                film enhancer, 0.01-3% alkaline substance, 0.01-10%                cosolvent, and water (remaining amount). The                foaming agent is anionic surfactants, nonionic                surfactants and/or amphoteric surfactants.
   USE - The carbon dioxide gas-soluble foaming agent                is useful for dense storage layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas-soluble foaming agent                has good solubility in supercritical carbon                dioxide, good thermal stability, high salinity                resistance and temperature resistance, long foaming                half-life time, and helps advance the development                of CCUS technology in the petroleum industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anionic                  surfactants are sodium succinate sulfonate,                  dioctyl succinate sodium sulfonate, fatty alcohol                  sodium isethionate, sodium cocoyl methyl taurine,                  sodium n-lauroyl sarcosinate, sodium lauryl                  alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, sodium                  lauryl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether carboxylate,                  and/or disodium lauryl polyoxyethylene ether                  sulfosuccinate. The nonionic surfactant is                  polyethylene glycol trimethyl nonyl ether,                  tetraethylene glycol monolauryl ether,                  tetraethylene glycol monododecyl ether,                  nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, octylphenyl                  polyoxyethylene ether, alkylphenol ether                  sulfosuccinate, ethylhexanol polyoxypropylene                  polyoxyethylene ether, lauryl polyoxyethylene                  polyoxypropylene ether and/or coconut fatty acid                  monoethanolamide. The amphoteric surfactant is                  dodecyl hydroxypropyl sulfobetaine, tetradecyl                  hydroxypropyl phosphate betaine, cetostearyl                  dimethyl hydroxypropyl sulfobetaine,                  lauramidopropyl betaine, and/or lauramidopropyl                  amine oxide. The foam stabilizer is carboxymethyl                  cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose, dimethyl                  carbonate, propylene carbonate, isocyanate and/or                  polyacrylamide. The foam accelerator is                  diethanolamine, triethanolamine,                  methyldiethanolamine, N,N-diethylethanolamine,                  diethyleneglycolamine, formamide, acetamide,                  lauric acid diethanolamide and/or coconut oleic                  acid. The liquid film enhancer is nano-titanium                  dioxide, nano-zirconia, nano aluminum oxide, nano                  zinc oxide, nano magnesium silicate,                  nano-aluminosilicate, nano-clay, nano-red earth,                  nano-petroleum coke powder, bentonite particles                  and/or surface-modified nano-silica. The alkaline                  substance is sodium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate,                  sodium borate, dimethylamine, butylamine and/or                  aniline. The cosolvent is ethanol, ethylene                  glycol, glycerol, hexylene glycol and/or                  polyether triol.

TI Synthesizing 2, 5-furandicarboxylic acid by furan carboxylic acid and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting furan formic acid, inorganic alkali and solvent under condition of carbon dioxide to obtain 2, 5-furandimethyl salt and processing.
AU WANG J
   LIU J
   WANG R
   LI Y
   ZHOU G
AE JILIN ZHONGKE POLYMER ENG PLASTIC CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2021B7438H
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2, 5-furandicarboxylic acid by furan carboxylic acid and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting furan formic acid, inorganic alkali and solvent under the condition of carbon dioxide to obtain 2, 5-furandimethyl salt, and processing to obtain 2, 5-furandicarboxylic acid. The used solvent is non-protonic compound.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing 2, 5-furandicarboxylic acid by furan carboxylic acid and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the conversion rate of furan formic acid, increases the yield of 2,5 furan dicarboxylic acid, and avoids the generation of impurities, increases the solubility in the solvent, and shortens the reaction time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises diphenyl ether, sulfolane, dimethyl sulfone, biphenyl, naphthaline, xylene, trimethylbenzene, N-Methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), dimethylacetamide (DMAc) or hexamethylphosphoramide (HMPT). The mass ratio of the furan formic acid to the solvent is (1-99):(99-1). The inorganic alkali comprises potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, caesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, sodium acetate, potassium acetate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate, potassium tert-butoxide or sodium tert-butoxide. The mol ratio of the furanformic acid to the inorganic alkali is 1: (0.1-50). The acid used in the acidification treatment comprises hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid, phosphoric acid and/or dilute nitric acid. The reaction can use 2-furanformate to replace the furan formic acid. The furanformate can be potassium salt or sodium salt, or the mixed salt. The alkaline compound comprises potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate and/or sodium carbonate.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 200-265degrees Celsius, the reaction time is 0.1-24 hours, and the reaction pressure is 0.1-25 MPa. The specific steps of the post-treatment are: cooling the reaction system, filtering and separating solvent and 2, 5-furanphthalate, and recycling solvent, adding water to dissolve the 2, 5-furandicarboxylic acid salt, removing insoluble substance in the reaction system, acidizing and adding acid until the pH is less than or equal to3, fully separating the solid and filtering. The method comprises further comprising a post-treatment step after acidifying, where the post-treatment comprises separating, drying and re-crystallizing.

TI Synthesizing organic matter with carbon dioxide and water as raw materials comprises adding an aqueous inorganic alkali solution and consuming carbon dioxide or carbonate raw materials.
AU LI J
   GU S
   WANG X
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 202084766V
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing organic matter with carbon dioxide and water as raw materials comprises adding an aqueous inorganic alkali solution and consuming carbon dioxide or carbonate raw materials, where the organic matter is cyclopentadiene and cyclohexanediol organic matter including dicyclopentadiene and dicyclopentadiene polymer and cyclopentene and cyclopentane, tetrahydrodicyclopentadiene, adamantine, cyclohexadiene, cyclohexanol, cyclohexane and ethylene glycol and propylene glycol and the inorganic base includes alkali metal hydroxides e.g. potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide or lithium hydroxide.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing organic matter with carbon dioxide and water as raw materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces production and costs by more than two times; and improves the safety factor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises selecting a stirred reactor, connecting a cyclopentadiene rectification tower to the gas outlet of the stirred reactor and configuring a controllable gas or solid feeder and a water feeder, sealing cyclopentadiene the outlet of the condenser of the olefin rectification tower with an oil seal to isolate carbon dioxide in the air in the reactor, placing calculated amount of ordinary metal powder and calculated amount of inorganic alkali aqueous solution with a mass concentration of 65% placing into the reactor and controlling the reaction to 180-220 degrees C, adding carbon dioxide gas or carbonate solids are continuously in a metered manner, and water is continuously added in a metered manner, outlet temperature of the cyclopentadiene rectification tower is controlled to be about 41-44 degrees C for 24 hours or continuous reaction for 100 days, flowing the mixed liquid of cyclopentadiene and cyclopentene out from the discharge port of the condenser of the rectification column and passing to the dimerization process, polymerizing the cyclopentadiene in the mixed liquid into dicyclopentadiene product, content is 98% and the separating cyclopentene product, the products left in the reactor are cyclopentane and cyclohexanediol and cyclohexadiene and cyclohexane and cyclohexane Hexanol and dicyclopentadiene polymers and tetrahydrodicyclopentadiene, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol and adamantane and other organic products, the metal powder represents all simple metals e.g. zinc, aluminum, iron, copper, thunder nickel catalyst, inorganic base means all alkali metal hydroxides e.g. potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide or lithium hydroxide. Preferred Components: The metal e.g. zinc, aluminum, iron or copper and also means raney nickel catalyst. The inorganic bases to all alkali metal hydroxides e.g. potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, or lithium hydroxide.

TI Processing supercritical carbon dioxide hydrogenation based on amorphous alloy, comprises e.g. mixing the solvent and organic amine in the reaction kettle to form a solution, and selecting the amorphous alloy as catalyst and adding hydrogen.
AU LI H
   ZHU J
   HE J
   QIN L
   JIAO W
   CHEN W
AE UNIV SHANGHAI NORMAL (USNO-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
   UNIV SHANGHAI NORMAL (USNO-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 202041401C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing supercritical carbon dioxide hydrogenation based on amorphous alloy, comprises mixing the solvent and organic amine in the reaction kettle to form a solution, selecting the amorphous alloy as the catalyst, adding hydrogen and carbon dioxide at low temperature, raising the temperature until the partial pressure of carbon dioxide reaches a supercritical state, and carrying out the reaction to obtain product formic acid and alkane compound.
   USE - The method is useful for processing supercritical carbon dioxide hydrogenation based on amorphous alloy/.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and saves hydrogen energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Preferred Components: The solvent is dimethylformamide , cyclohexane, acetonitrile, ethanol, and water; preferably, the solvent is dimethylformamide. The amount of the solvent is 2-40 vol.% of the volume of the reactor, preferably, the amount of the solvent is 20 nol.% of the volume of the reactor. The organic amine is triethylamine and/or triethanolamine, preferably, triethylamine. The volume ratio of the organic amine and solvent is 0.1-0.5: 1.0; preferably, the volume ratio of the organic amine and solvent is 0.25: 1.0. The amorphous alloy is an amorphous alloy formed by a group VIII metal element and boron and/ or phosphorus element, preferably, the amorphous alloy is a nickel-boron amorphous alloy. The mass volume ratio of amorphous alloy and solution is 0.01-1.0 g/ml, preferably, the mass volume ratio of the amorphous alloy and solution is 0.08 g/ml The after adding hydrogen and carbon dioxide, the hydrogen and carbon ratio is 0.1-6: 1.0, and the total pressure is 0.1-10MPa, preferably, after adding hydrogen and carbon dioxide, the hydrogen-to-carbon ratio is 0.1-0.3: 1.0, and the total pressure is 0.1-9MPa. The temperature rising temperature is at 20-100 degrees C, preferably, the temperature rising temperature is at 40 degrees C. The reaction time is 1-10 hours, the preferred reaction time is 4 hours.

   USE - Used as carbon dioxide absorbing material.

The second precursor is calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, calcium carbonate, basic calcium carbonate, calcium nitrate or calcium carboxylate. AE BAOSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2020157613
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire comprises 0.05-0.1 %mass carbon, 0.3-0.8 %mass manganese, 0.06-0.2 %mass titanium, 0.4-0.8 %mass rare earth ferrosilicon alloy, 0.008-0.015 %mass boron, 0.3-0.8 %mass nickel, 0.05-0.15 %mass molybdenum, balance iron and unavoidable impurities.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire for ultra-low heat input automatic welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire improves the toughness of weld metal formed by welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire preferably comprises 0.05-0.07 %mass carbon, 0.8-1.0 %mass silicon, 0.6-0.8 %mass manganese, 0.15-0.2 %mass titanium, 0.4-0.6 %mass rare earth ferrosilicon alloy, 0.010-0.015 %mass boron, 0.6-0.8 %mass nickel and 0.06-0.09 %mass molybdenum. Preferred Properties: The heat input to the carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire is not more than 0.25 kJ/mm. The microstructure of the weld metal obtained by carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire does not contain martensite structure and clustered martensite-austenite components. The weld metal has yield strength of 530-620 MPa and tensile strength of 600-720 MPa. Preferred Component: The main component of weld metal is ferrite bainite.
