PN CN109810213-A; CN109810213-B
TI Preparing fluoropolymer in water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixed system comprises stirring fluorine-containing vinyl monomer and initiator under water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixing system, and then subjecting for polymerization.
AU WAN X
   HE X
   ZHENG B
   LIU M
   CHEN J
   PENG Z
   WU C
   LI Y
AE RUYUAN HEC FLUORORESIN CO LTD (RUYU-Non-standard)
GA 2019488057
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluoropolymer in water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixed system comprises stirring fluorine-containing vinyl monomer and initiator under water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixing system, then subjecting for polymerization to obtain fluoropolymer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fluoropolymer in water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixed system.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluoropolymer can be separated from the aqueous phase, and has high purity, simple post treatment, small yellowing, low melt index and easy processing, and the molecular weight distribution coefficient (PDI) is usually between 1.5 and 2.5 less than the various methods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fluoropolymer obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fluoropolymer has molecular weight distribution coefficient PDI of 1.5-2.5. The mass ratio between water and carbon dioxide in the water/supercritical carbon dioxide mixed system is 1-25:1. The water-soluble initiator is inorganic peroxide, oxidation-reduction initiation system, water-soluble azo initiator and/or fluorine-containing initiator. The fluorine-containing vinyl monomer is fluoro-olefin monomer, fluoro-acrylate monomer or fluorinated vinyl ether monomer; and initiator is water-soluble initiator. The chain transfer agent is trichlorofluoromethane, 1,2-difluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrachloroethane, 1,1,2-trifluoro-1,2,2-trichloroethane and/or trichlorofluoromethane. The addition amount of chain transfer agent is 0.05-5 wt.% the amount of fluorine-containing vinyl monomer. Preferred Method: The pressure of the polymerization reaction is 7.39-15 MPa, and the temperature of the polymerization reaction is 31.5-95 degrees C. The step of adding chain transfer agent is also included in the polymerization reaction.

TI Judging and treating anaerobic granular sludge poisoning by connecting outlet of anaerobic reactor sewage treatment system to carbon dioxide detector, detecting carbon dioxide generation, discharging sludge, and adding inorganic salts.
AU ZHAN Z
   FANG M
   CHEN X
   GAO J
   SONG J
   QU X
AE SHANYING INT HOLDING CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201945186S
AB    NOVELTY - Judging and treating anaerobic granular sludge poisoning comprises connecting outlet of anaerobic reactor sewage treatment system to carbon dioxide detector, and detecting carbon dioxide generation amount; when carbon dioxide flow rate is unstable and pH value of the reactor outlet changes, discharging poisoned and rancid sludge from sludge discharge valve at bottom of reactor; stopping water intake and introducing normal granular sludge; controlling amount of water inflow and strengthening circulation cycle; controlling rising flow rate in reactor; adding sludge granulation accelerator to anaerobic reactor; adding inorganic salts to reactor when chemical oxygen demand removal rate is not less than 80%; and detecting methane output, and achieving normal operating conditions and completing process.
   USE - The method is used for judging and treating anaerobic granular sludge poisoning.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts combined detection method of carbon dioxide and pH value, and timely treats poisoned sludge when carbon dioxide and pH value change, and adds sludge granulation accelerator and inorganic salt after adding normal granular sludge to increase activity of sludge particles. The process improves methanogenic activity of biological particles, shortens granulation process, shortens start-up time of anaerobic reactor, and reduces amount of anaerobic sludge.

An insertion unit inserts new particulate process chemicals into the drum. A removing unit removes spent or old particulate process chemicals from the drum. PT P
PN KR2018015012-A; KR2073286-B1
TI Adsorbent useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide in            various industries, comprises enhancer comprising            eutectic mixture, and magnesium oxide.
AU HWANG S H
   LEE H Y
   SEO J G
   LEE H
AE UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACAD COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
GA 2018138683
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent comprises enhancer comprising                eutectic mixture, and magnesium oxide.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide (claimed) in various industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has excellent carbon dioxide                adsorption performance in middle temperature range,                can control adsorption and desorption start                temperatures of adsorbent by controlling eutectic                temperature and concentration of carbon dioxide in                the eutectic mixture, and provides information i.e.                composition, composition ratio, and content of the                eutectic mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing adsorbent, comprising (a)                preparing magnesium oxide by primary plasticizing                magnesium precursor, (b) mixing at least two alkali                nitrates and pulverizing to prepare eutectic                mixture, (c) heating the eutectic mixture at                temperature above the eutectic temperature to                homogenize the eutectic mixture, (d) cooling and                re-pulverizing the eutectic mixture to obtain                eutectic mixture powder, (e) mixing the eutectic                mixture powder with the magnesium oxide, and (f)                secondly plasticizing the magnesium oxide mixed                with eutectic mixture powder;
   (2) adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprising (i)                heating the adsorbent above the eutectic                temperature of the eutectic mixture to induce                melting of the eutectic mixture, and (ii)                introducing carbon dioxide gas into the adsorbent                in which the eutectic mixture is molten; and
   (3) regenerating adsorbent, comprising heating                carbon dioxide absorbed adsorbent at 450-600                degrees C for 5-20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent                  comprises either: 20-45 wt.% eutectic mixture; or                  30-42.5% eutectic mixture. Preferred Components:                  The eutectic mixture is mixture of at least two                  alkali nitrates including sodium nitrate, lithium                  nitrate and potassium nitrate, preferably mixture                  of potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate in molar                  ratio of 1:1-1:4. The magnesium precursor is                  magnesium hydroxide, magnesium nitrate, and                  magnesium acetate. Preferred Method: The primary                  plasticizing is carried out by heating at 600-900                  degrees C for 4-10 hours under heating rate of                  3-10 degrees C/minute. The homogenization step is                  carried out by heating at 80-150 degrees C higher                  than the eutectic temperature of the eutectic                  mixture for 8-16 hours. The secondary                  plasticizing is carried out at 300-500 degrees C                  for 4-8 hours under heating rate of 1-5 degrees                  C/minute in presence of nitrogen gas. In the                  regeneration method, heating is carried out under                  nitrogen atmosphere.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113214127-A
TI Efficiently removing peculiar smell of astaxanthin by supercritical technology comprises e.g. analyzing peculiar smell substances of astaxanthin oil, deodorizing, pressurizing, heating, carrying out extraction reaction and stirring.
AU ZHAO Z
   YANG F
AE KUNMING BIOGENIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 202193843D
AB    NOVELTY - Efficiently removing peculiar smell of astaxanthin by supercritical technology comprises e.g. (i) analyzing peculiar smell substances of astaxanthin oil by solid phase micro-extraction, (ii) using supercritical deodorization technology for deodorizing, adding astaxanthin oil to be deodorized into supercritical device, pressurizing by pressurizing unit of device and heating by microwave heating device, (iii) passing supercritical carbon dioxide solvent for carrying out extraction reaction and continuously stirring by ultrasonic generator during extraction reaction, and (iv) entering peculiar smell substances into separating groove together with supercritical carbon dioxide solvent under action of extraction pump, raising temperature of separation groove, reducing density of supercritical carbon dioxide solvents, forcing peculiar smell substances to separate from supercritical carbon dioxide solvent, and collecting and concentrating peculiar smell substances.
   USE - The method is useful for efficiently removing the peculiar smell of astaxanthin by supercritical technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has satisfactory effects; greatly improves the effect of removing the odor of astaxanthin; solves the single peculiar smell removal measures of the existing supercritical technology; and does not cause damage to astaxanthin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Efficiently removing peculiar smell of astaxanthin by supercritical technology comprises (i) analyzing peculiar smell substances of astaxanthin oil by solid phase micro-extraction, (ii) using supercritical deodorization technology for deodorizing based on type of odor substance, adding astaxanthin oil to be deodorized into supercritical device, pressurizing by pressurizing unit of device, and heating by microwave heating device, (iii) passing supercritical carbon dioxide solvent for carrying out extraction reaction, and continuously stirring by ultrasonic generator during extraction reaction, (iv) entering peculiar smell substances into separating groove together with supercritical carbon dioxide solvent under action of extraction pump, raising temperature of separation groove, reducing density of supercritical carbon dioxide solvents, forcing peculiar smell substances to separate from supercritical carbon dioxide solvent, collecting and concentrating peculiar smell substances for subsequent processing, cooling supercritical carbon dioxide solvent by cooler, exchanging heat by heat exchanger, and entering supercritical device to continue extracting peculiar smell substances, (v) processing deodorized astaxanthin oil into astaxanthin powder by drying device, and (vi) introducing astaxanthin powder into another supercritical device, pressurizing through pressurization unit of device, entering supercritical carbon dioxide gas, using supercritical carbon dioxide gas to bring out peculiar smell in astaxanthin powder, and harmlessly treating exhaust gas by subsequent treatment unit to obtain astaxanthin powder product without peculiar smell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the solid phase micro-extraction is specifically carried out by (a) introducing the astaxanthin oil in a penicillin bottle, heating and vaporizing the peculiar smell, (b) balancing the headspace composition of the penicillin bottle, (c) inserting an extraction head, (d) exposing the quartz filament of the extraction head to absorb gas components, (e) recovering the quartz filament into the extraction head, and pulling the extraction head out of the penicillin bottle, (f) inserting the extraction head into the gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS), exposing the quartz filament, starting GC-MS for analysis to obtain the unique algal fishy smell of Haematococcus plupenicillin bottleis, namely caproic aldehyde. In the step (ii), the heating of the astaxanthin oil to be deodorized is carried out at 40-45 degrees C via a microwave heating device. In the step (iii), the extraction reaction is carried out in first stage, a second stage and a third stage, where in the first stage, setting the supercritical extraction pressure under 15-20 Mpa for 15-30 minutes and supercritical carbon dioxide solvent flow to 20-25 l/hour, closing the supercritical device, and performing static processing, in the second stage, setting the supercritical extraction pressure under 20-30 Mpa for 60-80 minutes and supercritical carbon dioxide solvent flow to 25-30 l/hour, and opening supercritical device for dynamic processing, and in the third stage, setting the supercritical extraction pressure under 20-25 Mpa for 15-30 minutes, and the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent flow to 20-25 l/hour, and opening the supercritical device for dynamic processing. In the step (iv), the separating of the peculiar smell substance and the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent is carried out at 40-50 degrees C. In the step (vi), the supercritical extraction pressure is setting under 20-25 Mpa for 15-20 minute, and the flow rate of the supercritical carbon dioxide gas to 25-30 l/hour.

PN US10633313-B1
TI Maximization of gas streams in petrochemical industry, involves splitting carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen containing stream, forming carbon monoxide product stream, and introducing methanol-synthesis gas stream into reactor.
AU ROESCH A
   STROFFOLINO J T
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202034866V
AB    NOVELTY - Maximization of value of gas streams containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen involves splitting a carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen containing stream into a fraction (F1) and a fraction (F2), scrubbing at least a portion of the fraction (F1) in a carbon dioxide removal (104) system, introducing at least a portion of the scrubbed fraction (F1) into a temperature swing adsorption unit (106), separating at least a portion of the scrubbed and dried fraction (F1) to produce a carbon monoxide product stream and a hydrogen product stream, combining at least a portion of the hydrogen product stream and the fraction (F2) to produce a methanol-synthesis gas stream, and introducing at least a portion of the methanol-synthesis gas stream into a methanol synthesis reactor.
   USE - Maximization of value of gas streams containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen used in petrochemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Maximization of value of gas streams containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen involves splitting a carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen containing stream into a fraction (F1) and a fraction (F2), scrubbing at least a portion of the fraction (F1) in a carbon dioxide removal system to remove at least a portion of the carbon dioxide, introducing at least a portion of the scrubbed fraction (F1) into a temperature swing adsorption unit to further remove carbon dioxide and to remove water, separating at least a portion of the scrubbed and dried fraction (F1) to produce a carbon monoxide product stream and a hydrogen product stream, combining at least a portion of the hydrogen product stream and the fraction (F2) to produce a methanol-synthesis gas stream, and introducing at least a portion of the methanol-synthesis gas stream into a methanol synthesis reactor, in which at least a portion of a crude methanol stream, generated after a single pass through the methanol synthesis reactor, is withdrawn and used as a crude methanol product, and the methanol-synthesis gas stream is produced from a carbon monoxide and hydrogen containing stream without utilizing a water gas shift reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the process side and steam side.
   Feed gas stream (101)
   Carbon dioxide removal system (104)
   Temperature swing adsorption unit (106)
   Cryogenic separator (108)
   Carbon monoxide product stream (109)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fraction (F2) does not pass through a scrubber prior to combining with the fraction (F1). The carbon monoxide and hydrogen containing stream is not hydrogenated. The scrubbed and dried fraction (F1) is cryogenically separated to produce a carbon monoxide product stream (109) and a hydrogen product stream. At least a portion of the hydrogen rich stream is introduced into the temperature swing adsorption unit (106) during regeneration. The method further comprises a pressure swing adsorption unit downstream of the cryogenic separation, in which at least a portion of the hydrogen product stream is treated in the pressure swing adsorption unit to produce at least a portion of the hydrogen product stream. The pressure swing adsorption unit produces a tail gas stream, and in which at least a portion of the tail gas stream is used as a fuel gas product, a membrane separation unit downstream of the cryogenic separation, in which at least a portion of the hydrogen product stream is treated in the membrane separation unit to produce at least a portion of hydrogen product stream. The membrane separation unit produces a residual gas stream, and in which at least a portion of the residual gas stream is used as a fuel gas product. At least a portion of a crude methanol stream, generated after a single pass through the methanol synthesis reactor, is withdrawn and purified in a methanol distillation unit, and used as pure methanol product. Preferred Process: The method further involves combining a carbon dioxide-rich gas import stream with the fraction (F2), combining a hydrogen product gas import stream with the fraction (F2), combining a carbon monoxide-rich import gas stream with the fraction (F2), introducing medium pressure steam generated in the methanol synthesis reactor into a steam system and reducing the pressure of at least a portion of the steam in the steam system and introducing the reduced pressure steam into the methanol distillation unit as reboiling media, introducing medium pressure steam generated in the methanol synthesis reactor into a steam system, introducing at least a portion of the steam in the steam system into the temperature swing adsorption unit during a regeneration cycle, reducing the pressure of at least a portion of the steam in the steam system and introducing the reduced pressure steam into the carbon dioxide removal system as reboiling media, importing medium pressure steam into the steam system when an overall steam demand exceeds the medium pressure steam generated in the methanol synthesis reactor, and exporting medium pressure steam from the steam system when an overall steam demand is exceeded by the medium pressure steam generated in the methanol synthesis reactor.

PN US2020075981-A1; WO2020047199-A1; US10797332-B2; KR2021038667-A; CN112868120-A
TI Fuel cell system for removing carbon dioxide from anode exhaust gas of fuel cell, has anode gas oxidizer that is configured to receive and oxidize anode gas oxidizer input stream and portion of carbon dioxide-rich stream.
AU JAHNKE F C
   LAMBRECH M R
   LAMBRECH M A
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202019181C
AB    NOVELTY - The fuel cell system (100) has a fuel cell that having an anode outputs an anode exhaust gas (132) comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water. A shift reactor (110) receives a first portion (136) of the anode exhaust gas and to perform a water-gas shift reaction to produce an output stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide. An anode gas oxidizer (133) is provided. An absorption system comprising an absorber column (114) absorbs the carbon dioxide from the output stream in a solvent and to output resultant gas comprising hydrogen and a hydrocarbyl that is recycled to the anode. A stripper column (115) regenerates the solvent and to output a carbon dioxide-rich stream (118). The anode gas oxidizer receives and oxidizes an anode gas oxidizer input stream and a portion of the carbon dioxide-rich stream. The anode gas oxidizer input stream comprises one of a second portion (137) of the anode exhaust gas or a portion of the output stream from the shift reactor.
   USE - Fuel cell system for removing carbon dioxide from anode exhaust gas of fuel cell such as molten carbonate fuel cell (MCFC), solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) or other type of fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell system in which anode exhaust is recycled to improve the efficiency of a high temperature fuel cell and to increase the overall fuel utilization. The increased carbon dioxide helps to increase the removal or carbon from the recycle and export gas. The system for recovering more water, allows control of the anode pressure using the blower, and reduces the amount of water sent to the cathode, which increases the cell life. The anode exhaust is recycled to the fuel cell through the stream reduces the amount of fuel needed, thus increasing the efficiency of the high-temperature fuel cell system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for removing carbon dioxide from fuel cell anode exhaust gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the high temperature fuel cell system.
   Fuel cell system (100)
   Shift reactor (110)
   Absorber column (114)
   Stripper column (115)
   Carbon dioxide-rich stream (118)
   Anode exhaust gas (132)
   Anode gas oxidizer (133)
   First portion (136)
   Second portion (137)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The solvent comprises mixtures of dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol.

AU LIU Z
AE CHINA NAT COAL GROUP CORP (CNCO-C)

PN CN111850240-A
TI Smelting of austenitic stainless steel involves injecting molten iron into converter, adding ferrosilicon, blowing argon, analyzing content of alloy elements, and adjusting composition of silicon, manganese, chromium and nickel in steel.
AU CHEN X
   PAN J
   LI Z
   WU D
AE GANSU JIU STEEL GROUP HONGXING IRON & (JGHX-C)
GA 2020A9371E
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting of austenitic stainless steel by injecting carbon dioxide into an argon oxygen decarburization converter, involves injecting molten iron melted by the electric furnace with a temperature of 1450-1500 degrees C into the argon oxygen decarburization converter, adding 10-12 kg/ton of steel lime in the decarburization stage, entering the reduction stage, when the carbon content is less than 0.025%, controlling the reduction temperature to 1680-1720 degrees C, adding ferrosilicon and silicon-manganese alloy into the furnace for reduction, blowing argon at a flow rate of side-blown argon is 100-110 Nm3/minute, controlling the alkalinity of the slag in the converter at 2.2-2.5 by adding lime, desulfurizing, sampling and analyzing the content of alloy elements in the molten steel, adjusting the composition of silicon, manganese, chromium and nickel in the molten steel by adding ferrosilicon, silicon-manganese alloy, ferrochromium and metallic nickel, and maintaining the composition.
   USE - Smelting of austenitic stainless steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced the requirements of the converter for the silicon content and temperature in the pre-melted molten iron, Furthermore, reduced cost of steel smelting, and avoids the large amount of slag and molten steel pollution caused by the excessive addition of calcined dolomite and lime.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Smelting of austenitic stainless steel by injecting carbon dioxide into an argon oxygen decarburization converter, involves injecting molten iron melted by the electric furnace with a temperature of 1450-1500 degrees C into the argon oxygen decarburization converter, adding 10-12 kg/ton of steel lime in the decarburization stage, entering the reduction stage, when the carbon content is less than 0.025%, controlling the reduction temperature to 1680-1720 degrees C and basicity of slag at 1.8-2.0 and the flow of side blowing argon is 100-110 Nm3/minute, adding 18-20 kg/ton of molten steel ferrosilicon and 18-20 kg/ton of molten steel silicon-manganese alloy into the furnace for reduction for 5-6 minutes, pouring the slag to the greatest extent for 5-6 minutes, blowing argon at a flow rate of side-blown argon is 100-110 Nm3/minute, controlling the alkalinity of the slag in the converter at 2.2-2.5 by adding lime, desulfurizing for 5-6 minutes, sampling and analyzing the content of alloy elements in the molten steel, adjusting the composition of silicon, manganese, chromium and nickel in the molten steel by adding ferrosilicon, silicon-manganese alloy, ferrochromium and metallic nickel, and maintaining the composition comprising 0.03-0.045 %mass carbon, 0.30-0.50 %mass silicon, 1.00-1.30 %mass manganese, 0.043 %mass phosphorus, 0.002 %mass or less sulfur, 18.0-18.30 %mass chromium, 8.00-8.30 %mass nickel, 0.04-0.055 %mass nitrogen, balance iron and unavoidable impurities. The molten iron comprises 2.00-2.50 %mass carbon, 0.40-0.70 %mass silicon, 0.10-0.20 %mass manganese, 0.043 %mass phosphorus, 0.10 %mass or less sulfur, 8.0-9.0 %mass chromium, 8.00-8.30 %mass nickel, balance iron and unavoidable impurities. The decarburization is carried out at silicon aluminum oxidation period having top gun oxygen flow rate of 100-120 Nm3/minute, side gun oxygen flow rate of 100-110 Nm3/minute, nitrogen flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute, blowing time of 2-3 minute and adding 2-3 kg/ton of steel light burned dolomite, main decarburization period having top gun oxygen flow rate of 100-120 Nm3/minute, side gun oxygen flow rate of 100-110 Nm3/minute, nitrogen flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute and blowing time of 2-3 minute, dynamic decarburization period (D1) having top gun oxygen flow rate of 100-120 Nm3/minute, side gun oxygen flow rate of 100-110 Nm3/minute, nitrogen flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute and blowing time of 3-4 minute, dynamic decarburization period (D2) having side gun oxygen flow rate of 60-70 Nm3/minute, side gun nitrogen flow rate 10-20 of Nm3/minute, side gun carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-20 Nm3/minute, blowing time of 25-30 minute and adding 20-22 kg/ton of steel high carbon ferrochrome during the period, dynamic decarburization period (D3) having side gun oxygen flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute, side gun nitrogen flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute, side gun carbon dioxide flow rate of 30-40 Nm3/minute and blowing time of 8-10 minute, dynamic decarburization period (D4) having side gun oxygen flow rate of 20-30 Nm3/minute, side gun nitrogen flow rate of 40-50 Nm3/minute, side gun carbon dioxide flow rate of 40-50 Nm3/minute and blowing time of 6-8 minute, or dynamic decarburization period (D5) having side gun oxygen flow rate of 10-20 Nm3/minute, side gun nitrogen flow rate of 40-50 Nm3/minute, side gun carbon dioxide flow rate of 40-50 Nm3/minute and blowing time of 6-8 minute.

PN CN111248025-A
TI Cultivating Flammulina velutipes using cold storage comprises stirring cottonseed hull, corn cob, bran, rice sugar, wood chips, carbon, together to prepare a dry material for minutes, according to the material to water ratio.
AU LI X
AE GUIZHOU LVJIAN EDIBLE FUNGUS PLANTING (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202054676W
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Flammulina velutipes using cold storage comprises stirring 10 wt.% cottonseed hull, 34.5 wt.% corn cob, 15 wt.% bran, 30 wt.% rice sugar, 10 wt.% wood chips, 0.5 wt.% carbon, together to prepare a dry material for 30 minutes, according to the material to water ratio of 1:1.5, and stirring while adding water. The water is added, stirred for 60 minutes and loaded. The cultivation bottle is subjected to conventional high-pressure steam sterilization, and after reaching 0.15 MPa, it is kept for 3 hours and then cooled out of the pot, and inoculated when the material temperature drops below 22 degrees C. The inoculation process is performed aseptically, and the inoculation volume is based on the inoculation hole. The bottle temperature is preferably 20 degrees C, not higher than 22 degrees C, then after 25-30 days, until the mycelium can grow up to the full bottle, the ventilation is performed every morning and evening each time, for about 10 minutes each time.
   USE - Method for cultivating Flammulina velutipes using cold storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to cultivate Flammulina velutipes using cold storage, where the contents of temperature, humidity, and carbon dioxide in the cold storage are controllable, so that the production of Flammulina velutipes is high and the upward trend is good.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating Flammulina velutipes using cold storage comprises stirring 10 wt.% cottonseed hull, 34.5 wt.% corn cob, 15 wt.% bran, 30 wt.% rice sugar, 10 wt.% wood chips, 0.5 wt.% carbon, together to prepare a dry material for 30 minutes, according to the material to water ratio of 1:1.5, and stirring while adding water. The water is added, stirred for 60 minutes and loaded. The cultivation bottle is subjected to conventional high-pressure steam sterilization, and after reaching 0.15 MPa, it is kept for 3 hours and then cooled out of the pot, and inoculated when the material temperature drops below 22 degrees C. The inoculation process is performed aseptically, and the inoculation volume is based on the inoculation hole. The bottle temperature is preferably 20 degrees C, not higher than 22 degrees C, then after 25-30 days, until the mycelium can grow up to the full bottle, the ventilation is performed every morning and evening each time, for about 10 minutes each time. The periodic inspection of disinfection and timely disposal of contaminated bacteria bottles is performed. The mushroom production room must be disinfected, the site must be clean, and the room must be dark. The management measures for mushroom prevention, and after the hypha grows to 4/5 bottles, the fungus-free bottles is sent to the scrubbing room for scrubbing, and the time is 1-5 days. The room temperature is kept at 12-15 degrees C, with humidity above 95%, ventilate 2-3 times a day to make the air fresh and the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is maintained to 3500-4500 ml/m3. Then after raising seedlings for 4-6 days, amber drops of water will form on the upper portion of the medium, which is a precursor to the emergence of buds. The culture medium is contaminated with mixed bacteria. The contaminated bacteria bottle is taken out of the cultivation room in time. Then the temperature drops by 2 degrees C every day, morning and evening ventilation for 10 minutes on days 7-11, the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is kept at 3000-3500 ml/m3, and humidity is 85-90 wt.%. The rice grains of the cap are large, the growth of the single plant is inhibited and the growth of the single plant is suppressed, which promotes the whole plant to be neat and consistent. The air humidity is preferably 85%, the humidity is controlled at 4-6 degrees C, and the time is 5-7 days. This is the key period to increase the weight of the mushroom body and the quality of the product is improved. The indoor carbon dioxide concentration of 2000-2500 ml/m3 is kept. The contract is implemented, which can prevent the Flammulina velutipes from drooping and chaos, the oxygen supply is reduced to the mushrooms, the growth of the bacteria is inhibited, and the elongation of the stems are promoted. The sleeve is simplified. The humidity behind the sleeve is about 85%, the temperature is 6-8 degrees C, and the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is 50000-6000 ml/m3. The ventilation system is closed, the carbon dioxide concentration is kept above 800 ml/m3, the humidity is controlled at 80%-85%, the stem of Flammulina velutipes is 13-15 cm long, and the diameter of the cap is less than 1 cm, hemispherical, inwardly curled, when the umbrella opening is three minutes, it is the most suitable harvest period, and for immediate fresh sales, it is harvested when the mushroom cover is separated from the umbrella, and harvest time is 3-4 days.

TI Separating and purifying bovine serum albumin useful in medicine, comprises e.g. taking the blood of the bovine in a centrifuge tube, detaching the centrifuging tube in a sealed container and passing carbon dioxide into the centrifuge tube.
AU ZHANG C
AE HUAYUAN MEDICINE RES INST CO LTD (HUAY-Non-standard)
GA 201839331B
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying bovine serum albumin comprises taking the blood of the bovine in a centrifuge tube, detaching the centrifuging tube in a sealed container, passing the carbon dioxide into the centrifuge tube, sealing, placing the sealed container in a freezer, adjusting the temperature of the freezer, heating, taking out, adding 10% of the total mass of cattle blood to the centrifuge tube with 3.8% sodium citrate, passing carbon dioxide again, sealing, taking out centrifuge tube out of the sealed container, centrifuging and separating the blood cells to obtain plasma.
   USE - The method is useful in medicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the yield of plasma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and purifying bovine serum albumin comprises taking the blood of the bovine in a centrifuge tube, detaching the centrifuging tube in a sealed container, passing the carbon dioxide into the centrifuge tube, sealing, placing the sealed container in a freezer, adjusting the temperature of the freezer from -20 to -30 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 10 minutes, heating to 20 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 4 minutes, repeating for 3 times, taking out, adding 10% of the total mass of cattle blood to the centrifuge tube with 3.8% sodium citrate, passing carbon dioxide again, sealing, taking out centrifuge tube out of the sealed container, centrifuging, controlling the temperature to 2 degrees C and separating the blood cells to obtain plasma, where the centrifugal tube is an inverted circular platform, and the sealed container comprises: a sealing plug, which is detachably sealed and plugged at the opening of the top of the centrifugal tube. The sealing plug is recessed downwards to form a containing groove. The bottom surface of the accommodating groove penetrates through and forms the first perforation. The opening and closing mechanism includes a first guide tube fixed coaxially on the bottom surface of the receiving groove. The first elastic plug filled in a lower part of the first guide tube and a first fixed end of the first elastic plug. a spring, a first gland screwed onto the top end of the first spring, a second guide tube coaxially fixed on the top surface of the first gland. The second elastic plug filled in the lower part of the second guide tube a second spring fixed on the top surface of the second elastic plug, and a second gland screwed on the top of the second spring. The second perforation is provided through the top surface of the first pressurizing cover. The third through hole is penetrated through the top surface of the second pressure cover. The first elastic plug is located directly below the second through hole and a first elastic hole is vertically penetrated through the first elastic plug. The second elastic plug is located above the third through hole. The second elastic plug is provided with a second elastic hole. The first through hole, the second through hole, and the third through hole are the same distance from the central axis of the containing groove. The heating tube is detachably sleeved on the outside of the centrifugal tube to form a water storage chamber covering the outer wall of the centrifugal tube with the outer wall of the centrifugal tube. The annular rubber plug is arranged at the top of the water storage chamber to seal the water storage chamber. The fourth through hole is formed through the rubber plug. The fourth through hole is detachably connected to the air pipe and an end of the air pipe that is far from the fourth through hole is a rigid pipe body, and an upper portion of the outer wall of the heating pipe is opened hole. The feeding mechanism includes a circular table-shaped moving plug inserted on the through hole. The small-head-hinged movable rod is outside the heating tube. The sleeve is attached to the outside of the movable rod and the opening is fixed. The heating bag is located in a feeding bag on a circumferential side wall of the through hole, and a mixture of citric acid and sodium bicarbonate having a mass ratio of 1:3 is placed in the adding bag and covering the second gland and the first gland, rotating so that the first perforation, communicating the second perforation and the third perforation, where the gas conduit one end passes through the opening and closing mechanism in turn, communicating with the centrifuge tube, rotating the movable rod, adding the mixture in the filling bag into the water storage cavity, sealing the through hole, passing carbon dioxide into the centrifuge tube, extracting the end of the air tube and rotating the second gland and the first gland so that the first through hole, where the second through hole and the third through hole do not communicate to seal the centrifuge tube.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises placing the centrifuge tube in a sealed container, heating the device in the storage chamber to control the temperature of the water in the water storage chamber to 68-72 degrees C, adding the sodium octanoate with a plasma volume of 0.15-0.20% to the centrifuge tube, introducing carbon dioxide into the centrifuge tube through the vent tube for 10-12 minutes, adding 1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, adjusting the pH to 5-5.5, passing carbon dioxide for 5-6 minutes, adding 8 % ethanol with a plasma quality of 6%, passing carbon dioxide for 10-12 minutes, centrifuging, taking out the supernatant, placing the supernatant in an ultrafiltration machine, desalted using a filter membrane with a molecular weight of 18,000, concentrating to obtain a concentrate and lyophilizing the concentrate to obtain medicinal bovine serum albumin. The freeze-drying specifically comprises cooling the concentrate from room temperature to -40 degrees C at a cooling rate of 20 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 8 hours, heating to -25 degrees C at a heating rate of 20oC/minute, maintaining for 15-16 hours, heating to 0 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 1-2 hours and heating to 20 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The sealed container further comprises a vent pipe. The one end of the vent pipe is communicated and fixed to the lower end of the first perforation. The multiple of vent holes are circumferentially opened in the vent pipe, and the apertures of the multiple vent holes are near the first perforation, where the direction gradually becomes smaller. The sealing plug is recessed downward to form a receiving groove. The fifth through hole is formed through the bottom surface of the receiving groove and the receiving plug seals the receiving plug. The sixth perforation is formed through the top surface of the rubber plug. The fifth through hole and the sixth through hole communicate through the pipe body. The tube body is provided with a one-way valve with fluid flowing along the sixth perforation to the fifth perforation. The pipe body is provided with an exhaust fan between the sixth perforation and the one-way valve. In the step (ii), the exhaust fan works when carbon dioxide is introduced. The first guide tube and the second guide tube of the inner cavity comprises cylindrical chamber connected up and down, and inverted cone chamber. The first resilient plug filled in the first guide tube in the inverted cone chamber. The second resilient plug is filled in the second guide tube in the inverted cone chamber.

PN EP3741864-A1; EP3741864-B1; ES2914714-T3
TI Producing organic acid and/or alcohol using an            apparatus, involves providing a basic, aqueous medium            containing carbon dioxide in form of            hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions and            feeding basic, and aqueous medium.
AU HAAS T
AE EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2020B6559X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing organic acid and/or alcohol involves                providing a basic, aqueous medium containing carbon                dioxide in the form of hydrogencarbonate-anions                and/or carbonate-anions; and feeding the basic, and                aqueous medium containing the carbon dioxide into a                bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprising an                anode, a cathode, an ion-selective anion-exchange                membrane, and two water-dissociating bipolar                membranes, capable of providing for an acidic                compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar                membrane on the anode side and the ion-selective                anion-exchange membrane and for a basic compartment                between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on                the cathode side and the ion-selective                anion-exchange membrane.
   USE - Method for producing organic acid and/or                alcohol using an apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce organic acid                and/or alcohol, which is capable of reducing the                carbon dioxide concentration in the air and                provides a use for the carbon dioxide which is                stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide is transported in the form                of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions                across the anion-exchange membrane from the basic                compartment into the acidic compartment comprising                an acidic, aqueous solution, then contacting the                carbon dioxide in the acidic, aqueous solution and                hydrogen with acetogenic cell in an aqueous                production medium under suitable conditions to                produce organic acid and/or alcohol from the carbon                dioxide in the acidic, aqueous solution; and                optionally recovering the organic acid and/or                alcohol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing organic acid and/or                alcohol, comprises a bipolar membrane                electrodialysis unit comprising an anode, a                cathode, an ion-selective anion-exchange membrane,                a unit of receiving carbon dioxide in the form of                hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions,                and two water-dissociating bipolar membranes,                capable of providing for an acidic compartment                between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on                the anode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange                membrane and for a basic compartment between the                water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the cathode                side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane;                a fermenter comprising acetogenic cell, an aqueous                production medium, a unit of receiving a hydrogen                stream, and a unit of receiving the carbon dioxide                recovered from the bipolar membrane electrodialysis                unit, where the acetogenic cell is capable of                converting the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide to                organic acid and/or alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: A mixture of gases                  comprising carbon dioxide is contacted with a                  basic, aqueous medium containing base, capturing                  the carbon dioxide, which is contained in the                  basic, aqueous medium in the form of                  hydrogencarbonate and/or carbonate-anions. The                  mixture of gases comprises carbon dioxide at a                  concentration of 350ppm-450ppm, preferably the                  mixture of gases is air. The basic, aqueous                  medium is sprayed through a nozzle into a stream                  of the mixture of gases comprising carbon                  dioxide. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis                  unit comprises an alternating stack of                  ion-selective anion-exchange membranes and                  water-dissociating bipolar membranes of an amount                  of from 3 to 20 membranes. An amperage of 5-20 A,                  preferably 5-15 A most preferred 5-10 A is                  applied to the bipolar membrane electrodialysis                  unit. The contacting takes place in a fermenter                  comprising the acetogenic cell, the aqueous                  production medium, a unit of receiving a hydrogen                  stream, and a unit of receiving the carbon                  dioxide in the acidic, aqueous solution. The pH                  of the aqueous production medium is in the range                  between 5.0 and 6.5. The organic acid and/or                  alcohol produced is acetic acid and/or ethanol.                  The organic acid and/or alcohol produced is                  acetic acid and/or ethanol, preferably with                  acetogenic cell is Clostridium autoethanogenum,                  comprising a further step of contacting the                  acetic acid and/or ethanol with a second organism                  capable of converting acetic acid and/or ethanol                  to fatty acid. Preferred Components: The basic,                  aqueous medium contains a base selected from                  hydroxides and carbonates, preferably of alkaline                  and earth alkaline hydroxides and carbonates,                  preferably from sodium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide or calcium hydroxide. The acetogenic                  cell is selected from Clostridium aceticum (DSM                  1496), Clostridium autoethanogenum (DSM 10061,                  DSM 19630 and DSM 23693), Clostridium                  carboxidivorans (DSM 15243), Clostridium coskatii                  (deposited in American Type Culture Collection                  (ATCC)) under deposition number (ATCC NO:                  PTA-10522), Clostridium drakei (ATCC NO: BA-623),                  Clostridium formicoaceticum (DSM 92), Clostridium                  glycolicum (DSM 1288), Clostridium Ijungdahlii                  (DSM 13528), Clostridium Ijungdahlii C-01 (ATCC                  NO: 55988), Clostridium Ijungdahlii ERI-2 (ATCC                  NO: 55380), Clostridium Ijungdahlii O-52 (ATCC                  NO: 55989), Clostridium mayombei (DSM 6539),                  Clostridium methoxybenzovorans (DSM 12182),                  Clostridium ragsdalei (DSM 15248), Clostridium                  scatologenes (DSM 757), or Clostridium species                  (ATCC NO: 29797). The second organism is                  Clostridium kluyveri, preferably fatty acid is                  hexanoic acid. The apparatus further comprises a                  unit of recovering the organic acid and/or                  alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC D04-A01; D04-B07; D05-C09; D05-C15; E10-C04J2P; E10-C04K; E10-E04E

PN US2022316077-A1; CN115142074-A; JP2022156821-A; JP7168716-B2
TI Fuel producing system for producing fuel such as            methane using water and carbon dioxide, comprises            electrolyzing unit that is supplied with raw material            gas containing carbon dioxide and water vapor, and            electrolyzes raw material gas to generate product gas            containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
AU MAKI M
   YANAGISAWA K
   MOHRI M
   MORI M
   MORI A
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022C7860G
AB    NOVELTY - Fuel producing system (1) comprises an                electrolyzing unit (2) that is supplied with a raw                material gas containing carbon dioxide and water                vapor, and electrolyzes the raw material gas to                generate a product gas containing hydrogen and                carbon monoxide, and a first gas-liquid separator                that performs gas-liquid separation on the product                gas generated in electrolyzing unit. The water                vapor is produced from water separated in the first                gas-liquid separator, or water vapor produced from                water from a water supply source is allowed to be                supplied to electrolyzing unit in addition to the                raw material gas.
   USE - Fuel producing system used for producing fuel                such as methane using water and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel producing system enables to solve the                shortage of water vapor as a raw material and                reduces water waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of a fuel                producing system.
   1Fuel producing system
   2Electrolyzing unit
   3First gas-liquid separation
   4Synthesizing unit
   5Second gas-liquid separation unit

PN JP3239160-U
TI Gas emission valve device for carbon dioxide air            gun, has O-ring or V-ring arranged on both sides of            front carbon dioxide gas intake hole of shaft, and rear            carbon dioxide discharge hole formed in vicinity of            locking ring of shaft of cylindrical body.
AU KUBOTA K
   TERADA M
AE KSC CO LTD (KSCK-Non-standard)
GA 2022C1295H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a shaft which is incorporated                in a locking portion provided at the intermediate                position so that the shaft can advance and retract.                A coil spring is arranged on the other side of the                locking ring of the shaft, and a shaft pressing                portion is attached to the end portion of the shaft                with a coil spring interposed between main portion.                The shaft is in a retreated position by the                pressing force of the coil spring. The locking ring                is engaged with a locking part in the cylindrical                structure. The shaft is advanced when the shaft is                pressed by the shaft pressing part and the shaft is                advanced. The CO2 gas flows from the front CO2 gas                intake hole to the rear of the shaft and the shaft                is operated with a small force.
   USE - Gas emission valve device for carbon dioxide                (CO2) air gun.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas emission valve device can be operated                with small force.

PN CN114561665-A
TI Preparation of carbon-selective ferroelectric            modified copper-based electrode involves performing            nanocrystallization treatment, growing zinc oxide seed            layer, and growing vanadium-doped zinc oxide nano-sheet            array.
AU LI L
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2022773454
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-selective ferroelectrically                modified copper-based electrode comprises (a)                grinding the copper substrate on the surface, and                carrying out nano-crystallization treatment, (b)                growing a zinc oxide seed layer on the copper                substrate surface, (c) growing vanadium-doped zinc                oxide nanosheet array on the zinc oxide surface of                copper substrate, and (d) growing an array of zinc                oxide nanosheets on the nano-copper substrate                surface, where the zinc dioxide seed layer is made                of metal-oxide semiconductor material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                carbon-selective ferroelectric modified copper base                electrode used for electro-catalytic reduction of                catalytic carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-selective ferroelectric modified                copper-based electrode has high electro-catalysis                selectivity, and can effectively reduce the carbon                dioxide molecule in the system. The preparation                content in the product is relatively low, and the                efficiency of the electric reducing carbon dioxide                is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a carbon selective ferroelectric modified                copper base electrode; and
   (2) a method for reducing carbon dioxide by                electro-catalysis.

PT P
PN CN114146688-A
TI Preparing water-resistant metal organic frameworks-based material useful in carbon dioxide adsorption, comprises preparing mesoporous mesocellular silica foam material, and using mesoporous mesoporous mesocellular silica foam material as carrier and carrying out solvothermal synthesis.
AU LI F
   LI G
   ZHAO H
   LI L
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202239987E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing water-resistant metal organic frameworks (MOFs)-based material comprises (i) preparing mesoporous mesocellular silica foam (MCFs) material by sol-gel method, (ii) using mesoporous MCFs material as a carrier to support zero-valent iron nanoparticles, and (iii) preparing water-resistant HKUST1/MCFs composite by solvothermal synthesis.
   USE - The water-resistant MOFs based material is useful in carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good water resistance, and can convert the negative effect of water vapor in the flue gas into a positive effect, can reduce environmental pollution, does not corrode equipment, is easy to recycle, and has significantly enhanced carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, has low requirements on equipment, and is convenient to regenerate, and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for used method of water-resistant MOFs based material in carbon dioxide adsorption, comprising (A) pressing and sieving the adsorbent material, and taking 20-40 mesh, (B) degassing adsorbent in a room pressure argon atmosphere, and (C) adsorbing adsorption material in an atmosphere containing carbon dioxid gas under room pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) specifically comprises (a) adding concentrated hydrochloric acid into the aqueous solution of P123 to obtain solution A, and heating at 40-60degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours and stirring, where the mass ratio of P123, deionized water, concentrated hydrochloric acid, mesitylene and ethyl orthosilicate is 1:30:6:0.5-1:2, (b) adding mesitylene into solution A to obtain solution B, and heating at 40-60degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours and stirring, (c) adding ethyl orthosilicate dropwise into solution B to obtain solution C, and heating at 40-60degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours and stirring, (d) transferring solution C to a polytetrafluoroethylene-lined autoclave, and performing hydrothermal synthesis reaction at 100-120degrees Celsius for 18-24 hours, and (e) after the hydrothermal reaction is completed, subjecting product obtained to centrifugal washing and drying, and calcining and grinding. The step (ii) specifically includes (f) preparing 0.4-0.6 M ferric nitrate aqueous solution, (g) preparing the iron ion-loaded MCFs material by means of equal volume impregnation, and (h) preparing zero-valent nano-iron-supported MCFs material by hydrogen reduction at 800-1000degrees Celsius. The step (iii) specifically comprises (1) dissolving 3-4.16 g copper nitrate hydrate in 36-48 ml N,N-dimethylformamide to prepare solution M, (2) dissolving trimesic acid and zero-valent nano-iron-supported MCFs material in 36-48 ml N,N-dimethylformamide to prepare solution N, where the mass ratio of trimesic acid and zero-valent nano-iron-supported MCFs material is 1:0.05-0.1, (3) adding the solution M into the solution N and uniformly stirring, and transferring into an autoclave for solvothermal reaction at 100-120degrees Celsius for 18-24 hours, (4) after the reaction is completed, centrifugally washing the product, and vacuum drying overnight at 120-150degrees Celsius to obtain the water-resistant HKUST-1/MCFs composite material. In the step (B), the space velocity is 3000-4000 ml/hour-1, and the temperature is 120-200degrees Celsius under the condition of degassing for 2-3 hours. In the step (C), the space velocity is 3000-4000 ml/hour-1, and the adsorption is carried out under the condition of a temperature of 25-45degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Fresh air control system for public building, has monitoring module arranged in target building space, ventilation module for ventilating target building space, and control module for controlling ventilation module to ventilate target building space.

AE SHANGHAI AIRPORT GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2021B7946C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a monitoring module arranged in a target building space for monitoring concentration of carbon dioxide in target building space. PT P
PN CN112076761-A; CN112076761-B
TI Copper oxide nano-wire loaded silver particle            composite electrode useful for reduction of carbon            dioxide in electrochemical, comprises substrate layer,            copper(II) oxide nano-wire electrode layer and metal            particle layer.
AU LIU X
   YIN X
   CHEN Y
   SUN Y
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2020C80760
AB    NOVELTY - Copper oxide nano-wire loaded silver particle                composite electrode comprises a substrate layer, a                copper(II) oxide nano-wire electrode layer and a                metal particle layer loaded on the copper(II) oxide                nano-wire electrode layer.
   USE - The composite electrode is useful for                reduction of carbon dioxide in electrochemical                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite electrode has very high specific                surface area; improves shortage of traditional                metal foil electrode surface catalytic activity                site, activity of electrochemical reducing carbon                dioxide, simple preparation method, low cost, and                strong operability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   preparing copper oxide nano-wire loaded silver                particle composite electrode, comprising (i)                self-growing a layer of copper(II) hydroxide                nano-wire on surface of the substrate by chemical                oxidation method, (ii) annealing the copper(II)                hydroxide nano-wire in air, and cooling to obtain                copper(II) hydroxide nano-wire electrode, and (iii)                loading the metal silver particles on the                copper(II) oxide nano-wire electrode by using                photo-deposition method; and
   use of copper oxide nano-wire loaded silver                particle composite electrode for reduction of                carbon dioxide in electrochemical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal contained in                  the metal particle layer is metallic silver. The                  substrate includes foamed copper or copper foil.                  The used reference electrode is silver chloride                  electrode; the counter electrode is graphite rod;                  and the electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate                  solution. Preferred Method: The step (i) includes                  (ia) first dissolving sodium hydroxide in                  deionized water, then adding ammonium persulfate,                  and stirring for a while, and (ib) immersing the                  substrate in the step (ia) solution, taking out                  after period of time, rinsing in deionized water                  for many times, and drying under nitrogen flow to                  obtain copper(II) hydroxide nano-wire. The step                  (iii) includes (iiia) dissolving silver nitrate                  in deionized water, then adding less amount of                  anhydrous methanol and mixing evenly to obtain                  sedimentation liquid, and (iiib) immersing the                  copper(II) oxide nano-wire electrode in                  deposition solution, irradiating the copper(II)                  oxide nano-wire electrode with photocatalytic                  xenon lamp generator, rinsing the electrode in                  deionized water after deposition for many times,                  and drying under nitrogen atmosphere. The                  substrate needs to undergo surface pretreatment                  process before the oxidation reaction, comprises                  (1) soaking the substrate in acetone and                  ultrasonically treating for certain period of                  time, (2) transferring the treated substrate to                  certain concentration of hydrochloric acid                  solution, and ultrasonically treating for a                  period of time; and (3) rinsing the substrate in                  deionized water for many times and drying under                  nitrogen atmosphere. In step (iiib),                  photocatalytic xenon lamp generator is used to                  irradiate the side of the copper(II) oxide                  nano-wire electrode.

   CHO H (CHOH-Individual)
GA 2020386237
AB    NOVELTY - An air purifier comprises a power supply unit which receives power from the outside, ion generator which is connected to the power supply, and water particles generation unit for reducing the carbon dioxide generated in the interior space. The radical ions combine with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere to produce hydrogen carbonate ions. The hydrogen carbonate ions combine with water vapor particles sprayed into the atmosphere while heating water by power supplied from the power source, to produce hydrogen carbonate, which is harmless to the human body.

TI Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate involves reacting carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide in circular reactor using electrophilic-nucleophilic dual function system as catalyst at preset reaction pressure and reaction temperature.
AU REN W
   LV X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202007417F
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate involves reacting carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide in a circular reactor using an electrophilic-nucleophilic dual function system as a catalyst at a reaction pressure of 0.4-5 MPa and at a reaction temperature of 180 degrees C, by adding the catalyst to the reactor, heating at the reaction temperature through a heat exchanger, introducing carbon dioxide at the reaction pressure, continuously adding alkylene oxide, maintaining at constant reaction pressure, transferring to an aging tank, reacting until alkylene oxide conversion rate reaches 99.9%, transferring to a flash tank, discharging carbon dioxide, distilling under reduced pressure to obtain cyclic carbonate, recycling residual liquid containing catalyst, and maintaining the aging tank and flash tank in a steady state. The mass ratio of alkylene oxide with respect to catalyst is 5:1 to 50000:1. The mass ratio of alkylene oxide with respect to cyclic carbonate is 1:20 to 20:1.
   USE - Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables stable and efficient continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate having stable product quality, with high productivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises an electrophilic portion and a nucleophilic portion in a mass ratio of 1:1 to 1:50. The electrophilic portion is an organometallic complex of formula: M1a(M2(CN)bL1c)d.x(M3mXn).yL2.zH2O (I). The nucleophilic portion is a nitrogen-, phosphorus- or arsenic-containing compound of formula: R1R23Y'X1 (II). The mass ratio of the alkylene oxide with respect to catalyst is preferably 100:1 to 500:1. The mass ratio of the alkylene oxide with respect to cyclic carbonate is preferably 1:2 to 2:1. AU HU G
   TANG Q
   ZHANG R
AE DELAND WATER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DELA-Non-standard)
GA 201977109E
AB    NOVELTY - A salt-resistant antibacterial anti-hydrogen                sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion inhibitor                comprises 15-20 wt.% imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt, 5-10 wt.% pyridine quaternary                ammonium salt, 5-10 wt.% alkyl quaternary ammonium                salt, 3-7 wt.% alcoholamine, 4-6 wt.% methenamine,                1-3 wt.% thiourea, 0.1-0.3 wt.% surfactant, and                balance solvent.
   USE - Salt-resistant antibacterial anti-hydrogen                sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion inhibitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor has stable product performance                and good protection of field production                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the salt-resistant antibacterial                anti-hydrogen sulfide/carbon dioxide corrosion                inhibitor comprising washing reaction kettle,                closing discharging valve, adding 2/3 solvent into                reaction kettle, orderly adding methenamine and                thiourea, starting heating device, keeping                temperature at 40 plus minus 5 degrees C,                completely dissolving solid, adding surfactant,                uniformly stirring and mixing for 30 minutes,                orderly adding alkyl quaternary ammonium salt and                imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt, stirring for                30 minutes, adding pyridine quaternary ammonium                salt and alcoholamine, adding remaining solvent,                stirring for 30 minutes until solution is clear,                opening cooling water, cooling to 25-35 degrees C,                stopping stirring, opening discharging valve and                filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The imidazoline                  quaternary ammonium salt is acidic imidazoline                  quaternary ammonium salt, prepared by adding 80                  wt.% benzoic acid and triethylenetetramine, and                  20 wt.% xylene into three-neck bottle with water                  segregator, stirrer and condensing tube, adding                  zeolite, refluxing at 140-170 degrees C for 2                  hours, refluxing at 180-210 degrees C for 2                  hours, cooling reactant to 110-140 degrees C,                  distilling under reduced pressure for 2 hours,                  adding isopropanol of 1-chloro-3-phenylpropane,                  heating to 80 degrees C, reacting at 90 degrees C                  for 3-5 hours and cooling to obtain yellow                  liquid. GA 201962868Q

   USE - Catalyst for synthesizing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has large specific surface area, high catalytic activity, high carbon dioxide conversion rate and high methanol selectivity, and the preparation method is simple, rapid and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of catalyst which involves (1) mixing copper nitrate and zirconium nitrate in deionized water, slowly adding citric acid aqueous solution to the nitrate mixed solution under the action of magnetic stirring to obtain a uniformly mixed sol solution, (2) keeping the sol solution under the action of magnetic stirring, and evaporating the solvent of sol solution, and stirring to obtain a glassy amorphous product, (3) transferring to a vacuum drying oven and drying to obtain a gel-like catalyst precursor, (4) placing gel-like catalyst precursor in a tube furnace for high-temperature baking, programming to be heated, keeping at a baking temperature, cooling, grounding, and molding into a 40-80 mesh sieve to obtain a catalyst precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of metal atom of copper nitrate to zirconium nitrate is 1:(1-8), and the molar ratio of the metal atom to citric acid is 1:(1-1.5). The metal atom molar ratio of the copper nitrate to zirconium nitrate is 1:1, 1:2, 1:4, 1:8. Preferred Conditions: The magnetic stirring temperature is controlled between 75 degrees C and 95 degrees C. The magnetic stirring temperature is controlled between 75 degrees C and 95 degrees C. The drying treatment time is 24 hours, and the drying treatment temperature is 80-120 degrees C. The calcination temperature is 350-500 degrees C, and the holding time is 4 hours.

   HU Z
AE TONGCHENG JINGLUN PLASTIC CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 201908463N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated beverage comprises 0.3-0.9 wt.%                semaherb, 0.03-0.1 wt.% water-soluble peppermint                oil, 0.1-0.4 wt.% carbon dioxide, and the balance                is pure water.
   USE - Carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated beverage is convenient to                drink, gives relief from scorching summer sun,                reduces pathogenic fire, and clears heart. The                beverage is salty and delicious, and has refreshing                and pleasant taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing carbonated beverage, which                involves:
   (A) grinding and pulverizing semaherb in a                grinder, and passing through a 200-250 mesh                sieve;
   (B) dissolving semaherb powder with 2/5-3/4                amount of boiling pure water, mixing with the                remaining normal temperature pure water, cooling to                room temperature, adding water-soluble peppermint                oil, and stirring uniformly; and
   (C) cooling the obtained solution by a plate                heat exchanger at 2-7 degrees C, introducing carbon                dioxide into the steam-water mixer for gas-liquid                mixing, adjusting the pressure to 0.25-0.35 mega                Pascal to obtain carbonated beverage, filling,                sterilizing, and cooling the carbonated beverage,                and packaging the final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Carbonated beverage                  comprises 0.4-0.7 wt.%, preferably 0.7 wt.%, more                  preferably 0.5 wt.% semaherb, 0.05-0.09 wt.%,                  preferably 0.06 wt.%, more preferably 0.08 wt.%                  water-soluble peppermint oil, 0.25-0.4 wt.%,                  preferably 0.3 wt.%, more preferably 0.35 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, and the balance is pure water.                  Prefered Condition: The sterilization is                  performed at 105-108 degrees C for 20-30 seconds.                  Preferred Process: The Semaherb is prepared by                  boiling water, adding sea salt to make saturated                  brine solution. 0.05-0.08 wt.% Semaherb is added                  to the boiled saturated brine solution, and the                  content is boiled until the water evaporates                  completely. The crystals are obtained and                  grounded into powder form. The powder is placed                  in a covered porcelain bowl, and then placed in a                  furnace for high-temperature calcination. After                  the calcination is completed, it is cooled to                  room temperature to obtain the semaherb                  powder.

TI Method for measuring position of laser beam e.g. carbon dioxide beam, involves collecting and focusing EUV radiation using collector mirror, and directing EUV radiation to lithographic apparatus.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201888922Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating extreme UV (EUV) radiation using laser-produced plasma (LPP). The laser is a carbon dioxide laser in an LPP EUV radiation source. The carbon dioxide laser is set up, so that the laser is focused on a location through which a stream of tin droplets is passed. The laser light is made to hit the tin droplet to create plasma for generating EUV radiation. The EUV radiation is collected and focused using a collector mirror. The EUV radiation is directed to a lithographic apparatus. Lithography is a process of transferring a pattern to a radiation-sensitive substrate by exposing the substrate to radiation with the pattern. (Incomplete specification, abstract based on available information published by the patent office).

GA 201870812N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthetic breast milk fat substitute using silkworm pupa oil, comprises obtaining high-quality silkworm pupa oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and gradient freezing crystallization, and mixing the high-quality silkworm pupa oil with oleic acid and lipase, to obtain the breast milk fat substitute. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises: placing the silkworm pupa oil in a supercritical carbon dioxide device for supercritical extraction, where the co-solvent is an ethanol solution containing 5-10% ethyl acetate and 0.01-0.02% ofloxacin; and collecting the remaining liquid to obtain the preliminary refined oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthetic breast milk fat substitute using silkworm pupa oil.
   ADVANTAGE - Breast milk fat substitute has significant effect on the intelligence and memory enhancement, vision protection, as well as continue to reduce blood lipids, cholesterol and anti-platelet aggregation effect, lower blood pressure and thrombosis prevention, prevention of heart vascular disease and other functions, for the baby's growth and development, improve the immune system is very helpful.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction condition comprises: an extraction temperature of 35-40 degrees C; an extraction pressure of 30-40 MPa; an extraction time of 0.6-1 hour; separation kettle I pressure 10-12MPa, temperature 40-45 degrees C; and separation kettle II pressure 5-7MPa, and temperature 15-20 degrees C. The gradient freeze crystallization comprises: uniformly mixing the preliminary refined oil and the urea ethanol solution with mass fraction of 20-30% according to the mass ratio of 1:7-12; subjecting the mixture to freeze-crystallized by leaching; repeatedly freezing once the filtrate, filtering, and acidifying the filtrate to pH value of 2-3 with hydrochloric acid solution; extracting with petroleum ether; separating petroleum ether phase; washing petroleum ether phase with distilled water to neutral; drying petroleum ether phase; distilling petroleum ether under reduced pressure; and preparing high quality silkworm pupa oil. The gradient freeze-drying is at: 8-10 degrees C for 2.5-3 hours; 4-5 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours; 1-1.5 degrees C for 0.8-1 hours; -2 to -1.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hours; -4 to -3.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hours; -6 to -5.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hours; -8 to -7.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hours; or -12 to -11.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hours. The lipase is added at a concentration of 5-15%, the reaction temperature of 25-40 degrees C, and the reaction time of 0.5-3 hours. Preferred Components: The composition of the ofloxacin is 80-90% levofloxacin and 10-20% dexovafloxacin. The mass ratio of high quality silkworm pupa oil to oleic acid is 1:0.2-0.8. The lipase is lipozyme RM IM, lipozyme IM60, lipozyme IM20, lipase SP435, lipase SP382, lipozyme TLIM and Novozym 435.
