PN CN112500833-A
TI Preparing inert gas-driven cold aerosol explosion suppressant, comprises spraying ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing carbon dioxide or nitrogen.
AU WANG Y
   JI W
   ZHANG Y
   CAO J
   LIN S
   PEI B
   ZHENG L
   LI Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202128457K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing inert gas-driven cold aerosol explosion suppressant, comprises spraying 0.01-0.03 g/l ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 6-24 vol.% carbon dioxide or nitrogen to form carbon dioxide and potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol explosion suppressant or nitrogen and potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol explosion suppressant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing inert gas-driven cold aerosol explosion suppressant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides a suitable gas-solid ratio scheme; fully exerts the gas-solid synergistic explosion suppression effect; enhances the explosion suppression effect of the potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol; and enables the low-concentration potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol to completely inhibit methane explosion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dosage ratio of the carbon dioxide and potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol explosion suppressant is 0.03 g/l, the ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 6 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.02 g/l ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 12 vol.% carbon dioxide, and 0.01 g/l ultra-fine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 17 vol.% carbon dioxide. The dosage ratio of the nitrogen and potassium bicarbonate cold aerosol explosion suppressant is 0.03 g/l ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 16 vol.% volume fraction of nitrogen, 0.02 g/l ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 19 vol.% volume fraction of nitrogen, and 0.01 g/l ultra-fine potassium bicarbonate particles with air containing 24 vol.% volume fraction of nitrogen. The particle size of the ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles is less than 10 mu m. Preferred Method: The ultrafine potassium bicarbonate particles are prepared by (i) dropping potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution, which accounts for one-tenth of the volume of the absolute ethanol, into the absolute ethanol, where the mass concentration of the potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution is 10 g/l, and placing in an ice water bath and stirring at high speed, (ii) separating the precipitate after stirring, and drying, (iii) adding glidant and absolute ethanol, and stirring at high speed, and (iv) drying, stirring, and grinding. In the step (iii), the glidant is magnesium stearate with a mass percentage of 2-4% of the precipitate after drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the step (i) and/or (iii), a magnetic stirrer is used for stirring at a speed of 850 revolutions/minute (rpm) and stirring for 0.5-1 hour. In the step (ii), a vacuum filter is used to separate the precipitate. In the step (ii) and/or (iv), a blast drying oven is used for drying at 50 degrees C for is 24 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC E10-C04L2; E11-W; E12-C01; E31-H03; E31-N05C; E33-D

GA 202081965E
AB    NOVELTY - A palladium/cerium(IV) oxide catalyst comprises active component comprising 5-20% metal palladium, and carrier comprising 80-95% cerium(IV) oxide. The grain diameter of active component palladium is 5-30 nm.
   USE - The palladium/cerium(IV) oxide catalyst is used for preparing methane by carbon dioxide hydrogenation or catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high methane selectivity close to 100%, and facilitates further hydrogenation of carbon monoxide to methane in higher concentration of dissociated hydrogen on palladium particles.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the palladium/cerium(IV) oxide catalyst comprising dissolving soluble salt containing palladium in solvent to form solution, dipping solution on cerium(IV) oxide carrier, drying and roasting at 300-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours, or dispersing palladium or palladium oxide particles in solvent, dispersing cerium(IV) oxide carrier in same solvent, mixing dispersion containing palladium or palladium oxide particles and dispersion containing cerium(IV) oxide carrier, drying and roasting to 300-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours; and
   (2) catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction comprising pre-reducing palladium/cerium(IV) oxide catalyst before catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction under hydrogen or hydrogen/carbon dioxide mixed gas atmosphere at 400-700 degrees C at air speed of 2-20 L/gcat/hour and pressure of 0.1-1 MPa.

A fraction of the methane gas is                extracted from the liquid to provide a methane                enriched gas. A fraction of the carbon dioxide gas                is extracted from the liquid to provide a carbon                dioxide enriched gas, which is extracted from the                ascending flow of the liquid downstream of the                methane enriched gas. The methane enriched gas is                extracted. The carbon dioxide enriched gas is fed                as a fuel into an oxyfuel power generation system.                The power is generated from the oxyfuel power                generation system. An exhaust is expelled from the                oxyfuel power generation system. The exhaust                comprises carbon dioxide and water vapor. The                methane enriched gas has a carbon dioxide or                methane molar ratio less than or equal to 1. The                methane enriched gas is bubbled upwards in                liquefied natural gas (LNG).
   USE - Method for separating and utilizing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains purified methane from high                carbon dioxide gas sources. The non-compressibility                of water allows power-efficient compression to a                desirable pressure by utilization of multiple                piston-type fluid compression chambers operating in                cycle-sequence.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for separating and utilizing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view in a                vertical plane showing the method for separating                and utilizing gas.
   Bubble trapping device (2.2)
   Upflow system (2.3)
   Gas pump (2.6)
   Gas capturing degasser (2.7)
   Bubble extractor (2.35)

PN US2020148542-A1; US11407645-B2
TI Producing carbon dioxide by combusting            carbonaceous fuel with oxygen, processing flue gas            mixture, catalytically reacting part of oxygen with            auxiliary fuel, and catalytically reacting further part            of oxygen with another auxiliary fuel.
AU ALEKSEEV A
   KRISHNAMURTHY R K
   JOVANOVIC S
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 202039891R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing carbon dioxide, involves                combusting a carbonaceous fuel with oxygen or with                a gas mixture containing more than 25 mol% oxygen                to obtain a flue gas mixture, processing the flue                gas mixture to obtain a secondary gas mixture,                which contains carbon dioxide and oxygen,                catalytically reacting a part of the oxygen                contained in the secondary gas mixture with a first                auxiliary fuel in a reactor system comprising a                series of multiple reactors to obtain further                carbon dioxide and water, and catalytically                reacting a further part of the oxygen contained in                the secondary gas mixture with a second auxiliary                fuel in the reactor system.
   USE - The method is used for producing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces capital cost while                increasing recovered energy when used within a                multistage deoxidation reactor system with multiple                interstage coolers and multiport fuel                injections.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing carbon dioxide adapted for                combusting a carbonaceous fuel with oxygen or with                a gas mixture containing more than 25 mol % oxygen                to obtain a flue gas mixture, comprising a unit                adapted to process the flue gas mixture to obtain a                secondary gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and                oxygen, a unit adapted to catalytically react a                part of the oxygen contained in the secondary gas                mixture with a first auxiliary fuel in a reactor                system comprising a series of multiple reactors to                obtain further carbon dioxide and water, and a unit                adapted to catalytically react a further part of                the oxygen contained in the secondary gas mixture                with a second auxiliary fuel in the reactor                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first and the second                  auxiliary fuels are mixed to obtain an auxiliary                  fuel mixture, and parts of the auxiliary fuel                  mixture are supplied, separately to each other,                  into at least two of the multiple reactors. A                  reactor effluent from at least one of the                  multiple reactors is at least partially cooled                  and supplied to a different one of the multiple                  reactors, where a part of the auxiliary fuel                  mixture is mixed to the reactor effluent which is                  at least partially cooled and supplied to the                  different one of the multiple reactors. The first                  auxiliary fuel or parts of the first auxiliary                  fuel is/are supplied, separately from the second                  auxiliary fuel, to at least two of the multiple                  reactors, and parts of the second auxiliary fuel                  are supplied, separately from the first auxiliary                  fuel, to at least two of the multiple reactors. A                  reactor effluent from at least one of the                  multiple reactors is at least partially cooled                  and supplied to a different one of the multiple                  reactors, where a part of the first auxiliary                  fuel is mixed to the reactor effluent which is at                  least partially cooled and supplied the different                  one of the multiple reactors. A reactor effluent                  from a first one of the multiple reactors is at                  least partially cooled and supplied to a second                  one of the multiple reactors, where a reactor                  effluent from the second one of the multiple                  reactors is at least partially cooled and                  supplied to a third one of the multiple reactors,                  and the first auxiliary fuel is mixed to a feed                  to the first of the multiple reactors. Parts of                  the second auxiliary fuel are supplied to the                  first, the second and the third of the multiple                  reactors by directly injecting the parts into                  catalytic beds of these reactors. A reactor                  effluent from the third one of the multiple                  reactors is at least partially cooled and                  supplied to a fourth one of the multiple                  reactors, where the first auxiliary fuel is mixed                  to the first of the multiple reactors, a part of                  the second auxiliary fuel is directly supplied to                  the first of the multiple reactors, and further                  parts of the second auxiliary fuel are mixed to                  the reactor effluent from the first one of the                  multiple reactors which is at least partially                  cooled and supplied to the second one of the                  multiple reactors, to the reactor effluent from                  the second one of the multiple reactors which is                  at least partially cooled and supplied to the                  third one of the multiple reactors, and to the                  reactor effluent from the third one of the                  multiple reactors which is at least partially                  cooled and supplied to a fourth one of the                  multiple reactors. Preferred Components: The                  first auxiliary fuel comprises a lower minimum                  ignition temperature than the second auxiliary                  fuel. The first and second auxiliary fuels are                  selected such that an amount of heat generated by                  the catalytic reaction of an amount of oxygen                  with a stoichiometric amount of the first                  auxiliary fuel is lower than an amount of heat                  generated by the catalytic reaction of the amount                  of oxygen with a stoichiometric amount of the                  second auxiliary fuel. The first auxiliary fuel                  is a non-carbonaceous fuel and/or the second                  auxiliary fuel is a carbonaceous fuel. The first                  auxiliary fuel consists of or predominantly                  comprises hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The second auxiliary                  fuel consists of or predominantly comprises                  gaseous hydrocarbons.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC Q73-A01; Q73-T07
IP C01B-032/50; F23J-015/02

AU LEE J Y
   HYUN P B
   SIK C J
   KI K H
   GANG K J
   LEE J

   KIMHAEKI
   KIMJAEGANG
AE ANYTECH (ANYT-Non-standard)
GA 201951320F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sensor for detecting temperature and concentration of carbon dioxide included in indoor air and outside air of an alcoholic beverage manufacturing facility (10). A transfer path part (200) is provided with a transfer pipe in which the indoor air or the outside air is transferred. A storage (300) stores carbon dioxide liquid separated from the indoor air. A carbon dioxide removal part (400) performs absorption of the carbon dioxide liquid included in the indoor air. A control unit controls amount of the outside air flowed into an indoor when predetermined amount of the indoor air is ejected to an outdoor (20) according to temperature differential between the outside air and the indoor air.
   USE - Smart indoor air management system for use in an alcoholic beverage manufacturing facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces number of times of ventilation between the indoor air and the outside air of the alcoholic beverages manufacturing facility so as to reduce interior heating and cooling cost. The system facilitates reuse and absorption of the carbon dioxide liquid so as to effectively reduce concentration and consumption of the carbon dioxide liquid and carbon dioxide reduction cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a smart indoor air managing method.

   Carbon dioxide removal part (400)

TI 0.0056 flow coefficient carbon-dioxide compressor            module level impeller, has impeller body whose outlet            is provided with vane diffuser that is connected to            reflux device through conduit, where flow rate of            design point is specific in range.

AE SBW TURBO MACHINERY CO LTD (SBWT-Non-standard)
GA 201852101F
AB    NOVELTY - The impeller has an impeller body (1) i.e.                low-friction wedge impeller or closed binary                impeller, located at an inlet position of a module                stage. An outlet of the impeller body is provided                with a vane diffuser (2) that is connected with a                reflux device (4) through a curved conduit (3). PN WO2022155754-A1
TI Performing carbon dioxide reduction reaction in            electrolyzer, involves applying electrical potential            between anode and cathode, introducing hydrogen gas at            anode, supplying aqueous solution comprising            bicarbonate ions at cathode, oxidizing at anode, and            reacting hydrogen ions with bicarbonate ions.
AU ZHANG Z
   BERLINGUETTE C P
AE UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
GA 202295637A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing carbon dioxide reduction                reaction in an electrolyzer, involves applying an                electrical potential between an anode and a                cathode, introducing hydrogen gas at the anode,                supplying an aqueous solution comprising                bicarbonate ions at the cathode, where the aqueous                solution is substantially free of gaseous carbon                dioxide, oxidizing at the anode, the hydrogen gas                to form hydrogen ions, transporting the hydrogen                ions through an ion exchange membrane to the                cathode, and reacting the hydrogen ions with the                bicarbonate ions at the cathode to yield                products.
   USE - The method is used for performing carbon                dioxide reduction reaction in electrolyzer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for performing a carbon dioxide reduction reaction                comprising an electrolyzer comprising an anode                exposed in an anode chamber, adapted to oxidize                hydrogen gas to form hydrogen ions, a cathode                exposed in a cathode chamber, adapted to react                hydrogen ions formed from the anode with                bicarbonate ions contained in an aqueous solution,                where the aqueous solution is substantially free of                gaseous carbon dioxide, and an ion exchange                membrane separating the anode and the cathode,                where the ion exchange membrane is adapted to allow                the hydrogen ions formed from the anode to diffuse                through the ion exchange membrane to enter the                cathode chamber to react with the bicarbonate to                yield one or more products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  maintaining a current density of at least 100                  mA/cm-2, preferably at least 500 mA/cm-2,                  increasing a pressure of the aqueous solution at                  the cathode, maintaining the pressure of the                  aqueous solution at the cathode of 1.1-40 atm,                  preferably 1.1-10 atm. The pressure of the                  aqueous solution is increased by supplying a                  source of gas to a reservoir containing the                  aqueous solution. The method further involves                  delivering the hydrogen gas produced at the                  cathode to the anode, separating the hydrogen gas                  from the products prior to delivering the                  hydrogen gas to the anode, discharging excess                  hydrogen gas out of the anode, delivering the                  excess hydrogen gas into the anode, and                  transporting the hydrogen ions through a buffer                  layer after being transported through the ion                  exchange membrane and before entering a cathode                  chamber comprising the cathode. Preferred                  Conditions: The electrical potential has a                  magnitude that does not exceed 5 V, preferably                  does not exceed 2.5 V. The pH of the aqueous                  solution is greater than 4. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the                  biocarbonate ions in the aqueous solution is at                  least 0.1 M. The concentration of the                  biocarbonate ions in the aqueous solution is                  0.1-10 M. The products comprise not greater than                  50% carbon dioxide, preferably not greater than                  30% carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The                  products comprise a gaseous product, a liquid                  product, hydrogen gas, and a carbon-containing                  compound. The carbon-containing compound                  comprises formate, carbon monoxide, formic add,                  ethylene and/or propanol. The aqueous solution                  comprises potassium bicarbonate, caesium                  bicarbonate, and/or ammonium bicarbonate. The                  cathode is made of a metallic foam. The metallic                  foam comprises a conductive and/or catalytic                  metal. The metallic foam comprises silver. The                  cathode comprises a gas diffusion electrode. The                  gas diffusion electrode at the cathode is coated                  with a metal catalyst. The metal catalyst is                  bismuth. The anode comprises a gas diffusion                  electrode. The gas diffusion electrode at the                  anode comprises a platinum catalyst supported on                  carbon black.

GA 202217185B
AB    NOVELTY - Processing coffee grounds, involves drying coffee grounds to a predetermined dryness using a rotatory drier to heat and agitate the coffee grounds while measuring the dryness of the drying coffee grounds, and mixing the dried coffee grounds with supercritical carbon dioxide to extract liquid components of the coffee grounds from solid components.
   USE - Method for processing coffee grounds to extract coffee oil and to produce coffee flour.

PN US2022017555-A1
TI Recovering purified, solid lignin from black liquor of paper making process or crude lignin waste from biomass pulping process, involves pre-carbonating black liquor with carbon dioxide, and carbonating followed by washing granular lignin.
AU HAYDEN J G
   BLACKBURN J C
   LAKE M A
AE LIQUID LIGNIN CO (LIQU-Non-standard)
GA 2022146057
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering purified, solid lignin from black liquor, involves pre-carbonating the black liquor with carbon dioxide to reduce the pH to 11.5-12.5 and to form a partially carbonated black liquor, further carbonating partially carbonated black liquor with carbon dioxide sufficient to reduce the pH to 9-10.5, in a carbonation column, and subjecting the carbonated black liquor to a phase separation in a carbonation settler, where a dense liquid-lignin phase and a lignin-depleted liquid phase are formed, and sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and other basic components are neutralized. The dense liquid-lignin phase is removed and NaOH and other basic components are neutralized from the phase separation with sufficient acid to reduce the pH of below 4, to neutralize residual NaOH and other basic components, and to generate a granular lignin. The granular lignin is washed to remove residual acid and ash content.
   USE - Method for recovering purified, solid lignin from black liquor of papermaking operation or a crude lignin waste stream from a biomass pulping process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides purified, solid lignin with high yield by reducing the flow of carbon dioxide into the sparger of the carbonation column by one-third or more, and splitting the carbon dioxide load between the pre-carbonation column and the carbonation column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a pre-carbonation column connected to a carbonation column.
   Inlet for carbon dioxide (2)
   Inlet for recycled vent-gas (3)
   pH Probe (4)
   Line (7)
   Sparger (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The black liquor contains 10-70% solids content when entering the pre-carbonating step. The further carbonating step is carried out at 80-200degreesC and at a pressure of 50-200 psig in a carbonation column, by contacting the pre-carbonated black liquor with carbon dioxide counter-currently. The dense liquid-lignin acid is treated with sulfuric acid. The aqueous phase of the dense liquid-lignin exiting from the carbonating column is 30-40%. The pre-carbonation column includes a sparger to facilitate contact of the carbon dioxide with the incoming black liquor. The carbonation column includes a sparger to facilitate the contact of the acidic gases with the incoming partially carbonated black liquor. The carbonation settler is operated at a pressure lower than the pressure of the carbonation column used in the further carbonating step, where the dense liquid-lignin phase is transferred to the acidification step which is operated at a high-pressure allowing recycling of the vent gases to the pre-carbonation step. The sulfur-containing vent gas from the system is treated with a wash brine portion and an acid brine portion while purged air is removed, where NaOH, sodium hydrosulfide and sodium carbonate are separately removed.
DC F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)
MC F05-A02A
IP C07G-001/00

PN CN113926427-A
TI Preparation of modified graphene film used in system for capturing carbon dioxide in air, involves reducing graphene oxide sheets to obtain graphene sheets, using quaternary amine compounds to modify graphene sheet and preparing porous modified graphene membrane.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG J
   XU S
   BAI H
AE XIAN LVTAN RESOURCES RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022221214
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of modified graphene film, involves (1) reducing the graphene oxide sheets to obtain graphene sheets, (2) using quaternary amine compounds to modify the graphene sheet obtained in step (1) to obtain a modified graphene sheet solution, (3) using a vacuum filtration device to filter the modified graphene sheet solution obtained in step (2) through a cellulose filter membrane, when there is no free water on the filter membrane, stopping the vacuum filtration to form a modified graphene membrane, and (4) placing the modified graphene film in a freeze dryer to solidify and dry the water molecules inside the modified graphene film.
   USE - Preparation of modified graphene film used in system for capturing carbon dioxide in air (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures preparation of the modified graphene membrane material with the ability of adsorbing/desorbing carbon dioxide under different dry and wet conditions through synthesis and modification process, and the whole reaction process is simple and easy to control. The reaction raw materials are cheap and easy to obtain, and the production cost is low. The prepared modified graphene film has high selectivity, uniform skeleton pore size, high porosity and strong cycle stability, which can efficiently adsorb carbon dioxide in the air in a dry environment at room temperature and pressure. The adsorbed carbon dioxide can be desorbed by changing the environmental humidity, which has broad application prospects in the field of carbon dioxide capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (1) specifically involves (1.1) uniformly dispersing graphene oxide sheets in N-N dimethylformamide to prepare a graphene oxide/N-N dimethylformamide dispersion with a concentration of 2-5 mg.mL-1, (1.2) mixing the graphene oxide/N-N dimethylformamide dispersion with the reducing agent solution and stirring evenly, then refluxing at 85-105degrees Celsius to carry out the reduction reaction, and (1.3) after the completion of reaction in step (1.2), filtering the reaction solution and repeatedly washing with deionized water until the filtrate is neutral to obtain graphene sheets. The step (2) specifically involves adding the quaternary amine compound solution to the graphene sheet solution and dispersing to form a mixed solution, stirring, heating the mixed solution and reacting in a water bath at 100-120degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours to obtain a modified graphene sheet solution.Preferred Components: The reducing agent is anhydrous ethylenediamine, aqueous ammonia or hydrazine hydrate. The quaternary amine compound is tetramethylammonium hydroxide, methyldiethylisopropylammonium chloride, acryloyloxyethyltrimethylammonium chloride or 2,3-epoxypropyltrimethylammonium chloride.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the graphene oxide sheet to the reducing agent is 1:1.1-1.5. The mass ratio of the quaternary amine compound to the graphene sheet is 1.7-3.8:1.Preferred Property: In step (3.1), the diameter of the cellulose filter membrane is 3-5 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The graphene oxide used in step (1) is obtained by oxidizing natural graphite. In step (3), the modified graphene sheet solution used for preparing the porous modified graphene film is formed by mixing various modified graphene sheet solutions of different sizes. The modified graphene sheet solutions of different sizes are obtained from graphene oxide sheets of different sizes obtained by oxidizing natural graphite with different mesh numbers through steps (1) and (2).Preferred Property: The different mesh numbers refer to 50 mesh, 100 mesh, 325 mesh, 750 mesh, 1000 mesh, 1200 mesh and 8000 mesh.

TI Method for treating a gas stream in deep sea water, involves at least a methane, carbon dioxide and optionally hydrogen sulfide, in which the gas stream is brought into contact, within a membrane module immersed in deep water.
AU FAVRE E
AE UNIV LORRAINE (UYLR-C)
   UNIV LORRAINE (UYLR-C)
GA 202057259K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves at least a methane, carbon dioxide and optionally hydrogen sulfide, in which the gas stream is brought into contact, within a membrane module immersed in deep water, with at least one membrane that separates the gas flow from water. At least one membrane is impermeable to water while being more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane and, where appropriate, more permeable to hydrogen sulfide than to methane. The gas flow that leaves the module is depleted in carbon dioxide and, where appropriate, in hydrogen sulfide. The water in contact with the at least one membrane circulates by natural convection. The membrane module has a unit for evacuation of a portion of the gas flow.
   USE - Method for treating a gas stream in deep water, particularly deep sea water.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous fibers can allow the bubbling in seawater in contact with the hollow fibers of a fraction of the gas flow, and the gas bubbles thus formed circulate outside the bundle of hollow fibers, in seawater in contact with the hollow fibers, which allows convection on the surface of the membrane, and can thus, promote the dissolution of carbon dioxide, and where appropriate hydrogen sulfide, in water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for deep water treatment of a gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation of the evolution of the composition of the gas phase in a selective dense membrane contactor for dissolution of carbon dioxide in sea water in the deep ocean. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of purified gas stream with improved reliability and facility, and capable of increasing the window of operation with respect to impurities in the feed gas stream at given predetermined design specifics of the equipment. The controller is capable of preventing the amount of aromatic compounds in the AGRU outlet stream from exceeding a predetermined maximum threshold.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a purified gas stream-producing system.

PT P
PN KR2019129508-A; KR2061804-B1
TI Photocatalyst of p-n-p heterojunction structure used to remove and convert carbon dioxide to produce methane comprises base layer on which cuprous oxide dendrites are formed, sulfur doped titania microblock layer and cupric oxide nanowires.
AU IN S I
   HYELIM K
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 2019A34861
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst having p-n-p heterojunction structure comprises a base layer on which cuprous oxide dendrites are formed; a sulfur doped titanium dioxide microblock layer containing dendrites, formed on the base layer; and multiple cupric oxide nanowires formed on the microblock layer.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in removing and converting carbon dioxide to produce methane (all claimed) which is used in fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst converts carbon dioxide to methane at high efficiency under sunlight, effectively converts thermal gases i.e. carbon dioxide to methane, can be used to remove carbon dioxide or to produce methane in high yield, and has broad light absorption spectrum without using precious metal components. The method produces photocatalyst with high yield in economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) converting carbon dioxide to methane using the photocatalyst having p-n-p heterojunction structure; and
   (2) producing photocatalyst having p-n-p heterojunction structure comprising (a) anodizing copper substrate to prepare substrate on which copper sulfide dendrites are formed, (b) immersing the substrate in titanium precursor to coat the titanium precursor on the substrate, (c) leaving the titanium precursor coated substrate in atmosphere of 15-35 degrees C for 12-24 hours to form titanium(IV) hydroxide layer on the substrate on which the copper sulfide dendrites are formed, and (d) converting the titanium(IV) hydroxide layer into sulfur doped titanium dioxide microblock layer and growing cupric oxide nanowires by performing annealing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cupric oxide nanowires: are formed in vertical direction from the microblock layer; and have average diameter of 75-120 nm and lattice spacing of 2-2.8 Angstrom . The photocatalyst has 2 theta peaks of 25.5-26.5 degrees , 36-37.2 degrees and 38.2-39.5 degrees in X-ray powder diffraction patterns; and one peak at 167-170 eV, 930-936 eV and 950-955 eV, and two peaks at 525-535 eV in X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. The titanium precursor includes titanium(IV) isopropoxide, titanium(IV) ethoxide and/or titanium(IV) butoxide. Preferred Method: The methane conversation is carried out by gas phase reaction of carbon dioxide and water vapor in presence of the photocatalyst under artificial sunlight of AM 1.5 G for 3 hours to produce 1.7-2.8 mu mol/(m2 hour) methane. The step (a) comprises (a1) anodizing copper substrate in aqueous sodium sulfide solution by applying voltage of 2.5-3.5 V for 50 seconds to 1 minute 30 seconds, and (a2) drying the anodized copper substrate at 60-80 degrees C for 40 minutes to 2 hours. The annealing process is carried out by heating the substrate on which the titanium(IV) hydroxide layer is formed at 250-450 degrees C under air flow rate of 25-35 ml/minute for 2 hours 30 minutes to 4 hours.
   EXAMPLE - Copper foil was washed with acetone and ethanol and then with deionized water, introduced to 0.2 M sodium sulfide aqueous solution to perform anodization treatment using carbon paper (2 cm x 3 cm x 0.042 cm) as counter electrode and two-electrode cell having copper foil as a working electrode at 23-25 degrees C by applying constant voltage of 3 V for 1 minute, copper foil was washed with water and dried at 70 degrees C for 1 hour to synthesize substrate on which cuprous oxide dendrites were formed. The substrate formed with cuprous oxide dendrites was immersed in titanium(IV) isopropoxide for 5 seconds to coat on the substrate, and dried at 23-25 degrees C for 12 hours to form titanium(IV) hydroxide layer on the substrate on which the copper sulfide dendrites were formed. The substrate having titanium(IV) hydroxide layer was subjected to heat treatment at 400 degrees C for 3 hours while applying air at flow rate of 30 ml/minute to convert titanium(IV) hydroxide layer into sulfur doped titanium dioxide microblock layer and to grow cupric oxide nanowires to prepare photocatalyst having p-n-p heterojunction structure.

AU SHI J

AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019770910
AB    NOVELTY - Using process of metal sulfide-modified electrode for electrically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide comprises using low temperature plasma sulfurizing method, permeating sulfur element into surface layer of metal or alloy; using a metal sulfide modified electrode as cathode and inert electrode as anode, electrically reducing the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in diaphragm electrolytic cell; where the cathode chamber electrolyte of diaphragm electrolytic cell is organic electrolyte in which carbon dioxide is dissolved, where the anode chamber electrolyte is aqueous solution containing supporting electrolyte; obtaining carbon monoxide on the cathode and obtaining oxygen on the anode.
   USE - The method is useful for using process of metal sulfide-modified electrode for electrically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing metal sulfide modified electrode for electrically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide comprising (1) removing impurities on the metal or alloy by grinding or acid leaching; (2) bombarding the surface of metal or alloy treated in the step (1) with high-speed shot peening; and (3) placing the metal or alloy treated in step (2) into a sulfurizing furnace, evacuating to less than or equal to 10 Pa at temperature of 130-450 degrees C, introducing hydrogen sulfide, carbon disulfide or sulfur vapor, using the furnace shell as anode, using the metal or alloy as cathode, and applying high-voltage direct current of 500-800 V to form metal sulfide-modified electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal is silver, zinc, copper, gold, iron, molybdenum, tungsten, nickel, rhenium, aluminum, cadmium or titanium; and the alloy is an alloy of the metal formed according to any proportion. The metal sulfide modified electrode structure includes: the electrode substrate is metal or alloy, the surface of the electrode is metal sulfide modified layer, and there is transition layer between the substrate and the metal sulfide modification layer, such that the metal sulfide modifying layer is firmly attached to the surface of the metal or alloy, the metal sulfide-modified layer has thickness of 0.01-5 mu m. The inert electrode is a graphite electrode, glassy carbon electrode or iridium(IV) oxide-tantalum pentoxide coated titanium electrode. The separator in the diaphragm electrolytic cell is perfluorosulfonic acid membrane, porous polyethylene membrane, porous polypropylene membrane, porous polyvinylidene fluoride membrane, Porous polybenzimidazole film, porous silicone paper film or porous methyl silicone resin film; the control tank voltage during the reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide is 3.5-4.5 V. The cathode chamber electrolyte of the membrane electrolytic cell is organic electrolyte in which carbon dioxide is dissolved, the organic electrolyte contains organic solvent and electrolyte; the organic solvent is propylene carbonate, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone and/or N,N-dimethylformamide; the electrolyte is mixed electrolyte of tetraalkylammonium perchlorates, tetra-alkylammonium trifluoromethanesulfonate, tetraalkylammonium para-toluenesulfonate, imidazole ionic liquid, and/or pyridine ionic liquid; the alkyl group is C1-C4 saturated alkane, and concentration of electrolyte in the organic solvent is 0.1-5 mol/l. The anode chamber electrolyte is an aqueous solution containing a supporting electrolyte at a concentration of 0.1-5 mol/l; the supporting electrolyte is mixed electrolyte of sulfuric acid, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium hydrogen sulfate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, and/or potassium dihydrogen sulfate.

AU ASELL F
   WALHAGEN P
AE SCANIA CV AB (SCNI-C)
GA 201955396D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a carbon dioxide level in an interior space of a vehicle (V). A fresh air supply unit is adapted into the interior space of the vehicle based on the determined carbon dioxide level. A vehicle load is detected by a vehicle load detection element (112). The detected vehicle load is compared to a non-loaded vehicle to determine a load increase. The vehicle load is transferred to estimate number of persons. The carbon dioxide level is detected based on the estimated number of persons.
   USE - Method for regulating air composition in an interior space of a vehicle (claimed) i.e. passenger car.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables estimating an actual interior space volume based on a predetermined interior space volume, so that accurate determination of the carbon dioxide level in the interior space of the vehicle can be facilitated.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating coupled inert                gas injection flow rate. AE CARLETON LIFE SUPPORT SYSTEMS INC (CARL-Non-standard)

GA 201878255U
AB    NOVELTY - The onboard rebreathing loop system (10)                comprises a ceramic oxygen generating system module                (14), which is configured to receive an inlet air                and output a high purity oxygen gas (42) into the                breathing loop (16). A carbon dioxide scrubber                module (18) is provided, which is configured to                receive exhaled air (20) from the aircraft                personnel and output a carbon dioxide-scrubbed air                (32) into the breathing loop, where the high purity                oxygen gas and carbon dioxide-scrubbed air are                mixed to form a mixed gas (34) having a desired                partial pressure of oxygen suitable for breathing                by the aircraft personnel.
   USE - Onboard rebreathing loop system for use in an                aircraft for providing oxygen to aircraft                personnel.
   ADVANTAGE - The onboard rebreathing loop system ensures                sufficient replacement of oxygen consumed by the                aircraft personnel to maintain the desired level of                oxygen for the cabin altitude that is experienced                by the aircraft personnel while removing carbon                dioxide from the breathing loop in sufficient                quantity to prevent hypercapnia, and also minimizes                or eliminates a path for contaminants to enter the                breathing gas loop of the aircraft personnel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of an onboard rebreathing loop system.
   Onboard rebreathing loop system (10)
   Pilot (12)

   Carbon dioxide scrubber module (18)

   Carbon dioxide-scrubbed air (32)
   Mixed gas (34)
   High purity oxygen gas (42)

PN CN115127378-A
TI Particle/supercritical carbon dioxide heat            exchange experiment system for use in power generation            experiment system, has particle circulating loop and            fluidized bed communicating high temperature particles            in particle circulation loop.
AU WANG C
   JIA M
   XU R
   JIANG P
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2022C7117Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a supercritical carbon dioxide                circulation loop, a particle circulation loop and a                fluidized bed that are connected with each other. A                portion of the ultra-high-performance carbon                dioxide circulating loop is located in the                fluidization bed. The particle circulation circuit                is connected with the fluidizing bed to communicate                with high-temperature particles in the particle                circulation circulation circuit. The super-high                temperature particles are connected to the                super-critical air flow loop through the particle                circulating circuit to drive the air flow of the                fluidial bed under the driving of the driving air                flow. The heat exchange between the high-heat                particles and the supercritical air flows between                the fluidized beds is performed to exchange                heat.
   USE - Granule/supercritical carbon dioxide heat                exchange experiment system for use in a power                generation experiment system of a circulating                working substance i.e. gas turbine engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can effectively ensure the                circulating heat exchange of the high temperature                particles and supercritical carbon dioxide, reduce                the control difficulty of the operation process,                and improve the safety coefficient of the system.                The system prevents the problem of bridging and                blocking of particles, and improves the safety of                system operation, and ensures the high pressure                environment and heat exchange efficiency of the                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a power                generation experiment system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the particle/supercritical carbon                dioxide heat exchange experiment system.

PT P
PN WO2022183058-A1; US2022280912-A1
TI Metal ion-doped crystalline microporous            aluminosilicate composition for capturing carbon            dioxide, comprises three-dimensional aluminosilicate            framework containing alpha-cages with 8-MR openings            that are sized to accommodate molecular dimensions of            carbon dioxide, and d6r composite building blocks.
AU FU D
   DAVIS M E
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
GA 2022B2441E
AB    NOVELTY - Metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                aluminosilicate composition comprises (a) a                three-dimensional aluminosilicate framework                containing &#945; -cages with 8-MR openings                that are sized to accommodate the molecular                dimensions of carbon dioxide (3.3 &#8491; ),                and (b) the framework further comprising d6r (or                D6MR) composite building blocks having 6-membered                rings that face (are part of) or connect the                &#945; -cage of the framework, where the                crystalline microporous aluminosilicate comprises                1.2-8 metal ions per unit cell, the ratio of metal                ions to aluminum within the unit cell is 0.33-0.85,                and the metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                aluminosilicate composition adsorbs carbon dioxide                when exposed to a gaseous mixture comprising carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                aluminosilicate composition is used for capturing                carbon dioxide from gaseous source mixture                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method of preparing the metal ion-doped                crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                composition, which involves contacting a calcined                precursor crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                with an aqueous solution of a salt of the metal                ion, and optionally rinsing the resulting metal                ion-doped crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                with water and/or optionally drying the metal                ion-doped crystalline microporous aluminosilicate;                and
   a method for capturing carbon dioxide from a                gaseous source mixture, which involves contacting                the gaseous source mixture with the metal ion-doped                crystalline microporous aluminosilicate such that                carbon dioxide in the gaseous source mixture is                adsorbed by the metal ion-doped crystalline                microporous aluminosilicate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The three-dimensional                  aluminosilicate framework has an AEI, AFT, AFX,                  CHA, EAB, KFI, LEV, or SAS topology, preferably                  AEI topology, AFX topology, or CHA topology. The                  CHA is synthetic CHA. The metal ions are                  positioned within the lattice of the                  three-dimensional aluminosilicate framework. The                  (transition) metal ions are iron, cobalt, nickel,                  copper, zinc, or silver, preferably zinc. The                  metal ions are present within the framework                  lattice in a ratio of 7-8, preferably 1.21-2.6 or                  1.5-4 or 2.25-3 metal ions per unit cell. The                  calcined precursor crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate has an AEI, AFX, or CHA topology.                  The calcined precursor crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate having an AEI topology is SSZ-39.                  The calcined precursor crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate having an AFX topology is SSZ-16.                  The calcined precursor crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate having an CHA topology is SSZ-13.                  The metal ion is zinc ion. The salt of a metal                  ion is zinc acetate, zinc chloride, zinc nitrate,                  zinc sulfate, or zinc bromide. Preferred                  Composition: The composition has a silicon and                  aluminum atomic ratio of 1:1-20:1, preferably                  2:1-8.5:1, or 2:1-7.5:1, or 5.5:1-8.5:1, or                  6.5:1-7.5:1, or 7.5:1-8.5:1. The ratio of metal                  ions to aluminum within the unit cell is                  0.34-0.58, or 0.59-0.85. The gas source is 400                  parts per million (ppm) carbon dioxide/400 ppm                  argon balanced by helium at a flow rate of 20                  ml/minute at 30&#176; C, or 400 ppm carbon                  dioxide/1% argon/20% oxygen/remaining nitrogen,                  at a flow rate of 14 ml/minute at 30&#176; C.                  Preferred Method: The method further involves                  desorbing the carbon dioxide from the carbon                  dioxide laden metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate. The step of                  contacting the gaseous source mixture with the                  metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate involves passing the gaseous                  source mixture through a fixed-bed of adsorbent                  comprising the metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate. Preferred                  Conditions: The adsorbed carbon dioxide is                  desorbed at a temperature of less than                  130&#176; C, preferably less than                  100&#176; C. The adsorbed carbon dioxide is                  completely desorbed at a temperature that is                  lower than the temperature required to completely                  desorb carbon dioxide from zeolite 13X under                  otherwise the same conditions. The metal                  ion-doped crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                  composition has a selectivity for carbon dioxide                  over nitrogen of at least 800:1, preferably at                  least 900:1. The contacting of the metal                  ion-doped crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                  with the gaseous source mixture is carried out in                  the absence of, or without the use of, an added                  desiccant. The contacting of the metal ion-doped                  crystalline microporous aluminosilicate with the                  gaseous source mixture is carried out in the                  presence of, or with the use of, an added                  desiccant. The contacting of the gaseous source                  mixture with the metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate is carried out at                  less than 50&#176; C. The desorbing of the                  carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide laden                  metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate is carried out at less than                  130&#176; C. Preferred Properties: The                  composition has capacity to contain carbon                  dioxide of 0.3-1.7 molecules adsorbed carbon                  dioxide per unit cell, preferably 0.3-1.3                  molecules adsorbed carbon dioxide per unit cell,                  or 0.4-0.6 molecules adsorbed carbon dioxide per                  unit cell, or 0.6-1.25 molecules adsorbed carbon                  dioxide per unit cell. The exposure of the                  crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                  composition to a gas source having (a) a total                  pressure of 50-125 kPa, and (b) a carbon dioxide                  content of 350-425 ppm, results in adsorption of                  carbon dioxide of 0.3-1.7 molecules adsorbed                  carbon dioxide per unit cell, preferably 0.3-1.3                  molecules adsorbed carbon dioxide per unit cell,                  or 0.4-0.6 molecules adsorbed carbon dioxide per                  unit cell, or 0.6-1.25 molecules adsorbed carbon                  dioxide per unit cell. The exposure of the                  crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                  composition to a gas source having (a) a total                  pressure of 50-125 kPa, and (b) a carbon dioxide                  content of 350-425 ppm, results in adsorption of                  carbon dioxide of 0.2-0.7 mmols adsorbed carbon                  dioxide per gram of metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate composition. The                  exposure of the crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate composition to a gas source                  having (a) a total pressure of 50-125 kPa, and                  (b) a carbon dioxide content of 350-425 ppm,                  results in adsorption of carbon dioxide of                  0.3-0.7 mmol adsorbed carbon dioxide per gram of                  metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate composition. The exposure of the                  crystalline microporous aluminosilicate                  composition to a gas source having (a) a total                  pressure of 50-125 kPa, and (b) a carbon dioxide                  content of 350-425 ppm, results in adsorption of                  carbon dioxide of 0.5-0.7 mmol adsorbed carbon                  dioxide per gram of metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate composition. The gas                  source has a total pressure of 100 kPa. The gas                  source has a carbon dioxide content of 400 ppm.                  The passage of a gas source having (a) a total                  pressure of 50-125 kPa, and (b) a carbon dioxide                  content of 350-425 ppm, through a tube containing                  a fixed bed of the metal ion-doped crystalline                  microporous aluminosilicate composition, results                  in complete breakthrough of carbon dioxide after                  adsorption of 0.2-0.5 mmol of carbon dioxide per                  gram of metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate composition. The passage of a gas                  source having (a) a total pressure of 50-125 kPa,                  and (b) a carbon dioxide content of 350-425 ppm                  through a tube containing a fixed bed of the                  metal ion-doped crystalline microporous                  aluminosilicate composition, results in complete                  breakthrough of carbon dioxide after adsorption                  of an amount of carbon dioxide (on a mmol/g                  basis) that is 1.4-1.6 times greater than the                  amount of carbon dioxide adsorbed by an equal                  weight of zeolite 13X before complete                  breakthrough of carbon dioxide occurs under the                  same conditions.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

The method ensures the stability of the actual absorption, and lack of the relative mode of checking the absorption. PN CN113893819-A
TI Biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon useful for carbon dioxide adsorption and carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation, prepared by subjecting corn stalk segments to hydrothermal reaction, grinding obtained hydrothermal carbon with sodium amide, heating under inert gas atmosphere, and activating.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG T
   WANG B
   LI Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202214367V
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon having specific surface area of 65-1721 cm2/g, total pore volume of 0.12-0.94 cm3/g, micropores (with pore diameter of less than 2 nm) volume of 0.11-0.58 cm3/g, ultra-micropores (with pore diameter of less than 1 nm) volume of 0.04-0.21 cm3/g and nitrogen mass content of 2.41-7.14%, is claimed.
   USE - The biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon is useful for carbon dioxide adsorption and carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance, and carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of the biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon, comprising (1), cutting corn stalks, cleaning and drying to obtain corn stalk segments, (2) subjecting corn stalk segments to hydrothermal reaction using deionized water, separating the reaction product into solid and liquid, and washing the obtained solid and drying to obtain hydrothermal carbon, and (3), mixing the hydrothermal carbon with sodium amide, grinding to obtain mixture, heating the mixture to a target temperature under inert gas atmosphere, activating at a constant temperature, then cooling, washing and drying to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of the biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon for carbon dioxide adsorption and carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (1): the length of the corn stalk segments is 0.5-1 cm; and the drying temperature is 50-80 degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 10-15 hours. In step (2): the amount of deionized water added is 50-80 ml/2 g of corn stalk segments; the heating temperature is 150-200 degreesC, and the heating time is 20-30 hours; and the drying is freeze-drying, the freeze-drying temperature is 50 degreesC, and the freeze-drying time is 24 hours. In step (3): the target temperature is 400-600degreesC, and the heating rate is 2-4 degreesC/minute; the inert gas is argon, and the flow rate of the argon gas flow is 50-80 ml/minute; first the argon gas is passed for 15 minutes to drive out other gases in the tube furnace, and then heated; the constant temperature activation time is 1-3 hours; the washing is carried out by firstly washing with hydrochloric acid solution, and then washing with deionized water for multiple times until neutral; the molar concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 0.1-0.5 mol/l; and the drying temperature is 50-80 degreesC, and the drying time is 3-12 hours. The use of biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon for carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation, where the carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas selectivity coefficient is 5.65-19.72.Preferred Components: In the biomass nitrogen-doped porous carbon, the volume of micropores with pore diameter of less than 2 nm accounts for 44-66% of the total pore volume, and the volume of ultra-micropores with pore diameter of less than 1 nm accounts for 16-38% of the total pore volume. In step (3), the mass ratio of sodium amide to hydrothermal carbon is 1-3:1.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN US2021084832-A1; US11445669-B2
TI System used in capturing carbon dioxide from the            atmosphere and treating water, comprises enclosure            including aquatic macrophyte, water inlet opening in            enclosure configured to allow contaminated water to            enter enclosure, and solid sorbent.
AU OKEEFE F
AE CARBON SINK INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON SINK INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021422138
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprises (a) an enclosure having                an interior comprising at least one aquatic                macrophyte, (b) a water inlet opening in the                enclosure configured to allow contaminated water                (140) to enter the enclosure and contact the                aquatic macrophyte, and (c) a solid sorbent (180)                configured to capture carbon dioxide in air                exterior to the enclosure and release the captured                carbon dioxide to the interior of the                enclosure.
   USE - The system is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide from the atmosphere and treating water                (claimed), and to purify water, reduce indoor air                pollution, and produce biofuel, food products, and                fertilizer, and enhance growth of a photosynthetic                biomass in a greenhouse.
No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The system increases the growth or                reproduction rate of the duckweed by at least                2-fold, and improves plant yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                capturing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and                treating water, comprising (a1) introducing water                comprising a contaminant into an enclosure having                an interior containing an aquatic macrophyte, so                that the water comes into contact with the aquatic                macrophyte, where the aquatic macrophyte                facilitates the reduction of the level of the                contaminant in the water, (b1) capturing carbon                dioxide from air exterior to the enclosure with a                solid sorbent, and (c1) releasing captured carbon                dioxide into the interior of the enclosure, where                the interior of the enclosure has a higher carbon                dioxide concentration than the air exterior to the                enclosure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a                system.
   System (100)
   Runoff canal (120)
   Contaminated water (140)
   Cleaner water (150)
   Solid sorbent (180)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The aquatic                  macrophytes comprise Eichhornia, Spirodela,                  Salvinia, Azolla, Lemna, Pistia, heartleaf,                  duckweed, or members of the duckweed family. The                  contaminated water is a lake, a pond, a stream, a                  river, a canal, agricultural runoff, or a                  reservoir. The contaminant is a                  nitrogen-containing compound comprising a                  nitrate, nitrite or ammonium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The solid sorbent                  comprises an anion exchange material, and a                  sorbent material distributed on or in a support                  material. The enclosure comprises many vertically                  arranged tiers including aquatic macrophytes.                  Each tier is in fluid communication with a                  separate solid sorbent. The system further                  comprises an artificial light source configured                  to provide light to the aquatic macrophytes, a                  passive power source including a solar panel, a                  wind turbine, a hydroelectric generator, and a                  thermal energy converter, and a moisture control                  apparatus configured to expose the solid sorbents                  to increased moisture that facilitates the                  release of carbon dioxide, where the moisture                  control apparatus comprises either (i) a                  controlled water inlet configured to bring a                  controlled amount of water into contact with the                  solid sorbent, or (ii) a humidifier, where the                  moisture control apparatus is configured to                  expose the solid sorbent to water i.e. a gas,                  solid, or liquid. The air exterior to the                  enclosure is outdoor atmospheric air.Preferred Method: The solid sorbent is                  distributed about an axis around which the                  sorbent rotates, the sorbent comprises a first                  surface and a second surface, and the solid                  sorbent is positioned relative to the enclosure                  in which as the sorbent rotates about the axis at                  least a portion of the first surface enters the                  enclosure and at least a portion of the second                  surface exits the enclosure, where the method                  further comprises rotating the sorbent about the                  axis to alternate exposing the first surface and                  the second surface to the exterior of the                  enclosure. In the method, the releasing captured                  carbon dioxide comprises wetting the sorbent or                  exposing the sorbent to increased humidity. The                  contaminant in treated water exiting the                  enclosure has a concentration of at least 10%                  less, as compared to water entering the                  enclosure. The method further comprises                  maintaining the carbon dioxide concentration in                  the interior of the enclosure at a level at which                  the duckweed exhibits a growth rate or                  reproduction rate which is at least 2-fold                  increased as compared to growth or reproduction                  of the duckweed in the air exterior to the                  enclosure, and maintaining the carbon dioxide                  concentration in the interior of the enclosure at                  least 1000 ppm, 1200 ppm or 1500 ppm or more. The                  carbon dioxide concentration is maintained for at                  least 2 days.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN110483359-A
TI Performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction            process involves taking kale, drying and dehydrating            kale to control the dehydration rate, and then            pulverize the dried kale with a pulverizer.
AU SHEN L
AE NANTONG KEXIN SUPERCRITICAL EQUIP CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0409M
AB    NOVELTY - Performing supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction process involves taking kale, drying and                dehydrating kale to control the dehydration rate                above 95%, and then pulverize the dried kale with a                pulverizer, and repeating 3-5 times. The kale is                pulverized, and resulting particle size is 40-80                mesh. The granulated kale is put into the                extraction device, at the same time pass an                appropriate amount of entrainer into the extraction                device, and then use the booster pump and preheater                to make the carbon dioxide reach a critical state                and send it to the extraction device. The pressure                in the extraction device is 30-50 megapascal, and                the extraction time is 4-8 hours. The carbon                dioxide fluid in the extraction device is slowly                released by adjusting the drain valve. After the                carbon dioxide is separated, it is condensed by a                condenser and then returned to the storage tank for                recycling until the extraction reaches normal                pressure.
   USE - Method for performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction process, where by putting                the granulated kale into the extraction device and                passing an appropriate amount of entrainer into the                extraction device, and booster pump and preheater                are used to make the carbon dioxide reach a                critical state and send it to the extraction                device, so that the extraction process of lutein is                relatively simple, and extraction effect of lutein                has been significantly improved, and unnecessary                waste of resources has been greatly reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The entrainer is one                  of medium-chain triglyceride, ethanol, soybean                  oil, sunflower oil, rapeseed oil, and n-hexane,                  preferably medium-chain triglyceride. Preferred                  Conditions: The mass of the entrainer is 2-5 wt.%                  of the mass of the kale. The purity of the carbon                  dioxide is 98% or more, and the purity of the                  entrainer is 95% or more. The temperature is                  controlled at 10-20 degrees C and the pressure is                  controlled at 10-15 megapascal during the                  separation.

PT P
PN US10358392-B1
TI Converting crystalline energetic material to amorphous energetic material by condensing carbon dioxide gas on cryogenically cold surface, depositing vapors of heated crystalline energetic material on formed dry ice layer, and sublimating.
AU GUIRGUIS R H
AE US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
   DEPT NAVY (NAVY-Non-standard)
GA 201963520L
AB    NOVELTY - A crystalline energetic material is converted to an amorphous energetic material by condensing carbon dioxide gas on a cryogenically cold surface in a vacuum chamber to form a dry ice layer; depositing, on the dry ice layer, vapors of sublimation produced by heating the crystalline energetic material under a vacuum inside the vacuum chamber, where vapors of sublimation solidify to form a layer of amorphous energetic material on the dry ice layer; sublimating dry ice layer by warming up cold substrate on which it is formed, and breaking apart the layer of the amorphous energetic material into a powder; and collecting the powder of the amorphous energetic material.
   USE - Conversion of a crystalline energetic material to an amorphous energetic material.
   ADVANTAGE - Conversion of a crystalline energetic material to an amorphous energetic material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for conversion of crystalline energetic material to an amorphous energetic material which involves providing vacuum chamber with an access port, where vacuum chamber includes enclosing wall that is thermally conductive and mechanically sufficient to withstand evacuation to a pressure as low as 10e-3 torr and cryogenic cooling to as low as ? 196 degrees C, where vacuum chamber includes inlet with a two-way valve through which carbon dioxide gas flows into vacuum chamber, outlet with three-way valve, where in an open outlet position gas flows in or out of the vacuum chamber, in a closed outlet position flow is occluded, and in a vacuum position gas is evacuated from vacuum chamber, holder with piccolo tube with a set of perforations and heating element, and where holder is mounted on sealing plug for access port; loading piccolo tube with crystalline energetic material and positioning holder and piccolo tube in the vacuum chamber; closing access port with sealing plug; purging any air initially inside the vacuum chamber while the vacuum chamber is at a pressure of about one atmosphere by circulating the carbon dioxide gas for minutes through the vacuum chamber; submerging the vacuum chamber in a bath of liquid nitrogen condensing the circulating carbon dioxide gas forming a layer of dry ice on an inward side of the enclosing wall; continuing circulating the carbon dioxide gas until a dry ice layer sufficiently thick is formed, where circulating the carbon dioxide gas is ceased; evacuating the carbon dioxide gas remaining inside the vacuum chamber until the pressure in the vacuum chamber is reduced to 10 e-3 torr, and closing the three-way valve; using the heating element for heating the crystalline energetic material above its sublimation temperature corresponding to the pressure of 10 e-3 torr, where energetic material sublimates producing vapors of sublimation of energetic material; depositing vapors of sublimation on dry ice layer to form 2nd layer made of an amorphous energetic material; adjusting the rate of heating, where rate of generating the vapors of sublimation is about equal to the rate of depositing the vapors of sublimation on the dry ice layer, maintaining the pressure inside the vacuum chamber at 10 e-3 torr; turning off the heating element after a layer of the amorphous energetic material sufficiently thick is formed on the dry ice layer; allowing the deposition of the vapors of sublimation on the dry ice layer to continue for minutes, providing time for formed vapors of sublimation to be deposited; warming the vacuum chamber by removing it from the bath of liquid nitrogen, allowing the temperature inside the vacuum chamber to rise causing the dry ice layer to sublimate into carbon dioxide gas, and increasing the pressure inside the chamber; continuing the sublimation of the dry ice layer to produce more carbon dioxide gasses which dislodge and breakup the layer of the amorphous energetic material, separating the amorphous energetic material as powder from the inward side of the enclosing wall; opening the outlet three-way valve after the pressure inside the chamber builds up to about one atmosphere, enabling the carbon dioxide gas resulting from further sublimation of the dry ice layer to escape from the vacuum chamber; and collecting the amorphous energetic powder from the vacuum chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Enclosing wall is made of greater than or equal to 1, which is thermally conductive, and metal is selected from copper having thermal conductivity of 0.92 calorie/s.cm. degrees C; aluminum having thermal conductivity of 0.49 calorie/s.cm. degrees C; and brass having thermal conductivity of 0.26 calorie/s.cm. degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Energetic material is broadly a class of materials comprised of nitroamines selected from octahydro-1,3,5,7-tetranitro-1,3,5,7-tetrazocine (HMX), 1,3,5-trinitro-perhydro-1,3,5-triazine (RDX), 1-nitroguanidine, and hexanitrohexaazaisowurtzitane (CL-20). Amorphous energetic material, which is formed, is as energetically potent as the crystalline energetic material. Amorphous energetic material is softened when heated above its glass transition temperature but below its melting point, and where as a softened amorphous energetic material, it is moldable into a form. The form is a consolidated explosive charge comprised solely of the amorphous energetic material. The form comprises a binder. Preferred Processes: Dry ice layer is built up by circulating the carbon dioxide gas past a cryogenically cold surface in a vacuum chamber. Breaking the layer of the amorphous energetic material into the powder is augmented by an intercalation of dry ice during forming the layer of the amorphous energetic material on the dry ice layer. Amorphous energetic powder is collected by removing sealing plug from access port and pouring out powder through access port. Preferably, nitroamines are crystalline HMX, where when converting the crystalline HMX to amorphous HMX, the heater is nominally heated to 210 plus minus 10 degrees C, preferably 131 plus minus 10 degrees C.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); K04 (Explosives, matches - including detonators, chemical lighters, pyrophoric compositions, fire works, distress signals, chemical lasers, smoke generation, gas attack compositions, generation of gas for blasting or propulsion but only their chemical aspects (C06).)
MC E06-D18; E07-D13A; E07-D13C; E10-A17A; E11-R04A; E12-A02; K04-E02; K04-F01
