PN KR2020102372-A; KR2435114-B1
TI Producing high-lignan sesame oil involves            subjecting sesame seeds to supercritical extraction            using supercritical carbon dioxide followed by ethyl            esterification reaction carried out by mixing lipase            fixed to carrier.
AU HWAN K I
   KIM K
   KIM N
   KIM H J
   LEE C Y
   CHOI J H
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202087955B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high-lignan sesame oil involves                subjecting sesame seeds to supercritical extraction                using supercritical carbon dioxide, carried out at                a temperature of 35-45 degrees C, a pressure of                45-51 mega Pascals, and a flow rate of 2-L/minute                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for producing high-lignan sesame                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce high quality                lignan sesame oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The manufacturing                  method is carried out until an extract of 0.3-1                  pts. wt. is obtained based on 100 pts. wt. sesame                  seed meal. The ethyl esterification reaction is                  carried out by mixing the lipase fixed to the                  carrier in a mixed solution of the primary                  extract and ethanol. The lipase immobilized on                  the carrier is lipase derived from Thermomyces                  lanuginosus immobilized on Duolite A568 carrier.                  The ethyl esterification reaction is induced by                  stirring for 10-14 hours at 280-320 rotations per                  minute at a temperature of 38-42 degrees C.                  Preferred Process: The preparation of high-lignan                  sesame oil involves subjecting sesame seeds to                  supercritical extraction using supercritical                  carbon to obtain a primary extract; inducing an                  ethyl esterification reaction by mixing the                  primary extract with ethanol and subjecting the                  obtained ester to supercritical extraction at a                  temperature of 58-62 degrees C at a pressure of                  11.5-11.9 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 2-3                  L/minute. The method further involves producing a                  lignan concentrate by reflux cooling by mixing                  the first concentrate with a non-polar solvent;                  inducing crystals at - 10-40 degrees C for 6-48                  hours in the cooled primary concentrate;                  filtering the first concentrate from which the                  crystals are derived to recover the crystals;                  vacuum drying to remove the solvent, and                  obtaining the residue as a secondary lignan                  concentrate. Preferred Components: The solvent is                  one or more solvents selected from the group                  consisting of cyclohexane, n-hexane, and                  petroleum ether.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE HENAN HAOFENG FOOD CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2021513182
AB    NOVELTY - Sterilizing egg liquid comprises (i) soaking the poultry egg in the enzyme solution for 25-50 seconds at 15-35 degrees C, washing with clean water, drying, sterilizing the eggshells with ultraviolet rays, and beating the eggs to obtain egg liquid, where the composition of enzyme solution is 1.5-1.9 g/l protease and 2.5-3.4 g/l cellulase, and the ultraviolet sterilizing time is 20-40 minutes, (ii) adding epsilon -polylysine hydrochloride and Nisin into egg liquid, stirring uniformly, introducing carbon dioxide into the egg liquid under the ultrasonic treatment, making the concentration of carbon dioxide in egg liquid to 46-60 mmol/l to obtain primary processing egg liquid, where the adding amount of epsilon -polylysine hydrochloride is 0.005-0.03% of egg liquid, and (iii) sterilizing under the conditions of carbon dioxide pressure of 11-20 MPa, stirring speed of 110-170 revolutions/minute, and temperature of 35-5 degrees C for 50-80 minutes for one time, releasing pressure, and storing at 0-4 degrees C.
   USE - The egg liquid is useful for baked food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to sterilize egg liquid at low temperature, which overcomes the limitation of traditional heating sterilizing liquid egg method, prolongs the shelf life of the liquid egg, and ensures the quality of the baked food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sterilizing egg liquid comprises (i) soaking the poultry egg in the enzyme solution for 25-50 seconds at 15-35 degrees C, washing with clean water, drying, sterilizing the eggshells with ultraviolet rays, and beating the eggs to obtain egg liquid, where the composition of enzyme solution is 1.5-1.9 g/l protease and 2.5-3.4 g/l cellulase, the ultraviolet sterilizing time is 20-40 minutes, the ultraviolet wavelength is 200-280 nm, (ii) adding epsilon -polylysine hydrochloride and Nisin into egg liquid, stirring uniformly, introducing carbon dioxide into the egg liquid under the ultrasonic treatment, making the concentration of carbon dioxide in egg liquid to 46-60 mmol/l to obtain primary processing egg liquid, where the egg liquid includes 0.005-0.03% epsilon -polylysine hydrochloride and 0.006-0.02% Nisin, the ultrasonic power is 20-80 W and the temperature is 20-30 degrees C, and (iii) sterilizing under the conditions of carbon dioxide pressure of 11-20 MPa, stirring speed of 110-170 revolutions/minute, and temperature of 35-5 degrees C for 50-80 minutes for one time, releasing pressure, and storing at 0-4 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the enzyme solution includes 1.7-1.9 g/l protease, and 3-3.4 g/l cellulase, and the fresh poultry eggs are soaked in enzyme solution for 31-50 seconds, and the soaking temperature is 26-29 degrees C. In the step (2), the concentration of carbon dioxide in the egg liquid reaches 4650 mmol/l. The egg liquid includes 0.01-0.02% epsilon -polylysine hydrochloride, and 0.008-0.01% Nisin. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the ultraviolet sterilization time is 30-40 minutes, and the ultraviolet wavelength is 240-280 nm. In the step (2), the ultrasonic power is 30-50 W, and the temperature is 25-28 degrees C. In the step (3), the carbon dioxide pressure is 11-15 MPa, the stirring speed is 120-135 revolutions/minute, the temperature is 38-42 degrees C, and the sterilization treatment time is 60-70 minutes.

   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent response reversibility, emulsifying property and dispersing property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rosin-based carbon dioxide/nitrogen responsive surfactant i.e. 3-(((1S,4R,5R,9R,10R,12R,13R,17R)-15-(3-(dimethylamino)propyl)-5,9-dimethyl-14,16-dioxo-19-(propan-2-yl)-15-azapentacyclo(10.5.2.0(1,10).0(4,9).0(13,17))nonadec-18-en-5-yl)carbonyloxy)-2-hydroxypropyl 2-methylprop-2-enoate of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for :
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) use of (I) as emulsifier to prepare responsive Pickering emulsion, comprising dispersing 0.1-1 wt.% (relative to the aqueous phase) nano-silica in different concentrations of (I) carbon dioxide aqueous solution, high shearing the equal volume of aqueous phase and oil phase at 8000-15000 revolutions/minute for 3-10 minutes, introducing nitrogen into the obtained emulsion at 40~60 degrees C, demulsifying the emulsion to immediately break the emulsion, again shearing carbon dioxide to form stable emulsion, where the particle size is basically the same as the previous one, in addition, the aqueous phase after demulsification can be used to emulsify the new oil phase, the particle size is basically the same as that of the first emulsification, and the responsive surfactant can be reused; and
   (3) use of (I) as dispersing agent to disperse carbon nano-tubes, comprising dissolving 50-100 mg (I) into 20-30 ml carbon dioxide aqueous solution, dispersing 5-10 mg carbon nano-tubes with dispersing agent, ultrasonic dispersing for 10-30 minutes, centrifuging at 5000-8000 revolutions/minute for 5-20 minutes to obtain carbon nano-tube dispersion, adding initiator 1-3 wt.% V50 initiator, reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 6-10 hours to make polymer surfactant entangled on the surface of carbon nano-tubes to obtain uniform and stable carbon nano-tube dispersion, reacting at 40-60 degrees C under introducing nitrogen into the dispersion, settling carbon nano-tubes out of the solution, passing through the carbon dioxide again at room temperature, dispersing again into the aqueous solution by gently shaking many times without re-ultrasound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) subjecting rosin and maleic anhydride produces maleic acid to addition reaction using acetic acid as solvent at 120-140 degrees C for 3-6 hours, cooling the reaction system, adding into a large amount of acetic acid, cooling and crystallizing, and filtering to obtain maleopimaric acid, where the ratio between rosin and maleic anhydride is 1:1-1.5, (ii) subjecting the maleopimaric acid with glycidyl methacrylate (GMA) to ring-opening esterification reaction using 0.5-2 wt.% catalyst triethylamine, 0.2-0.5 wt.% polymerization inhibitor hydroquinone, and toluene as solvent at 80-120 degrees C for 6-8 hours to obtain 3-(((1S,4R,5R,9R,10R,12R,13R,17R)-5,9-dimethyl-14,16-dioxo-19-(propan-2-yl)-15-oxapentacyclo(10.5.2.0(1,10).0(4,9).0(13,17))nonadec-18-en-5-yl)carbonyloxy)-2-hydroxypropyl 2-methylprop-2-enoate (MPAG), the amount ratio between maleic acid and GMA is 1:1-1.2, and (iii) imidizing MPAG with N,N-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine in anhydrous ethanol solvent at 60-80 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain final product, the amount ratio between MPAG to N,N-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine is 1:1-5.

PN US2018362835-A1; US10829683-B2
TI Continuously making high quality fracturing involves supplying first stream of liquid carbon dioxide, adding proppant to first stream, supplying second stream of water, routing second stream to well head, and mixing first and second streams.
AU KELLY R M
   ZADEH A E
   WEISE M K
AE KELLY R M (KELL-Individual)
   ZADEH A E (ZADE-Individual)
   WEISE M K (WEIS-Individual)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   KELLY R M (KELL-Individual)
   ZADEH A E (ZADE-Individual)
   WEISE M K (WEIS-Individual)
GA 2018A1730K
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously making (P1) a high quality fracturing having a high concentration of proppant, utilized in a fracturing operation, comprises: (a) supplying a first stream containing liquid carbon dioxide; (b) continuously adding proppant only to the first stream containing liquid carbon dioxide at a concentration up to 20 lbs/gal, and routing the combined first stream through a high pressure pump disposed in the conduit to a well head; (c) continuously supplying a second stream containing water through at least one high pressure pump and routing the second stream to the well head; and (d) mixing the first and second streams forming a high quality fracture fluid having a Mitchell quality of at least 50%.
   USE - The process are useful for continuously making a high quality fracturing having a high concentration of proppant, and controlling the manufacturing of a high quality fracturing fluid stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides a high quality fracturing composition with a Mitchell quality of 50-95% (preferably 70-90%), a proppant loading of 0-18 (preferably 3.75) lb/gal so that the slurry quality does not exceed 95%, preferably 90% (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a high quality fracturing composition having a Mitchell quality of 50-95%, a proppant loading of 0-18 lb/gal so that the slurry quality does not exceed 95%; and
   (2) controlling (P2) the manufacturing of a high quality fracturing fluid stream, comprising providing a first stream comprising liquid carbon dioxide, forming a second stream comprising water and other additives, adding proppant only to the first stream comprising liquid carbon dioxide and volumetrically displacing the liquid carbon dioxide in an equal amount to the volumetric amount of proppant added, and mixing the proppant and liquid carbon dioxide stream provided at a constant flow rate with the second stream provided at a constant flow rate forming a high quality fracturing fluid having a Mitchell quality of at least 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, the additives are mixed to the either the first or second stream. Preferred Composition: The composition has Mitchell quality of 70-90%, proppant loading of 3.75 lb/gal so that the slurry quality does not exceed 90%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

PN CN107583461-A
TI Preparing titanium-manganese co-doped nano zinc oxide useful in visible light photocatalyst for removing formaldehyde, comprises e.g. adding zinc nitrate and complexing agent into distilled water and cooling carbon dioxide gas into a liquid.
AU HE D
   XU S
   WANG J
   LIN L
   CHEN C
   ZHANG X
   JIN C
AE SHANGHAI NAXU IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201806787C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium-manganese co-doped nano zinc oxide comprises e.g. adding zinc nitrate, titanium tetrachloride, manganese chloride and complexing agent into distilled water, adjusting pH by adding acetic acid, stirring and dissolving, and adding complexing agent sand stirring to obtain a mixed solution, and adding above mixed solution into the supercritical reactor, opening the carbon dioxide cylinder, cooling carbon dioxide gas into a liquid by cold machine, pressurizing by high-pressure pump into reactor and releasing carbon dioxide gas by reducing the system pressure to normal pressure.
   USE - The co-doped nano zinc oxide is useful in the visible light photocatalyst for removing formaldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The co-doped nano zinc oxide has high formaldehyde removal efficacy, long-term effect in removal of formaldehyde, has no toxic and hazardous substances; and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing titanium-manganese co-doped nano zinc oxide comprises (i) adding zinc nitrate, titanium tetrachloride, manganese chloride and complexing agent into distilled water, adjusting the pH to 2-3 by adding acetic acid, stirring and dissolving, and adding complexing agent sand stirring to obtain a mixed solution, where the molar ratio of zinc nitrate and titanium tetrachloride is 1:0.01-1, the molar ratio of zinc nitrate and manganese chloride is 1:0.01-1, the molar ratio of zinc nitrate and complexing agent is 1:0.01-1, and the molar ratio of zinc nitrate and distilled water is 1:1-500; and (ii) adding the above mixed solution into the supercritical reactor, opening the carbon dioxide cylinder, cooling carbon dioxide gas into a liquid by the cold machine, pressurizing by the high-pressure pump into the reactor in 15-30 MPa at 30-100 degrees C for 1-48 hours and releasing carbon dioxide gas by reducing the system pressure to normal pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent comprises monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, sodium nitrilotriacetate (NTA), disodium EDTA or tetrasodium, diethylenetriaminepentacarboxylate (DTPA), tartaric acid, heptanoate, sodium gluconate, sodium alginate, ethylenediamine tetramethylene phosphonate (EDTMPS), diethylene triam.ine pentamethylene phosphonate (DETPMPS) and/or amine trimethylene phosphate.

PN CN114261964-A
TI Processing of carbon dioxide raw material gas used in preparation of food grade, high purity carbon dioxide, involves removing hydrogen sulfide from compressed gas under action of first desulfurization agent under action of second desulfurization agent organic sulfur converted into inorganic sulfur.
AU ZHOU X
   JIANG J
   ZHANG X
   REN J
   CHEN Y
AE HUNAN KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202251471Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves (i) mixing a raw material gas, oxygen and high hydrogen content of carbon dioxide gas, increasing a volume content of the hydrogen in the obtained mixed gas by 1.3-1.7 % compared with the volume content of the hydrogen in the raw material gas; (ii) compressing the mixed gas, obtaining a pressure of the compressed gas is 2.1-2.5MPa; (iii) removing hydrogen sulfide from the compressed gas under the action of a first desulfurization agent under the action of the second desulfurization agent the organic sulfur is converted into inorganic sulfur; (iv) removing an inorganic sulfur under the action of a third desulfurization agent obtaining a desulfurization gas; (v) generating a mixed gas comprising high temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor, heat exchange with part of gas in the desulfurization gas; and (vi) evaporating and concentrating to obtain the carbon dioxide gas with high hydrogen content.
   USE - Method for processing of carbon dioxide raw material gas used in preparation of food grade, high purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the reaction heat of the hydrogen to improve the temperature rise of the bed layer, so as to reduce the load of the electric heater before dealkylation reactor. The method of high efficiency desulphurization reduces the concentration of the total sulfur before de-alkylating, improves the service life of the de-alkylation catalyst, at the same time, reduces the risk of de-lithation pre-heater sulfuride stress corrosion, and reduces the energy consumption. Part of the raw material gas after desulfurization does not enter into the drying process after removing water, and directly liquefies into the middle storage tank, because the boiling point of hydrogen is far lower than carbon dioxide, the hydrogen is Flash concentrated in the middle tank, the Flash steam contains 7% hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for processing carbon dioxide raw material gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the processing of carbon dioxide raw material gas (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN108728607-A; CN108728607-B
TI Performing carbon dioxide-argon refining method in bottom of dynamic LF furnace involves using control system which is based on carbon content of molten steel in furnace refining station, and determining blowing parameters of carbon dioxide.
AU ZHU R
   WEI G
   DONG K
   LIU R
   WU W
   HAN B
   WU X
   TANG T
   JIANG J
   PENG Y
   DONG J
   TIAN B
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2018872236
AB    NOVELTY - Performing a carbon dioxide-argon refining method in bottom of dynamic LF furnace involves using control system which is based on carbon content (%C)0 of molten steel in LF furnace refining station, determining bottom blowing process parameters of carbon dioxide and argon in the refining process, and blowing carbon dioxide and argon into inner bottom of the ladle through LF bottom blowing control valve group. The control system relies on the monitoring data of the differential pressure detection system of the furnace opening to control the gas injection flow rate of the furnace to ensure the micro-positive pressure in the refining process. The furnace gas composition analysis system analyzes the furnace gas composition in the refining process on-line, and the molten steel composition prediction module is based on the conservation of the argon trace.
   USE - Method for performing carbon dioxide-argon refining method in bottom of dynamic LF furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform carbon dioxide-argon refining method in bottom of dynamic LF furnace, which dynamically adjusts bottom blowing flow rate of carbon dioxide and argon and the blowing flow rate of furnace shielding gas in stages, strengthens molten pool in ladle to achieve rapid deep desulfurization, reduced nitrogen in molten steel, improves quality of molten steel, uses carbon dioxide in place of argon to complete refining operation, which reduces argon consumption and reduces production cost, shortens LF furnace refining cycle by 3-6 minutes, reaches desulfurization rate by over 90%, and reduces molten steel nitrogen content by 10-30x 10-6.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves using furnace gas composition data, inbound molten steel composition data, and gas injection flow data of refining process to calculate the predicted carbon content and nitrogen content of the molten steel. The control system dynamically fine-tunes carbon dioxide and argon bottom blowing flow according to molten steel composition prediction data, strengthens molten pool agitation, improves kin furnace refining reaction kinetic conditions, and realizes deep desulfurization and denitrification of molten steel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves calculating instantaneous production quantity of furnace gas of LF refining furnace according to argon trace conservation, and calculating denitrification and carbon content of molten steel in refining process according to the analysis data of furnace gas composition, and then predicting composition of molten steel. The instantaneous contents of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon in the furnace gas are Cg-carbon dioxide, Cg-co, Cg-N2 and Cg-Ar, respectively. The furnace protection gas blowing medium is carbon dioxide gas, and flow rate is QP-carbon dioxide. The bottom blowing gas is sprayed with a mixture of carbon dioxide and argon, and the flow rate is QB, where contents of carbon dioxide and Ar are CB-carbon dioxide and CB-Ar, respectively. The method involves calculating instantaneous flow rate QF-gas of furnace gas according to QB.CB-Ar=QF-gas.Cg-Ar, and calculating instantaneous nitrogen content of furnace gas according to QN2=QF-gas.Cg-N2 , and denitrification amount of the molten steel in refining process can be obtained by integral calculation, and then an instantaneous decarburization amount of the molten pool in the refining process is determined according to the calculation, which is considered as decarburization amount of the molten pool and the carbon addition amount of electrode, where change of the carbon content of the molten steel in the refining process is calculated according to delta(%C)=W electrode-Sd(%C)dt. The method is carried out by using steel liquid component data acquisition module to obtain the molten steel composition data of converter or electric arc furnace and quality and composition data of the smelting materials added in ladle between the tapping and LF furnace, and calculating the LF furnace refining inbound steel based on the conservation of materials, and then liquid composition data is transmitted to the control system. The pre-stage slagging is carried out by using control system, which controls the action of the furnace protection gas control valve group according to the micro-pressure difference data of the furnace opening, and controls the furnace gas flow rate 0-50Nm3/h on-line to ensure the furnace positive pressure in the refining process. The control system dynamically adjusts the bottom blowing process parameters of carbon dioxide and argon in the pre-stage slag stage according to the carbon content (%C)0 of molten steel in the LF furnace refining station, and when (%C)0 is not less than 0.40 the initial flow rate of bottom blowing gas is 10-20NL/(minute.t), and ratio of volume flow rate of carbon dioxide in bottom blowing gas is 80-99%, when 0.25 is less than or equal to 0.40 then initial flow rate of bottom blowing gas is 10-20NL/(minute.t), and ratio of the volume flow rate of the carbon dioxide in bottom blowing gas is 60-80%, and when (%C)0 is less than 0.25 then initial flow rate of bottom blowing gas is 10-20 NL/(minute.t), and ratio of volume flow rate of carbon dioxide in bottom blowing gas is 40-60%. The mid-stage desulfurization stage is carried out by using control system, which controls the action of the furnace protection gas control valve group according to micro-pressure difference data of the furnace mouth, and controls the furnace gas flow rate 0-50Nm3/h on-line to ensure furnace positive pressure in the refining process. The control system based is on furnace gas analysis data and considers the electrode carbon addition where according to the molten steel composition prediction module the carbon content (%C) of molten steel in LF furnace refining process is calculated, and bottom blowing of carbon dioxide and Ar in medium-term desulfurization stage is dynamically adjusted. Process parameters, and when (%C) is not less than 0.40 the initial flow rate of bottom blowing gas is 5-15NL/(minute.t), and ratio of volumetric flow rate of carbon dioxide in bottom blowing gas is 80-99%, and when (%C) is less than or equal to 0.40 then initial flow rate of bottom blowing gas is 5-15NL/(minute.t), and ratio of volume flow rate of the carbon dioxide in bottom blowing gas is 60-80%, and when (%C) is less than 0.25 then initial flow rate of the bottom blowing gas is 5-15NL/(minute.t), and ratio of volume flow rate of carbon dioxide in the bottom blowing gas is 40-60%. The late soft blowing stage is carried out by using control system which controls operation of the furnace protection gas control valve group according to micro-pressure difference data of furnace opening, and controls the furnace gas flow rate 0-100Nm3/h online to ensure the furnace positive pressure in the refining process. The bottom blowing gas is a mixture of carbon dioxide and argon, volume ratio of carbon dioxide in the blowing gas is 0-30%, and bottom blowing flow rate is 0.5-5NL/minute.t until the end of refining. The control system calculates denitrification amount of molten steel in refining process according to furnace gas analysis data of the LF furnace refining process and the gas injection data.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A05; M24-C01; M24-E

   SUN T

   ZHENG W
AE WENZHOU JNBAN LIGHT IND MACHINERY CO LTD (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 202158481S
AB    NOVELTY - Complete system comprises a gas storage tank, a tank body and a carbon dioxide compressor, where the gas storage tank is arranged on one side of the tank body, and the carbon dioxide compressor is arranged in the storage tank above the air box. The box body is provided with an extraction box, a low-pressure separation chamber, a bearing plate, a heating plate, a base, a pump box, a liquid suction pump, a liquid suction pipe, a liquid delivery pipe, a low pressure oil and gas separator, an oil leakage pipe, the oil suction pump, the second pump box, the oil suction pipe, the filter cotton layer, the oil discharge pipe, the control box, the controller, the groove and the liquid booster pump. The control box is arranged on the upper wall of the box body, and the controller is arranged in the control box inside.
   USE - The system is useful for refining high-purity cannabidiol in industrial hemp (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system and refining process produce cannabidiol with high yield and high purity, have simple process, produce low solvent toxicity, and ensure green environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Complete system comprises a gas storage tank, a tank body and a carbon dioxide compressor, where the gas storage tank is arranged on one side of the tank body, and the carbon dioxide compressor is arranged in the storage tank above the air box. The box body is provided with an extraction box, a low-pressure separation chamber, a bearing plate, a heating plate, a base, a pump box, a liquid suction pump, a liquid suction pipe, a liquid delivery pipe, a low pressure oil and gas separator, an oil leakage pipe, the oil suction pump, the second pump box, the oil suction pipe, the filter cotton layer, the oil discharge pipe, the control box, the controller, the groove and the liquid booster pump. The control box is arranged on the upper wall of the box body, and the controller is arranged in the control box inside. The carrying plate is arranged on the inner wall of the box, the base is arranged on the carrying plate, the groove is arranged on the base, the groove is a hollow cavity with an open upper end, and the heating plate is arranged on the bottom wall of the groove. The extraction box is arranged on the base, the heating end of the heating plate is connected to the bottom of the extraction box, the liquid booster pump is arranged on the side wall of the extraction box, and the power output end of the liquid booster pump is connected with the extraction box. The liquid suction pump is arranged on the supporting plate on one side of the base, the liquid suction pipe is connected between the power output end of the liquid suction pump and the extraction box, and the low-pressure separation chamber is arranged in the box. The carbon dioxide compressor is provided with a liquid outlet pipe, a suction pipe and a one-way valve. The liquid outlet pipe penetrates the side wall of the box body and is connected to the extraction box. The gas valve pipe is connected between the gas storage tank and the low-pressure separation chamber, and the cylindrical cavity is arranged near the gas valve pipe. In the air valve pipe at one end of the low-pressure separation chamber, the truncated cone-shaped cavity is connected to the side of the cylindrical cavity away from the low-pressure separation chamber. The control ball is arranged in the truncated cone-shaped cavity, and the radius of the contact portion between the truncated cone-shaped cavity and the control ball is less than the radius of the control ball, a gas outlet pipe is arranged between the cylindrical cavity and the gas output end of the low pressure oil and gas separator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for process for refining high-purity cannabidiol in industrial hemp, comprising (i) washing and drying the industrial hemp, and then grinding into a powder to ensure that there is no impurities in the industrial hemp; (ii) opening the box door, turning the box door around the door axis to open, inspecting the internal equipment of the box, closing the box door after the inspection is completed, opening the valve and adding carbon dioxide gas into the gas storage box through the gas filling pipe, and closing the valve after the addition is complete, unscrewing the lid and adding the industrial hemp powder into the extraction box through the powder inlet, after the addition is complete, tightening the lid and power on the equipment, compressing into carbon dioxide liquid and transporting to the extraction tank; (iii) pressing the compression button to control the start of the carbon dioxide compressor through the controller, where the carbon dioxide compressor compresses the carbon dioxide gas in the gas storage tank into carbon dioxide liquid through the suction pipe and transports it to the extraction tank through the liquid outlet pipe, extracting the industrial hemp powder, pressing the boost button to start the liquid booster pump of the controller, which pressurizes the carbon dioxide liquid, pressing the heating button to control the heating plate to start, heating to heat the carbon dioxide liquid so that the carbon dioxide is in a supercritical state, dissolving the supercritical carbon dioxide in the extraction tank and collecting plant extracts; (iv) allowing the extract to enter the bottom of the low-pressure separation chamber after filtering through the leaking pipe and falling into the filter cotton layer, where the carbon dioxide gas separated by the low-pressure oil and gas separator enters the cylindrical cavity through the outlet pipe, pushing the control ball to move away from the cylindrical cavity, connecting the gas valve pipe, and the gas flows from the cylindrical cavity into the gas storage box, when the gas outlet pipe stops delivering gas, the air pressure in the cylindrical cavity is lower than the air pressure in the gas storage box, thus the control ball is pushed to move to the side close to the cylindrical cavity, and the control ball fits the cylindrical cavity to block the cylindrical cavity, so that the gas in the air storage tank cannot return; and (v) collecting hemp oil containing a small amount of water, allowing the oil-water mixture to stand still, after the stratification is completed, the oil generally has a low density and floats on it, pressing the oil suction button to control the start of the suction pump through the controller, using the suction pump to suck out the water in the bottom of the low-pressure separation chamber through the oil discharge pipe through the suction pipe, draining the water, pressing the suction button to control the suction pump to turn off by the controller, and then pressing the suction button again to control the start of the suction pump through the controller, sucking out the cannabis oil in the low-pressure separation chamber through the drain pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the compete system.

GA 2022911329
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises an ionic membrane                electrolytic hydrogen production system comprising                a saturated sodium chloride solution refining and                concentrating device and a demineralized water                adding device prepared by hydrogen and caustic                soda. An ion membrane electrolytic hydrogen                production device comprising sequentially                distribution of cathode (19), sodium hydroxide for                a cation exchange membrane (20), an anion exchange                membrane (21), a cation exchange membrane (22) and                an anode (23) by hydrochloric acid. The inlet of                the channel formed between the cation exchange                membrane and the anode is provided with the inlet                of a device for removing the salt water. The outlet                of the sodium hydroxide solution collecting and                conveying device and the outlet of the hydrochloric                acid solution collecting and conveying device are                collected with the carbon dioxide. The inlet of the                storage system is connected with the inlet of the                conveying device.
   USE - The system is useful for using new energy to                produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide capture                coupling.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can green hydrogen, at the same                time solves the problem that the traditional carbon                dioxide capture technology energy consumption is                large and the cost is high. When the specific                operation, ionic membrane electrolytic hydrogen                production and carbon dioxide trapping and coupling                application in the system, the system can be                operated in the counter-current mode, by                controlling the conveying flow of the hydrochloric                acid solution, making excessive carbon dioxide                react with sodium hydroxide solution to generate                sodium bicarbonate solution, to remove carbon                dioxide. When it is necessary to collect carbon                dioxide, the sodium chloride diluted solution with                ion exchange is recycled to the saturated sodium                chloride solution refining concentration device                through sodium chloride dilute solution collecting                and conveying device to refine and                concentrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                coupling application of new energy hydrogen                production and carbon dioxide deposition.

TI Carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst comprises active component, and carrier, active component is nickel oxide, carrier is titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide composite oxide modified with lanthanum oxide.
AU LIAN Y
   WU H
   HUO X
   LAI W
   WANG L
   YU H
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2020445963
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst comprises active component, auxiliary agent and carrier. The active component is nickel oxide, auxiliary agent is M, M is La, Ce, Mn, Fe, Co, Ga, Ca, Mg and Zn oxide. The carrier is titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide composite oxide modified with lanthanum oxide, aluminum oxide and N, N is oxide of lanthanum, cerium, manganese, iron, cobalt, gallium, calcium, magnesium and zinc. The carrier is prepared from raw materials includes soluble lanthanum salt, soluble N precursor salt, pseudo-boehmite, dilute acid, vegetable gum, titanium oxide and Zr(OH)4.
   USE - Carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst is cheap and easily obtained raw materials, has characteristics of high activity, good low temperature activity, catalyst repeatability, industrial application prospects, strong anti-toxicity, simple preparation method and easy operation, control of reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst, which involves kneading soluble lanthanum salt, soluble N precursor salt, pseudo-boehmite, dilute acid and vegetable gum to obtain aluminum sol, adding titanium dioxide powder and Zr(OH)4 powder, kneading synthesis type, after keeping at room temperature, drying and baking to obtain carrier;
   (2) formulating soluble nickel salt and soluble M precursor salt into solution, impregnating above carrier, allowing to keep and drying at room temperature, roasting material obtained to obtain carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst, heating to 450-650 degrees C at heating rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute, roasting for 4-8 hours; and
   (3) a method for performing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalytic process, which involves using carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst as catalyst, composition of reaction gas contains carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen in ratio of 5-8:1, ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 10-12:1, space velocity of reaction gas is 5000-20000/h, reaction temperature is 250-500 degrees C, reaction time is 1-3 hours, and reaction pressure is 0.5-2 megapascal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide is rutile titanium dioxide and/or anatase titanium dioxide. The dilute acid is dilute nitric acid and/or acetic acid. Preferred Compositions: The carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide co-methanation catalyst comprises 10-30 wt.% nickel oxide, 50-80 wt.% titanium dioxide, 1-5 wt.% lanthanum oxide, 1-10 wt.% aluminum oxide, 1-5 wt.% M, 1-10 wt.% N, balance is zirconium oxide.

PN WO2020071107-A1; CN112752603-A; DE112019004951-T5; US2021197118-A1; BR112021005265-A2; JP2020550263-X
TI Gas separation for carbon dioxide in mixed gas, by preparing separation film composite with pores on porous support, supplying mixed gas having carbon dioxide and other gas to film composite to remove carbon dioxide to obtain permeated gas.
AU NODA K
   YAJIMA K
   SHIMIZU K
AE NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
   NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
   NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
GA 2020276295
AB    NOVELTY - Gas separation method involves (a) preparing a separation film composite in which a separation film having pores having an average pore diameter of 1 nm or less is formed on a porous support and (b) supplying a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and another gas from the separation film side to the separation film composite to remove carbon dioxide in the mixed gas, and obtaining a permeated gas by permeating the separation film and the support. The step (b) is performed in a state which a temperature on at least a portion of the permeation side surface from which the permeation gas is discharged among the support is 10 degrees C or more lower than the temperature of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite.
   USE - The method and device is used for separating carbon dioxide in a mixed gas. The method and device is used for separating carbon dioxide in combustion exhaust gas from a thermal power plant and separating carbon dioxide in various other mixed gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves carbon dioxide selection capability of the separation film. The method suppresses cooling of the impermeable gas and reduces energy required for cooling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas separation device including a separation film composite in which a separation film having pores with an average pore diameter of 1 nm or less is formed above a porous support, and a gas supplying unit which supplies a mixed gas containing carbon dioxde and other gases to the separation film composite from the separation film side. The carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is separated from the mixed gas as a result of passing through the separation film and the support in a condition in which the temperature of at least a portion of a permeation side surface of the support from which the gas having passed through the separation film is discharged is at least 10 degrees C lower than the temperature of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the temperature of the entire surface of the permeation side of the support is 10 degrees C or more lower than the temperature of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite. In step (b), the temperature of at least a portion of the permeation side surface of the support is 15 degrees C or more lower than the temperature of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite. In step (b), the pressure of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite is 1 MPa or more. In step (b), the temperature of the impermeable gas of the mixed gas discharged without passing through the separation film and the support is higher than the temperature of the permeation side surface of the support, and the temperature is lower than the temperature of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite. preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide concentration in the permeated gas obtained in step (b) is higher than the carbon dioxide concentration in the mixed gas. Preferred Components: The separation film is an inorganic membrane, preferably zeolite membrane. The other gas contains one or more gases chosen from hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxide, ammonia, sulfur oxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur fluoride, mercury, arsine, hydrogen cyanide, carbonyl sulfide, 1-8C hydrocarbons, organic acids, alcohols, mercaptans, esters, ethers, ketones and aldehydes. Preferred Properties: The maximum number of ring members of zeolite constituting the separation film is 8 or less. The water content of the mixed gas before supplying to the separation film composite in step (b) is 3000 ppm or less.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114507177-A
TI Preparing functionalized methyl            2-(3-oxoisoindoline-1-ylidene)acetate by carbon            dioxide, comprises e.g. reacting o-alkynyl amide and            carbon dioxide as raw material under the action of            alkali, N,N-dimethylformamide in oil bath, then adding            methyl iodide and continuously reacting.
AU LI M
   BAI J
   SHA Y
   SUN S
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202272245H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing functionalized methyl                2-(3-oxoisoindoline-1-ylidene) acetate by carbon                dioxide, comprises taking o-alkynyl amide and                carbon dioxide as raw material, then reacting under                the action of alkali, N,N-dimethylformamide in                60&#8451; oil bath for 12 hours, then adding                methyl iodide and continuously reacting for 1 hour                to obtain the product.
   USE - The functionalized methyl                2-(3-oxoisoindoline-1-ylidene)acetate by carbon                dioxide is useful in the fields of medicine,                organic chemical industry and fine chemical                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high yield and simple                post-treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pressure of the                  carbon dioxide is 0.1 MPa. The o-alkynyl amide is                  2-ethynylbenzamide compound of formula (I). The                  alkali is potassium carbonate or cesium                  carbonate, and the consumption of alkali is 3                  times of the mole number of o-alkynyl amide. The                  molar ratio between methyl iodide and o-alkynyl                  amide is 4:1.R1= benzyl, methyl, naphthyl, isopropyl, or                  H;R2= aryl, heteroaryl, naphthyl, alkyl, or                  alkenyl; andR3= methyl, methoxy, halo or                  trifluoromethyl.

The pyrolysis is done at 300degrees Celsius for 1 hour.

PN DK201970047-A; DK180253-B
TI Method for removing liquid from lumen of pipe by back-pulse flushing that is utilized for cleaning long dead-end pipes, involves adding carbon dioxide in liquid or supercritical state to pipe, followed by reducing pressure.
AU HOG THOMSEN J P
   AMUNDSEN E K
   THOMSEN J P H
AE OCEAN TEAM GROUP AS (OCEA-Non-standard)
GA 202094782E
AB    NOVELTY - A method for removing liquid from a lumen of a pipe by a back-pulse flushing involves adding carbon dioxide in liquid or supercritical state to a pipe, reducing the pressure after diffusion of the carbon dioxide through the liquid, such that the pressure reduction changes the carbon dioxide into expanding gas that presses the matter out of the pipe at the same end into which the carbon dioxide is inserted.
   USE - Method for removing liquid from lumen of pipe by back-pulse flushing that is utilized for cleaning long dead-end pipes, for example hydraulic control pipes for valves in offshore installations, especially in oil and gas industry.

PT P
PN US2019106716-A1; WO2019071172-A1; EP3692008-A1; US11008591-B2; EP3692008-A4
TI Biocementation involves e.g. decomposing calcium carbonate to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, reacting calcium oxide with ammonium chloride to form calcium chloride and ammonia gas and reacting ammonia with carbon dioxide to get urea.
AU DOSIER G K
   DOSIER J M
   SMITH K J
AE BIOMASON INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
   BIOMASON INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
   BIOMASON INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
GA 201932175E
AB    NOVELTY - Biocementation (m1) comprises: decomposing calcium carbonate to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide; reacting calcium oxide with ammonium chloride to form calcium chloride and ammonia gas; reacting ammonia with carbon dioxide in a process to form urea; and reacting urea with calcium chloride to form biocement.
   USE - The methods are useful for biocementation.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods increase sustainability, extend performance, work with local feedstock components, reduce production costs, and are rapidly deployable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biocementation (m2) comprising decomposing calcium carbonate to form calcium chloride and carbon dioxide, reacting calcium dioxide with ammonium in a process to form urea, and reacting calcium chloride with urea to form biocement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The urease-producing organisms comprise spores and/or vegetative cells. The urease-producing organisms comprise at least one of Sporosarcina spp., S. pasteurii, S. ureae, Proteus spp., P. vulgaris, P. mirabilis, Bacillus spp., B. sphaericus, B. megaterium, Myxococcus spp., M. xanthus, Helicobacter spp., H. pylori and/or their variants, serotypes, mutations, recombinant forms.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In method (m1), the decomposing step comprises treating calcium carbonate with elevated temperatures or an acid. In method (m2), the step of decomposing calcium carbonate is performed at elevated temperature or with an acid. The method (m2) further comprises decomposing ammonium chloride to form acid and ammonia. The methods further comprise reacting urea with calcium chloride in the presence of urease-producing organisms and supplemental materials. The elevated temperatures comprise greater than or equal to 850 degrees C. The process comprises elevated pressure and/or corona discharge. The step of reacting urea with calcium chloride further comprises urease and/or urease-producing organisms. The step of reacting urea with calcium chloride further forms ammonium chloride. Preferred Components: The acid comprises hydrochloric acid. The supplemental materials comprise organic or inorganic material, rock, glass, wood, paper, metal, plastic, polymers, fibers and/or minerals.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN108726910-A
TI Reinforcing recycled concrete coarse aggregate by using carbon dioxide comprises e.g. crushing the waste concrete, screening, spraying nano silicon dioxide slurry, drying and carrying out carbon dioxide strengthening treatment.
AU WU S
   ZHU L
   ZHANG G
   LI X
   ZHANG Y
   XU G
   ZHANG Z
AE ZHENGZHOU AERONAUTICS INST (UZZA-C)
GA 2018872574
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforcing recycled concrete coarse aggregate by using carbon dioxide comprises (i) firstly crushing the waste concrete by hand, then crushing by a jaw crusher, and screening to obtain recycled coarse aggregate, (ii) spraying the prepared nano silicon dioxide slurry on the regenerated coarse aggregate prepared in the step (i), and drying by airing, (iii) placing the recycled coarse aggregate prepared in the step (ii) in a self-made carbonization device for reinforcing to obtain high quality recycled concrete aggregate, and (iv) taking 100% recycled coarse aggregate after the step (iii), and carrying out carbon dioxide strengthening treatment with natural aggregate to obtain recycled concrete.
   USE - The method is useful for reinforcing recycled concrete coarse aggregate by using carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the recycled coarse aggregate has a particle size of 4.75-25 mm. In step (ii), the nano-silicon dioxide slurry has a concentration of 0.1-0.4%, and is intended to fill large pores and cracks in the recycled aggregate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method comprises spraying the phenolphthalein reagent on the surface of the regenerated coarse aggregate, if the recycled aggregate is completely carbonized, the surface of the aggregate does not change color; if the recycled aggregate is partially carbonized, and the surface of the aggregate becomes red. The water absorption rate of the recycled coarse aggregate decreased by 18.98-34.17%, the crushing index is reduced by 13.79-31.03%, the void ratio is reduced by 3.85-13.47%, and the apparent density increased by 1.13-4.55% after the intensive treatment. Preferred Components: In step (iii), the self-made carbonization device is composed of a carbon dioxide storage tank 1, a gas pressure regulating valve 2, a gas delivery pipe 3, a dial 4, and a hermetic container 5. The regenerated coarse aggregate is placed in the closed container 5. In step (iii), the carbon dioxide gas is stored in a carbon dioxide storage tank. The hermetic container 5 is connected to the carbon dioxide storage tank 1 by a gas delivery pipe 3. The gas pressure regulating valve 2 is disposed on the gas delivery pipe 3. In step (iii), the carbonization pressure is controlled by the gas pressure regulating valve 2, and the carbonization pressure is 10-150 KPa. The dial 4 controls the duration of carbonization, in particular, the duration of carbonization is from 10-90 minutes.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN CN108743505-A
TI Preparing post-sun repairing lotion involves crushing the coix seed with the shell to coix seed crude extract, pumping coix seed crude extract into carbon dioxide-containing rectification column for reverse contact of carbon dioxide.
AU SHAN X
AE WUHU HUIYU COMMERCE & TRADE CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201888747C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing post-sun repairing lotion involves crushing the coix seed with the shell to 30-50 mesh coix seed crude extract, pumping coix seed crude extract into a carbon dioxide-containing rectification column for reverse contact of carbon dioxide with a crude extract of coix seed at an extraction pressure of 25-45 megaPascals and 40-75 degrees C. The carbon dioxide and coix seed crude oil are continuously fed, and the mass flow rate ratio of the carbon dioxide to the barley crude oil is 8 or more, and extraction time is 3 hours or more and the flow rate is 100-1000 liters/hour. The extraction, acid bentonite is used for decolorization, vacuum deodorized, boiled in vacuum boiling water, degassed and dehydrated by vacuum heating, removed the substances and odor and moisture which cause the increase of peroxide price to obtain extract of coix seed. 2-4 pts. wt. Coix seed extract, 2-4 seed medium propolis extract, 0.5-1 pts. wt. protease, 0.5-1 pts. wt. aminopeptidase are added.
   USE - Method for preparing post-sun repairing lotion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing post-sun repairing lotion which has strong oxidation resistance, effect of inhibiting tyrosinase activity and obvious whitening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing post-sun repairing lotion involves crushing the coix seed with the shell to 30-50 mesh coix seed crude extract, pumping coix seed crude extract into a carbon dioxide-containing rectification column for reverse contact of carbon dioxide with a crude extract of coix seed at an extraction pressure of 25-45 megaPascals and 40-75 degrees C. The carbon dioxide and coix seed crude oil are continuously fed, and the mass flow rate ratio of the carbon dioxide to the barley crude oil is 8 or more, and extraction time is 3 hours or more and the flow rate is 100-1000 liters/hour. The extraction, acid bentonite is used for decolorization, vacuum deodorized, boiled in vacuum boiling water, degassed and dehydrated by vacuum heating, removed the substances and odor and moisture which cause the increase of peroxide price to obtain extract of coix seed. 2-4 pts. wt. Coix seed extract, 2-4 pts. wt. seed medium propolis extract, 0.5-1 pts. wt. protease, 0.5-1 pts. wt. aminopeptidase, 1-2 seed medium chamomile extract, 1-2 pts. wt. seed medium licorice extract, 1-3 pts. wt. seed medium yeast polypeptide, 2-4 pts. wt. Fucus serrata extract, 1-3 pts. wt. seed medium oligopeptide-20, 0.5-1 pts. wt. carbomer, 0.6-2 pts. wt. aminomethylpropanol, 3-6 pts. wt. seed medium propylene glycol, 3-6 pts. wt. seed medium 1,3-butane Alcohol, 0.5-1 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol-40 hydrogenated castor oil, 0.5-1 pts. wt. enamel oil, 0.5-1 pts. wt. octanoic acid glyceride, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. hydroxybenzyl ester, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. allantoin, 0.02-0.1 pts. wt. Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium, 0.03-0.1 pts. wt. seed medium sodium hyaluronate, and 70-100 pts. wt. seed medium deionized water, are mixed, deionized water is weighed and added into the water phase pot, and Ethylenediaminetetraacetic disodium, sodium hyaluronate, carbomer are added, stirred and dissolved at 60 degrees C, and then propylene glycol, 1,3-butanediol, methylparaben, allantoin are added at 60 degrees C for 30 minutes. The materials are added in the water phase pot is pumped into a vacuum homogenizing, emulsifier, and the lotus root extract, propolis extract, protease, aminopeptidase, chamomile extract are sequentially added under stirring, licorice extract, yeast peptide, Fucus fuliginea extract, oligopeptide-20 are added and stirred for 30 minutes, mixed well, cooled to 45 degrees C, polyethylene glycol-40 hydrogenated castor oil, enamel oil, caprylic acid glyceride are added and stirred well. The mixture is cooled to 35 degrees C, aminomethylpropanol is added, stirred well, cooled to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The propolis extract is nano-supercritical propolis. The nano-supercritical propolis is a nano-supercritical propolis processed by phospholipid nano-encapsulation technique. The filtering is three-layer gauze filtration. The three layers of gauze are filtered and then filtered through a membrane filter having a pore size of 0.2 mu m. The pulverized sieve is a sieve of 40 mesh. Preferred Process: The licorice extract is prepared by pulverizing and sieving 5 g of the medicinal material to obtain a powder. The body is added to 0.2 moles/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, mixed at a ratio of solid to liquid of 1:10, and extracted by hot dip at 80 degrees C for 2 h, repeated twice, filtered, and the filtrate is added with a mass concentration of 3% hydrochloric acid. The pH is adjusted to 1-2.5, centrifuged at 4000 revolutions/minute, and the supernatant is dehydrated to obtain a licorice extract. The chamomile extract is prepared by pulverizing and sieving 5 g chamomile to obtain a powder, adding 0.2 moles/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, mixing at a ratio of solid to liquid of 1:10, extracting by hot dip at 80 degrees C for 2 hours for 2 times, filtering, adding 3% hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH of the filtrate to 1-2.5, centrifuging at a rate of 4000 revolutions/minute. The supernatant is dehydrated to obtain chamomile extract. The Fucus serrata extract is prepared by washing, placing in a ventilated and dry place for air drying, placed in the microwave oven after the air-drying hornweed for 4 minutes on medium heat. The dried horned hornweed is placed in a pulverizer to be crushed, and the pulverized teeth are pulverized. The hornweed has 60 mesh sieve, and the horn horn algae powder is obtained. 10 grams horn algae powder and 100 grams deionized water are added to the container, and extracted at 90 degrees C for 100 minutes. The materials are taken out, cooled to room temperature, filtered with a cryogenic refrigerating centrifuge, the rotation speed is 3000 revolutions/minute and centrifuged for 5 minutes. The supernatant is collected and filtered to obtain the extract of Fucus sinensis.

PT P
PN CN111019627-A; CN111019627-B
TI Drag reducing agent useful for reducing resistance            during liquid carbon dioxide fracturing, comprises            polyether polyol, polydimethylsiloxane and poly(lauryl            methacrylate).
AU SUN X
   XU Q
   TIAN S
   MU J
   GUO X
   WANG H
AE SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202033636M
AB    NOVELTY - Drag reducing agent comprises polyether                polyol, polydimethylsiloxane and poly(lauryl                methacrylate). The mass ratio between polyether                polyol and poly(lauryl methacrylate) is 1:1-6:1.                The volume ratio between polyether polyol                containing poly (lauryl methacrylate) and                polydimethylsiloxane in solution is 2:1-4:1.
   USE - The drag reducing agent is useful for reducing                resistance during liquid carbon dioxide fracturing                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The drag reducing agent: solves the problem of                large resistance during the supercritical carbon                dioxide fracturing process without increasing the                viscosity of the supercritical carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the drag reducing agent,                comprising mixing polyether polyol with                polydimethylsiloxane, and then mixing with                poly(lauryl methacrylate); and
   (2) use of the drag reducing agent for                reducing resistance during liquid carbon dioxide                fracturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The molecular weight                  of the polyether polyol is 1000-2000. The                  polyether polyol is poly(propylene glycol),                  polytetrahydrofuran, poly(propylene                  oxide-co-tetrahydrofuran) or modified polyether                  polyol. The viscosity of the polydimethylsiloxane                  at 25 degrees C is 5000-10000. The molecular                  weight of the poly(lauryl methacrylate) is                  1500-3000. In the use method, the mass ratio of                  the drag reducing agent to liquid carbon dioxide                  is 0.3-0.5%:1. Preferred Method: The poly(lauryl                  methacrylate) is prepared by dissolving 30-60 ml                  lauryl methacrylate and 10-30 ml divinylbenzene                  in 80-100 ml N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain                  mixed solution, dissolving 2-4 g                  azobisisobutyronitrile in 20-50 ml absolute                  ethanol and dropping it into the mixed solution                  while stirring, heating at 70-90 degrees C to                  react under protection of nitrogen, cooling,                  washing, and freeze-drying to obtain block-shaped                  poly(lauryl methacrylate). In the preparation of                  poly(lauryl methacrylate), the reaction                  temperature time is 4-6 hours.

GA 202010539D

The mass of calcium acetate in the catalytic composition is 1-3% of dry fine coal powder.

PT P
PN WO2019043875-A1
TI Manufacture of ammonia used as raw material for producing e.g. fertilizer, involves reforming natural gas, converting carbon monoxide and steam to hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and using reduced carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide as feed gas.
AU TSUTOMU K
   YASUSHI F
   YOSHIYUKI W
   MOMOKO K
AE JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
GA 201922991X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of ammonia involves subjecting high nitrogen-containing natural gas (A) comprising 4 vol.% or more nitrogen to reforming (R1) using steam to obtain hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide from hydrocarbons contained in the natural gas (A), converting carbon monoxide and steam to hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a shift reaction, and using the reduced carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide as ammonia synthesis feed gas.
   USE - Manufacture of ammonia used as raw material for producing fertilizer and chemical product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically manufactures ammonia by effectively using nitrogen rich-natural gas, reduces need of combustion treatment for nitrogen rich gas and methane loss, and improves profitability of the high nitrogen-containing natural gas field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: A natural gas (B) is subjected to nitrogen disaggregation process which separates the natural gas (B) into a nitrogen rich gas having more nitrogen concentration than prior to processing, and a purified gas having low nitrogen concentration than prior to processing. The process uses one portion or all portion of nitrogen rich gas as ammonia synthesize raw material. The nitrogen rich gas, purified gas and the high nitrogen-containing natural gas (A) which did not undergo nitrogen disaggregation process are mixed to obtain a ratio of hydrogen concentration to nitrogen concentration in the ammonia synthesis raw material of 2-4:1. Hydrogen is separated and recovered from the recycled gas containing an unreacted source gas component recovered after ammonia synthesis reaction, and the hydrogen rich gas and/or hydrogen rich gas received from outside the facility (excluding the air introduced as an oxidizing agent at the time of reforming) are introduced into the source gas to adjust the hydrogen to nitrogen concentration in the ammonia synthesize feed gas to 2-4:1. The process performs reforming (R2) process using oxygen-enriched air as an oxidizing agent to reduce methane contained in the raw material. The nitrogen rich gas is not used as a fuel for modification process. Ammonia is manufactured in the high nitrogen-containing natural gas before supplied to the pipeline.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E35
MC C05-C01; C14-T03; E32-A01
IP C01B-003/38; C01B-003/48; C01B-003/50; C01C-001/04

A                repairing agent conveying device is provided with a                water pump and a conveying hose. A grouting                repairing device is provided with a liquid                injecting gun. The source of calcium-based                industrial solid material such as carbide slag, fly                ash, steel slag and so on is close to the discharge                source of the carbon dioxide, and many industrial                solid wastes are typical fine crystals, it has high                reaction specific surface area, almost no                pre-treatment, low production cost, green and                environment-friendly, and the preparation process                is simple. The repairing agent has high fluidity,                can penetrate the fine crack deep in the concrete                structure, and does not need to process the crack                surrounding area before grouting, the original                structure will not be damaged.

PN CN212725378-U; CN113555533-A
TI Graphene electrode manufacturing device, has tunnel kiln provided with conveying belt for bearing and conveying porous substrate, and carbon dioxide laser irradiator fixed in tunnel kiln for providing carbon dioxide laser irradiation to porous substrate.
AU YAO P
   XU P
AE APEX GRAPHENE TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (APEX-Non-standard)
GA 202131392R
AB    (CN212725378-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a graphene electrode manufacturing device, the manufacturing device comprises: a tunnel kiln, in which is provided with a conveying belt for conveying a porous substrate with a plurality of pores; a plurality of ultrasonic atomizer, set in the tunnel kiln, for setting a liquid polyimide, and making the liquid polyimide to spray to the porous base material; a plurality of carbon dioxide laser irradiator, set in the tunnel kiln, for providing carbon dioxide laser irradiation of the porous base material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for manufacturing an graphene electrode. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   tep for taking the liquid polyimide (201)
   Step for moving the porous coating substrate in a tunnel kiln by a conveyor belt (202)
   Step for forming a polyimide layer of the porous coating substrate into a high-purity porous graphene layer (203)
EA    NOVELTY - The method involves taking (201) the liquid polyimide, the liquid polyimide is formed into mist particles by ultrasonic spray, and a porous substrate is infiltrated with multiple pores. A porous substrate isn covered with a polyimide layer on the periphery of the pores to form a porous coating substrate. The porous coating substrate is moved (202) in a tunnel kiln by a conveyor belt, and a carbon dioxide laser irradiator is arranged inside the tunnel kiln. Multiple carbon dioxide laser irradiators continuously irradiate the porous coating substrate passing through the tunnel kiln with carbon dioxide lasers. The polyimide layer of the porous coating substrate is formed (203) into a high-purity porous graphene layer, and a porous graphene substrate is formed.
   USE - Method for manufacturing graphene electrode such as negative electrode of lithium ion battery or negative electrode of a super capacitor lithium battery by ultrasonic spraying applied to semiconductor, touch panel or solar battery field, photoelectric, green power generation, environmental medical sensing and composite functional material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves excellent production economic benefit. The method reaches the excellent economic benefit of graphene electrode manufacturing production.

PN CN109022121-A
TI Process for extracting Cinnamomum camphora oil involves soaking Cinnamomum camphora seeds in water for washing, filtering, adding petroleum ether, heating and distilling for separating petroleum ether to obtain Cinnamomum camphora oil.
AU LUO J
   ZHANG T
AE ZIYUN AUTONOMOUS COUNTY ZIXIANGYUAN AGRI (ZIYU-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5153K
AB    NOVELTY - The process for extracting Cinnamomum camphora oil involves soaking Cinnamomum camphora seeds in water, washing and drying, crushing, putting into round filter paper bag, putting filter paper bag into cable extractor, putting petroleum ether into cable extractor, heating, filtering, drying, distilling collected liquid to obtain C.camphora oil, putting oil cake into extraction reactor having initial temperature of -30 to -15 degrees C, increasing the temperature for 3 hours, and increasing temperature in reaction vessel to 0-5 degrees C, connecting carbon dioxide gas cylinder, pressurizing the extraction reactor, entering carbon dioxide into extraction reactor for liquefaction reaction, forming fluid, opening reaction vessel separation system valve, separating C.camphora oil, flowing carbon dioxide into extraction reactor for recycling, and flowing out C.camphora oil from separation system, and performing centrifugal purification.
   USE - Process for extracting C.camphora oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of Cinnamomum camphora oil, increases the unit extract of camphor oil, and obtains maximum use of C.camphora seeds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The process for extracting Cinnamomum camphora oil involves soaking Cinnamomum camphora seeds in water at 30-50 degrees C for 4 hours, washing and drying, putting the washed C.camphora seeds into crusher for crushing, putting into round filter paper bag with thin thread, putting the filter paper bag into cable extractor, putting petroleum ether into cable extractor, heating at 40-60 degrees C for 6-8 hours, removing the filter paper bag, filtering out the liquid, putting the oil in an oven for drying, distilling collected liquid in distiller for separating petroleum ether to obtain C.camphora oil, putting oil cake into extraction reactor having initial temperature of -30 to -15 degrees C, slowly increasing the temperature for 3 hours, and increasing temperature in reaction vessel to 0-5 degrees C, connecting carbon dioxide gas cylinder, pressurizing the extraction reactor to 10 atmospheric pressure, entering carbon dioxide into extraction reactor for liquefaction reaction, forming fluid using oil and liquid carbon dioxide, opening extraction reaction vessel separation system valve, decreasing solubility of carbon dioxide when pressure is reduced, separating C.camphora oil, flowing carbon dioxide into extraction reactor for recycling, and flowing out C.camphora oil from the separation system, and subjecting C.camphora oil to centrifugal purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The petroleum ether is 2-3 times the weight of C.camphora seed. Preferred Conditions: The temperature in the distiller is maintained at 80-90 degrees C, and the water level is maintained at 1/3 or more of pot portion.

PN WO2018211231-A1; GB2564725-A; EP3634134-A1
TI System for stunning animal with carbon dioxide or            mixture of carbon dioxide with other gas used for food,            has gas in chamber to be removed from chamber and            directed back to recycled gas container and/or gas            reservoir to be re-used.
AU HURFORD E B
AE GALLUS SOLUTIONS LTD (GALL-Non-standard)
   GALLUS SOLUTIONS LTD (GALL-Non-standard)
GA 201893185V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a chamber (6) for exposing the                animal used for food to a gas or gas mixture. An                inlet (5) enables ingress of a gas or gas mixture                into the chamber. A gas exchange unit arranged to                allow gas to enter and/or exit the chamber.                Multiple gas combinations provided in respective                pre-mixed gas combination containers (1-4) which                pre-mixed gas combination containers are                connectable to the chamber. A gas reservoir (8,9)                containing a gas and connected to the pre-mixed gas                combination containers. An outlet removes a gas or                gas mixture from the chamber. The gas exchange                means causes the chamber to be filled with multiple                pre-mixed gas combinations and/or the gas to allow                an animal used for food to be exposed and then                causes the gas in the chamber to be removed from                the chamber and directed back to a recycled gas                container and/or the relevant pre-mixed gas                combination container and/or the gas reservoir to                be re-used.
   USE - System for stunning animal with carbon dioxide                or mixture of carbon dioxide with other gas used                for food.
   ADVANTAGE - The delays in sensor detection of increase in                carbon dioxide levels are also avoided because the                stunning gas combination is pre-mixed to precise                levels and therefore there is no need to provide                the system with a mixing time window. The system of                the present invention reduces the carbon footprint                of known systems indirectly by reducing the number                of deliveries of carbon dioxide required by a plant                comprising the system when compared to a plant with                system having a single-use carbon dioxide format.                The pressure pump heating is prevented, thus                ensuring that the system is as efficient as                possible. The breathing is extracted or removed by                extraction unit to ensure that carbon dioxide                levels inside the chamber 6 remain constant,                reduces animal chemoreceptors being activated and                causing stress to the animal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for stunning animals used for food.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system                for stunning animals.
   Containers (1-4)
   Inlet (5)
   Chamber (6)
   Gas reservoir (8,9)
   Valve (10)
