PT P
PN CN113563189-A
TI Preparing dimethyl carbonate by one-step high-efficient catalysis of carbon dioxide conversion, comprises reacting propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol in presence of imidazolium salt based homogeneous ionic liquid catalyst.
AU GAO Y
   XU G
   WEI W
   WANG Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 2021C80387
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dimethyl carbonate by one-step high-efficient catalysis of carbon dioxide conversion, comprises reacting propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol as raw materials in presence of homogeneous ionic liquid catalyst to obtain high yield of dimethyl carbonate. The catalyst does not need to be separated in two-stage chain reaction process. The catalyst firstly catalyzes the synthesis of propylene carbonate from propylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the first stage of the fixed-bed reactor; the catalyst is not separated and mixed with methanol to cool down, and then it is placed the second stage reaction-rectification tower, and the dimethyl carbonate-methanol azeotrope is produced at the top of the tower. The tower kettle is 1,2-propylene glycol, which can synthesize dimethyl carbonate with high yield of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide in one step; and the catalyst is an ionic liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dimethyl carbonate by one-step high-efficient catalysis of carbon dioxide conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces structure of ionic liquid with high thermal stability and strong alkalinity, which contains 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole imidazole salt, has alkali strength of 18.4-22.3, equivalent to sodium tert-butoxide, 2.83 mmol/g alkali content, and decomposition temperature of more than 144degrees Celsius, achieves high yield (36.27%) of propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol raw materials to synthesize dimethyl carbonate in one step with a turn over number value of 37.04; achieves 97.4 % propylene oxide conversion and close to 97.40% propylene carbonate yield under mild conditions with a turn over number value of 99.49; has simple process, high thermal stability and excellent catalytic activity; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dimethyl carbonate by one-step high-efficient catalysis of carbon dioxide conversion, comprises reacting propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol as raw materials in presence of homogeneous ionic liquid catalyst to obtain high yield of dimethyl carbonate. The catalyst does not need to be separated in two-stage chain reaction process. The catalyst firstly catalyzes the synthesis of propylene carbonate from propylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the first stage of the fixed-bed reactor; the catalyst is not separated and mixed with methanol to cool down, and then it is placed the second stage reaction-rectification tower, and the dimethyl carbonate-methanol azeotrope is produced at the top of the tower. The tower kettle is 1,2-propylene glycol, which can synthesize dimethyl carbonate with high yield of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide in one step; the catalyst is an ionic liquid; and the ionic liquid includes cations and anions; and both the anion and the cation contain a nitrogen-containing heterocyclic ring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is performed at 100-130degrees Celsius for 2-9 hours, preferably 120degrees Celsius for 9 hours, the initial carbon dioxide pressure is 1.8-3.8 MPa, preferably 2.6 MPa, and the amount of catalyst is 0.5-10% of the raw material propylene oxide mass, preferably 3%; the reaction temperature during the transesterification is 68degrees Celsius; the cation is substituted methylimidazolium compound of formula (I) or substituted 4-methylmorpholin-4-ium compound of formula (II); the anion is imidazol-3-ide, morpholin-4-ide or pyrrol-1-ide; the molar ratio between propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol in the raw material containing propylene oxide, carbon dioxide and methanol is 1:1:10-1:2.8:15; the molar ratio of propylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the raw materials containing propylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 1:1-1:2.8; the molar ratio of propylene carbonate to methanol in the raw material containing propylene carbonate and methanol is 1:10; and the reaction reaches a chemical equilibrium at 68degrees Celsius for 30 minutes. The catalyst is imidazolium salt based homogeneous ionic liquid catalyst. The preparation of imidazolium salt based homogeneous ionic liquid catalyst comprises (a1) adding alkali into anion source of ionic liquid containing solution I, and reacting to obtain anion metal salt of ionic liquid, (a2) dissolving the ionic liquid anionic metal salt in a solvent, adding the ionic liquid cation salt, and reacting to obtain ionic liquid. The method process includes a two-stage chain reaction process in which the catalyst does not need to be separated, where the catalyst firstly catalyzes the synthesis of propylene carbonate from propylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the first stage of the fixed-bed reactor; then, the catalyst is not separated and mixed with methanol to cool down and directly enters the second stage reaction-rectification tower, and the dimethyl carbonate-methanol azeotrope is extracted from the top of the tower. The tower kettle is 1,2-propylene glycol, which can synthesize dimethyl carbonate with high yield of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide in one step.R1 = 1-6C alkane groups, 2-6C alkenyl or 3-6C aryl hydrocarbon groups, preferably CH3, -CH2CH3, -(CH2)2CH3 or -(CH2)3CH3.Preferred Components: In step (a1), the solvent in the solution I is ethanol, benzene, toluene and/or xylene; the alkali is organic alkali or inorganic alkali; the organic alkali includes sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium methoxide, potassium ethoxide or potassium tert-butoxide; the inorganic alkali includes lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or cesium hydroxide; the ionic liquid anion metal salt is ionic liquid anion lithium salt, anion sodium salt, anion potassium salt, and ionic liquid anion cesium salt; the concentration of ionic liquid anion source in the solution I is 0.05-0.8 g/ml; the molar ratio of the ionic liquid anion source to alkali is 0.9-1.1; the ionic liquid anion source includes imidazole, pyrrole or morpholine; the reaction is performed under ice bath for 24 hours; and the reaction further includes removing the solvent to obtain an imidazole anion salt, a pyrrole anion salt or a morpholine anion salt after the reaction is completed. In step (a2), the solvent includes a water-carrying agent; the water-carrying agent is methanol, ethanol, benzene, toluene and/or xylene; the ionic liquid cationic salt is 1-R1-3-methyl-imidazolium bromide salt, 1-R1-3-methyl-imidazolium iodide salt, N-methyl-N-R2-imidazolium bromide and/or N-methyl-N-R2-imidazolium iodide salt; the ratio of ionic liquid anionic metal salt to solvent is 0.1-0.9:0.05-1.2 g/ml; and the reaction is performed at room temperature for 24 hours. The step (a2) further includes removing the solvent to obtain the high-purity ionic liquid after the reaction is completed.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Selectively permeable membrane for separating carbon dioxide from methane in gaseous stream, comprises carbonization product of sulfonated poly(phenylene ether) copolymer.
AU SEKAR A K
   CHOWDHURY R
AE SHPP GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SHPP GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202281809X
AB    NOVELTY - The selectively permeable membrane has a carbonization product of a sulfonated poly(phenylene ether) copolymer. The membrane has a carbon dioxide permeability, a selectivity for carbon dioxide to methane of greater than 40, preferably 40-150, more preferably 50 to 150, as measured at 50 degrees C and 791 kPa in single gas testing conditions and a methane slip of less than 0.6 vol.%.
   USE - Selectively membrane for separating carbon dioxide from methane in a gaseous stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The selectively permeable membrane has high selectivity for the desired gas, high permeation flux, or throughput, and a long service life under harsh conditions, e.g. high temperatures and exposure to corrosive gases, so that replacement costs are minimized. It is economical and environmentally sustainable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for separating carbon dioxide from methane in a gaseous stream; and
   (2) an article for separating carbon dioxide from methane in a gaseous stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a selectively permeable membrane.

PN JP2022068715-A
TI Power generation system for manufacturing chemical substance, has chemical substance manufacturing unit that uses medium exhausted from carbon dioxide turbine as raw material gas to produce chemical substance.
AU OBARA R
   YOSHINO M
AE TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 202265832J
AB    NOVELTY - The power generation system comprises a power generation unit (100) that is used for generating power and a chemical substance manufacturing unit (200) is used for manufacturing the chemical substance. The power generation unit is a combustor (160) and a carbon dioxide turbine (110) driven by a medium including supercritical carbon dioxide supplied from the combustor. A generator (111) is used for generating power by driving the carbon dioxide turbine. The chemical substance manufacturing unit uses a medium exhausted from the carbon dioxide turbine as a source gas.
   USE - Power generation system for manufacturing chemical substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The power generation system is capable of efficiently realizing reduction of cost and reduction of a load on the environment when manufacturing a chemical substance such as fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Power generation unit (100)
   Carbon dioxide turbine (110)
   Generator (111)
   Combustor (160)
   Chemical substance manufacturing unit (200)

PN CN114243145-A
TI Recycling waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte useful in digital industry, comprises e.g. removing electric core, carrying out ultrasonic treatment, supercritical carbon dioxide static extraction, performing dynamic cyclic extraction of supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain electrolyte material.
AU ZHANG Z
   LIU H
AE SHENZHEN AIPAISI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202249384H
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte comprises (i) removing electric core, placing the obtained positive plate, negative plate and electrolyte in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction reactor, carrying out ultrasonic treatment and supercritical carbon dioxide static extraction; and (ii) performing dynamic cyclic extraction of supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain electrolyte raw materials. The electrolyte comprises organic solvent, electrolyte salt and additive. The organic solvent comprises ethylene carbonate, propylene carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, methyl ethyl carbonate, methyl propyl carbonate, and isopropyl carbonate, the electrolyte salt comprises lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium tetrafluoroborate, lithium perchlorate and lithium hexafluoroarsenate(V) and the additive comprises vinylene carbonate, biphenyl, and dimethyl sulfoxide.
   USE - The waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte is useful in digital industry and electric industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses ultrasonic auxiliary semi-static-semi-dynamic combined supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method, which can improve the extraction rate with simple operation, economical and high efficiency, and environmentally-friendly.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

AU HUANG Y
   LIU Q
AE ZHAOQING NANDU REGENERATION ALUMINIUM (ZHAO-Non-standard)
GA 202234919E
AB    NOVELTY - A detection alarm system of aluminium ingot production workshop, comprising a shell structure, an air inlet structure set on one end of the shell structure and a control system set on the shell structure, the shell structure comprises a shell, the control system comprises a processor, a carbon dioxide sensor fixed in the shell, a dust sensor and a first alarm fixed outside the shell; a second alarm, the processor is electrically connected with the carbon dioxide sensor, a dust sensor, a first alarm and a second alarm. The utility model is provided with a carbon dioxide sensor and a dust sensor in the shell, so as to detect the concentration of carbon dioxide and dust pumped into the air in the shell; when the concentration is high, it can control the first alarm and the second alarm to alarm, so as to timely send the alarm, ensuring the physical and psychological health of the worker.

PN JP2021113182-A
TI New polyethylene polyamine derivative which is derivative of polyethylene polyamine having specific nitrogen atoms, and (ii) derivative in which hydrogen atoms of primary amino group are substituted with alkyl group, used in amine composition for carbon dioxide absorption and dissipation agent.
AU SAKODA K
   AWANO H
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 2021897736
AB    NOVELTY - A polyethylene polyamine derivative which is (i) a derivative of polyethylene polyamine having 3 or more nitrogen atoms, and (ii) a derivative in which one or both hydrogen atoms of 20-100% of the primary amino group are substituted with an alkyl group having 1-6C (excluding the isopropyl group) among the primary amino groups of polyethylene polyamine having 3 or more nitrogen atoms, which is a precursor, is new.
   USE - The polyethylene polyamine derivative is used in an amine composition for a carbon dioxide absorption and dissipation agent (all claimed) for separating and recovering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyethylene polyamine derivative has excellent carbon dioxide absorption/emission efficiency (carbon dioxide absorption/emission amount per unit weight), is easy to acquire industrially, and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an amine composition comprising polyethylene polyamine derivative and water; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide absorption and dissipation agent comprising the amine composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorption and dissipation agent is made of silica, alumina, magnesia, porous glass, activated carbon, polymethylmethacrylate-based porous resin, or fiber carrying at least the polyethylene polyamine derivative.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The polyethylene polyamine derivative is a derivative in which one or both hydrogen atoms of 40-70% of the primary amino group are substituted with an alkyl group having 1-6C (excluding an isopropyl group) among the primary amino groups of polyethylene polyamine having 3 or more nitrogen atoms, which is a precursor. The polyethylene polyamine having 3 or more nitrogen atoms is a polyethylene polyamine having 4 or more, preferably 6 or more nitrogen atoms. The alkyl group having 1-6C (excluding isopropyl group) is a methyl group, an ethyl group, a propyl group, and a sec-butyl group. Preferred Composition: The amine composition comprises 5-70 wt.% polyethylene polyamine derivative.
   EXAMPLE - The examples describe the synthesis of precursor of the polyethylene polyamine derivative, so do not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)

AB    NOVELTY - A thermal insulation wall of refrigeration device comprises an inner layer, an insulating layer, an outer layer of refrigeration device from inside to the outside, and hydrate interlayer located between the inner layer of the refrigeration device and insulating layer. The hydrate interlayer is filled with generated liquid and carbon dioxide gas. The hydrate formed in hydrate interlayer is solid carbon dioxide hydrate with unit cell structure.
   USE - Thermal insulation wall of refrigeration device.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal insulation wall is economical and has simple structure, and can continue refrigeration even after power cut-off, without causing pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A thermal insulation wall of refrigeration device comprises an inner layer, insulating layer and outer layer of refrigeration device from inside to the outside, and hydrate interlayer located between the inner layer of the refrigeration device and insulating layer. The hydrate interlayer is filled with generated liquid and carbon dioxide gas. The hydrate formed in hydrate interlayer is solid carbon dioxide hydrate with unit cell structure of 8(51262).16(512).136H2O.

PN JP2022137740-A
TI Producing carbon dioxide-reducing photocatalyst            particles by providing gallium oxide particles and            silver source, adding gallium oxide particles and            silver source to reducing solution, ultrasonically            irradiating, and subjecting metallic silver-supported            gallium oxide particles to chromium treatment.
AU NISHIMOTO H
   TANAKA T
   TERAMURA K
AE SUMITOMO METAL MINING CO LTD (SUMM-C)
   UNIV KYOTO (KYOU-C)
GA 2022C5354B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing carbon dioxide-reducing                photocatalyst particles, involves (a) providing                gallium oxide particles and a silver source, (b)                adding the gallium oxide particles and silver                source to a reducing solution to prepare a reaction                solution, (c) ultrasonically irradiating the                reaction solution to produce metallic                silver-supported gallium oxide particles, and (d)                subjecting the metallic silver-supported gallium                oxide particles to chromium treatment, and                producing gallium oxide particles bearing metallic                silver nanoparticles and silver chromate                nanoparticles.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon                dioxide-reducing photocatalyst particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces carbon dioxide-reducing                photocatalyst particles with improved carbon                monoxide selectivity, and sufficiently reduced                silver nanoparticles can be supported without                heating after ultrasonic treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver source                  comprises silver oxide. Preferred Conditions: The                  ultrasonically irradiating frequency is 28-45                  kHz, and is performed for 1-10 hours. The                  metallic silver-supported gallium oxide particles                  before chromium treatment are heat-treated at                  100&#176; C or higher in an oxygen-containing                  atmosphere. The gallium oxide particles                  supporting metallic silver are soaked in an                  aqueous solution in which a chromium compound is                  dissolved to precipitate the chromium compound on                  the surface of the gallium oxide particles                  supporting metallic silver, when performing                  chrome treatment, and the chromium compound is                  precipitated, and subjecting the metallic                  silver-supported gallium oxide particles to a                  heat-treatment at 400-700&#176; C for 1-10                  hours. The average particle size of the metallic                  silver nanoparticles is 10-50 nm. The silver                  chromate nanoparticles have an average particle                  size of 1-30 nm. The supported amount of the                  silver component is 0.3-10 %mass with respect to                  the gallium oxide particles in terms of metallic                  silver. The photocatalyst particles have a carbon                  monoxide selectivity of 30% or more in a carbon                  dioxide reduction photocatalytic performance                  evaluation test.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing liquid                  contains an alcohol.

   ZHANG Y
   TIAN L
   ZHAO M
   CHANG Y
   CHANG J
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202283396Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of polyphenols in mung bean                husk involves using mung bean skin as a main                material, adopting enzymatic hydrolysis, adopting                ultrasonic microwave-assisted technology and                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology,                obtaining active components in mung bean skin and                obtaining mung bean polyphenol products by rotary                evaporation for concentration and low temperature                freeze drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing polyphenol in                mung bean peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the time for extracting                polyphenols by supercritical carbon dioxide, can                effectively improve the yield of polyphenols in                mung bean hulls by using three methods of enzymatic                method, microwave and ultrasonic technology and                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology,                which is the highest in mung bean hull and adopts                efficient high-yield extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the method, the main                  material is a by-product mung bean skin in mung                  bean processing, and after the microwave                  ultrasonic-assisted treatment, the active                  ingredients in the mung bean skin are fully                  released, and finally, supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction is used to obtain a product                  with excellent functional activity retention                  effect, where the enzymatic hydrolysis uses                  cellulase and pectinase to destroy the cell wall                  structure of the mung bean skin, and the                  ultrasonic microwave-assisted technology enables                  the cell wall in the mung bean skin to be further                  destroyed. The preparation involves (a) sieving                  the purchased mung bean skin, preliminarily                  screen out dust and small particles of                  impurities, after washing by water, dry at low                  temperature and crushing it to 50-70 meshes, (b)                  weighing a certain amount of mung bean skin                  powder, adding water to obtain material to liquid                  ratio of 1:8-1:15 and mix well, (c) heating the                  mixture to 45-60&#8451;, adjusting the pH                  value to 6-8, then add 7-10 mg/mL cellulase and                  3-7 mg/mL pectinase for enzymolysis, and                  enzymolysis time is 10-30 minutes, (d) processing                  for 7-15 minutes under the condition of microwave                  and ultrasonic power respectively 500-900 W and                  500-1000 W, (e) concentrating the mixed solution                  obtained in (d)is concentrated and freeze-dried                  to obtain a semifinished product, (f) extracting                  the product obtained in (e) by using carbon                  dioxide as extraction agent, the extraction                  temperature 40-55&#8451;,the extraction time                  is 60-100 minutes, the extraction pressure is                  30-45 MPa, and the entrainer is extracted under                  the condition of 1-4 mL/min of entrainer, (g)                  separating and obtaining the extract, freeze                  drying to obtain the polyphenol active substance                  in the mung bean skin. In the process, the                  selected raw material is the by-product in the                  process of the mung bean processing, so the                  polyphenol content will have large loss, but also                  the cost is low, enzymolysis and ultrasonic                  microwave auxiliary technology effectively                  improves the extraction rate of polyphenol,                  making up the shortage of raw material, and                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction can                  realize higher efficiency extraction, at the same                  time, protecting the effective component.Preferred Method:

TI Sorbent useful for carbon dioxide capture, comprises solid support with carbon dioxide-sorbing amine and ionic liquid.
AU ZHOU S J
   GUPTA R P
AE SUSTEON INC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 2022276866
AB    NOVELTY - A sorbent useful for carbon dioxide (CO2) capture, comprises solid support with CO2-sorbing amine and ionic liquid.
   USE - A sorbent useful for carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbents are characterized by high CO2 selectivity and high CO2 capacity, and can be regenerated at temperatures below 100 degrees C in repeated sorption/desorption cycles, with high desorption rate and retention of high CO2 selectivity and CO2 capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of CO2 capture sorbent;
   (2) method of CO2 capture, which involves contacting a CO2-containing gas with the sorbent to produce CO2-reduced gas, and sorbent having CO2 adsorbed on it; and
   (3) CO2 capture system comprising at least one sorption vessel containing a CO2 capture sorbent comprising a solid support with CO2-sorbing amine and ionic liquid, where the vessel is arranged for contacting of CO2-containing gas with the sorbent and discharge of CO2-reduced contacted gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Solid support comprises one or more material(s) selected from carbon, silica, porous silicon, zeolites, metal oxides, mixed metal oxides, aluminosilicates, metal organic frameworks (MOFs), clays, mesoporous materials, fabrics, non-woven materials, ceramic monoliths, metal monoliths, ceramic-metal monoliths, polymers, porous polymer networks, and mixtures, alloys, and their combinations. Solid support comprises silica, alumina, zirconia, or titania, preferably silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Solid support comprises one or more metal organic frameworks (MOFs) selected from Zn4O(BTE)(BPDC), where BTE is 4,4',4''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyl-tris(ethyne-2,1-diyl))tribenzoate, and BPDC is biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate; Zn4O(BTB)2, where BTB is 1,3,5-benzenetribenzoate; Zn4O(BBC)2, where BBC is 4,4',4''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyl-tris(benzene-4,1-diyl))tribenzoate; Zn4O(BDC)3, where BDC is 1,4-benzenedicarboxylate; Mn3((Mn4Cl)3(BTT)8)2, where BTT is benzene-1,3,5-tris(1H-tetrazole); and Cu3(BTC)2(H2O)3, where BTC is 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid. CO2-sorbing amine comprises one or more amine(s) selected from primary, secondary, and tertiary alkylamines and alkanolamines, aromatic amines, mixed amines, and/or polyamines. CO2-sorbing amine comprises one or more amine(s) selected from monoethanolamine (MEA), triethanolamine (TEA), diethanolamine (DEA), diethylenetriamine (DETA), 2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol, diisopropanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol, pentaethylenehexamine, tetramethylenepentaamine, tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), polyallylamines, aminosilanes, tetraalkoxysilanes, aminoalkylalkoxysilanes, hyperbranched aminosilica (HAS), and/or polymeric amines. Ionic liquid comprises one or more ionic liquid(s) selected from ammonium-, imidazolium-, phosphonium-, pyridinium-, pyrrolidinium-, and sulfonium-based ionic liquids. Ionic liquid comprises one or more ionic liquid(s) selected from ionic liquids comprising one or more of cations, such as imidazolium, pyridinium, pyrrolidinium, phosphonium, ammonium, sulfonium, and associated organic or inorganic anions, selected from alkylsulfate of formula R-O-S(=O)2-O- (I), tosylate, methanesulfonate, bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, hexafluorophosphate, tetrafluoroborate, and halide. Ionic liquid comprises one or more ionic liquid(s) selected from 1-decyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate; 1-ethyl-3-methylpyridinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-ethylpyridinium bromide; 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium triflate; 1,2-dimethyl-3-propylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1,2-dimethyl-3-propylimidazolium bromide; 1,2-dimethyl-3-propylimidazolium iodide; 1,2-dimethylimidazole; 1,2-dimethylimidazolium chloride; 1,2-dimethylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1,3-diethylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1,3-diethylimidazolium bromide; 1,3-diethylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate; 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-allyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-benzyl-3-methylimdiazolium 1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethanesulfonate; 1-benzyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-butyl-1-methylpiperidinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-decyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate; 1-dodecyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-ethyl-1-methylpyrrolidinium hexafluorophosphate; 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate; 1-ethyl-4-methylpyridinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-heptyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-hexadecyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate; 1-hexyl-1-methylpyrrolidinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; 1-propyl-4-methylpyridinium bromide; bis(1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium) tetrathiocyanatocobaltate; diethylmethylsulfonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; trihexyltetradecylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide; and triphenylcarbenium tetrakis(perfluoro-tert.-butoxy) aluminate. Ionic liquid comprises compound of formula (II). Ionic liquid comprises a substituted imidazolium group and a bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide group, where substituent(s) of the substituted imidazolium group are each selected from among organo substituents. Sorbent comprises 1-5000 ppm by weight, preferably 1-100 ppm by weight ionic liquid, based on total weight of the amine present on the solid support. CO2-containing gas is air. CO2-containing gas is supplied from a combustion process. CO2-containing gas comprises effluent from an electrical power-generating plant. CO2-containing gas comprises effluent from a motive vehicle. CO2-containing gas is supplied from an oxidation process. CO2-containing gas comprises CO2-containing gas produced by combustion of fossil fuel. CO2-containing gas comprises syngas from organic matter gasification. CO2-containing gas comprises blast furnace exhaust gas from steel making. CO2-containing gas comprises cement kiln exhaust gas. CO2-containing gas comprises one or more of: coal-fired power plant flue gas; natural gas combined cycle power plant flue gas; blast furnace exhaust gas; cement plant exhaust gas; natural gas fired once through steam generator gas; steam methane reformer syngas; steam methane reformer flue gas; steam methane reformer PSA byproduct gas; dry reforming syngas; integrated gasification combined cycles (IGCC) syngas; biogas; biomass gasification syngas; municipal waste gasification syngas; and/or atmospheric gas.R=not defined; andR1, R2=H, OH, F, Br, Cl, I, 1-12C alkyl, 1-12C alkoxy, 1-12C carboxy, 1-12C haloalkyl, 6-12C aryl, 6-14C arylalkyl, 5-10C cycloalkyl, optionally substituted amino, thiol, phosphate, sulfate, phosphonate, and/or sulfonate.Preferred Properties: Ionic liquid is interactive with the CO2-sorbing amine to enhance at least one of the sorbent characteristics of (i) CO2 sorption capacity, (ii) CO2 sorption rate, (iii) CO2 desorption capacity, (iv) CO2 desorption rate, and (v) regeneration temperature, in relation to a corresponding sorbent lacking the ionic liquid. Preferred Processes: Manufacture of CO2 capture sorbent, comprises i.e.: (a) depositing CO2-sorbing amine and ionic liquid on a solid support; (b) depositing ionic liquid on a solid support having an amine on it; or (c) depositing CO2-sorbing amine on a solid support, to form an aminated support and depositing ionic liquid on the aminated support to form the CO2 capture sorbent comprising the solid support with the CO2-sorbing amine and ionic liquid on it. Depositing ionic liquid on the aminated support comprises contacting the aminated support with an alkanolic solution of the ionic liquid to impregnate the aminated support with the ionic liquid, recovering the ionic liquid-impregnated aminated support from the alkanolic solution, and removing alkanol from the recovered ionic liquid-impregnated aminated support to yield the CO2 capture sorbent comprising the solid support with the CO2-sorbing amine and ionic liquid on it. Removing alkanol from the recovered ionic liquid-impregnated aminated support comprises evaporating the alkanol from the recovered ionic liquid-impregnated aminated support. CO2 capture further comprises regenerating the sorbent having CO2 adsorbed on it, to desorb CO2 to form regenerated sorbent, and CO2 desorbate; and recovering the CO2 desorbate from the regenerated sorbent. CO2 capture is conducted in a multi-bed system comprising multiple beds of the sorbent arranged for continuous CO2 capture processing of the CO2-containing gas, where one or more of the multiple beds is on-stream for contacting of the CO2-containing gas with the sorbent, and another or others of the multiple beds is off-stream and while off-stream, regenerating and recovering are carried out, with each of the multiple beds undergoing sequential on-stream and off-stream operations in a cyclic repeating sequence for continuous CO2 capture processing of the CO2-containing gas. Oxidation process is a biological oxidation process. Preferred Systems: Multi-bed system is a pressure-swing adsorption (PSA) multi-bed system, thermal-swing adsorption (TSA) multi-bed system, or PSA/TSA multi-bed system. The vessel is constituted and arranged for regeneration of the sorbent after at least partial loading of CO2, resulting from the contacting. CO2 capture system comprises multiple sorption vessels constituted and arranged for cyclic repeating operation comprising adsorption operation and desorption regeneration operation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: CO2-sorbing amine comprises one or more polyalkyleneimine(s), preferably polyethyleneimine(s). CO2-sorbing amine comprises polyethyleneimine, tetraethylenepentamine, or polypropyleneimine.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R2=1-12C alkyl.

TI Carbon dioxide throughput based oil wellbore asphaltene deposition amount measuring method, involves establishing relational expression, and determining asphaltene deposition amount, target reservoir temperature and pressure difference.
AU YE Z
   WANG C
   SHI L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202228294H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a stratum parameter of a target reservoir. A rock sample is prepared. Oil sample saturation operation is performed on the rock sample. Displacement speed of an oil sample saturated rock sample is performed for performing carbon dioxide displacement experiment. Asphalt content in the oil sample is measured. Asphaltene content in the oil sample saturated rock sample is removed according to asphalt content in the oil sample. A relational expression is established. Asphaltene deposition amount, target reservoir temperature, pressure difference and displacement speed are determined.
   USE - Carbon dioxide throughput based oil wellbore asphaltene deposition amount measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increasing measuring accuracy, repeatability rate and realizing strong operability.

PN CN113117637-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide absorbing material comprises grinding waste cobalt acid lithium battery anode into powder, reacting with sodium hydroxide solution, drying, mixing with activated carbon, filtering, evaporating and calcining.
AU QIN C
   TONG Y
   RAN J
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202183874X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide absorbing material comprises (1) grinding the waste cobalt acid lithium battery anode into powder and reacting with sodium hydroxide solution for constant temperature reaction, drying the filter residue and uniformly mixing with the activated carbon, and calcining under inert atmosphere; (2) dispersing the material in distilled water, stirring and dissolving at room temperature, filtering for many times and evaporating to dryness to obtain material containing lithium carbonate; and (3) uniformly mixing lithium carbonate-containing material with silicon dioxide and calcining at high temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide absorbing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique, high carbon dioxide adsorption amount and can be recycled; can relieve the secondary pollution of waste cobalt acid lithium battery to the environment toa certain extent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1), the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 2-4 mol/l, the reaction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-2 hours; the mass ratio of dry filter residue to activated carbon is (4-10):1, and the reaction temperature in an inert atmosphere is 650-700 degrees C, and the reaction time is 30-60 minutes. In the step (2), the stirring speed is 400-600 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 1-2 hours. In step (3), the molar ratio of the lithium carbonate-containing material and the silicon dioxide is (2-2.1):1, the high temperature calcination reaction temperature is carried out at 700-800 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-6 hours.

PN CN112645338-A
TI Preparation of silica aerogel material used for absorbing carbon dioxide, involves mixing dimethyl sulfoxide and deionized water, adding aqueous ammonia to mixed solution, allowing wet gel to stand, washing with acetone, and drying.
AU PENG X
AE NACHENG XINGTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NACH-Non-standard)
GA 202148673L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of silica aerogel material involves mixing dimethyl sulfoxide and deionized water, adding methyltrimethoxysilane, mixing to obtain a mixed solution, adding aqueous ammonia to mixed solution for gelation to obtain a wet gel, allowing the wet gel to stand for aging, soaking in ethanol, washing with acetone, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
   USE - Preparation of silica aerogel material used for absorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed), and used in military and civilian application such as air purification and energy-saving recycling application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces silica aerogel material with excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance, super-hydrophobicity and fine structure by adjusting the amount ratio of methyltrimethoxysilane and dimethyl sulfoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for silica aerogel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The volume ratio of methyltrimethoxysilane, dimethyl sulfoxide and deionized water is 1:(3-9):(0.5-1.5), preferably 1:(5-7):(0.8-1.2). The concentration of aqueous ammonia is 0.5-1.5 mol/L. The volume ratio of methyltrimethoxysilane to aqueous ammonia is 5:(1-2). The silica aerogel material has a water contact angle of 150.1-160.2 degrees .

PN CN110563585-A; CN110563585-B
TI Preparing dimethyl carbonate, involves adding supported catalyst to fixed-bed reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, heating, adding methanol and reacting to obtain dimethyl carbonate, where catalyst includes active center and carrier.
AU LU J
   LIU S
   YAO S
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A66652
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing dimethyl carbonate, involves using carbon dioxide and methanol as raw materials, adding a catalyst to the fixed-bed reactor, introducing carbon dioxide purge, continuously introducing carbon dioxide, heating at 120-140 degrees C, maintaining a constant temperature, adding methanol under a pressure of 0.5-0.8 MPa and reacting completely to obtain the dimethyl carbonate, where the catalyst is a supported catalyst including an active center and a carrier, the active center is a transition metal, and the carrier comprises silica, alumina and alkali or alkaline earth metals.
   USE - Method for preparing dimethyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the activity of the catalyst using the double metal active center while improving the reaction conversion rate and the selectivity of the target product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carrier in the catalyst is prepared by mixing the alkali metal or alkaline earth metal precursor with the silica and alumina precursor aqueous solution at 20-85 degrees C under stirring conditions, adding acid to adjust the pH value to 0.5-5.5, continuously stirring, spray-drying the mixture to obtain spherical particles having a particle size of 50-100 mu m, and calcining under air atmosphere to obtain the carrier. Preferred Composition: The carrier in the catalyst comprises 45-90 %mass silicon dioxide and 5-30 %mass aluminum oxide. The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal in the carrier is 5-20 %mass. Preferred Components: The active center in the catalyst includes at least two of cobalt, copper, manganese, nickel and molybdenum. The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal in the carrier is rubidium, cesium, magnesium or strontium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction is carried out 140 degrees C under the pressure 0.6 MPa.

PT P
PN CN109734590-A
TI Synthesis of 2-bromo-2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate by carbon dioxide method involves preparing 1-fluoro-2-methoxybenzene, preparing 2-fluoro-3-methoxybenzoic acid, and preparing ethyl 2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate.
AU HU Z
   XU L
   HE D
   DU X
   QIAN Z
   HE Y
AE ANHUI HUASHENG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201945177S
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of 2-bromo-2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate (I) by carbon dioxide method involves preparing 1-fluoro-2-methoxybenzene (II), preparing 2-fluoro-3-methoxybenzoic acid (III), and preparing ethyl 2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate (IV).
   USE - Synthesis of 2-bromo-2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate by carbon dioxide method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses economical and easily available synthetic raw materials, and is simple and convenient to operate and environmentally-friendly. The method avoids the use of methanesulfonyl chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of 2-bromo-2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate of formula (I) by carbon dioxide method involves preparing 1-fluoro-2-methoxybenzene of formula (II), preparing 2-fluoro-3-methoxybenzoic acid of formula (III), and preparing ethyl 2-(2-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)acetate of formula (IV).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis of compound (I) preferably involves dissolving 0.5-1.5 g, preferably 1.0 g of 1.0 equivalent compound (II) in 2-8 mL, preferably 2 mL of tetrahydrofuran (THF) solution, adding 1.2-1.4 equivalent, preferably 1.2 equivalent diisopropylamino lithium to THF in the reactor, cooling to -85 to -75 degrees C, preferably -78 degrees C, adding THF solution of the compound (II) to the reaction system, reacting to -55 to -45 degrees C, preferably -50 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, preferably 1 hours passing carbon dioxide into the reaction system until the thin layer chromatography detection reaction is complete, partitioning reaction solution between ethyl acetate and 0.5-1.5 N aqueous hydrochloric acid, separating organic phase, washing organic phase with brine and drying over sodium sulfate, concentrating to dryness to obtain compound (III), adding 0.5-1.5 g, preferably 1.0 g of 1.0 equivalent compound (III) to the reactor and dissolving in 1-3 mL, preferably 2 mL chloroform solution, adding 1-3 mL, preferably 2 mL thionyl chloride, heating to reflux for 3.5-4.5 hours, preferably 4 hours, concentrating solvent to dryness under reduced pressure to obtain residue, dissolving residue in 5-15 mL, preferably 10 mL THF, adding 1.2-1.4 equivalent, preferably 1.2 equivalent triethylamine, adding 1.2-1.4 equivalent, preferably 1.2 equivalent (trimethylsilyl)methyl azide solution, stirring in an ice bath for 48-72 hours, preferably 48 hours, extracting reaction solution with ethyl acetate, washing organic phase with 0.5-1.5 N, preferably 1 N aqueous hydrochloric acid solution, saturated aqueous sodium bicarbonate solution and saturated brine, concentrating solvent to obtain a residue, dissolving residue in 15-25 mL, preferably 20 mL absolute ethanol in a reactor, adding solution of silver benzoate in triethylamine, stirring at room temperature for 18-20 hours, preferably 18 hours, concentrating to dryness to obtain a compound (IV), adding 0.5-1.5 g, preferably 1.0 g of 1.0 equivalent compound (IV) to a reactor and dissolving in 5-15 mL, preferably 10 mL carbon tetrachloride, adding 1.2-1.4 equivalent, preferably 1.2 equivalent brominating agent and 0.1-0.15 equivalent, preferably 0.1 equivalent initiator, heating to reflux for 4.5-5.5 hours, preferably 4 hours to obtain reaction liquid, concentrating reaction liquid to dryness to obtain residue, and concentrating residue. The brominating agent is N-bromosuccinimide. The initiator is benzoyl peroxide.

AE GUANHAO BIOTECH CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201935943N
AB    NOVELTY - An animal tissues degreasing method involves washing and disinfecting the animal tissue, cutting into pieces, lumps or mashing into powder and dehydrating the animal tissue by ethanol solution. The animal tissue is placed in a closed extraction kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide degreasing, the fat and fat-soluble impurities enters the separation tank with carbon dioxide fluid, the fat and fat-soluble impurities are separated from carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide participates in the next extraction cycle, the fat and fat-soluble impurities are removed in the separation tank to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for degreasing animal tissues for use in removal or exfoliation of fat and fat-soluble substances in soft tissues, hard tissues of animal origin (claimed).

PN CN109571303-A; CN109571303-B
TI Method for infiltrating ceramic abrasive article with supercritical fluid, involves placing ceramic grinding tool and infiltration agent to be infiltrated in autoclave and injecting carbon dioxide, followed by stirring and cooling.
AU LIU B
   YANG W
   LI X
   WANG Z
   SUN P
AE ZHENGZHOU RES INST ABRASIVE & GRINDING C (CHNM-C)
GA 201933576U
AB    NOVELTY - A ceramic abrasive article with supercritical fluid infiltrating method involves placing a ceramic grinding tool and a infiltration agent to be infiltrated in the autoclave in a weight ratio of 65-80:4-12 and sealing, injecting carbon dioxide into the autoclave, adjusting the pressure and temperature to 72.8 atm and 31.1 degrees C, respectively, so that the carbon dioxide reaches a supercritical state, stirring and infiltrating for 0.5-1 hours, followed by converting the supercritical carbon dioxide into a gas by depressurization after the impregnation is completed, releasing, cooling to normal temperature and taking out the abrasive article.
   USE - Method for infiltrating ceramic abrasive article with supercritical fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables infiltrating ceramic abrasive article with supercritical fluid with improved uniformity of efficiency and impregnation of grinding wheel.

PN WO2019011814-A1; CA3069643-A1; IN202017002835-A; KR2020030561-A; SG11202000231-A1; CN111094367-A; EP3652224-A1; JP2020526639-W; US2021147590-A1; RU2764774-C2; RU2020106315-A
TI Preparation of isoolefin polymers e.g. polyisobutene, involves providing medium comprising organic diluent and isoolefin monomer, and ethane or carbon dioxide dissolved in medium, and polymerizing monomer in presence of initiator system.
AU PAUL H
   LOVEGROVE J G A
   LEIBERICH R
   FELLER R
   WAGNER P
   KIRCHHOFF J
   RITTER J
   GOLF P H
   LOVEGROVEZONZOANOLD
   PELLEROLF
   KIRCHHOF-YORG
   PAUL H I
   LOVEGROVE Y G A
   VAGNER P
   KIRCHHOFF Y
   RITTER Y
AE ARLANXEO DEUT GMBH (SAOI-C)
   ARLANXEO DEUT GMBH (SAOI-C)
   ARLANXEO DEUT GMBH (SAOI-C)
   ARLANXEO DEUT GMBH (SAOI-C)
   LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
GA 2019070384
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of isoolefin polymers involves providing a reaction medium comprising an organic diluent and at least one monomer which is an isoolefin, and ethane or carbon dioxide that is dissolved in the reaction medium, and polymerizing the monomer within the medium in the presence of an initiator system to form a product medium comprising the copolymer, the organic diluent and optionally residual monomers, where the ethane or carbon dioxide is at least partially evaporated.
   USE - Preparation of isoolefin polymers e.g. polyisobutene and isobutylene-isoprene butyl rubber polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides isoolefin polymers with improved temperature control. It is possible to reduce large consumption of carbon dioxide for cooling down the whole reaction medium by pre-cooling the monomers and/or organic diluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isoolefins are 4-16C isoolefins, preferably 4-7C isoolefins. The isoolefin is isobutene. The medium further comprises one or more multiolefins and isoprene. The medium comprises isobutene as sole monomer. The organic diluent is chosen from hydrochlorocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon and alkane. The method uses a boron or aluminum compound of formula: MX3 (I) or M(R)m(X)3-m (II), preferably chosen from methyl aluminum dibromide, methyl aluminum dichloride, ethyl aluminum dibromide, ethylaluminum dichloride, butyl aluminum dibromide, butyl aluminum dichloride, dimethyl aluminum bromide, dimethyl aluminum chloride, diethyl aluminum bromide, diethyl aluminum chloride, dibutyl aluminum bromide, dibutyl aluminum chloride, methyl aluminum sesquibromide, methyl aluminum sesquichloride, ethyl aluminum sesquibromide, ethyl aluminum sesquichloride, isobutyl aluminum dichloride, and diisobutyl aluminum chloride. The initiator is chosen from water, alcohol, phenol, hydrogen halide, carboxylic acid, carboxylic acid halide, carboxylic acid ester, carboxylic acid amide, sulfonic acid, sulfonic acid halide, alkyl halide, alkylaryl halide, and polymeric halide, preferably water. The initiator system, monomer, organic diluent, ethane and/or carbon dioxide, form a single phase.M = B or Al;X = halo;R = monovalent hydrocarbyl chosen from 1-12C alkyl and 7-14C alkylaryl;m = 1 or 2;andalkylaryl = radical containing aliphatic and aromatic structures, at aliphatic position.Preferred Process: The polymerization is carried out as solution process. The provision and polymerization are carried out continuously. The evaporated carbon dioxide and/or ethane are recycled again into the provision and polymerization steps. Preferred Condition: The polymerization is carried out at a reaction pressure of 500-100000 hPa, preferably 1300-5000 hPa.

PN CN108948238-A
TI Preparation of chlorinated polyvinyl chloride involves feeding polyvinyl chloride powder into feeding hopper, opening exhaust valve to discharge carbon dioxide, oscillating, dispersing, mixing with thermal solvent, and dispersing.
AU HUANG P
   LI Z
   LIU S
AE JIANGSU DUBLE ENG CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   HUANG P (HUAN-Individual)
   NANJING DUBLE METAL EQUIP ENG CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018A05211
AB    NOVELTY - A chlorinated polyvinyl chloride is prepared by feeding polyvinyl chloride powder into feeding hopper, opening exhaust valve to discharge carbon dioxide, oscillating, dispersing, mixing with thermal solvent, dispersing, feeding into chlorination reactor, injecting chlorine gas, dispersing, carrying out UV excitation, generating hydrochloric acid, reacting PVC with chlorine gas to obtain chlorinated polyvinyl chloride, moving into cooling tower, cooling, separating carbon dioxide and hydrochloric acid, performing subsequent absorption to obtain high purity hydrochloric acid, pumping carbon dioxide into filter chamber, cooling, conveying chlorinated polyvinyl chloride to puffing tower, and high pressure spraying.
   USE - Method for preparing chlorinated polyvinyl chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is short, energy efficient, environment-friendly, has high chlorination efficiency and has good product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for chlorinated polyvinyl chloride preparation device.

PN CN108786733-A; CN108786733-B
TI Preparing micro-nanoporous carbon sphere used as carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g. dissolving salt in milk solution, placing in polytetrafluoroethylene hydrothermal reaction kettle, and reacting to obtain hydrothermal carbon precursor.
AU CAI J
   WU X
   REN M
   WANG Y
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201892888P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-nanoporous carbon sphere, comprises (a) dissolving salt in milk solution, placing in polytetrafluoroethylene hydrothermal reaction kettle, reacting at 130-240 degrees C for 12-72 hours to obtain hydrothermal carbon precursor, (b) washing the hydrothermal carbon precursor with de-ionized water, drying, and performing pre-carbonization reaction under nitrogen atmosphere to complete convert into black pre-carbide, (c) mixing the black-carbide with potassium hydroxide activating agent, and grinding to activate, and (d) subjecting the activated-treated carbide for acid cleaning, water washing and drying to obtain nano-porous carbon sphere.
   USE - The micro-nanoporous carbon sphere is useful as carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-nanoporous carbon sphere has carbon dioxide/nitrogen adsorption selectivity and cyclic regeneration property, and can effectively reduce carbon dioxide concentration in atmosphere and relieve greenhouse effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out by adding 30-200 g/l, preferably 3-20 g sodium chloride, potassium chloride, lithium chloride, zinc chloride, and calcium chloride to 100 ml milk solution. In the step (b), the washing is carried out at 60-90 degrees C, the drying is carried out at 100-140 degrees C, and the carbonization reaction is carried out at 400-600 degrees C with heating rate of 2-10 degrees C/minute for 1-4 hours. In the step (c), the black-carbide and potassium hydroxide activating agent are mixed in weight ratio of 1:1.5-3, and the activation treatment is carried out at 600-900 degrees C with heating rate of 1-4 degrees C/minute for 1-4 hours. In the step (d), the acid cleaning is carried out using 2 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution at 60 degrees C for 2 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E05-U05D; E11-A01; E11-F03; E11-Q01B; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E12-A13; J01-D01; J01-E02B3; A04-E08; A12-W11; A12-W14

PN CN108722029-A
TI Carbon dioxide gas decomposing-type filter screen, has filter screen main body provided with calcium hydroxide filter core and formed with filter groove, where size of filter screen main body is about specified range.
AU LIU C
   ZHAN X
AE LIU C (LIUC-Individual)
   ZHAN X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2018873488
AB    NOVELTY - The screen has a filter screen main body (1) provided with a calcium hydroxide filter core (2) and formed with a filter groove (3), where size of the filter screen main body is about 374MM, 193MM, 20MM, the filter screen main body is made of 98% of calcium hydroxide adsorbed substance material.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas decomposing-type filter screen.
   ADVANTAGE - The screen is connected with a ventilation system or an air purifying device so as to reduce discomfort effect caused by carbon dioxide gas concentration in a sealing space, thus increasing indoor air quality and comfort effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a carbon dioxide gas decomposing-type filter screen.
   Filter screen main body (1)
   Calcium hydroxide filter core (2)
   Filter groove (3)

PT P
PN FR3065226-A1; WO2018193186-A1; EP3622053-A1; EP3622053-B1
TI Culturing eukaryotic cells in vitro, comprises adding uric acid to cell culture medium and culturing under atmosphere of ambient air not supplemented with carbon dioxide.
AU RAOULT D
   LA SCOLA B
AE FOND MEDITERRANEE INFECTION (MEDI-Non-standard)
   UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   ASSISTANCE PUBLIQUE-HOPITAUX PARIS (ASSI-C)
   INSERM NAT SANTE & RECH MEDICALE INST (INRM-C)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   INSERM INST NAT SANTE & RECH MEDICALE (INRM-C)
   FOND MEDITERRANEE INFECTION (MEDI-Non-standard)
   UNIV D'AIX MARSEILLE (UYMA-C)
   ASSISTANCE PUBLIQUE-HOPITAUX PARIS (ASSI-C)
   INSERM INST NAT SANTE & RECH MEDICALE (INRM-C)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 2018820197
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing eukaryotic cells in vitro, comprises adding uric acid to the cell culture medium and culturing under an atmosphere of ambient air not supplemented with carbon dioxide, where the cells are such that the multiplication of the cells is improved by culturing in an atmosphere supplemented with carbon dioxide in the absence of uric acid in the culture medium.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing eukaryotic cells in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for culture medium for the method, comprising a basic culture medium for cell culture, where the basic culture medium is enriched in uric acid, and the basic culture medium is able to allow the culture of the eukaryotic cells by culturing in an atmosphere supplemented with carbon dioxide in the absence of uric acid in the basic culture medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, intracellular culture of microorganisms is carried out in the cells in cell culture medium containing uric acid under an atmosphere of ambient air not supplemented with carbon dioxide, where the intracellular culture of a microorganism is strict intracellular bacteria, viruses and parasites, and the intracellular culture of a the microorganism is carried out in an open container under an atmosphere of ambient air not supplemented with carbon dioxide and the container is closed after 24 hours of culture. Preferred Components: The uric acid is used at a concentration of at least 0.4 g/l, preferably 0.4-1 g/l. The strict intracellular bacterium is from genera Rickettsia, Orientia, Lawsonia, Coxiella, Ehrlichia, Chlamydia, Chlamydophila, Anaplasma or Neorickettsia, preferably Coxiella burnetii or Rickettsia species. The virus is Poxviridae, Herpesviridae, Adeno viridae, Papilloma viridae, Polyomaviridae, Parvoviridae, Picornaviridae, Caliciviridae, Astroviridae, Flaviviridae, Togaviridae, Corona viridae, Paramyxo viridae, Rhabdo viridae, Filo viridae, Orthomyxo viridae, Arena viridae, Bunyaviridae, Reo viridae, Hepadnaviridae or Retroviridae, preferably adenovirus. The parasite is Plasmodium, Apicomplexa and Leishmania, preferably Plasmodium falciparum. The cells are cell lines of 3T3, BA886 (bovine aorta), baby hamster kidney-21, Caco-2, cellosaurus, carp leukocyte culture, CRL1439 (rat liver cell line), CRL1442 (rat liver cell line), chicken fibroblast, DH82 (canine macrophage cells), EC2, endothelial cells 53, human erythro leukemia, Hela, human epithelial type 2, human leukemia 60, human colon cancer cell 29, J774, Jurkat, L929 (mouse adipose tissue), McCoy (mouse fibroblasts), MRC5 (diploid human cell culture line composed of fibroblasts derived from lung tissue of a 14 week old aborted Caucasian male fetus), MSDHP, P388 (cellosaurus), Raw264 (murine macrophage cell line), Tatoo, TF1, THP1 (human monocytic cell line), U937, Vero, Madin-Darby Canine kidney, buffalo green monkey cells, MA104, viro E6, or CLN h11.4, where the cells are human erythrocytes and the intracellular culture is a parasite of the genus Plasmodium, preferably Plasmodium falciparum, which is carried out in a culture medium containing uric acid under an atmosphere not supplemented with carbon dioxide and not decreased to oxygen. The basic culture medium is a cell culture medium comprising at least the components including: many inorganic salts including calcium chloride, calcium nitrate, ferric nitrate, magnesium sulfate, potassium chloride, sodium bicarbonate, sodium chloride and sodium phosphate, preferably at least 4 the inorganic salts; amino acids including arginine, cystine, glycine, histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, threonine, tryptophan, tyrosine, valine, asparagine, aspartate, glutamine, glutamic acid, proline or serine, preferably all of the amino acids; many vitamins including choline, folic acid, inositol, niacinamide, pantothenic acid nicotinamide, pyridoxal, pyridoxine, riboflavin, thiamine, biotin, aminobenzoic acid and cobalamin, preferably at least 3 the vitamins; and a source of carbohydrate, preferably glucose. The base culture medium is minimum essential medium, Dulbecco's modified eagle medium, Roswell park memorial institute medium, or Iscove's modified Dulbecco's media. The culture medium contains no additional antioxidant compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F01; B04-F02; C04-F01; C04-F02; D05-H01; D05-H08

PN CN108636378-A
TI Organic amine functionalized three-dimensional ordered macroporous material carbon dioxide adsorbent includes three-dimensional ordered macroporous material.
AU LIU L
   LIU C
   LIU S
   ZHANG X
   ZHU Y
   ZHU B
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 201881404W
AB    NOVELTY - Organic amine functionalized three-dimensional ordered macroporous material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises three-dimensional ordered macroporous material and organic amine. The organic amine is supported on three-dimensional ordered macroporous material. The water absorption of three-dimensional ordered macroporous material is 1-2.2 centimeter cube per gram.
   USE - Organic amine functionalized three-dimensional ordered macroporous material carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has better adsorption activity and better cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the organic amine functionalized three-dimensional ordered macroporous material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising polymerizing monomer raw material of colloidal template and initiator in water to obtain monodisperse polymer emulsion, centrifuging monodisperse polymer emulsion, drying centrifuged product to obtain colloidal template, forming three-dimensional ordered macroporous material, dissolving material source in low boiling organic solvent, adding gel crystal template to a mixture of material source and organic solvent for immersion treatment, washing final impregnated product, drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The three-dimensional ordered macroporous material is alumina, silica or titania. The monomer raw material of colloidal template is styrene, methyl methacrylate or acrylic acid.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)

PN CN108570318-A; CN108570318-B
TI Carbon dioxide foaming liquid composition useful for oil field, comprises e.g. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, alpha-olefin sulfonate, coconut oil diethanolamide and hydrophobic modified nano silicon dioxide particle.
AU MA T
   LUN Z
   ZHAO S
   TAN Z
   ZHOU G
   LU G
   CHEN S
   WANG Q
   JIA H
   SUN A
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC PETROLEUM EXPLORATION & PRODN (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 201878496W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide foaming liquid composition comprises 0.05-1 wt.% fatty alcohol polyoxyethyleneether sulfate, 0.01-0.5 wt.% alpha -olefin sulfonate, 0.005-0.3 wt.% alkyl glycoside, 0.004-0.1 wt.% coconut oil diethanolamide, 0.001-0.05 wt.% hydrophobic modified nano silicon dioxide particle and water.
   USE - The liquid composition is useful for oil field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition produces rich and stable carbon dioxide foam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide foaming liquid composition, comprising (i) dissolving the fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate in water to form a solution A; (ii) dissolving alpha -olefin sulfonate, alkyl glycoside and coconut oil diethanolamide in the solution A to form a solution B; and (iii) dispersing completely hydrophobically modified nano silica particles in the solution B.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide foaming liquid composition preferably comprises 0.15-0.35 wt.% fatty alcohol polyoxyethyleneether sulfate, 0.01-0.0.3 wt.% alpha -olefin sulfonate, 0.01-0.025 wt.% alkyl glycoside, 0.005-0.01 wt.% coconut oil diethanolamide, 0.005-0.0.12 wt.% hydrophobic modified nano silicon dioxide particle and water. The hydrophobically modified nano silica particles have a specific surface area of 50 -300 m2/g. Preferred Components: The alpha -olefin sulfonate is sodium alpha -olefin sulfonate having a carbon number of 12-18. The alkyl glycoside is lauryl glycosides, octyl glucoside and cocoyl glycosides. The hydrophobic modified nano silicon dioxide particle comprises hexadecyl silane, hexamethyldisilazane, dimethyldichlorosilane, polydimethylsiloxane and octyl silane
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate has the general formula RO(CH2CH2O)nSO3X, where R is a C12-C15 alkyl group, X is sodium or ammonia and n=2 or 3. The fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate is a mixture of a fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate and a fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether ammonium sulfate with mass ratio of 1.5-3:1.

PT P
PN WO2018141646-A1
TI Catalyst useful for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen, comprises aluminum oxide, nickel active mass, iron and manganese.
AU KOEHLER K
   THOMYS O
   HINRICHSEN K
   KOSCHANY F
   BURGER T
AE CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
   UNIV TECH MUNCHEN (MUEN-C)
GA 201861432C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises aluminum oxide, nickel active mass, iron and manganese. The molar ratio of nickel and iron in the catalyst is 5-10.0, preferably 5.4-5.7.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity, and improved activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing catalyst, comprising (a) co-precipitating a solution containing aluminum, nickel, manganese and iron in dissolved form to obtain a precipitate, (b) isolating the precipitate from the step (a), (c) drying the isolated precipitate from the step (b), and (d) calcining the dried precipitate from the step (c); and
   (2) methanation of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, comprising contacting a gas containing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with the above mentioned catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of nickel and manganese in the catalyst is 5-32, preferably 9-10. The nickel active material has crystallites with a diameter of less than 20 nm, preferably less than 10 nm. The catalyst has a carbon dioxide-absorption capacity at 35 degrees C of greater than 200 mu mol, preferably 200-400 mu mol/g. The solution from the step (a) is an aqueous solution. In the method: nickel, aluminum, manganese and iron are present in dissolved form as ionic compounds in the solution and the ionic compounds have same anion; manganese present in the solution of the step (a) is in oxidation state (II); and iron present in the solution from the step (a) is in oxidation state (II) or (III). The anion is a nitrate, sulfate, a halide, chloride or acetate. Preferred Method: The precipitate is aged in the solution for at least 30 minutes. The isolated precipitate from the step (b) is washed. The calcination of the dried precipitate is carried out at 300-600 degrees C in air. In the methanation method, the gas is contacted with the catalyst at above 200 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Light-triggered self-repairing cable material comprises thermoplastic matrix resin and microcapsules comprising polymer capsule material, anthracene and photocatalyst.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201856081L
AB    NOVELTY - A light-triggered self-repairing cable material comprises thermoplastic matrix resin and microcapsules. The microcapsules comprise 4-18 %mass polymer capsule material, 80-94 %mass anthracene and 2-3 %mass photocatalyst.
   USE - Light-triggered self-repairing cable material.
   ADVANTAGE - The light-triggered self-repairing cable material has excellent efficiency and prolonged service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of light-triggered self-repairing cable material, which involves (A) adding polymer capsule powder to mixing container, and vacuumizing, (B) introducing carbon dioxide into mixing container, adding polymer capsule powder and thoroughly mixing, compressing mixed gas to supercritical state such that polymer capsule is swollen in supercritical carbon dioxide to form supercritical carbon dioxide swollen polymer capsule, (C) adding supercritical carbon dioxide swollen polymer capsules to mixing vessel, adding Eucalyptus oil, mixing at high speed and quickly releasing pressure to obtain microcapsules, and (D) dispersing microcapsules in thermoplastic matrix resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is mesoporous titanium dioxide photocatalyst. Preferred Conditions: The step (B) involves controlling supercritical temperature greater than or equal to 70.8 degrees C and pressure greater than or equal to 8.4 MPa for 180-240 minutes. Preferred Properties: The purity of carbon dioxide is greater than or equal to 99.9%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer capsule is polycaprolactone, polyglycolic acid, polyethylene glycol, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylic acid or polyisopropylacrylamide.

PN WO2018125668-A1; AU2017386385-A1
TI Providing fractured subterranean formation useful for capturing carbon dioxide, by providing wellbore and providing fractures in subterranean formation using fracturing fluid pressurized only by one or more low pressure pumps.
AU FONSECA OCAMPOS E R
   COOK R L
   HACKBARTH C J
   WELCH Z R
   NASSE D B
   FONSECA O E R
AE SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 201852491C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for providing a fractured subterranean formation, involves providing a wellbore in the subterranean formation, and providing fractures in the subterranean formation using a fracturing fluid pressurized only by one or more low pressure pumps.
   USE - The method is useful for providing a fractured subterranean formation for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method mitigates seismicity related to oil and gas activities, provides improved environmental impact, reduces footprint by using low pressure pumps, is cost-effective, consumes less time, and eliminates need for pressure at the surface during fracturing process and proppants which are unable to maintain initial conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for capturing carbon dioxide, which involves providing a wellbore in a subterranean formation and providing fractures in the subterranean formation using a fracturing fluid designed to entrain carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The low pressure pumps comprise mud pumps. The low pressure pumps comprise casing test pumps. The method further involves transporting the fracturing fluid to the wellbore using construction cement trucks and pumping the fracturing fluid from the cement trucks into the wellbore using mud pumps after the cement trucks pour the fracturing fluid into hoppers of the mud pumps. The fracturing fluid comprises a mortar slurry.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN CN114272931-A
TI Directly formed carbon-dioxide-using catalyst            comprises metal copper and transition metal oxide.
AU SONG Y
   WANG Y
   LI J
   LI Y
   JIA H
   LI Z
   FANG X
   DU T
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202251865G
AB    NOVELTY - A directly formed carbon-dioxide-using                catalyst comprises metal copper and transition                metal oxide.
   USE - Directly formed carbon dioxide using                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The directly formed carbon dioxide using                catalyst has excellent catalytic performance. The                preparation method is short and economical, and                does not generate toxic pollutant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A directly formed carbon-dioxide-using                catalyst comprises metal copper and transition                metal oxide, and is of formula: Cu0[MxN'yOz].
   M = m-valent transition metal element; N' =                n-valent transition metal element; transition metal                element = Zn, Zr, Ce, In or Mn; x = more than 0; y                = &#8805;0; and z = 0 to                (x&#215;m+y&#215;n)/2 (excluding 0 and                (x&#215;m+y&#215;n)/2).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the directly formed carbon dioxide                using catalyst, which involves premixing copper                nitrate, transition metal nitrate and organic acid                raw materials, grinding under inert gas protection                to obtain slurry mixture, transferring slurry                mixture to molding container, drying and shaping at                a set temperature and atmosphere to obtain catalyst                precursor, and calcining the catalyst precursor in                flowing inert gas atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The catalyst has                  sponge-like porous structure.Preferred Component: The transition metal                  nitrate is one or more of zinc nitrate, zirconium                  nitrate, cerium nitrate, magnesium nitrate,                  indium nitrate, and manganese nitrate. The inert                  gas is nitrogen, argon and/or helium gas.Preferred Composition: The ratio of                  transition metal nitrate to organic acid is                  4-8:9.6-13.2.Preferred Condition: The grinding process is                  carried out at 200-450 rpm. The drying is carried                  out at 100-160&#8451; in nitrogen, argon                  and/or helium atmosphere for 8-14 hours. The                  calcination is carried out at 280-500&#8451;                  under heating rate of 5-10&#8451;/minute for                  3-5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic acid is one                  or more of citric acid, formic acid, and oxalic                  acid.
