TI Method for extracting vanadium by blowing carbon            dioxide in converter section, involves adding            vanadium-containing molten iron into converter, blowing            mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and adding            coolant to end point of converter.
AU LI L
   WU G
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 2021346755
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding vanadium-containing                molten iron into a converter. Mixed gas of nitrogen                and carbon dioxide is blown into a first area, a                second area and a third area. The mixed gas of                nitrogen and carbon dioxide is blown into the first                area after blowing for 2 minutes. The volume ratio                of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the second area                and the third area is adjusted after blowing for                2-4 minutes. The converter bottom is switched to                nitrogen. The oxygen lance is used to blow oxygen                on top, and the coolant is added within 2 minutes                of the oxygen lance blowing, and blowing to the end                point.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting vanadium                by blowing carbon dioxide in converter                section.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the function of extracting                vanadium and protecting carbon; achieves the effect                of energy saving and emission reduction, and                effectively reduces the residual vanadium                content.

TI Distribution method of symbiotic products in carbon footprint evaluation of atmospheric, vacuum distillation units involves distributing carbon dioxide emission which to each fraction according to proportion of heat absorbing.
AU TIAN T
   XIE Y
   BAI L
   WANG B
AE SINOPEC ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY SERVICE CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
   SINOPEC ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY SERVICE (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2020540195
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating (S310) carbon dioxide emissions based on the fuel consumption of the heating furnace. The heat absorbed by the heating furnace for each distillate is calculated (S320) carbon dioxide emissions based on the fuel consumption of the heating furnace. The heat absorbed by the heating furnace for each distillate is calculated according to the enthalpy of the heating furnace inlet and outlet. The carbon dioxide emission is distributed to each fraction according to the proportion of the heat absorbed by the heating furnace of each fraction.

   ADVANTAGE - The evaluation of carbon footprint based on actual emissions are realized.

   Step for calculating heat absorbed by heating furnace for distillate (S320)
   Step for distributing carbon dioxide emission to fraction (S330)

PT P
PN CN110437200-A; WO2021003974-A1; CN110437200-B
TI Preparing propylene carbonate based on carbon dioxide raw material involves passing propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, a catalyst and a co-catalyst into the reaction tower, after the cycloaddition reaction occurs.
AU ZHONG J
   LI D
   ZENG Z
   LUO R
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
   HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201996848A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing propylene carbonate based on carbon dioxide raw material involves passing propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, a catalyst and a co-catalyst into the reaction tower under the temperature condition of 130-150 degrees C and the pressure condition of 1.5-1.8 mega pascal, after the cycloaddition reaction occurs to obtain ester-containing gas-liquid mixture. The ester-containing gas-liquid mixture is passed into a separator, and a gas-liquid separation operation is performed to obtain a mixed gas and an ester-containing mixed liquid. The ester-containing mixed solution is passed into a distillation column and performed distillation operation to obtain finished propylene carbonate product and a circulating catalytic concentrated solution. The circulating catalytic concentrated solution includes the catalyst and the cocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing propylene carbonate based on carbon dioxide raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare propylene carbonate that accelerates the carbon dioxide, has good catalytic effect, and improves the reaction is more rapid and efficient conversion of propylene oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is potassium bromide, sodium bromide, sodium iodide and potassium iodide. The fusiogenic catalyst is polyethylene glycol and polyethylene glycolfatty acid. The phase transfer catalyst is tetrabutylammonium bromide, benzyl triethyl ammoniumchloride, methyl trioctyl ammonium chloride, tetrapropyl ammonium chloride, tetramethylammonium bromide, tetrabutyl ammonium iodide, triethyl hexyl bromide and tri ethyl octylammonium bromide. Preferred Process: The gas-liquid separation operation includes a first gas-liquid separation operation and a second gas-liquid separation operation. The temperature of the first gas-liquid separation operation is 60-80 degrees C. The temperature of the second gas-liquid separation operation is 105-150 degrees C. The temperature of the distillation operation is 230-240 degrees C, and the pressure is 0.01-0.05 mega pascal. The operation of passing ester-containing gas-liquid mixture to the separator, the ester-containing gas-liquid mixture is also passed into a heat exchanger. The heat exchange operation is performed to make the circulating medium in the heat exchanger absorb the heat in the ester-containing gas-liquid mixture. The operation of passing the propylene oxide, the carbon dioxide, the catalyst, and the cocatalyst into the reaction tower. The propylene oxide, the carbon dioxide, the catalyst, and the cocatalyst are passed into a preheater, and performed preheating operation to increase the temperature of the propylene oxide, the carbon dioxide, the catalyst, and the co-catalyst to 130-150 degrees C.

The DMC reactor works at low pressure and low temperature and comprises at least one heat exchanger system at the output. The heat exchanger at the output of the DMC reactor is followed by at least one DMC condenser and DMC tank for cooling the produced DMC and separating it from the gaseous feed products and gaseous byproducts. The methanol condenser or tank is connected to a feed pipe of the methanol reactor by a pipe connection such that the untreated gaseous feed or gaseous byproducts are fed as recycled feed mixture, optionally mixed with fresh feed gas consisting of a mixture comprised of carbon monoxide, and/or carbon dioxide, and hydrogen. The weight hourly space velocity of the feed gas is 1200-125000 h-1.

TI Preparing viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid used to treat subterranean formation, comprises combining carbon dioxide-based fluid with viscosifying proppant comprising proppant particles coated with carbon dioxide-philic coating.
AU LIANG F
   AL-MUNTASHERI G A
   LI L
   REDDY B R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201863429S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid, comprises combining a carbon dioxide-based fluid having a first viscosity at a pressure of less than or equal to 1000 pounds per square inch (psi) and a temperature of less than or equal to 32 degrees C with a viscosifying proppant comprising proppant particles coated with a carbon dioxide-philic coating that has a solvable portion that viscosifies the carbon dioxide-based fluid to produce a viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid having a second viscosity at a pressure of less than or equal to 1000 psi and a temperature of less than or equal to 32 degrees C that is at least 25% greater than the first viscosity, in which the second viscosity is measured at least 30 minutes after combining the carbon dioxide-based fluid and the viscosifying proppant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid (claimed) which is used to treat subterranean formation, stimulate groundwater wells, to precondition or induce rock cave-ins for mining operations, to dispose of waste by injecting it deeply into rock, to measure stresses in the earth's crust, to generate electricity in enhanced geothermal systems, or to increase injection rates for the geologic sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydraulic fracturing fluid maintains and hold open subterranean fractures during or following subterranean treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid does not contain additional external viscosifiers. The second viscosity of the viscous hydraulic fracturing fluid is greater than or equal to 0.1 centipoises (cP) at a pressure of less than or equal to 1000 pounds per square inch (psi) and a temperature of less than or equal to 32 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The method of recycling semi-dry                desulfurization ash curing industrial flue gas                carbon dioxide, involves (1) grinding and mixing                semi-dry desulfurization ash to obtain slurry, (2)                introducing industrial flue gas (I) into slurry for                absorbing sulfur dioxide reaction and oxidation                reaction to obtain turbid slurry (I) and industrial                flue gas (II), (3) adding aqueous ammonia to turbid                slurry (I) and introducing industrial flue gas (II)                for curing carbon dioxide reaction to obtain turbid                slurry (II), (4) filtering and washing turbid                slurry (II) using two sections and obtaining                ammonium sulfate product from washing liquid, where                the solid slag is calcium carbonate product.

AU DING W
   TANG H
   WU D
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV HARBIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHS-C)
GA 2021C7262L
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-cobalt-based metal organic framework carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst comprises (Fe2Co(mu3-O)(C8H4O5)3(H2O)3), where mu3 is oxygen bridged, C8H4O5 is 2-hydroxyterephthalic acid, Fe2CoC24H18O19, and the molecular weight is 781.01, and its structural unit is the hexagonal crystal system, P63 mmc space group, unit cell parameters a=13.282(7)Angstrom, b=13.282(7), c=18 .169(15) , alpha=90degrees, beta=90degrees, gamma=120degrees, the unit cell volume is 2776(4)3, Z=2.
   USE - Iron-cobalt-based metal organic framework carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The iron-cobalt-based metal organic framework carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst has high visible light utilization rate, good photogenerated electron-hole separation effect, and good carbon dioxide reduction performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing iron-cobalt (Fe2Co)-based metal organic framework carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst, which involves: (a)dissolving respectively Fe2Co(mu3-O) (CH3COO)6 and 2-hydroxyterephthalic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide (b)adding glacial acetic acid;(c)dispersing the mixed system uniformly by ultrasonic;(d)transfering to a high-pressure reactor;(e)heating the reactor at 150degrees Celsius for 24 hours;(f)cooling naturally to room temperature;(g)filtering;(h)washing;(i)performing vacuum-drying the product at 120degrees Celsiusfor 12 hours to obtain iron-cobalt (Fe2Co)-based metal organic framework carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The Fe2Co(mu3-O) (CH3COO)6 and 2-hydroxyterephthalic acid is dissolving in N,N-dimethylformamide, adding 0.2-0.4 mL glacial acetic acid, dispersing the mixed system uniformly by ultrasonic at the ultrasonic frequency is 35-45 kilohertz, and the ultrasonic time is 20-30 minutes and stirring at speed of 300-350 revolutions per minute.

PN CN111111459-A; CN111111459-B
TI Preparing polyimide/surface modified metal-organic            framework (MOF) mixed matrix film useful for carbon            dioxide/inert gas separation and capturing carbon            dioxide in flue gas and purifying natural gas,            comprises e.g. modifying surface of MOF.
AU YI C
   WU D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2020421727
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide/surface modified                metal-organic framework (MOF) mixed matrix film                comprises (i) modifying the surface of MOF with                dopamine to obtain surface modified MOF, and (ii)                adding surface-modified MOF to organic solvent X to                disperse evenly, adding polyimide film material,                ultrasonic and magnetic stirring to make the                surface-modified MOF nanoparticles evenly dispersed                in the polymer solution, and preparing                polyimide/surface-modified metal-organic framework                mixed matrix membranes with different                surface-modified MOF contents by solution casting                and drying.
   USE - The matrix film is useful for carbon                dioxide/inert gas separation, and capturing carbon                dioxide in flue gas and purifying natural gas (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prevents MOF particles from                agglomeration; reduces the particle size; increases                the compatibility between the MOF material and the                polyimide polymer matrix; reduces the non-selective                voids at the polymer/MOF phase interface;                simultaneously increases the carbon dioxide                permeability coefficient and carbon dioxide/inert                gas selectivity of the membrane material; and                enhances the carbon dioxide separation performance                of the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                polyimide/surface-modified metal organic framework                mixed matrix film prepared by the above mentioned                preparation method, and the thickness of the mixed                matrix film is 80-190 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) specifically                  comprises (a) adding MOF to the buffer solution,                  ultrasonically and then magnetically stirring to                  disperse evenly to obtain suspension, (b) adding                  dopamine to the suspension, and ultrasonically                  and magnetic stirring, and (c) centrifuging,                  washing using deionized water to neutrality, and                  drying to obtain surface modified MOF. In the                  step (a), the buffer pH range is 7.5-9, the                  concentration of MOF in the buffer is 0.4-0.6                  mg/ml, ultrasonic dispersion time is 30-90                  minutes, and magnetic stirring time is 12-36                  hours. In the step (b), the mass ratio of MOF to                  dopamine is 1:(1-3), ultrasonic dispersion time                  is 15-45 minutes, and magnetic stirring time is                  3-18 hours. Preferred Components: In the step                  (i), the MOF is aluminum fumarate MOF, zeolitic                  imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8), MIL-53                  (aluminum), MOF5 and/or UiO-66. In the step (ii),                  the organic solvent X is N, N-dimethylformamide,                  N, N-dimethylacetamide or N-methylpyrrolidone. In                  the step (ii), the mass ratio of the polyimide to                  the surface-modified MOF is 1:(0.010-0.450),                  preferably 1:(0.170-0.185).

PN CN110511407-A
TI Manufacturing nano polyethylene comprises e.g. taking raw material storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank, plunger pump and heater, heating carbon dioxide and raw materials and performing automatic dust removal and separation.
AU WU H
   FU C
   YANG D
AE GUANGDONG YUZONG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2325T
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing nano polyethylene comprises (i) taking raw material storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank, plunger pump, heater, screw extruder, container pump, synthetic heater, speed regulating nozzle, collection and cooling storage tank, pneumatic eyepiece and the dust removal separation tank; (ii) heating carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide storage tank by using plunger pump with heater and transferring into the synthetic heater; (iii) heating raw materials in the raw material storage tank by the screw extruder and passing into the synthetic heater using container pump, where synthetic heater carbon dioxide critically melts polyethylene, passing through the speed regulating nozzle and raw materials into the collection cooling storage tank; (iii) performing automatic sub-heading through the pneumatic sub-header and automatic dust removal and separation using dust removal and separation tank, where the critical temperature of nano polyethylene is 180 degrees C and pressure is 72.9 kgf.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing nano polyethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes polyethylene material with good flexibility and low temperature resistance, impact toughness, good electrical insulation effect; applies self-developed carbon dioxide supercritical fluid mechanics process to crush polyethylene material to a diameter of (3-85) mu m with spherical wall nanospheres of below 0.1 mu m; and improves various properties of polyethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The raw material storage tank and the screw extruder are connected through a connecting pipe, screw extruder and container pump are connected through a connecting pipe and container pump is connected to the synthetic heater through a connecting pipe. The synthetic heater is connected to the speed regulating nozzle through a connecting pipe, the speed regulating nozzle is connected to the collection and cooling storage tank through a connecting pipe, the collection and cooling storage tank is connected to the pneumatic headpiece through a connecting pipe and pneumatic eyepiece is connected to the dust removal separation tank through a connection pipe. Preferred Method: The temperature of the screw extruder is 180 degrees C, heater is 300 degrees C and synthetic heater is 280 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The raw material is polyethylene resin, where carbon dioxide storage tank and the plunger pump are connected through a connecting pipe, plunger pump is connected to the through heater through a connecting pipe and heater and the synthetic heater are connected through a connecting pipe.

TI Transporting meat carcasses in container, comprises e.g. arranging meat carcasses by stowing or hanging meat carcasses inside container, and filling internal volume of container with predefined atmosphere composition.
AU LEMUS J
   LINDHARDT H
   BAERENTZ M R
AE MAERSK LINE AS (MAER-Non-standard)
   MAERSK LINE AS (MAER-Non-standard)
GA 2019675534
AB    NOVELTY - Transporting meat carcasses in a container, comprises: arranging the meat carcasses by stowing or hanging the meat carcasses inside the container; filling an internal volume of the container with a predefined atmosphere composition comprising a first carbon dioxide level having a percentage volume of carbon dioxide greater than 95%, where filling comprises flushing an existing atmosphere of the internal volume of the container with the predefined atmosphere composition; and maintaining the first carbon dioxide level in the atmosphere of the internal volume of the container.
   USE - The method is useful for transporting meat carcasses in a container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a container (1) for transporting meat carcasses (2) comprising a sealable internal volume for the receiving the meat carcasses, a valve (5) in fluid connection (6) with the sealable internal volume for filling the internal volume with a predefined atmosphere composition comprising a first carbon dioxide level having a percentage volume of carbon dioxide greater than 95%, and a gas monitor (7) configured to detect and signal that the current atmosphere of internal volume has a carbon dioxide level less than the first carbon dioxide level.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a side view of a container accommodating meat carcases.
   Container (1)
   Meat carcasses (2)
   Valve (5)
   Fluid connection (6)
   Gas monitor (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step of maintaining the first carbon dioxide level comprises refilling the internal volume of the container with additional carbon dioxide to maintain the first carbon dioxide level in pre-determined locations or sites such as a container vessel or a port/terminal where gas-flushing operations have been specifically set-up. The method comprises: detecting a current level of carbon dioxide in the internal volume of the container and refilling the internal volume of the container with additional carbon dioxide in dependence of detecting that the current level of carbon dioxide drops below the first carbon dioxide level; detecting the current level of carbon dioxide in the internal volume of the container and stopping the flushing step in dependence of the current level of carbon dioxide equaling or exceeding the first carbon dioxide level; refrigerating the internal volume of the container; wrapping the meat carcasses in a gas permeable packaging; and controlling atmosphere may be incorporated into a control logic of a reefer container to monitor the atmosphere. Preferred Components: The predefined atmosphere composition comprises a percentage volume of oxygen less than 2%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gas monitor is configured to signal that the internal volume requires refilling via the valve with additional carbon dioxide to maintain the first carbon dioxide level.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2018237808-A1; US10421981-B2
TI System for manufacturing short carbon chain, weak organic acids, e.g. acetic acid, using a gas stream rich in carbon dioxide, comprises liquid-gas contact unit, and a bacterial strain disposed in the liquid-gas contact unit.
AU WOODWARD B C
AE BIG MONKEY SERVICES LLC (BIGM-Non-standard)
GA 201865548P
AB    NOVELTY - System for manufacturing short carbon chain, weak organic acids using a gas stream rich in carbon dioxide, comprises liquid-gas contact unit; and a bacterial strain disposed in the liquid-gas contact unit. The bacterial strain reduces a concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas stream and produces organic acids. The organic acids comprise of acetic acid, butyric acid, propionic acid, or lactic acid.
   USE - The system or method is useful for manufacturing short carbon chain, weak organic acids, e.g. acetic acid, butyric acid, propionic acid, or lactic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Simple, inexpensive, and effective solution is provided for reducing carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing short chain weak organic acids, which involves providing a gas stream rich in carbon dioxide: introducing the gas stream into a liquid-gas contact unit; preparing an inoculum that comprises a bacterial strain adapted to reduce a concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas stream, and to produce organic acids, and inoculating the liquid-gas contact unit with a 1st amount of the inoculum such that the bacteria are present to consume carbon dioxide from the gas stream and to produce organic acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Strain: The bacterial strain is adapted to grow in the liquid-gas contact treatment unit. The 2nd bacterial strain forms a biofilm on contact surfaces in the FGD unit to inhibit crystalline buildup. The bacterial strain is a bacterial, proteobacterial, or archaeal species comprising Pediococcus, Propionibacterium, or its variants or combinations. It comprises Pediococcus acidilacti, Propionibacterium freudenreichii, or its variants or combinations. The bacteria do not produce any appreciable volume of biogas. The bacterium is adapted to reduce a concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas stream by greater than or equal to 10%, greater than or equal to 30%, or greater than or equal to 50%. Preferred Method: The method further involves inoculating the liquid-gas contact unit with a 2nd inoculum of a 2nd bacterial strain adapted to inhibit crystalline formation in the liquid-gas contact unit; where the gas stream is a flue gas stream, the liquid-gas contact unit is a liquid-gas contact FGD unit comprising: a vessel with a flue gas inlet and a flue gas outlet and a liquid reservoir containing a liquid flue gas treating agent; a recirculation/spray system configured to circulate the liquid flue gas treating agent through the vessel; contact surfaces in the vessel configured for contacting the flue gas and the liquid flue gas treating agent recirculated from the reservoir; where the bacterial strain and the 2nd bacterial strain are disposed in the liquid reservoir containing the liquid flue gas treating agent, where both bacterial strains are adapted to grow in the liquid flue gas treating agent, where the 2nd bacterial strain forms a biofilm on contact surfaces in the FGD unit to inhibit crystalline buildup; and where the bacterial strain is a bacterial, proteobacterial, or archaeal species comprising Pediococcus acidilacti, Propionibacterium freudenreichii, or its variants or combinations, and where the 2nd bacterial strain is bacterial, proteobacterial, or archaeal species comprising Bacilus subtilis, Bacillus chitinosporus, or its variants or combinations. It also involves inoculating the liquid-gas contact unit with a 3rd bacterial strain, where the 3rd bacterial strain is adapted to remediate heavy metals, and where the 3rd bacterial strain is bacterial, proteobacterial, or archaeal species comprising Bacilus amyloliquefaciens, Bacillus licheniformus, or its variants or combinations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The system does not produce any appreciable volume of biogas. The gas stream is a flue gas stream, and the liquid-gas contact unit is a liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit. The system further comprises a 2nd bacterial strain disposed in the liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit, where the 2nd bacterial strain inhibits crystalline formation in the liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit. The liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit comprises a vessel with a flue gas inlet and a flue gas outlet and a liquid reservoir containing a liquid flue gas treating agent; a recirculation/spray system configured to circulate the liquid flue gas treating agent through the vessel; contact surfaces in the vessel configured for contacting the flue gas and the liquid flue as treating agent recirculated from the reservoir; and the bacterial strain and the 2nd bacterial strain being disposed in the liquid reservoir containing the liquid flue gas treating agent, where both bacterial strains are adapted to grow in the liquid flue gas treating agent. Preferably, the liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit is a flue gas desulfurization (FGD) unit. The system also comprises a 3rd bacterial strain disposed in the liquid-gas contact flue gas treatment unit, where the 3rd bacterial strain is adapted to remediate heavy metals.

AB    NOVELTY - Operating system including electrochemical                two-cell apparatus involves passing carbon dioxide                to a first cathode cavity of a first                electrochemical cell, reducing electrochemically                the carbon dioxide at a first cathode in the first                electrochemical cell to carbon monoxide (CO),                flowing the CO from the first cathode cavity to a                second cathode cavity of a second electrochemical                cell, and forming at least one of ethanol or                ethylene from the CO at a second cathode in the                second electrochemical cell.
   USE - Method for operating system including                electrochemical two-cell apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced carbon dioxide emission                at the facility using carbon dioxide as a                feedstock, high efficiency, and                cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a system, which comprises: an                electrochemical two-cell apparatus (201) to                electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide into carbon                monoxide at a first cathode and convert the carbon                monoxide into at least one of ethanol or ethylene                at a second cathode, where the electrochemical                two-cell apparatus comprises a first                electrochemical cell (202) comprising a first                cathode cavity (208), the first cathode, a first                catalyst arranged along the first cathode, a first                anode, a first electrolyte to conduct oxygen ions                from the first cathode to the first anode, and a                first anode cavity to collect and discharge oxygen                gas formed from the oxygen ion, and a second                electrochemical cell (204) comprising a second                cathode cavity (216) to receive the carbon monoxide                from the first cathode cavity, the second cathode,                a second catalyst arranged along the second                cathode, a second anode to generate hydrogen ion, a                second anode cavity, a second electrolyte arranged                between the second anode and the second cathode to                diffuse the hydrogen ion from the second anode to                the second cathode, where the second electrolyte                comprises a proton conductor, a first conduit to                supply the carbon dioxide to the first cathode                cavity, and a second conduit to discharge the at                least one of ethanol or ethylene from the second                cathode cavity; and
   2. an electrochemical two-cell apparatus,                which comprises: a first electrochemical cell                comprising a first cathode cavity to receive carbon                dioxide, a first cathode to electrochemically                reduce the carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and                generate oxygen ion, a first anode to receive the                oxygen ions, a first anode cavity to collect and                discharge oxygen gas formed from the oxygen ion, a                first electrolyte arranged between the first                cathode and the first anode to conduct the oxygen                ion, and a first catalyst arranged along the first                cathode, and a second electrochemical cell                comprising a second cathode cavity to receive the                carbon monoxide, a second cathode to convert the                carbon monoxide into at least one of ethanol or                ethylene, a second anode to generate hydrogen ion,                a second anode cavity, a second electrolyte to                diffuse the hydrogen ion from the second anode to                the second cathode, and a second catalyst arranged                along the second cathode.

GA 201834190F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing formaldehyde-stabilised urea involves generating synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and steam in a synthesis gas generation unit, dividing the synthesis gas into a first synthesis gas stream and a smaller second synthesis gas stream, subjecting the first synthesis gas stream to one or more stages of water-gas shift in one or more water-gas shift reactors to form a shifted gas, cooling the shifted gas to below the dew point and recovering condensate to form a dried shifted gas, recovering carbon dioxide from the dried shifted gas.
   USE - Method for producing formaldehyde-stabilised urea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing formaldehyde-stabilised urea involves generating synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and steam in a synthesis gas generation unit, dividing the synthesis gas into a first synthesis gas stream and a smaller second synthesis gas stream, subjecting the first synthesis gas stream to one or more stages of water-gas shift in one or more water-gas shift reactors to form a shifted gas, cooling the shifted gas to below the dew point and recovering condensate to form a dried shifted gas, recovering carbon dioxide from the dried shifted gas in carbon dioxide removal unit to form a carbon dioxide-depleted synthesis gas, subjecting the carbon dioxide-depleted synthesis gas to stage of methanation in one or more methanation reactors to form ammonia synthesis gas. The ammonia from the ammonia synthesis gas in an ammonia production unit and recovering the ammonia. The portion of the ammonia and at least a portion of the recovered carbon dioxide stream are reacted in urea production unit to form urea stream. The urea is stabilized by mixing the urea stream and stabilizer prepared by using formaldehyde to form a stabilized urea. The formaldehyde is generated by passing the second portion of synthesis gas through a scrubber to remove contaminants therefrom and form a scrubbed synthesis gas, synthesizing methanol from the scrubbed synthesis gas in a methanol synthesis unit, and recovering the methanol and a methanol synthesis off-gas, combining the methanol synthesis off-gas with the shifted gas and subjecting at least a portion of the recovered methanol to oxidation with air in a formaldehyde stabilizer production unit to produce formaldehyde. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a facility, which comprises:
   (A) synthesis gas generation unit, one or more water-gas shift reactors, condensate removal unit, carbon dioxide removal unit, methanation unit, ammonia synthesis unit, urea synthesis unit and urea stabilization unit;
   (B) installing unit for recovering a synthesis gas stream from the synthesis gas generation unit, scrubber for removing contaminants from the recovered synthesis gas stream, methanol synthesis unit for synthesizing methanol from the scrubbed synthesis gas, recovering methanol unit and methanol synthesis off-gas, water-gas shift reactors for feeding the methanol synthesis off-gas to shifted gas; and
   (C) formaldehyde stabilizer production unit for converting the methanol into stabilizer prepared using formaldehyde, and urea stabilization unit for feeding at least a portion of the stabilizer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a facility.
   Installing unit (1)
   Scrubber (2)
   Methanol synthesis unit (3)
   Recovering methanol unit (4)
   Water-gas shift reactors (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The second synthesis gas stream is recovered from a primary reformed gas stream or a secondary reformed gas stream. The second synthesis gas stream consists of a portion of a secondary reformed gas. The amount of second synthesis gas stream is 0.25-30 vol.%, preferably 0.25 to 20 vol.%, more preferably 0.25-10 vol.%. The stage of water-gas shift comprises a stage of high temperature shift in a high temperature shift reactor. The water-gas shift stage comprises high temperature shift stage to form partially shifted gas and low temperature shift stage to form shifted gas mixture. The second synthesis gas stream is passed to the scrubber, is cooled to below the dew point so that steam present in the second synthesis gas condenses as water, which is removed by a separator, to provide dried second synthesis gas stream gas. Preferred Condition: The scrubber is fed with demineralised water and/or a purified condensate. The contaminated scrubber liquor is recovered from the scrubber which is combined with the condensate recovered from the shifted gas and the combined stream sent. The scrubbed second synthesis gas is heated to a methanol synthesis inlet temperature at 200-320 degrees C, preferably 200-270 degrees C. The scrubbed second synthesis gas is heated in a heating step by a gas-gas interchanger that recovers heat from the second synthesis gas stream. The steam is added to the scrubbed second synthesis gas. The methanol synthesis is operated on a once-through basis by using a single adiabatic methanol synthesis reactor containing bed of methanol synthesis catalyst. The methanol synthesis off-gas is fed with or without heating to a shifted gas recovered from a stage of high temperature shift. The methanol synthesis off-gas is fed, without heating, to a shifted gas after a stage of isothermal shift, medium temperature shift or low temperature shift.

PT P
PN CN108208832-A
TI Extracting total saponins in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises e.g. weighing Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea powder, and extracting total saponin in Gynostemma pentaphyllum.
AU HONG X
   ZHANG S
   LI W
AE ZHENJIANG YITE BIOTECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201853589B
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting total saponins in Gynostemma pentaphyllumtea by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises e.g. (1) accurately weighing 6.58 g Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea powder, and extracting total saponin in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction; extracting extract for twice to obtain blue transparent solution having rich fragrance; and making up the obtained extract solution to 50 ml to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide extraction extraction solution; (2) using traditional organic solvent extraction method for the extraction of total saponins from Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea as comparison, where the specific experimental (traditional organic solvent extraction method) steps comprises accurately weighing Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea powder, wrapping in filter paper sleeve, placing filter paper in Soxhlet extractor with tweezers.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting total saponins in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting total saponins in Gynostemma pentaphyllumtea by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises (1) accurately weighing 6.58 g Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea powder, and extracting total saponin in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction; where the extracting condition includes extraction pressure is 20 MPa, temperature is 40 degrees C, dynamic extraction time is 12 minutes, static extraction time is 8 minutes, carbon dioxide flow is 10 ml/minute, cosolvent ethanol is 1 ml/minute; extracting extract for twice to obtain blue transparent solution having rich fragrance; and making up the obtained extract solution to 50 ml to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide extraction extraction solution; (2) using traditional organic solvent extraction method for the extraction of total saponins from Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea as comparison, where the specific experimental (traditional organic solvent extraction method) steps comprises accurately weighing 1.5402 g Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea powder, wrapping in filter paper sleeve, placing filter paper in Soxhlet extractor with tweezers, refluxing Gynostemma pentaphyllum powder with petroleum ether at 80 degrees C for 3 hours, until the color of the extracting solution becomes colorless, discarding the petroleum ether, taking out the dregs, evaporating all petroleum ether remaining on dregs, adding the dregs into the Erlenmeyer flask, ultrasonically extracting with 50 ml water-saturated normal-butanol solution for 1.5 hours, filtering, evaporating the filtrate, dissolving residue in methanol, transferring to a 10 ml volumetric flask, and shaking to obtain organic solvent extraction extracting solution; and (3) accurately weighing 4.26 mg ginsenoside Rb1 reference substance, dissolving in methanol, diluting to 10 ml in volumetric flask to obtain reference stock solution with a mass concentration of 0.4206 mg/ml, accurately weighing 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, and 1.0 ml reference stock solution in 10 ml stoppered test tube, volatilizing in the water bath at 60 degrees C to remove solvent, adding 0.25% vanillin-glacial acetic acid solution (currently used) and 0.8 ml perchloric acid with mass fraction of 5%, mixing well, heating in water bath at 60 degrees C for 15 minutes, cooling in ice water bath, adding 5 ml glacial acetic acid, doing blank experiment at the same time, and measuring absorbance at 550 nm. where the linear regression of the ginsenoside Rb1 mass concentration (x) by absorbance (y) gave standard curve equation of y = 6.2226x - 0.0209 (r = 0.9992). where the content of total saponins in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea is determined by vanillin-perchloric acid colorimetry; precisely measuring 0.1 ml Gynostemma pentaphyllum extract according to the above Preparation of standard curve step; measuring the absorbance of the extract, and calculating the total saponin content in Gynostemma pentaphyllum tea according to the standard curve equation, and calculating the extraction rate; extraction rate = (total saponin mass / raw material mass) x 100%.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

   LI L
   DENG T
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)

GA 201896559N
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises carbon dioxide and aminophenylacetonitrile compound as raw materials. The catalyst is an aqueous solution containing a borate anion.
   USE - Catalyst for preparing 2,4(1H, 3H)-quinazolinedione or its derivative (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is natural and easy to obtain, and has simple and environment-friendly preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is a salt lake brine containing borate ion and/or carbonate ion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is used to catalyze the reaction without the addition of organic solvent. The ratio of the catalyst to aminophenylacetonitrile compound is 0.25-1.75 ml/mmol. Preferred Method: The reaction is catalyzed by the catalyst at 0.5-2 MPa and 100-150 degrees C for 1-24 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a main box body whose top                portion is rotatably connected with a box cover                through a hinge. A bottom portion of the box cover                is fixedly connected with a sealing ring. A side of                the main box body is fixed with a converter (4). A                bottom end of the converter is connected with a                pressure air chamber (5). A middle portion of an                air suction pipe (6) and an exhaust pipe (7) are                fixed with an one-way vent valve (8). A front                surface of the main box body is connected with an                air detection chamber. A sidewall plate is fixed on                a side of a detection chamber. A ventilating plate                is connected to a side surface of the sidewall                plate. A one way vent valve is connected with a                middle part of the ventilating plate.
   USE - Manned submersible carbon dioxide                high-efficiency absorbing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The sealing ring prevents the external gas                from entering into the main box body when the                device is used for closing the box cover. The first                one-way ventilation valve and the second one way                ventilation valve are closed to prevent the                external air exchange from the air in the device                when the converter does not work when the converter                is not worked, thus avoiding carbon dioxide in the                air to react in the adsorbent. The device is                convenient for storing high carbon dioxide                adsorbents, prolonging service life of the device                and saving cost. The outer carbon dioxide sensor is                electrically connected with the storage battery,                and the reciprocating motor drives the piston sheet                to move in the pressure cabin by reciprocating                supporting rod, thus improving efficiency of air                inlet device, increasing functionality and                absorption rate of the device and power                consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                manned submersible carbon dioxide high-efficiency                absorbing device.

PN CN114818359-A
TI Modelling catalytic cracking flue gas circulation            regeneration system involves establishing coke reaction            rate function about coke molar concentration and oxygen            molar concentration only considering reaction of carbon            burning and hydrogen burning.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG Y
   WU L
   YAN H
   MENG F
   WANG L
   SUN S
AE SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022A3459S
AB    NOVELTY - Modelling catalytic cracking flue gas                circulation regeneration system involves                establishing the coke reaction rate function about                the coke molar concentration and oxygen molar                concentration only considering the reaction of                carbon burning and hydrogen burning, establishing a                steady-state model of the regenerator to calculate                the content of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                water, and oxygen in the regenerated flue gas and                the content of catalyst coke after regeneration                according to the flue gas cyclic regeneration                process, target optimization of carbon dioxide                enrichment and regeneration effect of the catalyst                to be produced.
   USE - Method for modelling catalytic cracking flue                gas circulation regeneration system used in the                technical field of petrochemical industry,                particularly a catalytic cracking device with                highest carbon dioxide emission in each kind of oil                refining device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good regeneration efficiency,                provides a kinetic model of catalytic cracking                regeneration process, predicts the optimal oxygen                flow and regeneration flue gas circulation ratio,                regeneration flue gas, regenerated catalyst carbon,                helps operating personnel the regenerator                operation, and realizes efficient capture of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. a catalytic cracking flue gas cyclic                regeneration system modeling device, which                comprises build a scorch reaction kinetic model                module for establishing a scorch reaction rate                function with respect to coke molarity and oxygen                molarity, model module of the flue gas recycle                regeneration system is established to establish a                steady state model of the regenerator according to                the flue gas cycle regeneration process to                calculate the content of carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, water, and oxygen in the regenerated flue                gas and the content of catalyst coke after                regeneration, the catalytic cracking flue gas cycle                regeneration optimization module is used to                optimize the carbon dioxide enrichment and the                regeneration effect of the catalyst to be                produced;
   2. an electronic device, which comprises a                processor and a memory, where the memory stores                computer-readable instructions, when the                computer-readable instructions are executed by the                processor; and
   3. a readable storage medium on which a                computer program is stored, which comprises a                processor, the method for modeling a catalytic                cracking flue gas recycle regeneration system is                executed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction of the                  carbon element and the hydrogen element occurring                  in the dense phase bed includes r1, r2, and r3                  represent the reaction rates of carbon element                  oxidation to carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen element oxidation to water, k is the                  reaction rate constant, and the &#963;                  represents the molar ratio of carbon dioxide and                  carbon monoxide generated, &#961;c represents                  the density of catalyst particles in the dense                  phase zone, &#949;c, D represents the volume                  fraction of dense bed catalyst, wck, D represents                  the coke content on the catalyst, MWC represents                  the molar molecular mass of carbon, MWH                  represents the molar molecular mass of hydrogen,                  q is the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon in the                  coke, ci, j represents the molar concentration of                  the gas component i in the j region, j region is                  the D region corresponding to the dense phase bed                  or the F region corresponding to the dilute                  phase, and the gas component i includes carbon                  monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, oxygen or                  nitrogen. The carbon is oxidized to carbon                  dioxide after generating carbon monoxide, the ci,                  D is the molar flow rate of the gas in the dense                  phase zone, ni, D represents the molar flow rate                  of each gas component in the dense phase zone,                  vg, D represents the volume flow rate of the gas                  in the dense phase zone. The carbon monoxide                  oxidation reaction rate in the dilute phase zone                  includes F represent the molar concentration of                  each gas component in the dilute phase region, F                  represents the molar flow rate of each gas                  component, vg, F represents the volume flow rate                  of the gas in the dilute phase region. The method                  of establishing a regenerator steady state model                  includes carrying out the material balance                  calculation for the gas in the dense phase bed,                  setting the height of the dense phase bed                  unchanged to obtain the equation of the change                  rate of the molar flow rate of each gas component                  along the axial direction, where the ZD                  represents the height of the dense bed, Z=z/ZD,                  representing the dimensionless relative distance                  at the z-section in the bed. The gas molar flow                  rate at the inlet of the dilute phase zone is                  equal to the gas molar flow rate at the outlet of                  the dense phase zone, the n(0) represents the                  molar flow rate of each gas component at the                  inlet of the dilute phase zone, and represents                  the molar flow rate of each gas component at the                  outlet of the dense phase zone

GA 2022552052

The passenger cabin heating loop is orderly connected with the carbon dioxide electric compressor (1), an evaporation condenser A (2), a liquid storage tank (3), a middle heat exchanger (4), a gas-liquid separating tank (5) and an evaporation condenser B (6). The cabin refrigerating loop includes a carbon dioxide (electric), an evaporation condenser B, a liquid storage tank, a middle heat exchanger, a gas-liquid separating tank and an evaporation condenser a which are connected in turn. The battery box refrigerating loop is orderly connected with the battery box, and water pump. The water outlet of the battery box is connected with the water pump. The water pump is connected with the cooler. The cooler is connected on the middle heat exchanger.

PN CN113958380-A; CN113958380-B
TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide generating system            using gas-liquid separator and injector, has expander            for utilizing expansion of working medium at heat flow            outlet of cooler to do work, where working medium            outlet of expander is connected with working medium            inlet of gas-liquid separator.
AU ZHANG D
   SUN L
   HUANG C
   XIE Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022266155
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (3) for                pressurizing working medium of gas phase outlet of                a gas-liquid separator (1) to a predetermined                pressure. A turbine (6) is used for driving a                generator to generate electricity. An ejector (8)                is used for pressurizing the working medium output                by heat flow outlet of a regenerator (4) by the                working medium output under pressure from the                compressor. A cooler (9) is used for cooling the                working medium fluid output by the ejector under                pressure. An expander (10) is used for utilizing                the expansion of the working medium at the heat                flow outlet of the cooler to do work. A working                medium outlet of the expander is connected with                working medium inlet of the gas-liquid                separator.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide generating                system using gas-liquid separator and                injector.
   ADVANTAGE - The trans-critical carbon dioxide generating                system can reduce the system condensing cost, at                the same time, it can improve the circulating                efficiency of the system. The working medium inlet                of the compression pump is connected with the                liquid phase outlet of the gas-liquid separator.                The system cycle efficiency is reduced, therefore,                the system back heat is improved and uses the                injector, the working medium is expanded to lower                back pressure in the turbine, increasing the                expansion ratio of system, system output power is                increased, and the circulation efficiency is                improved. The saturated carbon dioxide liquid use                compression pump replaces the compressor for                pressurizing, and simplifies the development of the                pressurized component in the circulation                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                trans-critical carbon dioxide generating method                using gas-liquid separator and injector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                trans-critical carbon dioxide generating system                using gas-liquid separator and injector (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
   Gas-liquid separator (1)
   Compression pump (2)
   Compressor (3)
   Regenerator (4)
   Heater (5)
   Turbine (6)
   Generator (7)
   Ejector (8)
   Cooler (9)
   Expander (10)

PT P
PN WO2021022171-A1; US2022274876-A1
TI Preparing cast cementitious structure for carbon            dioxide sequestration by conditioning cementitious            composite material comprising a binder and water in a            mold by exposing to relative humidity, and removing and            drying the composite material.
AU LI V C
   ELLIS B R
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
   UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
GA 202112919Y
AB    NOVELTY - A cast cementitious structure for carbon                dioxide sequestration is prepared by conditioning a                cementitious composite material comprising a binder                and water in a mold by exposing the cementitious                composite material to 50-80% relative humidity for                3-24 hours; and removing the cementitious composite                material from the mold and drying the cementitious                composite material to remove greater than or equal                to 10 wt.% initial water in cementitious composite                material to form cast cementitious structure                capable of carbon dioxide (CO2) uptake level of                greater than or equal to 6 wt.% binder, where the                cast cementitious structure has a tensile strain                capacity of greater than or equal to 1% and                uniaxial tensile strength of greater than or equal                to 1 MPa.
   USE - Preparation of cast cementitious structure for                carbon dioxide sequestration.
   ADVANTAGE - The high material ductility potentially                eliminates the needs for steel reinforcement, thus,                extending structural service life by eliminating                corrosion-induced deteriorations. The low-carbon                ingredients, high CO2 storage capability and                enhanced material durability collectively serve to                create a more efficient, durable and sustainable                concrete infrastructure system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparing a cementitious structure for                carbon dioxide sequestration which involves                conditioning a cementitious composite material                comprising Portland cement and water by exposing                the cementitious composite material to 50-80%                relative humidity for 16-24 hours; and drying the                cementitious composite material to remove greater                than or equal to 20 wt.% initial water in the                cementitious composite material to form the                cementitious structure capable of CO2 uptake level                of greater than or equal to 25 wt.% Portland                cement; and
   (2) preparing cementitious structure for                carbon dioxide sequestration which involves                conditioning a cementitious composite material                comprising a binder and water by exposing                cementitious composite material to 50-80% relative                humidity for 3-4 hours; and drying the cementitious                composite material to remove greater than or equal                to 20 wt.% initial water in cementitious composite                material to form the cementitious structure capable                of CO2 uptake level of greater than or equal to 6                wt.% binder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The tensile strain                  capacity is optionally greater than or equal to                  2% and the uniaxial tensile strength greater than                  or equal to 2 MPa. Conditioning occurs at ambient                  temperatures. Preferred Components: The initial                  amount of water present in cementitious composite                  material that is removed from cementitious                  composite material after drying is 10-60%,                  preferably 20-40%. The binder comprises a                  pozzolanic material and the cementitious                  composite material further comprises greater than                  or equal to 1 aggregate and greater than or equal                  to 1 polymeric fiber. The cementitious composite                  material is free of Portland Cement and the                  pozzolanic material comprises a Type C fly ash                  and a Type F fly ash. The binder comprises                  Portland cement and has a carbon dioxide uptake                  level of greater than or equal to 30 wt.%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 35 wt.%                  Portland cement. Preferred Process: The method                  further comprises carbonating the cementitious                  composite material after the drying, and exposing                  the cast cementitious structure to water for                  rehydration after the carbonating. Drying is                  conducted for 2-6 hours.

PT P
PN CN111254229-A
TI Method for controlling carbon dioxide incubator,            involves generating set power according to minimum            power and adjusting set power to initial power of            indirect heating of inner wall in second preset period            continuous with first preset period.
AU LIU Z
   CHEN H
   WANG Y
   HU W
   ZHENG W
   XIA Y
   TANG X
AE QINGDAO HAIER BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAITE BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202053649E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S101) the                current temperature and preset temperature of                interior of carbon dioxide incubator. The indirect                heating power of inner wall is outputted (S102)                according to the preset PID control algorithm. The                temperature difference between the preset                temperature and the current temperature is                determined (S103) whether it belongs to the                steady-state interval. The minimum power for                indirect heating of inner wall in the first preset                period is acquired (S104) under the condition that                the temperature difference belongs to the                steady-state interval, if the temperature                difference between preset temperature and the                current temperature belongs to steady-state                interval. The set power is generated (S105)                according to minimum power. The set power is                adjusted (S106) to the initial power of indirect                heating of inner wall in the second preset period                continuous with the first preset period, where the                set power is greater than or equal to the minimum                power.
   USE - Method for controlling carbon dioxide                incubator using carbon dioxide incubator control                system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly pre intervenes the initial                power of the next cycle according to the current                operation state, realizes the initiative of                temperature control and improves the control                effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for controlling a carbon dioxide incubator.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining the current temperature                and preset temperature of the interior of the                carbon dioxide incubator (S101)
   Step for outputting indirect heating power of                the inner wall according to the preset PID control                algorithm (S102)
   Step for determining whether temperature                difference between the preset temperature and the                current temperature belongs to the steady-state                interval (S103)
   Step for acquiring minimum power for indirect                heating of the inner wall in the first preset                period under the condition that the temperature                difference belongs to the steady-state interval                (S104)
   Step for generating set power according to                the minimum power (S105)
   Step for adjusting set power to the initial                power of the indirect heating of the inner wall in                the second preset period continuous with the first                preset period (S106)

PN CN109553598-A
TI Extracting baicalein from the root of Scutellaria Comprises e.g. placing selected Scutellaria root in water, washing, placing in oven for drying, peeling, placing in a blender and stirring for crushing into a powder.
AU REN X
AE REN X (RENX-Individual)
GA 201932489R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting baicalein from the root of Scutellaria comprises e.g. placing selected Scutellaria root in water, and then washing each root of the Scutellaria, placing the washed Scutellaria root in an oven for drying, peeling the dried radix root, placing the peeled Scutellaria root in a blender and stirring for crushing the peeled Scutellaria root into a powder, placing the Scutellaria powder in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting in an extraction environment, performing the first extraction by introducing carbon dioxide, allowing to stand the first extracted object at a normal temperature for a period of time, taking the first extracted object, depositing the first upper layer object in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle for a second extraction, allowing to stand the second extracted object at a normal temperature for a period of time.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting baicalein from the root of Scutellaria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the extraction efficiency of baicalein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting baicalein from the root of Scutellaria comprises placing selected Scutellaria root in water for 12-24 hours, and then washing each root of the Scutellaria, placing the washed Scutellaria root in an oven for drying, peeling the dried radix root, placing the peeled Scutellaria root in a blender and stirring for crushing the peeled Scutellaria root into a powder having a particle diameter of 50-250 mu m, placing the Scutellaria powder in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting in an extraction environment with an analytical temperature of 15-60 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 6-20 MPa, performing the first extraction by introducing carbon dioxide having a flow rate of 20-50 l/hour, allowing to stand the first extracted object at a normal temperature for a period of time, taking the first extracted object, depositing the first upper layer object in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle for a second extraction, allowing to stand the second extracted object at a normal temperature for a period of time, and taking the second upper layer, absorbing the second upper layer object and analyzing by a resin, and drying to obtain an extract of baicalein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The Scutellaria root is placed in water for 12-24 hours, and the cleaning of each of the Scutellaria roots is then carried out by placing the picked Scutellaria roots in water for 12 hours and then washing each of the Scutellaria roots. The peeled Scutellaria root is placed in a blender and stirred to cause the peeled Scutellaria root to be crushed into a powder having a particle diameter of 100 mu m. The extraction environment is at an analytical temperature of 15-60 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 6-20 MPa. The first extraction is carried out after the introduction of carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 20-50 l/hour, comprising the first extraction with a flow rate of 35 l/hour of carbon dioxide in an extraction environment with an analytical temperature of 35 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 8 MPa. The adsorbing and analyzing the second upper layer object by the resin specifically comprises adsorbing and analyzing the second upper layer object through a resin having a pore diameter of 200 nm.

PN CN107583628-A
TI Production of deionized acetate-loaded silver resin adsorbent, involves adding deionized water to reactor, heating, removing carbon dioxide from deionized water and dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water.
AU WANG Z
AE WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
GA 201809237W
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of deionized acetate-loaded silver resin adsorbent involves adding deionized water to reactor, heating, removing carbon dioxide from deionized water, dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water, cooling, filtering to obtain filtrate and sodium hydroxide solution, dissolving silver nitrate in deionized water to remove carbon dioxide, cooling, filtering to obtain filtrate and silver nitrate solution, cooling, dripping silver nitrate solution to sodium hydroxide solution and controlling silver nitrate and sodium hydroxide at mass ratio of (2-4):1.
   USE - Production of deionized acetate-loaded silver resin adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of deionized acetate-loaded silver resin adsorbent ensuring strong binding ability and enhanced environmentally-friendly effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production method of deionized acetate-loaded silver resin adsorbent involves (1) adding deionized water to reactor, heating to boil for 10 minutes, removing carbon dioxide from deionized water, (2) dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water, cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain filtrate and sodium hydroxide solution, (3) dissolving silver nitrate in deionized water to remove carbon dioxide, cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain filtrate and silver nitrate solution, (4) cooling, dripping silver nitrate solution to sodium hydroxide solution, controlling mass ratio of silver nitrate in the silver nitrate solution and sodium hydroxide in sodium hydroxide solution at mass ratio of (2-4):1, stirring treatment, filtering and solid precipitate, washing solid precipitate with de-ionized water to remove both sodium and nitrate ions, washing until pH less than or equal to 7.5, adding acetic acid, stirring, adding raw resin to reactor, the mass ratio of raw material resin and acetic acid at 2:1, (2) heating with stirring at 80-100 degrees C for 1 hour, cooling and filtering, (3) filtering the resin, repeatedly washing until the resin is colorless, decolorizing cleaning resin, adding resin and deionized water to reactor, stirring, mixing silver oxide particles and deionized water, and removing carbon dioxide, mixing, stirring at room temperature for 2-4 hours and filtering to obtain de-iodized acetate loaded silver resin adsorbent.

TI System for monitoring respiration and weight loss of user in home or office by computing device, has computing device for determining mass of carbon exhaled in breath and concentration of carbon dioxide in breath.
AU BATTAGLIA V
   MEERMAN R
AE BREATHEFIT LLC (BREA-Non-standard)
   BREATHEFIT LLC (BREA-Non-standard)
GA 2022791417
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a capture device (102) provided with a first device to detect volume of air expelled during a breath. A second device detects concentration of carbon dioxide in the breath. A computing device (104) detects volumetric changes in chest and abdomen during breathing. The computing device receives the volume of air expelled during the breath from the capture device and concentration of carbon dioxide in the breath from the capture device. The computing device determines mass of the carbon exhaled in the breath using the correlated change in the chest and/or abdomen volume and concentration of carbon dioxide in the breath.
   USE - System for monitoring respiration and weight loss of a user in a home or office (claimed) by a computing device. Uses include but are not limited to smart phone, smart watch, tablet, and laptop or desktop computer.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively monitors and motivates weight loss by measuring exhaled carbon dioxide.

   Capture device (102)

   Display (112)

PT P
PN US2021114871-A1
TI Producing syngas of dihydrogen and carbon dioxide involves generating transition metal carbide by reacting corresponding transition metal oxide with fuel and combining transition metal carbide with oxygen to oxidize transition metal carbide.
AU MAHINPEY N
   VIRLA A L D
   FERNANDES D O C
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
GA 202142103V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing syngas including at least dihydrogen and carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) generating a transition metal carbide by reacting a corresponding transition metal oxide with a fuel to produce a stream of syngas; and (b) combining the transition metal carbide with oxygen to oxidize the transition metal carbide to regenerate the corresponding transition metal oxide and heat, thus producing a gas output comprising at least one oxidized carbon compound.
   USE - The process is useful for producing syngas including at least dihydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: produces syngas that is used for production of dihydrogen in a separate process, when the stream of syngas has a ratio of dihydrogen/carbon dioxide exceeding 3; produces syngas that is used in a separate Fischer-Tropsch process, methanol production process or dimethyl ether production process, when the stream of syngas has a ratio of dihydrogen/carbon dioxide below 3; runs at high temperatures of 575-1025degrees Celsius and usually is carried out in fluidized bed reactor, due to its operating conditions, sintering and attrition may develop and impact the process performance; and utilizes carbides that are used as catalyst for dry reforming reactions, which can improve the achievement of high conversion of methane into syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises: cycling between steps (a) and (b); and routing at least a portion of the gas output of the second reactor to mix with the fuel prior to providing the fuel to the first reactor. The step (a) is performed in a first reactor, step (b) is performed in a second reactor, where the transition metal carbide is transferred to the second reactor prior to step (b) and the corresponding transition metal oxide is transferred back to the first reactor before cycling to step (a). The transition metal of the transition metal carbide and the corresponding transition metal oxide is molybdenum, and the process is initiated using heptamolybdate tetrahydrate. The transition metal of the transition metal carbide and the corresponding transition metal oxide is tungsten and the process is initiated using ammonium metatungstate hydrate. The temperature in the first reactor is 525-1125degrees Celsius, preferably 525-1025degrees Celsius. The heat generated in the second reactor is used to provide heat to the first reactor. The excess heat generated in the second reactor not provided to the first reactor is used in a separate process. The carbon dioxide is mixed with the fuel in step (a). The nitrogen is mixed with the fuel in step (a) to produce nitrogen compounds for production of fertilizer or ammonia. The process is performed on fixed bed reactors with switching of gas composition to alternate between a reducing gas mixture and an oxidizing gas mixture with inert gas purging prior to the switching, thus generating the oxidized carbon compounds without moving the transition metal carbide and the transition metal oxide.Preferred Components: The oxygen is in a mixture of gases. The mixture of gases is air. The transition metal of the transition metal carbide and the corresponding transition metal oxide is molybdenum, tungsten (both preferred), manganese or zirconium. The fuel comprises methane, 1-4C hydrocarbon, a fossil fuel mixture, biomass or coal. The fuel is greater than 30% methane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing barium titanate, which involves mixing barium compound chosen from barium hydroxides, chlorides, inorganic acid salts other than carbonates, organic acid salts, and oxides, and carbon dioxide and/or water-soluble carbonate compounds, before and/or during the mixing step, obtaining barium carbonate by adding a water-soluble group (IV) transition metal compound to at least one of carbon dioxide and/or water-soluble carbonate compound or their mixture, mixing the barium carbonate and titanium compound, and calcining the mixture obtained in the mixing step.

The water-soluble group (IV) transition metal compound is a water-soluble titanium compound. Preferred Method: The method further involves before and/or during the mixing step, adding citric acid and/or its salt to alkaline earth metal compound and carbon dioxide and/or water-soluble carbonate compound or their mixture.

PN KR2132120-B1
TI Recycling of spent lithium-ion secondary battery            of electronic device, involves mechanically pretreating            electrode material, and electrolyte to obtain            pulverized product, extracting e.g. cobalt, and            preparing lithium peroxide solution.
AU PARK T H
AE PARK T H (PARK-Individual)
GA 202062414M
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of spent lithium-ion secondary                battery involves (a) separating and recovering the                positive electrode material, the negative electrode                material, the separator, and the electrolyte,                mechanically pretreating the electrode materials to                obtain a pulverized product, (b) subjecting                separation membrane to supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction at 30-55 degrees C at 20-40 Pa, (c)                separating graphite by pulverizing product, using                an air jet sieve, (d) acid-leaching by adding an                acid solution to the product, (e) extracting and                recovering metal(s) chosen from manganese, cobalt,                and nickel, (f) extracting lithium hydroxide by                adding hydroxide to acid solution, (g) adding                hydrogen peroxide to lithium hydroxide solution to                prepare lithium peroxide solution, (h) adding                carbon dioxide to the obtained lithium peroxide                solution to prepare a lithium carbonate solution,                in which the carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is                largely reduced by using carbon dioxide in the air                in the steps (b), (h), and (e).
   USE - Recycling of spent lithium-ion secondary                battery of electronic device. Uses include but are                not limited to electric vehicle, energy storage                device, mobile phone, and laptop.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly                recycling of spent lithium-ion secondary battery by                reducing carbon dioxide in the air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves recovering battery material(s) chosen                  from aluminum, copper, and iron, between the                  steps (c) and (d). Preferred Components: The                  current collector crushed product made of                  aluminum and copper, and carbon, lithium, iron,                  manganese, and cobalt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte is                  chosen from dimethyl sulfoxide, propylene                  carbonate, diethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate,                  and dimethyl carbonate.
