PT P
PN CN111007233-A; CN111007233-B
TI Method for analyzing movement behavior of            methane-carbon dioxide in micropores of shale, involves            constructing three-dimensional model of interlayer            structure of clay minerals, and analyzing substitution            effect of carbon dioxide on methane.
AU ZHU H
   LIANG X
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG C
   GUO X
   LI J
   SHU D
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   PETROCHINA ZHEJIANG OILFIELD CO (CNPC-C)
GA 2020322325
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting field profile                samples and core data, and simulating the                characteristics of shale reservoirs. The pore                structure characteristics of the shale surface are                visually observed, and fine pore structure images                are obtained. The influence mechanism of shale                organic matter and clay mineral nano-scale pore                structure on the occurrence state of shale gas is                analyzed. The adsorption and diffusion capacity of                shale micropores are evaluated. Three-dimensional                model of the interlayer structure of clay minerals                is constructed based on the composition of clay                minerals. The substitution effect of carbon dioxide                on methane is analyzed based on the quenching                density functional (QSDFT) molecular simulation                method and the dissipative particle dynamics (DPD)                method. The simulation results are tested and                verified by experiment. Comprehensive analysis is                carried out using gas content data, X-ray                diffraction test data and carbon dioxide methane                experimental data.
   USE - Method for analyzing movement behavior of                methane-carbon dioxide in micropores of                shale.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-dimensional pore structure                characteristics of shale heterogeneous reservoirs                can be characterized realistically, economically                and accurately. The analysis accuracy of the size                sample is greatly improved. Good application                prospect in actual production can be                facilitated.

A circulation pipe (21) is provided through which carbondioxide circulates. A first heater (22) heats carbon dioxide circulating in the circulation pipe through heat exchange with the exhaust gas. A carbondioxide turbine (23) is supplied and driven by carbondioxide that has passed through the first heater. A cooler (24) cools carbondioxide that has passed through the carbondioxide turbine. A pump or a second compressor (25) compresses carbondioxide that has passed through the cooler. An engine cooling heat exchanger (26) cools the engine through heat exchange with carbondioxide that has passed through the second compressor. The load of the cooler is reduced by heating the carbon dioxide before being supplied to heater to increase heating efficiency and cooling the carbon dioxide before being supplied to the cooler. The energy input is minimized, since an integrated power generation system that supplies that as a working fluid, thus increasing power generation efficiency and lowering the power generation cost.

AE BLUE UNIT AS (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE UNIT AS (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE UNIT AS (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE UNIT AS (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 2019139431
AB    NOVELTY - The sampling and detection system (1) has a                membrane block (2) including a liquid sample inlet                port and a sample outlet port, a sample flow path                extended between both ports, and two membrane units                arranged in series in the sample flow path. Each                membrane unit has a sample flow on one side and a                carrier gas flow on the other side. A closed loop                gas flow path (10) includes a gas circulation                device, such as an air pump (16), and the gas flow                sides of the unit. A carbon dioxide gas sensor (15)                and or a hydrogen sulfide detection unit are set in                the loop.
   USE - Carbon dioxide and or hydrogen sulfide                sampling and detection system for aqueous liquids,                e.g., freshwater, brackish water, seawater. Used                for determining the carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide content in a liquid broth, such as in                pharmaceutical production processes, food or feed                preparation processes, or similar processes, that                involves a fermentation processes and/or water                treatment processes, as well as in fish farms (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Allows for high measurement speed, resulting                in a rapid turn-around between measurements.                Enables to not only protect the sensor head from                condensation, but also allows the probe to rapidly                drop to a near zero carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide levels between measurements, providing                further a rapid turn-around between measurements.                Ensures effective circulation of the gas, resulting                in the equilibrium on carbon dioxide                concentration/partial pressure across the membranes                being obtained faster, and quickly obtaining                reliable results on the carbon dioxide content in                the liquid samples.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for sampling and detection of carbon dioxide                and/or hydrogen sulfide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the top view of a sampling                and detection system.
   Sampling and detection system (1)
   Membrane block (2)
   Closed loop gas flow path (10)
   Carbon dioxide gas sensor (15)
   Air pump (16)

PN CN114931853-A
TI Preparing low-carbon cement by collecting cement            kiln flue gas carbon dioxide used as raw material,            involves adding premixed steel slag micropowder,            spraying reinforcing agent, collecting transported            carbon-fixed steel slag powder, cement clinker and            gypsum in hopper and grinding.
AU LIU Z
   DONG S
   ZHONG J
   MO L
AE SUZHOU BONIUQIANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022B6012B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-carbon cement by collecting                cement kiln flue gas carbon dioxide involve (a)                adding steel slag micropowder into the mixing                equipment for pre-mixing, adding an activation                additive during the pre-mixing process. (b) adding                the premixed activated steel slag micro powder into                a flue gas suspension reactor through the feeding                system for mineralization and capturing of flue gas                carbon dioxide, (c) spraying the carbon dioxide                mineralization enhancer into the flue gas                suspension reactor through the atomization device,                (d) discharging the carbon-fixing steel slag                material from the reactor and transporting to a                storage hopper, after the steel slag fine powder                reaches the predetermined reaction time in the flue                gas suspension reactor, transporting the                carbon-fixing steel slag powder in the flue gas to                the hopper after gas-solid separation, and (e)                collecting the carbon-fixed steel slag powder,                cement clinker and gypsum in the hopper and                grinding.
   USE - Preparation of low-carbon cement (claimed)                used as basic raw material for construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of continuously                capturing high efficiency of steel slag continuous                mineralization and capturing cement kiln flue gas                carbon dioxide with high efficiency, is short                process flow, strong continuity, large flue-gas                carbon dioxide capturing amount to effectively                reduce the carbon emission from cement industry and                realizes high added value resource utilization of                steel-slag and large solid waste. The carbon                capture product can be used as high-hydration                active cement mixed material with excellent                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of low-carbon cement by collecting                cement kiln flue gas carbon dioxide involve (a)                adding steel slag micropowder into the mixing                equipment for pre-mixing, adding an activation                additive during the pre-mixing process. (b) adding                the premixed activated steel slag micro-powder into                a flue gas suspension reactor through the feeding                system for mineralization and capturing of flue gas                carbon dioxide, (c) spraying the carbon dioxide                mineralization reinforcing agent into the flue gas                suspension reactor through the atomization device,                (d) discharging the carbon-fixing steel slag                material from the reactor and transporting to a                storage hopper, after the steel slag fine powder                reaches the predetermined reaction time in the flue                gas suspension reactor, transporting the                carbon-fixing steel slag powder in the flue gas to                the hopper after gas-solid separation, and (e)                collecting the carbon-fixed steel slag powder,                cement clinker and gypsum in the hopper and                grinding. The carbon dioxide mineralization                reinforcing agent contains 95-100 parts mass water,                0.5-1.5 parts mass ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid,                and 0.1-1 part mass hydroxyethyldiisopropanolamine                or contains water, 1-3 parts mass                ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 0.5-1.5 parts mass                N,N-dimethylacetamide, 0.1-1 part mass piperazine,                and 0.1-1 part mass hydroxyethyl                diisopropanolamine. The usage amount of reinforcing                agent is 1-10 %mass of the steel slag micro-powder                in the step (a). The additive is prepared from                mixed solution of water, sodium carbonate,                salicylic acid, tartaric acid and polycarboxylic                acid in the mass ratio of                92-98:1-2:0.5-1.5:0.5-1.5:0.1-0.5. The flue gas is                obtained from kiln tail exhaust flue of the dry                rotary kiln. The carbon dioxide concentration in                the flue gas is 10-30%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The content of                  additive is 3-10 %mass of the steel slag                  powder.

PT P
PN WO2022163819-A1
TI Oxygen carrier used for reducing carbon dioxide            and chemical looping, comprises metal oxide comprising            indium and element other than indium.
AU KOJI T
   KEISUKE I
   RISA S
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202297854V
AB    NOVELTY - An oxygen carrier comprises a metal oxide                comprising indium and an element M other than                indium having a Pauling electronegativity of                1-2.
   USE - The oxygen carrier is used for reducing carbon                dioxide and chemical looping.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen carrier has high conversion                efficiency into carbon monoxide and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas                production method, involving contacting the oxygen                carrier with a raw material gas containing carbon                dioxide, and reducing the carbon dioxide to obtain                a product gas containing carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The element M is at                  least one of the elements belonging to 3-6th                  periods. The element M is element belonging to                  groups 3-14. The element M comprises aluminum,                  silicon, scandium, titanium, vanadium, chromium,                  manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc,                  gallium, yttrium, zirconium, niobium, silver,                  hafnium, lanthanum, cerium, samarium and                  gadolinium. The oxygen carrier comprises a binder                  binds the metal oxide. The binder is chosen from                  zeolite, aluminum Oxide, titanium dioxide,                  silicon dioxide, zirconium dioxide and magnesium                  oxide. Preferred Composition: The ratio of indium                  to all elements other than oxygen contained in                  the metal oxide is 10-85 mol%. The amount of the                  binder is 70 parts mass or less with respect to                  100 parts mass of the oxygen carrier. Preferred                  Property: The electronegativity of the element M                  is 1.1-1.91, preferably 1.1-1.88. Preferred                  Method: The oxygen carrier is reduced by                  contacting with a reducing gas containing                  hydrogen and used to produce carbon values by                  contacting the reduced oxygen carrier with carbon                  dioxide. The oxygen carrier is used to reduce                  carbon dioxide by contact with a feed gas                  containing carbon dioxide to produce a product                  gas containing carbon monoxide. The oxygen                  carrier is used in separate reaction steps for                  the reduction of carbon dioxide and the reduction                  of the oxidized oxygen carrier.
   EXAMPLE - Indium nitrate trihydrate (purity oof 97%) and                lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate (purity of 99.9%),                iron (III) nitrate nonahydrate (purity of 99.9%)                and strontium nitrate, the raw material was                dissolved in 200 ml of distilled water to prepare                an aqueous raw material solution. The molar ratio                of indium, lanthanum, iron and strontium in the                liquid was set to 0.15/0.15/0.35/0.35. Citric acid                was added to this raw material aqueous solution at                65&#176; C in molar amount of 2 equivalents                with respect to the total molar amount of metal                ions in the raw material aqueous solution. After 30                minutes, ethylene glycol (purity of 99.5%) was                added to the aqueous solution and the temperature                was raised to 80&#176; C. Ethylene glycol was                added in molar amount of 1.2 equivalents with                respect to the added citric acid. The temperature                of the mixture was maintained at 80&#176; C                with continuous stirring until viscous gel was                formed. The gel was transferred to drying oven and                dried at 100&#176; C for 20 hours. The obtained                mass was pulverized, heated to 850&#176; C at                rate of temperature increase of 5&#176;                C/minute and then fired at 850&#176; C for 7                hours oxygen carriers was obtained.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.)

TI Increasing permeability of non-solidified            sandstone reservoir by using carbon dioxide aqueous            solution comprises e.g. designing carbon            dioxide-aqueous solution extraction system, and            analyzing change of calcium ion after            implementation.
AU LEI H
   LIN H
   CUI Y
   BAI B
   FANG Z
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 2021A4493J
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing permeability of non-solidified                sandstone reservoir by using carbon dioxide aqueous                solution comprises (1) designing carbon                dioxide-aqueous solution extraction system, (2)                designing carbon dioxide-aqueous solution injection                system, (3) pumping and calculating the reservoir                permeability coefficient and performing pumping                experiment after perforating the wellbore, opening                the submersible pump and second valve through the                carbon dioxide-aqueous extraction system, and                calculating the initial reservoir permeability k                through the experimental formula of steady flow                pumping, (4) assembling mixer, first temperature                and pressure sensor and the carbon dioxide-aqueous                injection system in the basic system, detecting                bromine-concentration, analyzing the change of                calcium ion after the implementation of the                enhancement process, and (5) continuing well                cylinder to injection-simmering-withdrawal process                until calculated reservoir permeability coefficient                k reaches the mining requirement.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing                permeability of non-solidified sandstone reservoir                by using carbon dioxide aqueous solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves to certain extent the                problem that unconsolidated sandstone reservoirs                cannot be modified to increase permeability with                high strength e.g. hydraulic fracturing, strong                acid acidification; significantly improves the                permeability of the reservoir; and is simple and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Increasing permeability of non-solidified                sandstone reservoir by using carbon dioxide aqueous                solution comprises (1) designing carbon                dioxide-aqueous solution extraction system, (2)                designing carbon dioxide-aqueous solution injection                system, (3) pumping and calculating the reservoir                permeability coefficient and performing pumping                experiment after perforating the wellbore, opening                the submersible pump and second valve through the                carbon dioxide-aqueous extraction system, flowing                formation water through the steam-water separator,                entering the water to the water storage tank after                passing through the steam-water separator, entering                the gas to the atmosphere through the pipe at the                top of the steam-water separator until the second                liquid flow meter monitors the water storage tank,                stopping pumping after the amount of water stored                in reaches the experimental preset value, recording                the monitoring values of the first liquid flow                meter, second liquid flow meter, first gas flow                meter and the water level monitor at all times                during the pumping process, sampling the extracted                formation water and performing full analysis of ion                concentration, especially calcium ion to record,                and calculating the initial reservoir permeability                k through the experimental formula of steady flow                pumping according to the measured data and the                original data of the well, (4) assembling the                mixer, the first temperature and pressure sensor                and the carbon dioxide-aqueous injection system in                the basic system, injecting the carbon                dioxide-aqueous solution into the first one-way                valve and connecting the second one in the system                to the inlet end of the mixer, adding sodium                bromide tracer to the water in the water storage                tank before injection, opening the pressure                reducing valve, adjusting to the preset pressure,                opening the first valve, third valve, fourth valve,                first one-way valve, second one-way valve and water                injection pump, flowing the carbon dioxide in the                first carbon dioxide tanker and the water in the                water storage tank into the mixer, dissolving in                the mixer and continuously conveying to the shaft,                closing the pressure reducing valve, first valve,                third valve, fourth valve, first one-way valve,                second one-way valve, water injection pump and the                first valve when the injection volume of carbon                dioxide and water reaches the preset value,                stopping the injection, so that the cylinder is in                sealed state, starting simmering, using the first                temperature and pressure sensor to monitor the                wellhead pressure and temperature at all times                during the simmering period, and stopping the                simmering time when the required duration of the                simmering is reached, closing the mixer, first                temperature and pressure sensor and the carbon                dioxide-aqueous solution injection system in the                basic system after the simmering of the well,                repeating the step (3) through the carbon                dioxide-aqueous solution extraction system,                calculating the permeability coefficient k of the                reservoir after simmering the well according to the                steady flow pumping test formula, taking water                sample from the second sampling port on the                steam-water separator for ion concentration                analysis, comparing with the initial calcium ion                concentration, determining the degree of reaction                between the carbon dioxide-aqueous solution and the                rock, where the higher the calcium ion                concentration, the stronger the reaction and the                more obvious the effect of increasing permeability,                detecting the bromine-concentration and comparing                the changes of bromine concentration before and                after the reaction to determine the mixing ratio of                formation water and injection liquid during the                injection-simmering process, taking water samples                every 2-3 hours during the withdrawal process to                analyze the ion concentration, analyzing the change                of calcium ion after the implementation of the                enhancement process, and (5) continuing the well                cylinder to injection-simmering-withdrawal process                until the calculated reservoir permeability                coefficient k reaches the mining requirement if the                value of k cannot meet the requirements of                economical and production according to the                reservoir permeability coefficient k after braising                the well calculated.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing formate from carbon dioxide involves contacting carbon dioxide with a composition comprising (a) a metal-containing complex compound of formula (I), or its salts, optical isomers, geometric isomers, salts of isomers, derivatives, and solvent associated complexes of it, (b) a solution solvent, and (c) an acid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing formate from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to pre-treat the gas (e.g., air or flue gas) prior to the contacting (e.g., prior to contacting the composition, the first composition, or the second composition).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing formate from carbon dioxide involves contacting carbon dioxide with a composition comprising (a) a metal-containing complex compound of formula (I), or its salts, optical isomers, geometric isomers, salts of isomers, derivatives, and solvent associated complexes of it, (b) a solution solvent, and (c) an acid.
   R1 = monovalent H, halo, -CN, nitro (-NO2), -NH2, -N(CH3)2, or T1;#T1 = hydroxy, methanoyl, carboxy, 1-10C alkyl, 2-10C alkenyl, 2-10C alkynyl, 1-9C alkoxy, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl, or heteroaryl, which are optionally substituted with X1;#X1 = halo, oxo, hydroxy, methanoyl, carboxy, nitro, -NH2, -N(CH3)2, cyano, ethynyl, propynyl, sulfo, morpholinyl, -CO-morpholin-4-yl, phenyl, -CONH2, -CON(CH3)2, 1-3C alkyl, 1-3C perfluoronated alkyl, -CF3, -OCF3, or 1-3C alkoxy;#R2 = monovalent H, halo, -CN, nitro, -NH2, -N(CH3)2, or T1;#R3 = monovalent H, halo, -CN, or T1;#R3 and R4 = optionally bonded together to form a ring, where the ring that is formed can be cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl, or heteroaryl, which cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl, or heteroaryl optionally substituted with X1;and#M = Fe, Ni, Pd, Cd, Mn, Co, Cu, Ga, or Zn.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing formate from carbon dioxide involving (a) contacting carbon dioxide with a first composition to provide a second composition, where the first composition comprises the compound (I), and the solution solvent, and (b) adding acid to the second composition to produce formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution solvent comprises acetonitrile, methanol, ethanol, propanol, n-butanol, pentanol (all preferred), water, 1-5C alcohol, butanol, and/or hexanol. The acid is acetic acid (preferred), triethylammonium, piperidinium tetrafluoroborate, and/or phosphoric acid. The composition further comprises a hydride source, and electrode. The hydride source is a chemical hydride source or an electrochemical hydride source. The chemical hydride source is a metal hydride, sodium borohydride, or lithium aluminium hydride. The electrochemical hydride source or the electrode is a platinum electrode, a palladium electrode, or a glassy carbon electrode. The carbon dioxide is obtained from source chosen from air, waste gas of a powerplant, byproduct of a chemical reaction, and/or byproduct of a catalytic reaction.Preferred Method: In the method, the temperature of the composition is 0-99degrees Celsius.Preferred Composition: The concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas is 0.01-90%.Preferred Property: The acid has a pKa of not greater than 14.
   Preferred Definitions - R3 and R4 are bonded together to form cyclobutyl, cyclopentyl, cyclohexyl, chlorocyclohexyl, fluorocyclohexyl, methoxycyclohexyl, ethoxycyclohexyl, methylcyclohexyl, trifluoromethylcyclohexyl, cycloheptyl, cyclooctyl, pyrrolidinyl, pyrrolinyl, imidazolidinyl, pyrazolidinyl, piperidyl, piperazinyl, indolinyl, morpholinyl, 5-hydroxy pyridyl, indolyl, 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinolyl, furyl, 1,2-methylenedioxyphenyl, 2,3-methylenedioxyphenyl, 2-chlorophenyl, 3-chlorophenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 2-fluorophenyl, 3-fluorophenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 2-methoxyphenyl, 3-methoxyphenyl, 4-methoxyphenyl, 2-methylphenyl, 3-methylphenyl, 4-methylphenyl, 2-ethoxyphenyl, 3-ethoxyphenyl, 4-ethoxyphenyl, 2-trifluoromethyl phenyl, 3-trifluoromethyl phenyl, 4-trifluoromethyl phenyl, 2-trifluoromethoxy phenyl, 3-trifluoromethoxy phenyl, 4-trifluoromethoxy phenyl, 3,5-dimethoxyphenyl, 3,5-diethoxyphenyl, 3,5-dimethylphenyl, 3,5-diethylphenyl, 3,5-dihydroxyphenyl, 3,5-difluorophenyl, 3,5-dichlorophenyl, 3,5-bis-trifluoromethyl phenyl, phenyl, naphthyl, tolyl, xylyl, benzyl, pyridyl, 3-pyridyl, or methylenedioxyphenyl;and#M1 = Zn.

   ADVANTAGE - The polymer: can catalyze the cycloaddition                reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide to generate                cyclic carbonate under mild conditions; is                separated from the product by high-speed                centrifugation; can be reused; and provides more                selectivity for heterogeneous catalysts for the                cycloaddition of epoxides to carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Organic porous polymer of formula (I) is                new.
   R = 1-10 benzene rings-containing aromatic                aldehyde organic compound.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I) comprises (i) mixing aromatic aldehyde                  compound, ethidium bromide and reaction solvent                  uniformly, (ii) adding acetic acid and reacting                  under heating and negative pressure conditions,                  and (iii) centrifugating at room temperature,                  washing, and vacuum drying to obtain final                  product.Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  adding organic porous polymer and epoxide into a                  autoclave, sealing it, and filling it with carbon                  dioxide three times at room temperature to remove                  the air in the autoclave, (ii) filling carbon                  dioxide until the pressure in the reactor reaches                  0.6-1.8 MPa, and reacting at 50-150degrees                  Celsius for 6-15 hours, and (iii) centrifugal                  separating the catalyst organic porous polymer                  and the reaction product cyclic carbonate. In                  step (ii), the reaction temperature is                  100-160degrees CelsiusC, and the reaction time is                  2-6 days.Preferred Components: The aromatic aldehyde                  compounds are 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxaldehyde,                  (1,1'-biphenyl)-3,4',5-tricarbaldehyde,                  1,3,5-tris(p-formylphenyl)benzene or                  (1,1':4',1'':3'',1''':4''',1''''-Quinquephenyl)-4,4''''-dicarboxaldehyde,                  5''-(4'-formyl(1,1'-biphenyl)-4-yl)-. The                  reaction solvent is 1,4-dioxane standard                  solution, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  1,2-dichlorobenzene standard solution, n-butanol,                  1,3,5-trimethylhexahydro-1,3,5-triazine or                  dimethyl sulfoxide. In step (iii), the washing                  solvent is tetrahydrofuran, methanol and/or ethyl                  acetate. The molar ratio between aromatic                  aldehyde compound, ethidium bromide, reaction                  solvent and acetic acid is                  1.9-2.2:3:0.5-0.1:0.0008-0.001. The epoxide is                  propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin,                  epibromopropane, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl                  ether, epoxy cyclohexane, and/or epoxy                  cyclopentane.

TI Producing polyether carbonate polyols comprises            adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to            hydrogen-functional starter substance in presence of            double metal cyanide catalyst under atmosphere of            carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU VOR D E L
   WOLF A
   MACHAT M
   GUERTLER C
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021E30958
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyether carbonate polyols                comprises adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide                to hydrogen-functional starter substance in                presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst                or a metal complex catalyst based on the metals                zinc and/or cobalt under atmosphere of a gas                mixture containing carbon dioxide and 0.01-10 vol.%                hydrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for producing polyether                carbonate polyols.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether carbonate polyol is economical                with improved selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas mixture                  additionally contains carbon monoxide and/or                  nitrogen. The gas mixture has 0.5-7 vol.%                  hydrogen. The H-functional starter substance                  includes ethylene glycol, propylene glycol,                  1,3-propanediol, 1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol,                  1,5-pentanediol, 2-methylpropane-1,3-diol,                  neopentyl glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol,                  diethylene glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerin,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol,                  polyether carbonate polyols (molecular weight of                  150-8000 g/mol with a functionality of 2-3) and                  polyether polyols (molecular weight of 150-8000                  g/mol with a functionality of 2-3). Preferred                  Method: The method comprises placing partial                  amount of hydrogen-functional starter substance                  and/or suspending agent which does not contain                  any hydrogen-functional group in a reactor,                  optionally together with DMC catalyst and/or                  metal complex catalyst based on the metals zinc                  and/or cobalt, adding partial amount of alkylene                  oxide into the mixture at 90-150 degrees C,                  continuously transferring to a postreactor, where                  the content of free alkylene oxide in the                  reaction mixture is reduced in each case by means                  of post reaction. The hydrogen-functional starter                  substance, alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide are                  continuously metered into the reactor during the                  reaction.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of comparitive                example of production of polyether carbonate polyol                that do not support the claims.

PN CN112657477-A
TI Preparing cerium-doped mesoporous molecular sieve for high-efficiency adsorption of environmental carbon dioxide, involves preparing mesoporous molecular sieve containing organic matter, and calcining mesoporous molecular sieve.
AU YANG B
   BAI X
   WU H
   ZHOU W
   LI P
   YU X
   PAN X
   WANG L
   WU J
   LV P
   GENG M
   ZHAO Y
   HAN W
   JING Y
   WANG Y
   SHEN C
   MA J
AE STATE GRID XIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID SHAANXI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELE (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID JIBEI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELECT (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID SHAANXI ELECTRIC POWER CO (SGCC-C)
GA 202148307W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cerium-doped mesoporous molecular sieve involves preparing a mesoporous molecular sieve containing organic matter, and calcining the mesoporous molecular sieve in multiple stages at high temperature to remove the template agent to form mesopores.
   USE - Method for preparing cerium-doped mesoporous molecular sieve for high-efficiency adsorption of environmental carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to efficiently and stably eliminate carbon dioxide in the ambient atmosphere, and reduce the escape of carbon dioxide and secondary pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: P123, tetrabutyl titanate, aluminum sulfate, sodium silicate, n-butylamine and cerium salt are used to hydrothermally synthesize a mesoporous molecular sieve containing organic matter. The mesoporous molecular sieve is ZSM-5 type, T-type, sodium A-type or MOR type molecular sieve. The P123 comprises aluminum sulfate, tetrabutyl titanate, sodium silicate and n-butylamine. The cerium salt is cerium nitrate, cerium chloride, or cerium carbonate.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111960441-A; CN213202386-U
TI Preparing baking soda for desulfurization,            comprises using wet heavy alkali and carbon dioxide gas            after heating as raw materials and feeding wet heavy            alkali and carbon dioxide into revolving production            device, reacting and drying.
AU ZHANG J
   LI X
   TAO X
   WANG G
   YAO F
   ZHANG C
AE SHANDONG HAITIAN BIOCHEMICAL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8203X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing baking soda for desulfurization,                comprises using wet heavy alkali after                centrifugation and carbon dioxide gas after heating                as raw materials, and feeding wet heavy alkali and                carbon dioxide respectively into a revolving                production device, reacting and drying to obtain                baking soda products.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing baking soda                for desulfurization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the process flow and                reduces the production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The feed temperature of                  wet heavy alkali in the production device is                  20-30 degrees C, the inlet temperature of the                  carbon dioxide is 120-180 degrees C, the internal                  pressure of the production device is less than                  0.1 MPa and the rotation speed of the production                  device is 2-10 revolutions/minute. The discharge                  temperature of the baking soda product in the                  production device is 70-80 degrees C and the                  outlet temperature of the gas in the production                  device is 100-110 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The gas discharged from the                  production device is separated by the gas-liquid                  separator after the washing process of the                  washing tower. The collected gas enters the                  internal part of the production device to                  participate in the reaction after being heated by                  the heater.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN KR1985307-B1; US2019226036-A1; US10975448-B2
TI New Clostridium sp. JS66 strain useful for producing 4-6C metabolite, and fixing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU UM Y
   KIM J
   LEE S M
   GONG G T
   LEE S
   GONG G
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 201950491T
AB    NOVELTY - Clostridium sp. JS66 strain (KCTC13355BP), is new.
   USE - The strain is useful for producing 4-6C metabolite, and fixing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The strain reduces by-product production of acetic acid and ethanol and improves productivity of 6C metabolite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:(1)producing 4-6C metabolite, comprising treating substrate containing a carbon source with JS66 strain, and culturing; and (2)fixing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, comprising culturing Clostridium sp. JS66 strain by treating with gas mixture comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Method: The culturing process is carried out in a medium containing yeast extract at 25-30 degrees C under pH of 5-8, anaerobic conditions and total gas pressure of less than or equal to 3 bar. Preferred Components: The metabolite comprises at least one of butyric acid, butanol, hexanoic acid and hexanol. The JS66 strain produces 4-6C metabolite using at least one of glucose, fructose, galactose, xylose, arabinose, mannose, cellobiose, sucreose, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide as a carbon source, and a mixed gas containing at least one of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The JS66 strain has optimal culture temperature of 25-30 degrees C. The substrate comprises at least one of glucose, fructose, galactose, xylose, arabinose, mannose, cellobiose, and sucreose, and at least one of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The substrate further comprises hydrogen. The medium contains yeast extract at concentration of 0.1-10 g/l.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN114907525-A
TI Preparing low volatile organic            compounds-containing maleic anhydride grafted olefin            polymer, involves drying and melting maleic anhydride            grafted olefin polymer raw materials, injecting and            then removing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with            entrainer, into melt, granulating and drying            product.
AU ZHANG F
   YIN C
   ZHAN J
   ZHAO P
   JIA J
AE NINGBO NENGZHIGUANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2022A91827
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low volatile organic compounds                (VOC) maleic anhydride grafted olefin polymer,                involves: (1) mixing the raw materials of maleic                anhydride grafted olefin polymer raw material,                followed by drying and melting to form melt; (2)                injecting supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with                entrainer, into the melt; and (3) removing the                supercritical carbon dioxide from the melt,                followed by granulating and drying the                product.
   USE - Method is used for industrially preparing low                VOC maleic anhydride grafted olefin polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a simple and low cost process                which enables to prepare maleic anhydride-grafted                olefin polymers having low VOC content, with high                efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The maleic anhydride                  grafted olefin polymer is prepared using                  polyethylene, polypropylene and/or polyolefin                  elastomer as catalyst.The entrainer contained in carbon dioxide                  (CO2), is maleic anhydride used as entrainer                  and/or entrainer initiator. The maleic anhydride                  entrainer of CO2, is acetic acid, formic acid                  and/or ethyl acetate.The entrainer initiator of CO2, is methyl                  ether, aether, acetone and/or butanone.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  carbon dioxide to the melt, is (1 - 5): 100. The                  mass ratio of maleic anhydride entrainer and                  supercritical carbon dioxide used, is (2- 6):                  100. The mass ratio of the entrainer initiator                  and supercritical carbon dioxide, is (0.1- 2):                  100.Preferred Conditions: In step 2 of the                  preparation process, the pressure applied to                  inject the supercritical carbon dioxide into the                  melt, is 12- 20 millipascals (MPa).In step 3, the supercritical carbon dioxide                  removing process involved, is vacuum extraction                  process of vacuum degree more than 0.08                  MPa.Preferred Process: The preparation process                  is performed in a double-screw extruder. The                  temperature range of each area of the                  double-screw extruder is 120- 185 degrees                  centigrade and screw rotating speed is 180- 250                  rotations/minute (rpm). The supercritical carbon                  dioxide with entrainer, is injected into one of                  the three zones of twin screw extruders, namely                  third to fifth zone. The removal of CO2, is                  performed in two or three sections among the 10-                  12 sections of the extruder.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.)
MC A04-G01C; A04-F05; A10-E03; A10-E23; A11-A03; A11-A04; A08-A01A; A12-S09A
IP C08F-222/06; C08F-255/02; C08F-006/02

PN CN113698286-A
TI Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid, comprises            reacting first taking methanol and potassium hydroxide            as raw material to prepare potassium methylate, then            reacting potassium methylate and carbon dioxide and            reacting with phenol.
AU ZHANG X
   LIU L
   HE M
   KE Y
AE SUQIAN SIRUIYI NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUQI-Non-standard)
GA 2021E22338
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid (I), comprises                (i) first taking methanol and potassium hydroxide                as raw material to prepare potassium methylate,                then reacting potassium methylate and carbon                dioxide to generate monomethyl carbonate potassium                salt (II), and (ii) reacting phenol (III) with (II)                to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                p-hydroxybenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high product yield, easy                reaction operation, simple post-treatment and less                environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid of formula                (I), comprises (i) first taking methanol and                potassium hydroxide as raw material to prepare                potassium methylate, then reacting potassium                methylate and carbon dioxide to generate monomethyl                carbonate potassium salt of formula (II), and (ii)                reacting phenol of formula (III) with (II) to                obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The steps (i) comprises                  placing methanol and potassium hydroxide in a                  reaction vessel, heating at 90-110degreesC, and                  continuously removing the water produced in the                  reaction process until the content of potassium                  hydroxide and water is qualified in the reaction                  mixture to obtain potassium methoxide, and                  reacting potassium methoxide under 0.9-1.1 atm                  carbon monoxide atmosphere for 23-25 hours to                  obtain monomethyl carbonate potassium salt. The                  step (ii) comprises placing phenol and monomethyl                  carbonate potassium salt in a high pressure                  reaction vessel, sealing the high-pressure                  reaction vessel, and generating the gas pressure                  required for the reaction at the same time, then                  stirring, heating, and keeping the pressure                  constant, raising the temperature of the reactor                  to 190-210degrees Celsius, and reacting for 6-8                  hours, and post-treating to obtain                  p-hydroxybenzoic acid. The reactor temperature                  rose to 210degrees Celsius. The method comprises                  treating the reaction with water, extracting with                  toluene to remove unreacted phenol, acidifying                  the aqueous phase to pH to less than or equal to                  2 with hydrochloric acid, and separating the                  product by recrystallization from water, where                  fractionating the toluene extract to recover                  unreacted phenol, and then preparing                  p-hydroxybenzoic acid.Preferred Components: The gas is air, argon                  or carbon dioxide.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E11-F11; E11-F06; E11-F02B; E10-C03; E11-W

   USE - Preparation of glufosinate-ammonium.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, easy and                economical, and produces product with high yield                without need of desalination process, and does not                generate inorganic salts and large amount of waste                liquid during process, and is suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of glufosinate-ammonium involves                filling aqueous solution of hydantoin derivative of                formula (I) with carbon dioxide until the reaction                pressure is 1-4 MPa, heating to 120-180 degrees C,                reacting and post-treating.
   R = H or linear or branched 1-10C                hydrocarbyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The preparation                  involves reacting at 160-180 degrees C under                  pressure of 2-4 MPa, releasing the pressure,                  concentrating the under reduced pressure,                  recrystallizing, filtering and drying. TI Preparing nano flower-shaped sulfide useful in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, comprises mixing zinc salt, indium salt, thioacetamide and water, heating for reaction, cooling, centrifuging, washing and drying under vacuum.
AU GAO M
   CHI L
   NIU Z
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202176530H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano flower-shaped sulfide comprises mixing zinc salt, indium salt, thioacetamide and water, and heating to 120-180 degrees C for reaction, where the molar ratio of the indium salt and the zinc salt is 1:(0-2).
   USE - The nano flower-shaped sulfide is useful in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the adjustment of the morphology and phase of sulfide through a simple method of adjusting the ratio of reactants, can maintain the morphology and sulfur content unchanged for a long time under high current, so it has excellent carbon dioxide electrocatalytic stability, and high formic acid Faraday efficiency and current density in a long electrolysis time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfide is indium zinc sulfide when the molar ratio of the indium salt to the zinc salt is 1:(0.5-1), the sulfide is indium sulfide when the molar ratio of the indium salt to the zinc salt is 1:0, and the sulfide is indium zinc sulfide and zinc sulfide when the molar ratio of the indium salt to the zinc salt is 1:2. The zinc salt is zinc chloride, and the indium salt is indium chloride. The ratio of the total amount of the indium salt and the zinc salt to the water is (3-5) mmol:150 ml. The nano flower shape is composed of multiple large two-dimensional nano sheets. Preferred Method: The temperature of the hydro-thermal reaction is 140-160 degrees C and the time is 2-8 hours. The method further comprises cooling the obtained product to room temperature, centrifuging, firstly using de-ionized water to wash for 2-4 times, using ethanol to wash for 2-4 times, and drying under the vacuum for 4-5 hours.

PT P
PN CN112827498-A; CN112827498-B
TI Preparing iron oxide supported palladium catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide using palladium acetylacetonate, benzene series homolog as solvent and supporting palladium on surface of iron oxide by impregnation.
AU YANG B
   ZHANG D
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202159727K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing iron oxide supported palladium catalyst involves using palladium acetylacetonate as a palladium precursor, benzene series homolog or a benzene series derivative as a solvent and supporting metallic palladium on the surface of iron oxide by impregnation.
   USE - Method for preparing iron oxide supported palladium catalyst used in hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the residue of other active elements and improves the activity of the final catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing iron oxide supported palladium catalyst, where the loading amount of palladium is 0.02-8%; and
   (2) use of the iron oxide-supported palladium catalyst in the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i) mixing the palladium acetylacetonate with the solvent and ultrasonically dissolving to obtain the solution (A), (ii) adding iron oxide to solution (A) to obtain solution (B), and stirring the solution (B) at -10 to -80 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours under the condition of sealing to obtain solution (C), and (iii) subjecting the solution (C) to rotary distillation to separate the solvent, heat-treating the obtained solid at 190-400 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours in an oxidizing atmosphere to obtain the iron oxide-supported palladium catalyst. Preferred Conditions: In the solution (A), the concentration of palladium acetylacetonate is 0.003-0.77 g/l. In step (iii), the heat treatment process is carried out at 300 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the palladium acetylacetonate to iron oxide is 0.06-23%. In the reaction gas of the reaction, the volume ratio of hydrogen is 72%, the volume ratio of carbon dioxide is 24%, and the space velocity is 1.666 g/(lx minute), the product carbon monoxide selectivity is higher than 97%, and the conversion rate at 400 degrees C is not greater than 5%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The benzene series homologues or benzene series derivative comprises toluene, xylene, ethylbenzene and/or benzene.

PN CN112724416-A; CN112724416-B
TI New 2,2'-bipyridine based biological hydrogen-bonded organic framework material containing rhenium complex useful as catalyst in heterogeneous photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
AU YU B
   WANG H
   JIANG J
   LI L
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202147091U
AB    NOVELTY - 2,2'-Bipyridine based biological hydrogen-bonded organic framework material containing rhenium complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed), preferably heterogeneous photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 2,2'-Bipyridine based biological hydrogen-bonded organic framework material containing rhenium complex of formula (HOF-25-Re) (I) is new.
   G = 2-amino-9-octyl-6-oxo-6,7-dihydro-1H-purin-9-ium containing moieties of formula (1a); and
   A = 8-chloro-8,8,8-tris(oxidaniumylidynemethyl)-7lambdal(5),9lambdal(5)-diaza-8-rhenatricyclo(7.4.0.0(2,7))trideca-1(9),2,4,6,10,12-hexaene-7,9-bis(ylium).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises reacting 5,5'-diethynyl-2,2'-bipyridine (1a) with (2-(bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino)-8-bromo-9-octyl-purin-6-yl) tert-butyl carbonate (1b) in N,N-dimethylformamide under the action of triethylamine, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium and copper(I) iodide to obtain (2-(bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino)-8-(2-(6-(5-(2-(2-(bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino)-6-tert-butoxycarbonyloxy-9-octyl-purin-8-yl)ethynyl)-2-pyridyl)-3-pyridyl)ethynyl)-9-octyl-purin-6-yl) tert-butyl carbonate (II), and removing tert-butoxycarbonyl (boc) group of (II) to obtain 2,2'-bipyridine based biological hydrogen-bonded organic framework material of formula (HOF-25) (III), and reacting (III) with pentacarbonylchlororhenium(I) to obtain final product.A1 = 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-diyl.Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) making (1a), (1b) N,N-dimethylformamide and triethylamine into degassing suspension, adding tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) and copper(I) iodide respectively, stirring the resulting mixture at constant temperature under inert gas atmosphere to obtain reaction mixture, removing the organic phase in the reaction mixture under reduced pressure, and eluting to finally obtain (II), (ii) dissolving (II) in a mixture of dichloromethane and trifluoroacetic acid at room temperature, allowing it to stand for at least 55 hours to remove the tert-butoxycarbonyl (boc) group of (II), placing it in a closed room containing methanol to alcohol extract, filtering and washing to finally obtain (III), and (iii) mixing (III), pentacarbonylchlororhenium(I), toluene and methanol to obtain reaction mixture, stirring the reaction mixture at constant temperature under inert gas atmosphere, filtering, washing, and vacuum drying to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (i), the amount of (1a) is 14-18 mg/ml N,N-dimethylformamide; the amount of (1b) is 225-310 mg/ml N,N-dimethylformamide; the amount of tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) is 1.5-2.5 mg/ml N,N-dimethylformamide; and the amount of the copper(I) iodide is 0.5-2 mg/ml N,N-dimethylformamide. The amount of (II) in step (ii) is 7.5-25 mg/ml dichloromethane. In step (iii), the amount of (III) is 2-3 mg/ml toluene; and the amount of pentacarbonylchlororhenium(I) is 1.5-2.0 mg/ml toluene.

PN US10807924-B1
TI Separating components through distillation column by passing process stream comprising hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water into distillation column, fractionally distilling, and removing middle fluid stream and bottoms liquid stream.
AU BAXTER L
AE HALL LABS LLC (HALL-Non-standard)
   NEWVISTAS CAPITAL LLC (NEWV-Non-standard)
GA 2020A2516J
AB    NOVELTY - Components are separated through distillation column by passing process stream comprising hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water into distillation column at a bottom portion of distillation column; fractionally distilling process stream in the distillation column to form overhead vapor stream, middle fluid stream, and bottoms liquid stream; removing middle fluid stream from middle location of distillation column; removing bottoms liquid stream from distillation column; and removing overhead vapor stream from distillation column. Middle fluid stream comprises 1st portion of carbon dioxide and 1st portion of hydrocarbons; and bottoms liquid stream comprises 2nd portion of hydrocarbons as 1st phase and substantially all the water as 2nd phase.
   USE - Separation of components through distillation column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Compositions: The process stream further comprises hydrogen sulfide and/or nitrogen. Process stream is a liquid and passing the process stream into the distillation column causes a portion of the liquid to flash to a vapor. Process stream is a mixture of liquid phase and vapor phase, and passing the process stream into the distillation column directs the liquid phase to a reboiler and the vapor phase up the distillation column. Bottom portion of the distillation column is below all packing materials or trays. The 2nd phase is a solid. Middle fluid stream is a liquid stream. Middle fluid stream is a gas stream. Carbon dioxide is present in the middle fluid stream at a concentration of greater than or equal to 98 wt.% and contains greater than or equal to 90 wt.% of the carbon dioxide from the process stream. Preferred Processes: The method further comprises physically separating the 1st phase and the 2nd phase. Physically separating the 1st phase and the 2nd phase comprises passing the bottoms liquid stream through a solid-liquid separator. Solvent is added to the process stream, where solvent is miscible, mixes with 2nd phase, and depresses freezing point of 2nd phase such that the water and the solvent are a liquid. Physically separating the 1st phase and the 2nd phase comprises allowing the bottoms stream to gravity separate and removing the 1st phase by decanting the 1st phase from the 2nd phase. Fractionally distilling the process stream in the distillation column further comprises operating a condenser at a top portion of the distillation column and a reboiler below the bottom portion of the distillation column. Process stream is passed into the distillation column above a required reboil temperature such that the distillation column acts as a rectifying column. The method further comprises maintaining the distillation column at a pressure at most 3 bar below supercritical pressure. Preferred Device: Solid-liquid separator comprises a screw-filter press. Condenser is operated within 5 degrees C above the freezing point of the overhead vapor stream. Distillation column is a packed column.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Solvent is a water miscible polar molecule selected from alcohols, ketones, and/or aldehydes.

PN CN110721678-A
TI Ruthenium-based highly dispersed supported catalyst used for photothermal coupling catalytic methanation of carbon dioxide, comprises cerium(IV) oxide having selectively exposed (100) crystal plane, and ruthenium nanoparticles.
AU DAI W
   JIANG R
   CHEN X
   FU X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202010610C
AB    NOVELTY - A ruthenium (Ru)-based highly dispersed supported catalyst comprises cerium(IV) oxide (CeO2) having selectively exposed (100) crystal plane as carrier, and 1 wt.% Ru nanoparticles as active component.
   USE - The Ru-based highly dispersed supported catalyst is used for photothermal coupling catalytic methanation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is simple and easy to prepare, and improves CO2 methanation efficiency and reduces reaction temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the Ru-based catalyst by selectively exposing (100) crystal plane of CeO2, and loading Ru nanoparticles on the carrier by photoreduction method; and
   (2) application of Ru-based catalyst for photothermal coupling catalytic methanation of carbon dioxide (CO2) for converting CO2 to methane under light-assisted thermocatalytic conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises dropping 14 mol/L sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution to 0.113 mol/L cerium nitrate (Ce(NO3)3) solution, hydrothermally reacting at 180 degrees C for 24 hours, washing with water, drying, and calcining to obtain CeO2 (100) carrier; and adding, per 1 g CeO2 carrier, 2 mL ruthenium tetrachloride (RuCl4) solution, 5 mL deionized (DI) water and 5 mL anhydrous methanol to CeO2 (100) carrier, introducing nitrogen gas (N2), irradiating under xenon lamp for 1 hour, centrifuging, washing with DI water, and drying at 80 degrees C. Preferred Components: The volume ratio of NaOH solution and Ce(NO3)3 solution is 3:4. The concentration of Ru4+ in RuCl4 solution is 5 mg/mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The thermocatalytic conditions include illumination by visible light and temperature of 160-280 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110724793-A; CN110724793-B
TI Decarburizing using multi-gas segmented bottom blowing comprises supplying first gas by argon blowing pipe, blowing oxygen and carbon dioxide gas, stopping the supply of first gas, supplying second gas and starting the blow of argon gas.
AU LI L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 202011008N
AB    NOVELTY - Decarburizing using multi-gas segmented bottom blowing comprises (i) supplying first gas by argon blowing pipe when the pressure in the vacuum degassing tank is less than 17 kPa, (ii) blowing oxygen gas by oxygen lance and carbon dioxide gas by bottom blowing pipe when the argon blowing pipe is supplying first gas and (iii) stopping the supply of first gas when the pressure in the vacuum degassing tank is less than 300 Pa, supplying second gas and stopping the blow of carbon dioxide gas and starting the blow of argon gas.
   USE - The method is useful in decarburizing using multi-gas segmented bottom blowing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, rapid; and ensures good decarburization effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first gas is carbon dioxide-argon mixed gas. The second gas is argon gas, preferably argon-hydrogen mixed gas. In step (ii) the gas supply intensity of the bottom blowing pipe for blowing carbon dioxide gas is 0.7-1.2 Nm3/hx t. In step (iii) the bottom gas blowing intensity of argon gas is 0.7-1.2 Nm3/hx t. The amount of carbon dioxide in the first gas is 50-65 wt.%. The amount of hydrogen in the argon-hydrogen mixed gas is 55-70 wt.% Preferred Method: The process comprises performing decarburization of molten steel having 0.07-0.09 wt.% carbon content and 0.04-0.05 wt.% oxygen content at 1650-1700 degrees C.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

PN CN110240968-A
TI Extraction of pine pollen net oil by ultrafinely grinding pine pollen, refluxing twice with ethanol, filtering, concentrating, slowly pumping tosupercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting, and separating.
AU ZHENG Z
AE SHENZHEN XIYUN IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201991568K
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of pine pollen net oil comprises ultrafinely grinding pine pollen, refluxing twice with 4-8 times its weight 92% ethanol, filtering, concentrating to density of 1.2-1.4 g/cm3, slowly pumping to 5 L supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting at 35-45 degrees C, 20-40 MPa and at carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-25 L/hour, adding to primary separation tank, separating at 55-80 degrees C and at 6-12 MPa adding to secondary separation tank, and separating at 30-50 degrees C and at 5 MPa.
   USE - The method is for extraction of pine pollen net oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil has high quality. The method is simple, and has reduced processing cost, and convenient operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction is done at extraction kettle temperature of 50 degrees C, more than 99% pure food-grade carbon dioxide flow rate of 20 L/hour, and primary separation vessel temperature of 80 degrees C and pressure of 10 MPa.

PT P
PN CN109913318-A
TI Supercritical extracting borneol essential oil comprises drying borneol camphor leaves, mechanically activating, adjusting temperature and pressure, extracting active ingredient, separating solute-dissolved carbon dioxide, and precipitating.
AU FAN J
AE FAN J (FANJ-Individual)
GA 201957250U
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extracting borneol essential oil comprises (i) taking borneol camphor leaves and drying, (ii) mechanically activating the dried borneol leaves in a stirring ball mill to obtain powder, (iii) placing the powder into the raw material layer, adjusting the temperature and pressure of the system to a set value, and extracting the active ingredient in a supercritical carbon dioxide atmosphere, and (iv) after reaching the extraction time, separating the solute-dissolved carbon dioxide under reduced pressure and precipitating the solute to obtain finished product of borneol essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical extracting borneol essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has materials without phase change process, high extraction rate and no residual organic solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder has a mesh number of 10-50 mesh, preferably 20-40 mesh. Preferred Method: The drying process is carried out until to a moisture content of 8-12%. The stirring process is carried out at a speed of 90-130 revolutions/minute by stirring ball mill, and mechanically activating for 3-7 minutes. The extraction process is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under a pressure of 20-25 MPa for 2-3 hours.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN109589414-A; CN109589414-B
TI Carrying drug load using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device comprises taking graphene oxide or carbon nanotubes as a carrier, dissolving drug in absolute ethanol, transferring, drug loading and drying.
AU ZHANG J
   ZHU F
   ZHANG Y
   HU M
   CUI H
   SONG L
AE UNIV JIAMUSI (UYJM-C)
GA 201935089H
AB    NOVELTY - Carrying drug load using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device using graphene oxide or carbon nanotubes as a carrier, comprises (i) placing the carrier in extraction tank of supercritical extraction device; (ii) dissolving the weighed drug in absolute ethanol to obtain mixed solution, and transferring mixed solution to the cosolvent tank of the supercritical extraction device; and (iii) transfering the mixture in the cosolvent tank to the extraction tank containing the carrier using a transfer pump, adjusting the extraction tank pressure and extraction tank temperature, carrying out drug loading to obtain drug-loaded product, and drying drug-loaded product at room temperature for 22-26 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for carrying drug load using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves drug.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the carrier is graphene oxide or carbon nanotubes. The mass ratio of the drug to the carrier is (1-4): 1. In the step (ii), the drug is an alcohol-soluble aromatic drug, and the alcohol-soluble aromatic drug is aspirin, paclitaxel or adriamycin. In the step (iii), the pressure of the extraction tank is adjusted to 10-30 MPa. In the step (iii), the temperature of the extraction tank is adjusted to 35-50 degrees C. The time for carrying out the drug loading is 0.25-2 hours.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

TI Device used for converting solid organic materials into e.g. methane, comprises functions comprising pyrogasification function, steam reforming function, water gas reaction function, cooling and washing function, and biomethanation function.
AU MERCIER Y
   HEMATI M
AE YANNCO (YANN-Non-standard)
   INST NAT POLYTECHNIQUE TOULOUSE (TOUL-C)
   YANNCO (YANN-Non-standard)
   INST NAT POLYTECHNIQUE TOULOUSE (TOUL-C)
   TOULOUSE NAT POLYTECH INST (TOUL-C)
GA 2018914228
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises functions chosen from (i) a pyrogasification function Pygaz of solid organic materials to form gas, (ii) a steam reforming function of the pyrogasification oils contained in the gas, (iii) a water gas reaction function applied to the gases, (iv) a cooling and washing function of the gases, and (v) a biomethanation function of the gases.
   USE - The device is useful for converting solid organic materials into mixtures of methane, hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device converts solid organic materials into mixtures of methane, hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide in a simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process for converting solid organic materials into mixtures of methane, hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The device further comprises a separation function (F1) of the gases obtained from the biomethanation function to recover hydrogen, a separation function (F2) of gases from the function (F1) to recover methane, a circuit capable of conveying the condensates upstream and/or in the pyrogasification function Pygaz, a circuit capable of conveying the condensates upstream and/or in the steam reforming function, and a circuit capable of conveying the condensates upstream and/or in the water gas reaction function. The biomethanation function is performed in a biomethanation subunit comprising one or more anaerobic digesters.

TI Preparing branched network structured polyvinylamine membrane i.e. utilized for separating carbon dioxide, involves loading branched network structure of polyethyleneimine on halloysite nanotubes.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG X
   HOU M
   LIU C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201892038V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a branched network structured polyvinylamine membrane involves loading the branched network structure of polyethyleneimine onto the halloysite nanotubes and blending polyethyleneimine modified halloysite nanotube composite material with polyvinylamine to obtain a casting solution. The casting solution is coated on the surface of the microporous membrane to obtain the finished product having thickness of 1-100 microns.
   USE - Method for preparing branched network structured polyvinylamine membrane that is utilized for separating carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the branched network structured polyvinylamine membrane with better effect of reducing crystallization of polyvinylamine molecule chain and providing a rapid transmission channel of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing branched network structured polyvinylamine membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The micro-porous filtering film is a support film. The support film is made of polysulfone, polyether sulfone, polyvinylidene fluoride and PTFE. The surfactant is ethylene diamine.

PN KR1911633-B1
TI Recovery of lithium carbonate from lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide from waste battery, involves pyrolyzing lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide, heat-treating, washing and reacting resultant liquid phase material with carbon dioxide.
AU WANG J P
AE UNIV PUKYONG NAT (UYPN-C)
GA 201884478T
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of lithium carbonate from lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide involves pyrolyzing lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide in presence of carbon dioxide under anoxic atmosphere, obtaining a pyrolysis product containing lithium carbonate, heat-treating resultant product under reducing condition, obtaining a heat-treated product containing lithium oxide, washing with water, separating a solid phase material and a liquid phase material, purging carbon dioxide to the liquid phase containing lithium hydroxide and reacting.
   USE - Recovery of lithium carbonate from lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide generated from waste lithium secondary battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and environmentally-friendly recovery of lithium carbonate having high purity, with high selectivity and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The pyrolysis product along with lithium carbonate comprises nickel, manganese, cobalt and/or their oxides. Preferred Condition: The pyrolysis temperature is 600-800 degrees C. Preferred Process: The heat-treatment is performed using methane and carbon monoxide. The heat-treatment is carried out with metal powder comprising nickel, manganese and/or cobalt. The solid phase material is washed and dried to recover nickel, cobalt and manganese.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN108525677-A; CN108525677-B
TI Cerium dioxide/vulcanized zinc indium nanosheet composite photocatalyst obtained by dispersing cerium oxide in ethanol solution of e.g. zinc chloride and indium trichloride and hydrothermal reaction, useful in carbon dioxide reduction.
AU LV K
   YANG C
   LI T
   XIA Y
   DING Y
   LIU B
   ZHANG Z
   YE H
   DU D
AE UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
GA 2018742580
AB    NOVELTY - Cerium dioxide/vulcanized zinc indium nanosheet composite photocatalyst prepared by dispersing cerium oxide in an aqueous ethanol solution containing zinc chloride, indium trichloride tetrahydrate and thioacetamide to obtain a homogeneous mixture, taking the mixture into a reaction container, sealing at 160-200 degrees C for microwave hydrothermal reaction for 1-3 hours, cooling, washing the precipitate and drying, is claimed. The mol ratio of cerium dioxide, zinc chloride, indium trichloride tetrahydrate and thiacetamide is 0.025-0.25:1:2:8.
   USE - The composite photocatalyst is useful for preparing methanol by visible light reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has strong carbon dioxide reducing property under visible light.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of cerium dioxide comprises dissolving cerium nitrate hexahydrate in sodium hydroxide solution, stirring well to obtain a mixed solution, placing the mixed solution in a reaction vessel, sealing for hydrothermal reaction out at 160-200 degrees C for 22-26 hours to obtain a precipitate, filtering, washing and drying to obtain a solid in air and calcining at 400-500 degrees C for 2-4 hours. Preferred Components: The volume ratio of the amount of the aqueous ethanol solution, the amount of zinc chloride, and the reaction container is 20-40 ml: 1-2 mmol: 50 ml. The volume ratio of water to absolute ethanol in the aqueous ethanol solution is 2:1. The mol ratio of cerium dioxide, zinc chloride, indium trichloride tetrahydrate and thiacetamide is 0.1-0.2:1:2:8. The volume ratio of cerium nitrate, sodium hydroxide and reaction vessel is 1.5-3 mmol: 0.2-0.3 mol: 100 m.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
