PN KR2018024887-A; KR1913000-B1
TI Treatment of industrial waste involves mixing to-be-processed waste with solvent, reducing alkaline-earth-metal ions with ligand, reacting resultant complex with carbon dioxide, separating carbonate, and electrochemically processing.
AU YOU J Y
   AHN H R
   KOO J H
   KIM Y S
   CHOI S H
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 2018215334
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of industrial waste involves mixing a to-be-processed waste with a solvent, extracting alkaline-earth-metal ions, reducing alkaline-earth-metal ions with a ligand, obtaining a complex, separating the complex, reacting the complex with carbon dioxide gas, generating carbonate, separating the carbonate, processing resultant by electrochemical method, separating ligand and alkaline-earth-metal ions, and recycling alkaline-earth-metal ions to reaction of the complex with carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Treatment of industrial waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly and efficient treatment of industrial waste with high extraction of cerussite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for treatment of industrial waste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline-earth-metal ions are chosen from divalent magnesium ions and divalent calcium ions. The carbonate is chosen from calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate. Preferred Process: The separation of the complex is performed using a filter including pores having pore diameter of 30-50 mu m. The separation of the carbonate is performed using a filer including pores having pore diameter of 30-40 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ligand is chosen from acetic acid, nitrilotriacetic acid, nitrobenzene, iminodiacetic acid and oxalic acid.

The combustion efficiency is improved, the generation of the nitrogen pollutant in the gas flue is reduced, the consumption of the coal power generation is reduced and the integrated unit is cleaner. The carbon dioxide-rich product can reach the purity of 99.99% food grade. The portion of the rich carbon dioxide product can be conveyed back to the rich oxygen burner to meet the more stringent carbon emission standards and can be applied to the industry with high purity carbon dioxide demand in food industry.

PN US2018044573-A1; US10457857-B2
TI Petroleum or gas recovery from subterranean petroleum or gas formations, comprises mixing liquefied carbon dioxide with a solution of an oil and surfactant to give water-free foam, and injecting the water-free foam into a well.
AU BECKMAN E
   ENICK R M
   LEE J J
   PERRY R J
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
   UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
GA 201813627H
AB    NOVELTY - Petroleum or gas recovery from subterranean petroleum or gas formations, comprises mixing liquefied carbon dioxide with a solution of an oil and surfactant (I) to give water-free foam, and injecting water-free foam into well, where the surfactant is soluble in oil, does not gel the oil, and comprises two hydrophobic components comprising first hydrophobic component that is miscible with the oil and second hydrophobic component that is miscible with liquefied carbon dioxide and non-reactive with carbon dioxide, and where the method is water-free fracking method for petroleum or gas recovery.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering petroleum or gas from subterranean petroleum or gas formations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the water-free foam i.e. stable for up to 10 hours, preferably up to 6 hours (claimed); provides the water-free foam that has an apparent viscosity that is much greater than that of carbon dioxide and can transport higher concentrations of larger proppant particles than liquefied carbon dioxide alone, thus increasing the efficiency of the dry fracking process during recovery of petroleum or gas; provides the stable foams that eliminates the need to inject water and reduces the amount of produced water during petroleum or gas extraction, which is useful in regions where water is a scarce resource; utilizes the dry carbon dioxide-in-oil emulsions or foams that can be used for fracturing water-sensitive formations since there is reduced need for separation of water from the extracted petroleum or gas which reduces production costs, hence economical; and utilizes the oil-surfactant solution that can be re-used and the carbon dioxide can be vented or re-captured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Petroleum or gas recovery from subterranean petroleum or gas formations, comprises mixing liquefied carbon dioxide with a solution of an oil and surfactant of formula (I) to give water-free foam, and injecting the water-free foam into a well, where the surfactant is soluble in oil, does not gel the oil, and comprises two hydrophobic components comprising first hydrophobic component that is miscible with the oil and second hydrophobic component that is miscible with liquefied carbon dioxide and non-reactive with carbon dioxide, and where the method is water-free fracking method for petroleum or gas recovery.
   M1 = (R1)3SiO1/2;
   D1 = R2R3SiO2/2;
   T1 = R4R5SiO3/2;
   Q1 = SiO4/2;
   a = 2-10;
   x = 8-500;
   y, b = 0-10; and
   R1-R5 = 1-250C alkyl or 1-250C alkenyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method, 30-99 wt.%, preferably 50-75 wt.% liquefied carbon dioxide is mixed with 1-70 wt.%, preferably 25-50 wt.% of the solution of an oil and a surfactant. The viscosity of the water-free foam is at least two times the initial viscosity, where the initial viscosity is the viscosity of the liquefied carbon dioxide prior to mixing the liquefied carbon dioxide with the solution of oil and surfactant. The method further comprises injecting proppants into the petroleum or gas well. Preferred Components: The surfactant (I) is substituted siloxane compound of formulae (R13-Si(R13)2-O-(-Si-(R22)(R23)-O-)x2-(-Si-(R21)2(R12)) (III) (preferred) or (R11-Si(R21)2-O-(-Si-(R22)(R23)-O-)x1-(-Si-(R24)(R25)-O-)y1-Si(R21)2-R11) (II).R21-R23 = 1-6C alkyl, preferably methyl;R24 = 1-250C alkyl, preferably methyl;R25 = 1-250C alkyl, preferably -CH2-CH2-(CH2)n-CH3;at least one of R24 or R25 = 12-250C alkyl;x1 = 8-500;y1 = 1-10;x1+y1 = greater than 10 and less than or equal to 500;R11 = 1-6C alkyl;n = 9-250;R13 = 1-250C alkyl, preferably 1-6C alkyl, where at least one R13 is 12-250C alkyl;R12 = -CH2-CH2-(CH2)n-CH2-CH3; andx2 = greater than 8 and less than or equal to 500.The solution of an oil and a surfactant comprises 0.01-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-3 wt.% of the surfactant in the oil. The oil in the solution of oil and a surfactant is crude oil, mineral oil, a plant based oil, diesel, kerosene, gasoline, jet fuel or synthetic oil.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) includes: a first                methanation reactor (4) that produces methane from                a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen; a gas                ratio acquisition unit (3) that acquires a gas                ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen; a flow rate                adjusting unit that adjusts the flow rate of the                mixed gas; a second methanation reactor to which                the exhaust gas discharged from the first                methanation reactor is supplied and methane is                produced from the exhaust gas; a downstream gas                supply unit (2) that supplies carbon dioxide or                hydrogen to the second methanation reactor; and a                control unit (1) that controls the flow rate of                carbon dioxide or hydrogen supplied to the second                methanation reactor, where the control unit                includes the gas ratio, the time for delaying the                transportation of the exhaust gas generated by the                tank, and the downstream flow rate, which is the                flow rate of carbon dioxide or hydrogen supplied                from the downstream gas supply unit to the second                methanation reactor, is determined.

The first agglomeration chamber is fluid-connectable or fluid-connected to the first inlet port, at least a first compressing device being configured for compressing the dry ice snow made in the agglomeration chamber, at least partly into compressed dry ice, at least a first shredding device being configured for shredding the compressed dry ice, at least partly into dry ice particles, and an outlet for discharging the dry ice particles.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for surface treatment of human and/or animal tissue by using dry ice particles (19), preferably for surface treatment of human and/or animal skin and for making medical, preferably medical clean or dry ice particles (claimed) and for making medical, in particular medical clean, preferably sterile, dry ice particles of medical pressurized liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus avoids contamination of the carbon dioxide with pathogens during manufacturing the dry ice particle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) operating an apparatus comprising providing medical pressurized liquid carbon dioxide, - delivering the medical pressurized liquid carbon dioxide into at least one agglomeration chamber of the apparatus and expanding the pressurized liquid carbon dioxide within the agglomeration chamber for making medical dry ice snow, compressing the medical dry ice snow at least partly to medical dry ice preferably to a medical dry ice block and shredding the compressed medical dry ice at least partly into medical dry ice particles;

   (4) generating a medical particle jet for surface treatment of human and/or animal tissue, where the particle jet comprises medical carrier fluid flow and medical dry ice particles, comprising making medical dry ice particles by performing method and adding the manufactured medical dry ice particles, at least partly into a medical carrier fluid flow to generate the particle jet, and discharging by the outlet of the apparatus the particle jet generated.

PT P
PN CN114057902-A
TI Extracting high activity polysaccharide from            Grifola frondosa using alcohol precipitation method            involves removing impurities from the frondosa,            washing, drying, and pulverizing, and mixing Grifola            frondosa powder with ethanol solution.
AU WEI F
   MA Z
   LIU G
   ZHAO W
   ZHAO H
   JIAO F
   ZONG Z
   WEI X
AE LIAONING GUOZHONG HEAVY CARBON RESOURCES IND ENG              TECHNOLOGY (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 202237109L
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting high activity polysaccharide from                Grifola frondosa using alcohol precipitation method                involves removing impurities from the frondosa,                washing, drying, and pulverizing below 200 mesh,                mixing Grifola frondosa powder with 5-40 wt.%                ethanol solution, and putting into a sealed                extraction reactor. The carbon dioxide gas is                pressurized to a supercritical state, and is slowly                input into extraction kettle. The high activity                polysaccharide of Grifola frondosa powder is                extracted together in carbon dioxide supercritical                liquid and ethanol solution for 4-10 hours. The                carbon dioxide liquid and the ethanol liquid in the                extraction kettle are overflowed into the first                sealed container communicated with the extraction                kettle, and the first sealed container is                discharged into carbon dioxide gas transformed by                carbon dioxide solution. The remaining carbon                dioxide liquid and ethanol liquid overflow to a                second sealed container in communication with the                first sealed container.
   USE - Method for extracting high activity                polysaccharide from Grifola frondosa using alcohol                precipitation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The e process route is simple, the cost is                low, the efficiency is high, the structure of the                high-activity polysaccharide is maintained, the                yield of the high-activity polysaccharide reaches                7.12%, and the purity of the high-activity                polysaccharide can reach 99.37%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting high activity polysaccharide from                Grifola frondosa using alcohol precipitation method                involves removing impurities from the frondosa,                washing, drying, and pulverizing below 200 mesh,                mixing Grifola frondosa powder with 5-40 wt.%                ethanol solution, and putting into a sealed                extraction reactor. The carbon dioxide gas is                pressurized to a supercritical state, and is slowly                input into the extraction kettle. The high activity                polysaccharide of Grifola frondosa powder is                extracted together in carbon dioxide supercritical                liquid and ethanol solution for 4-10 hours. The                carbon dioxide liquid and the ethanol liquid in the                extraction kettle are overflowed into the first                sealed container communicated with the extraction                kettle, and the first sealed container is                discharged into the carbon dioxide gas transformed                by the carbon dioxide solution. The remaining                carbon dioxide liquid and ethanol liquid overflow                to a second sealed container in communication with                the first sealed container. The carbon dioxide gas                is released in the second sealed container,                completely converted to obtain a liquid containing                high activity polysaccharide ethanol, then                increased the ethanol concentration to the                high-activity polysaccharide ethanol liquid to                obtain high-activity polysaccharide crystals to                obtain ethanol liquid and high-activity                polysaccharide crystals. The highly active                polysaccharide crystals added into the pure aqueous                solution, so that the highly active polysaccharide                soluble in water is completely dissolved in the                pure aqueous solution. The insoluble impurities of                the highly active polysaccharide insoluble in water                are removed, and obtained a pure aqueous solution                containing the highly active polysaccharide                dissolved in water, then concentrated pure aqueous                solution containing the high-activity                polysaccharide dissolved in water, and performed                freeze-drying after concentration to obtain the                crude high-activity polysaccharide. The crude                high-activity polysaccharide is purified by gel                chromatography to obtain a pure high-activity                polysaccharide.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)

   USE - The mixture is useful for preserving fresh traditional Chinese herbal medicinal decoction (claimed).

PN CN111617316-A
TI Preparation of adipocyte extracellular matrix particles for preparing hydrogel, involves processing fat by liposuction technology, adding distilled water/hypotonic saline, centrifuging, supercritical-carbon-dioxide-extracting and degreasing.
AU YI C
   YANG J
   QIU L
   SU Y
   XIONG S
   ZHANG Z
AE YI C (YICC-Individual)
GA 202087817B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of injectable adipocyte extracellular matrix particles involves processing fat by liposuction technology to obtain fat particles of human or porcine origin, adding distilled water or hypotonic saline, freeze-thawing, centrifuging to obtain e.g. grease layer and fat matrix mixed layer, collecting cellular components from the mixed layer, adding distilled water or hypotonic saline, mixing to collect a fat matrix mixed tissue, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and degreasing by dissolving lipids and cells and reacting with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, passing through a separator to obtain polar amino acids and extracellular matrix, adjusting temperature and pressure to restore supercritical state of carbon dioxide to gas state to achieve separation of carbon dioxide and extracted grease, liquefying the fluid, repeating the processes, until lipids and cells in fat particles are completely extracted, processing, and performing cryopreservation.
   USE - Preparation of injectable adipocyte extracellular matrix particles used for preparing fat-derived extracellular matrix hydrogel for facial rejuvenation and soft tissue injury repair (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of injectable adipocyte extracellular matrix particles having high purity, in short time, without requiring chemical substances and enzymatic substances that are destructive to tissues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of injectable adipocyte extracellular matrix particles involves collecting (i) and processing fat by liposuction technology to obtain fat particles of human or porcine origin, rinsing the fat particles with deionized water to remove blood components, processing in a homogenizer at 8000 rpm, centrifuging for 2 minutes, adding distilled water or hypotonic saline, mixing, and maintaining for 20 minutes, performing (ii) freeze-thawing by placing the processed fat particles in a refrigerator at -80 degrees C for 2 hours, placing in 37 degrees C water bath until completely thawed, and repeating the process (ii) five times, performing (iii) mechanical emulsion processing of the fat particles through a metal conversion joint with an inner diameter of 1 mm, using a syringe or a fat mechanical emulsion processing device, and transferring back and forth 60-120 times at a uniform rate of 10 ml/second, performing (iv) high-speed centrifugation operation by collecting the fat particles in a centrifuge tube, performing high-speed centrifugation to obtain four layers of stratification, including a grease layer, a fat matrix mixed layer, a moisture layer and a cell layer from the top to bottom, detecting the fat matrix mixed layer, collecting cellular components from the fat matrix mixed layer, adding distilled water or hypotonic saline, mixing and maintaining for 20 minutes, repeating the processes (ii)-(iv) once or even several times until there are no residual cells to collect a fat matrix mixed tissue, adding (v) the grease layer to the fat matrix mixed tissue, and performing high-speed centrifugal degreasing, in which grease is deposited on the upper layer and removed, a middle layer is retained by the fatty matrix mixed tissue, and a small amount of water in the lower layer can be removed, continuously analyzing (vi) the fat content of the fat matrix mixed tissue, repeating the process (v), until no fat content is detected, to obtain a fat-derived extracellular matrix, performing (vii) supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and degreasing treatment by transferring the fat matrix mixed layer to a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, using a pressure regulator (r1) to adjust the pressure to 18-19 MPa, adjusting the temperature to 36.5-37.5 degrees C using a temperature regulator, adjusting flow of carbon dioxide in the state of supercritical fluid to 23-25 L/hour, flowing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid through the extractor to contact and react with the collected fat particles, dissolving lipids and cells and reacting with the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, passing through a separator to obtain polar amino acids and extracellular matrix, adjusting the temperature to more than 32 degrees C and the pressure to less than 7 MPa to restore the supercritical state of carbon dioxide to gas state to achieve separation of carbon dioxide and extracted grease, liquefying the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid by carbon dioxide liquefier and collecting in carbon dioxide storage unit, repeating the processes, until the lipids and cells in the fat particles are completely extracted, and processing for 3 hours to obtain a fat-derived extracellular matrix, and performing (viii) cryopreservation by cooling the fat-derived extracellular matrix to -80 degrees C, lyophilizing and sterilizing with gamma -radiation (25-35 kGy), placing in a sealed bag under vacuum, and maintaining at 4 degrees C for later use. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of fat-derived extracellular matrix hydrogel, which involves fully dissolving the obtained adipose extracellular matrix particles in pepsin at room temperature, adjusting to a pH to 7.4, and placing at 37 degrees c to form adipose extracellular matrix hydrogel; and
   (2) use of the fat-derived extracellular matrix hydrogel for facial rejuvenation and soft tissue injury repair. The adipose extracellular matrix hydrogel can be injected locally through 27 g small needle.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)

PT P
PN US2022267556-A1; WO2022182424-A1
TI Making foamed article, such as article of apparel,            involves placing article and carbon dioxide in vessel,            maintaining vessel at first pressure and temperature,            exposing infused article to second pressure and            temperature at which carbon dioxide remains infused in            portion of solid foamable material.
AU WATKINS R L
   KHLYABICH P
   HANSON L R
   CHANG Y
AE NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
   NIKE INNOVATE CV (NKEN-C)
   NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
GA 2022A9320J
AB    NOVELTY - Making a foamed article involves placing an                article and carbon dioxide in a vessel. The vessel                is maintained at a first pressure and first                temperature, where first pressure and first                temperature are a pressure and temperature at which                the carbon dioxide is a liquid and the liquid                carbon dioxide is soluble in solid foamable                material, and where maintaining includes holding                the article and the liquid carbon dioxide in the                vessel for a duration of time sufficient for at                least a portion of the liquid carbon dioxide to                infuse into solid foamable material of an article.                The infused article is exposed to a second pressure                and second temperature at which the carbon dioxide                remains infused within at least a portion of a                solid foamable material. The article is subjected                to a third pressure and third temperature at which                carbon dioxide infused in the solid foamable                material phase transitions to a gas, thus expanding                solid foamable material into a foamed material and                the foamed article is formed.
   USE - Method for making a foamed article, such as                article of apparel, an article of footwear, or an                article of sporting equipment, or a component of an                article of apparel, an article of footwear, or an                article of sporting equipment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed article is a stabilized foamed                article comprising the stabilized foamed material,                where the stabilized foamed material is free or                substantially free of infused carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making a foamed article involves placing an                article and carbon dioxide in a vessel, wherein the                article comprises a solid foamable material which                is a thermoplastic elastomeric material comprising                one or more first thermoplastic elastomers. The                vessel is maintained at a first pressure and first                temperature, where the first pressure and first                temperature are a pressure and temperature at which                the carbon dioxide is a liquid and the liquid                carbon dioxide is soluble in the solid foamable                material, and where the maintaining includes                holding the article and the liquid carbon dioxide                in the vessel for a duration of time sufficient for                at least a portion of the liquid carbon dioxide to                infuse into the solid foamable material of the                article. The infused article is exposed to a second                pressure and second temperature at which the carbon                dioxide remains infused within at least a portion                of the solid foamable material. The article is                subjected to a third pressure and third temperature                at which the carbon dioxide infused in the solid                foamable material phase transitions to a gas, thus                expanding the solid foamable material into a foamed                material and forming the foamed article, where the                article comprises a layered structure including a                series of three or more layers, including a first                cap layer comprising or consisting essentially of a                first cap layer material, the first cap layer                including a first cap layer outer surface defining                a first outer surface of the layered structure, a                first cap layer inner surface opposing the first                cap layer outer surface, a first cap layer                thickness extending from the first cap layer                interior surface to the first cap layer outer                surface, where the first cap layer outer surface                defines a first exterior surface of the article, a                first structural layer comprising or consisting                essentially of a first structural layer material,                the first structural layer including a first                structural layer first surface, a first structural                layer second surface opposing the first structural                layer first surface, a first structural layer                thickness extending from the first structural layer                first surface to the first structural layer second                surface, and a core layer, where the core layer                comprises or consists essentially of a core layer                material, the core layer includes a core layer                first surface, a core layer second surface, and a                core layer thickness extending from the core layer                first surface of the core layer second surface,                where the first structural layer is positioned                between the first cap layer inner surface and the                core layer first surface, where the first cap layer                or the first structural layer or both the first cap                layer and the first structural layer comprise or                consist essentially of the solid foamable material.                At least a portion of the solid foamable material                is expanded into the foamed material to form the                foamed article.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: One or more first                  thermoplastic elastomers comprise one or more                  thermoplastic elastomeric polyolefin homopolymers                  or copolymers, one or more thermoplastic                  elastomeric polyamide homopolymers or copolymers,                  one or more thermoplastic elastomeric polyester                  homopolymers or copolymers, one or more                  thermoplastic elastomeric polyurethane                  homopolymers or copolymers, one or more                  thermoplastic elastomeric styrenic homopolymers                  or copolymers, or any combination. One or more                  thermoplastic elastomeric polyurethane                  homopolymers or copolymers comprise or consist                  essentially of one or more thermoplastic                  elastomeric polyester-polyurethane copolymers,                  polyether-polyurethane copolymers,                  polycarbonate-polyurethane copolymers, or                  combinations. The first structural layer material                  comprises or consists essentially of a blend of                  the one or more first thermoplastic elastomers                  and a second material, and the second material                  comprises or consists essentially of one or more                  second thermoplastics. The blend is a                  phase-separated blend including one or more                  phase-separated regions having interfaces between                  the one or more first thermoplastic elastomers                  and the one or more second thermoplastics. One or                  more second thermoplastics includes one or more                  thermoplastic ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymers.                  One or more second thermoplastics includes one or                  more second non-elastomeric thermoplastics. The                  core material of the core layer comprises a                  barrier material. The first cap layer material is                  a foamed material. The first structural layer                  material is a foamed material.
DC A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); A86 (Fancy goods, games, sports, toys.); ; P22 (Footwear (A43).)

PT P
PN CN112028084-A
TI Preparing borax comprises e.g. adding prepared boron-magnesium ore powder, sodium bicarbonate produced by absorbing carbon dioxide and unreacted sodium carbonate mixed alkali liquor, borax crystal mother liquor and washing water.
AU WANG D
   FAN X
   WANG M
   ZOU M
   GUAN X
   FAN L
   TIAN W
   ZHOU S
   LIU W
   ZHAO H
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
   LIAONING BOSHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2020C5337J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing borax comprises (1) adding prepared boron-magnesium ore powder, sodium bicarbonate produced by absorbing carbon dioxide and unreacted sodium carbonate mixed alkali liquor, borax crystal mother liquor and washing water into the batching tank in sequence, and stirring and mixing to prepare carbon solution slurry with residence time of 0.5 hours, where the amount of alkali prepared by the carbon decomposition slurry is 95-110% of the theoretical amount, and the mass ratio of liquid to solid is (1.8-2):1, (2) conveying the kiln gas containing 38-40% carbon dioxide volume ratio delivered by the blower into the gas inlet of the jet agitator in the main carbon decomposition reactor, conveying the prepared carbon decomposition slurry in the batching tank to the main carbon decomposition tank through pipeline pressure, and using the first dynamic fluid pump to suck the carbon decomposition slurry in the main carbon decomposition reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing borax.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has carbon decomposition rate is 85-90%; reduces the volume content of the exhaust carbon dioxide to 0.5-1%; shortens the carbon decomposition time by 50%; and saves the comprehensive energy to 50%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing borax comprises (1) adding prepared boron-magnesium ore powder, sodium bicarbonate produced by absorbing carbon dioxide and unreacted sodium carbonate mixed alkali liquor, borax crystal mother liquor and washing water into the batching tank in sequence, and stirring and mixing to prepare carbon solution slurry with residence time of 0.5 hours, where the amount of alkali prepared by the carbon decomposition slurry is 95-110% of the theoretical amount, and the mass ratio of liquid to solid is (1.8-2):1, (2) conveying the kiln gas containing 38-40% carbon dioxide volume ratio delivered by the blower into the gas inlet of the jet agitator in the main carbon decomposition reactor, conveying the prepared carbon decomposition slurry in the batching tank to the main carbon decomposition tank through pipeline pressure, using the first dynamic fluid pump to suck the carbon decomposition slurry in the main carbon decomposition reactor, boosting the carbon decomposition slurry by the impeller of the first dynamic fluid pump, entering the slurry inlet of the jet stirrer through the pipeline, and using the first power fluid pump to circulate the power of the carbon solution slurry, so that the gas inlet of the jet stirrer generates negative pressure zone, so that the kiln gas containing 38-40% carbon dioxide volume ratio is removed from the gas inlet of the jet agitator, sucking into the mixing chamber in the jet stirrer, mixing the carbon dioxide, mixed alkali solution, and prepared boron-magnesium ore powder by the gas-liquid-solid jet to form mixed fluid and producing 300 m/s high-speed jet in the mixing chamber, accelerating the reaction of bicarbonate ion, carbon dioxide and prepared boron-magnesium ore powder, accelerating the discharge due to energy exchange, and shooting toward the bottom of the kettle at angle of 60 degrees in vertical downward direction, where the residence time of the materials in the main carbonization reactor is 4.5-5 hours, the reaction temperature is 135 degrees C, the pressure in the main carbonization reactor is 0.65 MPa, and the kiln gas discharged from the main carbonization reactor contains 13-15% carbon dioxide by volume, (3) conveying the carbon decomposition slurry after the reaction in the main carbon decomposition tank to the sub-tank through pipeline pressure, where the kiln gas containing 13-15% carbon dioxide volume ratio that did not participate in the reaction in the main carbon decomposition vessel escapes through the pipeline to the gas inlet of the jet stirrer in the sub-tank, using the second power fluid pump to suck the carbon decomposing slurry in the sub-tank of carbon decomposing, boosting the carbon decomposing slurry by the impeller of the second power fluid pump, entering the slurry inlet of the jet stirrer through the pipeline, using the second power fluid pump to circulate the power of the carbon solution slurry to generate negative pressure zone at the gas inlet of the jet stirrer, sucking the kiln gas with a volume ratio of 13-15% carbon dioxide into the mixing chamber in the jet mixer from the gas inlet of the jet stirrer, mixing the carbon dioxide, mixed alkali solution, and prepared boron-magnesium ore powder by the gas-liquid-solid jet to form a mixed fluid and produce 300 m/s high-speed jet in the mixing chamber, accelerating the reaction of bicarbonate ion, carbon dioxide and prepared boron magnesium ore powder, accelerating the discharge due to the energy exchange, shooting toward the bottom of the kettle at angle of 60 degrees in vertical downward direction, where the residence time of the materials in the sub-tank for carbonization is 4.5-5 hours, the reaction temperature is 135 degrees C, the pressure inside the sub-tank is 0.60 MPa, and the kiln gas discharged from the sub-tank contains 6-8% carbon dioxide by volume, (4) conveying the carbon decomposition slurry after the reaction in the carbon decomposition sub-tank to the carbon decomposition liquid intermediate tank through pipeline pressure for aging and cooling, where the residence time is 1 hours, at the same time, removing the kiln gas containing 6-8% carbon dioxide volume ratio that has not participated in the reaction in the carbon decomposition sub-tank will be discharged from the carbon decomposition sub-tank and cooling by the condenser before carbon dioxide tail gas absorption, (5) using compressed air to transfer the carbon solution slurry hydraulic pressure in the carbon solution intermediate tank to the filter press for pressure filtration, performing the filtrate produced by the pressure filtration enters the filtrate intermediate tank for buffering, washing the filter cake with carbon decomposing heating steam condensed water, and the washing water produced in the first washing enters the filtrate intermediate tank and merges with the filtrate, preparing the washing water produced in the second and third washings and sodium carbonate into 15-20% sodium carbonate aqueous solution with mass concentration of 15-20%, which is used as the absorbent for the absorption of carbon dioxide tail gas in the step (4), discharging the filter residue generated after washing the filter cake for solid waste treatment. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for preparing borax.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (6) conveying the filtrate in the filtrate intermediate tank to the crystallizer through pipeline pressure, and cooling and crystallizing in the crystallizer to form crystal slurry, (7) conveying the crystal slurry in the crystallizer to the crystal slurry intermediate tank through pipeline pressure, and the crystal slurry continues to crystallize and buffer in the intermediate tank, and (8) conveying the crystal slurry in the crystal slurry intermediate tank to the centrifuge through the pipeline pressure for centrifugal separation and washing, where the residence time of the material in the centrifuge is 5 minutes, discharging the borax, where the purity of sodium tetraborate decahydrate of borax is greater than or equal to 95%, the filtrate obtained by centrifugal separation and washing is mother liquor and washing water, and heating and returning to the batching tank in the step (1) to supply the carbon decomposition slurry for batching. In the step 4, the cooling temperature is 85-90 degrees C. The cooling temperature of the condenser is 30-40 degrees C. In the step 6, the temperature of the cooling crystallization is 60-40 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step 7, the temperature of the crystallization is 35-30 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours Preferred Components: The prepared boron magnesia ore powder is boron magnesia ore powder with particle size D90 of 75 mu m. In the step 5, the compressed air is 0.35 MPa compressed air. In the step 8, the centrifuge is a horizontal double-drum spiral discharge centrifuge. The outer drum for centrifugal separation is 2500 revolution per minute, and the inner drum is 2510 revolution per minute. The jet stirrer is composed of coupling distributor and jet. The coupling distributor is composed of mixed liquid inlet pipe, mixed liquid distribution cavity, carbon dioxide suction pipe, and carbon dioxide distribution cavity. The jet is composed of power fluid inlet, diversion ring, power nozzle, carbon dioxide suction inlet, mixing cavity, diffusion cavity, and mixed liquid outlet.

PT P
PN CN114272892-A; CN114272892-B
TI Preparing meso-microporous complex molecular sieve            useful in preparing adsorbent for capturing carbon            dioxide e.g. comprises decomposing fly ash, mixing            water, active silicon-aluminum complex, sodium            hydroxide, silicon source and aluminum source,            crushing, crystallizing, and roasting.
AU BAI Y
   WANG K
   WANG Z
   ZHANG X
   SUN L
   SHEN M
   HU X
AE HUADIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION SYSTEM (HUAD-C)
   CHINA HUADIAN ENG CO LTD (HUAD-C)
GA 202251994S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing meso-microporous complex molecular                sieve comprises decomposing fly ash to obtain                active silicon aluminum complex, mixing water,                active silicon-aluminum complex, sodium hydroxide,                silicon source and aluminum source to obtain                nano-scale seeds, mixing water, active                silicon-alumina complex, sodium hydroxide and                silicon source to obtain reaction product, mixing                reaction product and nano-scale seed crystal and                crystallizing to obtain microporous molecular                sieve, crushing microporous molecular sieve, and                mixing with mesoporous template agent and solvent                to obtain microporous molecular sieve slurry,                preparing mesoporous molecular sieve precursor                solution, dispersing microporous molecular sieve                slurry into mesoporous molecular sieve precursor                solution to obtain mesoporous complex molecular                sieve precursor solution, mixing precursor solution                of meso-microporous complex molecular sieve with                pore-enlarging agent and crystallizing, and                roasting crystallized product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                meso-microporous complex molecular sieve, which is                useful in preparing the adsorbent for capturing                carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts the modified nano-carbon                material as a hard template agent to carry out                pore-enlarging treatment on the meso-microporous                complex molecular sieve; makes a large number of                mesoporous structures formed inside the mesoporous                complex molecular sieve, which increases the pore                volume; increases the loading capacity of                mesoporous complex molecular sieves for amine-based                functional ionic liquids; and improves the                adsorption capacity and carbon dioxide molecular                adsorption/desorption rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing meso-microporous complex molecular                sieve comprises decomposing fly ash to obtain                active silicon aluminum complex, mixing water,                active silicon-aluminum complex, sodium hydroxide,                silicon source and aluminum source to obtain                nano-scale seeds, mixing water, active                silicon-alumina complex, sodium hydroxide and                silicon source to obtain reaction product, mixing                reaction product and nano-scale seed crystal and                crystallizing to obtain microporous molecular                sieve, crushing microporous molecular sieve, and                mixing with mesoporous template agent and solvent                to obtain microporous molecular sieve slurry,                preparing mesoporous molecular sieve precursor                solution, dispersing microporous molecular sieve                slurry into mesoporous molecular sieve precursor                solution to obtain mesoporous complex molecular                sieve precursor solution, mixing precursor solution                of meso-microporous complex molecular sieve with                pore-enlarging agent and crystallizing to obtain                crystallized product, and roasting crystallized                product to obtain meso-microporous complex                molecular sieve with gradient pore structure.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a meso-microporous complex molecular sieve                prepared by using above mentioned method;
   (2) a carbon dioxide capture adsorbent,                comprising a carrier and an active component loaded                on the carrier, where the carrier comprises the                meso-microporous complex molecular sieve, and the                active component comprises an amine-based                functional ionic liquid with carbon dioxide capture                function; and
   (3) preparing the above-mentioned carbon                dioxide capture adsorbent, comprising mixing the                amine-based functionalized ionic liquid with carbon                dioxide capture function and the meso-microporous                complex molecular sieve, and loading the                amine-based functionalized ionic liquid with carbon                dioxide capture function on the meso-microporous                complex molecular sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The active                  silicon-aluminum complex is prepared by                  performing alkali roasting the fly ash. The                  microporous molecular sieve slurry is prepared by                  mixing water, active silicon-alumina complex,                  sodium hydroxide and sodium silicate in a ratio                  of sodium oxide: aluminum oxide: silicon dioxide:                  water molar ratio of 8-12:1:6-10:250-350 at                  80-100degreesC to obtain microporous molecular                  sieve gel, and aging the microporous molecular                  sieve gel at 20-40degreesC for 6-36 hours to                  obtain nano-scale seed crystals. The microporous                  molecular sieve slurry is prepared by mixing                  water, active silicon-alumina complex, sodium                  hydroxide and sodium silicate in a ratio of                  sodium oxide: silicon dioxide:water molar ratio                  of 1-5:1:35-55, and aging at 20-40degreesC for                  12-36 hours to obtain the reaction product,                  mixing the reaction product and the nano-scale                  seed crystals in a volume ratio of 90-92:8-10,                  crystallizing at 90-120 degreesC for 8-12 hours                  to obtain a microporous molecular sieve,                  filtering the microporous molecular sieve,                  washing and drying and crushing to 200-400 meshes                  to obtain the microporous molecular sieve powder,                  and mixing the microporous molecular sieve                  powder, the mesoporous template agent and the                  solvent according to the mass ratio of                  1-10:0.1-2:10-150. The meso-microporous complex                  molecular sieve precursor solution is prepared by                  preparing the mesoporous molecular sieve                  precursor solution, mixing the mesoporous                  molecular sieve precursor solution and the                  organic solvent according to a mass ratio of                  100:5-15 to obtain a mesoporous molecular sieve                  gel, dispersing the microporous molecular sieve                  slurry into the mesoporous molecular sieve gel,                  and stirring at 600-1200 revolutions/minute (rpm)                  for 20-90 minutes. The mesoporous molecular sieve                  precursor solution is prepared by mixing                  tetrapropyl ammonium bromide, sodium silicate,                  sodium aluminate, cetyl trimethyl ammonium                  bromide and water according to                  tetrapropylammonium bromide: sodium oxide:                  silicon dioxide: aluminum oxide: cetyltrimethyl                  ammonium bromide:water in mole ratio of 3-8:9-10:                  30:1:4-5:1500-1800. The meso-microporous complex                  molecular sieve prepared by mixing the precursor                  solution of the meso-microporous complex                  molecular sieve and the pore-enlarging agent                  according to the mass ratio of 1:0.2-1.5, firstly                  placing at 20-40degreesC for 2-6 hours,                  crystallizing at 80-120degreesC for 8-12 hours,                  crystallizing at 80-120degrees Celsius for 24-48                  hours with the pH value of 9-10 to obtain                  crystallization product, filtering, washing and                  drying the crystallized product, and calcining at                  500-600 degreesC for 6-24 hours to obtain the                  meso-microporous complex molecular sieves with                  gradient pore structure.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Separation and purification device of            heptafluorobutyronitrile-carbon dioxide mixed gas after            recovery comprises carbon dioxide gas adsorption            pipeline, and carbon dioxide gas desorption            pipeline.
AU LIANG S

AE HENAN RELATIONS CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)

GA 2022269962
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and purification device of                heptafluorobutyronitrile-carbon dioxide mixed gas                after recovery comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) gas                adsorption pipeline, and carbon dioxide gas                desorption pipeline, heptafluorobutyronitrile                (C4F7N) gas purification and recovery pipeline, and                mixed exhaust gas treatment pipeline. The CO2 gas                adsorption pipeline includes first solenoid valve                (V1), second solenoid valve (V2), third solenoid                valve (V3), fourth solenoid valve (V4), first                absorption tower (1), second absorption tower (2),                fifth solenoid valve (V5), sixth solenoid valve                (V6). The CO2 gas desorption pipeline includes                first three-way solenoid valve (VT1), first                diaphragm pump (3), second three-way solenoid valve                (VT2), a third absorption tower (4), seventh                solenoid valve (V7). The purification and recovery                pipeline of the C4F7N gas comprises ball valve                (V10), second diaphragm pump (5), a rectification                and purification tower (6), and V-shaped storage                tank (7).
   USE - Separation and purification device of                heptafluorobutyronitrile-carbon dioxide mixed gas                after recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can not only separate C4F7N and CO2                gas, but also can remove impurities in the mixed                gas, the purity of C4F7N after treatment is more                than 95%, the process of the device is simple, and                the energy consumption is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for the separation and purification device of                C4F7N/CO2 mixed gas applied to the recovery, which                involves: (a)opening the first solenoid valve,                making the C4F7N/CO2 mixed gas enters the first                absorption tower through the first solenoid valve,                using first absorption tower absorbs most of the                CO2 gas in the C4F7N/CO2 mixed gas at this                time;(b)opening the fifth solenoid valve, the ball                valve and the second diaphragm pump (5), adsorbing                the mixed gas containing a small amount of CO2 by                the first absorption tower passes through the                second diaphragm pump (5) be compressed into the                rectification and purification tower                (6);(c)performing separation and purification;(d)                closing the first solenoid valve, the fifth                solenoid valve, opening the third solenoid                valve;(e) connecting first port and the third port                of the first three-way solenoid valve (VT1),                connecting the third and first port of the second                three-way solenoid valve (VT2);(f)starting the                first diaphragm pump to carry out negative pressure                desorption to the first absorption tower,                generating a large amount of CO2 and a small amount                of C4F7N gas by desorption are absorbed through the                third absorption tower (4) to absorb the C4F7N                gas;(g)opening the seventh solenoid valve (V7)                again, discharging CO2 gas through the seventh                solenoid valve (V7);(h)opening the ball valve                (V10), starting the second diaphragm pump (5),                adsorbing mixed gas by the first absorption tower                (1) is compressed into the rectification and                purification tower (6) for pressurized                refrigeration and liquefaction, opening the                V-shaped storage tank (7), making the liquefied                C4F7N flows into the V-shaped storage tank (7), and                making the gaseous CO2 and the slightly vaporized                C4F7N exhaust gas enter the mixed exhaust gas                treatment pipeline for processing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                separation and purification device of                heptafluorobutyronitrile-carbon dioxide mixed gas                after recovery.

   Second diaphragm pump (5)
   Rectification and purification tower                (6)
   V-shaped storage tank (7)

PN WO2021236746-A1
TI Combination used in direct electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide-containing feedstock, comprises cathode comprising conductive support substrate comprising hydrophobic regions and hydrophilic regions to aid in adsorption of feedstock from gas, co-catalyst, and coating of nickel phosphide.
AU DISMUKES C G
   LAURSEN A
   CALVINHO K
AE UNIV RUTGERS STATE NEW JERSEY (RUTF-C)
GA 2021D5249J
AB    NOVELTY - A combination comprises a cathode comprising a conductive support substrate, a co-catalyst other than nickel phosphide, and an electrocatalyst coating comprising nanoparticles of nickel phosphide (I), or comprising nanoparticles of compound (I) further alloyed with iron phosphide. The substrate comprises hydrophobic regions and hydrophilic regions to aid in adsorption of a feedstock from gas or aqueous phase to achieve separation from water molecules. Portion(s) of the electrocatalyst nanoparticles are in the hydrophobic regions of the substrate, and catalytically interact with the feedstock by electrical reduction to produce oxyhydrocarbon products. The co-catalyst is positioned to act together with the electrocatalyst by incorporation into the hydrophilic or hydrophobic regions or the by dissolution in the electrolyte or by direct anchoring/incorporation into the catalyst surface.
   USE - Combination is used as two-component catalyst system in direct electrochemical reduction of feedstock comprising water, carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide for generating oxyhydrocarbon products e.g. 2,3-furandiol, 2-formylfuran-3-ol, ethylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-propanediol and/or their stereoisomers (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combination as two-component catalyst system enables efficient direct electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide and/or alpha -hydrogen reactive aldehydes and ketones to hydrocarbons, carbohydrates and other useful products. The combination of the co-catalyst with the transition metal phosphide catalyst changes the carbon product selectivity while having only a small effect on direct carbon dioxide reduction reaction selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A combination comprises a cathode comprising a conductive support substrate, a co-catalyst other than nickel phosphide, and an electrocatalyst coating comprising nanoparticles of nickel phosphide of formula: NixPy (I), preferably Ni3P, Ni5P2, Ni12P5, Ni2P, Ni5P4, NiP2 or NiP3, or comprising nanoparticles of compound (I) further alloyed with iron phosphide (Fe2P), and having a weight ratio of compound (I) with respect to iron phosphide of 99:1 to 1:99. The combination is used for direct electrochemical reduction of a feedstock comprising one of more of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and carbohydrate containing aldehyde or ketone functional groups with active alpha -hydrogens to oxyhydrocarbon products. The substrate comprises hydrophobic regions and hydrophilic regions to aid in adsorption of the feedstock from gas or aqueous phase to achieve separation from water molecules. Portion(s) of the electrocatalyst nanoparticles are in the hydrophobic regions of the substrate, and catalytically interact with the feedstock by electrical reduction to produce oxyhydrocarbon products. The co-catalyst is positioned to act together with the electrocatalyst by incorporation into the hydrophilic or hydrophobic regions or the by dissolution in the electrolyte or by direct anchoring/incorporation into the catalyst surface.
   x,y = integer.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reduction of feedstock comprising water, carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide in generation of oxyhydrocarbon products via electrolysis reaction, which involves placing the cathode of the combination of in an electrolyte together with an anode, placing the anode and cathode in conductive contact with an external source of electric current, providing a source of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to the cathode, and applying the electric current to drive electrolysis reaction at the cathode, where oxyhydrocarbon products are generated selectively from the carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The source of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide is a flowing source. The cathode is in contact with an electrolyte solution comprising the co-catalyst, or the co-catalyst is an ionic liquid electrolyte that possesses bicarbonate or carbonate or hydride transport functionality and is in contact with the cathode. The cathode is in contact with the electrolyte solution comprising the co-catalyst, and the conductive support further comprises the same co-catalyst. The substrate further incorporates a to-be-reduced material, where the electrocatalyst coating catalytically interacts with the to-be-reduced material incorporated into the substrate. The to-be-reduced material comprises carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The substrate is an ionomer or a conducting polymer. The co-catalyst comprises a metal which is copper, silver, gold, zinc and/or their intermetallic compounds. The co-catalytic metal or intermetallic compounds are in the form of nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The co-catalyst comprises an acid which is Lewis acid or Bronsted-Lowry acid. The acid comprises mixtures of two or more of divalent zinc, divalent iron, trivalent iron, divalent calcium, divalent magnesium, trivalent aluminum, tetravalent silicon, alumanylidyneoxidanium, silicon dioxidanium (SiO2+) and boric acid (H3BO3, B(OH)2(OR) or B(OH)(OR)2). The co-catalyst comprises a base which is Lewis base or Bronsted-Lowry base. The base comprises mixtures of two or more of amine, carbamide, urea, hydrazine, primary amine, secondary amine, tertiary amine and pyridine. The co-catalyst comprises an ionomer or a conducting polymer. The co-catalyst comprises mixtures of two or more of a soluble salt of copper, silver, gold and zinc. The feedstock comprises hydrocarbon molecules containing aldehyde or ketone functional groups or reactive alpha -hydrogens.R = alkyl, aryl, arylalkyl, heteroaryl containing heteroatoms chosen from nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur, or heteroarylalkyl containing heteroatoms chosen from nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur.Preferred Process: The co-catalyst binds to a reaction intermediate on the electrocatalyst surface or in solution and influences the intermediate's binding orientation, and/or activates the intermediate for subsequent reaction with surface-bound hydrides or other carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide reaction intermediates, and/or influences the intermediate's binding strength to become stronger or weaker, and/or facilitates the formation of new reaction intermediates on the surface.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI System and method useful for treating wastewater generated in secondary system of nuclear power plant, comprises a persulfate reaction tank, a sulfuric acid reaction tank, an electrolysis tank and a catalyst device.

AE TECHWIN CO LTD (TECH-Non-standard)
GA 201949297S
AB    NOVELTY - Complex wastewater treatment system comprises a persulfate reaction tank (110) for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, a sulfuric acid reaction tank (140) for oxidizing the nitrogen treated in the persulfate reaction tank to nitrogen gas using sodium hypochlorite, an electrolysis tank (150) for electrolyzing chlorine ion remaining in treated water in the sodium chloride reaction tank and converting it into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize partially untreated ammonia nitrogen and COD component, and a catalyst device installed in the persulfate reaction tank to mix the persulfate and produce a persulfate salt and comprising a heater installed to heat the inside of the persulfate reaction tank.
   USE - The system and method are useful for treating wastewater (all claimed) generated in secondary system of nuclear power plant, and multiple desalination plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system and method treat refractory organic matter, can completely treat COD that cannot be decomposed even after conventional physicochemical treatment and advanced oxidation treatment, stably and efficiently operate nuclear power plant, reduce load of entire raw wastewater treatment process by setting treatment process differently depending on type and concentration of the raw wastewater, shorten processing time, reduce processing cost, produces energy from wastewater through reverse electrodialysis power plant, and fundamentally solve problem caused by scale generation occurring when the salt is administered for electrolytic treatment of raw wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Complex wastewater treatment system comprises either: a persulfate reaction tank (110) for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, a sulfuric acid reaction tank (140) for oxidizing the nitrogen treated in the persulfate reaction tank to nitrogen gas using sodium hypochlorite, an electrolysis tank (150) for electrolyzing chlorine ion remaining in treated water in the sodium chloride reaction tank and converting it into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize partially untreated ammonia nitrogen and COD component, and a catalyst device installed in the persulfate reaction tank to mix the persulfate and produce a persulfate salt and comprising a heater installed to heat the inside of the persulfate reaction tank; a persulfate reaction tank for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, an electrolysis tank for electrolyzing the water treated in the persulfate reaction tank to convert chlorine ions contained in the treated water into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize the separated ammonia nitrogen to nitrogen gas and to electrolyze the remaining COD component and a catalyst device installed in the persulfate reaction tank to mix the persulfate and produce a persulfate salt and comprising a heater installed to heat the inside of the persulfate reaction tank; a persulfate reaction tank for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, a sulfuric acid reaction tank for oxidizing the nitrogen treated in the persulfate reaction tank to nitrogen gas using sodium hypochlorite, and an electrolysis tank for electrolyzing chlorine ion remaining in treated water in the sodium chloride reaction tank and converting it into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize partially untreated ammonia nitrogen and COD component, where the persulfate added to persulfate reaction tank is liquid phase and potassium persulfate; a persulfate reaction tank for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, and an electrolysis tank for electrolyzing the water treated in the persulfate reaction tank to convert chlorine ions contained in the treated water into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize the separated ammonia nitrogen to nitrogen gas and to electrolyze the remaining COD component, where the persulfate added to persulfate reaction tank is liquid phase and potassium persulfate; a persulfate reaction tank for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, a sulfuric acid reaction tank for oxidizing the nitrogen treated in the persulfate reaction tank to nitrogen gas using sodium hypochlorite, and an electrolysis tank for electrolyzing chlorine ion remaining in treated water in the sodium chloride reaction tank and converting it into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize partially untreated ammonia nitrogen and COD component, where the persulfate added to persulfate reaction tank is liquid phase and potassium persulfate, and a heat exchanger (120) circulating the treated water through the persulfate reaction in the persulfate reaction tank to raise the temperature of the concentrated water or raw wastewater fed into the persulfate and lower the temperature of the treated water reacted with persulfate; or a persulfate reaction tank for oxidizing chemical oxygen demand (COD)-inducing components in COD and total nitrogen (T-N) co-precipitates contained in pretreated concentrated water or raw wastewater using carbon dioxide sulfate and separating T-N into ammonia nitrogen, an electrolysis tank for electrolyzing the water treated in the persulfate reaction tank to convert chlorine ions contained in the treated water into sodium hypochlorite to oxidize the separated ammonia nitrogen to nitrogen gas and to electrolyze the remaining COD component, and a heat exchanger circulating the treated water through the persulfate reaction in the persulfate reaction tank to raise the temperature of the concentrated water or raw wastewater fed into the persulfate and lower the temperature of the treated water reacted with persulfate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method of treating complex wastewater.

PN JP2022161440-A
TI Method for washing pestle or mortar for tableting,            involves cleaning washing objective surface of            tableting pestle by spraying from tip of injection            nozzle towards washing target surface of            pestle/mortar.
AU SAWAGUCHI T
AE MACHINE PARTS KK (MACH-Non-standard)
   NISSHIN KASEI CO LTD (NISS-Non-standard)
GA 2022D6091A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a tableting pestle                or a tableting mortar. The fine particles of dry                ice produced by using liquefied carbon dioxide are                provided. The washing objective surface of the                tableting pestle or the tableting mortar is cleaned                by spraying from the tip of an injection nozzle                (82) toward the washing objective surface of the                tableting pestle or the tableting mortar. The fine                particles of dry ice generated by using liquefied                carbon dioxide are accelerated by joining with a                carrier gas to be supplied toward the tip of a jet                nozzle. The accelerated fine particles of the dry                ice are obtained.
   USE - Method for washing tableting pestle or                tableting mortar in tableting machine for                manufacturing tablets e.g. pharmaceuticals.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduces the time and labor required for                washing the tableting pestle or tableting mortar,                without increasing the size of the washing                apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a washing                device for the tableting pestle or tableting                mortar.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                washing device for the tableting pestle or                tableting mortar.
   2Liquefied carbon dioxide cylinder
   3Air compressor
   4Water separator
   6Liquefied carbon dioxide gas supply                line
   82Injection nozzle

PT P
PN DE102021001193-A1
TI Carbon dioxide-based power generator based on            gasoline and diesel engines, has separate double-acting            pneumatic cylinder that is provided with hydraulic            cylinder with hydraulic pump connected as before, and            constantly sucks carbon dioxide (CO2) gas out of            container.
AU STRUNK H
AE STRUNK H (STRU-Individual)
GA 2022B58456
AB    NOVELTY - The generator has two identical systems that                are made available with regard to the vertical                hydraulic cylinder and the log splitter as well as                the heat exchanger. A membrane storage tank is                provided in front of the associated hydraulic                motor. The residual gas from the pneumatic cylinder                is fed into a container containing dry ice. A                separate double-acting pneumatic cylinder is                provided with a hydraulic cylinder with a hydraulic                pump connected as before, constantly sucks CO2 gas                out of the container and then compresses gas into                liquified carbon dioxide (LCO2), which fed back                into the process. A dry ice is produced and used                again in the process, if the pressure in container                rises above 20 bar. The resulting CO2 gas is used                for heat exchange, compressed, cooled and returned                to the process by the container, and involves                working with several containers in                circulation.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based power generator based on                gasoline and diesel engines.
   ADVANTAGE - The log splitter is used, which can reset one                or even more hydraulic cylinders by an additional                device. The environment damage through the                combustion of mainly fossil substances is                avoided.

PN CN114455585-A
TI Adsorbing carbon dioxide in air comprises e.g.            preparing carbon molecular sieve, crushing sunflower            seeds and sieving, placing sieved particles into            adsorbent, activating sample, discharging nitrogen and            oxygen, and absorbing carbon dioxidein air.

   TIE J
AE QINGHAI NORMAL UNIVERSITY (UYQI-Non-standard)
GA 202267789T
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbing carbon dioxide in air comprises (i)                preparing carbon molecular sieve, crushing the                sunflower seeds and sieving out particles with a                diameter of 0.6-0.8 mm, placing the sieved                particles into the adsorbent and soaking for 48-72                hours, and filtering the sample, carbonizing sample                for 1-3 hours at 500-700&#176;C under nitrogen                (N2) atmosphere, washing with distilled water and                0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid, drying at                105&#176;C for 9-12 hours under methane (CH4)                atmosphere, activating the sample for 30 minutes at                750-850&#176;C to obtain the carbon molecular                sieve, (ii) placing the carbon molecular sieve into                the absorption tower, and introducing air in the                absorption tower, the air flow rate is 80-160                cm3/minutes, absorbing the CO2 in the air by the                carbon molecular sieve, discharging N2 and oxygen                (O2), and (iii) placing the carbon molecular sieve                absorbed with CO2 gas in the atmosphere furnace at                200&#176;C, and storing in a steel                cylinder.
   USE - The method is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide in air.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon emission content in                the air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  adsorbent comprises phosphoric acid, zinc                  chloride, aluminum oxide, sodium oxide, calcium                  oxide and nickel oxide with ratio of 5:1:1:1:1:1.                  In the step (i), the bonding agent comprises                  paraffin and asphalt with the mass ratio of 3:4.                  In the step (i), the salt solution is a sodium                  nitrate solution, and the content of cobalt and                  nickel is 1% of the sodium nitrate solution. In                  the step (i), the carbonization temperature is                  600&#176;C, and nitrogen (N) flow velocity is                  100 cm/minutes.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  activation temperature is 800&#176;C, and                  heating rate is 10&#176;C /minutes, CH Or CO                  Flow velocity is 100 cm/minutes. In the step                  (iii), the H2 is introduced into the carbon                  molecular sieve adsorbed with CO2 before the                  desorption.

PN CN113831307-A
TI Super critically extracting carbon dioxide fluid to extract taxol comprises crushing European short-leaved yew, pumping carbon dioxide and modifier, flowing into collecting cavity, dynamically extracting, and using reversed phase high performance liquid chromatography to measure taxol.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 2022078168
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) selecting European short-leaved yew, drying, crushing to certain granularity, adding crushed yew leaves particles into the extraction unit of the supercritical fluid extraction device, (ii) pumping carbon dioxide (CO2) and modifier into the conical cavity of each pump by the CO2 pump and the modifier pump, then mixing by a fluid mixer, flowing into the collecting cavity in the extracting unit, (iii) reaching the extraction set temperature and pressure, extracting unit begins to extract, (iv) dynamically extracting, flowing supercritical CO2 fluid into a collecting bottle through the flow-limiting pipe, then vacuuming and discharging, dissolving the extracted product obtained by the fluid in the collecting liquid, (v) using reversed phase high performance liquid chromatography to measure the taxol, associating biological content in the extract, and using liquid mass spectrometry (LC/MS/MS) for product identification.
   USE - The method is useful for super critically extracting carbon dioxide fluid to extract taxol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has few steps; requires short time; has no solvent residue, and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises drying European short-leaved yew at 20-30degrees Celsius. The method comprises crushing European short-leaved yew to 10-20 meshes, where the amount of European short-leaved yew is 80-150 g. The method comprises setting extraction temperature and pressure at 30-45degrees Celsius, and 30-50 MPa. The method comprises dynamically extracting for 1-1.5 hours.Preferred Components: The CO2 flow rate in the extraction process of the extraction unit is 2 ml/minute. The setting ratio of CO2 and modifier in the fluid mixer is 10:1-2. The modifying agent is 60-70 vol.% ethanol solution.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The microelectrode array chip integrates four electrode structures, namely switch electrodes for current detection, microelectrodes (1) and reference electrodes for action potential detection, interdigital electrodes for cell-electrode impedance detection, and temperature Measured platinum thermal resistance. The software portion includes a portion programmed into the embedded system and a host computer (4). The portion programmed into the embedded system includes a portion for communicating with the hardware application delivery controller (ADC) for voltage signal acquisition, a portion for preprocessing the collected data, and a portion for sending the data to the host computer. A film structure is (0.5H0.5H0.5H)S and (0.5LH0.5LH0.5L)S, satisfying lambda /4 period symmetry, where lambda is the wavelength, H is the lambda /4 optical thickness layer of germanium, L is the lambda /4 optical thickness layer of silicon monoxide, and S is the number of cycles. PN CN111685209-A
TI Method for fermenting folium cortex eucommiae black tea under high pressure carbon dioxide environment involves picking fresh folium cortex eucommiae, removing impurity, soaking into liquid, withering, fermenting, drying and baking.
AU ZHENG H
   ZONG W
   DU H
   LIANG S
   LIU M
AE HENAN JINDUZHONG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202095906Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fermenting folium cortex eucommiae black tea under high pressure carbon dioxide environment comprises: (1) picking fresh folium cortex eucommiae, removing impurity, soaking into liquid containing sugar and citric acid and subjecting into ultrasonic treatment; (2) taking out the soaked folium cortex eucommiae, placing into ventilated and dry place for 24-48 hours and obtaining 40-70% water content of folium cortex eucommiae; (3) withering folium cortex eucommiae leaf at 80-120 degrees C and removing water for 15-60 seconds; (4) fermenting at 25-45 degrees C under 60-85% relative humidity and 0.1-1 MPa carbon dioxide pressure for 4-20 hours; (5) drying fermented raw material at 120-185 degrees C to adjust water content into less than 8-10%; and (6) baking obtained material at 185-220 degrees c to adjust water content into 6-8%.
   USE - Method for fermenting folium cortex eucommiae black tea under high pressure carbon dioxide environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can prevent aucubin, chlorogenic acid and other components over-oxidation, from destroying product color, generate fragrant substance, baking aroma and good folium cortex eucommiae characteristic flavor and provide excellent red brown color of tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The soaking liquid comprises 2-5% sugar and 0.1-0.5% citric acid. The sugar is glucose, sucrose or fructose. Preferred Method: The method comprises subjecting into ultrasonic treatment under 200-600 W power for 10-30 minutes.

GA 202075713L
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims a blood oxygen monitoring pre-warning medical system based on cloud database, comprising a data collecting module, at least one intelligent terminal and at least one server, the data collecting module is connected with the intelligent terminal wireless communication, for collecting pulse, blood oxygen and respiratory data and real-time transmitting to the intelligent terminal; the intelligent terminal is in wireless communication connection with the server, for analyzing and storing the pulse, blood oxygen and respiratory data, real-time displaying the terminal carbon dioxide waveform, and real-time transmitting the collected data and terminal carbon dioxide waveform to the server; the server obtains the diagnosis report according to the collected data and terminal carbon dioxide waveform, storing and transmitting to the intelligent terminal; The invention can timely prevent chronic respiratory disease and chronic disease, storing the data to the cloud server, analyzing data on the cloud server, and displaying the analysis result on the intelligent terminal, auxiliary medical staff and medical mechanism for remote diagnosis, effectively realizing the remote medical treatment.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.
